[B01] SEPARATING -- The CPC subclass B01 covers the separation of substances or mixtures by physical or chemical means. It includes processes and apparatus for extracting, refining, concentrating, or otherwise dividing components, such as filtration, centrifugation, decanting, drying and related operations.
[B01B] BOILING -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Documents where liquid evaporation or boiling without separation takes place.
[B01B 1/00] Boiling -- **Definition:** This place covers:
[B01B 1/005] Evaporation for physical or chemical purposes; Evaporation apparatus therefor, e.g. evaporation of liquids for gas phase reactions -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Evaporation or evaporation apparatus for physical or chemical purposes, i.e. transformation of a liquid into gaseous form to be introduced in a further step (mainly reaction), e.g. evaporation of liquids for gas phase reactions or evaporator for organic materials.  **Glossary:** - evaporation: liquid vaporisation that is a surface phenomenon that occurs at any temperature - boiling: liquid vaporisation that is a bulk phenomenon that only occurs when the temperature of the liquid is above the boiling point of the liquid
[B01B 1/02] Preventing foaming -- The B01B 1/02 subclass covers techniques for preventing foam formation during boiling processes, including the use of antifoaming chemicals, mechanical defoamers, and process adjustments that reduce surface agitation or gas entrainment. It relates to operations where controlling foam is essential to maintain efficient heat transfer and product quality.
[B01B 1/04] by chemical means -- The B01B 1/04 subclass covers processes that achieve boiling of a material by using chemical agents, such as adding reagents that alter solubility, promote selective precipitation, or cause reactions that generate vapour to separate components. These methods are applied in separation operations where the boiling step is driven or assisted by chemical means rather than solely by heat.
[B01B 1/06] Preventing bumping -- B01B 1/06 covers methods and apparatus for preventing bumping during boiling operations. It includes techniques such as adding nucleating agents, using reflux condensers, controlled heating rates, and surface treatments that promote smooth boil. The aim is to avoid sudden vigorous vapor eruptions that can damage equipment or affect product quality.
[B01B 1/08] Boiling apparatus provided with reflux condenser -- **Glossary:** - reflux condenser: condenser in which the hot solvent vapours of a liquid being heated are cooled and allowed to drip back into the boiling apparatus
[B01D] SEPARATION -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Processes and apparatus for evaporation, distillation, sublimation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, sedimentation, filtration, dust precipitation, gas cleaning, absorption, adsorption, separation of isotopes.  Cold traps, cold baffles.  Treating liquids by displacement, adsorption, separation or degasification.  Treating gases or vapours by separation, recovering, chemical or biological purification of waste gases.  Separation using semi-permeable membranes, dialysis, osmosis, ultrafiltration.  Separation of suspended particles from liquids by sedimentation, flocculation, settling, filtration or other processes.  Separation of dispersed particles from gases or vapours, by filtration, gravity, inertia or centrifugal forces, or using liquid as separating agent.  Similar processes which are not concerned with, or limited to, separation.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Separating solids from solids by wet methods -> B03B , B03D - Separating solids from solids using liquids or using pneumatic jigs or tables -> B03B - Magnetic or electrostatic separation of solid materials from solid materials or fluids, separation by high-voltage electric fields -> B03C - Flotation, differential sedimentation -> B03D - Centrifuges -> B04B - Vortex apparatus, e.g. cyclones -> B04C - Separating solids from solids by dry methods, e.g. sieving, screening, sifting or using gas currents -> B07B - Presses per se for squeezing-out liquid from liquid-containing material -> B30B9/02 - Making single crystals -> C30B - Into single crystals -> C30B23/00 - Manufacture of hollow fibres -> D01D5/24 , D01F1/08 - Separation of difficult-to-condense gases or air by liquefaction -> F25J3/00 - Condensers -> F28B  **Glossary:** - Filtration: The separation of a fluid-solid mixture, involving passage of most of the fluid through a porous barrier which retains most of the solid particulates contained in the mixture; includes straining solids from fluids. - Filter medium: A porous barrier or porous arrangement of material, which lets a fluid pass while retaining most of the solids which were mixed with it. - Filtering element: A section of filter medium in addition to parts to which the medium is demountably or permanently fixed, including other sections of medium, end caps, peripheral frames or edge strips, but excluding housings. - Filter housing: The fluid-constraining impervious vessel, whether open or closed, which contains, or is adapted to contain one or more filtering elements or filter media. - Filter chamber: The space within a housing where filtering elements or filter media are located; partitions may divide a single housing into a plurality of chambers. - Filtering apparatus: Filtering elements combined with housings, cleaning arrangements, motor or like parts, which are characteristic of the particular type of apparatus. Ancillary devices such as pumps or valves are considered part of a filtering apparatus when inside the apparatus. Ancillary devices performing similar or different unit operations such as comminutors, mixers or non-filtering separators, whether or not inside the apparatus, are not considered part of a filtering apparatus. The term does not extend to apparatus, e.g. washing machines, of which the filter forms only a part.
[B01D 1/00] Evaporating -- **Definition:** This place covers:  This group covers document where evaporation of a mixture is used for separation of components of said mixture.  Document concerning specific application should not be classified in this group.  Means for contacting gas and liquid for the specific purpose of concentration by evaporation  This group does not cover :  Evaporation and concentration with attendant crystallisation is not classified in this group.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Distillation -> B01D3/00 - Evaporation without separation -> B01B1/00 - evaporation in general, e.g. of liquids for gas phase reactions -> B01B1/005 - removal of incrustation -> B08B - preparation of starch -> C08B30/00 - sugar industry -> C13 - prevention of incrustation -> C23F - drying solid materials or objects by evaporating liquids therefrom -> F26
[B01D 1/0005] Evaporating devices suitable for floating on water -- B01D 1/0005 covers evaporating devices that are designed to float on the surface of water while performing the evaporation process. Such systems are used for water treatment, desalination or concentration of liquids by harnessing natural or assisted heat.
[B01D 1/0011] Heating features -- B01D 1/0011 relates to the heating elements and temperature-control measures incorporated in equipment that evaporates liquids. It covers heaters, heat-exchange devices, and methods for supplying or regulating heat to a separation system in order to raise the temperature of the material being evaporated.
[B01D 1/0017] Use of electrical or wave energy  B01D1/0029  takes precedence -- B01D 1/0017 covers processes that employ electrical or electromagnetic energy to effect evaporation of liquids, falling under the broader category of separation operations. This subclass is superseded by the more specific subclass B01D 1/0029, which details particular electrical or wave-energy driven evaporation methods.
[B01D 1/0023] Induction heating -- Induction heating uses alternating magnetic fields to induce electric currents in a conductive workpiece, generating heat directly inside the material without external flame or contact. It is employed for rapid, controllable heating in processes such as drying, melting, brazing, and surface treatment, offering high energy efficiency.
[B01D 1/0029] Use of radiation -- The subclass B01D 1/0029 concerns processes that employ radiation-such as infrared, microwave, ultraviolet or other electromagnetic energy-to effect evaporation of liquids. It covers the use of radiation as the energy source in drying, concentration or other separation operations where a fluid is transformed into vapor.
[B01D 1/0035] Solar energy  for treatment of water  C02F1/14 -- B01D 1/0035 in the Cooperative Patent Classification (EPO/USPTO) pertains to the use of solar energy for treating water, specifically falling under the broader categories of performing operations, separating, and evaporating. This classification is closely related to C02F1/14, which deals with methods or apparatus for removing dissolved substances from water by using heat.
[B01D 1/0041] Use of fluids -- B01D 1/0041 covers inventions that employ fluids to effect a separation by evaporation, such as processes where a liquid or gas stream is used to remove and transport vapour from a mixture, or to assist in drying, condensate removal, or related fluid-based evaporative operations.
[B01D 1/0047] in a closed circuit  B01D3/007  takes precedence -- This classification code, B01D 1/0047, pertains to evaporating processes conducted in a closed circuit within the broader category of separating operations. It specifies that when dealing with closed-circuit systems, the more specific code B01D3/007 should be used if applicable.
[B01D 1/0052] Use of a liquid transfer medium or intermediate fluid, e.g. bain-marie -- The class covers processes in which a secondary liquid or intermediate fluid is employed to convey heat or mass during evaporation, facilitating the separation of a component from a mixture. Typical examples include using a water bath or bain-marie to gently boil or evaporate a material while keeping the primary substance isolated from direct heating. This approach improves temperature control and can prevent degradation of heat-sensitive substances.
[B01D 1/0058] Use of waste energy from other processes or sources, e.g. combustion gas  for water treatment  C02F1/16 -- The B01D 1/0058 entry covers evaporative separation processes that utilise waste energy from other operations, such as heat or combustion gases, to treat water. It emphasizes the recovery and application of by-product heat for water evaporation, improving efficiency and reducing the need for dedicated energy inputs.
[B01D 1/0064] Feeding of liquid into an evaporator -- The subclass B01D 1/0064 covers methods and devices for introducing liquid feed into an evaporator, focusing on the delivery arrangement that ensures uniform distribution, adequate heat transfer and control of residence time. It includes spray nozzles, injectors, distributors and related feeding mechanisms used in evaporation-based separation processes.
[B01D 1/007] the liquid feed being split up in at least two streams before entering the evaporator -- B01D 1/007 relates to evaporating operations in which the incoming liquid feed is divided into at least two separate streams prior to entering the evaporator. This pre-splitting enables different portions to be processed under varying conditions such as temperature, pressure or concentration.
[B01D 1/0076] Maintaining the liquid in the evaporator at a constant level -- The CPC code B01D 1/0076 covers methods and apparatus for keeping the liquid in an evaporator at a constant height during the evaporation step of a separation process. It includes level-control devices, feed regulation and feedback systems that ensure steady evaporation conditions and product quality.
[B01D 1/0082] Regulation; Control -- B01D 1/0082 defines the means for regulating or controlling evaporative separation processes, including methods and apparatus that adjust parameters such as temperature, pressure, or vapor composition to achieve desired separation performance in drying, distillation or similar operations. It covers feedback systems, sensors and control strategies that maintain optimal operating conditions.
[B01D 1/0088] Cascade evaporators -- Cascade evaporators are a series of evaporation units arranged so that the vapor generated by one stage serves as the heating medium for the next, enabling progressive concentration of a liquid while recovering heat energy. This configuration improves efficiency in processes such as sugar crystallisation, seawater desalination, and chemical concentration.
[B01D 1/0094] with forced circulation -- B01D 1/0094 covers evaporating processes in which the liquid is actively moved past the heating zone, typically by a pump or mechanical agitation, to enhance heat transfer and speed evaporation. Forced circulation ensures uniform temperature distribution and improves efficiency compared with natural convection or static baths. This sub-group falls under the broader categories of performing operations, separating, and separation, specifically addressing evaporative separation techniques.
[B01D 1/02] Evaporators with heating coils -- B01D 1/02 covers evaporators that incorporate heating coils to transfer heat directly to the liquid being evaporated. These devices are used in processes that separate components by vaporizing a solvent or water, often in chemical production, food processing, or waste treatment, and fall under the broader categories of performing operations, separating, and evaporating.
[B01D 1/04] Evaporators with horizontal tubes -- Evaporators that contain heating tubes arranged horizontally, so the liquid feed spreads over or runs down the tubes while heat is transferred from the tube walls. These designs are common in falling-film or rising-film equipment for chemical, pharmaceutical and food-process applications.
[B01D 1/06] Evaporators with vertical tubes -- B01D 1/06 classifies evaporators that employ vertically oriented tubes as the primary heat-transfer element, allowing liquid to rise or be forced upward while the heated vapour is removed. The vertical configuration enhances separation efficiency and heat transfer in processes where evaporation is the principal operation.
[B01D 1/065] by film evaporating -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Evaporators with vertical tubes by film evaporating , e.g. documents describing a falling-film evaporator.  This subgroup does not cover the following:  Rising-film and wiped-film evaporators  Liquid being maintained as a film on a support by centrifugal force  Film supported on a travelling surface for evaporation
[B01D 1/08] with short tubes -- This subclass covers evaporators that use short tubes as the primary heating surface. The short tubular arrangement provides a compact path for vapor removal and heat transfer, often in multi-effect or falling-film units. It is applied to processes where space or pressure-drop constraints favor brief tube lengths.
[B01D 1/10] with long tubes, e.g. Kestner evaporators -- B01D 1/10 covers evaporating apparatus that employs long tubes, such as Kestner evaporators, where the liquid to be concentrated flows through extended tubular passages to enhance heat transfer and vapor removal. The design is used in separation processes that require efficient evaporation of solvents or water from mixtures.
[B01D 11/00] Solvent extraction -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Separation of isotopes by solvent extraction -> B01D59/24 - Tea extraction -> A23F3/16 - Coffee: reducing alkaloid content by extraction of the beans with selective solvents other than water or aqueous bean extracts, including supercritical gases -> A23F5/206 - Coffee: reducing alkaloid content by extraction of the beans with water, aqueous solutions without organic or inorganic solvents, or aqueous coffee extract -> A23F5/208 - Extraction of coffee; Coffee extracts -> A23F5/24 - Foods or foodstuffs; their preparation or treatment: natural spices obtained by solvent extraction -> A23L27/11 - Foods or foodstuffs; their preparation or treatment : plant extracts -> A23L33/105 - Cosmetic or similar toilet preparations containing material of vegetable origin, e.g. plant extracts -> A61K8/97 - Preparation of oxyacids of phosphorus involving liquid-liquid extraction -> C01B25/46 - Treatment of water, waste water or sewage by extraction -> C02F1/26 - Purification/separation of acyclic or carbocyclic compounds by extraction -> C07C7/10 - Separation/purification of compounds having hydroxy or O-metal groups bound to a carbon atom not belonging to a six-membered aromatic ring by liquid-liquid treatment -> C07C29/86 - Working-up pitch, asphalt, bitumen by selective extraction -> C10C3/08 - Production of liquid hydrocarbon mixtures from oil-shale, oil-sand, or non-melting solid carbonaceous or similar materials, e.g. wood, coal, by extraction -> C10G1/04 - Refining of hydrocarbon oils in the absence of hydrogen, by extraction with selective solvents -> C10G21/00 - Working-up used lubricants to recover useful products using extraction processes; apparatus therefor -> C10M175/005 - Production of fats/fatty oils by extraction -> C11B1/10 - Recovery/refining of essential oils/perfumes by solvent extraction -> C11B9/025 - Extraction of hop using carbon dioxide -> C12C3/10 - Extraction of metal compounds from ores or concentrates by wet processes -> C22B3/00 - Treating radioactively contaminated material by solvent extraction -> G21F9/125
[B01D 11/02] of solids -- B01D 11/02 covers solvent extraction processes that target solid materials. It includes techniques where a liquid solvent is brought into contact with a solid matrix to dissolve or leach desired components, followed by separation of the loaded solvent from the remaining solid-residue.
[B01D 11/0203] with a supercritical fluid -- The subclass B01D 11/0203 covers solvent-extraction operations in which a supercritical fluid, such as supercritical carbon dioxide, is employed as the extracting medium. It includes methods and apparatus that exploit the unique solvating power and tunable density of supercritical fluids to separate target components from mixtures.
[B01D 11/0207] Control systems -- B01D 11/0207 relates to control systems employed in solvent-extraction processes, encompassing instrumentation, feedback loops and automation that regulate solvent flow, temperature, mixing and phase-separation conditions to improve separation efficiency.
[B01D 11/0211] in combination with an electric or magnetic field -- A process for solvent extraction in which an electric or magnetic field is applied to the mixture to assist the separation of components, enhancing mass transfer or selective partitioning. This subclass covers technologies that combine conventional liquid-liquid extraction with electro- or magneto-driven mechanisms, typically used in chemical, metallurgical or environmental applications.
[B01D 11/0215] Solid material in other stationary receptacles -- The code covers solvent-extraction processes where the solid feed is kept in fixed containers such as columns, trays or chambers while a liquid solvent passes through to remove desired components. It applies to arrangements in which the solid remains stationary and the solvent moves, rather than being mixed or agitated.
[B01D 11/0219] Fixed bed of solid material -- The code B01D 11/0219 covers the use of a fixed bed of solid material as a stationary phase in solvent-extraction operations. It applies to processes where a solid packing remains immobile while a liquid solvent flows through it to separate components, improving contact and mass-transfer efficiency.
[B01D 11/0223] Moving bed of solid material  see  also  B01D11/0261 -- The classification covers solvent-extraction processes that use a moving bed of solid material to facilitate separation, such as continuous or cyclic movement of sorbents, adsorbents, or ion-exchange resins within the extraction zone. It includes designs where the solid phase is displaced or regenerated in situ to enhance mass transfer between the solid and liquid phases. This entry is cross-referenced with related moving-bed arrangements.
[B01D 11/0226] with the general transport direction of the solids parallel to the rotation axis of the conveyor, e.g. worm -- The CPC entry B01D 11/0226 covers solvent-extraction processes that employ a rotating conveyor in which the solid particles move parallel to the axis of rotation, such as a worm-type screw, to enhance contact between the solid phase and extracting solvent.
[B01D 11/023] using moving bands, trays fixed on moving transport chains -- The code B01D 11/023 covers solvent-extraction processes that employ continuous moving-band or tray elements mounted on transport chains to bring the liquid phases into contact. The moving carriers provide a high-throughput, repeatable surface for mass transfer, enabling efficient continuous separation of components.
[B01D 11/0234] using other slow rotating arms or elements, whereby the general transport direction of the solids is not parallel to the rotation axis, e.g. perpendicular  B01D11/0238  takes precedence -- This subclass covers solvent-extraction devices that use additional slow-rotating arms or elements that move solids in a direction not parallel to the rotation axis, for example perpendicular transport. It falls under performing operations → separating → separation → solvent extraction and is superseded by subclass B01D11/0238.
[B01D 11/0238] on fixed or rotating flat surfaces, e.g. tables combined with rotating elements or on rotating flat surfaces -- This subclass covers solvent-extraction operations that are carried out on flat surfaces, either stationary or rotating, such as tables or rotating plates. It includes apparatus and methods where the material to be extracted and the solvent contact each other on these flat surfaces to achieve separation.
[B01D 11/0242] in towers, e.g. comprising contacting elements -- The CPC entry B01D 11/0242 covers solvent-extraction operations that are carried out in tower-type apparatus, such as columns or scrubbers, where the feed and extracting phases are brought into contact by internal elements like plates, trays or packing. It pertains to the use of such contacting devices to achieve separation of components.
[B01D 11/0246] comprising rotating means -- This CPC subclass covers solvent extraction processes that incorporate rotating components such as rotors, mixers or agitators to enhance phase contact and separation. The rotating means are employed to improve mass transfer between the solvent and the material being extracted, typically in continuous or batch operations. It falls under Performing Operations → Separating → Separation → Solvent extraction, code B01D 11/0246.
[B01D 11/0249] comprising jet means -- The subclass B01D 11/0249 covers solvent-extraction operations that employ jet means, where a high-velocity jet of liquid or gas is used to mix, disperse or contact the solvent with the material to be extracted. It includes apparatus and methods that create turbulence or fine droplets with jets to improve mass transfer during the separation process.
[B01D 11/0253] Fluidised bed of solid materials -- This subclass covers processes where solid particles are kept in a fluidised state while a solvent passes through, enabling efficient extraction or leaching of components. The fluidised bed provides high surface contact and uniform temperature, and is applied in metallurgy, chemical processing, and waste treatment.
[B01D 11/0257] using mixing mechanisms, e.g. stirrers, jets  B01D11/0242  takes precedence -- This subclass covers solvent extraction processes that employ mixing mechanisms such as stirrers or jets to improve contact between phases. It falls under the broader categories of performing operations, separating and separation, specifically solvent extraction. Note that the related subclass B01D11/0242 is higher in the hierarchy and therefore takes precedence.
[B01D 11/0261] comprising vibrating mechanisms, e.g. mechanical, acoustical -- This subclass concerns solvent-extraction processes that incorporate vibrating mechanisms-mechanical shakers, ultrasonic or other acoustic devices-to agitate the phases, improve mixing and increase mass-transfer efficiency.
[B01D 11/0265] Applying ultrasound -- B01D 11/0265 covers the use of ultrasonic energy to facilitate solvent extraction, improving mass-transfer, emulsification or particle disruption during the separation step. It includes methods that apply ultrasound to enhance the efficiency of extracting desired components from mixtures.
[B01D 11/0269] Solid material in other moving receptacles  B01D11/0238  takes precedence -- The CPC subclass B01D 11/0269 covers solvent-extraction operations in which solid feed or product is conveyed in moving receptacles other than the primary extraction vessel, such as screws, belts, or rotating drums, to facilitate contact with the extracting liquid. It is a narrower step under B01D 11/0238, which deals with solid material handling in extracting processes.
[B01D 11/0273] in rotating drums -- A solvent-extraction process carried out in rotating drum equipment, where the drum's motion promotes intimate contact between liquid phases to transfer solutes and facilitates subsequent phase separation. The rotating drum design is used to improve mixing efficiency and residence-time control during extraction operations.
[B01D 11/0276] with the general transport direction of the solids parallel to the rotation axis of the conveyor, e.g. spirals -- The CPC entry B01D 11/0276 covers solvent-extraction equipment in which the solid feed is conveyed along a path parallel to the rotation axis of the device, such as by spiral or helical conveyors. This arrangement allows the solids to move in the same direction as the axis while being contacted by the extracting solvent, facilitating continuous separation.
[B01D 11/028] Flow sheets -- B01D 11/028 covers flow-sheets, i.e., schematic diagrams that illustrate the sequence of steps, equipment arrangement and material streams in solvent extraction operations.
[B01D 11/0284] Multistage extraction -- B01D 11/0284 is a Cooperative Patent Classification entry covering multistage extraction, where a material is contacted with a solvent in two or more successive steps to improve the efficiency or selectivity of separation. The process may involve recycling the solvent, varying operating conditions, or adding intermediate phases between stages. It falls under the hierarchy Performing operations → Separating → Separation → Solvent extraction.
[B01D 11/0288] Applications, solvents -- This subclass deals with the selection and use of solvents in solvent-extraction operations, outlining criteria for choosing appropriate liquids to isolate target substances. It also covers methods of applying those solvents to achieve efficient separation of components from mixtures.
[B01D 11/0292] Treatment of the solvent -- The entry B01D 11/0292 covers methods for treating the solvent used in liquid-liquid extraction, including regeneration, purification, removal of contaminants, and recovery of the extracting agent so it can be reused in further extraction cycles. It falls under the CPC hierarchy of Performing Operations → Separating → Separation → Solvent extraction.
[B01D 11/0296] Condensation of solvent vapours  condensation in general  B01D5/00 -- This CPC subclass deals with the condensation of solvent vapours, a common step in solvent-extraction operations where gaseous solvents are cooled and recovered as liquids. It includes general techniques for removing, condensing, and collecting vapour streams irrespective of the specific solvent or equipment used.
[B01D 11/04] of solutions which are liquid -- B01D 11/04 covers solvent extraction processes involving liquid solutions, where the feed, extractant or both are in the liquid phase. It pertains to the separation of components by contacting a liquid solution with another liquid that selectively dissolves desired substances. This subclass falls under the broader categories of performing operations, separating, separation and solvent extraction.
[B01D 11/0403] with a supercritical fluid -- This subclass covers solvent-extraction processes that use a supercritical fluid as the extracting medium, specifying operations where the fluid above its critical temperature and pressure acts as a solvent to separate components. It falls under the hierarchy Performing Operations → Separating → Separation → Solvent extraction in the CPC, coded B01D 11/0403.
[B01D 11/0407] the supercritical fluid acting as solvent for the solute -- B01D 11/0407 covers extraction processes in which a supercritical fluid is employed as the solvent to separate a solute from a mixture, detailing the use of supercritical fluids in solvent-based separation operations.
[B01D 11/0411] the supercritical fluid acting as solvent for the solvent and as anti-solvent for the solute, e.g. formation of particles from solutions -- This subclass covers processes in which a supercritical fluid functions simultaneously as the extraction solvent and as an anti-solvent for the dissolved material, causing the solute to precipitate or form particles directly from the solution. It is used in solvent-extraction operations to produce fine particles or powders by exploiting the fluid's phase-changing properties.
[B01D 11/0415] in combination with membranes -- B01D 11/0415 covers solvent-extraction processes that are carried out together with a membrane, such as using a membrane to separate the extractant and raffinate phases or to improve mass transfer and selectivity during extraction. The code is applied to patents describing combined membrane-assisted solvent-extraction methods.
[B01D 11/0419] in combination with an electric or magnetic field or with vibrations -- This CPC subclass covers solvent-extraction methods that are performed with the aid of an electric or magnetic field or that employ mechanical vibrations to enhance the separation process. It applies to technologies where such fields or vibrations are combined with the solvent-based extraction step to improve mass transfer, selectivity or efficiency.
[B01D 11/0423] Applying ultrasound -- B01D 11/0423 concerns the use of ultrasound in solvent extraction. It covers applying ultrasonic waves to improve the separation of substances, such as by increasing mass-transfer rates, breaking cell walls, or dispersing solvents, and is classified under Performing Operations, Separating, Separation, Solvent extraction.
[B01D 11/0426] Counter-current multistage extraction towers in a vertical or sloping position -- This subclass covers counter-current multistage extraction towers that are arranged vertically or on a slope, allowing successive solvent-to-feed contacts in a downward or inclined configuration. The design enhances separation efficiency by repeatedly contacting fresh solvent with material that has already undergone partial extraction. It is used in processes where solvent extraction is performed in a series of stages within a single tower.
[B01D 11/043] with stationary contacting elements, sieve plates or loose contacting elements -- This subclass covers solvent-extraction methods that employ stationary contacting elements such as sieve plates or loose packing to bring the extracting and extracted phases into contact, enabling separation of components during continuous operation.
[B01D 11/0434] comprising rotating mechanisms, e.g. mixers, rotational oscillating motion, mixing pumps -- B01D 11/0434 covers solvent extraction operations that employ rotating equipment such as mixers, oscillating rotors or mixing pumps to agitate the phases and improve mass transfer. The rotating mechanisms create turbulence or cyclic motion, facilitating separation of components.
[B01D 11/0438] comprising vibrating mechanisms, electromagnetic radiations -- This CPC subclass relates to solvent extraction methods that incorporate vibrating devices or apply electromagnetic radiation to aid the separation of components. It falls under performing operations - separating - separation - solvent extraction. The code B01D 11/0438 specifies processes where mechanical vibration or EM energy is used to improve mass transfer during extraction.
[B01D 11/0442] Mixers with gas-agitation -- B01D 11/0442 covers mixers that incorporate gas agitation to enhance solvent-extraction operations. The classification applies to devices where a gas phase is introduced or dispersed in the liquid to improve mass transfer during the separation of components. It falls under the hierarchy of performing operations, separating, separation, and specifically solvent extraction.
[B01D 11/0446] Juxtaposition of mixers-settlers -- The CPC entry B01D 11/0446 covers solvent-extraction equipment in which mixers and settlers are arranged side by side, allowing the mixing zone to flow directly into the settling zone. This juxtaposition improves phase contact and facilitates continuous separation of immiscible liquids. It is classified under Performing Operations → Separating → Separation → Solvent extraction.
[B01D 11/0449] with stationary contacting elements -- B01D 11/0449 is a subclass of solvent-extraction processes that use stationary contacting elements such as packed beds, plates, membranes or other fixed media to bring the extractant and the feed into contact. It covers equipment and methods where the phases move relative to a stationary phase for separation.
[B01D 11/0453] with narrow passages limited by plates, walls, e.g. helically coiled tubes  B01D11/0461  takes precedence -- B01D 11/0453 in the Cooperative Patent Classification refers to solvent extraction processes that utilize equipment with narrow passages limited by plates or walls, such as helically coiled tubes. This classification is specific to configurations where the geometry of the passages plays a critical role in the separation process.
[B01D 11/0457] comprising rotating mechanisms, e.g. mixers, mixing pumps -- This subclass covers solvent-extraction operations that employ rotating equipment such as mixers or mixing pumps to enhance contact between phases. The devices are intended to improve mass transfer during the separation of a solute from a liquid mixture.
[B01D 11/0461] mixing by counter-current streams provoked by centrifugal force -- This CPC subclass covers solvent-extraction operations in which two streams flow counter-currently and are mixed by the action of centrifugal force, enhancing mass transfer between the phases. It applies to equipment and methods that create a rotating field to induce turbulent contact while maintaining opposite flow directions, commonly used in liquid-liquid separations.
[B01D 11/0465] comprising vibrating mechanisms, radiations -- This CPC subclass covers solvent-extraction processes that employ vibrating mechanisms or applied radiation to aid separation. It relates to methods where mechanical vibrations or electromagnetic radiation enhance extraction efficiency in chemical or industrial operations.
[B01D 11/0469] with gas agitation -- This subclass covers solvent-extraction methods that employ gas agitation to promote mixing of the solvent and feed phases. It includes processes where bubbles or gas flow are used to create turbulence, improve mass transfer, or aid phase separation during extraction. It falls under the hierarchy of Performing Operations → Separating → Separation → Solvent extraction.
[B01D 11/0473] Jet mixers, venturi mixers -- Jet mixers and venturi mixers are devices that create high-velocity fluid jets or a constricted flow passage to induce intense turbulence and intimate contact between immiscible liquid phases, enhancing mass transfer in solvent-extraction processes. Their design exploits kinetic energy to achieve rapid dispersion and efficient separation.
[B01D 11/0476] Moving receptacles, e.g. rotating receptacles -- The B01D 11/0476 subclass covers solvent-extraction apparatus in which the containers holding the material are moved, such as by rotation, to enhance contact between phases. It includes rotating or otherwise mechanically driven receptacles that continuously change position during the extraction process.
[B01D 11/048] Mixing by counter-current streams provoked by centrifugal force, in rotating coils or in other rotating spaces -- B01D 11/048 concerns mixing of liquids by counter-current streams generated through centrifugal force, typically within rotating coils, tubes or other rotating spaces. The method is employed to enhance mass transfer and separation efficiency in solvent-extraction and related operations.
[B01D 11/0484] Controlling means -- B01D 11/0484 covers the devices, mechanisms or methods used to regulate or adjust the operation of solvent-extraction processes, such as controlling flow rates, temperature, mixing intensity, phase separation or reagent addition, to maintain optimal extraction conditions.
[B01D 11/0488] Flow sheets -- The B01D 11/0488 entry classifies flow-sheet designs used in solvent-extraction separations, detailing the arrangement and interconnection of equipment such as mixers, settlers, and extraction stages. It pertains to the overall scheme that governs the sequence of operations for separating components by liquid-liquid extraction.
[B01D 11/0492] Applications, solvents used -- B01D 11/0492 covers the use of solvents in extraction operations, specifying how particular solvents are applied to separate target components from mixtures, including selection criteria, operating conditions and integration of the solvent-based step within the overall process.
[B01D 11/0496] by extraction in microfluidic devices -- This subclass covers solvent-extraction techniques carried out in microfluidic devices, where tiny channels and chambers are used to separate substances based on their solubilities. It applies to processes that integrate extraction steps into lab-on-a-chip platforms, enabling rapid, low-volume separations within a compact, controlled environment.
[B01D 1/12] and forced circulation -- B01D 1/12 pertains to evaporating operations in which the liquid being treated is moved by a forced-circulation system, such as pumps or fans, to enhance heat transfer and mass removal. The classification covers apparatus and processes that deliberately circulate the feed through the evaporator to improve efficiency or control. It falls under the broader categories of performing operations, separating, and separation.
[B01D 1/14] with heated gases or vapours in contact with the liquid -- This subclass covers methods of evaporating a liquid by bringing it into direct contact with gases or vapours that are heated, allowing the liquid to vaporise and separate from the remaining material. Typical techniques include steam stripping, hot-air drying and flash evaporation where the heat source is a gas phase.
[B01D 1/16] by spraying -- A method of evaporating a liquid by atomising it into a fine spray that contacts a hot gas or vaporising medium, thereby increasing the surface area for rapid heat transfer and moisture removal. This technique is used in drying, cooling and product recovery processes.
[B01D 1/18] to obtain dry solids -- **Definition:** This place covers:  This class is for classifying documents where the feed is a liquid mixture and after treatment a stream of vapour is obtained as well as some solid(s).  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Evaporating to obtain dry solids -> B01D1/24
[B01D 1/20] Sprayers -- B01D 1/20 covers devices that atomize liquids or gases to apply them as a fine mist or spray, typically for coating, cooling, or disinfection. The sprayers are used in processes where a liquid is evaporated after being dispersed, facilitating separation of components. They fall under the broader category of evaporating operations within the separating field.
[B01D 12/00] Displacing liquid, e.g. from wet solids or from dispersions of liquids or from solids in liquids, by means of another liquid -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Drying solid materials or objects by dipping them into or mixing them with a chemical liquid, e.g. organic; chemical, e.g. organic, dewatering aid -> F26B5/005
[B01D 1/22] by bringing a thin layer of the liquid into contact with a heated surface -- A method of evaporating a liquid by spreading it as a thin film onto a heated surface, which promotes rapid heat transfer and vaporisation. This approach is employed in thin-film, flash-evaporation and related drying processes.
[B01D 1/221] Composite plate evaporators -- Composite plate evaporators are heat-exchange units that combine multiple thin metal plates to create a series of channels through which a liquid feed is spread, heated and partially vaporised, enabling efficient separation by evaporation. Their compact design offers high heat-transfer rates and low fouling risk, making them suited for food, chemical and pharmaceutical processing.
[B01D 1/222] In rotating vessels; vessels with movable parts -- B01D 1/222 concerns evaporating operations carried out in rotating vessels or in vessels that contain movable parts such as rotors, drums or internal agitators, where the motion assists heat transfer and solvent removal. The classification includes designs in which the vessel itself or its internal components rotate to enhance the separation of liquids from vapours.
[B01D 1/223] containing a rotor -- B01D 1/223 covers evaporative separation processes that use a rotor, such as rotary evaporators for removing solvents or water by heating under reduced pressure. The rotor creates a thin film on a large surface, improving evaporation efficiency.
[B01D 1/225] with blades or scrapers -- B01D 1/225 covers evaporating processes that employ mechanical blades or scrapers to enhance liquid film breakup and increase surface area for evaporation. The technique is used in equipment where rotating or oscillating blades continuously scrape a liquid film, facilitating rapid heat transfer and vapor removal.
[B01D 1/226] in the form of a screw or with helical blade members -- The CPC class B01D 1/226 covers devices that achieve evaporation by means of a screw-shaped rotor or helical blade members. It includes apparatus where the rotating element is formed as a screw or equipped with spiral blades to increase surface area and promote heat transfer for separating liquids or vapours. This subclass falls under the broader categories of performing operations, separating, separation, and evaporating.
[B01D 1/227] with brushes -- The B01D 1/227 entry covers evaporating operations that employ brushes as the primary means of spreading, contacting, or removing liquid from a surface. It includes apparatus and methods where rotating, oscillating or stationary brush elements facilitate drying of coatings, inks, films or other thin liquid layers by enhancing evaporation through increased surface area and airflow.
[B01D 1/228] horizontally placed cylindrical container or drum  B01D1/223  takes precedence -- B01D 1/228 covers apparatus in which a cylindrical vessel or drum is positioned horizontally for performing evaporative separation. The design utilizes the shape and orientation to enhance heat transfer and vapor removal during the drying or concentrating of liquids. It is a subclass of B01D 1/223, which addresses similar containers but with broader placement options.
[B01D 1/24] to obtain dry solids -- **Definition:** This place covers:  This group is for classifying documents where the feed is a liquid mixture and after treatment a stream of vapour is obtained as well as some solid(s).
[B01D 1/26] Multiple-effect evaporating -- Multiple-effect evaporating is a separation technique in which vapor generated in one evaporation chamber is recycled to supply heat for subsequent chambers, improving energy efficiency. The process is used in industrial and chemical settings to concentrate liquids by successive boiling stages while maintaining low pressure and temperature differentials. This method falls under the broader class of performing operations, separating, and specifically evaporating.
[B01D 1/28] with vapour compression -- The code covers processes or apparatus that achieve separation by evaporation through a vapour-compression cycle, where a refrigerant is compressed, condensed, expanded and evaporated to remove moisture or other components. It is commonly applied in refrigeration, heat-pump drying and other systems that use compressed vapour to effect separation.
[B01D 1/2803] Special features relating to the vapour to be compressed -- B01D 1/2803 covers particular characteristics of the vapor that is to be compressed in evaporative separation operations, including pre-treatment, moisture content control, super-heating or other conditioning steps that affect compression efficiency.
[B01D 1/2806] The vapour is divided in at least two streams and only a part of the vapour is compressed -- This CPC subclass covers processes where vapor generated by evaporation is split into at least two streams and only part of the vapor is compressed, a technique often used to enhance energy efficiency or achieve selective separation, and it resides under performing operations, separating, separation, evaporating.
[B01D 1/2809] At least two streams are compressed -- This subclass (B01D 1/2809) relates to evaporative separation techniques where at least two streams are simultaneously compressed, as in vapor-compression or multi-stream condensers that raise pressure on multiple flows to improve heat-transfer-driven separation. It resides under Performing Operations → Separating → Separation → Evaporating.
[B01D 1/2812] The vapour is coming from different sources -- B01D 1/2812 covers evaporation-based separation processes in which the vapor fed to the separator originates from two or more distinct sources, such as mixed streams or combined condensates, and the apparatus or method is designed to handle the combined vapor flow for efficient recovery of components.
[B01D 1/2815] At least one source is a compressor -- The subclass B01D 1/2815 covers evaporative separation methods in which at least one of the vapor sources is supplied by a compressor, such as in refrigeration or heat-pump cycles where compressed gas feeds the evaporator. It pertains to processes that combine compression with evaporation to achieve separation of components.
[B01D 1/2818] Cleaning of the vapour before compression, e.g. demisters, washing of the vapour -- The entry covers techniques for cleaning vapour before it is compressed in evaporating operations, such as using demisters, spray washing or other methods to remove droplets, particles or contaminants from the vapour stream. This falls under the separating-separation-evaporating hierarchy of the CPC.
[B01D 1/284] Special features relating to the compressed vapour -- B01D 1/284 covers the design and control of compressed vapour used in evaporating operations, including features such as pressure regulation, superheating, vapour recycling, and specialized condensers that improve efficiency or enable specific process conditions. The classification applies to equipment and methods that manipulate the vapour's thermodynamic state to achieve desired separation results.
[B01D 1/2843] The compressed vapour is divided in at least two streams -- The classification B01D 1/2843 relates to processes in which a compressed vapor is separated into at least two distinct streams, typically within multi-stage evaporation or vapor-compression systems. It covers apparatuses that direct the vapor into separate pathways for purposes such as heat recovery, condensation, or further processing. This enables more efficient energy use and product separation in chemical or industrial operations.
[B01D 1/2846] The compressed vapour is not directed to the same apparatus from which the vapour was taken off -- This subclass covers systems in which vapor taken from a unit is compressed and then routed to a different apparatus rather than being returned to the same unit for further processing. It addresses configurations where the compressed vapor is used elsewhere, such as in separate condensers, reactors or heat exchangers, enabling distinct separation or recovery steps.
[B01D 1/285] In combination with vapour from an other source -- The CPC subclass B01D 1/285 covers evaporation operations where the material to be dried or separated is processed together with vapour supplied from an external source, such as using a steam or gas stream to enhance heat transfer or to carry away moisture. It includes apparatus and methods that combine the target material with this auxiliary vapour to improve efficiency or achieve specific product qualities.
[B01D 1/2853] At least one of the other sources is a compressor, ejector -- This subclass covers evaporators in which at least one of the additional heat-or-material sources is a compressor or an ejector, meaning the device relies on a mechanically driven compression or jet-ejection process to supply the necessary energy for evaporation. It is positioned under the hierarchy of performing operations, separating, separation and specifically evaporating.
[B01D 1/2856] The compressed vapour is used for heating a reboiler or a heat exchanger outside an evaporator -- This CPC entry covers processes where compressed vapor generated in an evaporator is diverted to supply heat to a reboiler or an external heat-exchanger, improving energy efficiency by using the vapor's latent heat outside the evaporation vessel.
[B01D 1/2881] Compression specifications (e.g. pressure, temperature, processes) -- The CPC subclass B01D 1/2881 covers the definition of compression parameters-such as pressure, temperature and related process conditions-used in evaporative separation operations. It focuses on the specifications that control the compressive phase of the evaporation step, ensuring effective separation of components.
[B01D 1/2884] Multiple effect compression  B01D1/2815  takes precedence -- B01D 1/2884 in the Cooperative Patent Classification pertains to multiple effect compression within evaporating processes, a method used for increasing energy efficiency by reusing steam. This classification is part of a broader category dealing with separation techniques and is specifically noted to take precedence over B01D1/2815 when applicable.
[B01D 1/2887] The compressor is integrated in the evaporation apparatus -- This subclass covers evaporators in which the compressor is built into the same unit as the evaporation chamber, enabling direct compression of vapor generated during evaporation. The integration simplifies the system, reduces equipment count, and can improve energy efficiency for processes such as refrigerant-based or heat-pump evaporation.
[B01D 1/289] Compressor features (e.g. constructions, details, cooling, lubrication, driving systems) -- B01D 1/289 pertains to the structural and functional elements of compressors used in evaporative separation processes, covering constructions, cooling methods, lubrication systems and drive mechanisms. These features relate to performing operations that separate substances by evaporation.
[B01D 1/2893] Driving systems -- B01D 1/2893 pertains to the power-transmission arrangements that drive evaporating equipment, encompassing motors, gears, belts, hydraulic or pneumatic systems and other mechanisms that rotate, agitate or otherwise energize the evaporator.
[B01D 1/2896] Control, regulation -- B01D 1/2896 covers devices or methods for controlling or regulating the evaporation step within a separation process. It includes temperature, pressure, or flow control systems that maintain desired evaporation conditions to improve efficiency or product quality. The classification falls under performing operations, separating, separation, evaporating.
[B01D 1/30] Accessories for evaporators -- The CPC subclass B01D 1/30 designates accessories used with evaporators in processes that separate components by vaporisation. Typical items include trays, baffles, heat-exchange surfaces and control devices that facilitate heat transfer or vapor handling during evaporation.
[B01D 1/305] Demister (vapour-liquid separation) -- The CPC entry B01D 1/305 covers demisters, devices that aid vapor-liquid separation by causing entrained liquid droplets to coalesce and fall out of a steam or gas stream. They are used in evaporation and similar processes to improve product purity and reduce carry-over of liquids. This falls under Performing Operations → Separating → Separation → Evaporating.
[B01D 15/00] Separating processes involving the treatment of liquids with solid sorbents -- **Definition:** This place covers:  **Glossary:** - adsorption: a separation process that involves the transfer and resulting equilibrium distribution of one or more solutes between a fluid phase and adsorbing particles - sorbent: a material that separates a constituent from a fluid mixture. The action in most instances is that of selective retention (i.e. the sorbent removes only the part of the fluid mixture for which it has the greatest affinity). The retained constituent cannot be removed by shaking or brushing or by a similar mechanical action, but generally can be removed by heating, pressure reduction or use of a stripping or denuding fluid. - chromatography: a separation process in which a liquid flows along a linear path comprising a sorbent with which the liquid competes in affinity for a constituent of the liquid. The constituent is sorbed from the moving liquid by the relatively immobile sorbent and re-dissolved by a later passing portion of the liquid until an equilibrium of the sorbing-dissolving step is set up, causing the constituent to concentrate in a specific volume of the sorbent and to move along the path of the liquid at a rate slower than that of the liquid. - adsorption chromatography: a separation process that is based mainly on the differences between the adsorption affinities of sample components for the surface of an active solid - partition chromatography: a separation process that is based mainly on differences between the solubilities of the sample components in the stationary phase (gas chromatography) or on differences between the solubilities of the components in the mobile and stationary phases (liquid chromatography) - exclusion chromatography: a separation process that is based mainly on exclusion effects, such as differences in molecular shape and size (size-exclusion chromatography) and/or charge (ion-exclusion chromatography) - affinity chromatography: a separation process that is a variant of chromatography that utilises the unique biological specificity of an interaction between an analyte and a ligand - bonded phase: a stationary phase that is covalently bonded to the support particles or to the inside wall of a column tubing - serial arrangements: two or more columns in series
[B01D 15/02] with moving adsorbents -- This subclass covers separation methods where a solid sorbent is continuously moved relative to the liquid being treated, such as in moving-bed, fluidized-bed, or rotating-disk adsorbers. The process relies on adsorption of components from the liquid onto the solid, allowing their removal or recovery as the sorbent travels through the system.
[B01D 15/08] Selective adsorption, e.g. chromatography -- This subclass covers methods where liquid mixtures are passed over solid-sorbents that preferentially retain certain components, enabling selective separation such as in chromatographic columns. It includes processes that exploit differences in adsorption affinity to achieve purification or analysis.
[B01D 15/10] characterised by constructional or operational features -- Group B01D15/10 is impacted by reclassification into group B01D15/102 . Groups B01D15/10 and B01D15/102 should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[B01D 15/102] Process control, e.g. determination or optimization of process parameters; Programmable logic control [PLC] -- Group B01D15/102 is incomplete pending reclassification of documents from groups B01D15/08 , B01D15/10 , B01D15/18 , B01D15/1807 , B01D15/1814 , B01D15/1821 , B01D15/1828 , B01D15/1835 , B01D15/1842 , B01D15/185 , B01D15/1857 , B01D15/1864 , B01D15/1871 , B01D15/1878 , B01D15/1885 and B01D15/1892 . All groups listed in this Warning should be considered in order to perform a complete search.

[B01D 15/122] Solid phase extraction -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Solid phase extraction [SPE] used to concentrate, clean up, separate or extract compounds, e.g. impurities, that are in a sample before it is processed by chromatography.
[B01D 15/125] Pre-filtration -- Group B01D15/125 is impacted by reclassification into group B01D15/122 . Groups B01D15/125 and B01D15/122 should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[B01D 15/14] relating to the introduction of the feed to the apparatus -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Any details concerning constructions or processes related to the feed, e.g. pumps or injection systems.
[B01D 15/16] relating to the conditioning of the fluid carrier -- B01D 15/16 covers methods for conditioning the fluid carrier in separation processes that treat liquids with solid sorbents, such as adjusting temperature, pH, or adding co-solvents to improve sorption efficiency or prevent clogging of the sorbent bed. It applies to operations where the liquid's properties are modified before or during contact with the solid material to achieve better separation performance.
[B01D 15/161] Temperature conditioning -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Stabilising a chromatographic column and detector by controlling the temperature that is measured in the column during the process.
[B01D 15/163] Pressure or speed conditioning -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Stabilising a chromatographic column and detector by controlling the pressure that is measured in the column during the process.
[B01D 15/165] Flash chromatography -- Flash chromatography is a rapid liquid-chromatography method where a pressurised gas forces solvent through a column packed with a solid sorbent to separate mixture components. The technique is commonly used for quick purification of reaction products in organic synthesis.
[B01D 15/166] Fluid composition conditioning, e.g. gradient -- B01D 15/166 covers methods for conditioning the composition of a liquid by passing it through a solid sorbent in order to create a gradient or otherwise modify its constituents. The techniques are used in separating processes where the sorbent selectively adsorbs or releases components, enabling controlled changes in concentration along the flow path.
[B01D 15/168] pH gradient or chromatofocusing, i.e. separation according to the isoelectric point pI -- B01D 15/168 covers separation techniques that create a pH gradient in a liquid to isolate components at their isoelectric point, known as chromatofocusing. This method uses solid sorbents to treat the liquid and achieves separation based on each substance's pI.
[B01D 15/18] relating to flow patterns -- Group B01D15/18 is impacted by reclassification into groups B01D15/102 , B01D15/1801 , B01D15/1894 and B01D15/1896 . All groups listed in this Warning should be considered in order to perform a complete search.

All groups listed in this Warning should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[B01D 15/1807] using counter-currents, e.g. fluidised beds -- Group B01D15/1807 is impacted by reclassification into groups B01D15/102 , B01D15/1801 , B01D15/1894 and B01D15/1896 . All groups listed in this Warning should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[B01D 15/1814] Recycling of the fraction to be distributed -- Group B01D15/1814 is impacted by reclassification into groups B01D15/102 , B01D15/1801 , B01D15/1894 and B01D15/1896 . All groups listed in this Warning should be considered in order to perform a complete search.

[B01D 15/1867] with intermediate treatments or steps performed between columns -- Group B01D15/1867 is incomplete pending reclassification of documents from groups B01D15/1864 , B01D15/36 , B01D15/361 , B01D15/362 , B01D15/363 , B01D15/364 , B01D15/365 , B01D15/366 , B01D15/367 , B01D15/368 , B01D15/38 , B01D15/3804 , B01D15/3809 , B01D15/3814 , B01D15/3819 , B01D15/3823 , B01D15/3828 , B01D15/3833 , B01D15/3842 , B01D15/3847 , B01D15/3852 , B01D15/3857 , B01D15/3861 , B01D15/3866 , B01D15/3871 , B01D15/3876 , B01D15/388 and B01D15/3885 . [B01D 15/1892] the sorbent material moving as a whole, e.g. continuous annular chromatography, true moving beds or centrifugal chromatography -- Group B01D15/1892 is impacted by reclassification into groups B01D15/102 , B01D15/1801 , B01D15/1894 and B01D15/1896 . [B01D 15/1894] Liquid-liquid chromatography, e.g. centrifugal partition chromatography or extraction chromatography -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Processes for separating components of a mixture by distribution of the solutes between two immiscible liquid phases, both stationary and mobile phases being liquid.  Liquid-liquid chromatography [LLC] combines the separation principles of liquid-liquid extraction and chromatography. As in extraction, a biphasic liquid system is used and the basis for the separation is the differing partitioning behaviour of the mixture of solutes between the two phases.
[B01D 15/1896] Membrane chromatography or membrane adsorbers -- **Definition:** This place covers:
[B01D 15/20] relating to the conditioning of the sorbent material -- B01D 15/20 covers methods for preparing or conditioning solid sorbents used in liquid-solid separation operations. It includes steps such as activation, regeneration, drying, or surface modification of the sorbent before or during use. The aim is to improve the sorbent's capacity, selectivity or durability in processes that treat liquids with solid adsorbents.
[B01D 15/203] Equilibration or regeneration -- It covers methods for restoring or re-activating solid sorbents after they have adsorbed liquids, such as flushing, heating, or chemical treatment to return the sorbent to its original capacity. These steps are part of liquid-solid separation operations.
[B01D 15/206] Packing or coating -- This subclass covers the use of packing or coating materials in separation processes where liquids are treated with solid sorbents. It includes techniques for filling columns, beds or reactors with packed media and for applying coating layers to sorbent particles to enhance contact, flow or selectivity.
[B01D 15/22] relating to the construction of the column -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Features related to the construction or shape of the column.
[B01D 15/222] Arrangements of modules or cassettes -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Arrangements, devices or systems where at least a part (i.e. cassette or module) can be removed, replaced or exchanged.  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this place:  A parallel arrangement of three chromatography column modules in a rigid housing.
[B01D 15/24] relating to the treatment of the fractions to be distributed -- B01D 15/24 covers post-sorption handling of liquid fractions, including washing, drying, extraction or other treatments applied to the solid sorbent and its retained components to recover, purify or prepare the separated materials for further use or disposal. It focuses on operations performed after the initial liquid-solid separation step.
[B01D 15/242] Intermediate storage of effluents -- The B01D 15/242 sub-class covers methods and apparatus for the intermediate storage of liquid effluents during separation operations that use solid sorbents. It addresses handling, buffering or holding treated streams before further processing or disposal within liquid-solid treatment systems.
[B01D 15/245] Adding materials to the effluents -- B01D 15/245 relates to separating processes that treat liquids with solid sorbents and involve adding further substances to the effluent, such as reagents, modifiers or carriers, to enhance the removal, recovery or purification of target components.
[B01D 15/247] Fraction collectors -- Fraction collectors are devices used in liquid-solid separation operations to gather distinct portions of a processed stream after it passes through a solid sorbent. They enable automated sampling or continuous collection of separated phases, commonly in chromatography, filtration or centrifugation systems, supporting precise fractionation of the treated liquid.
[B01D 15/26] characterised by the separation mechanism -- Group B01D15/26 is impacted by reclassification into group B01D15/267 . Groups B01D15/26 and B01D15/267 should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[B01D 15/265] Adsorption chromatography -- Group B01D15/265 is impacted by reclassification into group B01D15/267 . Groups B01D15/265 and B01D15/267 should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[B01D 15/267] using neutral sorbents, e.g. activated carbon or diatomaceous earth -- Group B01D15/267 is incomplete pending reclassification of documents from groups B01D15/00 , B01D15/08 , B01D15/26 and B01D15/265 . All groups listed in this Warning should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[B01D 15/30] Partition chromatography -- Group B01D15/30 is impacted by reclassification into group B01D15/1894 . Groups B01D15/30 and B01D15/1894 should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[B01D 15/305] Hydrophilic interaction chromatography [HILIC] -- Hydrophilic interaction chromatography (HILIC) is a liquid-chromatography technique that separates polar or highly hydrophilic analytes by using a water-rich layer on a solid, typically polar, sorbent. The method exploits partitioning between the aqueous layer and a less polar organic mobile phase, providing retention of compounds that are poorly retained in conventional reversed-phase systems.
[B01D 15/32] Bonded phase chromatography -- B01D 15/32 covers bonded phase chromatography, where a liquid mixture is passed through a solid sorbent whose surface is chemically bonded to a selective ligand. The bonded phase provides specific interactions that separate components based on polarity, size or affinity, enabling high-resolution analysis and purification. This subclass falls under separating processes that treat liquids with solid sorbents.
[B01D 15/322] Normal bonded phase -- The B01D 15/322 "Normal bonded phase" entry covers separating processes that treat liquids with solid sorbents whose surfaces are coated with a polar, normally-alkyl-substituted bonded phase, such as silica bonded with silanol groups. It is used chiefly in liquid chromatography and related purification techniques to separate mixture components based on their interaction with the bonded stationary phase.
[B01D 15/325] Reversed phase -- Reversed-phase chromatography employs a solid sorbent with a relatively non-polar stationary phase while the liquid mobile phase is comparatively polar, enabling separation of compounds based on hydrophobic interactions. The method is used for treating liquid mixtures where analytes are retained on the sorbent and eluted in order of decreasing polarity, facilitating purification and analysis of organic substances.
[B01D 15/327] with hydrophobic interaction -- B01D 15/327 covers separating processes in which liquids are treated with solid sorbents that effect separation through hydrophobic interaction, i.e., the sorbent preferentially adsorbs non-polar components. It belongs to the hierarchy of performing operations → separating → separation and focuses on liquid-solid sorption methods that exploit hydrophobic affinity.
[B01D 15/34] Size-selective separation, e.g. size-exclusion chromatography -- B01D 15/34 relates to size-selective separation methods, notably size-exclusion chromatography, where a liquid stream passes through a solid sorbent that retains or excludes molecules according to their dimensions. It is applied to separate biomolecules, polymers, and particles based on molecular size.
[B01D 15/36] involving ionic interaction, e.g. ion-exchange, ion-pair, ion-suppression or ion-exclusion -- Group B01D15/36 is impacted by reclassification into groups B01D15/1867 and B01D15/1896 . Groups B01D15/36 , B01D15/1867 and B01D15/1896 should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
