[B01D 2201/0423] not in the inner side of the cylindrical filtering elements -- The CPC subgroup B01D 2201/0423 covers filtering apparatus in which the described feature is situated on the outer side rather than the inner side of cylindrical filtering elements, such as drums or tubes, and pertains to separation operations.
[B01D 2201/043] Filter tubes connected to plates -- The CPC entry B01D 2201/043 falls within the hierarchy of Performing Operations → Separating → Separation and covers filter tubes that are attached to plates as part of a filtering apparatus. It details the design and configuration of these tube-plate connections used to improve fluid flow and separation efficiency.
[B01D 2201/0438] mounted substantially vertically on plates at the lower side of the filter elements -- This subclass covers filter assemblies where the supporting structure or plates are positioned mainly vertical below the filter media, providing support and directing flow. It pertains to designs in which filter elements are mounted on plates that stand substantially upright on their lower side, a feature common in certain liquid or gas filtration units.
[B01D 2201/0446] suspended from plates at the upper side of the filter elements -- This subclass covers filter apparatus where the filtering medium or cake is held in place by plates positioned above the filter elements, so the material is suspended from the upper side of the elements. The arrangement aids in uniform flow distribution and facilitates removal of the filtered material.
[B01D 2201/0453] positioned between at least two plates -- The B01D 2201/0453 CPC entry pertains to filtering apparatus in which the filtration medium is positioned between at least two plates. It falls under the hierarchy of performing operations, separating, and separation, and addresses designs where the plate-sandwiched configuration facilitates the separation of solids from liquids or gases.
[B01D 2201/0461] Springs -- B01D 2201/0461 - Springs - This subgroup covers spring components used in filtering apparatus for separating solids from fluids, including coil, leaf and compression springs that provide mechanical support, sealing or biasing within the filter structure.
[B01D 2201/0469] Filter tubes connected to collector tubes -- The CPC entry B01D 2201/0469 covers filtration devices where the filter tubes are directly connected to collector tubes that gather the filtrate. It specifies arrangements that integrate the filter and collection components into a unified conduit system, facilitating efficient transfer of filtered material in industrial or laboratory filters.
[B01D 2201/0476] mounted substantially vertically on collector tubes at the lower side of the filter elements -- This subcategory describes filter configurations in which collector tubes are mounted substantially vertically on the lower side of the filter elements, allowing gravity-assisted removal of separated material. It pertains to the arrangement of supporting hardware within filtering apparatus.
[B01D 2201/0484] suspended from collector tubes at the upper side of the filter elements -- This subclass concerns filter apparatus in which collector tubes are placed above the filter element and the filter media are suspended from them, allowing solids or liquids to be collected on the upper side of the element.
[B01D 2201/0492] positioned between at least two collector tubes -- The code covers filtering devices in which a filter element or media is positioned between at least two collector tubes, allowing a fluid stream to pass and particles to be captured as part of a separation operation.
[B01D 2201/06] Resilient foam as filtering element -- This CPC entry (B01D 2201/06) concerns the use of resilient foam as the filtering element in separation equipment, describing how the foam performs filtration within apparatuses. It is classified under performing operations - separating - separation - details relating to filtering apparatus.
[B01D 2201/08] Regeneration of the filter -- The entry B01D 2201/08 addresses techniques for restoring a filter's performance after use, including cleaning, backwashing, chemical or thermal treatment, and other regeneration methods that restore the filter medium's ability to separate solids or liquids. It falls under the broader categories of performing operations, separating, and separation, and relates specifically to filtering apparatus.
[B01D 2201/081] using nozzles or suction devices -- This CPC subclass B01D 2201/081 relates to filtering apparatus where the separation process is aided by nozzles or suction devices that direct fluid flow to improve particle removal, and it falls under the hierarchy of performing operations → separating → separation → details relating to filtering apparatus.
[B01D 2201/082] Suction devices placed on the cake side of the filtering element -- The CPC subclass B01D 2201/082 covers suction devices that are positioned on the cake side of a filtering element, i.e., on the side where the retained solid material collects. These devices remove or convey the cake, often to improve filtration efficiency or enable continuous operation.
[B01D 2201/083] Suction devices placed on the filtrate side of the filtering element, e.g. with variable edge filters -- A suction device positioned on the filtrate side of a filtering element, often incorporating variable-edge filters to enhance fluid removal, falls under CPC B01D 2201/083. This subclass pertains to separating operations where the apparatus draws filtrate through the filter medium to improve separation efficiency.
[B01D 2201/084] Nozzles placed on the filtrate side of the filtering element -- The classification B01D 2201/084 covers filtering apparatus that includes one or more nozzles installed on the filtrate side of the filter element, used to direct, spray or distribute the filtrate for purposes such as cleaning, drying or process control within separation operations.
[B01D 2201/085] using another chemical than the liquid to be filtered -- The B01D 2201/085 entry covers filtration processes that introduce a chemical different from the liquid being filtered to improve performance, such as adding flocculants, coagulants or cleaning agents within the filtering apparatus. This approach is classified under PERFORMING OPERATIONS → SEPARATING → SEPARATION and relates specifically to the use of auxiliary chemicals during filtration.
[B01D 2201/086] using fluid streams co-current to the filtration direction -- The B01D 2201/086 subclass covers filtration processes in which the feed fluid streams move in the same direction as the filtration flow, i.e., co-current streams, rather than opposite the filter medium. It falls under the broader categories of performing operations, separating, separation, and details relating to filtering apparatus. This classification is used in the Cooperative Patent Classification shared by the EPO and USPTO.
[B01D 2201/087] using gas bubbles, e.g. air -- The CPC subclass B01D 2201/087 covers filtration or other separation processes that employ gas bubbles, such as air, to assist the removal of solids or liquids. It includes methods where bubbles are introduced into a medium to enhance particle capture, improve flow, or promote separation in filtering apparatus.
[B01D 2201/088] Arrangements for killing microorganisms -- B01D 2201/088 covers filter arrangements that incorporate means to kill microorganisms as part of the separation process. It includes designs that embed biocidal agents, UV radiation, heat or chemical disinfectants within the filtering media for water, air or other fluids, combining physical separation with microbial inactivation.
[B01D 2201/089] using rollers having projections to clear the filter apertures -- The CPC subclass B01D 2201/089, within PERFORMING OPERATIONS → SEPARATING → SEPARATION, pertains to filtration devices and describes the use of rollers equipped with projections to remove debris and clear the apertures of a filter. It specifies a mechanical cleaning method that dislodges particles lodged in the filter media.
[B01D 2201/10] Filtration under gravity in large open drainage basins -- B01D 2201/10 pertains to filtration processes that utilize gravity, specifically in the context of large open drainage basins. This classification is part of a detailed subset focusing on specific aspects and applications of filtering apparatus within the broader categories of performing operations and separating techniques.
[B01D 2201/12] Pleated filters -- B01D 2201/12 designates pleated filters, a form of filtering apparatus employed in separation operations. The class lies within performing operations - separating - separation and concerns devices that use pleated media to increase surface area for improved filtration performance.
[B01D 2201/122] with pleats of different length -- This CPC subclass covers filters that incorporate pleats of different lengths, creating zones with variable surface area and flow resistance. Such designs are employed to improve particle capture efficiency and manage pressure drop in baghouses, cartridge or panel filters.
[B01D 2201/125] with non-parallel pleats -- The CPC entry B01D 2201/125, titled "with non-parallel pleats," falls under Performing Operations → Separating → Separation and pertains to filtering apparatus that employ pleated filter media arranged at varying angles rather than parallel, enhancing flow distribution and particle capture.
[B01D 2201/127] with means for keeping the spacing between the pleats -- This CPC subclass B01D 2201/127 pertains to filtering apparatus that incorporate structures or devices for maintaining a constant distance between pleats of the filter medium. It is located under the hierarchy Performing Operations - Separating - Separation, focusing on the mechanical means that keep pleat spacing uniform during operation.
[B01D 2201/14] Particulate filter materials with a lower density than the liquid mixture to be filtered -- The CPC entry B01D 2201/14 refers to filter media composed of particulate materials that are less dense than the liquid mixture being filtered, enabling buoyant separation of the solid phase within a filtering apparatus. It falls under the hierarchy of performing operations → separating → separation and relates specifically to details of such filtering devices.
[B01D 2201/16] Valves -- The CPC subclass B01D 2201/16 (Valves) pertains to valves employed in filtration equipment, where the primary function is to separate substances by controlling fluid flow. It falls under the hierarchy of performing operations, specifically separating and separation, and addresses components that regulate the passage of liquids or gases through a filter system.
[B01D 2201/162] with snap, latch or clip connecting means -- The code B01D 2201/162 designates filtering devices that incorporate a snap, latch or clip mechanism to connect components, facilitating separation processes within the apparatus. It falls under the hierarchy performing operations - separating - separation.
[B01D 2201/165] Multi-way valves -- The code B01D 2201/165 pertains to multi-way valves used in filtration systems, enabling the direction of fluid flow among multiple ports for separating solids or liquids. These valves are designed to control the passage of process streams in equipment that performs separation operations.
[B01D 2201/167] Single-way valves -- B01D 2201/167 designates single-way valves that are incorporated in filtering apparatus for separation processes. These valves permit fluid flow in only one direction, enabling controlled separation within the broader performing-operations category of separating and separation.
[B01D 2201/18] Filters characterised by the openings or pores -- Filters defined primarily by the size, shape or distribution of their openings or pores, where these characteristics dictate the separation performance. The classification covers designs, materials and constructions whose pore geometry is the principal feature for controlling flow and particle retention. It applies to any filtering device whose operation is described in terms of its opening dimensions rather than auxiliary functions.
[B01D 2201/182] for depth filtration -- B01D 2201/182 pertains to apparatus that carries out depth filtration, in which a fluid passes through a porous medium and particles are retained within its internal structure rather than on the surface. It is classified under performing operations, separating, separation, and details relating to filtering apparatus.
[B01D 2201/184] Special form, dimension of the openings, pores of the filtering elements -- The CPC subclass B01D 2201/184 falls under Performing Operations, Separating, Separation and addresses filtering apparatus where the special form, size and pore dimensions of the filter elements are defined. It is applied to inventions that specify particular shapes or measurements of openings that affect the separation performance.
[B01D 2201/186] Pore openings which can be modified -- B01D 2201/186 covers filtering devices whose pore openings are designed to be adjustable, allowing the size or characteristics of the pores to be changed during operation. This subclass falls under performing operations → separating → separation and relates specifically to filter apparatus where the permeability can be modified to suit different separation needs.
[B01D 2201/188] Multiple filtering elements having filtering areas of different size -- This class covers filter devices that incorporate several filter elements where each element provides a filtration area of a different size, allowing staged or graded separation. The configuration is used to improve efficiency or selectivity in separating mixtures.
[B01D 2201/20] Pressure-related systems for filters -- The entry B01D 2201/20 concerns systems that use pressure to operate filters, including pressurized housings, pumps or suction arrangements that force fluid through a filtering medium. It covers apparatus where the pressure differential-either positive or negative-is a primary means of driving the separation process. This falls under the CPC hierarchy of Performing Operations > Separating > Separation > Details relating to filtering apparatus.
[B01D 2201/202] Systems for applying pressure to filters -- Systems designed to exert mechanical pressure on filter elements to enhance solid-liquid separation, including filter presses, plate-and-frame units and other apparatus that compress filter media or cakes during operation, thereby improving filtration efficiency and throughput. This subclass falls under the broader categories of separating and separation processes.
[B01D 2201/204] Systems for applying vacuum to filters -- The CPC entry B01D 2201/204 covers devices that create a vacuum to draw fluid through a filter, enhancing separation efficiency. It includes systems where pumps, vents or suction chambers are integrated with the filter housing to maintain a pressure differential during operation. These arrangements are used in various industrial and laboratory applications to improve contaminant removal and product quality.
[B01D 2201/206] by the weight of the liquid in a tube, e.g. siphon, barometric leg -- The subclass B01D 2201/206 covers separation techniques that rely on the weight of a liquid column within a tube to move the fluid, such as siphons or barometric legs, typically applied in filtering apparatus. It falls under Performing Operations → Separating → Separation.
[B01D 2201/208] by venturi systems -- This CPC subclass covers separation processes that employ venturi-based filtering apparatus, where a constricted flow creates a vacuum to draw liquids or gases through a filter medium. It falls under Performing Operations → Separating → Separation.
[B01D 2201/22] Filtering bands with supporting discs -- The CPC class B01D 2201/22 covers filtering devices that employ filtering bands mounted on or supported by discs, typically arranged around a rotating drum or similar support. It relates to the construction and arrangement of the bands and discs that form the filter medium.
[B01D 2201/24] Tools used for the removal of filters -- B01D 2201/24 covers tools and implements designed to detach or extract filters from filtration equipment, enabling maintenance, cleaning or replacement of the filter element. It applies to devices used in separating processes where removal of the filter is required.
[B01D 2201/26] Transport systems for filtering devices -- The subclass B01D 2201/26 defines transport systems used with filtering devices, encompassing pumps, conveyors, ducts, and other mechanisms that move fluids, gases, or solids to and from a filter element as part of the filtration apparatus.
[B01D 2201/265] mounted on vehicles -- The B01D 2201/265 subclass covers filtration devices that are installed directly on vehicles, such as air, oil, fuel or other fluid filters mounted on cars, trucks, ships or aircraft. It pertains to the design, placement and integration of these filters into the vehicle's systems to separate contaminants while the vehicle is in operation.
[B01D 2201/28] Position of the filtering element -- The code B01D 2201/28 covers patents that specify where the filtering element is placed within a filtration system, such as its position relative to the fluid flow, orientation, or mounting arrangement. It pertains to the design of the filter's location as part of separating operations in a filtering apparatus.
[B01D 2201/282] Filtering elements with a horizontal rotation or symmetry axis -- This CPC subclass (B01D 2201/282) deals with filtering elements that have a horizontal rotation or symmetry axis, such as drum or disk filters designed to spin or be symmetrical around a horizontal line. It falls under the hierarchy of performing operations, separating, and separation, and pertains to apparatus used to separate solids from liquids or gases.
[B01D 2201/285] Filtering elements with a symmetry axis not parallel to the rotation axis -- The CPC entry B01D 2201/285 covers filter components whose geometric symmetry axis is inclined relative to the rotation axis of the apparatus. Such elements include angled, helical or skewed filter plates or meshes that create non-uniform flow patterns, improving separation efficiency in rotating filtration devices.
[B01D 2201/287] Filtering elements with a vertical or inclined rotation or symmetry axis -- The code B01D 2201/287 pertains to filter elements whose design or operation involves a vertical or inclined axis of rotation or symmetry, such as vertically oriented cartridges, tilted disks or cones that create a rotating or symmetric flow pattern for separating solids from liquids or gases. It falls under Performing Operations → Separating → Separation → Details relating to filtering apparatus.
[B01D 2201/29] Filter cartridge constructions -- The B01D 2201/29 entry covers constructions of filter cartridges, detailing their design, materials and assembly within filtering apparatus. It falls under the hierarchy of Performing Operations - Separating - Separation, and is used to classify inventions that improve or modify cartridge-based filtration systems.
[B01D 2201/291] End caps -- End caps are sealing components used in filtering apparatus to close the ends of a filter housing, contain the filter media and direct the flow of fluid through the filter. They are classified under CPC B01D 2201/291 within the separation operations segment.
[B01D 2201/293] Making of end caps -- The CPC code B01D 2201/293 covers processes for forming end caps used in filtration devices, including shaping, molding or attaching the caps to filter housings. It falls under performing operations, specifically separating and filtration apparatus details, and addresses manufacturing steps that produce the final sealing element of a filter system.
[B01D 2201/295] with projections extending in a radial outward direction, e.g. for use as a guide, spacing means -- The CPC subclass B01D 2201/295 belongs to the performing operations-separating-separation group and covers filter devices that incorporate projections extending outward radially, such as guides or spacing elements, to improve flow distribution or maintain clearances within the apparatus.
[B01D 2201/296] Other than having a circular shape -- The B01D 2201/296 entry addresses filtering elements used in separation operations that are not circular in shape, such as rectangular, square, or irregularly shaped filters, and includes related design details for apparatus that separates solids from liquids or gases.
[B01D 2201/298] End caps common to at least two filtering elements -- This subclass covers end caps that are shared by two or more filtering elements in a filtering device. The caps provide sealing and structural support at the ends of the filter train, helping to retain the filter media and maintain flow path integrity. It falls under the broader category of separation operations in the CPC.
[B01D 2201/30] Filter housing constructions -- The CPC subclass B01D 2201/30 covers constructions of filter housings, i.e., the enclosures, frames, or supporting structures that contain filter media in apparatus for separating solids from fluids. It includes designs that facilitate installation, removal, sealing and maintenance of the filter element within the overall separating system. This subclass falls under PERFORMING OPERATIONS → SEPARATING → SEPARATION → Details relating to filtering apparatus.
[B01D 2201/301] Details of removable closures, lids, caps, filter heads -- B01D 2201/301 covers the design and features of removable closures, lids, caps and filter heads used on filtering apparatus. It addresses how these components seal, attach and detach to control flow during separation processes, fitting within the broader category of performing operations and separating.
[B01D 2201/302] having inlet or outlet ports -- This CPC subgroup covers filtering devices that include one or more inlet or outlet ports, allowing fluid to enter and leave the filter medium. It applies to separation equipment where such ports are integral to the apparatus for feeding or discharging the material being filtered.
[B01D 2201/303] not arranged concentrically -- The CPC subclass B01D 2201/303, within the hierarchy of Performing Operations → Separating → Separation, deals with filtering apparatus whose elements are not arranged concentrically. It covers designs where the filter media, screens or membranes are positioned in non-circular configurations, such as planar, stacked or irregular layouts.
[B01D 2201/304] Seals or gaskets -- The CPC subclass B01D 2201/304 covers seals or gaskets employed in separation and filtering equipment. These components prevent fluid or gas leakage, maintain pressure differentials, and protect moving parts within filtration or separation systems.
[B01D 2201/305] Snap, latch or clip connecting means -- B01D 2201/305 pertains to snap, latch or clip connecting means used in separation operations, particularly within filtering apparatus. The classification covers devices that attach or release components by a quick-acting mechanical snap, latch or clip, facilitating the assembly, disassembly or maintenance of filters and related equipment.
[B01D 2201/306] Closures, lids, caps or filter heads forming one element with the filtering element -- The CPC code B01D 2201/306, under Performing Operations → Separating → Separation, covers filter apparatus where the closure, lid, cap or filter head is formed as a single piece with the filtering element itself, integrating sealing and support functions in a unified component.
[B01D 2201/307] Filtering elements contained in an insert body mounted in a filter housing (double casing), e.g. to avoid contamination when removing or replacing the filter element -- This subclass deals with filter assemblies that employ a removable insert body within a double-casing housing, allowing the filtering media to be taken out or replaced without contaminating the process stream. It falls under separation operations in the CPC hierarchy.
[B01D 2201/308] Use of foils, membranes or other means to protect the filter before its use or for protecting the environment, e.g. during removal of the filter -- This subclass covers methods and devices that employ foils, membranes or similar protective layers to shield a filter before it is put into service or to prevent environmental release when the filter is removed. The protection may be applied to the filter surface or surrounding area to contain contaminants during handling.
[B01D 2201/309] Housings with transparent parts -- The entry B01D 2201/309 relates to filtering apparatus and covers housings that incorporate transparent components, such as windows or clear panels, to allow visual inspection of the interior or flow path. It is situated within the hierarchy of Performing Operations → Separating → Separation and concerns the design of enclosures that facilitate monitoring while maintaining the function of the filter.
[B01D 2201/31] Other construction details -- B01D 2201/31 covers miscellaneous structural features of filtering apparatus used in separation operations, including supports, housings, seals and mounting arrangements that are not specifically defined in other subclasses. It addresses any other construction details of filters that affect their performance or integration.
[B01D 2201/313] Means for protecting the filter from the incoming fluid, e.g. shields -- This CPC subclass covers protective elements that shield a filter from the fluid entering the filtration system, such as shields or guards that prevent damage, fouling or blockage.
[B01D 2201/316] Standpipes -- B01D 2201/316 - Standpipes are vertical piping elements incorporated in filtration apparatus to conduct fluid or gas flow through a filter medium, providing a conduit for distribution or collection within separation processes.
[B01D 2201/32] Flow characteristics of the filter -- B01D 2201/32 addresses the flow characteristics of filters, describing how fluids pass through filtering media, the associated pressure drops, permeability and flow distribution. It is applied to patents that detail performance testing, analysis or optimisation of filtration apparatus.
[B01D 2201/325] Outward flow filtration -- The CPC subclass B01D 2201/325 concerns outward-flow filtration, describing filtering apparatus in which the fluid passes from the interior of the filter element to the exterior, typically employed in separation processes that direct the flow outward through the filter medium.
[B01D 2201/34] Seals or gaskets for filtering elements -- B01D 2201/34 covers seals or gaskets designed specifically for filtering elements in filtration devices. It pertains to components that prevent leakage around filter media while allowing fluid flow, and falls under the CPC hierarchy performing operations - separating - separation - details relating to filtering apparatus.
[B01D 2201/342] Axial sealings -- The CPC subgroup B01D 2201/342 covers axial sealing components used in filtering equipment, such as rings, gaskets, or thrust bearings that prevent fluid bypass along the axis of a filter housing. These sealings are designed to maintain pressure differentials and protect the filter media from contamination while allowing smooth axial movement of the filter elements.
[B01D 2201/345] Pressurized seals or gaskets -- The CPC subclass B01D 2201/345, titled "Pressurized seals or gaskets," pertains to sealing elements that maintain a pressure-tight connection in filtering apparatus. It includes designs for seals and gaskets used in filter housings, frames or cartridge assemblies to prevent leakage of fluids or gases during separation operations.
[B01D 2201/347] Radial sealings -- The CPC subgroup B01D 2201/347 covers radial sealing mechanisms used in filtering apparatus. It includes seals that encircle a filter element or housing to prevent fluid leakage across the radial plane, such as O-rings, gaskets and lip seals employed in centrifugal, plate or tubular filters.
[B01D 2201/36] Filtering elements containing a rotating housing construction -- This CPC entry covers filter components that incorporate a rotating housing, such as drums or cylindrical cages, to enhance particle separation. It relates to apparatus where the housing itself moves to improve filtration efficiency.
[B01D 2201/38] Preventing rewetting of the filter cake on the filter media -- B01D 2201/38 in the Cooperative Patent Classification addresses methods and devices for preventing the filter cake from becoming rewetted on the filter medium, a critical aspect in maintaining filtration efficiency. This entry is part of a broader category that deals with details relating to filtering apparatus.
[B01D 2201/40] Special measures for connecting different parts of the filter -- This subclass covers special means for joining components of a filter, such as clamps, couplings, seals, or fastening structures that assemble or disassemble filter elements. It is used for filtration apparatus where the connection of filter parts is critical to performance, cleaning or replacement.
[B01D 2201/4007] Use of cam or ramp systems -- This subclass concerns filtering apparatus where separation is achieved using cam or ramp mechanisms to move or direct fluid or particles, falling under performing operations and separating categories in the CPC. It specifies the employment of cam or ramp systems to control flow or material in filters.
[B01D 2201/4015] Bayonet connecting means -- The CPC subclass B01D 2201/4015 covers bayonet-type connecting mechanisms used in filtering equipment. It pertains to devices that join filter elements or housings by a quick-turn, twist-lock action, facilitating rapid assembly and disassembly while maintaining a sealed flow path. This falls under the broader categories of performing operations, separating, and separation.
[B01D 2201/4023] Means for connecting filter housings to supports -- A means for joining filter housings to their supporting structures, covering devices, brackets, clamps or other connectors that secure filter units in place within filtering apparatus.
[B01D 2201/403] allowing dilatation, e.g. by heat -- The subclass B01D 2201/403 covers separation and filtering apparatus that incorporate means for allowing the filter medium or its supporting structure to expand or contract, typically by applying heat. This dilatation feature helps maintain sealing, flow characteristics, or mechanical integrity of the filter during operation.
[B01D 2201/4038] for connecting at least two filtering elements together -- This CPC subgroup covers devices or structures that join at least two filter elements within a filtration apparatus, including connectors, frames, or linking mechanisms that hold separate filter media together to form a composite or modular filter assembly. It also encompasses features that enable mechanical attachment, sealing, or alignment of multiple filtering elements for improved separation performance.
[B01D 2201/4046] Means for avoiding false mounting of different parts -- This CPC entry covers features incorporated in filtering equipment to prevent the wrong parts from being mounted together, ensuring correct assembly of components. It falls under the hierarchy of performing operations, separating and separation, and is identified by code B01D 2201/4046.
[B01D 2201/4053] using keys -- B01D 2201/4053 is a CPC subclass for separation processes that involve filtering apparatus in which "keys" are employed to control or direct the flow of material. It covers devices or methods that use keyed elements-such as removable inserts, adjustable gates, or lock-in mechanisms-to select, retain, or release particular particles or phases during filtration. This subclass falls under PERFORMING OPERATIONS → SEPARATING → SEPARATION.
[B01D 2201/4061] between a cartridge and a filter head or manifold -- This CPC subclass covers the region or interface that joins a filter cartridge to its filter head or manifold, describing components, fittings, or sealing arrangements that connect the cartridge within a filtering apparatus. It falls under the broader categories of performing operations, separating and separation, specifically dealing with filtering equipment.
[B01D 2201/4069] Magnetic means -- The CPC subclass B01D 2201/4069 covers magnetic means employed in separation processes, specifically for filtering apparatus. It defines devices that use magnetic fields to attract, capture or retain magnetic particles from a fluid stream, facilitating the removal of contaminants or the recovery of magnetic materials.
[B01D 2201/4076] Anti-rotational means -- The entry covers devices that prevent undesired rotation of filter components within a filtration system, such as anti-rotational sleeves, baffles, or locking mechanisms that secure filter media or housing during operation. These means ensure stability of the filtration element, improve sealing, and maintain consistent flow paths while the apparatus is in use.
[B01D 2201/4084] Snap or Seeger ring connecting means -- Snap or Seeger ring connecting means are fasteners that use a flexible ring to snap onto a mating part, providing a quick, secure, and often reversible joint in filtering apparatus. The rings are pressed or clipped onto a shaft or housing to hold filter media or other components in place without the need for tools, facilitating assembly, maintenance, and replacement.
[B01D 2201/4092] Threaded sections, e.g. screw -- The subclass B01D 2201/4092 includes filtering apparatus that employs threaded elements such as screws or threaded sections to form, adjust or secure filter media. It addresses designs where the threading creates separations within the filter structure, facilitating the removal of particles or liquids. This falls under the broader categories of performing operations, separating, and separation.
[B01D 2201/44] Special measures allowing the even or uniform distribution of fluid along the length of a conduit -- This CPC subclass covers devices or methods that ensure a fluid is distributed evenly along a pipe or channel, often using internal baffles, diffusers or other flow-equalizing features. It is applied in filtration apparatus where uniform flow prevents channeling and improves performance.
[B01D 2201/46] Several filtrate discharge conduits each connected to one filter element or group of filter elements -- The subclass B01D 2201/46 covers filter units that provide multiple filtrate-discharge conduits, each conduit being linked to a single filter element or to a group of elements. This arrangement allows separate collection or flow control of the filtered liquid from each element, typical of multi-channel filtration apparatus.
[B01D 2201/48] Overflow systems -- B01D 2201/48 - Overflow systems cover devices and methods for handling excess liquid flow in filtration equipment, typically directing surplus feed to a bypass or collection chamber to prevent overload and maintain performance. This subclass falls under the separating hierarchy of the Cooperative Patent Classification.
[B01D 2201/50] Means for dissipating electrostatic charges -- B01D 2201/50 covers means incorporated in filtration equipment to eliminate or reduce static electricity that can build up on filter media or housings. It includes antistatic fibers, conductive coatings, grounding elements, or other devices that safely dissipate electrostatic charges during operation of the separator.
[B01D 2201/52] Filter identification means -- The CPC subclass B01D 2201/52 falls under Performing Operations → Separating → Separation and concerns filter identification means. It covers features that enable a filtration apparatus to recognize, label or sense a particular filter, such as indicators, sensors or coding that determine filter type, position or condition.
[B01D 2201/54] Computerised or programmable systems -- The CPC subclass B01D 2201/54 covers computerized or programmable systems employed in filtering apparatus, including control, monitoring, and automation of separation operations. It is situated within the hierarchy of Performing Operations - Separating - Separation - Details relating to filtering apparatus.
[B01D 2201/56] Wireless systems for monitoring the filter -- The CPC entry B01D 2201/56 covers wireless systems that monitor filter performance, such as sensors and communication modules used to detect clogging, pressure drop or flow changes in filtration equipment. It falls under the hierarchy of Performing Operations → Separating → Separation and relates specifically to filtering apparatus.
[B01D 2201/58] Power supply means for regenerating the filter -- In the CPC scheme B01D 2201/58 is nested under Performing Operations → Separating → Separation and pertains to power-supply means used to regenerate a filter in a filtering apparatus. It covers devices that provide energy for restoring filter function, such as back-flushing or electro-static regeneration systems.
[B01D 2201/583] using the kinetic energy of the fluid circulating in the filtering device -- This CPC subclass covers filtering devices that exploit the kinetic energy of the fluid moving through them to enhance the separation process. It falls under the hierarchy of Performing Operations → Separating → Separation and concerns methods where the fluid's motion drives or assists filtration.
[B01D 2201/586] using regenerative sources, e.g. wind, sun -- This subclass groups separation or filtering methods that are powered by renewable energy sources such as wind or solar power. It applies to apparatuses where the regenerative source directly drives the operation, falling under CPC B01D 2201/586 for filtering devices using renewable energy.
[B01D 2201/60] Shape of non-cylindrical filtering elements -- This CPC subclass covers inventions relating to the geometric shape of filtering elements that are not cylindrical, such as flat, conical, or irregularly shaped filters used in separation processes. It includes designs that improve flow distribution, packing density, or filtration efficiency by deviating from the traditional round cross-section.
[B01D 2201/602] Oval -- The CPC entry B01D 2201/602 "Oval" concerns oval-shaped components or configurations in filtering apparatus used for separation operations. It falls under the hierarchy of performing operations → separating → separation and addresses design aspects of filters where an oval geometry is employed.
[B01D 2201/605] Square or rectangular -- The B01D 2201/605 subclass covers filtering devices that incorporate square or rectangular elements, such as filter frames, screens, or compartments, within the broader category of separating operations. It includes designs where the geometry of the filter is specifically defined as square or rectangular to achieve particular flow or separation characteristics.
[B01D 2201/607] Triangular -- The CPC subclass B01D 2201/607, titled "Triangular", covers filtering apparatus that incorporates a triangular-shaped element or geometry to facilitate the separation of solids or liquids. It falls under the hierarchy Performing Operations → Separating → Separation and specifies designs where the triangular configuration improves flow distribution, particle retention or cleaning efficiency.
[B01D 2201/62] Honeycomb-like -- B01D 2201/62 - Honeycomb-like refers to filtering apparatus that uses a honeycomb-like structure to separate particles or fluids. It is placed in the CPC hierarchy under performing operations, separating, separation, and covers designs where the filter media is arranged in a honeycomb configuration to improve flow and filtration efficiency.
[B01D 2201/64] Filters having floating elements -- Filters that incorporate floating components within the filtering media to improve particle capture and prevent clogging, typically used in liquid or gas filtration systems. The floating elements move with the flow, maintaining porosity while enhancing separation efficiency in apparatus classified under separating operations.
[B01D 2202/00] Details concerning evaporation, distillation or condensation -- This subclass covers technical information on the mechanisms and conditions of phase-change operations such as evaporation, distillation and condensation, including equipment design, heat-transfer methods and process control. It is applied to patents that address the operational details of separating substances by changing their phase rather than their chemical composition.
[B01D 2202/10] Use of a microdevice for separation -- The CPC subclass B01D 2202/10 covers the use of microdevices-such as microreactors, chips or MEMS components-to perform separation operations, including evaporation, distillation or condensation of fluids. It falls under the broader category of separation techniques in chemical processing.
[B01D 2202/20] Use of an ionic liquid in the separation process -- The CPC subclass B01D 2202/20 covers the use of ionic liquids as media in separation processes, specifically in operations involving evaporation, distillation or condensation. It falls under performing operations - separating - separation and addresses applications where ionic liquids replace conventional solvents to improve efficiency or selectivity.
[B01D 2221/00] Applications of separation devices -- **Definition:** This place covers:  This group relates to the application of the separation devices. It should be assigned if the application implicitly requires certain features (e.g. for separators installed in gullies, a shortcut between inlet and outlet for the fluid to be treated) and if it is deemed not necessary to assign a class corresponding to the technical field of application ( E03F in the example before) or if a field of application is not reflected by a class (e.g. cleaning the polluted ambient air in cities).
[B01D 2221/02] Small separation devices for domestic application, e.g. for canteens, industrial kitchen, washing machines -- This class includes compact separation devices intended for domestic or similar use, such as units that separate liquids from solids in canteens, industrial-kitchen equipment, or washing machines. It is part of the hierarchy for performing operations, separating, separation and applications of separation devices.
[B01D 2221/04] Separation devices for treating liquids from earth drilling, mining -- The entry covers devices designed to separate and treat liquids generated during earth-drilling or mining operations, such as mud, slurry or groundwater. It focuses on equipment that removes solids, filters contaminants, or otherwise conditions the fluid for disposal or reuse.
[B01D 2221/06] Separation devices for industrial food processing or agriculture -- The CPC subclass B01D 2221/06 covers separation devices used in industrial food processing or agriculture, such as equipment for sorting, cleaning, or extracting edible products from raw materials. These devices are designed to improve efficiency and product quality in the food and agricultural sectors.
[B01D 2221/08] Mobile separation devices -- B01D 2221/08 covers mobile separation devices and their applications, referring to equipment that can be moved to a work site to separate solid, liquid or gaseous mixtures, such as portable centrifuges, transportable filtration units or mobile screening machines.
[B01D 2221/10] Separation devices for use in medical, pharmaceutical or laboratory applications, e.g. separating amalgam from dental treatment residues -- This subclass covers separation devices designed for medical, pharmaceutical or laboratory use, such as equipment that isolates amalgam from dental treatment residues. The devices employ physical or chemical means to separate components in a controlled environment.
[B01D 2221/12] Separation devices for treating rain or storm water -- B01D 2221/12 covers separation devices designed to treat rain or storm water, removing pollutants, sediments, oils, or other contaminants before discharge. These systems may include filters, sedimentation basins, oil-water separators, and related apparatus used in storm-water management.
[B01D 2221/14] Separation devices for workshops, car or semiconductor industry, e.g. for separating chips and other machining residues -- B01D 2221/14 covers separation devices used in workshops, automotive or semiconductor manufacturing to remove chips, debris and other machining residues. The entry includes equipment designed to collect, filter or otherwise separate solid particles generated during machining processes.
[B01D 2221/16] Separation devices for cleaning ambient air, e.g. air along roads or air in cities -- B01D 2221/16 concerns separation devices intended for cleaning ambient air, including systems installed along roads or in cities to remove pollutants from the surrounding atmosphere.
[B01D 2239/00] Aspects relating to filtering material for liquid or gaseous fluids -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Details of filtering material as put forward under the corresponding maingroup B01D39/00
[B01D 2239/02] Types of fibres, filaments or particles, self-supporting or supported materials -- This CPC subclass concerns the kinds of fibres, filaments or particles that form self-supporting or supported filter media used for separating liquid or gaseous fluids. It is located under Performing Operations → Separating → Separation.
[B01D 2239/0208] Single-component fibres -- B01D 2239/0208 pertains to the use of single-component fibres as filter media for separating liquid or gaseous fluids. It covers filtration applications where a homogeneous fibre is employed to capture particles, droplets or contaminants from a fluid stream.
[B01D 2239/0216] Bicomponent or multicomponent fibres -- B01D 2239/0216 covers bicomponent or multicomponent fibres, i.e., filaments composed of two or more distinct polymer or material phases that are extruded together or later combined. These fibres are employed in filtration media for separating liquids or gases, benefiting from differing physical or chemical properties of each component.
[B01D 2239/0225] Side-by-side -- The side-by-side entry (B01D 2239/0225) covers filter configurations in which two or more filtering elements are arranged adjacent to one another, allowing fluid to pass through them in parallel or sequentially side by side. This design is used to increase capacity, improve flow distribution or enable staged separation of liquids or gases.
[B01D 2239/0233] Island-in-sea -- B01D 2239/0233 refers to a specific configuration in filtration materials where discrete regions of one material are dispersed within a continuous matrix of another, often used to enhance separation efficiency in liquid or gaseous fluids. This structure is known as an island-in-sea arrangement.
[B01D 2239/0241] comprising electrically conductive fibres or particles -- This CPC subclass covers filters for liquids or gases that contain electrically conductive fibres or particles as part of the filtering medium. The conductive elements may be used to improve particle capture by electrostatic attraction or to enable heating or sensing functions within the filter.
[B01D 2239/025] comprising nanofibres -- This subclass covers filters that incorporate nanofibre materials for separating liquids or gases, focusing on the use of nanofibres as the filtering medium within separation processes. It is part of the broader CPC group for performing operations, separating, and specifically addresses nanofibre-based filtration technologies.
[B01D 2239/0258] comprising nanoparticles -- This subclass covers separation processes that use filters containing nanoparticles to remove particles from liquids or gases. It includes methods where the filter medium incorporates nanoscale particles to enhance filtration efficiency, falling under CPC B01D for separating.
[B01D 2239/0266] comprising biodegradable or bio-soluble polymers -- This classification covers filters that incorporate biodegradable or bio-soluble polymers as the filtering medium, used for separating liquids or gases. It pertains to filters where the polymer matrix can break down naturally or dissolve, enabling environmentally friendly separation processes.
[B01D 2239/0275] comprising biologically produced plastics, e.g. bioplastics -- The code B01D 2239/0275 designates separation methods that employ filters or membrane elements fabricated from biologically produced plastics such as bioplastics, targeting the removal of particles or contaminants from liquid or gaseous streams. It sits within the hierarchy Performing Operations → Separating → Separation.
[B01D 2239/0283] comprising filter materials made from waste or recycled materials -- The B01D 2239/0283 class covers filters for liquids or gases in which the filtering medium is produced from waste or recycled materials, addressing separation operations that involve filtering fluids. It applies to any performing-operations context where such sustainable filter media are employed.
[B01D 2239/0291] comprising swelling polymers -- B01D 2239/0291 covers separation processes that employ swelling polymers as the active filtering material for liquids or gases. The polymers expand on contact with the fluid, trapping contaminants or selectively allowing passage of desired components. This entry falls under Performing Operations → Separating → Separation in the CPC scheme.
[B01D 2239/04] Additives and treatments of the filtering material -- The B01D 2239/04 classification covers additives and treatments applied to filter media used in liquid or gas separation processes. It includes chemical, physical, or surface modifications that enhance performance, durability, selectivity, or resistance to fouling of the filtering material.
[B01D 2239/0407] comprising particulate additives, e.g. adsorbents -- This classification covers methods or apparatuses for separating liquids or gases where the filter medium contains particulate additives such as adsorbents. It sits under the broader hierarchy of performing operations, separating and separation, and relates specifically to filtering material for fluid streams.
[B01D 2239/0414] Surface modifiers, e.g. comprising ion exchange groups -- The code B01D 2239/0414 covers surface-modifier additives applied to filter media, especially those that contain ion-exchange groups to tailor the material's interaction with liquids or gases. These modifiers enhance selective retention, adsorption or passage of targeted substances during separation processes.
[B01D 2239/0421] Rendering the filter material hydrophilic -- The CPC subclass B01D 2239/0421 covers methods for making filter media water-attracting, i.e., rendering the filter material hydrophilic. It relates to techniques that modify the surface properties of filters used in liquid or gas separation processes to improve wettability and capture efficiency. This subclass falls under the broader categories of performing operations, separating, and separation of fluids.
[B01D 2239/0428] Rendering the filter material hydrophobic -- This subclass covers methods for imparting water-repellent properties to filter media used in liquid or gas filtration, such as surface treatments, coatings or chemical modifications that make the material hydrophobic, thereby enhancing separation performance.
[B01D 2239/0435] Electret -- An electret is a permanently charged dielectric that creates an electrostatic field to attract and retain particles, improving the efficiency of filters for liquids or gases. By embedding this charge in the filter media, it enhances separation of fine particulates without relying solely on mechanical sieving. This technology is applied in air purifiers, respirators and liquid filtration systems.
[B01D 2239/0442] Antimicrobial, antibacterial, antifungal additives -- The entry B01D 2239/0442 covers additives incorporated into filtration media that provide antimicrobial, antibacterial or antifungal activity, preventing microbial growth during the separation of liquids or gases. These substances are used in filter designs that fall under the broader categories of performing operations, separating and separation.
[B01D 2239/045] Deodorising additives -- B01D 2239/045 covers additives used to remove or mask unpleasant odors during the filtration of liquids or gases. These deodorising compounds are incorporated into filter media or introduced into the fluid stream to improve the sensory quality of the treated product. The classification falls under performing operations, separating, and separation of fluids.
[B01D 2239/0457] Specific fire retardant or heat resistant properties -- The CPC subclass B01D 2239/0457 falls under Performing Operations - Separating - Separation and covers filtration media for liquids or gases that have specific fire-retardant or heat-resistant properties. It identifies materials designed to retain filtration performance while withstanding fire exposure or high temperatures.
[B01D 2239/0464] Impregnants -- Impregnants are chemicals or compounds applied to filter media to modify its properties, improving durability, selectivity, wetting, or adsorption capacity during liquid or gas filtration. They may include binders, wetting agents, catalysts or antimicrobial agents that enhance the performance of separating operations. This entry covers such treated filter materials within the CPC hierarchy for separation processes.
[B01D 2239/0471] Surface coating material -- B01D 2239/0471 covers surface coating materials applied to filter media used in liquid or gaseous separation processes. The coatings are intended to modify the filter surface to enhance properties such as wettability, chemical resistance, durability or particle capture efficiency, and thus improve overall filtration performance. This entry falls under the hierarchy of performing operations, separating, and separation of fluids.
[B01D 2239/0478] on a layer of the filter -- The code B01D 2239/0478 covers operations performed on a filter layer in liquid or gas filtration, such as applying, cleaning, coating, regenerating or otherwise treating that specific layer to alter its performance or lifespan. It pertains to any step that directly modifies the filter material itself.
[B01D 2239/0485] on particles -- The CPC subclass B01D 2239/0485 covers techniques for separating particles by filtering liquids or gases, describing methods and apparatus that retain solid matter while allowing the fluid to pass. It pertains to the broader categories of performing operations, separating, and separation, focusing specifically on filtration of particulate material.
[B01D 2239/0492] on fibres -- This subclass covers separation processes that employ fibrous filter media to remove suspended particles from liquids or gases. It includes apparatuses, filters, and methods where fibers form the primary filtering element for fluid purification.
[B01D 2239/06] Filter cloth, e.g. knitted, woven non-woven -- B01D 2239/06 covers filter cloths such as knitted, woven or non-woven fabrics used to separate solid particles from liquids or gases. These textiles act as filtering media in filtration units, retaining contaminants while allowing the fluid to pass. The class includes various material constructions designed for filtration applications.
[B01D 2239/0604] Arrangement of the fibres in the filtering material -- This subclass covers the geometric configuration of fibres within a filter medium that is used to separate liquid or gaseous streams. It addresses how fibre orientation, spacing, layering or patterning affects the flow path, capture efficiency and pressure drop of the filtering material. The focus is on the structural arrangement rather than the fibre composition.
[B01D 2239/0609] Knitted -- The B01D 2239/0609 entry pertains to knitted materials employed as filter media in separation operations for liquids or gases. It classifies knitted structures designed to capture, retain, or pass fluids during filtration processes, fitting within the broader hierarchy of performing operations, separating, and separation.
[B01D 2239/0613] Woven -- B01D 2239/0613 refers to woven filtering elements used to separate liquids or gases, such as cloth or mesh fabrics that act as filter media. The classification falls under performing operations - separating - separation and covers filters made by weaving fibers into a structured web.
[B01D 2239/0618] Non-woven -- This CPC subclass covers non-woven filter media such as fabrics, webs or felts employed to separate particles from liquids or gases.
[B01D 2239/0622] Melt-blown -- B01D 2239/0622 - Melt-blown describes a method of forming fine polymer fibers by forcing molten material through tiny nozzles while a high-velocity gas stream draws and attenuates the fibers, producing a non-woven web that serves as filtration media for liquids or gases.
[B01D 2239/0627] Spun-bonded -- Spun-bonded refers to a nonwoven fabric produced by extruding continuous filaments that are immediately bonded together, typically by heat or chemical means, to form a web. This web is used as a filtration medium for liquids or gases, providing a porous structure that captures particles while allowing fluid flow. The process is classified under the separation field of the Cooperative Patent Classification system.
[B01D 2239/0631] Electro-spun -- B01D 2239/0631 covers the use of electro-spinning techniques to produce fibrous mats that serve as filter media for liquids or gases. The entry relates to separation operations where the electro-spun structure captures particulates, droplets or contaminants, facilitating filtration.
[B01D 2239/0636] Two or more types of fibres present in the filter material -- This CPC subclass concerns filter media that incorporate two or more distinct fibre types within the filtering material, used for separating liquid or gaseous fluids. It is nested under the hierarchy of performing operations, separating, and separation.
[B01D 2239/064] The fibres being mixed -- The code B01D 2239/064 covers methods in which fibers are mixed to form or modify a filter medium used for separating liquids or gases. It addresses the preparation of composite filter media, detailing how the blending of fibers influences the structure and performance of the filtration system.
[B01D 2239/0645] Arrangement of the particles in the filtering material -- The subclass covers inventions that define how particles are organized within a filter medium used to separate liquids or gases. It includes specific geometries, layering or distribution patterns that improve filtration performance, such as aligned fibers, graded porosity or structured packing.
[B01D 2239/065] More than one layer present in the filtering material -- This subclass covers filters that incorporate a composite structure formed of two or more distinct layers of filtering material, each layer potentially differing in composition, pore size or function, to enhance the separation of liquid or gaseous streams.
[B01D 2239/0654] Support layers -- Support layers are structural components that back or reinforce filter media in liquid or gas filtration devices, helping to maintain the shape, porosity and mechanical strength of the filter while allowing fluid flow. They are typically made of porous fabrics, meshes or polymeric foams and are used in a wide range of separating operations.
[B01D 2239/0659] The layers being joined by needling -- B01D 2239/0659 relates to the manufacturing of filter media where separate layers are mechanically joined by needling, a process that uses barbed needles to interlock fibers. This technique creates a composite structure that enhances separation performance for liquid or gaseous streams.
[B01D 2239/0663] The layers being joined by hydro-entangling -- This subclass covers methods where separate layers of filter media are interlaced using hydro-entangling techniques to create a bonded composite. It falls under the separation field of the CPC, focusing on filtration of liquids or gases.
[B01D 2239/0668] The layers being joined by heat or melt-bonding -- The classification B01D 2239/0668 covers filter assemblies in which separate layers of filtering material for liquids or gases are permanently joined by applying heat or melt-bonding. It addresses the creation of composite filter structures where the bonding process fuses the layers together to improve integrity, durability, or performance.
[B01D 2239/0672] The layers being joined by welding -- This CPC subclass B01D 2239/0672 falls under Performing Operations → Separating → Separation and covers filtration devices for liquids or gases where the filter layers are joined by welding, addressing the bonding of separate media through a welding process.
[B01D 2239/0677] by spot-welding -- B01D 2239/0677 covers separation processes in which spot-welding is employed to join or secure filter components, enabling the removal of particles from liquids or gases. It pertains to the use of localized welding to create or fix filter media within systems that perform fluid filtration. This sub-class falls under the broader categories of performing operations, separating, and separation.
[B01D 2239/0681] The layers being joined by gluing -- The subclass B01D 2239/0681 covers filtration methods where the filter media are constructed by bonding separate layers together with an adhesive. It deals with the manufacturing of liquid or gas filters that rely on glued layers to form the separating structure, emphasizing the joining technique rather than the filtering material itself.
[B01D 2239/0686] by spot-gluing -- The CPC class B01D 2239/0686 "by spot-gluing" covers a method of assembling filter media in which adhesive is applied at discrete points to join layers while maintaining overall porosity. This technique is used in the manufacture of liquid or gas filtration elements to create a separation structure without fully sealing the filter surface.
[B01D 2239/069] Special geometry of layers -- B01D 2239/069 covers filters whose layers have a non-standard geometry, such as curved, angled or three-dimensional configurations, used to improve separation of liquids or gases. The classification applies to devices where the shape of the filtering media is intentionally varied to control flow paths, pressure drop or capture efficiency.
[B01D 2239/0695] Wound layers -- The code B01D 2239/0695 covers wound-layer filter media-structures in which one or more filtration layers are wound around a core to form a compact, high-surface-area element for separating liquids or gases. It relates to the design and arrangement of these layered materials to achieve specific filtration or separation performance.
[B01D 2239/08] Special characteristics of binders -- B01D 2239/08 concerns the special characteristics of binders used in filter media for liquid or gas separation, covering properties such as composition, porosity, adhesive strength, chemical stability and moisture resistance that affect the performance and durability of the filter.
[B01D 2239/083] Binders between layers of the filter -- The code covers any adhesive or binding material placed between successive layers of a filter medium to hold the layers together while allowing fluid flow. Such binders may be polymeric, inorganic or natural compounds and are used in filters for liquids or gases to improve mechanical integrity without significantly hindering separation performance.
[B01D 2239/086] Binders between particles or fibres -- The code covers the use of binders that join particles or fibres in filter media designed for liquid or gaseous separation. It includes methods and materials that improve the mechanical strength and stability of filters while maintaining flow characteristics, enabling effective removal of contaminants during the separation process.
[B01D 2239/10] Filtering material manufacturing -- The B01D 2239/10 subclass concerns the manufacture of filtering materials employed in liquid or gas separation processes, covering the composition, structure and fabrication techniques of filter media. It falls under the broader categories of performing operations, separating and separation, and addresses the production of components that capture particles, droplets or contaminants from fluid streams.
[B01D 2239/12] Special parameters characterising the filtering material -- B01D 2239/12 covers the special parameters that characterize filtering materials used in separating operations, such as pore size, surface area, thickness, fiber density or chemical composition. These attributes define the performance of filters for liquid or gaseous streams.
[B01D 2239/1208] Porosity -- The entry B01D 2239/1208 Porosity classifies technical features that describe the pore structure of filter media used to separate liquids or gases, including pore size, distribution and open-vs-closed porosity. It is applied when the porosity of the material directly influences its filtering performance.
[B01D 2239/1216] Pore size -- B01D 2239/1216 concerns the pore size of filter media used in the separation of liquids or gases, detailing the dimensions of openings that determine retention or passage of particles. It falls under Performing Operations → Separating → Separation and applies to any filtration material whose pore diameter is a relevant functional parameter.
[B01D 2239/1225] Fibre length -- The code B01D 2239/1225 classifies inventions that specify the length of fibres used in filter media for separating liquids or gases. It covers design, selection or control of fibre length as a functional feature affecting filtration performance.
[B01D 2239/1233] Fibre diameter -- The CPC entry B01D 2239/1233 covers the specification of fibre diameter in filter media designed for separating liquids or gases. It addresses how the size of individual fibres influences the performance of filters, including permeability, retention efficiency and pressure drop, within broader separating operations.
[B01D 2239/1241] Particle diameter -- The CPC subgroup B01D 2239/1241 covers specifications of particle diameter for filter media used in the separation of liquids or gases, focusing on how particle size influences filtration efficiency and performance.
[B01D 2239/125] Size distribution -- The CPC entry B01D 2239/125 "Size distribution" covers techniques and apparatus for measuring and characterising the particle-size distribution of filter media used in liquid or gaseous separations, including methods for sampling, analysis and reporting of size ranges. It is applied to any process that determines how filter granules, fibres or pores are distributed to assess performance in separating operations.
[B01D 2239/1258] Permeability -- B01D 2239/1258 deals with the permeability of filter media employed in liquid or gas separation operations, describing how readily fluids can pass through the material and its impact on separator efficiency.
[B01D 2239/1266] Solidity -- The B01D 2239/1266 "Solidity" subclass covers filter media whose primary characteristic is a solid, non-fluid structure used to separate particles or phases from liquids or gases. It includes porous solids, membranes, and other rigid filtering elements designed to retain contaminants while allowing the fluid to pass. This subclass falls under the broader categories of performing operations, separating, and separation.
[B01D 2239/1275] Stiffness -- The CPC subclass B01D 2239/1275 designates patents dealing with the stiffness of filter materials employed in separation processes for liquids or gases. It addresses how a filter's rigidity affects its performance in retaining or passing fluids, and includes methods for measuring or adjusting that property. This subclass falls under Performing Operations → Separating → Separation.
[B01D 2239/1283] Stability index -- The CPC entry B01D 2239/1283, titled Stability index, falls under Performing Operations - Separating - Separation and relates to assessing the stability of filter media used for liquid or gas filtration. It covers methods or parameters that quantify how a filter's performance remains consistent under operating conditions.
[B01D 2239/1291] Other parameters -- The subclass B01D 2239/1291 covers supplementary characteristics of filter materials used to separate liquids or gases, such as pressure drop, flow rate, or durability, that are not addressed in more specific categories. It falls under performing operations, separating, separation, focusing on aspects of filtering material for liquid or gaseous fluids.
[B01D 2247/00] Details relating to the separation of dispersed particles from gases, air or vapours by liquid as separating agent -- B01D 2247/00 covers methods for removing solid particles that are suspended in gases, air or vapours by contacting them with a liquid that acts as the separating medium. The liquid captures or washes out the particles, enabling their collection and subsequent treatment. This falls under the broader categories of performing operations, separating and separation.
[B01D 2247/02] Enhancing the particle separation by electrostatic or magnetic effects -- The entry pertains to techniques that improve the removal of dispersed solid particles from gases, air or vapours using a liquid separating agent, where electrostatic or magnetic forces are employed to boost separation efficiency. It is classified under B01D 2247/02 within the broader group for particle separation by liquid media.
[B01D 2247/04] Regenerating the washing fluid -- The CPC entry B01D 2247/04 covers techniques for renewing or restoring the washing fluid used in liquid-based gas cleaning processes, where dispersed particles are removed from air, gases or vapours by a liquid separating agent. It focuses on methods that treat, clean or recycle the scrubbing liquid so it can be reused in further separation cycles.
[B01D 2247/06] Separation units provided with means for cleaning and maintenance -- This CPC subclass covers separation units that remove dispersed particles from gases, air, or vapour streams by passing the stream through a liquid medium and that incorporate built-in systems for cleaning or maintaining the equipment.
[B01D 2247/08] Means for controlling the separation process -- B01D 2247/08 refers to means for controlling the separation of dispersed particles from gases, air, or vapors by a liquid separating agent. It falls under the CPC hierarchy of performing operations, separating, separation. The code covers devices or mechanisms that regulate the liquid-based particle removal process.
[B01D 2247/10] Means for removing the washing fluid dispersed in the gas or vapours -- The entry concerns devices that extract liquid washing fluid dispersed in gases or vapours, employing a liquid medium to capture and separate the droplets from the air stream. It falls under the CPC subclass for separation of dispersed particles from gases by a liquid separating agent.
[B01D 2247/101] using a cyclone -- The entry covers cyclone devices that separate dispersed solid particles from gases, air or vapours by introducing a liquid as the separating agent, falling under the broader category of separating operations and particle separation.
[B01D 2247/102] using electrostatic or magnetic effects -- B01D2247/102 pertains to methods that separate dispersed particles from gases, air or vapours by employing a liquid as the separating agent, with the process enhanced by electrostatic or magnetic effects. It resides in the CPC hierarchy under Performing Operations, Separating, Separation.
[B01D 2247/103] using fluids, e.g. as a fluid curtain or as large liquid droplets -- The entry B01D 2247/103 describes methods that employ fluids, such as a fluid curtain or large liquid droplets, to remove dispersed particles from gases, air or vapours. It falls under the CPC hierarchy of Performing Operations → Separating → Separation and addresses liquid-based particle capture.
[B01D 2247/104] using an impeller -- This subclass covers methods that employ an impeller to separate dispersed particles from gases, air or vapours using a liquid as the separating medium. It falls under the broader category of separating operations that involve liquid-based removal of particles from a gaseous stream.
[B01D 2247/105] by gas flow reversal -- The B01D 2247/105 class covers methods that separate dispersed particles from gases, air or vapours by using a liquid as the separating medium and employing a reversal of the gas flow direction to enhance removal. This falls under the broader groups of performing operations, separating and separation.
[B01D 2247/106] using a structured demister, e.g. tortuous channels -- This sub-class covers methods that employ a structured demister, such as tortuous-channel designs, to separate solid particles suspended in gases, air or vapours by contacting them with a liquid. It falls under the CPC group for separation operations that use a liquid separating agent.
[B01D 2247/107] using an unstructured demister, e.g. a wire mesh demister -- The CPC entry B01D 2247/107 covers the use of unstructured demisters-typically wire-mesh assemblies-to remove dispersed particles from gases, air or vapours by employing a liquid as the separating medium. It falls under Performing Operations → Separating → Separation.
[B01D 2247/108] using vortex inducers -- The CPC subgroup B01D 2247/108 covers separation techniques that use vortex inducers to improve liquid-based removal of dispersed particles from gases, air or vapours. It relates to processes where a rotating flow created by a vortex enhances contact between the gas stream and the liquid separating agent, increasing particle capture efficiency.
[B01D 2247/12] Fan arrangements for providing forced draft -- B01D 2247/12 covers fan configurations that generate a forced draft to enhance liquid-based removal of dispersed particles from gases, air or vapours. The arrangement supplies the airflow needed for the liquid separating agent to contact and collect the particulates.
[B01D 2247/14] Fan arrangements for providing induced draft -- Fan arrangements that create an induced draft are classified under B01D 2247/14, covering equipment that draws air or vapor through a liquid separating medium to remove dispersed particles. These systems use fans to generate the necessary flow, facilitating the liquid-based separation of gases, air or vapours.
[B01D 2251/00] Reactants -- B01D 2251/00 - Reactants pertains to the chemicals or substances employed to carry out a separation operation within the broader field of performing operations. It covers the selection, preparation, and use of reactants that facilitate the division of mixtures into distinct components. This subclass is nested under Performing Operations → Separating → Separation.
[B01D 2251/10] Oxidants -- The CPC entry B01D 2251/10 under Performing Operations → Separating → Separation → Reactants designates oxidants, which are chemical agents that promote oxidation by accepting electrons from other substances. It covers reagents used to increase the oxidation state of reactants in various industrial and laboratory processes.
[B01D 2251/102] Oxygen -- The CPC subclass B01D 2251/102 covers technologies for separating oxygen as a reactant in chemical processes. It includes methods and equipment that isolate or produce oxygen from mixtures, typically as part of performing operations that involve separation steps.
[B01D 2251/104] Ozone -- B01D 2251/104 relates to the use of ozone as a reactant in separation operations. It covers methods that employ ozone's oxidative power to bleach, degrade or otherwise treat materials to achieve purification or removal of contaminants. The code applies to processes where ozone is generated and applied to effect the desired separation.
[B01D 2251/106] Peroxides -- The CPC subclass B01D2251/106 groups patents that disclose peroxide compounds used as reactants in processes for separating mixtures, such as oxidation- or radical-initiated separations, bleaching, or other separation-related operations. It is nested under Performing Operations → Separating → Separation → Reactants.
[B01D 2251/1065] Organic peroxides -- B01D 2251/1065 pertains to the use of organic peroxides as reactants in processes that separate substances. It falls under the performing operations > separating > separation > reactants hierarchy and covers methods where peroxide compounds facilitate or drive the separation of materials.
[B01D 2251/108] Halogens or halogen compounds -- The B01D 2251/108 subgroup includes patents that use halogen elements or halogen-containing compounds as catalysts, co-catalysts, promoters, or as components in the preparation of catalytic materials for processes such as cracking, reforming and other catalytic operations.
