[B01D 2253/204] Metal organic frameworks (MOF's) -- This classification covers the use of metal-organic frameworks as adsorbent materials for separating gases and vapours. MOFs are porous crystalline compounds composed of metal nodes linked by organic ligands, providing high surface area and tunable pore chemistry for selective adsorption. The entry is nested under performing operations, separating and separation adsorbents.
[B01D 2253/206] Ion exchange resins -- The subclass B01D 2253/206 includes ion-exchange resins used as adsorbent materials for separating or purifying gases and vapours. These resins work by exchanging ions with the gas phase to selectively retain or remove target components, enabling processes such as drying, dehumidification, or removal of acidic gases.
[B01D 2253/25] Coated, impregnated or composite adsorbents -- Coated, impregnated or composite adsorbents are materials whose surfaces or internal structures are modified by a coating, impregnation or combination of substances to improve adsorption of gases or vapours for separation processes. They are employed in gas-phase treatments such as purification, drying or selective capture.
[B01D 2253/30] Physical properties of adsorbents -- This CPC subclass B01D 2253/30 covers the measurement and characterization of physical attributes of adsorbents-such as surface area, pore-size distribution, density, and heat of adsorption-that are relevant to gas and vapour separation processes. It is placed under the hierarchy of Performing Operations → Separating → Separation.
[B01D 2253/302] Dimensions -- This subclass pertains to the dimensional characteristics of adsorbents employed in the separation of gases and vapours, including particle size, shape, pore-size distribution and other geometric parameters that influence separation efficiency.
[B01D 2253/304] Linear dimensions, e.g. particle shape, diameter -- The subclass B01D 2253/304 concerns patents that address the linear dimensions of adsorbent particles, such as shape and diameter, because these physical attributes influence the efficiency of gas and vapour separation. It is positioned under Performing Operations → Separating → Separation, focusing on adsorbents used in treatment processes.
[B01D 2253/306] Surface area, e.g. BET-specific surface -- The CPC entry B01D 2253/306 covers adsorbent materials employed in the separation of gases or vapours, specifically focusing on the determination of their surface area such as BET-specific surface. It falls under the hierarchy of Performing Operations → Separating → Separation and pertains to the characterization of adsorbents used in treatment processes.
[B01D 2253/308] Pore size -- This CPC subgroup concerns adsorbent materials whose pore dimensions are defined to enable selective separation of gases and vapours. It includes the specification, measurement and control of pore size to influence adsorption capacity and selectivity, and related processes that rely on pore-size tailoring for effective separation.
[B01D 2253/31] Pore size distribution -- This CPC subclass B01D 2253/31 covers methods and apparatus for determining the pore size distribution of adsorbent materials used to separate gases and vapours. It relates to measuring the range and frequency of pore diameters in porous solids employed in separation treatment.
[B01D 2253/311] Porosity, e.g. pore volume -- The code B01D 2253/311 classifies adsorbent materials whose porosity-specifically their pore volume-is a defining feature for separating gases or vapours. It covers inventions that describe, measure, or modify pore structure to improve the efficiency of separation processes.
[B01D 2253/34] Specific shapes -- The CPC subclass B01D 2253/34 covers adsorbent materials employed to separate gases or vapours where the adsorbent is provided in particular geometric forms. It pertains to the design of particles, pellets, granules or other shapes that influence separation performance. This subclass falls under the broader hierarchy of performing operations, separating, separation.
[B01D 2253/342] Monoliths -- Monoliths are single-piece, often porous, structured adsorbent bodies employed to separate gases and vapours, offering high surface area and open flow channels that enhance mass transfer. They are used in processes such as gas chromatography, pressure-swing adsorption, and catalytic reactors to improve efficiency and reduce pressure drop.
[B01D 2253/3425] Honeycomb shape -- Honeycomb-shaped adsorbent structures are engineered with a network of uniform cells to maximize surface area while maintaining low pressure drop, enabling efficient separation of gases and vapours. The geometry facilitates rapid diffusion and uniform flow across the material, improving adsorption capacity and regeneration speed in industrial separation processes.

It includes both homogeneous and heterogeneous catalysts specifically designed to facilitate the removal of components from a feed stream.

[B01D 2255/40] Mixed oxides -- Mixed oxides are solid compounds comprising two or more metal oxides combined in a defined stoichiometry, often used as heterogeneous catalysts in chemical processes such as oxidation, reduction, and reforming. They may be prepared by co-precipitation, impregnation or calcination and exhibit synergistic catalytic properties differing from the individual oxides. This entry covers such mixed-oxide catalyst compositions and their use in separation operations.

[B01D 2255/407] Zr-Ce mixed oxides -- The CPC entry B01D 2255/407 covers mixed oxides of zirconium and cerium used as catalysts in separation processes. These Zr-Ce mixed oxides facilitate the selective removal or transformation of components in chemical streams, such as in catalytic cracking, oxidation, or pollutant abatement, where their combined redox and acid properties enhance performance.
[B01D 2255/50] Zeolites -- Zeolites are crystalline aluminosilicate minerals with a uniform microporous framework that act as solid-acid catalysts and molecular sieves. Their high surface area and shape-selective pores enable catalytic cracking, isomerisation and the selective adsorption or ion-exchange separation of molecules. This makes them central to processes that combine catalytic conversion with separation of reactants and products.
[B01D 2255/502] Beta zeolites -- Beta zeolites are high-silica zeolitic materials used as solid-acid catalysts in separation operations such as hydrocracking, isomerization and catalytic cracking of hydrocarbons. Their three-dimensional pore system and thermal stability make them valuable for improving product selectivity in petrochemical processes.
[B01D 2255/504] ZSM 5 zeolites -- The CPC entry B01D 2255/504, titled ZSM-5 zeolites, falls under Performing Operations - Separating - Separation - Catalysts. It pertains to the use of ZSM-5 zeolite materials as solid acid catalysts in processes that separate chemical mixtures, such as hydrocarbon cracking or isomerisation. The classification captures inventions that employ these porous aluminosilicate structures to enhance selectivity and efficiency in catalytic separation operations.

[B01D 2255/707] Additives or dopants -- B01D 2255/707 refers to additives or dopants employed in separation operations that involve catalysts. [B01D 2255/80] Type of catalytic reaction -- B01D 2255/80 classifies patents according to the particular type of catalytic reaction employed-such as oxidation, reduction, hydrogenation, or hydrolysis-within separation processes. [B01D 2255/802] Photocatalytic -- B01D 2255/802 is placed under Performing Operations → Separating → Separation → Catalysts and relates to photocatalytic processes, where light energy activates a catalyst to accelerate chemical reactions such as pollutant breakdown, water splitting or organic synthesis, enabling efficient, environmentally friendly transformations.

[B01D 2255/806] Electrocatalytic -- Electrocatalytic refers to processes that use an electric current to activate a catalyst, accelerating chemical reactions during a separation operation. This includes electrodes or electrically powered catalytic systems that promote reduction or oxidation of target compounds to achieve selective separation. The classification covers apparatus, methods, and materials designed for such electrically driven catalytic separation steps.
[B01D 2255/808] Hydrolytic -- The CPC subclass B01D2255/808 covers hydrolytic catalysts used in separation operations, that is, substances that promote hydrolysis to split compounds during a separating process such as liquid-phase extraction or purification. It includes enzymes, acids, bases and other materials that facilitate the breaking of chemical bonds by water. This entry falls under Performing Operations → Separating → Separation → Catalysts.
[B01D 2255/90] Physical characteristics of catalysts -- The CPC entry B01D 2255/90 covers the physical properties of catalysts used in chemical processes, including attributes such as particle size, shape, surface area, porosity, density and mechanical strength. It pertains to how these characteristics affect catalyst performance and handling.
[B01D 2255/902] Multilayered catalyst -- B01D 2255/902 designates a multilayered catalyst, a catalyst structure formed from two or more distinct layers that may differ in composition, size or function to optimise activity, selectivity or durability. Such catalysts are employed in processes that involve separating substances, improving performance in chemical reactions.
[B01D 2255/9022] Two layers -- This CPC subgroup pertains to processes or devices where separation is achieved by employing a catalyst arranged in two distinct layers, such as a dual-layer catalytic membrane or a two-phase reactor where each layer provides different catalytic functions. It falls under separation operations within performing operations.
[B01D 2255/9025] Three layers -- The CPC entry B01D 2255/9025 "Three layers" covers separation processes that employ a three-layer catalyst arrangement, typically in reactors or devices where each layer performs a distinct catalytic function to facilitate the selective separation of components. This classification is used for technologies that integrate layered catalysts to improve efficiency or selectivity in chemical separations.
[B01D 2255/9027] More than three layers -- B01D 2255/9027 pertains to catalytic separation processes that employ a catalyst arranged in more than three distinct layers, a configuration used to improve reaction control, selectivity or product separation. It is classified under performing operations, separating, separation, catalysts.
[B01D 2255/903] Multi-zoned catalysts -- Multi-zoned catalysts are engineered particles or structures that contain separate functional zones, each tailored to promote a specific reaction step within a single catalytic system. By spatially isolating active sites, they enhance selectivity and efficiency in processes that combine reaction and separation, such as staged oxidation-reduction or multi-phase transformations.
[B01D 2255/9032] Two zones -- Two zones denotes a separation process where the reactor or column is divided into two distinct zones, each employing different catalysts or operating conditions to achieve staged conversion or selective separation. This configuration improves efficiency, selectivity and product purity in catalytic or catalytic-distillation operations.
[B01D 2255/9035] Three zones -- The CPC entry B01D 2255/9035 "Three zones" covers catalysts designed with three distinct functional regions, each providing a different reaction environment to enhance selective separation or conversion steps. By spatially separating active, intermediate, and support zones, these catalysts enable controlled multi-stage processes such as staged oxidation-reduction or staged adsorption-desorption, improving efficiency and selectivity.
[B01D 2255/9037] More than three zones -- This subclass covers catalytic separation processes in which the equipment is divided into more than three distinct zones, each zone operating under different conditions or containing different catalyst distributions to achieve the desired separation. It falls under the hierarchy of performing operations, separating, separation, and catalysts, and is identified by the CPC code B01D 2255/9037.
[B01D 2255/904] Multiple catalysts -- The B01D 2255/904 entry covers inventions that employ two or more catalysts together in a single chemical process, often to achieve synergistic activity, selectivity or efficiency. It includes methods where distinct catalytic species act simultaneously or sequentially, such as combined homogeneous-heterogeneous systems, tandem catalysis or cascade reactions, and related apparatus for handling multiple catalysts.
[B01D 2255/9045] in parallel -- B01D 2255/9045 covers catalytic separation operations carried out in parallel, for example using multiple catalyst beds or simultaneous reactor streams to achieve component separation. It applies to processes where parallel configuration improves efficiency or selectivity in separating mixtures.
[B01D 2255/905] Catalysts having a gradually changing coating -- Catalysts having a gradually changing coating are those whose surface layer varies in composition, thickness or structure across its depth, creating a gradient that can modify catalytic activity, selectivity or stability during a process. The classification covers heterogeneous catalysts designed with such graded coatings, often applied by techniques like diffusion, sputtering or layer-by-layer deposition to achieve controlled reaction zones.
[B01D 2255/906] Catalyst dispersed in the gas -- A catalyst that is distributed as fine particles throughout a gaseous phase, allowing the reactants to contact the active surface while the catalyst remains suspended in the gas. This arrangement is used in gas-phase reactions such as oxidation, hydrogenation or cracking, where the catalyst can be recovered by separating the gas from the solid particles after the reaction.

[B01D 2255/915] Catalyst supported on particulate filters -- A catalyst that is immobilised on a particulate filter, where the filter medium serves both to separate solids or liquids and to provide a surface for catalytic reactions, typically used in processes that combine filtration with chemical conversion such as removal of pollutants or synthesis steps.
[B01D 2255/9155] Wall flow filters -- Wall flow filters are devices that separate solid particles from liquids or gases by guiding the stream along a porous wall where filtration takes place, often used in catalytic or chemical processes. The filter comprises a cylindrical or planar wall coated with a filtration medium that traps particulates while allowing the fluid to pass, enabling continuous separation without moving filter elements.
[B01D 2255/92] Dimensions -- B01D 2255/92 "Dimensions" covers processes in which the size, shape, surface area or particle size distribution of a catalyst is controlled, measured or modified for use in separating operations. [B01D 2255/9205] Porosity -- The CPC entry B01D 2255/9205 concerns the porosity of catalysts, including methods for creating, modifying, and measuring pore structures that affect catalytic performance. [B01D 2255/9207] Specific surface -- B01D 2255/9207 covers catalysts defined by their specific-surface properties, such as surface area, porosity, or active site distribution, which influence their performance in separation or reaction processes. The code is used for patents describing materials where the surface characteristics are critical to the catalytic function.

[B01D 2259/40022] with two sub-steps -- This CPC subclass pertains to separation methods in which the overall treatment is divided into two distinct sub-steps, each contributing to the final separation outcome. [B01D 2259/40026] with more than three sub-steps -- B01D 2259/40026 classifies separation processes that are carried out as a type of treatment comprising more than three distinct sub-steps. [B01D 2259/40028] Depressurization -- B01D 2259/40028 Depressurization covers methods that lower pressure to aid the separation of mixtures, such as liquids, gases, or solids, by causing phase changes, releasing dissolved gases, or reducing solubility. [B01D 2259/4003] with two sub-steps -- This subclass addresses separation methods that are carried out in two distinct sub-steps, wherein the material undergoes an initial treatment followed by a second complementary step to achieve the desired separation. [B01D 2259/40032] with three sub-steps -- The Cooperative Patent Classification entry B01D 2259/40032, under the hierarchy of PERFORMING OPERATIONS, SEPARATING, and SEPARATION, specifically addresses a type of treatment involving separation processes that consist of three distinct sub-steps.
[B01D 2259/40033] with more than three sub-steps -- The CPC subclass B01D 2259/40033 covers separation processes that are performed by a treatment consisting of more than three distinct sub-steps. It applies to operations where a material is divided into components through a sequence of at least four individual actions, such as successive washing, filtration, precipitation, or other treatment stages. This subclass falls under the hierarchy of performing operations → separating → separation → type of treatment.
[B01D 2259/40035] Equalization -- Equalization in the CPC class B01D 2259/40035 covers processes that balance or equalize properties such as concentration, temperature, pressure, or flow rates among streams or phases during a separation operation. It includes techniques for mixing, redistribution, or adjustment to achieve uniform conditions before, during, or after the separation step. The class is used for patents dealing with treatment methods that improve efficiency or product quality by equalizing variables.
[B01D 2259/40037] with two sub-steps -- B01D2259/40037 designates separation methods that are carried out in two consecutive sub-steps, for example an initial separation followed by a further treatment or refinement. The classification is used for processes where the overall separation is divided into two distinct operational stages.
[B01D 2259/40039] with three sub-steps -- The CPC group B01D 2259/40039 covers separation operations that are performed in three distinct sub-steps, specifying processes where the overall treatment is divided into three successive stages to achieve the desired split of components. This subclass falls under the broader hierarchy of Performing Operations → Separating → Separation.
[B01D 2259/40041] with more than three sub-steps -- In the CPC, B01D 2259/40041 designates separation operations that are carried out in more than three sub-steps, referring to multi-stage treatment processes used to isolate components. It falls under the performing operations - separating - separation - type of treatment hierarchy.
[B01D 2259/40043] Purging -- The CPC code B01D 2259/40043 - Purging denotes a separating treatment that removes unwanted substances, gases or liquids from a system or reactor during an operation. It belongs to the hierarchy Performing Operations > Separating > Separation, Type of treatment.
[B01D 2259/40045] with two sub-steps -- B01D 2259/40045 covers separation processes that are carried out in two distinct sub-steps, such as a primary division followed by a secondary refinement or extraction stage. It falls under the CPC hierarchy "Performing operations - separating - separation - type of treatment," and is used to classify inventions where sequential treatment steps are essential to achieve the desired separation.
[B01D 2259/40047] with three sub-steps -- B01D 2259/40047 designates a separation process that is carried out in three distinct sub-steps. It falls under the CPC hierarchy performing operations, separating, separation, type of treatment, and covers methods where a material is divided into components through a sequence of three treatment stages.
[B01D 2259/40049] with more than three sub-steps -- The CPC subclass B01D 2259/40049 refers to separation processes that are performed in more than three distinct sub-steps, describing a multi-stage treatment where a material is partitioned into components. It is nested under Performing Operations → Separating → Separation - type of treatment.
[B01D 2259/4005] Nature of purge gas -- This subclass covers the specification of the gas employed to purge a system during a separation process, describing whether the purge medium is inert, reactive, steam, or another type. It addresses how the chosen purge gas assists in removing residual material, maintaining pressure, or enhancing the efficiency of the separation operation.
[B01D 2259/40052] Recycled product or process gas -- A CPC subclass that covers methods for separating a recycled product or process gas from a mixture, typically in chemical or industrial operations that recover and reuse the gas or material as part of a treatment step. It falls under the hierarchy of Performing Operations → Separating → Separation → Type of Treatment.
[B01D 2259/40054] treated before its reuse -- The code B01D 2259/40054 pertains to processes where a material is treated before being reused, generally by a separation operation that removes impurities or recovers valuable components as part of the overall treatment.
[B01D 2259/40056] Gases other than recycled product or process gas -- The subclass B01D 2259/40056 covers separation techniques that remove gases which are not recycled product or process gases. It includes methods for isolating and extracting such gases from mixtures during chemical or industrial operations. This entry falls under the broader categories of performing operations, separating, and separation, specifically addressing the type-of-treatment aspect.
[B01D 2259/40058] Number of sequence steps, including sub-steps, per cycle -- The CPC entry B01D 2259/40058, titled "Number of sequence steps, including sub-steps, per cycle," classifies patents that specify how many individual or sub-steps occur within each cycle of a separation operation. It falls under the hierarchy Performing Operations → Separating → Separation.
[B01D 2259/4006] Less than four -- The subclass B01D 2259/4006 designates separation processes applied to mixtures that contain fewer than four distinct components, covering binary and ternary separations performed by physical, chemical or mechanical means. It is used to classify patents describing any treatment that isolates two or three substances from a feed stream.
[B01D 2259/40062] Four -- In the Cooperative Patent Classification, code B01D 2259/40062 falls under Performing Operations → Separating → Separation → Type of Treatment and designates the "Four" treatment, a specific method of separating materials defined by a four-component or four-step process. This narrow subclass groups patents that employ that particular technique for achieving separation.
[B01D 2259/40064] Five -- The CPC subclass B01D 2259/40064, titled "Five," falls under Performing Operations → Separating → Separation → Type of treatment and covers separation methods or apparatuses that employ a five-step, five-stage, or five-component approach for treating and dividing mixtures, such as processes that sequentially use five distinct phases, reagents or operations to achieve the desired separation.
[B01D 2259/40066] Six -- The CPC entry B01D 2259/40066 falls under the "Separation" branch of "Performing Operations" and designates a type of treatment that involves a six-step or six-component separation process. It covers methods or apparatuses that use six distinct stages, phases, or reagents to achieve the separation of mixtures, typically in chemical or industrial contexts.
[B01D 2259/40067] Seven -- The CPC entry B01D 2259/40067, titled "Seven," defines a specific type of separation treatment in which a mixture is divided into its components by a seven-stage or seven-unit process, falling under the hierarchy Performing Operations → Separating → Separation. It covers methods and apparatuses that implement this seven-step approach to achieve the desired component separation.
[B01D 2259/40069] Eight -- The CPC subclass B01D 2259/40069 falls under Performing Operations → Separating → Separation → Type of treatment and designates a specific treatment method in separation processes identified as "Eight." It is used to classify patents that describe an eight-part or eight-stage technique for separating components, typically in chemical or industrial contexts.
[B01D 2259/40071] Nine -- This CPC subgroup (B01D 2259/40071 - Nine) classifies a specific type-of-treatment separation method that involves a nine-step or nine-stage process for isolating components of a mixture. It falls under the broader categories of Performing Operations, Separating, and Separation, and is used to catalogue specialized treatment techniques in chemical or material processing.
[B01D 2259/40073] Ten -- B01D 2259/40073 refers to a specific type of separation process classified under Performing Operations - Separating - Separation - Type of treatment, where the treatment is designated by the term "Ten". It covers methods that apply the "Ten" treatment step to separate desired components from a mixture, typically in chemical or industrial processing.
[B01D 2259/40075] More than ten -- CPC code B01D 2259/40075 falls under "Performing operations → Separating → Separation → Type of treatment" and designates processes that involve separating a material using a method that handles more than ten distinct separation stages or components. This entry is used for patents describing complex multi-step separation treatments, such as advanced filtration, centrifugation, or chromatography sequences that exceed ten individual operations.
[B01D 2259/40077] Direction of flow -- In separation processes, "direction of flow" denotes how the moving medium-liquid, gas, or solid particles-is guided through the apparatus, influencing the path and zones where separation occurs. Controlling flow direction enables selective retention or removal of components, optimizing efficiency and product purity.
[B01D 2259/40079] Co-current -- The CPC code B01D 2259/40079 designates a type of separation process in which the feed and the separating medium move in the same direction, known as co-current operation. It falls under the hierarchy of Performing Operations → Separating → Separation → Type of treatment. This classification covers equipment and methods where parallel flow enhances mass transfer or phase disengagement.
[B01D 2259/40081] Counter-current -- The CPC entry B01D 2259/40081 "Counter-current" covers separation methods in which two streams flow in opposite directions, allowing continuous mass transfer between phases. It is applied to processes such as liquid-liquid extraction, solvent recovery and other counter-current operations that improve efficiency and product purity.
[B01D 2259/40083] Regeneration of adsorbents in processes other than pressure or temperature swing adsorption -- B01D 2259/40083 covers techniques for restoring used adsorbent materials without employing pressure or temperature swing cycles. It includes chemical washing, solvent extraction, vacuum desorption and other non-PSA/TSA methods used to remove adsorbed species and reactivate the adsorbent in separation processes.
[B01D 2259/40084] by exchanging used adsorbents with fresh adsorbents -- The subclass B01D 2259/40084 defines a separation method in which the adsorbent that has become saturated is removed and substituted with a fresh adsorbent, rather than being regenerated in place. It falls under the "type of treatment" category of separating operations, describing the exchange of used adsorbents with new ones to maintain adsorption efficiency.
[B01D 2259/40086] by using a purge gas -- This CPC subclass covers separation procedures that employ a purge gas to remove or isolate components from a mixture. It falls under the hierarchy performing operations - separating - separation - type of treatment, and is identified by code B01D 2259/40086.
[B01D 2259/40088] by heating -- B01D 2259/40088 refers to a separation technique in which the material is treated by heating to achieve separation, typically applied in processes that involve thermal decomposition, phase change, or volatilization to isolate desired components.
[B01D 2259/4009] using hot gas -- B01D 2259/4009 refers to separation processes that employ a hot gas as the treatment medium, using heat to vaporise, desorb or otherwise mobilise components in a mixture for their subsequent separation. It is situated under the hierarchy Performing Operations → Separating → Separation.
[B01D 2259/40092] using hot liquid -- B01D 2259/40092 covers separation techniques that employ a hot liquid as the treatment medium. It includes processes where heated liquids are used to dissolve, wash, or extract substances to achieve separation of components. This falls under the hierarchy of performing operations, separating, separation, type of treatment.
[B01D 2259/40094] by applying microwaves -- The CPC subclass B01D 2259/40094 covers separation processes that employ microwave energy as the treatment method. It includes any technique where microwaves are applied to induce heating, dielectric breakdown or other effects that facilitate the division of mixtures, such as extracting, drying or fractionating components. The classification is situated under Performing Operations → Separating → Separation → Type of treatment.
[B01D 2259/40096] by using electrical resistance heating -- This CPC subclass B01D2259/40096 relates to separation methods that employ electrical resistance heating to achieve the split, such as by heating a mixture until components separate by phase-change or viscosity differences. It is located under the hierarchy performing operations - separating - separation and covers processes that use resistive heating as the treatment type.
[B01D 2259/40098] with other heating means -- The CPC entry B01D 2259/40098 classifies separation operations that employ auxiliary heating methods in addition to the primary heating step. It pertains to processes where extra heat sources-such as steam, hot gases or electric heaters-are used to enhance the separation of substances. This falls under the hierarchy of Performing Operations → Separating → Separation → Type of treatment.
[B01D 2259/401] using a single bed -- This CPC subclass (B01D 2259/401) covers separation processes that employ a single stationary-phase bed-such as an adsorption, ion-exchange, or chromatography column-to treat a mixture and isolate desired components. It is used for patents describing single-bed operations in liquid or gas purification, recovery or removal.
[B01D 2259/402] using two beds -- This subclass covers separation processes in which the feed is passed through two distinct packed beds, each possibly containing different adsorbents or media, to achieve purification, impurity removal or fractionation. It is classified under performing operations, separating, separation, and a type of treatment, and is identified by CPC code B01D 2259/402 with the title "using two beds".
[B01D 2259/403] using three beds -- B01D 2259/403 pertains to separation processes that employ three sequential beds, each performing a distinct treatment such as adsorption, reaction, or filtration, within a single unit. It is classified under the type-of-treatment subcategory of separation operations in the Performing Operations section of the CPC. This code is used for inventions that achieve material separation by arranging three beds in series.
[B01D 2259/404] using four beds -- The CPC subclass B01D 2259/404 relates to separation processes that use four beds as the treatment method, describing configurations where four adsorption, desorption or reaction beds operate in series or parallel. It is nested under the broader categories of performing operations, separating and separation type of treatment, and is applied to patents detailing multi-bed designs for continuous or batch separation operations.
[B01D 2259/406] using more than four beds -- The code B01D 2259/406 designates separation processes that employ more than four beds, such as multi-bed chromatography or other multistage treatment systems. It falls under the CPC hierarchy of Performing Operations → Separating → Separation → Type of treatment, covering methods where several parallel or successive beds are used to enhance product purity or recovery.
[B01D 2259/4061] using five beds -- B01D 2259/4061 classifies separation operations that employ a five-bed configuration, typically in a series of packed or structured beds to achieve staged removal of components from a fluid stream. The code falls under the CPC hierarchy of Performing Operations → Separating → Separation, indicating a design where multiple beds are used to enhance efficiency, capacity, or selectivity in chemical or physical separation processes.
[B01D 2259/4062] using six beds -- B01D2259/4062 designates processes that achieve separation by employing six packed or fluidised beds, such as staged columns or reactors, to treat mixtures and isolate desired components. It is classified under Performing Operations → Separating → Separation → Type of treatment in the CPC system.
[B01D 2259/4063] using seven beds -- The B01D 2259/4063 entry covers separation operations that employ seven distinct beds, such as multi-stage chromatography or adsorption columns arranged in series or parallel. It pertains to methods where the use of seven beds provides improved control of loading, contact time, or fractionation during a treatment process. This classification falls under the hierarchy of Performing Operations → Separating → Separation → Type of treatment.
[B01D 2259/4065] using eight beds -- The CPC subclass B01D 2259/4065 relates to separation processes that use a configuration of eight beds, such as multi-stage adsorption, ion-exchange, or chromatographic systems. It covers operations where the material passes sequentially through eight distinct packed columns or beds to achieve the desired separation.
[B01D 2259/4066] using nine beds -- This CPC subclass describes separation processes that employ nine parallel beds, such as columns or chambers, to treat a feed stream in a single operation. It is used for continuous or batch separation where multiple beds work in sequence or simultaneously to improve efficiency. The entry is nested under performing operations → separating → separation → type of treatment.
[B01D 2259/4067] using ten beds -- A CPC subclass for separation processes that employ a configuration of ten beds-typically packed or fluidised-in series or parallel to treat a material stream. It covers operations where multiple beds are used to enhance efficiency, capacity or selectivity in separating components of a mixture.
[B01D 2259/4068] using more than ten beds -- The CPC subclass B01D 2259/4068 covers separation processes in which the operation employs more than ten packed or liquid-filled beds, such as multiple chromatography, ion-exchange, or adsorption columns arranged in series or parallel. It specifies the scale of the bed array rather than the chemical principle, classifying methods that require a large number of treatment stages to achieve the desired separation.
[B01D 2259/41] using plural beds of the same adsorbent in series -- A process that treats a fluid stream by passing it through two or more adsorption beds containing the same material, arranged one after another so that each successive bed further removes contaminants. The series configuration extends contact time and improves separation efficiency compared with a single bed. This method is used in gas or liquid purification where repeated adsorption stages are needed.
[B01D 2259/414] using different types of adsorbents -- The subclass B01D 2259/414 covers separation processes that employ more than one kind of adsorbent material, each selected for its specific affinity toward different components of a mixture. It includes methods where distinct adsorbents are used sequentially or simultaneously to achieve selective removal in water, air, or other media. This treatment type falls under performing operations → separating → separation → type of treatment.
[B01D 2259/4141] within a single bed -- The CPC subclass B01D 2259/4141 covers separation processes carried out in a single-bed unit, where the feed material passes through one packed column or reactor without recirculation. It includes methods such as adsorption, ion-exchange or chromatographic separation that are performed in one stage within a single bed.
[B01D 2259/4143] arranged as a mixture -- This CPC subclass covers separation techniques in which the output is intentionally maintained as a mixture rather than isolated pure components. It includes processes that arrange the separated phases together for subsequent use or further processing, such as emulsions, suspensions, or blended streams. The classification falls under B01D 2259/4143 in the performing operations - separating - separation - type of treatment hierarchy.
[B01D 2259/4145] arranged in series -- The CPC subclass B01D 2259/4145 covers separation methods in which the treatment steps are organized sequentially, with the product of one stage feeding directly into the next. It includes cascade or series arrangements of reactors, filters, centrifuges or other separating units, allowing progressive purification or fractionation in a single continuous line.
[B01D 2259/4146] Contiguous multilayered adsorbents -- This CPC subclass covers adsorbent materials that consist of two or more layers placed in direct contact, forming a contiguous multilayer structure used for separation operations. The layered design enables sequential or combined adsorption of components within a single unit, improving selectivity or capacity compared with single-layer adsorbents.
[B01D 2259/4148] Multiple layers positioned apart from each other -- The CPC entry B01D 2259/4148 concerns methods or devices for producing or handling composites in which several layers are arranged with an intentional gap between them, such as multilayer films, laminates or stacked structures where the layers are kept apart to achieve specific mechanical, chemical or optical properties.
[B01D 2259/416] involving cryogenic temperature treatment -- B01D 2259/416 classifies separation processes that employ cryogenic temperatures, where substances are cooled to extremely low temperatures to solidify, condense, or otherwise isolate components. This type of treatment is used in gas liquefaction, cryogenic distillation, and related low-temperature separation operations.
[B01D 2259/418] deleted -- B01D 2259/418 was a CPC subgroup within Performing Operations → Separating → Separation that dealt with methods for separating substances, such as solids, liquids or gases, by physical or chemical means. The entry has been deleted, meaning it is no longer used for new patent classifications, though older documents may still reference it.
[B01D 2259/45] Gas separation or purification devices adapted for specific applications -- B01D 2259/45 covers gas separation or purification devices that are specially configured for particular uses such as industrial, medical or environmental applications. The classification falls under performing operations, separating, separation, type of treatment.
[B01D 2259/4508] for cleaning air in buildings -- The B01D 2259/4508 entry covers technologies that separate and remove pollutants from indoor air to clean the atmosphere within buildings, including filtration, scrubbing or other treatment methods used in ventilation systems.
[B01D 2259/4516] for fuel vapour recovery systems -- The CPC subclass B01D 2259/4516 covers processes and apparatuses that separate fuel vapour from gas streams in recovery systems, such as those used in vehicles or refineries to capture and reuse evaporated fuel. It relates to separation treatments specifically designed for fuel-vapour recovery, reducing emissions and improving fuel efficiency.
[B01D 2259/4525] for storage and dispensing systems -- The CPC subclass B01D 2259/4525 covers devices or methods that provide storage and dispensing functions for materials that have been separated, such as chemicals, gases, or liquids, within the broader context of performing operations and separating processes. It includes systems that hold the separated product and deliver it in controlled amounts for further use, testing, or processing.
[B01D 2259/4533] for medical purposes -- B01D 2259/4533 covers methods of separating substances that are specifically intended for medical applications, such as purification of blood components, isolation of cells or biomolecules for therapeutic use, and preparation of sterile pharmaceuticals. These processes fall under the broader categories of performing operations and separating techniques.
[B01D 2259/4541] for portable use, e.g. gas masks -- The B01D 2259/4541 subclass covers portable separation devices that treat air or gases for immediate personal use, such as gas-mask filters. It includes equipment designed to separate contaminants from a breathing stream in a mobile, handheld format. These items are classified under separation processes applied in on-site or personal protection contexts.
[B01D 2259/455] for transportable use -- B01D 2259/455 designates separation processes or equipment intended for transportable use, meaning the apparatus can be moved and operated at various sites rather than being fixed in a plant. It falls under performing operations, separating, separation, type of treatment in the CPC system.
[B01D 2259/4558] for being employed as mobile cleaners for ambient air, i.e. the earth's atmosphere -- This CPC entry (B01D 2259/4558) covers technologies that function as mobile cleaners for ambient air, i.e., the Earth's atmosphere, by performing separation operations to remove pollutants or particulates. It falls under the hierarchy of Performing Operations - Separating - Separation, indicating a type of treatment aimed at improving air quality.
[B01D 2259/4566] for use in transportation means -- The CPC sub-group B01D 2259/4566 classifies separation processes or devices that are specifically designed for use in transportation means such as cars, trains, aircraft, or ships. It falls under the hierarchy of Performing Operations → Separating → Separation → Type of treatment.
[B01D 2259/4575] in aeroplanes or space ships -- This CPC heading covers separation operations conducted in aircraft or spacecraft, including any processes for isolating, filtering, or extracting substances while the vehicle is in flight. It applies to methods specifically designed for aeronautical or space-flight environments.
[B01D 2259/4583] for removing chemical, biological and nuclear warfare agents -- This CPC subgroup covers processes and equipment designed to separate and eliminate chemical, biological and nuclear warfare agents from contaminated media or surfaces. It includes chemical treatment, filtration, adsorption and other remediation techniques aimed at neutralising or extracting hazardous agents in decontamination operations.
[B01D 2259/4591] Construction elements containing cleaning material, e.g. catalysts -- This subclass concerns built-in elements such as plates, walls or other structural components that incorporate a cleaning material, often a catalyst, to aid separation processes like filtration, adsorption or reactive removal of contaminants. It addresses devices where the cleaning agent is integral to the construction rather than added separately.
[B01D 2259/65] Employing advanced heat integration, e.g. Pinch technology -- The CPC entry B01D 2259/65 covers separation processes in which advanced heat-integration techniques, such as pinch technology, are applied to improve energy efficiency. It addresses the use of systematic heat recovery and temperature-profile optimization during the separation stage, reducing external heating and cooling needs. This treatment falls under the broader categories of performing operations, separating, and separation.
[B01D 2259/652] using side coolers -- This CPC subclass B01D 2259/652 covers separation processes that use side coolers to remove heat from a flowing stream, enhancing efficiency or product quality. It falls under performing operations - separating - separation - type of treatment.
[B01D 2259/655] using heat storage materials -- This CPC subclass covers separation processes that incorporate heat-storage materials to supply or recover thermal energy during the operation. It includes methods where the material absorbs heat in one stage and releases it to aid the separation, such as in distillation, adsorption, or membrane systems. The code is B01D 2259/655.
[B01D 2259/657] using latent heat, e.g. with phase change materials -- This subclass covers separation methods that exploit latent-heat effects, typically by employing phase-change materials to absorb or release heat during the process. It includes techniques where heat storage or transfer facilitates the partitioning of mixtures, fitting within the broader performing-operations and separating categories.
[B01D 2259/80] Employing electric, magnetic, electromagnetic or wave energy, or particle radiation -- This CPC subgroup covers separation methods that utilise electric, magnetic, electromagnetic, wave or particle-radiation energy to achieve the desired separation. It includes processes such as electrophoretic, magnetic, microwave, laser or radiation-driven separation techniques applied to solids, liquids or gases.
[B01D 2259/802] Visible light -- The CPC subclass B01D2259/802 covers processes that use visible light as a treatment method to separate substances, such as photochemical or photophysical techniques that induce phase or component separation. [B01D 2259/804] UV light -- The subclass B01D 2259/804 UV light refers to the use of ultraviolet radiation as a treatment step in separation processes, such as photochemical or photophysical methods that facilitate the isolation of components from mixtures. [B01D 2259/806] Microwaves -- B01D 2259/806 covers the use of microwave energy as a treatment method in separation processes, where dielectric heating is applied to facilitate the division of mixtures, removal of solvents or moisture, or selective extraction of components. It pertains to performing operations that employ microwaves to enhance or enable separation steps.
[B01D 2259/808] Laser -- B01D 2259/808 (Laser) is a CPC subgroup within PERFORMING OPERATIONS → SEPARATING → SEPARATION → Type of treatment. It covers the use of laser radiation to effect separation of materials, including laser cutting, ablation, laser-induced breakdown or other laser-driven sorting and removal processes.
[B01D 2259/81] X-rays -- The CPC subclass B01D 2259/81 "X-rays" refers to the use of X-ray radiation as a type of treatment within separation operations. [B01D 2259/812] Electrons -- The CPC subclass B01D 2259/812 pertains to the use of electrons as a treatment method for separating or modifying materials, such as by electron-beam irradiation to sterilise, cross-link, decompose or otherwise alter substances. This falls under Performing Operations → Separating → Separation → Type of treatment.
[B01D 2259/814] Magnetic fields -- B01D 2259/814 defines the use of magnetic fields as a separation technique, covering methods and apparatus that apply magnetic forces to separate particles, compounds or phases in a mixture. It falls under the CPC hierarchy of Performing operations → Separating → Separation → Type of treatment.
[B01D 2259/816] Sonic or ultrasonic vibration -- The CPC subclass B01D 2259/816 covers the use of sonic or ultrasonic vibration as a treatment method in separation operations. It includes processes where high-frequency sound waves are applied to facilitate the separation of mixtures, particles or phases in chemical or physical treatment.
[B01D 2259/818] Employing electrical discharges or the generation of a plasma -- The entry covers methods that utilize electrical discharges or plasma generation to effect separation of materials, typically by creating high-energy conditions that break down or detach components. Such techniques are applied in mineral processing, waste treatment and other processes where rapid, non-mechanical separation is required.
[B01D 2265/00] Casings, housings or mounting for filters specially adapted for separating dispersed particles from gases or vapours -- **Definition:** This place covers:  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Details for filters specially adapted for separating dispersed particles from liquids -> B01D2201/00
[B01D 2265/02] Non-permanent measures for connecting different parts of the filter -- A non-permanent measure for joining filter components describes temporary or detachable means-such as clamps, snap-fits, magnetic attachments or quick-release fasteners-used to connect the various parts of a casing or housing that separates dispersed particles from gases or vapours. This approach facilitates assembly, maintenance or replacement without permanent bonding.
[B01D 2265/021] Anti-rotational means -- Anti-rotational means are features such as tabs, keys, lugs or interlocking elements that keep filter casings, housings or mounts from turning when the filter separates dispersed particles from gases or vapours, ensuring stable positioning during operation.
[B01D 2265/022] Bayonet connecting means -- The CPC subclass B01D 2265/022 defines bayonet-type connecting means used to attach or secure filter casings, housings or mounts that are specially adapted for separating dispersed particles from gases or vapours. It covers the snap-fit, rotational locking mechanisms that permit quick, reliable installation and removal of filter elements.
[B01D 2265/023] making use of magnetic forces -- This subclass addresses filter casings, housings or mounts that employ magnetic forces to assist the separation of dispersed particles from gases or vapours, using magnets to attract, retain or guide solid matter within the filtration unit.
[B01D 2265/024] Mounting aids -- Mounting aids are components that support or attach filters used to separate dispersed particles from gases or vapours. They include casings, housings, brackets or other structures that hold the filter elements in place within a separation system.
[B01D 2265/025] making use of ramps or cams -- The CPC entry B01D 2265/025 refers to filter casings, housings or mounting structures that incorporate ramps or cams to direct the flow of gases or vapours and aid the separation of dispersed particles. These mechanical features create changes in direction or velocity that improve particle capture within the filter system.
[B01D 2265/026] with means for avoiding false mounting -- The CPC subclass B01D 2265/026 covers casings, housings or mounting structures for filters that are specifically designed to separate dispersed particles from gases or vapours, and that include features to prevent incorrect or false mounting of the filter unit.
[B01D 2265/027] Quick closing means for, e.g. filter heads, caps, maintenance openings -- This classification concerns devices that provide a rapid closure mechanism for filter housings such as filter heads, caps or maintenance openings, designed for casings that separate dispersed particles from gases or vapours. The quick-closing means facilitate easy access and secure sealing during operation or service.
[B01D 2265/028] Snap, latch or clip connecting means -- The CPC subclass B01D 2265/028 covers snap, latch or clip connecting means used in casings, housings or mountings for filters that are specially adapted to separate dispersed particles from gases or vapours. It falls under the hierarchy of Performing Operations → Separating → Separation. This code identifies the mechanical fasteners that secure filter components in industrial air-cleaning equipment.
[B01D 2265/029] Special screwing connections, threaded sections -- The CPC subclass B01D 2265/029 covers casings, housings or mounting parts for filters that are specially designed for separating dispersed particles from gases or vapours and feature special screwing connections or threaded sections. It classifies filter components where the attachment method is a distinctive threaded or screwed joint.
[B01D 2265/04] Permanent measures for connecting different parts of the filter, e.g. welding, glueing or moulding -- The entry covers permanent methods for joining separate parts of a filter-such as welding, gluing or moulding-used in casings, housings or mounts that separate dispersed particles from gases or vapours.
[B01D 2265/05] Special adapters for the connection of filters or parts of filters -- The CPC subclass B01D 2265/05 covers special adapters that join filters or filter components within casings, housings or mounts specially designed for separating dispersed particles from gases or vapours. These adapters facilitate the connection of filter elements in equipment used for particulate separation.
[B01D 2265/06] Details of supporting structures for filtering material, e.g. cores -- This subclass covers designs of internal supports such as cores that hold filtering media within filter housings used to separate solid particles from gas streams. It addresses geometries, materials, and mounting methods that keep the filter element in place.
[B01D 2267/00] Multiple filter elements specially adapted for separating dispersed particles from gases or vapours -- **Definition:** This place covers:  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Details for filters specially adapted for separating dispersed particles from liquids -> B01D2201/00
[B01D 2267/30] Same type of filters -- B01D 2267/30 pertains to devices that use several filter elements of the same type, specially designed to separate dispersed particles from gases or vapours. The entry falls under the hierarchy of performing operations → separating → separation and describes filter arrangements where uniform elements work together to achieve particulate removal.
[B01D 2267/40] Different types of filters -- The B01D 2267/40 class covers multiple filter elements that are specially designed to separate dispersed particles from gases or vapours, detailing various filter types, media and configurations used to achieve efficient particle removal. It falls under the broader categories of performing operations, separating and separation.
[B01D 2267/60] Vertical arrangement -- The B01D 2267/60 CPC subclass defines filter systems that use several filter elements arranged in a vertical stack to separate dispersed particles from gases or vapours. It focuses on the geometry of the filter assembly, where the flow path moves upward or downward through successive filtering stages. This vertical arrangement improves particle capture efficiency in air-cleaning and exhaust-treatment applications.
[B01D 2267/70] Horizontal arrangement -- This CPC subclass (B01D 2267/70) covers filters in which several filter elements are arranged horizontally to separate dispersed particles from gases or vapours. The configuration is specially adapted for multi-stage purification, focusing on the layout rather than the filter material itself.

[B01D 2271/02] Gaskets, sealings -- The CPC entry B01D 2271/02 covers gaskets and sealing elements that are specially designed for filters used to separate dispersed particles from gases or vapours. These sealings ensure a reliable barrier between filter media and the fluid stream, preventing leakage and maintaining filtration efficiency. It falls under the hierarchy of Performing Operations → Separating → Separation.
[B01D 2271/022] Axial sealings -- Axial sealings are components fitted along the length of a filter housing to prevent gas or vapour leakage around the filter element, ensuring that the flow passes through the filter media for effective particle separation. They are designed to match the axial geometry of the filter and maintain a tight seal under operating pressures.
[B01D 2271/025] Making of sealings -- This CPC subclass covers processes for producing sealing components used in filter assemblies that capture solid particles from gas or vapor streams. It includes forming, shaping, bonding, or attaching the seal material to ensure a tight fit and maintain filtration efficiency. The sealings are designed for filters that separate dispersed particles in gaseous media.
[B01D 2271/027] Radial sealings -- This CPC subclass covers radial sealing elements used in filter units designed to trap or separate dispersed particles from gases or vapours. The sealings are positioned radially to form a barrier that prevents bypass and improves filtration efficiency.

The bypass operates continuously during normal operation.

[B01D 2273/18] Testing of filters, filter elements, sealings -- This classification covers methods and procedures for evaluating the performance of filters, filter elements and sealing components used in systems that separate suspended particles from gases or vapours. [B01D 2273/20] High temperature filtration -- High-temperature filtration refers to the use of filters designed to operate at elevated temperatures while removing solid particles from hot gases or vapors. [B01D 2273/22] Making use of microwaves, e.g. for measurements -- This subclass (B01D 2273/22) covers processes that employ microwave energy in filter systems specifically adapted to separate dispersed particles from gases or vapours, often for diagnostic or measurement purposes. [B01D 2311/2603] Application of an electric field, different from the potential difference across the membrane -- The subclass B01D 2311/2603 concerns membrane separation operations in which an external electric field is applied that is separate from the voltage drop across the membrane, used to influence transport, fouling or cleaning. [B01D 2311/2638] Reduction -- The B01D 2311/2638 "Reduction" subclass addresses techniques for lowering a parameter within membrane-based separation operations, such as reducing pressure, concentration, fouling or energy consumption, and includes related control strategies for improving process efficiency.
[B01D 2311/2642] Aggregation, sedimentation, flocculation, precipitation or coagulation -- This CPC subclass covers membrane-based separation operations that use aggregation phenomena such as sedimentation, flocculation, precipitation or coagulation to aid or control the separation process. [B01D 2311/2643] Crystallisation -- The CPC subclass B01D 2311/2643 falls under Performing Operations → Separating → Separation and covers crystallisation steps that are part of membrane-based separation processes, including the operational parameters and control methods used to achieve solid-phase formation within or alongside membrane systems.
[B01D 2311/2646] Decantation -- The CPC entry B01D 2311/2646 covers decantation as a membrane-based separation technique, describing the operation and control of letting a liquid phase settle and be drawn off from a suspended solid or heavier fluid. [B01D 2311/2649] Filtration -- This subclass encompasses filtration techniques used in membrane-based separation operations, describing the methods and control strategies for separating substances through porous membranes.
[B01D 2311/2653] Degassing -- This class covers methods and equipment for removing dissolved gases from liquids in membrane-based separation processes. It includes techniques for degassing feed streams to improve membrane performance, control operating conditions, and prevent gas-related fouling or inefficiencies.
[B01D 2311/2657] Deaeration -- The B01D 2311/2657 deaeration entry covers membrane-based processes that remove dissolved gases from liquids, typically by creating a pressure differential or vacuum across a selective barrier. It includes operational steps, equipment design, and control strategies for achieving efficient gas removal in industrial separations. This falls under the broader categories of performing operations, separating, and separation.
[B01D 2311/2661] Addition of gas -- The subclass B01D 2311/2661 addresses methods and apparatus for introducing a gas into membrane-based separation processes, such as bubbling, pressurising or otherwise adding a gas stream to the feed or permeate side to influence permeation, mixing or process control. It covers the technical features of the gas-addition step within membrane operations.
[B01D 2311/2665] Aeration other than for cleaning purposes -- The code pertains to aeration steps used in membrane-separation processes for purposes other than cleaning, such as supplying oxygen, removing dissolved gases, or enhancing mass-transfer efficiency, together with the control of these aeration operations.
[B01D 2311/2669] Distillation -- The CPC code B01D 2311/2669 classifies distillation processes used to separate mixture components, emphasizing the operational steps and control techniques employed. It falls under the hierarchy of performing operations, separating, and separation, and is related to membrane-based separation technologies. This entry covers apparatus designs and methods that achieve component separation through vapor-liquid equilibrium and subsequent condensation.
[B01D 2311/2673] Evaporation -- The CPC entry B01D 2311/2673 concerns evaporation processes applied in membrane-based separation operations, describing how evaporation is used to remove solvent or concentrate streams and the associated control techniques. It falls under the hierarchy performing operations - separating - separation and is used in both EPO and USPTO classifications.
[B01D 2311/2674] Condensation -- B01D 2311/2674 covers condensation as it occurs in membrane-based separation processes, focusing on the operation and control of this step. It falls under the hierarchy of Performing Operations → Separating → Separation and pertains to the technical details of managing condensate within membrane systems.
[B01D 2311/2676] Centrifugal separation -- The CPC entry B01D 2311/2676 titled "Centrifugal separation" covers technologies that use centrifugal forces to separate components in membrane-based processes, including the equipment design, operating parameters and control strategies required to achieve efficient separation.
[B01D 2311/268] Water softening -- The B01D 2311/268 entry covers membrane-based water-softening operations, i.e., processes that use semi-permeable membranes to remove hardness-causing ions such as calcium and magnesium from water. It includes the design, operation and control of nanofiltration, reverse-osmosis or electrodialysis units for this purpose.
[B01D 2311/2684] Electrochemical processes -- B01D 2311/2684 covers electrochemical processes that are part of membrane separation operations, including the generation, use, or control of electric fields or currents to drive separation through membranes. It encompasses techniques such as electrodialysis, electro-filtration, and related process control methods.
[B01D 2311/2688] Biological processes -- The CPC subclass B01D 2311/2688, titled "Biological processes," pertains to membrane-based separation operations that employ biological mechanisms, covering the design, operation and control of such processes within the broader categories of performing operations, separating and separation.
[B01D 2311/2692] Sterilization -- The CPC entry B01D 2311/2692 covers sterilization methods that employ membrane separation processes, describing the operation and control of membranes to remove or inactivate microorganisms from liquids or gases. It falls under the broader categories of performing operations, separating, and specifically separation.
[B01D 2311/2696] Catalytic reactions -- The CPC subclass B01D 2311/2696 refers to catalytic reactions that are performed within membrane-based separation processes, covering the use of membranes as catalysts or catalyst supports and the control of reaction conditions in such systems. It falls under the broader hierarchy of performing operations, separating, and separation, specifically addressing membrane-assisted catalytic operations.
[B01D 2311/2697] Chromatography -- B01D 2311/2697 designates chromatography, a separation technique that passes a fluid mixture through a stationary phase to divide its constituents according to differing affinities. It falls under membrane separation operations and encompasses the equipment, procedures, and control strategies used to carry out chromatographic separations.
[B01D 2311/2698] Compression -- The entry B01D 2311/2698 covers the use of compression in membrane-based separation operations, focusing on how pressure is applied and regulated to drive fluids through selective membranes. It includes equipment, methods and control strategies for maintaining optimal compression conditions during the separation process.
[B01D 2311/2699] Drying -- B01D 2311/2699 - Drying refers to membrane-based separation operations where moisture is removed from the material being processed, including techniques and control strategies used to enhance drying efficiency. It addresses the design and operation of drying stages integrated with membrane systems for liquids or gases.
[B01D 2313/00] Details relating to membrane modules or apparatus -- The code B01D 2313/00 covers technical information about membrane modules and the apparatus that incorporates them, such as design features, module configuration, mounting arrangements, sealing methods and performance-enhancing elements. It is applied to inventions that improve how membranes are assembled, operated or maintained within separation processes.
[B01D 2313/02] Specific tightening or locking mechanisms -- The CPC entry B01D 2313/02 covers specific tightening or locking mechanisms used in membrane modules or apparatus, such as clamps, bolts, levers or other devices that secure the membranes in place during operation. It focuses on designs that provide a reliable seal and prevent module movement, ensuring consistent separation performance.
[B01D 2313/025] Specific membrane holders -- The code B01D 2313/025 designates inventions that provide specific holders for membrane modules or apparatus used in separation processes. It includes designs that secure, position, or support flat, tubular or spiral-wound membranes during filtration, ensuring proper flow and stability. The classification falls under performing operations - separating - separation.
[B01D 2313/04] Specific sealing means -- B01D 2313/04 covers the design and implementation of particular sealing devices used in membrane modules or related apparatuses that perform separation processes. The classification addresses features such as gaskets, O-rings, clamp systems or other means that ensure a leak-tight connection between membrane components and supporting structures.
[B01D 2313/041] Gaskets or O-rings -- B01D 2313/041 covers gaskets or O-rings used in membrane modules or related apparatus for separation processes. These components provide sealing between module parts to prevent fluid leakage during operation.

It falls under Performing Operations → Separating → Separation, specifically for membrane-module equipment.
[B01D 2313/08] Flow guidance means within the module or the apparatus -- B01D 2313/08 relates to flow-guidance means incorporated in membrane modules or separation apparatus, such as baffles, distributors or internal channels that direct the movement of fluids through the membrane. These features are intended to improve mass transfer, prevent fouling and increase the efficiency of the separating operation.
[B01D 2313/083] Bypass routes -- In the CPC, B01D 2313/083 designates bypass routes within membrane modules or apparatus used for separation operations. It covers features that provide alternative flow paths around or through the membrane element, enabling continued operation when fouling or blockage occurs. This falls under Performing Operations - Separating - Separation.
[B01D 2313/086] Meandering flow path over the membrane -- The code B01D 2313/086 covers membrane modules where the feed fluid is directed along a serpentine or meandering channel that passes over the membrane surface, enhancing contact and mass transfer. It is used in separation processes such as filtration, desalination, gas-liquid separations, and falls under the broader category of separating operations.
[B01D 2313/10] Specific supply elements -- The CPC subclass B01D 2313/10 covers specific supply elements used with membrane modules or apparatus, such as pipe fittings, manifolds, distributors, and other components that deliver feed, permeate or recirculating streams to the membrane system. These items are integral to the operation of separation processes that employ membranes.

It falls under the hierarchy of Performing Operations → Separating → Separation.

[B01D 2313/13] Specific connectors -- B01D 2313/13 covers specific connectors used in membrane modules or apparatus for separation processes. The code addresses design features that maintain sealing and mechanical integrity during operation.

[B01D 2313/14] Specific spacers -- The B01D 2313/14 subclass defines specific spacers employed within membrane modules or apparatus used for separation operations. [B01D 2313/143] on the feed side -- The CPC subclass B01D2313/143 covers aspects of membrane modules or apparatus that pertain to the feed side, including design, configuration, or treatment of the feed stream before it contacts the membrane. [B01D 2313/203] Open housings -- The B01D 2313/203 subclass refers to open-housing constructions for membrane modules and related separation equipment. It includes designs where the housing is not fully enclosed, facilitating access, cleaning or component replacement in separation processes.

[B01D 2313/22] Cooling or heating elements -- B01D 2313/22 defines cooling or heating elements incorporated in membrane modules or related apparatus for separation operations, covering temperature-control components that regulate feed, permeate or retentate streams to improve performance or stability of the separation process.

[B01D 2313/24] Specific pressurizing or depressurizing means -- The code B01D 2313/24 covers specific pressurising or depressurising devices applied to membrane modules or apparatus, such as pumps, compressors, valves or vacuum generators that create the pressure differential needed for separation. [B01D 2313/246] Energy recovery means -- The code B01D 2313/246 designates components that reclaim and reuse energy generated by pressure differences in membrane-based separation systems. It includes devices such as pressure exchangers, turbines or pumps that recover hydraulic or mechanical energy from the feed or permeate streams and feed it back into the process to improve overall efficiency.
[B01D 2313/26] Specific gas distributors or gas intakes -- B01D 2313/26 covers specific gas distributors or gas intake devices used in membrane modules or apparatus for separating gases or liquids. [B01D 2313/28] Specific concentration chambers -- B01D 2313/28 covers specific concentration chambers used in membrane modules or apparatus for separating substances. It pertains to separation operations and includes designs that concentrate feed-streams within the membrane system.

[B01D 2313/32] Intermediate chambers -- B01D 2313/32 covers intermediate chambers within membrane modules or apparatus used in separation operations, such as spacers or compartments that provide staged flow paths or support structures to enhance mass transfer. [B01D 2313/34] Energy carriers -- The CPC class B01D 2313/34 refers to membrane-based modules or apparatus that handle energy carriers such as hydrogen, oxygen, fuels or other chemical energy vectors. [B01D 2313/36] Energy sources -- This subclass pertains to the energy sources employed to operate membrane modules or apparatus used for separation processes, encompassing power supplies such as pressure, electricity, thermal or chemical energy that drive the membranes.
