[B01D 2313/367] Renewable energy sources, e.g. wind or solar sources -- The entry B01D 2313/367 in the Cooperative Patent Classification (CPC) by EPO/USPTO pertains to details relating to membrane modules or apparatus that utilize renewable energy sources, such as wind or solar power, for separation processes.
[B01D 2313/40] Adsorbents within the flow path -- This subclass covers membrane modules or separation apparatus that incorporate adsorbent material directly within the fluid flow path to improve removal of target components. It addresses designs where the adsorbent is positioned inside the channel or housing through which the feed passes, enhancing separation efficiency.
[B01D 2313/42] Catalysts within the flow path -- The subclass B01D 2313/42 covers membrane modules or apparatus in which catalytic material is placed inside the fluid flow path, allowing reactions to occur as the stream passes through the membrane during separation operations.
[B01D 2313/44] Cartridge types -- This CPC subclass B01D 2313/44 covers cartridge-type membrane modules or apparatus, describing designs where the membrane is housed in a replaceable or modular cartridge used for liquid or gas separation operations.
[B01D 2313/46] Supply, recovery or discharge mechanisms of washing members -- B01D 2313/46 concerns the mechanisms that supply, recover or discharge washing members used in membrane modules or apparatus for separation processes. It includes designs for delivering cleaning fluids, retrieving spent liquids, and removing waste from the washing elements during operation.
[B01D 2313/48] Mechanisms for switching between regular separation operations and washing -- B01D 2313/48 refers to mechanisms that enable a membrane separation system to alternate between normal filtration and a washing or cleaning mode, often by diverting flow, adjusting pressure, or changing valve positions. These devices are used in membrane modules to remove fouling and restore performance without dismantling the apparatus.
[B01D 2313/50] Specific extra tanks -- B01D 2313/50 covers the design and use of particular extra tanks incorporated into membrane separation equipment, such as vessels for holding feed, permeate or recirculating streams that are separate from the main membrane module. It pertains to apparatus where these additional tanks assist in process control, pressure regulation or product collection in membrane-based separation operations.
[B01D 2313/501] Permeate storage tanks -- B01D 2313/501 refers to permeate storage tanks, components of membrane separation systems that collect and hold the filtrate that passes through the membrane. These tanks are designed to receive, temporarily store, and possibly convey permeate for further processing or disposal, and are part of apparatus related to separation and membrane modules.
[B01D 2313/502] Concentrate storage tanks -- The B01D 2313/502 entry covers concentrate storage tanks used in membrane separation systems, describing tanks that receive and hold the concentrated retentate stream after the separation process. It includes design features for handling high pressure, chemical compatibility, and efficient transfer to downstream processes. This subclass falls under Performing Operations-Separating-Separation, focusing on apparatus for managing the concentrated phase.
[B01D 2313/54] Modularity of membrane module elements -- B01D 2313/54 pertains to the modular design of membrane module components used in separation processes, covering interchangeable or replaceable plates, cartridges, spacers and similar elements that can be assembled into larger membrane systems. It falls under the broader hierarchy of performing operations, separating and separation, focusing on apparatus that enables flexible membrane module construction.
[B01D 2313/56] Specific mechanisms for loading the membrane in a module -- The CPC subclass B01D 2313/56 covers particular ways of loading a membrane into a filtration or separation module, including mechanical, hydraulic or pneumatic techniques that position, insert or secure the membrane sheet or element inside the housing. It addresses apparatus features and operational steps that facilitate consistent placement, tensioning or sealing of the membrane during assembly or replacement.
[B01D 2313/57] Tools used for removal of membranes -- B01D 2313/57 refers to tools used for removing membranes in separation processes, especially those involving membrane modules or apparatus. The entry falls under the CPC hierarchy of Performing Operations - Separating - Separation. It covers equipment designed to detach or extract membrane components during cleaning, replacement, or maintenance.
[B01D 2313/58] Parts of membrane modules specifically adapted for single use -- This CPC subclass covers components of membrane modules such as membranes, housings or support structures that are designed for single-use operation and are discarded after one cycle, typically in filtration or separation processes. It includes disposable cartridges, single-use modules and related accessories intended to eliminate cleaning and prevent cross-contamination.
[B01D 2313/60] Specific sensors or sensor arrangements -- This CPC subclass covers specialized sensors or sensor arrangements incorporated into membrane modules or related apparatus used for separation operations. It includes configurations that monitor pressure, flow, concentration, fouling or other process parameters within the membrane unit. The code B01D2313/60 is applied to patents describing these detection and control elements.
[B01D 2313/62] Displays -- B01D 2313/62 pertains to display devices associated with membrane modules or apparatus used for separation operations, providing visual output of operating conditions, performance data, or control status. These displays may be integrated on the equipment or located remotely to facilitate monitoring and management of the separation process.
[B01D 2313/64] Bar codes -- B01D 2313/64 covers the use of bar-code markings on membrane modules or related apparatus to identify, track, or record data about the components during separation operations. It pertains to labeling methods that facilitate inventory control, performance monitoring, and maintenance of filtration or other membrane-based systems. This code is applied where bar-code technology is integrated into the design or documentation of separation equipment.
[B01D 2313/66] Biodegradability of parts of the module -- B01D 2313/66 deals with the biodegradability of components in membrane modules or related apparatus, such as housings, spacers or support structures. It is classified under Performing Operations → Separating → Separation, focusing on the environmental fate of module parts.
[B01D 2313/68] Biocompatibility of parts of the module -- The entry B01D 2313/68 covers the assessment and assurance of biocompatibility for components of membrane modules used in separation operations. It pertains to the suitability of all parts that come into contact with biological fluids or tissues, ensuring that the materials do not provoke adverse biological responses during the performing of separation processes.
[B01D 2313/70] Control means using a programmable logic controller [PLC] or a computer -- The CPC entry B01D 2313/70 falls under the separating-separation group for membrane modules or apparatus and specifies the use of control means implemented with a programmable logic controller or a computer to regulate the operation of the membrane system.
[B01D 2313/701] comprising a software program or a logic diagram -- The CPC subclass B01D 2313/701 relates to membrane modules or apparatus where the separation operation is governed or represented by a software program or a logic diagram, integrating digital or algorithmic control into the performing-operations hierarchy of separating.
[B01D 2313/702] comprising telecommunication features, e.g. modems or antennas -- B01D 2313/702 pertains to membrane modules or apparatus employed in separation operations that incorporate telecommunications elements such as modems or antennas, enabling remote monitoring, data communication or control of the separation process. The classification captures devices where the communication function is integral to the membrane system's performance.
[B01D 2313/90] Additional auxiliary systems integrated with the module or apparatus -- CPC B01D 2313/90 pertains to auxiliary systems that are incorporated into or directly associated with a membrane module or apparatus used for separation. It includes integrated equipment such as feed pretreatment, recirculation, temperature regulation, cleaning or monitoring devices that support the membrane's operation.
[B01D 2313/901] Integrated prefilter -- B01D 2313/901 covers integrated prefilters that are built into membrane modules or separation apparatus, combining a preliminary filtration stage with the main membrane unit to remove coarse particles and protect the membrane.
[B01D 2313/902] Integrated cleaning device -- The B01D 2313/902 entry covers integrated cleaning devices designed for membrane modules or related apparatus, enabling in-situ removal of fouling or deposits during separation processes. Such devices combine cleaning mechanisms with the membrane unit to improve performance and reduce downtime.
[B01D 2313/903] Integrated control or detection device -- B01D 2313/903 covers integrated control or detection devices incorporated in membrane modules or apparatus. These are sensors, controllers or monitoring systems that directly measure or regulate parameters such as pressure, flow, concentration, fouling or integrity of the membrane during operation. The classification applies to any built-in or tightly coupled electronic or mechanical unit used to oversee membrane performance.
[B01D 2315/00] Details relating to the membrane module operation -- The CPC subclass B01D 2315/00 concerns technical information on operating membrane modules, including parameters such as flow rate, pressure, temperature, cleaning procedures, module configuration and performance monitoring within separation processes.
[B01D 2315/02] Rotation or turning -- The subclass B01D 2315/02 covers membrane modules that employ rotation or turning of the module during operation to improve separation performance, such as rotating hollow-fiber cartridges or spinning spiral-wound elements to reduce fouling and maintain flux. It includes any mechanical turning action applied to the membrane unit while it is separating substances.
[B01D 2315/04] Reciprocation, oscillation or vibration -- B01D 2315/04 covers the use of reciprocal, oscillatory or vibratory motion in membrane modules to enhance separation processes. It pertains to operations where the membrane module is driven back-and-forth, shaken or vibrated to reduce fouling or improve mass transfer. This falls under the broader categories of performing operations, separating and separation.
[B01D 2315/05] Moving the membrane in one direction, e.g. displacement, translational movement -- This subclass covers membrane modules where the membrane is shifted linearly, such as by displacement or translational motion, during operation. It relates to separation processes that require controlled movement of the membrane to enhance flow, mass transfer, or cleaning within the module.
[B01D 2315/06] Submerged-type -- The B01D 2315/06 subclass covers submerged-type membrane modules used in separation processes. It includes designs where the membrane is placed below the liquid surface, such as submerged membrane bioreactors and filtration units, focusing on the operation and configuration of the module.
[B01D 2315/08] Fully permeating type -- The B01D 2315/08 class covers membrane modules that operate in a fully permeating mode, where the feed stream passes completely through the membrane and the permeate is collected on the opposite side. It applies to separation processes that rely on selective transport through the membrane rather than partial or surface-only flow. This subclass falls under the hierarchy of Performing Operations → Separating → Separation.
[B01D 2315/10] Cross-flow filtration -- Cross-flow filtration refers to a membrane separation technique where feed flows tangentially across the membrane surface, allowing permeate to pass while retaining particles in the bulk stream. It is used in continuous processing of liquids such as water treatment, dairy and biopharmaceutical streams, and enables efficient cleaning and reduced fouling.
[B01D 2315/12] Feed-and-bleed systems -- Feed-and-bleed systems refer to membrane filtration arrangements in which a portion of the feed is periodically withdrawn and then re-introduced to the module to control fouling, maintain pressure, and improve separation efficiency. These systems are used in processes such as reverse osmosis and ultrafiltration to manage concentration polarization and extend membrane life.
[B01D 2315/14] Batch-systems -- The code B01D 2315/14 refers to batch-type membrane processes in which a fixed volume of feed is treated in a closed or semi-closed unit, allowing the membrane module to operate without continuous feed. It covers the operation, control and performance aspects of such batch systems, including loading, processing and unloading steps, and is classified under performing operations, separating, separation.
[B01D 2315/16] Diafiltration -- Diafiltration is a membrane-based separation operation in which a solvent (often water) is added to a feed stream to continuously wash out low-molecular-weight components while retaining larger molecules on the membrane surface. The method is used in biotechnology, pharmaceutical and food processing to purify proteins, remove salts, and achieve precise concentration adjustments.
[B01D 2315/17] Depth filtration, asymmetric membranes arranged with wider pore size side towards feed -- This CPC subclass defines depth-filtration processes that employ asymmetric membranes in which the side with larger pores faces the feed stream. It covers the operation of membrane modules that separate particles or contaminants by directing flow through a graded pore structure, enhancing retention while maintaining flux.
[B01D 2315/18] Time sequence of one or more process steps carried out periodically within one apparatus -- B01D 2315/18 covers the timing arrangement of one or more process steps that are performed periodically in a single membrane-module apparatus, such as cyclic filtration, back-washing, or cleaning cycles. The code is used to classify technologies that manage the sequence and duration of operations within the same equipment.
[B01D 2315/20] Operation control schemes defined by a periodically repeated sequence comprising filtration cycles combined with cleaning or gas supply, e.g. aeration -- The entry defines control strategies for membrane-module processes that use a cyclic sequence of filtration steps alternated with cleaning actions or gas-supply operations such as aeration. It covers the timing, duration and coordination of these repeated cycles to sustain flux, limit fouling and optimise overall separation performance.
[B01D 2315/22] Membrane contactor -- Membrane contactors are devices that employ a porous membrane to enable mass transfer between two fluid streams while keeping them physically separated, supporting separation operations such as gas absorption, stripping, or liquid-liquid extraction. The entry covers the design, materials, flow arrangement and operating conditions of the membrane module that carries out these separating functions.
[B01D 2315/24] Counter-current operation -- Counter-current operation describes a membrane module configuration in which the feed and permeate streams flow in opposite directions, creating a concentration gradient that improves mass-transfer efficiency and product recovery. This arrangement is commonly employed in reverse-osmosis, ultrafiltration and related separation processes to enhance separation performance and reduce fouling.
[B01D 2317/00] Membrane module arrangements within a plant or an apparatus -- The CPC subclass B01D 2317/00 covers the design and layout of membrane modules as they are integrated into larger plants or equipment for separation processes. It includes arrangements of flat-sheet, tubular or spiral-wound modules within the apparatus, addressing flow routing, module stacking, and connections that facilitate the selective passage of gases, liquids or solutes.
[B01D 2317/02] Elements in series -- The B01D 2317/02 entry covers configurations in which membrane modules are arranged in series within a plant or apparatus, so that the effluent of one module becomes the feed for the next. This series connection enhances separation performance by providing multiple purification stages in a single system.
[B01D 2317/022] Reject series -- B01D 2317/022 designates the reject series of membrane modules arranged in a plant or apparatus, covering configurations where permeate is collected and the remaining feed (reject) is directed onward. It falls under performing operations - separating - separation.
[B01D 2317/025] Permeate series -- This subclass deals with plant or apparatus designs where membrane modules are linked so that the permeate exiting one module is directed into the next, forming a series flow path. The arrangement is used to boost product recovery, improve overall efficiency, or attain specific concentration targets.
[B01D 2317/027] Christmas tree arrangements -- Christmas tree arrangements refer to the configuration of membrane modules and associated piping in a separation plant where multiple inlet and outlet streams are converged or distributed using a central manifold that branches outward like a tree, facilitating efficient flow distribution and collection within the system.
[B01D 2317/04] Elements in parallel -- The subgroup B01D 2317/04 concerns components of membrane modules that are installed side-by-side so they operate in parallel within a separation plant or apparatus, such as parallel channels, sheets, or stacked elements that share the same feed and provide simultaneous filtration.
[B01D 2317/06] Use of membrane modules of the same kind -- This subclass relates to the configuration or operation of membrane modules of the same-type within a separation plant or apparatus, covering how identical modules are employed together to perform the separation process.
[B01D 2317/08] Use of membrane modules of different kinds -- It covers processes where a system incorporates multiple membrane modules that differ in type, material or configuration within the same separation plant. The classification applies to arrangements that combine heterogeneous membrane units to achieve a desired separation performance.
[B01D 2319/00] Membrane assemblies within one housing -- B01D 2319/00 concerns membrane assemblies that are integrated into a single housing. It includes arrangements where two or more membrane modules are combined in one unit to perform separation processes such as filtration, dialysis, or gas separation. The classification is used for technologies that package multiple membranes together for compact operation.
[B01D 2319/02] Elements in series -- The CPC subclass B01D 2319/02 "Elements in series" covers membrane separation devices in which two or more membrane elements are arranged consecutively within a single housing, allowing staged filtration or concentration in one enclosure. It relates to series-linked membrane modules used for performing separation operations.
[B01D 2319/022] Reject series -- The B01D 2319/022 reject-series entry covers membrane assemblies that are incorporated into a single housing for separation operations, such as filtration or purification. It pertains to designs where multiple membrane modules are combined in one unit to streamline installation, enhance performance, or allow staged removal of unwanted components.
[B01D 2319/025] Permeate series -- B01D 2319/025 in the Cooperative Patent Classification refers to membrane assemblies within a single housing where permeate flows in series, indicating a sequential flow through multiple membranes for enhanced separation efficiency.
[B01D 2319/027] Christmas tree arrangements -- A "Christmas tree" arrangement is a configuration in which several membrane modules are stacked inside a single housing, each module positioned at a different level like the branches of a tree. The design enables sequential or parallel separation stages within one unit, improving space efficiency and simplifying fluid distribution for gas or liquid membrane processes. It falls under membrane assemblies within a single housing in the CPC hierarchy.
[B01D 2319/04] Elements in parallel -- The code B01D 2319/04 covers components that are arranged side-by-side inside a single membrane housing, such as multiple membrane sheets, spacers or support structures positioned in parallel to one another. This configuration increases the effective membrane surface area and flow capacity within the same unit, facilitating higher flux or reaction rates in separation, filtration, or electro-chemical processes.
[B01D 2319/06] Use of membranes of different materials or properties within one module -- B01D 2319/06 pertains to membrane separation units that house multiple membranes made of different materials or possessing distinct properties within a single module. The arrangement allows combined or sequential separation steps in one housing, improving efficiency or selectivity.
[B01D 2321/00] Details relating to membrane cleaning, regeneration, sterilization or to the prevention of fouling -- B01D 2321/00 covers methods and apparatuses for cleaning, regenerating, sterilizing or preventing fouling of membranes used in separation processes. It falls under Performing Operations - Separating - Separation in the CPC system.
[B01D 2321/02] Forward flushing -- Forward flushing is a cleaning method for filtration membranes where fluid is introduced in the direction of normal flow to remove retained particles, dissolve foulants, or restore performance. It is used to regenerate, sterilise or prevent fouling of membrane surfaces during operation. This technique falls under the CPC subclass B01D 2321/02 for membrane cleaning and regeneration.
[B01D 2321/04] Backflushing -- Backflushing is the reversal of fluid flow through a membrane to dislodge and remove accumulated contaminants, thereby restoring performance and preventing fouling. It is used as a cleaning or regeneration step in separation processes such as filtration or dialysis.
[B01D 2321/06] Use of osmotic pressure, e.g. direct osmosis -- This subclass relates to membrane‐based operations where osmotic pressure, for example by direct osmosis, is applied to clean, regenerate, sterilise or otherwise prevent fouling of the membrane surfaces.
[B01D 2321/08] Use of hot water or water vapor -- This subclass concerns methods that use hot water or water vapor to clean, regenerate, sterilise or prevent fouling of membranes employed in separation processes such as filtration, dialysis or desalination, falling under the broader categories of performing operations, separating and separation.
[B01D 2321/10] Use of feed -- B01D 2321/10 relates to the use of the feed stream in membrane processes for cleaning, regeneration, sterilisation or fouling-prevention of the membrane, describing how the feed itself can be employed to maintain membrane performance.
[B01D 2321/12] Use of permeate -- The entry B01D 2321/12 concerns the use of permeate-the purified stream that passes through a membrane - in downstream applications such as water reuse, concentration, feed for further reactions or processes, and in strategies for membrane cleaning, regeneration, sterilization or fouling prevention.
[B01D 2321/14] Use of concentrate -- B01D 2321/14 covers the use of a concentrate, such as a highly-loaded cleaning or regeneration solution, applied to membranes to remove fouling, sterilize, or restore performance, exploiting the higher active-ingredient content to achieve effective treatment while reducing the volume of fluid required.
[B01D 2321/16] Use of chemical agents -- The CPC class B01D 2321/16 covers the use of chemical agents to clean, regenerate, sterilize or prevent fouling of membranes used in separation processes. It is situated within the hierarchy performing operations - separating - separation.
[B01D 2321/162] Use of acids -- The CPC subclass B01D 2321/162 covers the use of acids in membrane cleaning, regeneration, sterilization or in preventing fouling during separation processes. It falls under the hierarchy of performing operations, separating, and separation. This entry pertains to acid-based treatments applied to membranes to maintain or restore their performance.
[B01D 2321/164] Use of bases -- The classification B01D 2321/164 covers the use of alkaline substances or bases in membrane cleaning, regeneration, sterilization or other methods that prevent fouling during separation operations.
[B01D 2321/166] Use of enzymatic agents -- This CPC sub-group (B01D 2321/166) concerns the application of enzymatic agents to clean, regenerate, sterilise or prevent fouling of membranes used in separation processes. It falls under the hierarchy Performing Operations → Separating → Separation.
[B01D 2321/167] Use of scale inhibitors -- The CPC entry B01D 2321/167 covers the use of scale-inhibiting agents in membrane-based processes, aiming to prevent mineral deposition that can foul or block the membrane. It pertains to methods of cleaning, regenerating or sterilizing membranes by adding inhibitors that limit scale formation and thus improve performance and longevity.
[B01D 2321/168] Use of other chemical agents -- This CPC subclass covers the use of various chemical agents for cleaning, regeneration, sterilizing membranes or preventing fouling in separation processes. It falls under B01D 2321/168 within the performing operations-separating-separation hierarchy.
[B01D 2321/18] Use of gases -- B01D 2321/18 covers the use of gases to clean, regenerate, sterilise or otherwise prevent fouling of separation membranes, as part of membrane-based separation processes. It falls under the broader categories of performing operations, separating and separation.
[B01D 2321/185] Aeration -- Aeration involves introducing gas bubbles into a membrane system to assist cleaning, regeneration, sterilization or to curb fouling. The bubbles create shear forces and improve mass transfer, helping to detach deposits and maintain membrane performance.
[B01D 2321/20] By influencing the flow -- The entry covers methods that improve membrane cleaning, regeneration, sterilization or fouling prevention by modifying the flow of fluid through or across the membrane. It includes techniques that adjust pressure, velocity, turbulence or flow direction to enhance the removal of deposits or to maintain membrane performance.
[B01D 2321/2008] statically -- The B01D 2321/2008 subclass pertains to static techniques for membrane cleaning, regeneration, sterilisation or fouling prevention applied without mechanical agitation or flow. It includes methods that rely on chemical, thermal or other non-dynamic actions to maintain membrane performance. This entry falls under the hierarchy of Performing Operations → Separating → Separation.
[B01D 2321/2016] Static mixers -- Static mixers are devices that generate turbulent flow and promote thorough blending of fluids within a pipe or channel, commonly employed in membrane cleaning, regeneration, sterilization or fouling-prevention processes. By continuously mixing cleaning agents with the feed-stream, they increase contact with membrane surfaces and improve the effectiveness of fouling control.
[B01D 2321/2025] Tangential inlet -- The B01D 2321/2025 entry titled Tangential inlet covers membrane separation devices in which the feed stream is introduced parallel to the membrane surface, creating a cross-flow that helps minimise fouling and facilitates cleaning, regeneration or sterilisation. This arrangement is used in processes such as ultrafiltration, nanofiltration and reverse osmosis to improve performance and prolong membrane life.
[B01D 2321/2033] dynamically -- B01D 2321/2033 in the Cooperative Patent Classification refers to methods for dynamically preventing fouling during membrane separation processes, which can involve continuous or periodic actions to maintain membrane performance and efficiency.
[B01D 2321/2041] Mixers -- Mixers applied in membrane-related processes for cleaning, regeneration, sterilization or fouling prevention, facilitating the separation operations by ensuring uniform contact between cleaning agents and the membrane surface.
[B01D 2321/205] Integrated pumps -- Integrated pumps are combined pump assemblies incorporated into membrane separation units to facilitate cleaning, regeneration, sterilization or fouling control while performing the separation operation. The classification groups such pump-membrane systems under B01D 2321/205 within the performing-operations-separating hierarchy.
[B01D 2321/2058] by vibration of the membrane, e.g. with an actuator -- This CPC entry covers methods for cleaning, regenerating, sterilising or preventing fouling of membranes by applying vibration, such as using an actuator, to the membrane surface. It falls under separating-separation operations.
[B01D 2321/2066] Pulsated flow -- The B01D 2321/2066 subclass covers the use of pulsated flow - periodic variations in speed or direction of a liquid stream - to improve membrane cleaning, regeneration, sterilization, or fouling prevention. Pulsation can be induced by mechanical or hydraulic means to enhance shear forces and disrupt deposits on membrane surfaces.
[B01D 2321/2075] Ultrasonic treatment -- B01D 2321/2075 relates to applying ultrasonic energy to membranes in separation processes, using high-frequency sound waves to clean, regenerate, sterilize or prevent fouling of the membrane surface, thereby maintaining or improving filtration performance.
[B01D 2321/2083] By reversing the flow -- The subclass B01D 2321/2083 covers membrane cleaning, regeneration, sterilization or fouling-prevention methods that achieve the effect by reversing the flow direction across the membrane.
[B01D 2321/22] Electrical effects -- This subclass covers the use of electrical phenomena to clean, regenerate, sterilise or prevent fouling of filtration membranes, including methods that apply electric fields, currents, voltages or electro-chemical reactions to remove deposits, deactivate microorganisms or restore membrane performance.
[B01D 2321/223] Polarity reversal -- Polarity reversal is a membrane-cleaning technique that switches the direction of an applied electric field, causing accumulated ions or particles to detach and be flushed from the membrane. The process restores performance and helps prevent fouling in separation operations such as electrodialysis or reverse osmosis.
[B01D 2321/226] Interruption of electric currents -- This subclass covers methods or devices that interrupt electric currents to facilitate membrane cleaning, regeneration, sterilization, or fouling-prevention during separation processes. It is part of the broader hierarchy of performing operations, separating, and separation.
[B01D 2321/24] Magnetic effects -- B01D 2321/24 - Magnetic effects covers the use of magnetic fields or magnetic particles to clean, regenerate, sterilise or prevent fouling of separation membranes, improving their performance in filtration or other separation operations.
[B01D 2321/26] By suction -- The CPC sub-group B01D 2321/26 covers processes that employ suction to clean, regenerate or sterilize membranes and to prevent fouling. It falls under the hierarchy performing operations - separating - separation and describes the removal of deposits by creating a pressure differential that draws contaminants away from the membrane.
[B01D 2321/28] by soaking or impregnating -- The entry B01D 2321/28 covers procedures that clean, regenerate, sterilise or protect membranes from fouling by soaking or impregnating them with a suitable medium. These methods fall under the Performing Operations → Separating → Separation group in the CPC system.
[B01D 2321/281] by applying a special coating to the membrane or to any module element -- The entry covers methods that protect membranes or their module components from fouling by applying a special coating, which also enables cleaning, regeneration or sterilisation of the membrane during separation processes. It falls under the CPC group for separation operations focused on membrane maintenance.
[B01D 2321/282] by spray flush or jet flush -- This subgroup covers methods for cleaning, regenerating or sterilising filtration membranes by directing a spray or jet of fluid to flush the membrane surface, thereby removing or preventing fouling. It falls under separation processes that address membrane maintenance and fouling control.
[B01D 2321/30] Mechanical cleaning, e.g. with brushes or scrapers -- This CPC subclass covers mechanical cleaning of membranes-using brushes, scrapers or similar tools-to remove fouling, regenerate performance or sterilize surfaces during separation operations.
[B01D 2321/32] By heating or pyrolysis -- This group covers processes for cleaning, regenerating, sterilising or fouling-prevention of membranes that employ heat or pyrolysis. It falls under separation operations, specifically membrane cleaning and regeneration, and is coded B01D 2321/32 in the CPC.
[B01D 2321/34] by radiation -- The B01D 2321/34 entry classifies the use of radiation techniques for cleaning, regenerating, sterilising or preventing fouling of membranes employed in separation processes. It falls under the broader categories of Performing Operations, Separating and Separation.
[B01D 2321/343] By UV radiation -- The B01D 2321/343 subclass covers membrane cleaning, regeneration, sterilisation or fouling-prevention methods that employ ultraviolet radiation. It addresses the use of UV light to degrade contaminants, restore permeability and maintain membrane performance.
[B01D 2321/346] by gamma radiation -- The CPC subclass B01D 2321/346 covers the use of gamma radiation to clean, regenerate, sterilise or otherwise prevent fouling of separation membranes. It falls under the hierarchy of Performing Operations → Separating → Separation. This entry details how high-energy photons are applied to maintain membrane performance and prolong service life.
[B01D 2321/35] with acoustic waves -- The code B01D 2321/35 designates processes for cleaning, regenerating, sterilising or fouling-prevention of filtration membranes that employ acoustic wave energy. It resides within the broader hierarchy of performing operations, separating and separation.
[B01D 2321/40] Automatic control of cleaning processes -- This subclass covers systems that automatically regulate membrane cleaning, regeneration or sterilization to prevent fouling, using sensors, controllers and feedback loops to adjust chemicals, flow rates or timing. It applies to separation processes where maintaining membrane performance is essential.
[B01D 2321/42] Chemical regeneration -- This subclass covers methods that use chemical agents to restore the performance of filtration membranes by removing fouling, cleaning, regenerating or sterilising them, enabling continued separation operations. It includes processes that dissolve or chemically alter contaminants on the membrane surface to recover permeability.
[B01D 2321/44] Specific cleaning apparatus -- This subclass covers apparatus specifically engineered to clean, regenerate or sterilise membrane-based separation units and to prevent fouling. It includes devices such as cleaning jets, circulation systems or automated cleaning modules used in membrane processes.
[B01D 2323/00] Details relating to membrane preparation -- The CPC subclass B01D 2323/00 covers technical information on preparing membranes used in separation operations. It includes methods, materials and steps for forming, treating or modifying membranes that facilitate filtration, pervaporation, or other membrane-based separations. This subclass falls under the hierarchy of Performing Operations → Separating → Separation.
[B01D 2323/02] Hydrophilization -- The code B01D 2323/02 covers processes for making membrane surfaces more water-attracting, i.e., hydrophilizing them, as part of membrane preparation for separation operations. It falls under the broader classes of performing operations, separating, and separation.
[B01D 2323/04] Hydrophobization -- This subclass covers methods for rendering separation membranes water-repellent, including the application of hydrophobic coatings, surface treatments or chemical modifications that increase a membrane's resistance to wetting. It is used in processes where maintaining non-wetting characteristics improves separation performance.
[B01D 2323/06] Specific viscosities of materials involved -- B01D 2323/06 pertains to the determination or specification of the specific viscosity of materials employed in the preparation of separation membranes, including polymer solutions, solvents or additives, used in processes classified under performing operations and separating.
[B01D 2323/08] Specific temperatures applied -- The CPC subclass B01D 2323/08 concerns separation processes in which particular temperature conditions are applied during membrane preparation, covering heating, cooling or temperature-controlled steps that affect membrane formation, structure or performance. [B01D 2323/082] Cooling -- B01D 2323/082 addresses cooling operations performed during the preparation of separation membranes, including temperature control while forming, solidifying or treating the membrane material. [B01D 2323/10] Specific pressure applied -- The code B01D 2323/10 pertains to membrane-based separation processes in which a defined pressure is applied to drive the flow of fluid through a membrane. It covers operations such as reverse osmosis, nanofiltration and ultrafiltration where specific pressures are set to achieve the desired separation.
[B01D 2323/12] Specific ratios of components used -- B01D 2323/12 refers to the part of the CPC that classifies the definition of precise component ratios in the preparation of separation membranes, including polymer blends, additives or solvents used to achieve specific membrane properties. [B01D 2323/14] Ageing features -- The subclass B01D 2323/14 addresses the ageing characteristics of membranes employed in separation processes, covering alterations in performance, structure or durability over time and methods for assessing or mitigating these effects.
[B01D 2323/15] Use of additives -- The CPC subclass B01D 2323/15 covers the incorporation of additives during the preparation of membranes used for separation processes. [B01D 2323/16] Swelling agents -- The B01D 2323/16 subclass designates swelling agents used in membrane preparation, comprising chemicals that expand or soften the membrane structure to enhance its permeability and selectivity during separation operations.

It applies to membrane-preparation steps in separation processes where pore structure is critical.
[B01D 2323/20] Plasticizers -- B01D 2323/20 refers to the incorporation of plasticizers in the preparation of membranes used for separation processes. Plasticizers are added to polymeric membranes to improve flexibility, permeability, and mechanical stability during operations such as filtration or gas separation.
[B01D 2323/21] Fillers -- B01D 2323/21 designates the incorporation of filler materials into membranes to enhance separation processes, covering the selection, addition and distribution of particulate, polymeric or inorganic fillers that modify permeability, selectivity or mechanical properties during membrane fabrication.
[B01D 2323/216] Surfactants -- Surfactants employed in the preparation or treatment of separation membranes, helping to modify surface properties, improve membrane formation, or assist in cleaning and performance during separation processes.
[B01D 2323/217] Emulgator or emulsion/foam forming agents -- B01D 2323/217 covers emulsifiers and agents that create emulsions or foams, specifically for use in the preparation of membranes within separation processes.
