[B01D 2323/225] Use of supercritical fluids -- The CPC subclass B01D 2323/225 covers the use of supercritical fluids in the preparation of membranes for separation processes. It falls under the hierarchy of Performing Operations - Separating - Separation - Details relating to membrane preparation.
[B01D 2323/226] Use of ionic liquids -- The CPC subclass B01D 2323/226 concerns the application of ionic liquids in the preparation of separation membranes. It falls under the hierarchy of performing operations, separating, and separation, specifically addressing how ionic liquids are employed to enhance membrane fabrication and performance.
[B01D 2323/24] Use of template or surface directing agents [SDA] -- The CPC subclass B01D 2323/24 covers methods for preparing membranes in which a template or surface-directing agent (SDA) is employed to control pore formation or surface structure. It includes the use of organic or inorganic templates, surfactants, polymers or other directing molecules that guide the arrangement of the membrane material during synthesis. This subclass falls under Performing Operations → Separating → Separation → Details relating to membrane preparation.
[B01D 2323/26] Spraying processes -- This CPC subclass pertains to spraying methods employed in the preparation of separation membranes, including the atomisation of liquids to form or coat porous layers on a substrate. It covers processes that apply coating materials or other membrane-forming substances by spray techniques to achieve desired separation properties.
[B01D 2323/28] Pore treatments -- B01D 2323/28 refers to pore treatments in membrane preparation, focusing on processes that modify, fill or functionalise the pores of filtration or separation membranes to adjust selectivity, flux or durability. It includes chemical, physical or biological modifications performed before or after membrane formation.
[B01D 2323/283] Reducing the pores -- The subclass B01D 2323/283 concerns methods for reducing the pore size of membranes during their preparation. It includes techniques such as pore sealing, coating or chemical modification that decrease membrane permeability to achieve finer separation performance. This entry falls under performing operations, separating, and membrane-preparation details.
[B01D 2323/286] Closing of pores, e.g. for membrane sealing -- B01D 2323/286 refers to methods for sealing or closing pores in a membrane, often to achieve a desired selectivity or barrier property during membrane preparation. The entry falls under the hierarchy of Performing Operations → Separating → Separation → Details relating to membrane preparation, and it covers techniques that block or fill membrane pores to create a sealed structure.
[B01D 2323/30] Cross-linking -- B01D 2323/30 covers cross-linking techniques applied to membranes used in separation operations. It includes chemical or physical methods that link polymer chains to enhance membrane strength, selectivity and chemical resistance during filtration or other separation processes.
[B01D 2323/32] Use of chain transfer agents or inhibitors -- This CPC subclass B01D 2323/32 covers the use of chain transfer agents or inhibitors in membrane preparation processes, situated under the separation branch of performing operations. It relates to methods that modify polymerization during membrane fabrication to control polymer molecular weight and resulting membrane performance.
[B01D 2323/34] Use of radiation -- Code B01D 2323/34 in the CPC falls under performing operations, separating, separation, details relating to membrane preparation, and covers the use of radiation to prepare or modify membranes. This includes applying ionizing or non-ionizing radiation to create pores, cross-link polymeric membranes or alter surface properties for separation processes.
[B01D 2323/345] UV-treatment -- The classification B01D 2323/345 pertains to the use of ultraviolet radiation in membrane preparation. It covers UV-treatment methods that modify, clean, or sterilize membranes during separation processes, including surface activation and removal of contaminants to improve performance.
[B01D 2323/35] Use of magnetic or electrical fields -- The CPC subclass B01D 2323/35 falls under Performing Operations → Separating → Separation and concerns the use of magnetic or electrical fields during membrane preparation. It addresses techniques that apply such fields to influence membrane formation, structure or performance in separation processes.
[B01D 2323/36] Introduction of specific chemical groups -- This subclass covers methods for introducing designated functional groups onto membranes during their membrane-preparation, enabling tailored separation properties. It includes chemical grafting, coating, or covalent attachment techniques applied to polymeric or inorganic membranes to modify selectivity or permeability.
[B01D 2323/38] Graft polymerization -- B01D 2323/38 covers graft polymerization techniques used to modify or create membranes for separation processes. It includes methods of attaching polymer chains onto a pre-formed membrane substrate to tailor permeability or selectivity.
[B01D 2323/385] involving radiation -- This CPC subclass B01D 2323/385 pertains to separation processes where membranes are prepared or modified by radiation, encompassing methods such as radiation-induced polymerisation, cross-linking, grafting or degradation of membrane materials to enhance filtration performance. It falls under Performing Operations → Separating → Separation → Details relating to membrane preparation.
[B01D 2323/39] Electrospinning -- Electrospinning is a membrane-forming technique that uses an electrically charged polymer solution or melt to produce fine fibers, which are collected as a non-woven mat. In the Cooperative Patent Classification it falls under PERFORMING OPERATIONS → SEPARATING → SEPARATION, covering methods for preparing separation membranes. This entry (B01D 2323/39) specifically addresses the use of electrospinning for creating such membrane structures.
[B01D 2323/40] in-situ membrane formation -- This subclass covers processes that create a selective membrane directly within the separation unit, rather than inserting a pre-made film. It includes in-situ polymerisation, precipitation or coating methods that generate a functional barrier at the point of use, facilitating separation operations.
[B01D 2323/42] Details of membrane preparation apparatus -- The CPC subclass B01D 2323/42 defines details of membrane preparation apparatus, covering equipment and configurations used to form, treat, dry, and support membranes for separation operations. It addresses casting methods, surface modification, support structures, and control mechanisms that affect membrane performance.
[B01D 2323/44] Relaxation steps -- B01D 2323/44 covers relaxation steps applied during membrane preparation for separation processes, where the membrane is allowed to rest or equilibrate after formation or treatment to reduce internal stresses and improve selectivity. Such steps may involve holding the membrane at specific temperatures, pressures or chemical environments before use.
[B01D 2323/46] Impregnation -- The CPC subclass B01D 2323/46 - Impregnation - covers processes in which a liquid, solution, suspension or melt is introduced into the pores or structure of a membrane or support material during its preparation, often to deposit catalysts, binders or functional layers that enhance separation performance. This operation is a specific step within the broader category of separating operations and membrane-related separation technologies.
[B01D 2323/48] Influencing the pH -- B01D 2323/48 covers techniques for modifying or controlling the pH during the preparation of membranes, such as adding acids, bases, buffers or pH-adjusting steps. Adjusting pH influences membrane structure, charge and performance in separation processes.
[B01D 2323/50] Control of the membrane preparation process -- The CPC subclass B01D 2323/50 covers methods and systems for controlling the preparation of membranes used in separation processes. It includes monitoring and adjusting formulation, casting, curing, and other parameters to ensure consistent membrane properties and performance.
[B01D 2323/52] Use of a mould -- B01D 2323/52 covers the use of a mould in preparing membranes for separation operations, detailing mould design, casting and shaping steps that form the membrane structure used in filtration or other separating processes.
[B01D 2323/54] Recycling of scrap material from production process or membrane fragments -- The class covers methods for recovering and re-using waste generated during membrane fabrication, including scrap material from the production process and broken membrane fragments, with a focus on recycling steps incorporated in membrane preparation.
[B01D 2323/56] Use of ultrasound -- B01D 2323/56 covers the application of ultrasound techniques in the preparation of separation membranes, such as cleaning, pore formation or surface modification, to improve filtration performance.
[B01D 2323/58] Fusion -- B01D 2323/58 Fusion refers to processes that join or combine membrane materials to create a continuous or composite separation barrier. The code covers techniques such as thermal, solvent or pressure-induced bonding used in the manufacture or modification of membranes for filtration, dialysis or other separation applications.
[B01D 2323/60] Co-casting -- The CPC subclass B01D 2323/60, titled Co-casting, relates to membrane preparation methods in which two or more layers of material are simultaneously cast to form a composite membrane. It is placed under Performing Operations - Separating - Separation.
[B01D 2323/62] Cutting the membrane -- B01D 2323/62 pertains to the cutting of a membrane as part of its preparation, typically within separation processes. It includes methods and equipment for shaping or segmenting membranes before or after they are formed.
[B01D 2323/64] Use of a temporary support -- The CPC entry B01D 2323/64 covers processes that employ a temporary support during membrane preparation to aid handling, shaping or mounting of the membrane, after which the support is removed. It pertains to separation operations where the support is used only during fabrication and not as part of the final filtration device.
[B01D 2323/66] Avoiding penetration into pores of support of further porous layer with fluid or counter-pressure -- The code covers membrane-preparation methods that stop a fluid or counter-pressure from entering the pores of a support when a further porous layer is applied, thereby preventing infiltration of the underlying structure. It relates to techniques for controlling flow or pressure during the fabrication of multilayer separation membranes.
[B01D 2325/00] Details relating to properties of membranes -- The CPC entry B01D 2325/00 covers information on the characteristics of membranes employed in separation operations, including their permeability, selectivity, mechanical strength, chemical resistance and other functional properties that affect performance in processes such as filtration, dialysis or gas separation. This sub-category falls under Performing Operations → Separating → Separation.
[B01D 2325/02] Details relating to pores or porosity of the membranes -- B01D 2325/02 concerns the characterization of membrane pores and porosity, describing pore size, distribution, and related structural features that influence separation performance. It falls under the hierarchy of performing operations - separating - separation - details relating to properties of membranes.
[B01D 2325/021] Pore shapes -- B01D 2325/021 covers the specific shapes of pores in membranes that are employed in separation operations. It relates to how pore geometry-such as cylindrical, conical, or irregular forms-affects filtration, selectivity, and flow characteristics in processes that separate liquids or gases.
[B01D 2325/0212] Symmetric or isoporous membranes -- The code B01D 2325/0212 classifies symmetric or isoporous membranes, which have uniform pore size and structure across their thickness, enabling consistent separation performance. It covers the design, fabrication, and properties of such membranes used in processes that separate substances based on size, charge, or affinity.
[B01D 2325/0214] Tapered pores -- The CPC class B01D 2325/0214 pertains to membranes whose pores gradually change diameter along their length, known as tapered pores. It falls under Performing Operations → Separating → Separation → Details relating to properties of membranes, and covers designs and applications of such tapered-pore membranes in filtration and related separation processes.
[B01D 2325/022] Asymmetric membranes -- The CPC subclass B01D 2325/022 covers asymmetric membranes, concentrating on their structural and functional properties in separation processes. It includes details such as layer composition, thickness gradients, permeability and selectivity characteristics.
[B01D 2325/023] Dense layer within the membrane -- B01D 2325/023 designates the dense, non-porous layer of a membrane and its material characteristics that control selective separation performance. It covers aspects such as thickness, composition and structural properties of this layer as they affect the membrane's ability to separate components.
[B01D 2325/0231] Dense layers being placed on the outer side of the cross-section -- The subclass B01D 2325/0231 covers membrane designs in which a dense selective layer is applied to the outer side of the membrane's cross-section, affecting its transport and separation properties. It pertains to separation operations that rely on the specific placement of dense layers on the membrane surface.
[B01D 2325/0232] Dense layer on both outer sides of the membrane -- The subclass B01D 2325/0232 covers membranes that feature a solid, dense layer applied to both outer surfaces, affecting the membrane's mechanical strength, selectivity and barrier performance. It is relevant to separation technologies where dual-sided dense coatings are used to enhance durability or control flux.
[B01D 2325/0233] with clearly distinguishable layers -- The CPC entry B01D 2325/0233 covers membrane-based separation techniques in which the membrane is constructed from clearly distinguishable layers, each layer having specific properties that affect the separation performance. It pertains to the detailed description of these layered membrane characteristics within the broader category of separating operations.
[B01D 2325/025] Finger pores -- The subclass B01D 2325/025 covers "finger pores," which are finger-shaped microscopic channels incorporated into membranes to improve separation performance. It pertains to the design and properties of such pores in filtration or other membrane-based separation processes.
[B01D 2325/026] Sponge structure -- B01D 2325/026 - Sponge structure refers to porous, sponge-like membrane configurations employed in separation operations, where the material's pore geometry and mechanical properties are tailored to enhance filtration or selective transport. The subclass addresses design and characterization of such membranes within the broader category of performing operations, separating, separation.
[B01D 2325/027] Nonporous membranes -- The CPC subclass B01D 2325/027 covers nonporous membranes and the characteristics that affect their performance in separation processes, such as thickness, selectivity, permeability, mechanical strength and chemical stability. It falls under the hierarchy of performing operations, separating, separation and details relating to membrane properties.
[B01D 2325/028] Microfluidic pore structures -- B01D 2325/028 covers microfluidic pore structures used in membrane-based separation processes. It classifies devices that incorporate precisely engineered pores at the microscale to control fluid flow, enhance selectivity, and improve performance in filtration, dialysis, or analytical applications. The entry falls under the hierarchy of Performing Operations → Separating → Separation, focusing on the properties and design of such membrane pores.
[B01D 2325/0281] Fibril, or microfibril structures -- B01D 2325/0281 concerns separation methods that employ membranes built from fibrils or microfibril structures, describing their composition, morphology and how these fine fibers influence filtration or selective transport properties.
[B01D 2325/0282] Dynamic pores-stimuli responsive membranes, e.g. thermoresponsive or pH-responsive -- This CPC subclass concerns membranes whose pores can open, close or change size in response to external stimuli such as temperature or pH, allowing controlled separation performance. It includes dynamic or smart membranes that reversibly alter permeability through reversible structural changes.
[B01D 2325/0283] Pore size -- The CPC subclass B01D 2325/0283, titled "Pore size," falls under Performing Operations → Separating → Separation → Details relating to properties of membranes. It covers the definition, measurement, control and distribution of pores in membrane materials, and the influence of pore size on filtration, sieving and other separation processes.
[B01D 2325/02831] less than 1 nm -- The CPC code B01D 2325/02831 covers separation methods that rely on membranes whose pore diameters are smaller than one nanometre, focusing on the characterization and performance of such ultra-fine membrane structures. It falls under the hierarchy of Performing Operations - Separating - Separation - Details relating to properties of membranes.
[B01D 2325/02832] 1-10 nm -- The entry B01D 2325/02832 covers separation methods that rely on membrane characteristics, specifically those with pore dimensions ranging from 1 to 10 nanometres. It falls under the broader categories of performing operations, separating, and separation, and focuses on how membrane properties influence the filtration or selective transport of particles at the nanoscale.
[B01D 2325/02833] more than 10 and up to 100 nm -- The CPC entry B01D 2325/02833 relates to separation processes that employ membranes whose pore diameter is greater than 10 nm and up to 100 nm, covering the properties and performance of such micro-filtration or ultrafiltration membranes. It falls under the hierarchy of Performing Operations - Separating - Separation - Details relating to properties of membranes.
[B01D 2325/02834] more than 0.1 and up to 1 µm -- B01D 2325/02834 designates membrane-based separation methods that employ membranes whose pore size ranges from more than 0.1 to up to 1 µm, focusing on the physical and functional properties of such membranes. It is situated under the performing operations hierarchy, specifically within separating, separation, and details relating to membrane properties. This classification is used by the EPO and USPTO for patents involving fine-scale filtration or sieving processes.
[B01D 2325/04] Characteristic thickness -- The subclass covers specifications, measurement methods and relevance of membrane thickness as a key property in separation operations. It addresses how characteristic thickness influences performance, durability and selectivity of membranes used in processes such as filtration, pervaporation or gas separation.
[B01D 2325/06] Surface irregularities -- This subclass addresses membrane surface irregularities that influence separation performance, covering texture, roughness or defects and their effect on filtration, pervaporation or related processes. It is used for patents describing design, analysis or modification of membrane surfaces to enhance separating operations.
[B01D 2325/08] Patterned membranes -- This subclass deals with membranes that have deliberately created patterns or surface structures to modify their separation behavior. It includes designs where micro- or nano-scale patterns are used to enhance selectivity, flux, or resistance to fouling in filtration and other separation processes.
[B01D 2325/10] Catalysts being present on the surface of the membrane or in the pores -- B01D 2325/10 covers membrane-based separation processes where the membrane's surface or its pores are functionalised with catalytic substances. The catalysts facilitate or accelerate chemical reactions directly at the membrane, enhancing selectivity or reaction rates during filtration, gas-liquid contact, or other separation operations.
[B01D 2325/12] Adsorbents being present on the surface of the membranes or in the pores -- B01D 2325/12 covers membranes whose performance is enhanced by adsorbent materials located on the membrane surface or within its pores. The classification is used for technologies that combine separation functions with surface-or pore-bound adsorbents to improve selectivity or capacity.
[B01D 2325/14] Membrane materials having negatively charged functional groups -- B01D 2325/14 is a CPC subclass that covers membrane materials incorporating negatively charged functional groups, used to influence separation performance. These membranes are designed for processes where charge repulsion or attraction enhances selective transport of ions or molecules.
[B01D 2325/16] Membrane materials having positively charged functional groups -- The subclass B01D 2325/16 denotes membrane materials that incorporate positively charged (cationic) functional groups. These membranes are designed for separation processes where the charge enhances attraction of anionic species or repulsion of cations, improving selectivity and performance in filtration, ion exchange, or purification applications. It falls under the CPC hierarchy of Performing Operations → Separating → Separation, focusing on membrane property specifications.
[B01D 2325/18] Membrane materials having mixed charged functional groups -- B01D 2325/18 covers membrane materials that contain both cationic and anionic functional groups in the same polymer structure. The mixed charge sites enhance ion selectivity, conductivity and stability, making the membranes suitable for applications such as ion exchange, electrodialysis and other separation processes.
[B01D 2325/20] Specific permeability or cut-off range -- The CPC entry B01D 2325/20 falls under PERFORMING OPERATIONS → SEPARATING → SEPARATION and concerns the specific permeability or cut-off range of membranes. It addresses how membrane properties determine the size or type of substances that can pass through, defining performance limits for selective separation processes. This classification is used to categorize patents describing membrane selectivity characteristics.
[B01D 2325/22] Thermal or heat-resistance properties -- The entry B01D 2325/22 covers membranes whose thermal or heat-resistance characteristics are specified, including how they maintain separation performance when exposed to elevated temperatures or heat-induced stress. It is part of the CPC hierarchy under Performing Operations → Separating → Separation → Details relating to properties of membranes.
[B01D 2325/24] Mechanical properties, e.g. strength -- The B01D 2325/24 subclass addresses the mechanical characteristics of membranes employed in separation operations, such as strength, elasticity, toughness and durability. It includes methods for testing, measuring and evaluating these properties to ensure the membrane can withstand operational stresses. This information supports the design and selection of membranes for reliable separating processes.
[B01D 2325/26] Electrical properties -- B01D 2325/26 classifies membrane-based separation processes in which the electrical behavior of the membrane is a key factor, covering properties such as conductivity, permittivity, charge transport and related measurements that influence separation performance.
[B01D 2325/28] Degradation or stability over time -- B01D 2325/28 relates to the assessment of membrane durability, describing how membrane materials degrade or retain their performance over time due to chemical, thermal, mechanical or fouling effects. It is used for patents that test, improve or predict membrane stability in separation processes.
[B01D 2325/30] Chemical resistance -- The CPC subclass B01D 2325/30 defines the chemical resistance of membranes employed in separating processes. It concerns how membranes withstand exposure to various chemicals, affecting durability, selectivity and performance in industrial and laboratory separations.
[B01D 2325/32] Melting point or glass-transition temperatures -- The CPC class B01D 2325/32 deals with the melting point or glass-transition temperature of membranes used in separation processes. It falls under the hierarchy of performing operations, separating, separation and details relating to membrane properties.
[B01D 2325/34] Molecular weight or degree of polymerisation -- The CPC entry B01D 2325/34 falls under Performing Operations → Separating → Separation and covers membranes whose characteristics are expressed by molecular weight or degree of polymerisation, describing how these polymer properties affect separation performance.
[B01D 2325/341] At least two polymers of same structure but different molecular weight -- The subclass B01D 2325/341 covers membrane separation techniques that use a combination of two or more polymers having the same chemical structure but different molecular weights. By blending polymers of varying chain lengths, the resulting membrane exhibits tailored porosity or selectivity for effective separation of fluids or particles. This entry pertains to the design, manufacture and application of such heterogeneous polymer membranes.
[B01D 2325/36] Hydrophilic membranes -- This CPC subgroup covers hydrophilic membranes used in separation processes, focusing on their properties such as water affinity, permeability, and material characteristics. It falls under B01D 2325/36 within the separation category of performing operations.
[B01D 2325/38] Hydrophobic membranes -- The CPC subclass B01D 2325/38 classifies hydrophobic membranes, detailing their material properties and behavior as they are used in separation operations, especially within processes that rely on membrane selectivity.
[B01D 2325/39] Amphiphilic membranes -- Amphiphilic membranes are specially designed barrier materials that combine water-attracting (hydrophilic) and water-repelling (hydrophobic) regions, enabling selective transport of substances based on polarity or size. In separation technologies they are used to achieve efficient filtration, dialysis or solvent extraction while maintaining stability and controllable permeability.
[B01D 2325/40] Fibre reinforced membranes -- The CPC subclass B01D 2325/40 pertains to fibre-reinforced membranes employed in separation operations. It covers the design, composition and performance characteristics of such membranes, including strength, durability and permeability. This subclass falls under the broader categories of performing operations, separating and separation, focusing specifically on membrane properties.
[B01D 2325/42] Ion-exchange membranes -- B01D 2325/42 pertains to ion-exchange membranes employed in separation operations, emphasizing the membranes' material characteristics, structure and functional properties. The class covers membranes that selectively transport ions to enable processes such as electrodialysis, electrolytic separations and other ion-based purification technologies.
[B01D 2325/43] Specific optical properties -- The CPC subclass B01D 2325/43 covers membrane-based separation technologies where the focus is on the membrane's optical characteristics. It includes inventions that define, measure or exploit properties such as light transmission, reflectance, refractive index or color to monitor, control or improve the separation process.
[B01D 2325/44] Specific light transmission -- The subclass B01D 2325/44 deals with the specific transmission of light through membrane materials, covering methods for measuring, controlling or enhancing optical clarity, transmittance and spectral characteristics of membranes used in separation processes. It includes apparatuses and procedures that assess how membranes permit or filter light across different wavelengths.
[B01D 2325/46] Magnetic properties -- The CPC subclass B01D 2325/46, titled "Magnetic properties," concerns the magnetic characteristics of membranes employed in separation processes. It includes features such as magnetic permeability, susceptibility and the response of membrane materials to magnetic fields, relevant to patents on membrane-based separating operations.
[B01D 2325/48] Antimicrobial properties -- B01D 2325/48 refers to membranes employed in separation processes that exhibit antimicrobial activity, meaning they inhibit or kill microorganisms on or through the membrane surface. This subclass addresses the functional properties of such membranes in applications like water treatment, food processing, and medical filtration, where preventing microbial contamination is essential.
[B01D 2325/50] Membrane in gel form -- The subclass B01D 2325/50 covers gel-form membranes employed in separation processes, emphasizing their material properties, structure and performance characteristics. It pertains to the use and design of gel-based membranes for filtering, purifying or extracting substances in chemical or biotechnological operations.
[B01D 2325/52] Crystallinity -- The B01D 2325/52 subclass addresses the crystallinity of separation membranes, covering the degree of crystallinity, crystal structure, and methods for measuring or modifying it, as these characteristics influence membrane performance in separating substances.
[B01D 2325/54] Interpenetration relationship between layers in supported or composite membranes -- The CPC subclass B01D 2325/54 covers the interpenetration relationship between layers in supported or composite membranes, describing how one membrane layer penetrates or intertwines with another within a multilayer structure. It pertains to the physical properties and interactions of membrane layers that affect separation performance.
[B01D 2325/56] Biodegradability of membrane -- This CPC subclass addresses the biodegradability of membrane materials employed in separation processes, covering how membranes decompose biologically and the resulting effects on performance. It is located in the hierarchy performing operations - separating - separation and is coded B01D 2325/56.
[B01D 2325/58] Biocompatibility of membrane -- This subclass concerns the assessment of how a membrane interacts with biological media, including tests of toxicity, fouling and compatibility with cells or tissues, to ensure safe use in separation processes such as filtration, dialysis or medical implants.
[B01D 24/00] Filtration -- **Definition:** This place covers:  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Cartridge filters of the throw-away type with cartridges made from a mass of loose material -> B01D27/02
[B01D 24/001] Making filter elements not provided for elsewhere -- B01D 24/001 covers processes for producing filter elements that are not otherwise specifically classified. It includes the manufacture of filter media, housings or assemblies used in filtration systems where the specific type of filter is not covered by a more detailed subclass. The entry is situated in the hierarchy under Performing Operations - Separating - Separation - Filtration.
[B01D 24/002] with multiple filtering elements in parallel connection -- This subclass concerns filtration processes that use several filter elements arranged in parallel, allowing the feed stream to be divided among them simultaneously, which can increase throughput or extend the service life compared to a single filter.
[B01D 24/004] arranged concentrically or coaxially -- The B01D 24/004 subclass covers filtration arrangements in which the filtering media are positioned concentrically or coaxially, such as a tube within a tube or a series of nested cylinders. This design creates a flow path that passes radially or axially through successive layers, enhancing separation efficiency while maintaining a compact form. It falls under the broader categories of performing operations, separating, separation and filtration.
[B01D 24/005] Filters being divided into a plurality of cells or compartments  B01D24/004  takes precedence -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Arranged concentrically or coaxially -> B01D24/004
[B01D 24/007] with multiple filtering elements in series connection -- This subclass covers filtration processes that employ two or more filter elements arranged in series, so the material to be cleaned passes sequentially through each element, enhancing removal efficiency or allowing staged separation.
[B01D 24/008] arranged concentrically or coaxially -- In this subclass, filtration units are designed with the filter medium arranged concentrically or coaxially around a central-element such as a pipe, tube, or rotor, so that the fluid passes radially inward or outward through the filter.
[B01D 24/02] with the filter bed stationary during the filtration -- B01D 24/02 covers filtration processes where the filter medium remains fixed in place while the fluid to be cleaned passes through it. This sub-category applies to systems such as sand or cartridge filters where the filter bed does not move during operation.
[B01D 24/04] the filtering material being clamped between pervious fixed walls -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - The filtering material being held in a closed container -> B01D24/10 - The filtering material being held in an open container -> B01D24/20
[B01D 24/042] the filtering material being held in a flexible porous bag -- This subclass covers filtration processes in which the filtering medium is contained within a flexible porous bag, allowing the bag to act as both support and filter. It falls under the hierarchy of Performing Operations → Separating → Separation → Filtration, coded B01D 24/042.
[B01D 24/045] with at least one flat vertical wall -- B01D 24/045 covers filtration arrangements that incorporate at least one flat vertical wall, such as filter housings or chambers where the separating medium is bounded by a vertical planar surface. The classification is used for devices that rely on this geometry to improve flow distribution and solid-liquid separation within the broader performing-operations, separating, filtration subgroup.
[B01D 24/047] with vertical tubes distributing the liquid to be filtered or for collecting filtrate -- This subclass covers filtration apparatus that use vertical tubes to spread the liquid over the filter medium or to direct the filtrate away, improving distribution and collection. The tubes are arranged upright within the filter housing, serving either as distributors for incoming fluid or as collectors for the filtered stream.
[B01D 24/06] the pervious walls comprising a series of louvres or slots -- This CPC subclass covers filtration structures that use a pervious wall built from a series of louvers or slots to let fluid pass while retaining solid particles. It applies to screens, separators and similar devices where the open-work wall provides the primary filtering action.
[B01D 24/08] the filtering material being supported by at least two pervious coaxial walls -- B01D 24/08 relates to filtration arrangements in which the filter medium is mounted between at least two permeable coaxial walls, the walls forming concentric tubes that allow fluid to pass through both the walls and the media. The configuration provides structural support and uniform flow distribution, improving durability and efficiency of the filter.
[B01D 24/10] the filtering material being held in a closed container -- This subclass covers apparatus or processes where the filter medium is enclosed within a sealed housing, so filtration occurs without exposing the filter to the external environment. It includes designs where the filter cake or media remains inside a closed container during operation, often for safety, contamination control, or recovery of filtered substances.
[B01D 24/105] downward filtration without specifications about the filter material supporting means -- The B01D 24/105 subclass covers downward filtration processes in which a fluid passes through a filter medium, without any requirement to define the filter material or the supporting structure. It is part of the broader filtration group under separating operations.
[B01D 24/12] Downward filtration, the filtering material being supported by pervious surfaces -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Combined upward and downward filtration -> B01D24/18
[B01D 24/14] Downward filtration, the container having distribution or collection headers or pervious conduits -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Combined upward and downward filtration -> B01D24/18
[B01D 24/16] Upward filtration -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Combined upward and downward filtration -> B01D24/18
[B01D 24/165] the filtering material being supported by pervious surfaces -- This CPC subclass (B01D 24/165) relates to filtration arrangements in which the filter material is mounted on a pervious or porous support that allows fluid to pass while retaining particles. The support provides mechanical strength and flow channels, and the design is used in industrial, water-treatment and similar filtration systems.
[B01D 24/167] the container having distribution or collection headers or pervious conduits -- The CPC entry B01D 24/167 refers to filtration containers that incorporate distribution or collection headers or pervious conduits, enabling uniform flow of liquid or gas through the filter medium. It covers designs where such headers or permeable channels are integral to the container, improving media loading, back-washing, or collection of filtered streams.
[B01D 24/18] Combined upward and downward filtration -- B01D 24/18 covers filtration systems that employ both upward and downward flow of the fluid through a filter medium at the same time. The combined flow arrangement enhances particle capture and reduces clogging by allowing contaminants to be removed from both directions, improving overall efficiency and throughput. This approach is used in processes where continuous, high-rate filtration is required.
[B01D 24/183] the filtering material being supported by pervious surfaces -- The subclass B01D 24/183 covers filtration arrangements in which the filtering material is mounted on a pervious, open-structure support such as mesh, screen or foam. The support itself allows fluid to pass through while the filter media retains particles, differing from designs that use solid backing. This category is used for devices where the support's permeability is integral to the filtration function.
[B01D 24/186] the container having distribution or collection headers or pervious conduits -- A filtration apparatus in which the filter container incorporates distribution or collection headers, or pervious conduits, to guide fluid uniformly across the filter medium or to gather filtered fluid, facilitating efficient separation within the overall performing-operations hierarchy.
[B01D 24/20] the filtering material being provided in an open container -- The CPC subclass B01D 24/20 covers filtration processes in which the filtering material-such as filter media, bags, or cartridges-is placed in an open container rather than a sealed housing. It pertains to designs where the filter element is exposed to the feed stream within a vessel or tank.
[B01D 24/205] Downward filtration without specifications about the filter material supporting means -- The CPC subclass B01D 24/205 covers downward filtration operations where a fluid passes through a filter medium under gravity or pressure, without specifying any particular filter material or supporting structure. It falls under the hierarchy of Performing Operations - Separating - Separation - Filtration.
[B01D 24/22] Downward filtration, the filter material being supported by pervious surfaces -- This subclass covers filtration processes in which the fluid moves downward through a filter medium that is placed on a pervious (porous) support such as a mesh, plate or other open structure, allowing the filter material and the support to both permit flow. It is used for separating solids from liquids or gases by gravity-driven passage through the supported filter media.
[B01D 24/24] Downward filtration, the container having distribution or collection headers or pervious conduits -- The entry covers devices that perform downward filtration in a vessel equipped with internal distribution or collection headers, or permeable conduits, allowing the fluid to flow through the filter medium from above while the filtrate is collected or fed through these pervious passages.
[B01D 24/26] Upward filtration -- Upward filtration refers to a separation operation in which a fluid or suspension is passed upward through a filter medium, allowing the filtrate to flow against gravity while solids are retained on or within the filter. This method is used to improve cake formation, reduce channeling and enhance separation efficiency in processes such as water treatment, food processing and chemical filtration.
[B01D 24/263] the filtering material being supported by pervious surfaces -- B01D 24/263 covers filters in which the porous filtering medium is mounted on a pervious support such as a mesh, screen or porous plate, allowing fluid to pass through both the support and the filter material. This arrangement improves mechanical stability and distributes the flow, and is used in liquid and gas purification, water treatment and industrial filtration processes.
[B01D 24/266] the container having distribution or collection headers or pervious conduits -- This CPC subclass B01D 24/266 pertains to filtration equipment where the container incorporates distribution or collection headers or pervious conduits. It covers designs in which the container itself integrates channels that guide fluid to or from the filter medium, facilitating uniform flow and collection of filtered liquid or gas.
[B01D 24/28] with the filter bed moving during the filtration -- B01D 24/28 covers filtration processes in which the filter medium itself is in motion while the liquid or gas passes through it, such as rotating drums, moving belts, or fluidized beds that continuously shift to enhance particle capture and prevent clogging.
[B01D 24/30] Translation -- The CPC entry B01D 24/30 relates to filtration processes in which the filter medium, filter cake, or the material being filtered is moved relative to another part of the apparatus, i.e., translation of the filter element during or after filtration. It covers mechanisms that shift, slide or transport the filter to enable continuous operation, cleaning or cake removal within the broader class of separating operations.
[B01D 24/305] Vibrations -- The B01D 24/305 entry classifies filtration techniques that employ vibrations to improve the separation of solids from liquids or gases. It covers devices such as vibratory screens, shakers, and other equipment where periodic motion assists particle movement, prevents clogging, or enhances filter efficiency. This subclass falls under the broader categories of separating, separation and filtration in the CPC system.
[B01D 24/32] Rotation -- Techniques that employ a rotating filter element to separate solids from liquids, such as rotary drum or rotary vacuum filters, wherein the motion enhances the filtration surface and aids removal of the retained material. This includes devices that continuously turn the filter medium to improve efficiency and allow continuous operation.
[B01D 24/34] with the filtering material and its pervious support moving -- This subclass covers filtration operations in which the filtering material and its permeable support are moved during the process, such as belt, drum or rotating-disk filters where the filter medium and the backing travel relative to the fluid or each other.
[B01D 24/36] with the filter bed fluidised during the filtration -- This subclass covers filtration processes in which the filter-bed is kept in a fluidised state during operation, allowing solid particles to be suspended and improving flow and cleaning. The fluidisation may be achieved by upward gas or liquid flow, vibration or other means, and is applied to continuous or batch filtration.
[B01D 24/38] Feed or discharge devices -- B01D 24/38 classifies apparatus for introducing the feed material into a filtration unit or for removing the filtered product from it. It includes devices such as inlet distributors, feed channels, outlet ports and discharge mechanisms that control the flow of liquids or slurries during the separation process.
[B01D 24/383] using multiple way valves -- This subclass covers filtration operations that use multiple-way valves to direct fluid streams, enabling the selection or diversion of feed, concentrate, permeate or wash streams during a separation process. It includes designs where the valve can be switched without halting the filtration cycle.
[B01D 24/386] internal recirculation -- B01D 24/386 pertains to filtration processes involving internal recirculation, where a portion of the filtrate or retentate is cycled back into the system to enhance separation efficiency. This method is used in various industrial applications for improving the purity and yield of the filtered products.
[B01D 24/40] for feeding -- B01D 24/40 covers filtration apparatus features that introduce material to be filtered, specifically the feeding mechanisms or arrangements that supply feed to the filter medium. It is situated under the CPC hierarchy performing operations - separating - separation - filtration.
[B01D 24/402] containing fixed liquid displacement elements or cores -- B01D 24/402 covers filtration apparatus in which the filtering medium is combined with fixed liquid displacement elements or cores that remain stationary during operation, such as porous plates, meshes, or structured inserts that direct flow and enhance separation.
[B01D 24/405] Special treatment of the feed stream before contacting the filtering material, e.g. cutting  B01D35/24 ,  B01D37/02 ,  B01D37/03  take precedence -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Providing loose granular material to scratch the filters clean -> B01D35/24 - Precoating the filter medium; Addition of filter aids to the liquid being filtered -> B01D37/02 - Using flocculating agents -> B01D37/03
[B01D 24/407] provoking a tangential stream -- B01D 24/407 covers filtration methods that create a tangential or cross-flow along the filter surface to enhance particle removal, reduce cake buildup and maintain higher flux. By provoking a side-stream parallel to the membrane or filter medium, the process improves separation efficiency in liquid or gas filtration operations.
[B01D 24/42] for discharging filtrate -- The B01D 24/42 subclass covers apparatus or techniques used to remove or discharge filtrate from a filter during a filtration operation, typically involving a channel, valve or pump that allows the liquid phase to exit the filter medium while retaining solids. It falls under the broader categories of Performing Operations, Separating, Separation and Filtration.
[B01D 24/425] containing fixed liquid displacement elements or cores -- B01D 24/425 covers filtration methods that use fixed liquid-displacement elements or cores incorporated into the filter medium. These devices retain a stationary structure that forces the fluid to flow around or through it, enhancing separation efficiency while preventing the element itself from moving during operation. The code lies under the CPC hierarchy for performing operations, separating and filtration.
[B01D 24/44] for discharging filter cake, e.g. chutes -- This CPC subclass B01D 24/44 falls under Performing Operations - Separating - Separation - Filtration and covers apparatus or methods for removing the accumulated filter cake from a filter, typically by means of chutes or similar discharge structures.
[B01D 24/46] Regenerating the filtering material in the filter -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - For discharging filter cake -> B01D24/44
[B01D 24/4605] by scrapers, brushes, nozzles or the like placed on the cake-side of the stationary filtering material and only contacting the external layer  B01D24/4631  takes precedence -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Counter-current flushing -> B01D24/4631
[B01D 24/461] by scrapers -- The B01D 24/461 subgroup covers filtration techniques in which the filter medium is cleaned or cleared by means of mechanical scrapers that remove the accumulated solid particles from the surface. This method belongs to the broader class of separating operations, specifically filtration, and is used where continuous or periodic scraping improves flow and extends filter life.
[B01D 24/4615] by brushes -- B01D 24/4615 relates to filtration techniques that use brush-like structures to separate solid particles from liquids or gases. The subclass covers devices or methods where rotating or stationary brushes create a filter media that captures contaminants while allowing the fluid to pass through. It falls under the broader hierarchy of performing operations, separating, and separation by brushes.
[B01D 24/4621] by nozzles acting on the cake side of the filter material, or by fluids acting in co-current direction with the feed stream -- B01D 24/4621 covers filtration methods that clean the filter cake by directing jets or streams of fluid onto the cake side of the filter medium, using nozzles or fluids that flow in the same direction as the incoming feed. This co-current action helps dislodge and remove accumulated solids without reversing the flow.
[B01D 24/4626] Construction of spray heads specially adapted for regeneration of the filter material or for filtrate discharging -- B01D 24/4626 relates to filtration apparatus and specifically to the construction of spray heads that are designed to regenerate filter material or to discharge filtrate. The subclass includes spray head designs with features such as adjustable nozzles, back-wash capability or directed flow paths that facilitate cleaning of the filter media or removal of the filtered liquid from the unit.
[B01D 24/4631] Counter-current flushing, e.g. by air -- A counter-current flushing method for filtration involves introducing a cleaning medium, such as air, that flows opposite to the direction of the filtrate stream to dislodge and remove retained particles from the filter medium. This reverse-flow technique enhances cleaning efficiency and extends filter life.
[B01D 24/4636] with backwash shoes; with nozzles -- The subclass B01D 24/4636 relates to filtration devices that employ backwash shoes together with nozzles to direct fluid flow for cleaning the filter media. It falls under the hierarchy of performing operations → separating → separation → filtration and specifies arrangements where backwash shoes and nozzle assemblies are combined to facilitate efficient backwashing.
[B01D 24/4642] with valves, e.g. rotating valves -- B01D 24/4642 designates filtration devices that incorporate valves, for example rotating valves, to regulate the passage of material through the filter media. These valves may be used to start, stop, or reverse flow during the separation process.
[B01D 24/4647] with a rectilinear movement of the closing means -- The subclass B01D 24/4647 covers filtration devices in which the mechanism that opens or closes the flow path moves in a straight-line (rectilinear) direction, such as a sliding valve or shutter that seals the filter. It applies to apparatuses that separate solids from liquids or gases by filtration where the closing means is actuated by linear motion.
[B01D 24/4652] by using gasbumps -- The CPC subclass B01D 24/4652 covers filtration processes that separate particles or liquids by employing gas-bump techniques, where a stream of gas is introduced to create turbulence or lift that assists in moving the material through a filter medium. This method is used to enhance separation efficiency, reduce clogging, and improve the handling of fine or low-viscosity substances. It falls under the broader categories of performing operations, separating, separation and filtration.
[B01D 24/4657] by using membranes -- This subclass covers filtration processes that employ a membrane as the separating medium, where a fluid passes through a selective barrier to retain particles or separate components. It is used for inventions related to membrane-based filtration technologies.
[B01D 24/4663] by using pistons -- The CPC subclass B01D 24/4663 covers filtration methods in which pistons are used to pressurise a fluid or slurry and drive it through a filter medium, effecting solid-liquid separation. It applies to devices or processes that rely on piston-generated pressure to achieve filtration.
[B01D 24/4668] by moving the filtering element  B01D24/4605  and  B01D24/4631  take precedence -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - By scrapers, brushes, nozzles or the like placed on the cake-side of the stationary filtering material and only contacting the external layer -> B01D24/4605 - Counter-current flushing -> B01D24/4631
[B01D 24/4673] using rotary devices or vibration mechanisms, e.g. stirrers -- The CPC subclass B01D 24/4673 covers filtration processes that use rotary or vibrating devices, such as stirrers, to aid the separation of solids from liquids. It is part of the hierarchy Performing Operations → Separating → Separation → Filtration.
[B01D 24/4678] using free vortex flow -- The CPC subclass B01D 24/4678 relates to filtration methods that employ a free vortex flow to achieve separation. It covers processes where the fluid circulates in a self-sustaining vortex, allowing solids or immiscible liquids to be removed without mechanical agitation, and is used in vortex filters, cyclonic separators and similar devices.
[B01D 24/4684] using spray devices -- A spray-device method for filtration, classified under CPC B01D 24/4684, applies a fine atomized liquid to a filter medium to capture or remove particulates from a gas or liquid stream. This technique falls within the broader categories of performing operations, separating, and separation, specifically addressing the use of spray mechanisms to enhance filtration efficiency.
[B01D 24/4689] Displacement of the filtering material to a compartment of the filtering device for regeneration -- This subclass covers a method in which the filter medium is shifted into a separate compartment of the filter housing for regeneration, allowing the filter to be refreshed without dismantling the device. The displacement of the filtering material to an internal regeneration chamber is the focus of the entry.
[B01D 24/4694] containing filter material retaining means (e.g. screens, balls) placed on the surface of the filter material -- The entry B01D 24/4694 concerns filtration systems where a retaining structure such as screens, balls or similar elements is positioned on the surface of the filter material to hold the filtering medium in place during operation.
[B01D 24/48] integrally combined with devices for controlling the filtration -- B01D 24/48 designates filtration apparatus that are integrally combined with devices for controlling the filtration, such as valves, sensors or feedback regulators. The class covers arrangements where the filter and its control mechanisms form a single, unified unit rather than separate components.
[B01D 24/4807] Handling the filter cake for purposes other than regenerating -- The subclass B01D 24/4807 covers techniques for dealing with the filter cake after a filtration step when the aim is not to regenerate the filter medium. It includes removal, transport, storage, disposal or further processing of the cake for alternative uses such as recycling, analysis or waste handling.
[B01D 24/4815] for washing -- The subclass B01D 24/4815 defines washing operations integrated with filtration, where a liquid is passed through a filter cake or medium to remove residual solids, contaminants, or entrapped substances. It covers methods that introduce a washing fluid before, during or after the main filtration step to improve purity or recover material.
[B01D 24/4823] for drying -- The CPC subclass B01D 24/4823 covers filtration techniques that incorporate a drying step, defining methods or apparatus where the separation medium is dried to enhance solid recovery or remove moisture from the filtrate. It falls under the broader categories of performing operations, separating, separation and filtration.
[B01D 24/483] by compression -- This subclass covers filtration methods where the separation of solids from liquids or gases is achieved by applying compression, using pressure to force the feed through a filter medium. Typical applications include membrane or cake filtration performed under hydraulic or mechanical pressure.
[B01D 24/4838] by gases or by heating -- B01D 24/4838 covers filtration processes in which a gas stream or the application of heat is used to separate solid particles from a liquid or slurry. The method relies on driving the solid phase through a filter medium by pressure from the gas or by reducing the liquid's viscosity through heating, thereby enhancing particle removal.
[B01D 24/4846] Retarding cake deposition on the filter during the filtration period, e.g. using stirrers  B01D24/407  takes precedence -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Provoking a tangential stream -> B01D24/407
[B01D 24/4853] by clearness or turbidity measuring -- The subclass B01D 24/4853 covers filtration processes in which the clarity or turbidity of the filtered medium is measured, typically by optical or sensor methods, to evaluate filtration performance. It is applied to inventions that incorporate turbidity or clearness detection during or after separation operations.
[B01D 24/4861] by flow measuring -- This subclass covers filtration operations in which the flow of the process fluid is measured to control or assess the separation. It includes devices or methods that use flow meters, pressure drop calculations, or other flow-based indicators to determine filter life, cleaning intervals, or product quality. It is used in liquid or gas filtration where precise flow control is required.
[B01D 24/4869] by level measuring -- This subclass covers filtration processes where the separation of solids from liquids is controlled by monitoring the liquid or filtrate level in the equipment. Level-measuring may be used to stop the operation, open valves, or adjust pressure once a predetermined level is reached.
[B01D 24/4876] in which the filtering elements are moved between filtering operations; particular measures for removing or replacing the filtering elements  B01D24/46 ,  B01D24/4807  take precedence -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Regenerating the filtering material in the filter -> B01D24/46 - Handling the filter cake for purposes other than regenerating -> B01D24/4807
[B01D 24/4884] by pressure measuring -- B01D 24/4884 relates to filtration operations in which the pressure drop across the filter or the filtrate is measured to monitor or control the process. The pressure measurement is used to assess filter performance, detect clogging, or regulate flow during separation.
[B01D 24/4892] by temperature measuring -- The CPC subclass B01D 24/4892 covers filtration processes that are controlled or monitored through temperature measurements, such as adjusting filter media or operating conditions based on temperature data. It falls under the hierarchy of performing operations, separating, separation, and specifically filtration, and is used for inventions that integrate temperature sensing to improve filtration efficiency or selectivity.

This includes systems that combine separate filter elements without permanent bonding, allowing easy replacement or reconfiguration.
[B01D 25/12] Filter presses, i.e. of the plate or plate and frame type -- A filter press of the plate or plate-and-frame type is a separation device that forms a sealed filtration cavity by clamping together multiple filter plates or sections of plates. The pressed plates create a stack of filter elements that retain solids while liquid is forced through the filter medium under pressure.
[B01D 25/127] with one or more movable filter bands arranged to be clamped between the press plates or between a plate and a frame during filtration, e.g. zigzag endless filter bands -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Plate spreading means -> B01D25/172 - Attaching the filter elements to the filter press plates -> B01D25/176 - Clamping means for closing the filter press -> B01D25/19
[B01D 25/1275] the plates or the frames being placed in a non-vertical position -- This subgroup covers filter assemblies in which the plates or frames that hold the filtering elements are arranged in a non-vertical orientation, such as inclined, horizontal or angled positions, to aid loading, cleaning or fluid flow while the elements are clamped together.
[B01D 25/164] Chamber-plate presses, i.e. the sides of the filtering elements being clamped between two successive filtering plates -- Chamber-plate presses are filtration devices in which the sides of one or more filter elements are clamped between two successive plates to form a sealed chamber, allowing liquid or gas to pass through while retaining solids. This arrangement facilitates efficient separation by applying pressure across the filter media.
[B01D 25/1645] the plates being placed in a non-vertical position -- This subclass covers filters where the plate elements are arranged at an angle rather than vertical, achieved by clamping multiple plates together. It pertains to filter constructions where the orientation of the plates is non-vertical to affect flow or separation.
[B01D 25/172] Plate spreading means -- B01D 25/172 covers plate-spreading means used in filters formed by clamping together multiple filtering plates or plate sections. The devices spread and hold the plates in a uniform arrangement to create a continuous filtering surface, improving flow distribution and filtration efficiency.
[B01D 25/176] attaching the filter element to the filter press plates, e.g. around the central feed hole in the plates -- The entry covers means for fixing a filter element to the plates of a filter press, particularly around the central feed hole, in assemblies where several filtering elements are clamped together to form a filter. It pertains to the attachment of the element to the press plates as part of the overall separating operation.
[B01D 25/19] Clamping means for closing the filter press, e.g. hydraulic jacks -- The CPC subclass B01D 25/19 covers clamping mechanisms used to close a filter-press, such as hydraulic jacks that bring together multiple filter plates or elements. These means apply pressure to seal the filter chamber during operation, facilitating filtration by separating solid and liquid phases.

[B01D 25/22] Cell-type filters -- B01D 25/22 in the Cooperative Patent Classification refers to cell-type filters, which are specific types of filters formed by clamping together several filtering elements or parts. These filters are used for separating substances in various industrial and laboratory processes.
[B01D 25/24] Cell-type roll filters -- Cell-type roll filters are filtration units made by clamping together several filter elements or parts of such elements to form a continuous roll. The tightly packed media separates solids from liquids or gases as the fluid passes through the roll.
[B01D 25/26] Cell-type stack filters -- Cell-type stack filters are filtration devices built by clamping together several filtering elements that each have a cellular geometry, creating a network of interconnected pores. The stacked arrangement yields a high surface area and uniform flow path, enabling efficient separation of solids from liquids or gases.
[B01D 25/28] Leaching or washing filter cakes in the filter -- Leaching or washing filter cakes in the filter refers to a separation operation where the retained solid cake on a clamp-assembled filter is treated with a liquid that passes through the filter media to dissolve, extract or rinse desired components while the filter remains in place, often using multiple filtering elements arranged together. This method improves recovery of valuable substances and cleans the cake without dismantling the filter structure.
[B01D 25/281] specially for chamber filter presses -- B01D 25/281 covers chamber filter presses that employ filters created by clamping together multiple filtering elements or portions of such elements. The design is aimed at improving separation efficiency in processes that require solid-liquid separation, fitting within the broader CPC groups for performing operations, separating, and separation.
[B01D 25/282] for drying -- B01D 25/282 pertains to filters used in the process of drying, specifically those constructed by clamping multiple filtering elements together. This classification is part of a broader category dealing with separation techniques within performing operations.
[B01D 25/284] by gases or by heating -- This subclass covers filters that are assembled by clamping together multiple filtering elements or segments of those elements, where the separation of particles is achieved using a gas stream or by applying heat to the filter assembly.
[B01D 25/285] by compression using inflatable membranes -- This subclass covers filters formed by clamping together multiple filtering elements or portions thereof and achieving separation by compressing the assembly with inflatable membranes. The expandable membrane applies uniform pressure, tightening the filter media without permanent bonding.
[B01D 25/287] by compression using pistons -- B01D 25/287 refers to filters that are built by clamping multiple filtering elements or portions of them together, where the clamping is achieved through compression using pistons. This method of joining creates a sealed filtration assembly and falls under the CPC hierarchy of Performing Operations → Separating → Separation.
[B01D 25/288] Retarding cake deposition on the filter during the filtration period, e.g. using stirrers -- The subclass B01D 25/288 deals with techniques that lessen filter-cake buildup on a filter while a separation is taking place, for instance by using stirrers or other agitation means to retard the deposition of material during the filtration period.
[B01D 25/30] Feeding devices -- B01D 25/30 classifies feeding devices used with filters that are made by clamping together several filtering elements or portions thereof, facilitating the introduction of material into the composite filter assembly. These devices are part of separating operations within the broader Performing Operations category.
[B01D 25/302] specially adapted for chamber filter presses -- This CPC subclass covers filter constructions made by clamping together multiple filtering elements or parts of them, with designs specifically adapted for use in chamber filter presses.
[B01D 25/305] for discharging filtrate -- This CPC subclass covers filter assemblies that are created by clamping together two or more filtering elements or portions of them, enabling the removal of the filtrate from the filter structure. It is used for designs where the clamped configuration forms the discharge path for the filtrate.

[B01D 25/38] by moving parts, e.g. scrapers, contacting stationary filter elements -- This CPC subgroup relates to filters that are assembled by clamping together multiple filtering elements and use moving parts such as scrapers to contact stationary filter elements, helping remove deposited material or enhance the separation process.
[B01D 25/383] Brushes -- B01D 25/383 covers brushes employed as filter elements in separation processes, where multiple brush strands or portions are clamped together to form a filtering medium.
