[B01D 33/0108] with bag, cage, hose, tube, sleeve or the like filtering elements -- This CPC subclass B01D 33/0108 covers separating operations that employ movable filtering elements such as bags, cages, hoses, tubes or sleeves. It relates to filters where the filtering media themselves shift or rotate during the filtration process, distinguishing them from static filter arrangements. The entry falls under the hierarchy of Performing Operations → Separating → Separation.
[B01D 33/0116] arranged for inward flow filtration -- This CPC subclass covers filters whose filtering elements move while the filter operates and are configured so the process fluid enters from the outer surface and passes inward through the medium. The moving elements may rotate, vibrate or be otherwise displaced to enhance particle capture or cleaning. Such inward-flow designs are typical in self-cleaning drum or rotating disc filters.
[B01D 33/0125] open ended -- This subclass covers filters whose filtering elements are not fixed but can move while the filtration takes place, such as open-ended designs where the media shifts or is displaced during operation. It includes systems that rely on the motion of the filtering material to achieve separation.
[B01D 33/0133] arranged for outward flow filtration -- B01D 33/0133 covers filters whose filtering elements move while the fluid passes, arranged so that the flow proceeds outward from the filter medium. This subclass pertains to separation devices where the motion of the elements aids particulate capture during the filtering operation.
[B01D 33/0141] open ended -- B01D 33/0141 in the Cooperative Patent Classification refers to filters that have filtering elements which are open-ended and move during the filtering process, enhancing their operational efficiency.
[B01D 33/015] with flat filtering elements -- B01D 33/015 covers filters that employ flat filtering elements which move during the filtering operation. It is situated under Performing Operations → Separating → Separation and pertains specifically to filters with moving flat filter media.
[B01D 33/0158] self-supporting -- Self-supporting filters whose filtering elements shift or move while the fluid passes through them, used in separation operations. The design allows the filter to hold its shape without external supports, with the active filtering medium repositioning during the filtering process. This entry is classified under CPC B01D 33/0158.
[B01D 33/0166] Bar screens -- Bar screens are filtration devices consisting of parallel bars spaced to trap solid material while allowing liquid to pass, commonly used in water treatment, sewage and industrial processes. They fall under filters with moving filtering elements, where the bars can be lifted or vibrated for cleaning.
[B01D 33/0175] with curved filtering elements -- This subgroup covers filtration devices in which the filter media are shaped as curved elements that move relative to the fluid flow, enabling dynamic filtering action. It falls under the broader categories of performing operations, separating and separation.
[B01D 33/0183] supported -- The subclass B01D 33/0183 covers filters whose filtering elements are supported yet move relative to the filter housing while the filtration takes place, enabling dynamic separation of particles or liquids. It pertains to designs where the supporting structure allows the filtering media to shift during operation.
[B01D 33/0191] with corrugated, folded or wound filtering sheets -- Filters that employ corrugated, folded or wound filtering sheets which move while the filtration is performed, falling under the broader category of separating operations and more specifically within separation filters that use movable filtering elements.
[B01D 33/03] with vibrating filter elements -- This subclass covers filter devices in which the filtering elements themselves are set into vibration during operation to enhance separation. It includes designs where the filter media moves back-and-forth or oscillates to prevent clogging and improve flow, applicable to liquid or gas filtration systems.
[B01D 33/0307] with bag, cage, hose, tube, sleeve or the like filtering elements -- B01D 33/0307 covers separating devices in which the filtering elements-such as bags, cages, hoses, tubes or sleeves-are designed to move while the filtration takes place, enabling continuous or batch separation of solids or liquids. This sub-class falls under the broader hierarchy of Performing Operations → Separating → Separation.
[B01D 33/0315] arranged for inward flow filtration -- This CPC subclass covers filtration devices where the filter media moves during the filtering operation and the arrangement directs fluid toward the interior of the filter, typical of inward-flow filters used in separation processes.
[B01D 33/0323] open ended -- B01D 33/0323 covers open-ended filters in which the filtering elements are capable of moving while the filter is operating, typically to adjust flow or prevent clogging during separation processes.
[B01D 33/033] arranged for outward flow filtration -- The subclass B01D 33/033 covers filters that are arranged for outward-flow filtration, where the filtering elements move during the filtering operation. It includes devices in which the media shift, rotate or otherwise change position as fluid passes outward, enhancing separation efficiency.
[B01D 33/0338] open ended -- B01D 33/0338 pertains to filters featuring filtering elements that are open-ended and move during the filtration process, enhancing efficiency in separating substances. This classification is part of a detailed system for patent documentation, specifically addressing dynamic filter designs within the broader category of separation techniques.
[B01D 33/0346] with flat filtering elements -- Filters that employ flat filtering elements-such as plates, sheets or membranes-that move relative to the feed or collection side during the filtering operation, enabling continuous separation of solids from liquids or gases within the broader category of separating processes.
[B01D 33/0353] self-supporting -- The CPC class B01D 33/0353 covers self-supporting filters whose filtering elements move while the filter operates. Such devices incorporate a rigid or semi-rigid framework that holds the media without external supports, allowing the filter to maintain its shape while particles are removed from liquids or gases.
[B01D 33/0361] Bar screens -- Bar screens are filtering devices that incorporate moving bars or similar elements to screen out solid particles from a fluid stream during a separation operation. The screens form a barrier that allows liquids or fine materials to pass while retaining larger debris, and the bar elements can be adjusted or moved to accommodate varying flow rates or cleaning cycles.
[B01D 33/0369] with curved filtering elements -- This CPC subclass covers filters that use curved filtering elements which move during the filtration process, describing designs where the element's shape and motion improve separation efficiency. It falls under the hierarchy of performing operations, separating, and separation, and is indexed as B01D 33/0369.
[B01D 33/0376] supported -- Supported filters with moving filtering elements are devices in which the filter medium is mounted on a support that travels during the filtration operation, such as rotating drums, traveling belts or sliding screens, allowing continuous separation of solids while the supporting structure moves.
[B01D 33/0384] with corrugated, folded or wound filtering sheets -- The entry pertains to filters that use corrugated, folded or wound filtering sheets which move during the filtration process, facilitating the separation of solids from liquids or gases.
[B01D 33/0392] with curved filtering elements -- The CPC subclass B01D 33/0392 covers filtration devices that use curved filtering elements which are set in motion during the filtering process. It falls under the hierarchy of Performing Operations → Separating → Separation, describing dynamic, curved media for fluid separation.
[B01D 33/04] with filtering bands or the like supported on cylinders which are impervious for filtering -- This subclass concerns filters that employ filtering bands or similar elements mounted on a solid, non-permeable cylinder; the cylinder serves only as a support while the attached bands perform the separation as they move during operation. It is a specific type of separating process within the broader category of performing operations.
[B01D 33/042] whereby the filtration and squeezing-out take place between at least two filtering bands -- The subclass addresses filter devices whose filtering elements move during operation, enabling the filtration and squeezing-out steps to occur between at least two moving filter bands. It covers arrangements where the bands themselves act as the filtering medium and provide simultaneous dewatering as the material passes from one band to the next.
[B01D 33/044] with filtering bands or the like supported on cylinders which are pervious for filtering -- Filters that use pervious bands or similar elements mounted on rotating cylinders, where the cylinders move during operation to effect filtration, are classified under CPC B01D 33/044. This subclass falls under the hierarchy of performing operations → separating → separation and covers cylindrical, rotating filter media that allow fluid to pass through while retaining solids.
[B01D 33/048] with endless filtering bands -- This subclass covers filters that incorporate moving filtering elements during the filtering operation, specifically those using endless filtering bands that continuously loop to separate solids from liquids or gases.
[B01D 33/052] combined with a compression device -- B01D 33/052 covers filters that incorporate a compression device to enhance filtration; the filtering elements move during operation and are simultaneously pressed or compacted by a compression mechanism. The design improves sealing, reduces media deformation and maintains consistent flow, typically used in liquid-solid separation processes.
[B01D 33/056] Construction of filtering bands or supporting belts, e.g. devices for centering, mounting or sealing the filtering bands or the supporting belts -- This subclass covers the design and assembly of filter ribbons or belts and the associated accessories that position, hold or seal them during filtration, such as centring frames, mounting brackets and sealing elements.
[B01D 33/0565] combined with means to fasten the opposite edges of the filtering band together, e.g. Zipper -- B01D 33/0565 pertains to filters where the filtering elements move during operation, specifically those that include a mechanism to fasten the opposite edges of the filtering band together, such as a zipper. This classification is part of the broader category of separating operations within the Cooperative Patent Classification system used by the EPO and USPTO.
[B01D 33/06] with rotary cylindrical filtering surfaces, e.g. hollow drums -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - With filtering bands or the like supported on cylinders which are pervious for filtering -> B01D33/044
[B01D 33/067] Construction of the filtering drums, e.g. mounting or sealing arrangements -- The entry covers the design and assembly of filtering drums used in filters whose filtering elements move during operation, focusing on how the drums are mounted, secured or sealed to the surrounding apparatus. It addresses arrangements that enable reliable positioning, rotation or reciprocation while maintaining a leak-tight seal.
[B01D 33/073] arranged for inward flow filtration -- Filters in which the filtering elements are mobile during operation and the fluid is drawn from the outside toward the interior, such as rotating, vibrating or otherwise moving filter media that directs flow inward for separation.
[B01D 33/09] with surface cells independently connected to pressure distributors -- The subclass B01D 33/09 pertains to filter devices whose surface is divided into individual cells, each cell being connected to its own pressure-distributor unit, so that flow can be regulated separately for each cell during the filtering operation. This arrangement enables precise control of pressure and flow across the filter surface in separation processes.
[B01D 33/11] arranged for outward flow filtration -- This subclass covers filter devices in which the filtering elements themselves are set in motion while the fluid passes outward-flow through the filter. Examples include rotating drum filters, vibrating screen filters and other arrangements where the media moves to enhance separation or prevent clogging. The movement is integral to the filtration operation, directing flow from the interior to the exterior of the device.
[B01D 33/13] with surface cells independently connected to pressure distributors -- The subclass B01D 33/13 covers filters whose surface cells are each connected independently to pressure distributors, enabling separate pressure control for each cell during the filtering operation. It lies within the separating-operations hierarchy under filters with moving elements.
[B01D 33/15] with rotary plane filtering surfaces -- Filters in which the filtering element rotates within a plane, such as rotating disc or plate filters, are covered by this subclass. The rotary motion creates a moving filtering surface that continuously presents fresh area for separation, improving throughput and cake removal in liquid-solid or gas-solid filtration operations.
[B01D 33/155] the filtering surface being parallel to the rotation axis -- This CPC subclass covers rotating filtration devices whose filtering surface is oriented parallel to the axis of rotation, such as cylindrical or disc filters that move during operation. It is nested within the broader categories of performing operations, separating, and separation.
[B01D 33/17] with rotary filtering tables -- B01D 33/17 covers filters that employ rotary filtering tables, in which the filtering elements are mounted on a rotating platform that moves during the filtration operation to enhance solid-liquid separation.
[B01D 33/19] the table surface being divided in successively tilted sectors or cells, e.g. for discharging the filter cake -- The entry covers filter devices whose supporting table is divided into a series of adjacent, successively tilted sectors or cells that can be angled during operation to discharge the accumulated filter cake, allowing continuous or stepwise cleaning of the filter surface.
[B01D 3/32] Other features of fractionating columns -- B01D 3/32 covers auxiliary or specialized elements of fractionating columns used in distillation or stripping operations, such as plates, packings, distributors, reflux devices, heat-exchangers or control mechanisms that improve separation efficiency. It includes designs that affect liquid-gas contact, column-internals geometry, or temperature and pressure regulation within the column.
[B01D 33/21] with hollow filtering discs transversely mounted on a hollow rotary shaft -- This subclass covers filters that use hollow discs mounted transversely on a rotating hollow shaft, where the discs serve as filtering elements that move during operation. It pertains to separation processes employing moving disc filters to separate solids or liquids.
[B01D 33/215] the filtering discs being fixed inwardly on a rotating construction -- This CPC entry covers filters in which the filtering elements are discs that are secured on the inner side of a rotating structure, so the discs move with the rotation during operation, providing separation of a fluid or suspension while the filter media itself remains fixed to the rotating assembly.
[B01D 3/322] Reboiler specifications -- This CPC subclass covers reboilers, the equipment used in distillation columns to supply the heat required for vaporising the liquid phase. It specifies characteristics such as heat duty, construction material, pressure rating and type of heat source, and falls under the broader category of separating operations.
[B01D 33/23] Construction of discs or component sectors thereof -- The CPC subclass B01D 33/23 covers the manufacture or assembly of disc-shaped filter elements or their component sectors, including processes for forming, shaping, cutting, or joining the disc portions that later move as part of a filtering operation. It relates to constructing the movable filtering media used in separation equipment.
[B01D 3/324] Tray constructions -- The CPC sub-group B01D 3/324 concerns the design and arrangement of trays inside columns used for distillation, stripping or similar liquid-gas contact operations. It includes trays that support liquid distribution, promote mass transfer and may incorporate perforations, weirs or structured packing.
[B01D 33/25] with hollow frames axially mounted on a hollow rotary shaft -- Filters that incorporate movable filtering elements are mounted in hollow frames which are attached axially to a hollow rotary shaft, allowing the shaft to drive the elements while also serving as a conduit for fluid or product passage through the filter assembly.
[B01D 3/326] Tray supports -- B01D 3/326 covers the structures that hold or position trays inside distillation or stripping columns, allowing liquid or gas to be spread across the column height. Tray supports are designed to endure mechanical loads, aid fluid flow and often can be adjusted or removed for maintenance.
[B01D 33/27] with rotary filtering surfaces, which are neither cylindrical nor planar, e.g. helical surfaces -- The CPC subclass B01D 33/27 covers filters that employ moving filtering elements with rotary surfaces that are neither cylindrical nor flat, such as helical or other non-planar rotating faces, used to separate solids or liquids during the filtering operation.
[B01D 33/275] using contiguous impervious surfaces -- The subclass B01D 33/275 covers filter devices in which the filtering elements move during operation, using adjacent impervious surfaces that guide or seal the elements as they shift. This arrangement enables continuous separation of solids or liquids while the filter media travels or slides across a contiguous non-porous surface.
[B01D 3/328] Sealing between the column and the trays -- B01D 3/328 covers sealing arrangements between the shell of a distillation or stripping column and its internal trays, aimed at preventing bypass of liquid or vapor and ensuring proper contact. The classification falls under separation processes where liquids are contacted with a gaseous phase.
[B01D 33/29] the movement of the filter elements being a combination of movements -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - The table surface being divided in successively tilted sectors or cells -> B01D33/19
[B01D 33/31] Planetary movement -- A filter in which the filtering elements are mounted on a carrier that moves in a planetary or orbiting fashion during operation, causing the media to rotate or orbit relative to the fluid and each other. This motion enhances contact with the material being filtered, improving separation efficiency.
[B01D 33/327] Tipping buckets, trays or like sections -- This CPC subclass covers filter devices that use movable sections such as tipping buckets, trays or similar elements to collect and discharge filtered material. It applies to separation processes where the filtering element shifts during operation to aid cleaning or material removal.
[B01D 33/333] with individual filtering elements moving along a closed path -- Filters in which each filtering element travels continuously along a closed trajectory, such as rotating drums, moving mesh belts, or rotating cylindrical screens, allowing the medium to be separated while the filtering medium repeatedly cycles through the same path. This arrangement enables continuous operation and periodic cleaning of the elements without stopping the filtration process.
[B01D 33/35] with multiple filtering elements characterised by their mutual disposition -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Whereby the filtration and squeezing-out take place between at least two filtering bands -> B01D33/042 - With hollow filtering discs transversely mounted on a hollow rotary shaft -> B01D33/21
[B01D 33/37] in parallel connection -- This subclass covers filter devices in which several filtering elements are arranged in parallel and move during operation, such as rotating plates, vibrating screens or travelling belts, to separate solids from fluids. The parallel connection allows simultaneous processing, higher throughput and uniform flow distribution across the elements.
[B01D 33/39] concentrically or coaxially -- Filters whose filtering elements move in a concentric or coaxial manner during operation, such as rotating drums or shafts that slide along a shared axis, enabling separation of solid or liquid phases while maintaining a common geometric center.
[B01D 3/34] with one or more auxiliary substances -- This subclass covers distillation or related gas-liquid exchange operations, such as stripping, in which one or more auxiliary substances are added to facilitate the separation of the liquid phase.
[B01D 33/41] in series connection -- This subclass deals with filtration processes where the filter elements move during operation and are arranged in a series connection, so the material passes sequentially through each element. It falls under separating operations involving moving filters.
[B01D 33/42] concentrically or coaxially -- B01D 33/42 covers filters in which the filtering elements move relative to each other in a concentric or coaxial arrangement, such as rotating or reciprocating media within a surrounding housing. It is a subclass of separation operations where the motion of the filter media is integral to the filtration process.
[B01D 3/343] the substance being a gas -- B01D 3/343 covers liquid-gas contacting methods and apparatus in which the substance being processed is a gas, such as stripping or other distillation-related exchange operations. It applies to separation techniques where a gaseous feed or product is contacted with a liquid to achieve purification, concentration or removal of components.
[B01D 33/44] Regenerating the filter material in the filter -- B01D 33/44 refers to filter systems whose filtering elements move during operation and include methods for regenerating the filter material while the filter remains in use, such as cleaning, back-flushing, or in-situ rejuvenation of the filter media. This subclass falls under Performing Operations → Separating → Separation and addresses the restoration of filter performance without disassembly.
[B01D 3/346] the gas being used for removing vapours, e.g. transport gas -- The code B01D 3/346 covers processes where a gas is introduced into a liquid to strip or remove volatile components, such as in distillation or related exchange operations. It specifically relates to the transport gas that carries away vapours from the liquid phase, enhancing separation efficiency. This classification falls under the broader categories of performing operations, separating, and separation.
[B01D 33/46] by scrapers, brushes or the like acting on the cake-side of the filtering element -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - The backwash arms, shoes acting on the cake side -> B01D33/503
[B01D 33/461] brushes -- Brushes are moving filtering elements used in filtration processes to aid separation of solids from liquids or gases. By rotating or oscillating within a filter medium, they dislodge trapped particles and improve flow, making them integral to dynamic filtration systems.
[B01D 33/463] nozzles -- The subclass B01D 33/463 covers filters whose filtering elements move during operation and includes the nozzles that direct fluid to or from the moving media, enabling controlled flow during the separation process. It relates to devices where the nozzle is integral to the moving filter assembly, facilitating efficient filtration and particle removal.
[B01D 33/465] take-off rollers -- Take-off rollers are moving components of a filtration system that convey filtered material away from the filter medium, providing continuous separation by transporting the collected product off the filter surface during operation.
[B01D 33/466] scrapers -- The CPC subclass B01D 33/466 covers filters whose filtering elements move during operation and employ scrapers to clear accumulated material. It includes devices that continuously or intermittently scrape the filter medium to maintain flow and prevent clogging, such as rotating or reciprocating scraper mechanisms integrated into the filter housing.
[B01D 33/468] wires, strands, strings or the like -- This subclass concerns filters whose filtering elements move while the filter operates, using wires, strands, strings or similar components that are displaced, vibrated or otherwise set in motion to achieve separation of particles.
[B01D 33/48] by flushing, e.g. counter-current air-bumps -- B01D 33/48 pertains to filters where the filtering elements are flushed or subjected to counter-current air-bumps during the operation to maintain efficiency and cleanliness. This method is used in dynamic filtration processes to prevent clogging and enhance performance.
[B01D 33/50] with backwash arms, shoes or nozzles -- Filters whose filtering elements are designed to move during operation and are equipped with backwash arms, shoes or nozzles to facilitate cleaning or regeneration of the filter media. This arrangement enables periodic displacement of the media to remove retained particles while maintaining continuous filtration. It applies to devices where the movement is integral to the backwashing process.
[B01D 33/503] the backwash arms, shoes acting on the cake side -- B01D 33/503 covers the backwash arms or shoes that act on the cake side of a filter during operation. These movable elements engage the filter cake to break it up or lift it, facilitating its removal and improving the cleaning cycle of the filtration unit.
[B01D 33/506] with a stirrer placed on the filtrate side -- A filter in which a stirring element is positioned on the side where the liquid passes through, moving during the filtration process to agitate the filtrate. This arrangement helps prevent clogging, improves flow and enhances separation efficiency in operations involving filters with moving filtering elements.
[B01D 33/52] by forces created by movement of the filter element -- This subgroup relates to filters whose filtering element moves during operation, using the forces generated by that motion to achieve separation. It includes devices where vibration, rotation, reciprocation or other movement of the filter media creates the driving force for the filtering process.
[B01D 33/54] involving vibrations -- This subclass covers filter arrangements in which the filtering elements are deliberately vibrated during operation to improve particle separation, such as vibrating screens, shaker screens or acoustically assisted filters for liquids or gases, where the motion of the media enhances the filtering efficiency.
[B01D 33/56] involving centrifugal force -- Filters that use rotating or swirling motion to generate centrifugal force, causing the filtering medium or elements to move while the fluid passes through. This dynamic action enhances separation of solids from liquids by forcing particles against the moving filter surface or outward radially, improving throughput and efficiency in processes such as clarifying, dewatering, or fine-particle capture.
[B01D 33/58] Handling the filter cake in the filter for purposes other than for regenerating -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - For discharging the filter cake -> B01D33/76
[B01D 3/36] Azeotropic distillation -- Azeotropic distillation is a separation technique in which a third component is added to the liquid mixture to form an azeotrope, lowering the boiling point and allowing the components to be separated by simple distillation. This method is used when the original components have close-boiling points and cannot be separated by ordinary distillation.
[B01D 33/60] for washing -- The subclass B01D 33/60 relates to washing operations that use filters whose filtering elements move during the filtration step, such as rotating, oscillating or otherwise shifting filter media, enabling continuous cleaning or separation of solids while the wash action proceeds.
[B01D 33/62] for drying -- The CPC subclass B01D 33/62 covers filter devices whose filtering elements move during operation, specifically those intended for drying. These moving media enhance moisture removal while the filtration process proceeds.
[B01D 33/64] by compression -- This subclass covers filter devices that achieve separation by compressing the filtering medium, causing the filter elements to move during operation. It falls under the hierarchy of performing operations, separating, separation, and filters with moving filtering elements.
[B01D 33/642] by pressure belts -- Filters that use moving filtering elements driven by pressure belts, where the belts press the filter media against a substrate or each other to effect separation of particles or liquids during the filtering operation. This subclass falls under separating processes within the broader performing operations field.
[B01D 33/644] by pressure plates, membranes -- Filters that employ moving filtering elements driven by pressure plates or flexible membranes to effect separation, where the plate or membrane deforms under pressure to create a variable pore structure and control flow through the filter media. This approach is used for precise separation processes in liquids or gases, enabling adjustable filtration characteristics during operation.
[B01D 33/646] by pressure rollers -- Filters that employ pressure rollers as the moving filtering elements, where the rollers compress the filter medium to separate solids from liquids or gases. The classification covers devices that use rolling compression to enhance filtration efficiency and control cake formation during the filtering operation.
[B01D 33/648] by screws -- This CPC subgroup deals with filters whose filtering elements are moved by screw mechanisms during operation, enabling adjustable positioning or displacement of the media while separating substances.
[B01D 33/66] by gases or by heating -- Filters whose filtering elements shift or move while the filtration takes place, employing either a gas stream or heat to effect separation of solids or liquids from a mixture. This covers mechanisms where the motion of the filter medium is driven by gas flow or thermal expansion, enabling continuous or cyclic cleaning and improved throughput. The classification is used for patents describing such dynamic filtration systems.
[B01D 33/663] by direct contact with a fluid -- Filters whose filtering elements move while the separation takes place through direct contact with a fluid, such as rotating, oscillating or otherwise displaced media that are wetted by the stream to achieve particle removal. This subclass applies to devices where the moving element itself acts as the filtration surface.
[B01D 33/666] by indirect heat-exchange -- The subclass B01D 33/666 covers filters whose filtering elements move during operation and whose heat is transferred indirectly to another medium, such as a separate heating or cooling fluid, rather than being exchanged directly with the product stream. It applies to filtration devices that incorporate a secondary heat-exchange loop to control temperature while the filter media is in motion.
[B01D 33/68] Retarding cake deposition on the filter during the filtration period, e.g. using stirrers -- The classification B01D 33/68 covers methods for reducing filter-cake buildup during a filtration operation by using moving filtering elements such as stirrers or agitators. It falls within the hierarchy of performing operations, separating, and separation.
[B01D 33/70] having feed or discharge devices -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Means for pressure distribution -> B01D33/82
[B01D 33/705] with internal recirculation through the filter -- Filters that incorporate moving filtering elements and a built-in recirculation path, allowing the fluid to pass through the filter medium repeatedly within the same device. The internal flow loop improves separation efficiency by continually re-exposing the material to the filter media during operation.
[B01D 33/72] for feeding -- This group relates to feeding mechanisms for filters whose filtering elements move during operation, such as continuously supplied filter bags, drums, or rotating media, ensuring that material to be filtered is delivered to the active filtering zone.
[B01D 33/722] containing fixed liquid displacement elements or cores -- B01D 33/722 covers filters whose filtering elements move while the filter operates, yet incorporate fixed liquid-displacement components or cores that remain stationary. The moving media perform the separation, while the fixed elements serve to maintain flow paths, structural integrity, or pressure distribution within the device.
[B01D 33/725] Special treatment of the feed stream before contacting the filtering element, e.g. cutting  B01D35/24 ,  B01D37/02 ,  B01D37/03  take precedence -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Providing loose granular material to scratch the filters clean -> B01D35/24 - Precoating the filter medium; Addition of filter aids to the liquid being filtered -> B01D37/02 - Using flocculating agents -> B01D37/03
[B01D 33/727] provoking a tangential stream -- This subclass covers filters whose filtering elements move during operation and where a tangential fluid stream is intentionally created to sweep across the filter surface, enhancing separation efficiency and preventing clogging. The technique is used in processes that require continuous or semi-continuous filtration with improved particle removal.
[B01D 33/74] for discharging filtrate -- The CPC subclass B01D 33/74 covers filters whose filtering elements move during operation to aid discharge of the filtrate. It falls under the hierarchy of Performing Operations → Separating → Separation and pertains to mechanisms that actively remove liquid from the filter medium.
[B01D 33/742] containing fixed liquid displacement elements or cores -- Filters that incorporate stationary liquid-displacement components or solid cores, such as fixed plates, meshes or porous bodies, which remain in place while the fluid passes through during the filtration process. These elements shape the flow path and aid in separating solids from liquids without moving.
[B01D 33/745] Construction of suction casings, pans, or the like -- This CPC subclass covers designs for the housing or support structures of suction devices used in filters where the filtering element moves during operation. It includes the construction details of suction casings, pans, and similar components that channel fluid into the moving filter media.
[B01D 33/747] moving during the filtration period -- B01D 33/747 covers filter devices whose filtering elements shift, rotate or otherwise move while the filtration process is underway, allowing the media to adjust during operation. The moving elements can enhance separation efficiency, reduce clogging or enable continuous cleaning of the filter surface.
[B01D 33/76] for discharging the filter cake, e.g. chutes -- This CPC subclass deals with devices that remove the accumulated filter cake from a moving filter, such as chutes that convey the cake away during operation. It covers arrangements for discharging the cake in systems where the filtering elements shift as part of the separation step.
[B01D 33/763] for continuously discharging concentrated liquid -- This CPC subgroup pertains to filtration apparatuses where the filter elements are mobile during operation and the device continuously discharges a concentrated liquid stream, typically to maintain a high solid content in the filtrate.
[B01D 33/766] Devices for breaking the filter cake, e.g. cutting -- This subclass covers equipment that mechanically disrupts or removes the filter cake that builds up on filtering elements during operation, such as cutters, scrapers or other breaking tools. It applies to devices that move with the filter while filtering to restore flow.
[B01D 3/38] Steam distillation -- Steam distillation is a separation technique in which steam is introduced into a liquid mixture, causing volatile components to vaporize at temperatures below their normal boiling points and be carried out with the steam for condensation. This method is especially suited for extracting heat-sensitive materials and is classified under CPC B01D 3/38.
[B01D 33/80] Accessories -- A classification for auxiliary components that support or enhance filters whose filtering elements move while operating, including drive mechanisms, supports, cleaning devices, or other add-ons that assist in the movement, positioning or maintenance of the moving filter media.
[B01D 33/801] Driving means, shaft packing systems or the like -- The class B01D 33/801 covers mechanisms that provide motion to filtering elements during a separation process, such as drives, shaft-packing arrangements, or similar devices that move the filter media while it performs its function. It includes any apparatus that translates power into the required movement of the filter components.
[B01D 33/802] Device for changing the inclination of the filtering element -- This classification covers devices that adjust the tilt or inclination of a filter element while the filtration process is underway. By changing the angle of the filtering media, the device can modify flow distribution, reduce clogging, and improve cleaning or back-washing efficiency in separators such as drum or plate filters.
[B01D 33/803] in which the filtering elements are moved between filtering operations  B01D33/52  takes precedence ; Particular measures for removing or replacing the filtering elements; Transport systems for filters -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - By forces created by movement of the filter element -> B01D33/52
[B01D 33/804] integrally combined with devices for controlling the filtration -- B01D 33/804 relates to filters whose filtering elements move during the filtration process and are integrally combined with devices that control the filtration, such as valves, actuators or feedback systems. This subclass falls under Performing Operations → Separating → Separation in the CPC.
[B01D 33/805] by clearness or turbidity measuring -- B01D 33/805 covers methods and devices that assess the clarity or turbidity of a fluid while it passes through a filter whose filtering elements move during operation. It includes optical or electronic sensors that monitor the filtrate in real time to indicate the degree of particle removal or filter performance.
[B01D 33/806] by flow measuring -- Filters whose filtering elements are set in motion during operation and where the separation is achieved or controlled by measuring the flow of fluid through the filter.
[B01D 33/807] by level measuring -- The subclass B01D 33/807 covers filters whose filtering elements move while the filtration takes place, with the separation achieved by measuring the level or height of the material or element. It applies to devices that adjust or control the filtering process through level-based monitoring of the moving media.
[B01D 33/808] by pressure measuring -- Filters whose filtering media are movable during operation and where the filtration process is monitored or controlled by measuring the pressure across the filter, enabling adjustments based on pressure readings. This sub-class covers devices that integrate pressure-sensing mechanisms to manage flow and separation efficiency.
[B01D 33/809] by temperature measuring -- This CPC subclass covers filters whose filtering elements are moved or adjusted during the separation operation by means of temperature measurement, enabling control of the filtering process. It falls under the hierarchy of Performing Operations → Separating → Separation and relates to technologies that use temperature data to modulate filter behavior.
[B01D 33/82] Means for pressure distribution -- A means for pressure distribution in a filter where the filtering elements move during operation, facilitating uniform flow and preventing channeling. This device is classified under the CPC heading for separating processes, specifically within the subcategory for filters with moving filtering elements. It addresses the mechanical arrangement that evenly distributes pressure across the filter media.
[B01D 3/40] Extractive distillation -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Azeotropic distillation -> B01D3/36
[B01D 3/42] Regulation -- B01D 3/42 covers control and regulation techniques applied to liquid-gas contacting operations such as distillation, stripping or related exchange processes, including methods for maintaining temperature, pressure, vapor composition or flow rates to achieve desired separation performance. It addresses devices, sensors and feedback systems used to adjust these parameters during operation.
[B01D 3/4205] Reflux ratio control splitter -- The CPC entry B01D 3/4205 covers devices that split a reflux stream to adjust the reflux ratio in distillation or related gas-liquid contacting processes. Such splitters enable precise control of liquid return to the column, improving separation efficiency.
[B01D 3/4211] of columns -- This subclass covers column configurations used in distillation, stripping or similar liquid-gas contacting operations. It includes the design features, internals and operating principles of columns where a liquid phase is distributed over packing or trays and contacted by an upward or downward gas stream.
[B01D 3/4216] Head stream -- A head stream is the vapor portion drawn from the top of a distillation or stripping column, containing the more volatile components that have been transferred from the liquid phase to the gaseous phase during the separation process. It is typically the product stream rich in the desired light constituents.
[B01D 3/4222] Head- and side stream -- B01D 3/4222 relates to apparatus or methods for separating liquids by contacting them with a gaseous medium, where a portion of the vapor or liquid is removed as a head or side stream, such as in distillation or stripping columns to collect specific fractions.
[B01D 3/4227] Head- and bottom stream -- The entry covers distillation or related gas-liquid contact processes where the product streams are divided into a head (overhead) stream and a bottom stream. It addresses equipment and methods for separating the lighter, vapor-rich fraction from the heavier, liquid-rich fraction within such operations.
[B01D 3/4233] Head- and feed stream -- The subclass B01D 3/4233 concerns the arrangement and management of the liquid feed entering a distillation or stripping column and the overhead (head) vapor stream that exits it. It includes the design, flow paths, composition and control of these streams within processes where liquids are contacted by a gaseous phase.
[B01D 3/4238] Head-, side- and bottom stream -- The entry covers distillation or similar liquid-gas contacting operations in which the product streams are taken from the head (top), side sections, or bottom of the column. It relates to the design, control or use of equipment for separating these distinct streams during the process.
[B01D 3/4244] Head-, side- and feed stream -- The entry covers the configuration of a distillation or similar separation column where distinct liquid streams are taken from, or introduced into, the apparatus: the overhead or head stream removed at the top, side streams drawn from intermediate trays or zones, and the feed stream introduced at a designated point. It addresses the design and operation of these multiple points of contact between liquid and vapor phases within the column.
[B01D 3/425] Head-, bottom- and feed stream -- The B01D 3/425 subclass covers distillation or related liquid-gas separation operations that specify the arrangement of the head, bottom and feed streams within a column, such as stripping or other processes where the feed, overhead and residue streams are defined and managed.
[B01D 3/4255] Head-, side-, bottom- and feed stream -- The B01D 3/4255 entry covers distillation, stripping or similar liquid-gas contacting operations in which the liquid feed or product streams are introduced or withdrawn at specific locations such as the top (head), the side, the bottom or dedicated feed points of the apparatus. It defines how these stream positions are used to control mass transfer and separation efficiency within the column.
[B01D 3/4261] Side stream -- B01D 3/4261 Side stream is a separation step in distillation or stripping where a portion of the liquid-gas mixture is withdrawn from the main column for separate treatment, recovery or recycle. The side stream may be processed in a different unit or returned to the system, allowing control of composition or removal of specific components.
[B01D 3/4266] Side- and bottom stream -- The code covers equipment or methods that withdraw or handle side and bottom product streams in distillation or gas-liquid contacting operations such as stripping. It pertains to the collection, removal, or further processing of the lower-lying fractions that exit the column at the side or bottom. This includes designs for taking off these streams while maintaining separation efficiency.
[B01D 3/4272] Side- and feed stream -- This CPC subclass B01D 3/4272 covers side- and feed-stream handling in distillation or related exchange processes where a liquid contacts a gaseous medium, such as stripping. It pertains to the specific management of auxiliary streams that feed into or are taken from the main separation column.
[B01D 3/4277] Side-, bottom- and feed stream -- B01D 3/4277 covers distillation or similar gas-liquid contacting operations in which the liquid is introduced or withdrawn from side, bottom or feed streams rather than a conventional overhead. It includes apparatus and methods that manage these alternative stream positions to improve separation efficiency.
[B01D 3/4283] Bottom stream -- The "Bottom stream" entry (B01D 3/4283) covers the portion of liquid withdrawn from the lower end of a distillation or stripping column, where the separated components are collected after contacting the liquid with a gaseous medium. It is applied to processes that separate mixtures by vapor-liquid exchange, focusing on the bottom product stream.
[B01D 3/4288] Bottom- and feed stream -- The B01D 3/4288 classification covers distillation or stripping installations where a liquid feed is introduced into the column and the residual liquid, known as the bottom stream, is withdrawn from the lower part of the apparatus. It addresses the arrangement and control of these feed and bottom streams in processes that contact liquids with a gaseous phase.
[B01D 3/4294] Feed stream -- B01D 3/4294 covers the feed stream in distillation or related liquid-gas contacting processes such as stripping. It refers to the portion of material introduced to the separation unit before contact with the gaseous phase. The entry is used to classify patents dealing with the characteristics, preparation and handling of that feed stream.
[B01D 35/00] Filtering devices having features not specifically covered by groups -- **Definition:** This place covers:
[B01D 35/005] Filters specially adapted for use in internal-combustion engine lubrication or fuel systems -- B01D 35/005 covers filters specially adapted for use in internal-combustion engine lubrication or fuel systems, where the filter design is tailored to handle oil or fuel under engine operating conditions. These devices remove contaminants while maintaining the flow and pressure requirements specific to engines.
[B01D 35/02] Filters adapted for location in special places, e.g. pipe-lines, pumps, stop-cocks, -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Floating filters -> B01D35/05
[B01D 35/023] Filler pipe filters -- Filler pipe filters are filtering devices that incorporate a pipe filled with porous or granular material to trap contaminants as fluid flows through. The pipe acts as both the conduit and the filtering medium, allowing continuous operation while providing resistance to debris buildup. This arrangement is used where compact, replaceable filtration is needed in processes such as water treatment or chemical handling.
[B01D 35/027] rigidly mounted in or on tanks or reservoirs -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Plug, tap or cock filters -> B01D35/04
[B01D 35/0273] Filtering elements with a horizontal or inclined rotation or symmetry axis submerged in tanks or reservoirs -- The entry B01D 35/0273 covers filtering elements that operate with a horizontal or inclined axis of rotation or symmetry while being submerged in a tank or reservoir, as part of separating processes. It includes devices whose filter geometry or motion does not fall under more specific groups in the CPC hierarchy.
[B01D 35/0276] Filtering elements with a vertical rotation or symmetry axis mounted on tanks or reservoirs -- The B01D 35/0276 subclass covers filter elements that are mounted on tanks or reservoirs and feature a vertical axis of rotation or symmetry. These devices are designed so the filter can rotate or be symmetric around a vertical line, allowing continuous or intermittent filtration while attached directly to the holding vessel. This is used in processes where space-saving, ease of cleaning, or uniform flow distribution is required.
[B01D 35/04] Plug, tap, or cock filters -- B01D 35/04 covers plug, tap, or cock filters, which are filtering devices that incorporate a removable or controllable element such as a plug, tap, or cock to isolate or regulate the flow through the filter. These components allow the filter to be bypassed, cleaned, or replaced without dismantling the entire system. The classification falls under separating, separation, and filtering devices not otherwise specified.
[B01D 35/043] Reversible faucet filters -- Reversible faucet filters are water-filter units designed to be mounted on a faucet in either direction, allowing easy installation and replacement while providing filtration of tap water for domestic use. They typically incorporate a replaceable cartridge and a flow-control mechanism, and are classified under CPC B01D 35/043 for filtering devices that do not fall within more specific groups.
[B01D 35/046] the filtering element being mounted in the faucet plug -- B01D 35/046 covers filtering devices in which the filtering element is incorporated directly into the faucet plug, allowing the plug itself to perform separation of contaminants from the water flow. This subgroup deals with arrangements where the filter is mounted inside the plug rather than as a separate cartridge, fitting within the broader category of separating and filtration technologies not otherwise specified.
[B01D 35/05] Floating filters -- Floating filters are filtering devices that employ a floating element-typically a membrane, screen or porous plate-that remains suspended within the fluid stream rather than being fixed to a frame. This arrangement allows continuous contact with the medium, enhancing separation efficiency while accommodating variations in flow or particle load, and is not specifically covered by other CPC groups.
[B01D 35/06] Filters making use of electricity or magnetism -- B01D 35/06 covers filtration apparatus that employs electric or magnetic forces to separate particles or fluids. These devices use fields rather than purely mechanical media to capture or retain contaminants, and are grouped under separation operations where the specific features are not covered by other more detailed CPC groups.
[B01D 35/10] Brush filters -- Brush filters are filtration devices that employ one or more rotating or stationary brushes to capture and remove suspended particles from fluids. They are used in water treatment, industrial processes, and air filtration where conventional screens are insufficient, and they fall under CPC class B01D 35/10.
[B01D 35/12] Devices for taking out of action one or more units of multi- unit filters, e.g. for regeneration -- Devices designed to disengage or isolate one or more sections of a multi-unit filter, allowing those sections to be regenerated, cleaned, or replaced while the remaining filter units continue operating. Such mechanisms enable maintenance without shutting down the entire filtration system.
[B01D 35/14] Safety devices specially adapted for filtration -- B01D 35/14 covers safety devices specially adapted for filtration, which are filtering devices having features not specifically covered by other groups within the separating and separation subclass. These devices are designed to protect equipment or personnel during filtration processes.
[B01D 35/143] Filter condition indicators -- B01D 35/143 covers devices or systems that monitor and display the operating condition of a filter, such as fouling level, pressure drop, breakthrough, or flow performance, without being a filter itself. It includes indicators, sensors, or visual aids that signal when maintenance or replacement is needed, provided the features are not covered by more specific CPC groups.
[B01D 35/1435] with alarm means -- B01D 35/1435 covers filtering devices that include an alarm system to signal abnormal conditions, such as blockages or pressure changes, when the device's features are not otherwise covered by more specific groups within the separating-separation subclass. The classification is used for patents describing filters equipped with warning or alarm means that alert users to operational faults.
[B01D 35/147] Bypass or safety valves -- Bypass or safety valves are filtering devices that provide an alternate flow path or relieve pressure when a system exceeds designated limits, preventing damage or contamination. They function within separation processes but possess characteristics not detailed in more specific groups of the classification.
[B01D 35/1475] Pressure relief valves or pressure control valves -- B01D 35/1475 pertains to pressure relief or pressure control valves that incorporate filtering functions not specifically addressed in other groups. These devices protect equipment by automatically releasing excess pressure or maintaining set pressure levels while allowing fluid to pass through a filter element, and are used in chemical, petrochemical and industrial processing plants.
[B01D 35/15] Bidirectional working filters -- Bidirectional working filters are separation devices that can filter a fluid while allowing the flow direction to be reversed without dismantling the unit. This capability facilitates continuous operation, cleaning, or regeneration of the filter media, improving efficiency in processes where flow direction may need to change.
[B01D 35/153] Anti-leakage or anti-return valves -- Anti-leakage or anti-return valves are filtration-related components designed to prevent fluid or gas from flowing backward through a filter, allowing flow in only one direction and sealing off any reverse pressure that might cause leakage. These devices are used in systems where maintaining a unidirectional flow is critical to protect equipment and ensure consistent process performance.
[B01D 35/157] Flow control valves: Damping or calibrated passages -- The B01D 35/157 subclass covers flow-control valves that incorporate damping mechanisms or calibrated passages to precisely regulate fluid movement. These devices are used where a steady, adjustable flow is needed and are categorized under filtering devices whose features are not captured by more specific groups.
[B01D 35/1573] Flow control valves -- Flow control valves are devices that regulate the rate, direction, or pressure of a fluid flow within a system, typically incorporating movable elements such as disks, plates, or pistons. This subclass captures valve designs that do not fall under more specific filtering-device groups.
[B01D 35/1576] Calibrated passages -- B01D 35/1576 in the Cooperative Patent Classification refers to filtering devices that feature calibrated passages, which are not covered by more specific groups. These passages are designed with precise dimensions to control flow or filtration characteristics.
[B01D 35/16] Cleaning-out devices -- B01D 35/16 covers cleaning-out devices that are used to remove accumulated material from filters or filtration units when the filter itself does not fall within a more specific subclass. These devices may include brushes, sprays, back-flushing mechanisms or other means designed to restore filter function without being defined by the filter's particular structure. The group collects cleaning solutions that are not covered by more narrowly defined cleaning-out subclasses.
[B01D 35/18] Heating or cooling the filters -- B01D 35/18 covers filtering devices in which the filter element is intentionally heated or cooled to improve performance. Heating may be used to reduce viscosity, prevent clogging, or aid desorption, while cooling can condense vapors or preserve temperature-sensitive media. The code applies when temperature control is integral to the filter's operation rather than a peripheral accessory.
[B01D 35/185] comprising a vaporizing unit -- B01D 35/185 designates filtering devices that incorporate a vaporizing unit as part of the separation operation. It falls under Performing Operations - Separating - Separation and is used for devices whose vaporizing features are not covered by more specific groups.
[B01D 35/20] Vibrating the filters -- B01D 35/20 covers filtering devices that are vibrated to improve separation performance. By applying oscillatory motion to the filter medium, the method helps dislodge retained particles, reduce clogging and increase throughput in liquid or gas filtration processes.
[B01D 35/22] Directing the mixture to be filtered on to the filters in a manner to clean the filters -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Directing the mixture to be filtered on the filtering element in a manner to clean the filter continuously -> B01D29/904
[B01D 35/24] Providing loose granular material to scratch the filters clean -- B01D 35/24 concerns a method for cleaning filter elements by introducing loose granular material that physically scratches the filter surface to remove buildup. It falls under the CPC hierarchy of performing operations, separating, and separation, and addresses filtering devices not specifically covered by other groups.
[B01D 35/26] Filters with built-in pumps -- B01D 35/26 covers filters that incorporate an integral pump to move fluid through the filtering media, combining filtration and pumping in a single unit. These devices are used where self-circulating filtration is required, such as in laboratory, industrial or environmental applications.
[B01D 35/28] Strainers not provided for elsewhere -- B01D 35/28 is situated in the CPC hierarchy under performing operations, separating and separation. It is used for strainers that are not covered by any more specific groups, essentially generic filtering devices whose particular features are not described elsewhere.
[B01D 35/30] Filter housing constructions -- B01D 35/30 covers filter housing constructions, i.e., the structural components that support and contain filter media in devices used for separating liquids or gases. The classification applies to filter housings that have features not specifically defined in other groups within the separation sub-class.
[B01D 35/301] Constructions of two or more housings  B01D35/12  takes precedence -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Devices for taking out of action one or more units of multi-unit filters -> B01D35/12
[B01D 35/303] the housings being modular, e.g. standardised -- This CPC subgroup (B01D 35/303) covers filtering devices whose housings are modular or standardised, used in separation operations where the filter's case can be assembled from interchangeable parts. It applies when the modular housing features are not specifically addressed by other, more detailed groups within the filtering-device hierarchy.
[B01D 35/305] with features related to crash tests or crash safety measures -- B01D 35/305 covers filtration devices that incorporate or are adapted for crash-test or crash-safety functions, such as filters built into vehicle safety systems or test rigs used to evaluate debris retention during impacts. The code lies within the separation class, specifically for filters whose special features are not covered by more specific groups.
[B01D 35/306] Filter mounting adapter -- The B01D 35/306 entry covers filter-mounting adapters used in separation processes, specifically devices that hold or connect filters but are not otherwise defined by more detailed groups. These adapters belong to the broader class of filtering devices within the performing-operations-separating-separation hierarchy.
[B01D 35/308] Made of at least two different materials, e.g. metal and plastic -- B01D 35/308 classifies filtering devices that combine at least two distinct materials, such as metal and plastic, within the broader CPC hierarchy of Performing Operations - Separating - Separation - Filtering devices not otherwise covered. The code distinguishes multi-material constructions from single-material filters.
[B01D 35/31] including arrangements for environmental protection, e.g. pressure resisting features -- B01D 35/31 is a CPC subclass under Performing Operations → Separating → Separation that pertains to filtering devices whose designs include environmental-protection measures, such as pressure-resisting features or arrangements intended to safeguard the surrounding environment during operation.
[B01D 35/32] against radiation -- B01D 35/32 covers filtering devices that incorporate features for protection against radiation, such as shields or absorptive layers that block or attenuate ionising or non-ionising radiation while performing a separation operation. These devices are used where filtered materials must be handled in environments where radiation exposure poses a hazard.
[B01D 35/34] open-topped -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Including arrangements for environmental protection -> B01D35/31
[B01D 36/00] Filter circuits or combinations of filters with other separating devices -- **Definition:** This place covers:
[B01D 36/001] Filters in combination with devices for the removal of gas, air purge systems -- B01D 36/001 covers filter units that are combined with gas-removal or air-purge devices, forming integrated circuits that separate solids, liquids or contaminants from a gas stream while simultaneously exhausting or purging unwanted gases. The classification applies to arrangements where filters work together with scrubbers, venting or evacuation systems to achieve combined filtration and gas-removal functions.
[B01D 36/003] Filters in combination with devices for the removal of liquids  B01D35/185  takes precedence -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Comprising a vapourizing unit -> B01D35/185
[B01D 36/005] Liquid level sensing means, e.g. for water in gasoil-filters -- The CPC entry B01D 36/005 falls under performing operations → separating → separation → filter circuits or combinations of filters with other separating devices and concerns liquid-level sensing means incorporated in filtration systems. It specifically addresses devices that detect water or other liquids accumulating in gasoil-filters to monitor and control separation performance.
[B01D 36/006] Purge means -- B01D 36/006 in the Cooperative Patent Classification refers to specific methods and apparatus for purging, typically used in filter circuits or combinations of filters with other separating devices. This classification covers techniques aimed at removing contaminants or unwanted substances from a system to maintain efficiency and performance.
[B01D 36/008] Means to filter or treat the separated liquid -- B01D 36/008 pertains to apparatus that filters or chemically treats liquids after they have been separated, such as filter units, filter housings, or combined filter-treatment systems integrated with other separating devices.
[B01D 36/02] Combinations of filters of different kinds -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - With multiple filtering elements, characterized by their mutual disposition -> B01D29/50 - With multiple filtering elements, characterized by their mutual disposition -> B01D33/35
[B01D 36/04] Combinations of filters with settling tanks -- B01D 36/04 covers integrated arrangements that pair filtration units with settling-tank devices to improve the separation of solids from liquids. The classification includes designs where filters and sedimentation tanks operate together, allowing simultaneous filtration and gravitational settling in a single process stream.
[B01D 36/045] Combination of filters with centrifugal separation devices -- This CPC subclass covers apparatus that integrate filter circuits with centrifugal separation devices, enabling simultaneous filtration and centrifugation of liquids or gases. It includes configurations where filter media are placed upstream or downstream of a centrifuge or combined in a single unit to enhance removal of particulates.
[B01D 37/00] Processes of filtration -- **Definition:** This place covers:  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Gas Filters -> B01D46/00
[B01D 37/02] Precoating the filter medium -- B01D 37/02 relates to filtration processes where the filter medium is coated before use, improving its performance by enhancing retention, flow characteristics or chemical resistance. The precoating step is carried out prior to the actual separating operation.
[B01D 37/025] additives incorporated in the filter -- B01D 37/025 relates to the use of additives that are integrated into a filter during filtration processes, where they serve to improve filtration efficiency, prevent clogging or modify flow characteristics. It falls under the broader categories of performing operations, separating, separation, and processes of filtration.
[B01D 37/03] using flocculating agents -- B01D 37/03 covers processes of filtration that employ flocculating agents to aggregate suspended particles, improving separation efficiency. It falls under the broader categories of performing operations, separating, separation, and processes of filtration.
[B01D 37/04] Controlling the filtration -- B01D 37/04 covers techniques for regulating the filtration process, such as controlling flow rate, pressure, temperature, or concentration to achieve desired separation performance. It includes devices and methods that adjust these parameters in real-time to maintain consistent filtrate quality or prevent filter fouling.
[B01D 37/041] by clearness or turbidity measuring -- B01D 37/041 concerns methods for evaluating the clarity or turbidity of a filtrate during a filtration operation. It includes techniques that measure the amount of suspended particles or cloudiness to monitor and control the effectiveness of the separation process.
[B01D 37/043] by flow measuring -- B01D 37/043 covers filtration processes in which the separation is performed or controlled by measuring the flow of the liquid or gas through the filter medium. This subclass includes methods that use flow-rate monitors, pressure differentials, or other flow-sensing techniques to regulate or assess the filtration operation.
[B01D 37/045] by level measuring -- B01D 37/045 relates to filtration processes that are controlled by monitoring the level of the material being filtered. The method uses level-measuring devices to determine when the filter is full or when a desired separation has been reached, allowing automatic shutdown or adjustment of the operation. This ensures efficient separation without over-loading the filter medium.
[B01D 37/046] by pressure measuring -- B01D 37/046 covers filtration processes that employ pressure measurement to monitor or control the separation of solids from liquids or gases. The method uses the pressure drop across the filter medium to assess filter condition, efficiency or clogging, allowing adjustments during operation.
[B01D 37/048] by temperature measuring -- B01D 37/048 relates to filtration processes in which the separation of solids from liquids or gases is controlled or effected by temperature measurement. The method monitors temperature changes to determine when a filter cake is formed or when the filtrate reaches a set temperature, allowing automated termination or adjustment of the filtration step. This sub-group falls under the broader categories of separating and separation within Performing Operations.
[B01D 39/00] Filtering material for liquid or gaseous fluids -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Mainly materials for mechanical filtration, however materials with additives e.g. adsorbents are also included. Also composite filtering materials with combined activity (mechanical filtration, adsorption, antimicrobial action etc. are also under the present group.
[B01D 39/02] Loose filtering material, e.g. loose fibres -- B01D 39/02 covers loose filtering media such as loose fibres used to separate particles from liquids or gases. The classification applies to unbound filter elements that are not formed into a rigid structure, enabling filtration by entrapment or adsorption.
[B01D 39/04] Organic material, e.g. cellulose, cotton -- The B01D 39/04 subclass covers filters that use organic fibrous materials such as cellulose, cotton or similar natural substances to separate particulate matter from liquids or gases. It includes designs, structures and methods that employ these organic media for filtration in industrial, environmental or laboratory processes.
[B01D 39/06] Inorganic material, e.g. asbestos fibres, glass beads or fibres -- The CPC subclass B01D 39/06 covers inorganic filtering media such as asbestos fibres, glass beads or glass fibres that are employed to separate or filter liquid or gaseous streams. These materials are used in filtration devices to retain particles while allowing the fluid to pass.
[B01D 39/08] Filter cloth, i.e. woven, knitted or interlaced material -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Metallic material -> B01D39/10 , B01D39/12
[B01D 39/083] of organic material -- B01D 39/083 covers methods and apparatus for filtering organic material from liquids or gases, including the removal of organic contaminants or particles by means of filter media or membranes in separation processes. It pertains to the design, operation and optimization of filtration steps that isolate organic substances during liquid or gaseous fluid treatment.
[B01D 39/086] of inorganic material -- B01D 39/086 pertains to the filtration of inorganic materials from liquid or gaseous fluids, a process within the broader category of separation techniques. This classification is used by the European Patent Office and the United States Patent and Trademark Office for organizing patents related to specific filtering methods.
[B01D 39/10] Filter screens essentially made of metal -- B01D 39/10 pertains to filter screens that are essentially made of metal and used for separating solids from liquids or gases. The subclass includes metal mesh, perforated plates or woven wire screens that function as the primary filtering element in filtration devices.
[B01D 39/12] of wire gauze -- B01D 39/12 covers the use of wire gauze as a filtering element for separating liquids or gases. Wire gauze consists of interwoven metallic wires forming a mesh that retains particles while allowing fluid flow. This sub-class falls under the broader category of filtering material for liquid or gaseous fluids in the CPC.
