This classification is used by the European Patent Office and the United States Patent and Trademark Office to categorize inventions related to this specific type of filtration process.
[B01D 39/1607] the material being fibrous  B01D39/18  takes precedence -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Fibrous cellulosic material -> B01D39/18
[B01D 39/1615] of natural origin -- The subclass B01D 39/1615 covers filter media that are obtained from natural sources, such as plant fibers, animal tissues, or mineral minerals. These materials are employed in devices for separating liquid or gaseous streams, providing a biodegradable or renewable alternative to synthetic filters.
[B01D 39/1623] of synthetic origin -- This subgroup concerns filtration media of synthetic origin used to separate liquid or gaseous fluids, covering synthetic fabrics, membranes and composite materials designed for removing contaminants during the separation process.
[B01D 39/163] sintered or bonded -- The B01D 39/163 subclass covers filter media made by sintering or bonding particles together to form a porous structure used for separating liquid or gaseous streams. Such materials provide a rigid, permeable matrix that can retain solids or contaminants while allowing the fluid phase to pass, and are employed in a range of filtration applications.
[B01D 39/1638] the material being particulate -- This subclass deals with separation processes that filter liquids or gases using a filter medium made up of discrete particles rather than a solid sheet or continuous matrix. It covers the use of particulate filter material in performing operations for liquid or gaseous fluid filtration.
[B01D 39/1646] of natural origin, e.g. cork or peat -- B01D 39/1646 covers filtering materials of natural origin, such as cork or peat, employed for separating liquids or gases. These natural porous media are used as filter elements to retain contaminants while allowing the fluid to pass through.
[B01D 39/1653] of synthetic origin -- A filtering material for liquid or gaseous fluids that is manufactured from synthetic substances, such as polymers, resin-bonded fibers, or artificial ceramics, rather than natural or mineral sources. This subclass covers all synthetic-origin filter media used in separation processes.
[B01D 39/1661] sintered or bonded -- A sintered or bonded filter medium is produced by compacting and heating particles until they fuse together, forming a porous structure that can retain solids while allowing liquids or gases to pass. This type of material is used in filtration devices for separating contaminants from fluids.
[B01D 39/1669] Cellular material -- The code covers porous, cellular structures used as filter media to separate particles from liquids or gases. Such materials include foam, sintered metal or polymeric matrices that permit fluid flow while retaining contaminants, and they are applied in filtration, separation and purification processes.
[B01D 39/1676] of synthetic origin -- B01D 39/1676 pertains to filtering materials of synthetic origin used in the separation processes of liquid or gaseous fluids. This classification is part of a detailed system for patent documentation, focusing on specific types of filter media.
[B01D 39/1692] Other shaped material, e.g. perforated or porous sheets -- The CPC subclass B01D 39/1692 pertains to filter media that are formed into specific shapes, such as perforated or porous sheets, for separating liquid or gaseous streams. It covers designs where the material's geometry-holes, pores, or patterned structures-provides the filtering function, distinguishing them from flat or unshaped filters.
[B01D 39/18] the material being cellulose or derivatives thereof -- The subclass concerns filter media employed for liquid or gaseous fluid filtration where the filter material is cellulose or one of its derivatives, such as paper, cotton or regenerated-cellulose fibres. It is part of B01D 39/00, which groups all filtering materials used in fluid separation processes.
[B01D 39/20] of inorganic material, e.g. asbestos paper, metallic filtering material of non-woven wires -- B01D 39/20 relates to filtration media composed of inorganic substances such as asbestos paper or non-woven metallic wires. The entry covers structures in which these inorganic filter elements are employed to separate liquid or gaseous streams by trapping particles or droplets. It is used for applications where mechanical filtration by a solid inorganic matrix is required.
[B01D 39/2003] Glass or glassy material -- B01D 39/2003 refers to filters and filtration devices that are made from glass or glassy materials, used to separate liquids or gases from a stream. The subclass includes the composition, structure and manufacturing of such glass filter elements and related methods for their use in filtration operations.
[B01D 39/2006] the material being particulate -- B01D 39/2006 designates the use of particulate filter media for separating liquid or gaseous fluids. It covers filters whose filling consists of solid particles that trap contaminants as the fluid passes through, falling under the CPC hierarchy Performing Operations > Separating > Separation. This class includes any apparatus or method that employs granular or powdered materials as the filtering element.
[B01D 39/201] sintered or bonded by inorganic agents -- This subclass covers filter media for liquids or gases that are formed by sintering or by bonding particles together with inorganic binding agents, such as ceramics or mineral glues. The resulting structures are porous, rigid, and designed to retain particles while allowing fluid flow, and are used in separation processes within the broader "separating" hierarchy.
[B01D 39/2013] otherwise bonded, e.g. by resins -- The B01D 39/2013 subclass designates filter media whose particles are bonded together by means other than sintering, typically using adhesives such as resins. These bonded filters are employed to separate liquids or gases by retaining unwanted substances while allowing the desired fluid to pass. This category falls under performing operations → separating → separation → filtering material for liquid or gaseous fluids.
[B01D 39/2017] the material being filamentary or fibrous -- B01D 39/2017 covers filter media for separating liquids or gases when the filtering material is filamentary or fibrous, such as woven, non-woven or mesh fibers that trap particulates. It belongs to the hierarchy Performing Operations > Separating > Separation and relates to devices that use fiber-based structures to perform filtration.
[B01D 39/202] sintered or bonded by inorganic agents -- The B01D 39/202 subclass covers filter elements whose porous structure is created by sintering or by bonding particles together with inorganic agents. Such media are employed in the separation of liquids or gases, providing mechanical strength and chemical resistance for filtration applications.
[B01D 39/2024] otherwise bonded, e.g. by resins -- B01D 39/2024 covers filter media that are kept together by means other than sintering, such as bonding with resins or other adhesives. The classification applies to solid or porous structures used to separate liquid or gaseous fluids where the particles or fibers are held in place by a resin-based matrix.
[B01D 39/2027] Metallic material -- B01D 39/2027 covers the use of metallic materials as filter media in devices that separate liquids or gases. It includes filters made of metals or metal alloys, whether woven, sintered, perforated or coated, employed for removing particles, droplets or other contaminants from fluid streams.

It is classified under Performing Operations → Separating → Separation → Filtering material for liquid or gaseous fluids.

[B01D 39/2037] otherwise bonded -- B01D 39/2037 pertains to filtering media for liquids or gases where the individual filter particles are bonded together by means other than sintering, welding or mechanical fastening, such as adhesives, polymer binders or other chemical glues. [B01D 39/2044] sintered or bonded by inorganic agents -- This CPC subclass B01D 39/2044 pertains to filter media for liquids or gases that are produced by sintering or by bonding the particles together with inorganic agents. [B01D 39/2048] otherwise bonded -- The B01D 39/2048 entry covers filter media for liquid or gas filtration whose components are held together by means other than conventional mechanical bonding, such as electrostatic, magnetic, adhesive or chemical bonds, enabling separation of particles from the fluid.
[B01D 39/2051] Metallic foam -- The CPC subclass B01D 39/2051 concerns metallic foams employed as filtering material for liquid or gaseous fluids. [B01D 39/2055] Carbonaceous material  solid sorbent compositions comprising free carbon  B01J20/20 -- The classification covers solid sorbent compositions that contain carbonaceous material with free carbon, employed as filtering media for liquids or gases. [B01D 39/2062] Bonded, e.g. activated carbon blocks -- The CPC code B01D 39/2062 relates to bonded porous filters, notably activated-carbon blocks, used to separate contaminants from liquids or gases. Such filters are made by compressing carbon particles with binders to create a rigid, high-surface-area structure that adsorbs impurities in water, air and other fluid streams.
[B01D 39/2065] the material being fibrous -- B01D 39/2065 covers fibrous filter media used to separate liquid or gas mixtures. It includes sintered ceramic elements, oxide-based screens and similar structures used in filtration devices for removing solids or contaminants.
[B01D 39/2072] the material being particulate or granular -- This CPC subclass covers filtration media that consist of particulate or granular materials used to separate liquids or gases from a fluid stream. It applies to filters where the separating element is composed of granules, sand, beads or similar solids that trap contaminants while allowing the carrier fluid to pass.
[B01D 39/2075] sintered or bonded by inorganic agents -- This subclass covers filter media formed by sintering or bonding inorganic particles into a porous structure for separating liquid or gaseous fluids. Such materials are used in filtration units where durability and chemical resistance are required.
[B01D 39/2079] otherwise bonded, e.g. by resins -- This CPC subclass deals with filter media whose constituent particles are held together by non-conventional bonding methods, such as resin adhesives, rather than by sintering or mechanical interlocking. The bonded structures are employed for separating liquids or gases in filtration processes.
[B01D 39/2082] the material being filamentary or fibrous -- B01D 39/2082 covers filtration media that consists of filamentary or fibrous materials, used in devices that separate liquid or gaseous fluids. The entry specifies that the filter material's structure is composed of threads, fibers, or similar elongated elements to trap contaminants while allowing the desired fluid to pass.
[B01D 39/2086] sintered or bonded by inorganic agents -- A filter material whose particles are joined together by sintering or by inorganic binding agents, forming a solid structure that retains liquids or gases while allowing the desired fluid to pass. This manufacturing method provides mechanical strength and chemical resistance suited to separation and filtering applications.
[B01D 39/2089] otherwise bonded, e.g. by resins -- This CPC subclass pertains to filter media for liquid or gaseous streams whose constituent fibers or particles are held together by means other than mechanical interlocking, most often by adhesives such as synthetic resins, providing structural integrity through chemical bonding rather than weaving or sintering.
[B01D 39/2093] Ceramic foam -- Ceramic foam is a porous, inorganic filter material composed of a network of interconnected ceramic cells that allows fluids to pass while retaining particles. Its high temperature resistance, chemical inertness and mechanical strength make it suitable for separating solids from liquids or gases in industrial filtration, catalytic and thermal-shielding applications.
[B01D 41/00] Regeneration of the filtering material or filter elements outside the filter for liquid or gaseous fluids -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Regeneration of liquid filters under: -> B01D24/00 , B01D25/00 , B01D29/00 , B01D33/00 - Regeneration of gas filters under: -> B01D46/00
[B01D 41/02] of loose filtering material -- The B01D 41/02 subclass concerns the regeneration of loose filtering material or filter elements that have been removed from a liquid- or gas-filtering device. It includes processes for cleaning, restoring, or re-activating such media so they can be reused in subsequent filtration operations.
[B01D 41/04] of rigid self-supporting filtering material -- B01D 41/04 relates to methods for regenerating rigid, self-supporting filter media outside the filter housing, covering cleaning or restoring the performance of such filters used with liquid or gaseous streams. It falls under the broader category of separating operations, specifically the regeneration of filtering material.
[B01D 43/00] Separating particles from liquids, or liquids from solids, otherwise than by sedimentation or filtration -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Separation of immiscible liquids -> B01D17/00 - Sedimentation or flotation -> B01D21/00 , B03D1/00 - Liquid filtration -> B01D24/00 - B01D33/00 - Gas filtration -> B01D46/00
[B01D 45/00] Separating dispersed particles from gases or vapours -- **Definition:** This place covers:
[B01D 45/02] by utilising gravity -- This CPC subclass covers methods of separating dispersed particles from gases or vapours that rely on gravity as the driving force. It includes processes where particles settle out due to weight differences without additional mechanical aids.
[B01D 45/04] by utilising inertia -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Separating dispersed particles by centrifugal forces -> B01D45/12
[B01D 45/06] by reversal of direction of flow -- B01D 45/06 in the Cooperative Patent Classification refers to methods for separating dispersed particles from gases or vapours through the process of reversing the direction of flow. This technique is used to enhance particle collection efficiency by utilizing changes in fluid dynamics.
[B01D 45/08] by impingement against baffle separators -- B01D 45/08 covers separating solid particles dispersed in gases or vapours by directing the flow against a baffle so that the particles impact and are collected while the cleaned stream passes around or through the baffle. This impingement method is used in filtration, dust removal and emission-control applications where a simple barrier provides efficient particle separation.
[B01D 45/10] which are wetted -- B01D 45/10 in the Cooperative Patent Classification refers to methods and apparatus for separating dispersed particles that are wetted from gases or vapours, a process often used in air purification and industrial exhaust treatment.
[B01D 45/12] by centrifugal forces -- The subclass covers processes that separate dispersed particles from gases or vapours by using centrifugal forces, typically employing rotating devices such as cyclones or centrifuges to drive heavier particles outward for collection.
[B01D 45/14] generated by rotating vanes, discs, drums or brushes -- A method for separating dispersed particles from gases or vapours that employs rotating elements such as vanes, discs, drums or brushes to generate the separating action. The motion creates centrifugal or drag forces that cause particles to be collected on the rotating surfaces or expelled from the gas stream. This approach is classified under CPC B01D 45/14.
[B01D 45/16] generated by the winding course of the gas stream -- B01D 45/16 covers separation techniques that remove dispersed particles from gases or vapours by inducing a winding or swirling gas-stream path, causing particles to be driven outward and collected. It includes devices such as cyclones and vortex separators that exploit the curved flow for particle removal.
[B01D 45/18] Cleaning-out devices -- B01D 45/18 covers cleaning-out devices that separate dispersed particles from gases or vapours, such as filters, cyclones, scrubbers or other apparatus used to remove solid contaminants from gas streams during industrial processes.
[B01D 46/00] Filters or filtering processes specially modified for separating dispersed particles from gases or vapours -- **Definition:** This place covers:  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Filtering elements -> B01D24/00 - B01D35/00 - Filter material -> B01D39/00 - Regeneration of filtering material or filter elements outside the filter for liquid or gaseous fluids -> B01D41/00
[B01D 46/0001] Making filtering elements -- B01D 46/0001 covers the manufacturing of filtering elements that are specially adapted to separate dispersed particles from gases or vapours. It falls under the CPC hierarchy of Performing Operations - Separating - Separation - Filters or filtering processes specially modified for separating dispersed particles from gases or vapours.
[B01D 46/0002] Casings; Housings; Frame constructions -- This subclass concerns the structural components-casings, housings and frame constructions-that support or contain filters specially adapted to separate dispersed particles from gases or vapours. It includes designs that provide sealing, mounting and access for such filters, enabling efficient particle removal while maintaining the integrity of the filtration system.
[B01D 46/0004] Details of removable closures, lids, caps or filter heads -- The entry covers the design and specifications of removable closures such as lids, caps, or filter heads that are used on filters or filtering equipment specifically adapted for separating dispersed particles from gases or vapors. It includes details on how these closures attach, seal, and can be taken off for replacement or maintenance.

[B01D 46/0006] Filter elements or cartridges installed in a drawer-like manner -- B01D 46/0006 covers filter elements or cartridges that are mounted in a drawer-like fashion, designed for filtration systems that separate dispersed particles from gases or vapours. These drawer-type inserts allow easy replacement or maintenance within the overall filter assembly.
[B01D 46/0008] Two or more filter elements not fluidly connected positioned in the same housing -- The subclass defines apparatuses that house two or more filter elements within a single enclosure, where the elements are not fluidly connected, and are employed to separate dispersed particles from gases or vapours. It pertains to specially modified filtering processes for such multi-element configurations.
[B01D 46/0009] Tray-like arrangements of filters in a vessel -- This CPC subclass concerns filter systems that employ tray-like arrangements mounted inside a vessel to separate dispersed particles from gases or vapours. It addresses specially modified filters or filtering processes designed for such in-vessel separation.
[B01D 46/001] Means for connecting filter housings to supports -- This subclass addresses components that attach filter housings to supporting structures, such as brackets, clamps, flanges or similar joining devices, used in filters specially adapted for separating dispersed particles from gases or vapours. It includes any mechanical means that secure the housing to frames, cartridges, or other support elements.
[B01D 46/0012] In-line filters -- B01D 46/0012 covers in-line filters that are integrated directly into a gas or vapor stream to continuously remove dispersed particles. These filters are specially designed for separation processes, allowing filtration to occur without diverting the flow from its path.
[B01D 46/0013] Modules -- Modules are self-contained filter units designed for incorporation into larger gas-cleaning systems, each comprising a housing, inlet and outlet, and the filtering media required to separate dispersed particles from gases or vapours. They are often interchangeable and may be pre-assembled for rapid replacement or scaling of a separation process.
[B01D 46/0015] Throw-away type filters -- B01D 46/0015 covers disposable filters designed for separating dispersed particles from gases or vapours. These throw-away type filters are specially modified to perform a one-time filtration operation and are not intended for reuse. They fall under the broader categories of performing operations, separating and separation.
[B01D 46/0016] Folded frame or housing constructions -- B01D 46/0016 covers filter or filtration devices that employ folded-frame or housing structures to support filter media, designed to separate dispersed particles from gases or vapours. These constructions typically use corrugated or folded plates to provide a rigid yet lightweight enclosure, improving mechanical stability and flow distribution.
[B01D 46/0017] Filter elements installed in a branch of a pipe, e.g. with an y-shaped tubular housing -- The subclass B01D 46/0017 covers filter elements that are mounted in a branch of a pipe, such as a Y-shaped tubular housing, for separating dispersed particles from gases or vapours. It falls under the hierarchy of performing operations-separating-separation in the Cooperative Patent Classification.
[B01D 46/0027] with additional separating or treating functions -- This subclass concerns filters or filtering processes that are specially adapted to separate dispersed particles from gases or vapours while also performing an additional separating or treating function, such as moisture removal, catalytic conversion, adsorption of specific compounds, or simultaneous purification steps.
[B01D 46/0028] provided with antibacterial or antifungal means -- This CPC subclass (B01D 46/0028) covers filters or filtering processes specially adapted to separate dispersed particles from gases or vapours and that incorporate antibacterial or antifungal means to inhibit microbial growth. It is situated under Performing Operations → Separating → Separation.
[B01D 46/003] including coalescing means for the separation of liquid -- This subclass covers filters or filtering processes that are specially adapted to separate dispersed particles from gases or vapours by incorporating coalescing means that collect and merge liquid droplets, enabling the removal of liquid from the gas stream.
[B01D 46/0031] with collecting, draining means -- Filters or filtering processes that are specially adapted to separate dispersed particles from gases or vapours and include means for collecting or draining the captured material. The design incorporates features that allow the separated particles to be removed from the filter medium, facilitating continuous operation and preventing buildup. This subcategory addresses technologies where removal of the collected solid or liquid is integral to the filtration system.
[B01D 46/0032] using electrostatic forces to remove particles, e.g. electret filters -- This subclass covers filter media that employ electrostatic attraction to capture solid or liquid particles from a gas or vapor stream, such as electret-type filters. The technology relies on permanent or induced charges on the filter surface to enhance particle removal efficiency. It is indexed under CPC code B01D 46/0032.
[B01D 46/0034] using magnetic forces to remove particles -- This subclass covers filtration methods that employ magnetic forces to extract dispersed particles from gases or vapours. It includes filters or processes specially adapted to magnetically attract and remove the particles during separation.
[B01D 46/0035] by wetting, e.g. using surfaces covered with oil -- This CPC subclass deals with filters or filtering processes that separate dispersed particles from gases or vapours by wetting the filter medium, for example using surfaces coated with oil to capture the particles.
[B01D 46/0036] by adsorption or absorption -- B01D 46/0036 designates filters or filtering processes specially modified to separate dispersed particles from gases or vapours through adsorption or absorption. It is a subclass within the CPC hierarchy for performing operations, separating, and separation.
[B01D 46/0038] with means for influencing the odor, e.g. deodorizing substances -- The B01D 46/0038 subclass covers filters or filtering processes that separate dispersed particles from gases or vapours and incorporate features to modify the odor, such as the use of deodorizing substances. It falls under the hierarchy of performing operations, separating, and separation.
[B01D 46/0039] with flow guiding by feed or discharge devices -- This CPC subclass (B01D 46/0039) covers filters or filtering processes that are specially modified to separate dispersed particles from gases or vapours, using flow-guiding feed or discharge devices to control the direction of the stream. It falls under the hierarchy of Performing Operations → Separating → Separation.
[B01D 46/0041] for feeding -- A feeding arrangement for filters or filtering processes that are specially adapted to separate dispersed particles from gases or vapours, describing the means of introducing the gas or vapour stream into the filter media or device. This includes inlet devices, distribution systems and methods for delivering the flow to the separation element.
[B01D 46/0042] Use of the inlet flow in order to clean the filter surface -- A technique in which the incoming fluid stream is directed so that it removes collected particles from the filter surface, thereby cleaning the filter during operation. This method uses the inlet flow itself to dislodge and transport the trapped material, maintaining filter efficiency while separating dispersed particles from gases or vapours.
[B01D 46/0043] containing fixed gas displacement elements or cores -- B01D 46/0043 covers filters that incorporate fixed gas-displacement elements or cores to aid the removal of dispersed particles from gases or vapours. These devices create a defined flow path, improving particle capture efficiency in continuous separation processes.
[B01D 46/0045] by using vanes -- This subclass covers filter or filtering processes that separate particles from gases or vapours by employing vanes to direct flow and capture particles. The vanes are arranged to create a forced-air path that enhances separation efficiency.
[B01D 46/0046] provoking a tangential stream  B01D46/0045  takes precedence -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Flow guiding by feed devices using vanes -> B01D46/0045
[B01D 46/0047] for discharging the filtered gas -- The entry concerns apparatus or methods that remove the cleaned gas from a filter system specially adapted to separate dispersed particles from gases or vapours. It includes discharge passages, outlets or blowing arrangements that convey the filtered gas to downstream processes or the atmosphere.
[B01D 46/0049] containing fixed gas displacement elements or cores -- This subclass covers filters or filtering processes specially adapted for removing dispersed particles from gases or vapours that incorporate fixed gas-displacement elements or cores, such as stationary plates, meshes or inserts that maintain a constant gas flow while trapping solids.
[B01D 46/005] Crossflow filtration, i.e. having an inlet and two outlets -- Crossflow-filtration is a separation technique in which the feed gas or vapor enters a filter and flows tangentially across the filter surface, creating two separate streams: a permeate that passes through the filter and a retentate that continues along the surface. This arrangement enables continuous removal of dispersed particles while maintaining high-flow rates and reduces filter clogging.
[B01D 46/0052] with filtering elements moving during filtering operation  B01D46/22 ,  B01D46/32  take precedence -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Filters having travelling belts -> B01D46/22 - Loose filtering material moving during filtration -> B01D46/32
[B01D 46/0053] with vibrating filtering elements -- Filters that incorporate vibrating elements to enhance the separation of dispersed particles from gases or vapours, where the vibration assists in dislodging and moving trapped particles through the filter medium, improving efficiency and preventing clogging.
[B01D 46/0054] with translational movement -- Filters or filtering processes designed to separate dispersed particles from gases or vapours in which the filter medium or the particle-laden stream moves linearly relative to each other, providing translational motion to enhance separation efficiency. This subclass falls under separating operations for gas-phase particle removal.
[B01D 46/0056] with rotational movement -- A filtration process in which the separating element or the gas stream is given a rotational motion to improve removal of dispersed particles from gases or vapours. This includes rotating filter discs, drums, or cyclonic devices that use spin to enhance particle capture.
[B01D 46/0084] provided with safety means -- A filter or filtering process that has been specially modified to separate dispersed particles from gases or vapours and includes safety features such as protective devices or fail-safe mechanisms to prevent hazardous release or equipment damage.
[B01D 46/0086] Filter condition indicators -- B01D 46/0086 covers devices or methods that monitor or signal the condition of filters used to separate dispersed particles from gases or vapours, such as pressure-drop gauges, visual or electronic clogging indicators, or other diagnostic elements that show filter performance or need for replacement.
[B01D 46/0087] Bypass or safety valves -- Bypass or safety valves are devices incorporated into filtration systems that divert flow or release pressure when a filter becomes clogged or overloaded, allowing continued operation and protecting downstream equipment. They are used in processes that separate dispersed particles from gases or vapours, ensuring safety and maintaining performance.
[B01D 46/0089] Anti-return means -- Anti-return means are devices incorporated in filtration systems to prevent backflow of particles or gas, ensuring that separated solid or liquid particles cannot re-enter the upstream side of the filter. These features are used in specialized filters that separate dispersed particles from gases or vapours, improving efficiency and safety.
[B01D 46/009] Identification of filter type or position thereof, e.g. by transponders or bar codes -- The entry B01D 46/009 relates to systems that identify the type of filter or its position within a filtration arrangement, using identification means such as transponders, RFID tags, bar-codes or similar markers. It is applied to filters specially adapted for separating dispersed particles from gases or vapours, enabling automated tracking, maintenance scheduling or performance monitoring.
[B01D 46/0091] Including arrangements for environmental or personal protection -- B01D 46/0091 covers filters or filtering processes specially modified for separating dispersed particles from gases or vapours, including arrangements for environmental-or-personal protection.
[B01D 46/0093] against fire or explosion -- This classification covers filters or filtering processes specially adapted to separate dispersed particles from gases or vapours and designed to prevent fire or explosion. It sits within the hierarchy of performing operations, separating and separation, and is identified by the code B01D 46/0093.
[B01D 46/0094] against radiation -- B01D 46/0094 covers filters and filtering methods specially adapted to operate in radiation environments, enabling the separation of dispersed particles from gases or vapours while resisting ionising radiation damage. These technologies are used in nuclear facilities, radiological protection equipment, and space applications where radiation exposure is significant.
[B01D 46/0095] Means acting upon failure of the filtering system, e.g. in case of damage of the filter elements; Failsafes -- B01D 46/0095 covers devices or mechanisms that respond when a filter element is damaged or fails, such as automatic shut-off valves, pressure-relief triggers, or other safety systems that prevent the release of unfiltered gases or vapours. It belongs to the separation subgroup for filters specially modified for separating dispersed particles from gases or vapours.
[B01D 46/0097] Special means for preventing bypass around the filter, i.e. in addition to usual seals -- B01D 46/0097 covers special devices that stop gas or vapor streams from flowing around a filter, such as additional seals, gaskets or structural features that complement standard sealing methods. These means are used in filtration systems designed to separate dispersed particles from gases or vapors.
[B01D 46/0098] Protecting coverages on the filter which is removed before the filter is used, protection of filter, packaging -- The entry B01D 46/0098 covers protective coverings, films or packaging applied to gas- or vapour-filter elements that are removed before the filter is put into service. It addresses ways to shield the filter media during storage, transport or handling to prevent contamination or damage.
[B01D 46/02] Particle separators, e.g. dust precipitators, having hollow filters made of flexible material -- B01D 46/02 covers particle separators such as dust precipitators that employ hollow filters made of flexible material to capture solid particles from gases or vapours. The flexible hollow filter structure provides a large surface area and can be shaped to fit diverse flow paths, enhancing collection efficiency in industrial air-cleaning and emission-control systems.
[B01D 46/023] Pockets filters, i.e. multiple bag filters mounted on a common frame -- Pocket filters are assemblies of multiple bag-style filter elements mounted on a common frame, enabling simultaneous separation of dispersed particles from gases or vapours. The arrangement provides a high filtration surface area while keeping the unit compact and allowing easy element replacement.
[B01D 46/026] Means for maintaining a space between filters, e.g. avoiding contact between adjacent filters -- The classification B01D 46/026 covers arrangements that keep a defined clearance between adjacent filter elements used in gas or vapor separation, preventing direct contact and allowing uninterrupted flow. Such means may include spacers, frames or supports that maintain the gap and reduce pressure loss.
[B01D 46/04] Cleaning filters -- B01D 46/04 covers cleaning filters specially adapted to separate dispersed particles from gases or vapours. It includes filters that can be regenerated, back-flushed or otherwise cleaned during operation to maintain performance in air-handling, ventilation or industrial gas-purification systems.
[B01D 46/06] with means keeping the working surfaces flat -- B01D 46/06 covers filtering or filtration methods that are specially adapted to separate dispersed particles from gases or vapours, incorporating features that keep the filter's working surfaces flat to maintain uniform flow and effectiveness. [B01D 46/08] the working surfaces forming a star shape -- This subclass covers filters whose internal working surfaces are formed in a star shape to improve separation of dispersed particles from gases or vapours. The star-shaped ribs or chambers create turbulence and increase surface area, enhancing particle capture during the filtering process.
[B01D 46/10] Particle separators, e.g. dust precipitators, using filter plates, sheets or pads having plane surfaces -- B01D 46/10 in the Cooperative Patent Classification covers particle separators such as dust precipitators that utilize filter plates, sheets, or pads with plane surfaces for removing dispersed particles from gases or vapours. This classification is specific to devices where the filtering media has a flat surface design.
[B01D 46/103] Curved filtering elements -- This subclass covers filtering devices that incorporate curved elements, such as warped or arched filter media, designed to separate particles from gases or vapors. The curvature may improve flow distribution or collection efficiency.
[B01D 46/106] Ring-shaped filtering elements -- Ring-shaped filtering elements are specially designed filter components used to separate dispersed particles from gases or vapours. They are employed where conventional filters are adapted for improved separation performance.
[B01D 46/12] in multiple arrangements -- B01D 46/12 covers filters or filtering processes that are specially adapted for separating dispersed particles from gases or vapours when the filtering elements are employed in more than one arrangement, such as series, parallel or hybrid configurations. [B01D 46/121] V-type arrangements -- A V-type arrangement refers to a filter or filtering process where the inlet and outlet channels are configured in a V-shaped geometry to enhance separation of dispersed particles from gases or vapours. The shape creates a converging flow that promotes particle deposition on the interior surfaces, improving efficiency compared with straight-through designs. This subclass falls under B01D 46/121 within the Cooperative Patent Classification system.
[B01D 46/14] arranged in a star shape -- This subclass covers filters arranged in a star shape that are specially modified for separating dispersed particles from gases or vapours. It falls under CPC B01D 46/14 within the separating operations hierarchy.

[B01D 46/18] Particle separators, e.g. dust precipitators, using filtering belts -- B01D 46/18 covers particle separators that employ moving filtering belts, such as dust precipitators, to remove solid or liquid particles from gases or vapours. The classification applies to filtering processes specially adapted for separating dispersed particles, where the belt acts as a continuous filter medium.
[B01D 46/185] Construction of filtering belts or supporting belts including devices for centering, mounting or sealing thereof -- This subclass deals with the construction of filter belts and their supporting belts, emphasizing features that centre, mount or seal the belts within filtration equipment used to separate dispersed particles from gases or vapours.
[B01D 46/20] the belts combined with drums -- This subcategory refers to filter arrangements where a moving belt is wrapped around a drum, the belt-drum combination serving to capture and transport dispersed particles from a gas or vapour stream. The design integrates the belt and drum to improve separation efficiency in filtration processes.
[B01D 46/22] the belts travelling during filtering -- This subclass deals with the moving belts that form part of a filter apparatus, where the belt itself travels to convey or collect particles separated from a gas or vapor stream. It includes designs of belt-driven filtration units and belt-driven cleaning mechanisms.
[B01D 46/24] Particle separators, e.g. dust precipitators, using rigid hollow filter bodies -- Particle separators that employ rigid hollow filter bodies, such as dust precipitators, are designed to remove dispersed solid particles from gases or vapours. The rigid, hollow structure functions as a filter medium through which the gas stream passes, allowing particles to be captured while the cleaned gas continues onward. This technology is classified under B01D 46/24 in the Cooperative Patent Classification for separating operations.
[B01D 46/2403] characterised by the physical shape or structure of the filtering element -- B01D 46/2403 covers filters or filtering processes for gases or vapours that are distinguished specifically by the physical shape or internal structure of the filtering element, such as mesh geometry, corrugation, pleating, or other form-defining features that affect particle separation.
[B01D 46/2407] Filter candles -- Filter candles are cylindrical filtering devices, often resembling candles, that contain a porous medium for removing dispersed solid particles from gases or vapours. [B01D 46/2418] Honeycomb filters  used for filtering exhaust gases of an internal combustion engine  F01N3/022 ; ceramic honeycomb structures  per se   C04B38/0006 -- Honeycomb filters, typically ceramic structures, are employed to clean exhaust gases from internal-combustion engines by trapping dispersed particles. [B01D 46/2425] characterized by parameters related to the physical properties of the honeycomb structure material -- The B01D 46/2425 subclass covers filters or filtering processes that are specially adapted to separate dispersed particles from gases or vapours and are defined by the physical-property parameters of a honeycomb-structured material, such as cell geometry, porosity, density or thermal conductivity, which influence the separation performance.

[B01D 46/24492] Pore diameter -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Specific pore diameter with numerical values, distribution of pores, pore diameter in relationship with other parameters.  **Glossary:** - Pore diameter: The greatest distance between any two points on the boundary of the inside or outside of cylindrical and non-cylindrical openings in a filter
[B01D 46/24493] Modulus of rupture -- This subclass deals with the determination or specification of the modulus of rupture-the breaking strength-of filter media that are specially modified for separating dispersed particles from gases or vapours. [B01D 46/2451] characterized by the geometrical structure, shape, pattern or configuration or parameters related to the geometry of the structure -- This subclass covers filters or filtering processes for removing dispersed particles from gases or vapours where the defining feature is the geometrical structure, shape, pattern, configuration or related dimensional parameters of the filter. [B01D 46/2462] the outer peripheral sealing -- The entry B01D 46/2462 covers the outer peripheral sealing of filters or filtering devices that are specially modified for separating dispersed particles from gases or vapours.
