It includes methods and apparatus where such unlisted organic substances serve as absorbents, solvents, or carriers to achieve selective removal or purification of gaseous components.
[B01D 53/73] After-treatment of removed components -- The CPC subclass B01D 53/73 covers processes applied to gases or vapours after they have been separated from a mixture, including cleaning, drying, removal of residual contaminants, and other conditioning steps to prepare the removed components for reuse, disposal or further processing.
[B01D 53/74] General processes for purification of waste gases -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Chemical or biological purification of engine exhaust gases -> B01D53/92
[B01D 53/75] Multi-step processes -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Processes comprising a sequence of different treatment steps. The individual steps as such do not represent the inventive concept.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - A catalytic treatment with more than one catalytic step -> B01D53/869
[B01D 53/76] Gas phase processes, e.g. by using aerosols -- B01D 53/76 covers gas-phase processes used to separate gases or vapours, typically employing aerosols or particulate suspensions as the medium. The entry includes methods where a gas mixture is contacted with droplets or particles to extract the desired components.
[B01D 53/77] Liquid phase processes -- This subclass of the Cooperative Patent Classification (CPC) under B01D 53/77 covers liquid-phase techniques for separating gases or vapours, including absorption, stripping, scrubbing and related processes that use a liquid medium to achieve the separation. It is part of the broader hierarchy of performing operations, separating, and separation of gases or vapours.
[B01D 53/78] with gas-liquid contact -- B01D 53/78 covers methods for separating gases or vapours by bringing them into contact with a liquid, such as absorption, scrubbing, or solvent-based extraction. It includes apparatuses and processes where the gas phase dissolves or reacts in the liquid to achieve purification or removal of components. This entry falls under Performing Operations, Separating, Separation of gases or vapours.
[B01D 53/79] Injecting reactants -- B01D 53/79 covers processes in which reactants are injected into a gas-or-vapor stream to achieve separation of the components. It falls under the broader category of separation of gases or vapours within the performing operations section of the CPC.
[B01D 53/80] Semi-solid phase processes, i.e. by using slurries -- B01D 53/80 covers processes that separate gases or vapours by employing a semi-solid phase, typically a slurry, as the medium for the separation. The method uses a dispersed solid in a liquid to capture, dissolve or react with the target gas, allowing its removal from a mixture. It is applied where conventional liquid-only or solid-only techniques are unsuitable.
[B01D 53/81] Solid phase processes -- B01D 53/81 covers solid-phase techniques for separating gases or vapours, including processes that use adsorbents, solid sorbents or porous materials to capture, retain or release gaseous components. It encompasses methods such as adsorption, solid-phase extraction and membrane-based separation where the active medium is a solid rather than a liquid. This subclass falls under Performing Operations → Separating → Separation of gases or vapours.
[B01D 53/82] with stationary reactants -- B01D 53/82 covers processes for separating gases or vapours in which one or more reactants are fixed in place, such as on a solid surface or within a membrane, while the gas mixture flows past them. This subclass falls under the broader separation of gases or vapours category within the Performing Operations group.
[B01D 53/83] with moving reactants -- This subclass covers methods for separating gases or vapours in which the reactants are in motion, such as flow-through, moving-bed, or moving-stream techniques. It includes processes that rely on the relative movement of the gas or vapour phase and the separating medium to achieve separation.
[B01D 53/84] Biological processes -- **Definition:** This place covers:  All biological concepts except those using solid bed reactors.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Concepts with solid bed reactor -> B01D53/85
[B01D 53/85] with gas-solid contact -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Concepts with solid bed reactor.
[B01D 53/86] Catalytic processes -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Catalytic concepts for engine exhaust gases -> B01D53/94 - B01D53/9495
[B01D 53/8603] Removing sulfur compounds -- A process or apparatus that extracts sulfur-containing gases or vapours-such as hydrogen sulfide, sulfur dioxide or mercaptans-from a gas stream, typically by absorption, adsorption, oxidation, catalytic conversion or membrane separation, thereby reducing sulfur content in the treated gas.
[B01D 53/8606] only one sulfur compound other than sulfur oxides or hydrogen sulfide -- This CPC subclass B01D 53/8606 covers processes for separating gases or vapours where the feed contains only a single sulfur compound that is not a sulfur oxide or hydrogen sulfide. It is situated under the separation of gases or vapours category in the performing operations hierarchy.

It falls under the broader category of gas-vapor separation within the performing operations hierarchy.

It falls under the CPC group for separating gases or vapours, addressing methods such as absorption, adsorption, membrane or cryogenic techniques to isolate or remove these components.
[B01D 53/8621] Removing nitrogen compounds -- This CPC subgroup concerns processes for eliminating nitrogen-containing substances such as ammonia, nitrogen oxides or nitriles from gas or vapor mixtures. Techniques may include adsorption on solid sorbents, chemical absorption, membrane separation, cryogenic condensation or catalytic conversion. The methods are applied in air purification, flue-gas treatment or chemical-process streams.

[B01D 53/8668] Removing organic compounds not provided for in  B01D53/8603  -  B01D53/8665 -- B01D 53/8668 covers processes for separating gases or vapours in which organic compounds that are not covered by the preceding groups B01D 53/8603 to B01D 53/8665 are removed from the gas mixture. [B01D 53/8671] Removing components of defined structure not provided for in  B01D53/8603  -  B01D53/8668 -- B01D 53/8671 covers methods for separating gases or vapours in which specific components having a defined molecular structure are removed, but the technique is not described in the earlier sub-groups B01D 53/8603 to B01D 53/8668. It addresses removal approaches that fall outside those more narrowly defined separation processes.
[B01D 53/8675] Ozone -- The CPC class B01D 53/8675 covers technologies dealing with ozone as a separable gas or vapour, including its generation (typically by electrical discharge), purification, storage, and use in industrial or environmental processes. It encompasses apparatuses and methods for producing, handling, or applying ozone in applications such as disinfection, oxidation, or chemical synthesis.
[B01D 53/8678] Removing components of undefined structure -- This CPC subclass covers processes for separating gases or vapours in which one or more components have no specific, defined chemical structure. It includes methods that remove such undefined or broadly described substances from gas streams, typically using physical or chemical separation techniques.
[B01D 53/8681] Acidic components  B01D53/8687  takes precedence -- B01D 53/8681 covers processes for separating gases or vapours that involve acidic components, such as using acids to aid in the removal or capture of specific gas constituents. The earlier subgroup B01D 53/8687 on acidic components takes precedence over this entry.

[B01D 53/9472] in different zones -- A CPC subgroup for separating gases or vapours by directing them into distinct spatial zones, such as compartmented reactors or staged flow paths, where each zone provides different conditions that enable selective separation of the components. This approach is used to achieve fractionation or purification without mixing the zones.
[B01D 53/9477] with catalysts positioned on separate bricks, e.g. exhaust systems -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Exhaust systems where a specific sequence of catalytic functions is given, for example DOC-DPF or LNT-SCR. It also covers exhaust systems where the DPF is not coated.
[B01D 53/9481] Catalyst preceded by an adsorption device without catalytic function for temporary storage of contaminants, e.g. during cold start -- This subclass concerns gas or vapour separation processes in which an adsorption device without catalytic function is placed before the catalyst, allowing temporary storage of contaminants such as during a cold-start condition. The arrangement enables the adsorber to capture impurities before the catalyst becomes active, improving overall separation efficiency.
[B01D 53/9486] for storing hydrocarbons -- The CPC entry B01D 53/9486 falls under Performing Operations → Separating → Separation of gases or vapours and covers technologies for storing hydrocarbons during or after a gas-vapor separation process, including apparatus or methods that retain the separated hydrocarbon phase for later use.
[B01D 53/949] for storing sulfur oxides -- Subclass B01D 53/949 relates to equipment or methods used to store sulfur oxides in processes that separate gases or vapours. It falls under the broader category of performing operations for separating gases, addressing the handling and containment of SO₂ and related oxides after separation.
[B01D 53/9495] Controlling the catalytic process -- This class covers methods and apparatus for regulating catalytic reactions during the separation of gases or vapours, such as adjusting temperature, pressure, flow or catalyst activity to optimise separation efficiency. It applies to processes that use a catalyst to facilitate selective gas-vapour separation.
[B01D 53/96] Regeneration, reactivation or recycling of reactants -- B01D 53/96 in the Cooperative Patent Classification pertains to processes for regenerating, reactivating, or recycling reactants used in the separation of gases or vapours. This classification is specific to methods that aim to restore or reuse materials involved in gas separation techniques.
[B01D 53/965] including an electrochemical process step -- B01D 53/965 covers methods for separating gases or vapours that incorporate an electrochemical step, such as electrodialysis, electro-reduction or electro-oxidation, to selectively transport or transform components. The classification includes processes where electrical energy drives the separation, often in combination with other physical techniques, and is used for producing purified gases, removing contaminants, or recovering valuable substances from gas mixtures.
[B01D 57/00] Separation, other than separation of solids, not fully covered by a single other group or subclass, e.g. -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Any process for separation not fully covered by any of the other groups in B01D provided that it is not related to the separation of solid particles.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Separation by distillation -> B01D3/00 - Separation by extraction -> B01D11/00 - Separation by sorption -> B01D15/00 - Separation of liquids from liquids -> B01D17/00 - Degasification of liquids -> B01D19/00 - Dead end filtration processes -> B01D37/00 - Gas separation -> B01D53/00 - Membrane separation processes -> B01D61/00
[B01D 57/02] by electrophoresis -- **Definition:** This place covers:  B01D57/02 includes only processes involving migration of species due to an electrical potential in the absence of any kind of separation membrane. Otherwise the respective subgroups of B01D61/42 apply. The class is given for preparative purposes only (opposed to investigation or analysis purposes).
[B01D 59/00] Separation of different isotopes of the same chemical element -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The separation of isotopes of the SAME element. A starting compound comprising several isotopes of the same element is separated in at least two fractions wherein each fraction is enriched/depleated in a least one of the isotopes in comparison with the starting material and with the other fraction(s). This implies that the difference in isotopes ratios is not only due to radioactive decay  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - The preparation of nuclides, (e.g. for medical purposes) from other elements (e. g. preparation of 99Tc from 99Mo and their separation from the mother solution (when these are not separated from other isotopes of the same element (otherwise B01D59/00 ). -> G21G - Mass spectrometers. -> H01J49/00
[B01D 59/02] Separation by phase transition -- The B01D 59/02 subclass covers techniques that separate isotopes of the same element by exploiting a phase transition, such as liquid-to-gas, solid-to-liquid or solid-to-gas changes. Methods may include fractional distillation, sublimation, crystallisation or other processes where isotopic differences affect the transition conditions, allowing the isotopes to be collected in separate phases. This approach falls under the broader category of separating operations for isotope separation.
[B01D 59/04] by distillation -- This subclass deals with separating isotopes of the same chemical element by employing distillation, using differences in boiling points or vapor pressures to enrich one isotope over another. It is situated under the CPC hierarchy of performing operations, separating, separation, and isotope separation.
[B01D 59/06] by fractional melting -- This subclass covers methods for separating isotopes of the same element using fractional melting, where a melt is partially solidified and the solid and liquid phases have different isotope compositions, allowing enrichment of one isotope in the melt or residue. It falls under B01D 59, separation of isotopes.
[B01D 59/08] by fractional crystallisation, by precipitation, by zone freezing -- Methods for isolating isotopes of a single element using physical-chemical techniques such as fractional crystallisation, selective precipitation, or zone-freezing, wherein the isotopic composition is altered by controlled solid-liquid transitions to achieve enrichment or purification. These processes exploit differences in solubility, crystal growth rates, or melting behavior between isotopic species.
[B01D 59/10] Separation by diffusion -- This CPC subclass B01D 59/10 covers methods that separate isotopes of the same element by exploiting differences in their diffusion rates, typically using gaseous or liquid media where lighter isotopes diffuse faster than heavier ones.
[B01D 59/12] by diffusion through barriers -- This subclass covers methods for separating isotopes of a single chemical element by diffusion through barriers such as membranes, porous walls or other selective layers, exploiting differences in mass or kinetic behavior to achieve enrichment. It is nested under performing operations, separating, separation and isotope separation.
[B01D 59/14] Construction of the barrier -- The code B01D 59/14 covers the construction of physical barriers such as membranes or walls that are employed to separate isotopes of the same element. It is part of the CPC hierarchy under Performing Operations, Separating, Separation of different isotopes of the same chemical element.
[B01D 59/16] by thermal diffusion -- The B01D 59/16 subclass covers processes that separate different isotopes of the same chemical element by means of thermal diffusion, using a temperature gradient to drive isotopic migration. This method falls under the broader CPC hierarchy of Performing Operations → Separating → Separation.
[B01D 59/18] by separation jets -- The B01D 59/18 subclass covers processes that separate isotopes of a single element by means of high-velocity separation jets. It falls under the broader categories of performing operations, separating, and separation of different isotopes, and specifies the use of jet streams to achieve isotopic enrichment.
[B01D 59/20] Separation by centrifuging -- B01D 59/20 covers methods for separating isotopes of the same chemical element by centrifugation. It is classified under the broader categories of performing operations, separating and separation in the cooperative patent classification system.
[B01D 59/22] Separation by extracting -- A method for isolating one isotope of an element from a mixture by removing it into a different phase or medium, typically using solvents, ion-exchange resins, or gaseous carriers. The process relies on differences in chemical or physical behavior to extract the target isotope while leaving the others behind. It is a sub-category of isotope separation using extraction techniques.
[B01D 59/24] by solvent extraction -- The CPC subgroup B01D 59/24 covers methods that separate isotopes of the same element by using a solvent-extraction process, where a liquid phase preferentially extracts one isotope based on subtle chemical or physical differences. It falls under the broader hierarchy of performing operations, separating, and finally separation of different isotopes.
[B01D 59/26] by sorption, i.e. absorption, adsorption, persorption -- The B01D 59/26 subclass covers processes that separate isotopes of the same chemical element using sorption methods such as absorption, adsorption, or persorption, where isotopes are selectively retained on a solid or liquid medium and later recovered. This approach falls under the broader categories of performing operations, separating, and separation of isotopes.
[B01D 59/28] Separation by chemical exchange -- B01D 59/28 relates to chemical-exchange methods for separating isotopes of the same element, where isotopic atoms are transferred between different chemical compounds to achieve enrichment or depletion. The process exploits slight differences in chemical behavior to achieve the desired separation.
[B01D 59/30] by ion exchange -- B01D 59/30 pertains to the separation of isotopes of a single chemical element using ion-exchange methods. It falls under the CPC hierarchy Performing Operations → Separating → Separation and addresses processes that exploit differences in ionic behavior to isolate isotopic species.
[B01D 59/32] by exchange between fluids -- B01D 59/32 covers methods for separating isotopes of the same element using an exchange process between two fluid phases, where the isotopic species migrate from one fluid to the other to achieve enrichment or depletion. This technique falls under the broader category of separating operations that involve isotope separation.
[B01D 59/33] involving dual temperature exchange -- B01D 59/33 covers methods for separating isotopes of the same chemical element that employ a dual-temperature exchange mechanism. The process uses differing temperatures to create a favorable isotopic exchange gradient, enabling selective enrichment or depletion of one isotope relative to another within a single element.
[B01D 59/34] Separation by photochemical methods -- B01D 59/34 covers techniques that use light-induced chemical reactions to separate isotopes of a single element. Methods include photolysis, photo-induced exchange or selective excitation to achieve isotopic enrichment. It is nested under performing operations, separating, and separation of isotopes.
[B01D 59/36] Separation by biological methods -- B01D 59/36 covers methods that use biological agents such as microorganisms, enzymes, or biomolecules to separate isotopes of the same chemical element. These techniques rely on biological selectivity or metabolic processes to achieve isotopic enrichment or depletion.
[B01D 59/38] Separation by electrochemical methods -- B01D 59/38 covers electrochemical methods - such as electric-field driven cells or electrolytic processes - used to separate isotopes of a single chemical element, providing enrichment or depletion for nuclear, analytical or industrial purposes.
[B01D 59/40] by electrolysis -- This CPC subclass covers methods that separate isotopes of a single chemical element by electrolysis, classified under B01D 59/40. It falls within the hierarchy of performing operations, separating, and separation.
[B01D 59/42] by electromigration -- The subclass covers methods that achieve isotope separation by applying an electric field that causes differential migration of isotopes through a medium. It is used in processes such as isotope enrichment for nuclear or analytical purposes, where isotopic species move at different velocities due to mass-dependent electrophoretic behavior.
[B01D 59/44] Separation by mass spectrography -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Processes for separating isotopes by using mass spectrometers.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Mass spectrometers -> H01J49/00
[B01D 59/46] using only electrostatic fields -- This subclass covers methods for separating isotopes of the same element by applying only electrostatic fields. The technique relies on differences in ionic mobility or charge-to-mass ratios to achieve isotope separation without magnetic or mechanical forces.
[B01D 59/48] using electrostatic and magnetic fields -- B01D 59/48 covers methods that separate isotopes of a single chemical element by applying electrostatic and magnetic fields to exploit differences in mass or charge-to-mass ratios, enabling isotope enrichment without chemical reactions. It falls under the broader categories of performing operations, separating, and separation.
[B01D 59/50] Separation involving two or more processes covered by different groups selected from groups -- The B01D 59/50 subclass covers methods that combine two or more distinct separation techniques, each belonging to different groups within the CPC, to achieve separation of isotopes of the same element. It addresses hybrid processes where, for example, an initial physical step is followed by a chemical or electromagnetic step, allowing more efficient or selective isotope enrichment.
[B01D 61/00] Processes of separation using semi-permeable membranes, e.g. dialysis, osmosis, or ultrafiltration -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Processes for separation of liquid feed by means of a membrane in general which selectively allows passage of components from liquid feed mixtures including suspensions of particles, heterogeneous or homogeneous mixtures.  Apparatus specifically used in the processes are also classified here.  These types of membrane processes include:  For microfiltration processes only cross-flow processes, i.e. applying a tangential flow of the feed solution over the membrane) are considered.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Processes for separation of gases or vapour by means of membranes -> B01D53/22
[B01D 61/002] Forward osmosis or direct osmosis -- Forward osmosis, also called direct osmosis, is a membrane separation process in which water moves across a semi-permeable membrane from a low-solute feed solution to a higher-solute draw solution driven by the natural osmotic pressure gradient. It is used for water purification, concentration, or energy generation without external pressure. The method falls under CPC B01D 61/002 for membrane-based separation.
[B01D 61/0021] comprising multiple forward osmosis steps -- Processes that separate substances by passing a solution through a semi-permeable membrane in two or more successive forward-osmosis stages, using the natural osmotic pressure gradient to draw water across the membrane. The multiple steps enhance water flux or solute concentration beyond a single-stage system.
[B01D 61/0022] Apparatus therefor -- B01D 61/0022 pertains to apparatus for processes of separation using semi-permeable membranes, such as dialysis, osmosis, or ultrafiltration. This classification covers the design and construction of devices that facilitate these membrane-based separation techniques.
[B01D 61/0023] Accessories; Auxiliary operations -- The subclass B01D 61/0023 concerns auxiliary devices and accessories that support membrane-based separation processes such as dialysis, osmosis or ultrafiltration. It includes items such as supports, spacers, holders, and other supplemental components that facilitate the operation of semi-permeable membranes.
[B01D 61/0024] Controlling or regulating -- The CPC class B01D 61/0024 covers techniques for controlling or regulating semi-permeable-membrane separation processes such as dialysis, osmosis or ultrafiltration. It includes methods that adjust pressure, temperature, concentration gradients, flow rates or membrane potential to optimise separation efficiency and product quality.
[B01D 61/005] Osmotic agents; Draw solutions -- This subclass covers chemicals that generate an osmotic pressure gradient across a semi-permeable membrane, enabling water or solvent to be drawn through the membrane in processes such as dialysis, reverse osmosis, or ultrafiltration. It includes salts, polymers and other draw-solution agents that create the required concentration difference.
[B01D 61/007] Separation by stereostructure, steric separation -- The CPC entry B01D 61/007 covers separation techniques that exploit differences in the three-dimensional shape or steric hindrance of molecules. It includes methods such as chiral or size-exclusion selectors that distinguish components by their stereostructure, often used in chromatography, membrane filtration, or related processes.
[B01D 61/02] Reverse osmosis -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Pressure-driven membrane processes for separation with membranes relating to reverse osmosis, nanofiltration and hyperfiltration.
[B01D 61/025] Reverse osmosis; Hyperfiltration -- B01D 61/025 covers the use of semi-permeable membranes to separate solutes from liquids by applying pressure greater than the natural osmotic pressure, i.e., reverse osmosis and hyperfiltration. These processes force water through a membrane, retaining dissolved substances and producing purified permeate.
[B01D 61/026] comprising multiple reverse osmosis steps -- A process that uses two or more consecutive reverse-osmosis stages to separate components, typically to achieve higher purity or greater concentration of solutes by forcing feed fluid through successive semi-permeable membranes under pressure. It belongs to membrane-based separation operations.
[B01D 61/027] Nanofiltration -- This subclass covers nanofiltration processes, a type of membrane separation that removes particles in the nanometer range, typically employing semi-permeable membranes to retain solutes while allowing water and small ions to pass. It falls under broader categories of separation, separating, and performing operations.
[B01D 61/0271] comprising multiple nanofiltration steps -- This subclass covers membrane-based separation methods, including dialysis, osmosis or ultrafiltration, that are carried out in more than one nanofiltration stage to achieve finer or sequential separation of components. It is used for processes where multiple nanofiltration steps are integrated into a single operation.
[B01D 61/029] Multistep processes comprising different kinds of membrane processes selected from reverse osmosis, hyperfiltration or nanofiltration -- The subclass B01D 61/029 describes multistep separation processes that integrate different membrane operations-specifically reverse osmosis, hyperfiltration or nanofiltration-within a single sequence to achieve a desired separation.
[B01D 61/04] Feed pretreatment -- When classifying in this group, the type of pretreatment must be specified with the indexing codes of groups B01D2311/04 and B01D2311/10 - B01D2311/2696 .
[B01D 61/06] Energy recovery -- The B01D 61/06 subclass concerns methods and apparatus that reclaim energy while performing membrane-based separations such as dialysis, osmosis or ultrafiltration. It includes techniques that capture pressure, thermal or electrochemical energy generated by concentration or pressure differentials and feed it back into the process or an external system.
[B01D 61/08] Apparatus therefor -- Devices for carrying out membrane-based separation methods such as dialysis, osmosis or ultrafiltration, including the housing, modules, supports, flow channels and ancillary components that enable a semi-permeable membrane to separate substances in a liquid or gas stream.
[B01D 61/081] used at home, e.g. kitchen -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Apparatuses which are compact and suitable for private use, for instance household applications.
[B01D 61/10] Accessories -- B01D 61/10 covers accessories for membrane-based separation processes such as dialysis, osmosis or ultrafiltration, including support structures, housings, connectors and other peripheral devices that facilitate the operation of the semipermeable membrane system.
[B01D 61/12] Controlling or regulating -- B01D 61/12 covers methods for controlling or regulating separation processes that employ semi-permeable membranes such as dialysis, osmosis or ultrafiltration, including the adjustment of operating parameters like pressure, temperature, flow rate or membrane characteristics to achieve desired separation performance.
[B01D 61/14] Ultrafiltration -- B01D 61/14 pertains to ultrafiltration, a membrane-based separation process that removes particles and macromolecules from liquids by forcing the feed through a semi-permeable membrane with a defined molecular weight cut-off. It is employed in water treatment, biotechnology, food processing and similar fields to concentrate or purify solutions while allowing solvents and small solutes to pass.
[B01D 61/145] Ultrafiltration -- The B01D 61/145 entry covers ultrafiltration, a membrane-based separation technique that employs semi-permeable filters to retain larger molecules while allowing smaller species to pass, commonly used for concentrating proteins, removing particulates, and purifying liquids in chemical and biomedical processes.
[B01D 61/146] comprising multiple ultrafiltration steps -- This CPC subclass covers separation processes that employ semi-permeable membranes and involve two or more ultrafiltration stages. It includes methods where a feed stream passes sequentially through ultrafiltration membranes to progressively retain larger particles or molecules while allowing smaller species to pass, improving purification or concentration efficiency.
[B01D 61/147] Microfiltration -- The B01D61/147 microfiltration entry covers processes that employ semi-permeable membranes with pore sizes typically 0.1-10 µm to separate suspended particles, bacteria, or macromolecules from fluids. It is used in water treatment, beverage clarification, and biotechnology for removing larger contaminants while allowing smaller molecules to pass.
[B01D 61/1471] comprising multiple microfiltration steps -- A process in which a fluid is passed through two or more consecutive microfiltration membranes to achieve higher removal efficiency or finer separation of suspended particles, falling within the semi-permeable membrane separation group of the CPC.
[B01D 61/149] Multistep processes comprising different kinds of membrane processes selected from ultrafiltration or microfiltration -- This CPC subclass deals with multistage separation sequences that integrate different membrane operations, such as ultrafiltration and microfiltration, within a single process flow. The methods may arrange the membrane steps sequentially or in parallel to achieve refined filtration or concentration of mixtures.
[B01D 61/16] Feed pretreatment -- In group B01D61/16 the type of pretreatment must be specified with the indexing codes B01D2311/04 and B01D2311/10 - B01D2311/2696 .
[B01D 61/18] Apparatus therefor -- B01D 61/18 covers apparatus designed for membrane-based separation processes such as dialysis, osmosis and ultrafiltration, including the modules, housings, supports and fluid handling components that enable the selective passage of substances through semi-permeable membranes.
[B01D 61/20] Accessories -- The entry B01D 61/20 in the CPC pertains to accessories used in membrane-based separation processes such as dialysis, osmosis or ultrafiltration. It includes items like holders, seals, connectors and other auxiliary components that facilitate or improve the operation of semi-permeable membrane equipment.
[B01D 61/22] Controlling or regulating -- The B01D 61/22 entry covers methods for controlling or regulating separation processes that use semi-permeable membranes such as dialysis, osmosis, or ultrafiltration. It includes techniques for adjusting operating parameters-pressure, temperature, concentration gradients, or flow rates-to maintain desired separation efficiency, selectivity, or product quality.
[B01D 61/24] Dialysis -- B01D 61/24 covers dialysis, a separation technique that uses a semi-permeable membrane to allow selective passage of solutes while retaining larger molecules, often applied in medical treatment and laboratory purification. It falls under the CPC hierarchy of Performing Operations, Separating, Separation.
[B01D 61/243] Dialysis -- B01D 61/243 defines dialysis, a membrane-based separation method where a semi-permeable barrier allows small solutes to pass while retaining larger molecules, enabling removal of waste from fluids such as blood or concentration of solutions. This subclass falls under the hierarchy of performing operations, separating, and separation.
[B01D 61/244] comprising multiple dialysis steps -- The code B01D 61/244 covers separation processes that use semi-permeable membranes such as dialysis, osmosis, or ultrafiltration, specifically when the method includes multiple dialysis steps. It is classified under Performing Operations to Separating to Separation.
[B01D 61/246] Membrane extraction -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Aspects relating specifically to the membrane separation process and its apparatus.
[B01D 61/2461] comprising multiple membrane extraction steps -- This subclass deals with separation processes that employ two or more consecutive membrane extraction steps, such as sequential dialysis, osmosis or ultrafiltration, to progressively isolate or concentrate components. It is used when a single membrane operation is insufficient to achieve the desired purity or selectivity.
[B01D 61/28] Apparatus therefor -- B01D 61/28 covers devices designed to carry out separation processes that employ semipermeable membranes such as dialysis, osmosis or ultrafiltration. The class includes apparatus that provide membrane support, flow control and pressure management needed for these membrane-based separations.
[B01D 61/30] Accessories -- Accessories are components that support or enhance membrane-based separation equipment, such as spacers, holders, seals, connectors and piping that enable the operation of dialysis, osmosis or ultrafiltration systems. They are not the membranes themselves but facilitate fluid flow, pressure distribution and assembly of the units.
[B01D 61/32] Controlling or regulating -- The entry covers methods for controlling or regulating membrane-based separation processes such as dialysis, osmosis and ultrafiltration, including techniques for adjusting pressure, flow rate, concentration, temperature or membrane characteristics to attain the desired separation performance.
[B01D 61/36] Pervaporation -- B01D 61/36 - Pervaporation relates to membrane-based separation where a liquid mixture is partially vaporized as it passes through a semi-permeable membrane, allowing selective removal of components such as water or solvents. The method combines permeation and evaporation and is used for dehydration, solvent recovery and purification of temperature-sensitive compounds.
[B01D 61/362] Pervaporation -- Pervaporation is a membrane separation technique in which a liquid feed contacts a semi-permeable membrane and the preferentially permeating component vaporizes on the low-pressure side, allowing selective removal of solvents or water. It combines aspects of dialysis, osmosis and ultrafiltration and is used for dehydration, organic solvent recovery and similar processes.
[B01D 61/3621] comprising multiple pervaporation steps -- B01D 61/3621 covers separation processes that employ two or more pervaporation stages, in which a semi-permeable membrane selectively transmits vapor-phase components to achieve component separation. The subclass falls under membrane-based separation methods such as dialysis, osmosis and ultrafiltration.
[B01D 61/363] Vapour permeation -- Vapour permeation is a membrane-based separation in which vapor molecules pass through a semi-permeable barrier while liquids or larger species are retained, enabling processes such as drying, solvent recovery and gas purification.
[B01D 61/3631] comprising multiple vapour permeation steps -- This subclass covers separation processes that employ semi-permeable membranes and involve more than one vapor permeation stage, such as multi-step dialysis, osmosis or ultrafiltration operations designed to enhance selective transport of components.
[B01D 61/364] Membrane distillation -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The process of membrane distillation and apparatus therefor.
[B01D 61/3641] comprising multiple membrane distillation steps -- The code covers separation methods that employ two or more membrane-distillation stages, where a semi-permeable membrane allows vapor to pass while retaining liquids, enabling concentration or purification of solutions such as desalinated water or concentrated chemicals. This approach builds on membrane-based separation techniques like dialysis, osmosis, or ultrafiltration.
[B01D 61/365] Osmotic distillation or osmotic evaporation -- Osmotic distillation or osmotic evaporation is a membrane-assisted separation process in which water or volatile components are transferred from a hot feed solution to a colder receiving stream through a semi-permeable membrane driven by an osmotic pressure difference, without phase change of the liquid. It is used for concentrating solutions, recovering water, and producing high-purity products while operating at relatively low temperatures.
[B01D 61/3651] comprising multiple osmotic distillation or evaporation steps -- This subclass deals with membrane-based separation methods that use two or more successive osmotic distillation or evaporation steps to improve solute removal or concentration, such as in dialysis, osmosis or ultrafiltration applications.
[B01D 61/366] Apparatus therefor -- B01D 61/366 denotes apparatus designed for membrane-based separation processes such as dialysis, osmosis or ultrafiltration. It includes any equipment that holds, supports or directs fluid flow through semi-permeable membranes, for example modules, housings, spacers and filtration cells.
[B01D 61/368] Accessories; Auxiliary operations -- This subclass covers devices, components and supporting steps used in membrane-based separation processes such as dialysis, osmosis and ultrafiltration, including fixtures, holders, seals, pumps and other ancillary equipment that facilitate the operation of the primary membrane unit.
[B01D 61/38] Liquid-membrane separation -- Liquid-membrane separation covers processes that use a semi-permeable membrane to divide liquids, allowing selective passage of solvents, solutes or particles. Techniques include dialysis, osmosis and ultrafiltration, where separation is achieved by differences in pressure, concentration or molecular size across the membrane.
[B01D 61/40] using emulsion-type membranes -- B01D 61/40 covers processes that employ emulsion-type membranes to achieve separation of components. The membranes consist of a dispersed phase within a continuous phase, providing selective transport similar to dialysis or ultrafiltration. This subclass falls under the broader category of separation using semi-permeable membranes.
[B01D 61/42] Electrodialysis -- Electrodialysis is a membrane separation process that uses an electric potential to drive ions through selective ion-exchange membranes, concentrating or desalinating solutions. It belongs to the CPC class B01D 61/42, under separation processes employing semi-permeable membranes such as dialysis, osmosis, or ultrafiltration.
[B01D 61/422] Electrodialysis -- Electrodialysis is a membrane-based separation method in which an electric field drives ions through semi-permeable membranes to achieve purification, desalination, or concentration of aqueous solutions. It falls under CPC B01D 61/422, covering processes that use electrophoretic migration across ion-exchange membranes for separation.
[B01D 61/423] comprising multiple electrodialysis steps -- This subclass covers membrane-based separation processes that employ more than one electrodialysis stage, such as sequential dialysis or ultrafiltration steps, to achieve the desired separation of ions or molecules. It is part of the broader group for semi-permeable membrane techniques under CPC B01D 61/423.
[B01D 61/425] Electro-ultrafiltration -- Electro-ultrafiltration is a membrane-based separation process that applies an electric field across a semipermeable ultrafiltration membrane to improve the removal of charged particles, colloids or macromolecules, often achieving higher flux, selectivity or fouling resistance compared with conventional ultrafiltration.
[B01D 61/4251] comprising multiple electro-ultrafiltration steps -- This subclass covers separation processes that use semi-permeable membranes in a series of electro-ultrafiltration stages, where an electric field is applied during ultrafiltration to improve the removal of charged particles or macromolecules. The multiple steps enhance selectivity and flux compared with a single-stage operation.
[B01D 61/427] Electro-osmosis -- Electro-osmosis is a membrane-based separation technique that uses an applied electric field to induce fluid flow through a semi-permeable membrane, allowing selective transport of ions or molecules. It is employed in processes such as dialysis, desalination, and ultrafiltration.
[B01D 61/4271] comprising multiple electro-osmosis steps -- This classification covers processes that separate substances by passing them through semi-permeable membranes and includes methods that employ more than one electro-osmosis step. It falls under the broader categories of performing operations, separating, and separation, and is designated B01D 61/4271 in the CPC.
[B01D 61/428] Membrane capacitive deionization -- Membrane capacitive deionisation is an electro-driven water treatment method that combines ion-exchange membranes with porous carbon electrodes to adsorb and remove dissolved ions when a voltage is applied, enabling selective desalination or decontamination. The process is classified under membrane-based separation techniques such as dialysis and ultrafiltration.
[B01D 61/4281] comprising multiple membrane capacitive deionization steps -- This CPC subclass covers processes that employ more than one membrane capacitive deionisation stage to separate dissolved ions from a fluid, often combined with semi-permeable membrane actions such as dialysis or ultrafiltration. It falls under B01D 61/428 dealing with separation using semi-permeable membranes.
[B01D 61/44] Ion-selective electrodialysis -- Ion-selective electrodialysis is a membrane separation process in which an electric field drives the movement of specific ions through ion-exchange membranes, allowing selective concentration or removal of target ions from a solution. The technique combines principles of electrodialysis with ion-selective membranes to achieve separation based on ion charge and size.
[B01D 61/445] with bipolar membranes; Water splitting -- This CPC subclass covers separation processes that employ bipolar membranes to split water, such as in electrodialysis or electrolysis, facilitating ion transport across semi-permeable barriers.
[B01D 61/46] Apparatus therefor -- This subclass covers apparatus designed to carry out separation processes that employ semi-permeable membranes, such as dialysis, osmosis or ultrafiltration. The devices enable selective passage of solvents or solutes across a membrane to achieve purification, concentration or selective removal of components.
[B01D 61/461] comprising only a single cell, only one anion or cation exchange membrane or one pair of anion and cation membranes -- This subclass covers membrane separation processes that use a single cell configuration with either a single ion-exchange membrane or a single pair of anion and cation exchange membranes, such as dialysis, osmosis or ultrafiltration.
[B01D 61/462] comprising the membrane sequence AA, where A is an anion exchange membrane -- B01D 61/462 defines separation methods that use a membrane formed by a repeated sequence AA, where each A is an anion-exchange membrane, applied in processes such as dialysis, osmosis or ultrafiltration.
[B01D 61/463] comprising the membrane sequence AC or CA, where C is a cation exchange membrane -- This subclass covers separation processes that use a membrane arrangement where a cation-exchange membrane (C) is placed adjacent to an anion-exchange membrane (A) in either the AC or CA order. The configuration is employed in dialysis, electrodialysis, or related membrane filtration methods to enhance ion selectivity.
[B01D 61/464] comprising the membrane sequence CC -- This CPC subclass covers separation processes that employ a semi-permeable membrane whose polymer includes a repeating carbon-carbon (CC) sequence, used in methods such as dialysis, osmosis or ultrafiltration.
[B01D 61/465] comprising the membrane sequence AB or BA, where B is a bipolar membrane -- The CPC entry B01D 61/465 falls under Performing Operations → Separating → Separation and covers processes that use semi-permeable membranes such as dialysis, osmosis or ultrafiltration, specifically when the membrane assembly follows the sequence AB or BA with B being a bipolar membrane.
[B01D 61/466] comprising the membrane sequence BC or CB -- B01D 61/466 pertains to separation methods that use semi-permeable membranes arranged in the sequence BC or CB. The classification applies to processes such as dialysis, osmosis or ultrafiltration where the specific ordering of membrane layers B and C is essential to the operation.
[B01D 61/467] comprising the membrane sequence BB -- This subclass covers separation processes that use semi-permeable membranes arranged in a membrane sequence identified as BB, encompassing techniques such as dialysis, osmosis and ultrafiltration. It focuses on the specific configuration of the BB membrane order to achieve selective transport or filtration of substances.
[B01D 61/468] comprising more than two electrodes -- The CPC subclass B01D 61/468 pertains to membrane-based separation processes such as dialysis, osmosis or ultrafiltration that are carried out in a system containing more than two electrodes, typically to apply an electric field that assists ion or solute transport across the semi-permeable membrane.
[B01D 61/48] having one or more compartments filled with ion-exchange material -- This subclass concerns separation processes that use semi-permeable membranes arranged in one or more compartments filled with ion-exchange material, such as dialysis, osmosis or ultrafiltration devices where the ion-exchange phase assists selective transport of ions or molecules.
[B01D 61/485] Specific features relating to the ion-exchange material -- This CPC subclass deals with the design and properties of ion-exchange materials employed in membrane-based separation methods such as dialysis, osmosis or ultrafiltration. It outlines features of the resin or polymer matrix that affect ion selectivity, capacity, stability or regeneration within the B01D 61/48 family of semi-permeable membrane processes.
[B01D 61/50] Stacks of the plate-and-frame type -- Stacks of the plate-and-frame type refer to membrane modules constructed from a series of flat plates separated by frames that hold a semi-permeable membrane, creating multiple parallel flow channels. These stacked assemblies are used in separation processes such as dialysis, osmosis or ultrafiltration to selectively transfer solvents or solutes across the membrane surface.
[B01D 61/52] Accessories -- The B01D 61/52 subclass covers accessories employed in membrane-based separation processes such as dialysis, osmosis or ultrafiltration. It includes items like housings, seals, support structures, modules and other components that aid the operation of semi-permeable membranes.
[B01D 61/54] Controlling or regulating -- The CPC sub-group B01D 61/54 encompasses methods for controlling or regulating separation processes that employ semi-permeable membranes such as dialysis, osmosis, or ultrafiltration. It includes adjustments of pressure, temperature, concentration gradients, or membrane characteristics to achieve desired separation performance.
[B01D 61/56] Electro-osmotic dewatering -- Electro-osmotic dewatering employs an electric field to drive water through semi-permeable membranes, such as in dialysis, osmosis or ultrafiltration, thereby separating liquid from solid particles in slurries or soils. The technique enhances water removal efficiency by inducing electro-osmotic flow, which can be used in waste-water treatment, mineral processing and other separation processes.
[B01D 61/58] Multistep processes -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Any multi-step process comprising more than one different membrane separation process and not solely falling within B01D61/026 , B01D61/0271 , B01D61/029 , B01D61/146 , B01D61/1471 , B01D61/149 , B01D61/244 , B01D61/2461 , B01D61/3621 , B01D61/3631 , B01D61/3641 , B01D61/3651 , B01D61/423 , B01D61/4251 , B01D61/4271 , B01D61/4281 .
[B01D 63/00] Apparatus in general for separation processes using semi-permeable membranes -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Modules for membrane-based separation processes. These modules could be suitable for liquid ( B01D61/00 - B01D61/58 ) and/or gaseous ( B01D53/22 ) feed/ permeate streams.  The devices classified in this group provide details on the integration of a selective separation membrane into a technically applicable module concept as e.g.:  The technical concept may include membrane fixing or sealing means or certain feed or permeate side ducts or spaces.
[B01D 63/005] Microfluidic devices  Microfluidic devices comprising semi-permeable hollow fibre membranes  B01D63/028 ; Microfluidic devices comprising semi-permeable flat membranes  B01D63/088 -- B01D 63/005 covers microfluidic devices that utilize semi-permeable membranes, specifically detailing those with hollow fiber and flat membrane configurations. This classification is part of the broader category of apparatus for separation processes using semi-permeable membranes within the Cooperative Patent Classification system.
[B01D 63/02] Hollow fibre modules -- Hollow fibre modules are apparatuses in which bundles of thin, tube-like semi-permeable fibers are arranged in a housing to allow fluid to pass either through the fiber walls or along their length, enabling separation of solutes based on size or diffusivity. They are used in filtration, dialysis, and gas-liquid separation.
[B01D 63/021] Manufacturing thereof -- The code B01D 63/021 covers general apparatus and methods for producing semi-permeable membranes used in separation processes such as filtration, reverse osmosis and similar technologies. It includes equipment for casting, forming, treating and assembling membrane materials, as well as related supporting structures that enable large-scale or continuous membrane manufacture.
[B01D 63/022] Encapsulating hollow fibres -- The B01D 63/022 class covers apparatus that encloses hollow-fiber semi-permeable membranes in a protective shell or capsule, forming a bundled or layered unit that enhances mechanical strength, prevents fouling and facilitates handling in liquid or gas separation processes such as water treatment, dialysis or gas purification.
[B01D 63/0221] using a mould -- This CPC subclass covers general apparatus for membrane-based separation processes in which a mould is used to shape or support the semi-permeable membrane, facilitating the separation operation.
[B01D 63/0222] using centrifugal forces -- The code B01D63/0222 covers apparatus for membrane-based separation that utilizes centrifugal forces to move liquids or gases through a semi-permeable membrane, typically by rotating the membrane or the feed stream. Such devices use the centripetal acceleration to enhance concentration gradients and improve separation efficiency.
[B01D 63/0223] by fixing the hollow fibres prior to encapsulation -- The subclass B01D 63/0223 defines apparatus for membrane-based separation in which hollow-fiber membranes are fixed in position before they are encapsulated, providing a stable configuration for the module. This arrangement improves mechanical integrity and flow control during semi-permeable membrane operations.
[B01D 63/0224] Opening the fibre ends after encapsulation -- B01D 63/0224 pertains to apparatus for membrane-based separation processes, focusing on methods that open the ends of fibres after they have been encapsulated, enabling fluid flow through the semi-permeable membrane.
[B01D 63/0225] Multiple encapsulation layers -- The entry B01D 63/0225 covers apparatus for separation that uses semi-permeable membranes arranged in multiple encapsulation layers. It pertains to devices that stack or layer membranes to enhance selectivity, protect the membranes, or control mass transfer during processes such as filtration, dialysis, or gas separation. This classification groups technologies that rely on layered membrane configurations to improve performance in separation operations.
[B01D 63/023] Encapsulating materials -- B01D 63/023 designates materials that encapsulate active substances or particles within a protective coating for use in membrane-based separation apparatus. Such encapsulating materials enhance durability, reduce fouling and enable selective transport across semi-permeable membranes.
[B01D 63/0231] using supporting structures, e.g. filaments for weaving mats -- This CPC subclass B01D 63/0231 covers apparatus for separation processes that use semi-permeable membranes supported by structures such as woven filament mats. The supporting structures give the membrane mechanical strength and stability, improving filtration or dialysis efficiency.
[B01D 63/0232] using hollow fibers mats as precursor, e.g. wound or pleated mats -- This CPC entry covers apparatus that utilizes hollow-fiber mats, such as wound or pleated configurations, as precursors in separation processes that employ semi-permeable membranes. It classifies general membrane-based separation equipment where the hollow-fiber mat forms the functional medium for selective transport.
[B01D 63/0233] forming the bundle -- B01D 63/0233 covers the step of forming a bundle of semipermeable membrane elements within a separation apparatus. It includes arranging individual hollow-fiber or flat-sheet modules into a compact assembly to increase effective membrane area and ensure uniform flow distribution. This sub-category falls under the broader class of separating processes using membranes.
[B01D 63/024] with a single potted end -- This CPC subclass covers membrane separation vessels that are potted on only one side, meaning the membrane is secured in a single potted end while the opposite side remains open to the process stream. Such designs are used in filtration, dialysis or gas-liquid separation where a single-ended configuration simplifies sealing and flow handling.
[B01D 63/0241] being U-shaped -- The subclass B01D 63/0241 defines apparatus that are U-shaped and used in separation processes employing semi-permeable membranes. It falls under Performing Operations → Separating → Separation and covers general-purpose equipment whose shape facilitates fluid flow across a membrane in a curved configuration.
[B01D 63/025] Bobbin units -- Bobbin units are cylindrical or tubular components onto which a semi-permeable membrane sheet is wound, forming a rotating drum that supports continuous filtration in membrane-based separation equipment such as reverse-osmosis or ultrafiltration systems. They provide a stable, replaceable membrane housing within apparatus designed for separating liquids or gases.
[B01D 63/026] Wafer type modules or flat-surface type modules -- This CPC subclass B01D 63/026 covers wafer-type or flat-surface modules used in apparatus for separation processes that employ semi-permeable membranes. These modules consist of flat plates or wafers that hold the membranes for applications such as filtration, dialysis, or gas separation. They are designed for compact, scalable configurations in industrial separation systems.
[B01D 63/027] Twinned or braided type modules -- B01D 63/027 covers membrane separation equipment built as twinned or braided modules, where two or more membrane elements are combined in parallel or interlaced form to increase surface area and flux while maintaining structural integrity. These modules are used in processes such as filtration, dialysis or gas separation.
[B01D 63/028] Microfluidic devices comprising semi-permeable hollow fibre membranes -- The B01D 63/028 subclass covers microfluidic devices that integrate semi-permeable hollow-fibre membranes for separation tasks. These systems combine miniature fluid channels with membrane technology to enable selective transport of species, often for analytical, biomedical, or chemical processing applications within compact, chip-scale platforms.
[B01D 63/031] Two or more types of hollow fibres within one bundle or within one potting or tube-sheet -- A membrane-based separation apparatus in which a single bundle, potting, or tube-sheet contains at least two different types of hollow-fibre membranes, each possibly differing in material, pore size or configuration, to achieve combined or complementary separation functions within one unit.
[B01D 63/032] More than two tube sheets for one bundle -- The code B01D 63/032 covers membrane-separation equipment in which a bundle of tubes is fitted with more than two tube sheets, typically three or more, allowing additional flow paths or structural support within the module. This configuration is used to improve mass-transfer efficiency or to accommodate complex inlet-outlet arrangements in semi-permeable membrane processes.
[B01D 63/033] Specific distribution of fibres within one potting or tube-sheet -- The code B01D 63/033 pertains to membrane-based separation equipment in which the fibres inside a potting or tube-sheet are arranged in a particular pattern or density, influencing flow distribution, structural support, or filtration performance. This classification covers devices where such fibre placement is engineered for specific operational advantages.
[B01D 63/034] Lumen open in more than two directions -- This subclass covers general membrane separation equipment whose internal passage (lumen) is open to the outside in three or more directions, facilitating multi-directional flow or exchange across the membrane. It is used in processes such as dialysis, filtration and gas separation.
[B01D 63/04] comprising multiple hollow fibre assemblies -- This CPC subclass covers devices that perform separations using semi-permeable membranes assembled as multiple hollow-fibre bundles, such as filtration, dialysis or gas-separation units. The apparatus typically incorporates several hollow-fibre assemblies housed in a pressure vessel or cartridge, providing a large membrane surface area for feed-permeate contact.
[B01D 63/043] with separate tube sheets -- This CPC subclass pertains to apparatus for membrane-based separation processes in which the semi-permeable membranes are supported by separate tube sheets, each tube sheet being distinct and not shared among the membrane modules.
[B01D 63/046] in separate housings -- B01D 63/046 covers apparatus in general for membrane-based separation processes where the semi-permeable membrane is placed in separate housings, such as individual modules or canisters, rather than integrated in a single vessel. The classification is used for devices that employ distinct, detachable membrane units to achieve filtration, dialysis, or related separations. It falls under the broader categories of performing operations, separating, and separation.
[B01D 63/06] Tubular membrane modules -- B01D 63/06 covers tubular membrane modules, which are assemblies of hollow-type membrane tubes or fibers arranged in bundles to provide a semi-permeable surface for filtration, dialysis, reverse osmosis and related separation processes. The modules serve as the basic apparatus for membrane-based separation operations, allowing fluid streams to be processed through the tube walls while retaining or rejecting specific solutes.
[B01D 63/061] Manufacturing thereof -- The entry B01D 63/061 covers equipment and processes for producing semi-permeable membranes used in separation operations, including the fabrication of polymeric, ceramic or composite membranes and related manufacturing steps such as casting, coating, drying and assembly into modules. It pertains to general apparatus for membrane-based separations rather than any specific application.
[B01D 63/062] with membranes on a surface of a support tube -- A general apparatus for separation processes that employs semi-permeable membranes fixed to the exterior of a support tube, enabling selective transport of substances across the membrane surface while the tube core conveys the feed stream. This design combines structural support with membrane functionality for efficient filtration or dialysis operations.
[B01D 63/063] on the inner surface thereof -- B01D 63/063 covers apparatus for separation processes that employ semi-permeable membranes, specifically those in which the functional features, coatings, or structures are situated on the inner surface of the membrane module. The claim addresses designs that modify the interior wall to enhance selectivity, flux, or fouling resistance.
[B01D 63/065] on the outer surface thereof -- The CPC B01D 63/065 entry covers apparatus in general for separation processes that employ semi-permeable membranes with the functional or selective layer positioned on the outer surface of the membrane. It includes devices where the active component is applied externally to achieve selective transport of substances.
[B01D 63/066] with a porous block having membrane coated passages -- The code B01D 63/066 covers apparatus for separation processes that use a porous block with membrane-coated passages, allowing selective transport across a semi-permeable membrane. It is classified under performing operations, separating, separation.
[B01D 63/067] with pleated membranes -- The CPC subclass B01D 63/067 covers apparatus for separation processes that use semi-permeable membranes formed with pleats to increase surface area and improve flux. It resides under the hierarchy Performing Operations → Separating → Separation and applies to any device employing pleated membranes for filtration, dialysis or related membrane-based separations.
[B01D 63/068] with flexible membrane tubes -- B01D 63/068 covers apparatus for separation processes that employ semi-permeable membranes formed as flexible tubes. It includes equipment where the membrane is wound or arranged in tubular form to allow selective transport of substances, typically used in filtration, dialysis, or gas-liquid separation.
[B01D 63/069] comprising a bundle of tubular membranes -- This CPC subclass covers general apparatus for membrane-based separation that employs a bundle of tubular membranes. Such devices use semi-permeable tubes arranged together to increase surface area for filtration, dialysis, or gas-liquid transfer. The classification is used for equipment where the membrane elements are integrated as a bundled pack rather than as flat sheets or single tubes.
[B01D 63/08] Flat membrane modules -- Flat membrane modules are components of separation apparatus that incorporate sheet-like semi-permeable membranes arranged in a planar configuration. They provide a high surface area for processes such as filtration, dialysis, or reverse osmosis, allowing selective passage of solvents or solutes while retaining larger molecules. These modules are used in chemical, biomedical, and water-treatment applications.
[B01D 63/081] Manufacturing thereof -- This CPC subclass covers methods and processes for producing equipment that utilizes semi-permeable membranes to carry out separation operations. It includes the manufacture of such membrane-based separation apparatus, encompassing material selection, membrane formation, module assembly, and related fabrication steps.
[B01D 63/082] comprising a stack of flat membranes -- This CPC entry covers apparatuses for separation processes that employ semi-permeable membranes, specifically designs that use a stack of flat membranes to achieve the separation.
[B01D 63/0821] Membrane plate arrangements for submerged operation -- The code B01D 63/0821 covers membrane plate arrangements designed for submerged operation in separation processes that employ semi-permeable membranes. These assemblies position flat or corrugated plates with membrane surfaces below the liquid level to enhance mass transfer and fouling control. They are used in applications such as filtration, dialysis, and gas-liquid separation.
[B01D 63/0822] Plate-and-frame devices -- Plate-and-frame devices are membrane filtration units in which a series of flat plates are held in a frame to create narrow channels through which a feed solution passes, allowing separation by diffusion across semi-permeable membranes. They are employed for processes such as reverse osmosis, ultrafiltration and nanofiltration in water treatment and chemical industries.
[B01D 63/084] at least one flow duct intersecting the membranes -- The CPC subclass B01D 63/084 defines apparatus for separation processes that employ semipermeable membranes and include at least one flow duct that intersects the membrane, allowing the process fluid to pass through or across the membrane surface. It belongs to the hierarchy Performing Operations - Separating - Separation.
[B01D 63/085] specially adapted for two fluids in mass exchange flow -- The B01D 63/085 subclass covers apparatus that employ semi-permeable membranes specially adapted for two fluid streams that exchange mass, such as hollow-fiber, plate-and-frame, or similar modules arranged for concurrent or counter-current flow. It includes designs where the membrane structure is configured to optimise transfer of substances between the two fluids.
[B01D 63/087] Single membrane modules -- The CPC class B01D 63/087 designates single-membrane modules employed in apparatus for separation processes that utilize semi-permeable membranes. It falls under the hierarchy of Performing Operations → Separating → Separation and covers devices where a lone membrane is integrated as the functional separation element.
[B01D 63/088] Microfluidic devices comprising semi-permeable flat membranes -- The CPC subclass B01D 63/088 covers microfluidic devices that integrate flat semi-permeable membranes to perform separation operations. These apparatuses use membrane-based filtration or dialysis on a microscale, enabling precise, continuous processing of fluids for chemical, biological, or analytical applications.
[B01D 63/089] Modules where the membrane is in the form of a bag, membrane cushion or pad -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Modules wherein the bag-, cushion- or pad-form membrane can be flat but also encompasses additional configurations.
[B01D 63/10] Spiral-wound membrane modules -- B01D 63/10 covers spiral-wound membrane modules, which are compact assemblies of semi-permeable sheets rolled together to form a high-surface-area filtration element for separating liquids or gases. These modules are used in processes such as reverse osmosis, ultrafiltration and nanofiltration, providing efficient mass transfer in a relatively small footprint.
[B01D 63/101] Spiral winding -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Details relating to the action/process of winding the membranes into a spiral-wound membrane module as well as details relating to the apparatus used for winding the membranes if applicable.
[B01D 63/103] Details relating to membrane envelopes -- This CPC subclass B01D 63/103 covers details relating to membrane envelopes used in apparatus for separation processes that employ semi-permeable membranes. It includes the design, construction and material aspects of the envelope that contains or supports the membrane, such as housings, spacers and protective layers, as part of equipment for filtration, dialysis, gas separation or similar operations.
[B01D 63/1031] Glue line or sealing patterns -- Refers to the configuration of adhesive lines or sealing patterns that join or seal components of a membrane-based separation apparatus, ensuring fluid tightness and structural integrity. It covers designs that prevent leaks while maintaining the performance of semi-permeable membranes in separation processes.
[B01D 63/106] Anti-Telescopic-Devices [ATD] -- Anti-telescopic devices are structural components incorporated in membrane-based separation equipment to keep individual membrane modules from collapsing or sliding into one another during operation. By maintaining a fixed spacing, they preserve flow channels, improve pressure distribution and prevent performance loss in processes such as ultrafiltration, reverse osmosis or dialysis. This entry classifies such devices under the broader group of apparatus for separation using semi-permeable membranes.
[B01D 63/107] Specific properties of the central tube or the permeate channel -- B01D 63/107 covers apparatus for membrane-based separation where the defining feature is the central tube or permeate channel, detailing its specific material, geometry, surface treatment or structural attributes that influence flow, pressure resistance and selective transport of substances through the semi-permeable membrane.
[B01D 63/12] comprising multiple spiral-wound assemblies -- B01D 63/12 covers apparatus for separation processes that employ semi-permeable membranes arranged as multiple spiral-wound assemblies. The design uses several coiled membrane modules stacked or placed in series to increase surface area and filtration efficiency, typically applied in desalination, gas separation, or liquid purification.
[B01D 63/14] Pleat-type membrane modules -- Pleat-type membrane modules are apparatuses for separation processes that employ semi-permeable membranes formed into pleated sheets, increasing surface area within a compact housing. The pleated configuration improves flow distribution and filtration efficiency and is commonly used in reverse-osmosis, nanofiltration, and related membrane-based separations.
[B01D 63/16] Rotary, reciprocated or vibrated modules -- Rotary, reciprocated or vibrated modules are apparatuses that employ semi-permeable membranes in a moving configuration to enhance mass transfer during separation processes, such as filtration or dialysis, by cyclically rotating, reciprocating, or vibrating the membrane elements.
[B01D 65/00] Accessories or auxiliary operations, in general, for separation processes or apparatus using semi-permeable membranes -- Attention is drawn to the Note following B01D59/50 , which indicates the last place rule is not applied in B01D65/00 .
[B01D 65/003] Membrane bonding or sealing -- This subclass covers methods for joining or sealing membranes in separation equipment, including adhesive bonding, thermal welding, ultrasonic or laser sealing, and other techniques that create a leak-proof interface between membrane sheets or between membranes and supporting structures. It addresses the practical means of maintaining integrity in semi-permeable membrane systems.
[B01D 65/02] Membrane cleaning or sterilisation -- The B01D 65/02 subclass covers auxiliary operations that clean or sterilise semi-permeable membranes used in separation processes. It includes devices, chemicals, or procedures designed to remove fouling, bio-film, or contaminants to restore membrane performance. This entry falls under the broader categories of performing operations, separating, and separation.
[B01D 65/022] Membrane sterilisation -- The CPC subclass B01D 65/022 relates to auxiliary operations that employ semi-permeable membranes to achieve sterilisation of liquids or gases, typically by filtering out microorganisms or other contaminants. It covers the use of membrane-based barrier or filtration techniques as part of a separation process.
[B01D 65/025] Removal of membrane elements before washing -- The B01D 65/025 entry covers auxiliary steps that involve taking out membrane modules or elements from a filtration system prior to any cleaning cycle. It pertains to the handling of semi-permeable membranes in separation apparatuses, focusing on the removal operation that precedes washing or regeneration.
[B01D 65/027] Cleaning of other parts of the apparatus than the membrane -- This subclass covers cleaning procedures and devices for the non-membrane parts of a membrane-based separation system, such as housings, channels, tubing or support structures, usually employing flushing, chemical agents or mechanical action to remove fouling and maintain performance.
[B01D 65/04] with movable bodies, e.g. foam balls -- B01D 65/04 covers auxiliary or accessory operations for separation processes that employ semi-permeable membranes and use movable bodies, such as foam balls, to enhance or control the separation. The movable bodies may be introduced, removed, or agitated within the membrane system to improve mass transfer or prevent fouling.
[B01D 65/06] with special washing compositions -- The B01D 65/06 subclass concerns separation operations that employ semi-permeable membranes together with special washing compositions as accessories or auxiliary steps. It covers methods and apparatus where such formulations are used to clean, rinse or enhance the membrane-based separation process. This falls under Performing Operations → Separating → Separation.
[B01D 65/08] Prevention of membrane fouling or of concentration polarisation -- The subclass B01D 65/08 deals with measures that prevent fouling or concentration-polarisation on semi-permeable membranes used in separation processes, encompassing cleaning methods, surface-treatment techniques, flow-control arrangements and additives that keep the membrane surface clear and maintain efficient operation.
[B01D 65/10] Testing of membranes or membrane apparatus -- B01D 65/10 covers methods and equipment for evaluating the performance of semi-permeable membranes and membrane-based apparatus, including measurements of flux, selectivity, permeability, mechanical strength and durability. It is used for auxiliary testing operations that support separation processes.
[B01D 65/102] Detection of leaks in membranes -- The entry covers techniques and apparatus for locating or indicating leaks in semi-permeable membranes employed in separation operations. It includes auxiliary measures such as pressure monitoring, flow testing, or optical methods that assess membrane integrity during use.
[B01D 65/104] Detection of leaks in membrane apparatus or modules -- The B01D 65/104 class concerns techniques for identifying leaks in membrane-based apparatus or modules. It includes any detection methods-such as pressure, flow, or tracer monitoring-employed during separation operations that use semi-permeable membranes. This aids in maintaining system integrity and performance.
[B01D 65/106] Repairing membrane apparatus or modules -- The CPC subclass B01D 65/106 concerns repairing membrane equipment or modules used in separation processes that employ semi-permeable membranes. It includes methods, tools and auxiliary operations for restoring or maintaining the performance of such membrane apparatus.
[B01D 65/108] Repairing membranes -- B01D 65/108 concerns techniques and apparatus for repairing or refurbishing semi-permeable membranes used in separation processes, including methods for patching, cleaning, or restoring membrane integrity to prolong their operational life.
[B01D 65/109] Testing of membrane fouling or clogging, e.g. amount or affinity -- This CPC subclass deals with methods and apparatus for evaluating fouling or clogging of semi-permeable membranes, including measuring the amount of deposit on the membrane or the affinity of contaminants for the membrane surface.
[B01D 67/00] Processes specially adapted for manufacturing semi-permeable membranes for separation processes or apparatus -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Processes for the production of membranes for separation applications per se, i.e. including the production of the selective layer if applicable onto the support structure or self-supporting.  Semipermeable membranes comprise a continuous layer of material with or without pores capable for mass transfer of a certain species, solvent or gas.  Further process for after-treatment of a membrane are covered under B01D67/0081
[B01D 67/0002] Organic membrane manufacture -- B01D 67/0002 covers processes specially adapted for producing organic semi-permeable membranes used in separation operations. The methods include polymer casting, phase inversion, and other techniques to create membranes suitable for filtration, dialysis, or gas separation. These processes are classified under the performing operations - separating - separation hierarchy.
[B01D 67/0004] by agglomeration of particles -- This subclass concerns methods for producing semi-permeable membranes used in separation operations where the membrane material is created by agglomerating particles into a coherent structure. Techniques may involve sintering, binding agents, or other particle-joining processes to yield a membrane with the required permeability and mechanical strength.
[B01D 67/00041] by sintering -- A process for producing semi-permeable membranes in which powdered or granulated material is compacted and then sintered to bond the particles, creating a porous structure suited for filtration or dialysis. The sintering step typically involves heating the compacted mass below its melting point to achieve sufficient strength while maintaining controlled porosity. This method falls under separating operations for manufacturing membranes used in various separation apparatuses.
[B01D 67/00042] by deposition of fibres, nanofibres or nanofibrils -- The B01D 67/00042 entry covers processes for producing semi-permeable membranes used in separation applications by depositing fibers, nanofibers or nanofibrils onto a substrate, forming a selective barrier for filtration or other membrane-based separations.
[B01D 67/00043] by agglomeration of nanoparticles -- B01D 67/00043 covers methods for producing semi-permeable membranes used in separation processes in which nanoparticles are agglomerated to form the membrane structure, providing a route to tailor pore size and material properties for filtration or diffusion applications.
[B01D 67/00044] by plasma spraying -- A plasma-spraying method for producing semi-permeable membranes used in separation operations, falling under the CPC hierarchy of Performing Operations → Separating → Separation.
