The technique is specifically adapted for manufacturing membranes applied in various separation processes. Code B01D 67/00044.
[B01D 67/00045] by additive layer techniques, e.g. selective laser sintering [SLS], selective laser melting [SLM] or 3D printing -- The CPC entry B01D 67/00045 covers processes for producing semi-permeable membranes used in separation operations that employ additive layer manufacturing such as selective laser sintering, selective laser melting or other 3-D printing methods.
[B01D 67/00046] by deposition by filtration through a support or base layer -- This subclass covers processes for making semi-permeable membranes in which membrane material is deposited onto a support or base layer by passing a casting solution or suspension through that layer, allowing the forming film to filter onto and adhere to the underlying substrate. The method is used to create membranes tailored for separation applications such as filtration, dialysis or gas separation.
[B01D 67/0006] by chemical reactions  in-situ polymerisation, polycondensation, cross-linking or reaction for manufacturing composite membranes  B01D69/125 -- B01D 67/0006 covers processes for producing semi-permeable membranes in which the membrane material is formed by in-situ polymerisation, polycondensation, cross-linking or other chemical reactions, including the fabrication of composite membranes. These methods are used in separation technologies such as filtration, gas separation and dialysis.
[B01D 67/0009] by phase separation, sol-gel transition, evaporation or solvent quenching -- B01D 67/0009 pertains to methods of creating semi-permeable membranes through processes such as phase separation, sol-gel transition, evaporation, or solvent quenching. These techniques are specifically tailored for manufacturing membranes used in separation technologies.
[B01D 67/00091] by evaporation -- This CPC subclass B01D 67/00091 covers processes for producing semi-permeable membranes by evaporation, used in separation technologies. It is situated in the hierarchy under performing operations, separating and separation.
[B01D 67/0011] Casting solutions therefor -- This subclass covers the preparation of casting solutions used to form semi-permeable membranes, including the formulation, mixing and handling of polymer or inorganic solutions prior to membrane casting, as part of processes specially adapted for manufacturing membranes for separation applications.
[B01D 67/00111] Polymer pretreatment in the casting solutions -- B01D 67/00111 covers methods of pretreating polymers in the casting solutions used to form semi-permeable membranes, such as adding additives, adjusting pH, or applying treatments that modify viscosity or compatibility before membrane formation. These steps aim to improve membrane performance in separation applications.
[B01D 67/00113] Pretreatment of the casting solutions, e.g. thermal treatment or ageing -- The entry B01D 67/00113 covers pretreatment steps applied to casting solutions used in making semi-permeable membranes, such as thermal treatment or ageing, to modify solution properties before casting the membrane structure. These operations are part of separation-process manufacturing methods for producing membrane-based separation apparatus.
[B01D 67/0013] Casting processes -- B01D 67/0013 covers casting processes used to form semi-permeable membranes for separation applications. It includes methods of pouring, solidifying and shaping membrane material to produce sheets, tubes or other forms employed in filtration, pervaporation and related membrane-based separations. This entry falls under the hierarchy of performing operations, separating, separation.
[B01D 67/00135] Air gap characteristics -- The B01D 67/00135 entry covers the characteristics of the air gap used in the production of semi-permeable membranes, including its size, shape, pressure and flow conditions. It addresses how these parameters influence membrane formation, strength and separation efficiency in processes such as phase-inversion or hollow-fiber spinning.
[B01D 67/0016] Coagulation -- The CPC class B01D 67/0016 - Coagulation - relates to processes and apparatus for producing semi-permeable membranes by inducing coagulation, precipitation or gelation of polymer solutions, thereby shaping membrane structure and pore size. It covers methods that control phase separation to create membranes used in filtration, dialysis and other separation applications.
[B01D 67/00165] Composition of the coagulation baths -- The entry covers the chemical formulations used as coagulation baths in the production of semi-permeable membranes, specifying bath constituents, additives and operating conditions that influence membrane formation, structure and separation performance. It also addresses bath reuse, temperature control and pH adjustment.
[B01D 67/0018] Thermally induced processes [TIPS] -- This subclass covers methods that use temperature changes to induce phase separation and form porous or semi-permeable membranes, known as thermally induced phase separation (TIPS). It includes steps such as heating a polymer solution and cooling to precipitate a membrane structure used for separation.
[B01D 67/002] from melts -- The sub-class B01D 67/002 covers processes for producing semi-permeable separation membranes by shaping or forming them directly from a melt, such as polymer or glass melts, prior to solidification. It pertains to manufacturing methods that create membrane structures in a molten state for use in filtration or diffusion applications.
[B01D 67/0023] by inducing porosity into non porous precursor membranes -- B01D 67/0023 covers processes for producing semi-permeable membranes in which a non-porous precursor membrane is transformed into a porous structure by inducing porosity, such as through chemical, thermal or mechanical treatments, to create selective separation media. This subclass falls under the broader hierarchy of performing operations, separating, and separation, and pertains specifically to manufacturing methods for membrane-based separation apparatus.
[B01D 67/0025] by mechanical treatment, e.g. pore-stretching -- This subclass covers methods of producing semi-permeable membranes where the membrane structure is modified through mechanical actions such as pore-stretching. The techniques aim to improve filtration, dialysis or gas-separation performance and are applied in membrane-manufacturing processes. It is situated within the CPC hierarchy for separating operations.
[B01D 67/0027] by stretching -- This subgroup covers methods where a polymer or other film is mechanically stretched to create a semi-permeable membrane used in separation operations. The stretching induces pores or oriented structures that enable selective transport of substances. It applies to membrane manufacturing for filtration, dialysis, or gas separation.
[B01D 67/003] by selective elimination of components, e.g. by leaching -- This subclass covers methods for producing semi-permeable membranes in which unwanted constituents are removed after membrane formation, typically through chemical leaching or similar selective elimination techniques. The goal is to create a membrane with pores or channels that allow certain species to pass while rejecting others.
[B01D 67/0031] by elimination of at least one of the blocks of a block copolymer -- B01D 67/0031 covers processes for making semi-permeable separation membranes in which at least one block of a block copolymer is eliminated, producing a porous structure that enables selective transport of molecules.
[B01D 67/0032] by elimination of segments of the precursor, e.g. nucleation-track membranes, lithography or laser methods -- The entry B01D 67/0032 covers processes for producing semi-permeable membranes by removing portions of a precursor material, such as nucleation-track membranes, lithographic patterning or laser ablation. These methods create the membrane structure through selective elimination of segments, enabling precise control of pore size and distribution for separation applications.
[B01D 67/0034] by micromachining techniques, e.g. using masking and etching steps, photolithography -- The B01D 67/0034 class covers processes for making semi-permeable separation membranes that employ micromachining methods such as masking, etching steps and photolithography to shape or pattern the membrane material. This includes techniques adapted for precise micro-scale fabrication of membrane structures.
[B01D 67/0037] by deposition from the gaseous phase, e.g. CVD, PVD -- The CPC subclass B01D 67/0037 covers processes for producing semi-permeable membranes used in separation operations by depositing membrane material from a gas, such as chemical vapor deposition (CVD) or physical vapor deposition (PVD). It includes methods where the membrane is formed directly on a support or substrate through vapor-phase reactions or physical condensation, enabling precise control of membrane thickness and pore structure for filtration, gas separation, or similar applications.
[B01D 67/0039] Inorganic membrane manufacture -- This subgroup covers processes specifically designed for producing inorganic semi-permeable membranes used in separation operations. It includes methods such as forming, shaping, sintering, coating and functionalizing inorganic materials to create membranes for filtration, gas separation or liquid purification.
[B01D 67/0041] by agglomeration of particles in the dry state -- B01D 67/0041 covers processes for producing semi-permeable separation membranes in which dry particles are compacted, fused or otherwise agglomerated without the use of liquids to form a coherent membrane structure. The method is used to create filter or membrane elements for gas or liquid separation by binding powders or granules in the dry state.
[B01D 67/00411] by sintering -- B01D 67/00411 covers processes for producing semi-permeable membranes used in separation operations by sintering, where powdered material is heated to fuse particles and create a porous structure. The classification applies to manufacturing methods that tailor membrane porosity and thickness for filtration, dialysis, or gas-liquid separation applications.
[B01D 67/00412] by deposition of fibres, nanofibres or nanofibrils -- This CPC subclass covers processes for producing semi-permeable membranes used in separation operations by depositing fibres, nanofibres or nanofibrils onto a substrate. The method is tailored for membrane manufacturing and may involve techniques such as electrospinning or other fibre-laying approaches. It falls under B01D 67/00412 within the broader hierarchy of performing operations, separating and separation.
[B01D 67/00413] by agglomeration of nanoparticles -- The CPC subclass B01D 67/00413 defines processes for making semi-permeable membranes used in separation operations by agglomerating nanoparticles. It is placed under the hierarchy of performing operations, separating, and separation, and pertains to manufacturing membrane structures specially adapted for filtration and related separation technologies.
[B01D 67/00414] by plasma spraying -- B01D 67/00414 covers the manufacturing of semi-permeable membranes by plasma spraying. The method applies a high-temperature plasma jet to deposit membrane material onto a substrate, creating a thin, porous layer suitable for filtration or gas separation. It falls under the broader class of processes for separating and the specific subclass for membrane production.
[B01D 67/00415] by additive layer techniques, e.g. selective laser sintering [SLS], selective laser melting [SLM] or 3D printing -- Processes for manufacturing semi-permeable membranes through additive layer techniques, such as selective laser sintering, selective laser melting or 3D printing, are classified under the separating operations hierarchy. This entry addresses specialized membrane production methods used in separation processes.
[B01D 67/00416] by deposition by filtration through a support or base layer -- B01D 67/00416 covers methods of making semi-permeable membranes where the membrane material is deposited onto a support by filtration, creating a membrane layer on a base substrate. The process is adapted for producing membranes used in separation operations.
[B01D 67/0044] by chemical reaction -- B01D 67/0044 covers processes that create semi-permeable membranes through a chemical reaction, specifically for use in separation operations. It is a sub-category of performing operations, separating, and separation, describing manufacturing methods adapted to produce membrane materials that enable selective permeation.
[B01D 67/0046] by slurry techniques, e.g. die or slip-casting -- This subclass covers processes for making semi-permeable membranes by slurry methods, such as die-casting or slip-casting, where a liquid suspension of membrane material is shaped and solidified to form the separating structure. It is part of the broader category of separation processes under performing operations.
[B01D 67/0048] by sol-gel transition -- This CPC subclass covers processes that create semi-permeable membranes using a sol-gel transition, a method where a colloidal solution undergoes gelation to form a solid network. The technique is used to produce membrane structures for separation operations such as filtration, dialysis or gas separation.
[B01D 67/0049] by evaporation -- B01D 67/0049 concerns processes for manufacturing semi-permeable membranes by evaporation, a technique employed in the production of membrane materials used in separation operations. This entry falls under the hierarchy of Performing Operations → Separating → Separation.
[B01D 67/0051] by controlled crystallisation, e,.g. hydrothermal growth -- The code B01D 67/0051 covers processes for producing semi-permeable membranes by means of controlled crystallisation, for example hydrothermal growth. It falls under the hierarchy of performing operations, separating, and separation, and relates to manufacturing membrane materials specially adapted for separation applications.
[B01D 67/0053] by inducing porosity into non porous precursor membranes -- It covers methods that create pores in initially non-porous precursor membranes to form semi-permeable membranes for separation processes. Techniques such as chemical, physical or radiation treatments induce porosity in solid polymer or inorganic films, enabling fabrication of filtration, dialysis or gas-separation membranes.
[B01D 67/0055] by mechanical treatment -- B01D 67/0055 covers processes that employ mechanical treatment-such as grinding, milling, extrusion, calendering or other physical deformation-to produce or shape semi-permeable membranes used in separation operations. The subclass addresses mechanical actions that form, texture, or finish the membrane material as part of its manufacturing.
[B01D 67/0058] by selective elimination of components, e.g. by leaching -- B01D 67/0058 covers processes for manufacturing semi-permeable membranes that achieve separation by selectively removing components, for example through leaching. The method creates a membrane with selective permeability for use in filtration, dialysis or other separation technologies.
[B01D 67/006] by elimination of segments of the precursor, e.g. nucleation-track membranes, lithography or laser methods -- B01D 67/006 covers membrane-forming processes that create pores by selectively removing parts of a precursor material. It includes methods such as nucleation-track etching, lithographic patterning, or laser ablation to produce semi-permeable membranes for separation applications.
[B01D 67/0062] by micromachining techniques, e.g. using masking and etching steps, photolithography -- This subclass covers processes for producing semi-permeable membranes by micromachining methods such as photolithography, masking and etching steps, which are tailored for separation operations. The techniques enable precise fabrication of membranes used in various separation apparatuses.
[B01D 67/0065] by anodic oxidation -- The B01D 67/0065 entry covers processes that create semi-permeable membranes for separation applications by using anodic oxidation. It specifically addresses manufacturing steps in which an electrically-driven oxidation reaction forms a porous oxide layer on a conductive substrate, yielding a membrane suitable for filtration, dialysis or similar separation techniques.
[B01D 67/0067] by carbonisation or pyrolysis -- The B01D 67/0067 CPC entry covers processes that employ carbonisation or pyrolysis to produce semi-permeable membranes used in separation operations. It falls under the hierarchy of Performing Operations → Separating → Separation and details methods specially adapted for manufacturing such membrane apparatus.
[B01D 67/0069] by deposition from the liquid phase, e.g. electrochemical deposition  B01D67/0046  takes precedence -- A process for producing semi-permeable membranes used in separation operations, wherein the membrane material is formed by depositing from a liquid phase such as electrochemical deposition. It is classified under B01D 67/0069 and is subordinate to the broader category B01D 67/0046.
[B01D 67/0072] by deposition from the gaseous phase, e.g. sputtering, CVD, PVD -- This CPC subclass covers processes for manufacturing semi-permeable separation membranes by depositing material from the gaseous phase, such as sputtering, chemical vapour deposition and physical vapour deposition. It falls under the B01D 67 series within the broader categories of performing operations, separating and separation.
[B01D 67/0074] from melts -- This subclass covers processes specially adapted for making semi-permeable membranes from melt materials for use in separation operations. It includes manufacturing methods that produce membranes suitable for filtration, selective transport or other separation applications.
[B01D 67/0076] Pretreatment of inorganic membrane material prior to membrane formation, e.g. coating of metal powder -- It covers methods of preparing inorganic membrane substrates before they are formed into membranes, such as applying a coating to metal powder to improve properties, enabling later fabrication of semi-permeable membranes for separations.
[B01D 67/0079] Manufacture of membranes comprising organic and inorganic components -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Processes for formation of membranes made of or comprising metal organic framework [MOF].
[B01D 67/00791] Different components in separate layers -- This CPC subclass B01D 67/00791 addresses processes for making semi-permeable membranes in which the membrane comprises separate layers of different components. The methods focus on assembling distinct material layers to achieve specific separation performance in filtration or purification systems.
[B01D 67/00793] Dispersing a component, e.g. as particles or powder, in another component -- The B01D 67/00793 entry covers methods for dispersing a component-such as particles, granules or powder-within another material during the production of semi-permeable membranes used for separation. It relates to operations that separate and blend substances to form membrane structures.
[B01D 67/0081] After-treatment of organic or inorganic membranes -- The subclass B01D 67/0081 covers post-fabrication treatments of organic or inorganic semi-permeable membranes, such as cleaning, conditioning, surface modification or stabilization steps that improve membrane performance in separation processes.
[B01D 67/0083] Thermal after-treatment -- Thermal after-treatment is a heat-based step applied after a membrane is formed to modify its structure, enhancing selectivity, mechanical strength, or pore characteristics. In semi-permeable membrane production it may involve annealing, sintering or controlled heating to improve separation performance.
[B01D 67/0086] Mechanical after-treatment -- Mechanical after-treatment refers to post-fabrication mechanical operations applied to semi-permeable membranes, such as stretching, rolling, cutting, polishing or surface texturing. These steps adjust thickness, surface roughness, or dimensions to enhance the membrane's separation efficiency and mechanical stability.
[B01D 67/0088] Physical treatment with compounds, e.g. swelling, coating or impregnation -- The CPC entry B01D 67/0088 covers processes that physically treat semi-permeable membranes with compounds such as swelling agents, coating materials, or impregnating substances to enhance their separation performance. It falls under the broader hierarchy of performing operations, separating, and separation, and pertains to manufacturing methods for membrane-based separation apparatus.
[B01D 67/009] with wave-energy, particle-radiation or plasma -- The B01D 67/009 subclass covers manufacturing processes for semi-permeable membranes that employ wave-energy, particle-radiation, or plasma to effect separation. These methods fall under the broader categories of performing operations, separating and separation in the Cooperative Patent Classification.
[B01D 67/0093] Chemical modification -- This CPC subclass (B01D 67/0093) pertains to chemical modification steps used to produce semi-permeable membranes for separation applications. It covers processes that alter membrane material chemically to enhance selectivity or durability, often as part of larger membrane-manufacturing methods.
[B01D 67/00931] by introduction of specific groups after membrane formation, e.g. by grafting -- This subclass covers processes for making semi-permeable membranes in which functional groups are introduced after the membrane has been formed, typically by grafting or similar chemical attachment methods. The purpose is to tailor the membrane's surface or interior chemistry for enhanced separation performance.
[B01D 67/00933] by addition of a layer chemically bonded to the membrane -- The code B01D 67/00933 covers processes for making semi-permeable separation membranes in which a chemically bonded coating layer is applied to the membrane. This added layer is integrated by covalent bonding to improve selectivity or durability in filtration, dialysis or gas-separation applications.
[B01D 67/0095] Drying -- The B01D 67/0095 entry covers drying steps applied in the production of semi-permeable membranes, where moisture or solvent is removed from the membrane material to achieve the desired structure and performance for separation processes. This includes methods such as heated air, vacuum, or infrared treatment that are specifically adapted to membrane manufacturing.
[B01D 67/0097] Storing or preservation -- B01D 67/0097 covers techniques for storing or preserving semi-permeable membranes employed in separation operations. It includes methods to maintain membrane performance during idle periods, such as drying, refrigerating, or protective packaging, and addresses measures to prevent fouling, degradation, or loss of selectivity.
[B01D 69/00] Semi-permeable membranes for separation processes or apparatus characterised by their form, structure or properties -- The code B01D 69/00 classifies semi-permeable membranes used in separation operations where the defining factor is the membrane's form, structure or intrinsic properties. It includes devices such as filtration, dialysis or gas-separation membranes whose performance depends on selective permeability rather than a specific chemical composition or end-use.
[B01D 69/02] characterised by their properties -- This subclass covers semi-permeable membranes used in separation processes where the classification is based on the membranes' intrinsic properties such as material composition, pore-size distribution or functional coatings, rather than on their shape or mechanical design.
[B01D 69/04] Tubular membranes -- Tubular semi-permeable membranes are cylindrical hollow fibers used in separation processes, distinguished by their form, wall structure, pore size and material properties that enable selective transport of specific substances.
[B01D 69/043] characterised by the tube diameter -- B01D 69/043 covers semi-permeable membrane elements used in separation processes where the membranes are distinguished primarily by the diameter of the tubes that form them. The classification applies to apparatus or membranes whose performance or suitability depends on this geometric dimension.
[B01D 69/046] characterised by the cross-sectional shape of the tube -- This CPC subclass (B01D 69/046) relates to semi-permeable membrane tubes used in separation processes where the defining feature is the cross-sectional shape of the tube, such as circular, elliptical, rectangular or other geometries that influence performance.
[B01D 69/06] Flat membranes -- Flat membranes are semi-permeable sheets used in separation processes, distinguished by their planar shape, internal structure or material properties. They serve to filter or selectively transfer substances across a uniform surface, often employed in filtration, dialysis or gas separation systems.
[B01D 69/061] Membrane bags or membrane cushions -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Properties of the membranes as such being in the form of membrane bags, pads or cushions, whereas B01D63/089 covers the membrane module features.  Modules wherein the membrane is not necessarily in the form of a flat bag, cushion or pad.
[B01D 69/08] Hollow fibre membranes -- Hollow-fibre membranes are semi-permeable, tube-shaped membranes used in separation processes, distinguished by their specific form, internal structure and material properties. They are employed to filter or selectively transport substances in chemical, biomedical and water-treatment applications, offering high surface area and efficient mass transfer. This subclass groups devices and methods that rely on the unique geometry of hollow fibre configurations.
[B01D 69/081] characterised by the fibre diameter -- B01D 69/081 defines semi-permeable membranes or separation devices whose defining characteristic is the fibre diameter of the membrane material. The classification is used for technologies where the size of the fibres influences filtration efficiency, selectivity or mechanical properties.
[B01D 69/082] characterised by the cross-sectional shape of the fibre -- B01D 69/082 designates semi-permeable membranes or filtration devices whose defining feature is the cross-sectional shape of the fibre. It falls under the CPC hierarchy Performing Operations - Separating - Separation and applies to membrane forms, structures or properties distinguished by that geometry.
[B01D 69/084] Undulated fibres -- This CPC subclass deals with semi-permeable membrane technologies where the separating element consists of undulated fibers, i.e., wavy or corrugated filamentary structures that provide a defined pore geometry. These fibers may be arranged to form membranes whose form, structure or intrinsic properties enable selective transport of substances in separation processes.
[B01D 69/085] Details relating to the spinneret -- The B01D 69/085 subclass deals with the design and characteristics of the spinneret used to produce semi-permeable membranes, covering its geometry, material composition and operational features that influence membrane formation and performance. It falls under the broader category of separating operations, specifically focusing on membrane-based separation apparatuses.
[B01D 69/087] Details relating to the spinning process -- The B01D 69/087 subclass covers aspects of the spinning process used to manufacture semi-permeable membranes employed in separation operations, focusing on how the membranes' form, structure or material properties are achieved during production. It pertains to details such as parameters, techniques or equipment that influence the resulting membrane characteristics.
[B01D 69/0871] Fibre guidance after spinning through the manufacturing apparatus -- The code B01D 69/0871 covers techniques for directing fibers immediately after they are spun through a manufacturing apparatus, specifically when the fibers are part of semi-permeable membrane systems used for separation. It includes features of the apparatus that shape, structure or otherwise control the fiber path to ensure proper alignment and functionality within the membrane-based process.
[B01D 69/088] Co-extrusion; Co-spinning -- A CPC subclass for semi-permeable membranes used in separation, focusing on manufacturing methods that combine two or more polymer streams in a single step. It covers co-extrusion and co-spinning techniques that produce membranes whose form, structure or properties are tailored for selective separation applications.
[B01D 69/10] Supported membranes -- Supported membranes are semi-permeable membranes that are mounted on a backing material or structure to provide mechanical strength and stability during separation processes. The support may be porous or non-porous and can be made of polymers, metals, ceramics or composites, allowing the membrane to retain its selective transport properties while resisting pressure or handling stresses.
[B01D 69/105] Support pretreatment -- This CPC entry covers methods and treatments applied to the supporting material before a semi-permeable membrane is formed or installed, aiming to improve the membrane's form, structure or functional properties for separation processes. It includes physical, chemical or mechanical pretreatment steps that modify the support's surface, porosity or mechanical strength, thereby enhancing membrane performance in filtration or selective separation applications.
[B01D 69/106] Membranes in the pores of a support, e.g. polymerized in the pores or voids -- B01D 69/106 covers semi-permeable membranes that are formed inside the pores or voids of a supporting material, often by polymerizing a substance directly within those spaces, and are employed in separation processes.
[B01D 69/107] Organic support material -- Organic support material denotes the polymeric or carbon-based substrate that forms the structural matrix of semi-permeable membranes used in separation processes. It provides mechanical strength, chemical stability and controlled porosity, allowing a selective layer to be applied for filtration, dialysis or gas-separation applications.
[B01D 69/1071] Woven, non-woven or net mesh -- A subclass for semi-permeable membranes used in separation processes that are constructed as woven, non-woven or net meshes, emphasizing their form, structure and material properties. It falls under CPC B01D 69/1071 within the broader categories of performing operations, separating and separation.
[B01D 69/108] Inorganic support material -- B01D 69/108 covers inorganic support materials used in semi-permeable membranes for separation processes. These supports, such as ceramics, glass fibres or metal frameworks, provide the structural backbone that defines the membrane's form, rigidity and durability, and are chosen for their chemical stability and mechanical strength.
[B01D 69/12] Composite membranes -- Semi-permeable membranes used in separation processes or apparatus, defined by their specific form, structure or material properties, are classified under CPC B01D 69/12. This entry covers composite membranes that combine multiple layers or components to achieve desired separation performance.
[B01D 69/1212] Coextruded layers -- B01D 69/1212 covers semi-permeable membranes made by co-extruding multiple polymer layers to form a composite structure where each layer contributes distinct mechanical, chemical or transport properties. The co-extruded design aims to improve separation efficiency, durability or selectivity in filtration, dialysis, gas-separation or related processes.
[B01D 69/1213] Laminated layers -- Laminated layers refer to multi-layered semi-permeable membranes whose form, structure or material properties are engineered to enhance separation performance. By combining distinct layers-each with specific permeability, mechanical strength or chemical resistance-the membrane can achieve selective transport of substances while maintaining durability in various separation processes. This classification covers such layered membrane designs and related apparatus.
[B01D 69/1214] Chemically bonded layers, e.g. cross-linking -- The code B01D 69/1214 covers semi-permeable membranes whose selective layer is created by chemically bonding materials, such as cross-linked polymer networks, to form a stable, defined structure for separation processes. It includes membranes characterised by their form, structure or properties that arise from the chemical linkage of the layers.
[B01D 69/1216] Three or more layers -- This CPC subclass B01D 69/1216 covers semi-permeable membranes used in separation processes that are constructed from three or more distinct layers, focusing on the form, structure, or material properties of multilayer membranes. It falls under the broader categories of performing operations, separating, and separation.
[B01D 69/1218] Layers having the same chemical composition, but different properties, e.g. pore size, molecular weight or porosity -- This subclass refers to semi-permeable membranes built from multiple layers of the same chemical material that are deliberately given different properties-such as distinct pore sizes, molecular-weight cut-offs or porosity-to achieve selective separation in filtration or gas-separation processes.
[B01D 69/122] Separate manufacturing of ultra-thin membranes -- The code B01D 69/122 covers the separate manufacturing of ultra-thin semi-permeable membranes, emphasizing the formation, structuring or property control of the membrane itself rather than its integration into an apparatus. It applies to processes that create membranes for filtration, gas separation or dialysis where membrane thickness is in the nanometre to micrometre range.
[B01D 69/125] In situ  manufacturing by polymerisation, polycondensation, cross-linking or chemical reaction -- B01D 69/125 relates to the in-situ fabrication of semi-permeable membranes used for separation, where the membrane is formed directly in the device by polymerisation, polycondensation, cross-linking or similar chemical reactions. The classification addresses processes that generate the membrane's form, structure or functional properties during operation rather than by pre-manufacturing.
[B01D 69/1251] by interfacial polymerisation -- This classification covers semi-permeable membranes for separation processes that are produced by interfacial polymerisation, a technique in which monomers react at the interface of two immiscible phases to form a thin polymer film. The entry emphasizes membranes distinguished by their specific form, structure or properties.
[B01D 69/127] using electrical discharge or plasma-polymerisation -- This subclass covers semi-permeable membranes and related apparatus whose form, structure or properties are produced or modified by electrical discharge or plasma-polymerisation techniques, used in separation processes. It falls under the hierarchy of Performing Operations → Separating → Separation.
[B01D 69/14] Dynamic membranes -- Dynamic membranes are self-forming, reversible filtration layers created in situ on a support during operation, consisting of a cake of suspended particles that act as a semi-permeable barrier. They are used for liquid-solid separation, providing adjustable pore size and low fouling compared with conventional fixed membranes. This classification covers their form, structure and functional properties.
[B01D 69/141] Heterogeneous membranes, e.g. containing dispersed material; Mixed matrix membranes -- B01D 69/141 pertains to heterogeneous or mixed-matrix membranes used in separation processes. These membranes consist of a continuous phase, typically a polymer, that incorporates dispersed materials such as particles, fibers or other phases to modify transport or mechanical properties. The classification covers membranes defined by their form, structure or properties rather than a specific application.
[B01D 69/1411] containing dispersed material in a continuous matrix -- The B01D 69/1411 subclass includes semi-permeable membranes in which a dispersed component such as particles, fibers or droplets is embedded within a continuous matrix material. These composites are defined by their specific form, structure or properties and are used to achieve selective separation in filtration or other membrane-based processes.
[B01D 69/14111] with nanoscale dispersed material, e.g. nanoparticles -- This subclass covers semi-permeable membranes used in separation processes that are defined by their form, structure or properties and contain nanoscale dispersed materials such as nanoparticles. The inclusion of nanoparticles aims to enhance membrane performance, selectivity or durability.
[B01D 69/142] with "carriers" -- A subclass of B01D 69/142 covering semi-permeable membranes used in separation processes or apparatuses that are defined by their form, structure or properties and that contain carrier substances to facilitate selective transport of components.
[B01D 69/144] containing embedded or bound biomolecules -- This CPC subgroup covers semi-permeable membranes that incorporate or bind biomolecules such as enzymes, antibodies, nucleic acids or other functional proteins within their matrix or on their surface. The membranes are intended for separation, filtration or catalytic processes where the embedded biomolecules provide specific selectivity, activity or recognition.
[B01D 69/145] containing embedded catalysts -- Semi-permeable membranes used in separation processes, defined by their particular form, structure or material properties, that incorporate catalysts within the membrane matrix. The embedded catalysts enable simultaneous catalytic reactions and selective permeation, improving efficiency in processes such as reactive filtration, gas separation, or solvent recovery. This design merges separation and catalysis in a single apparatus.
[B01D 69/147] containing embedded adsorbents -- The CPC subclass B01D 69/147 covers semi-permeable membranes used in separation processes that contain embedded adsorbent materials. These membranes are defined by their particular form, structure or functional properties that enable selective adsorption during filtration or related operations.
[B01D 69/148] Organic/inorganic mixed matrix membranes -- The B01D 69/148 entry covers semi-permeable membranes that combine organic and inorganic phases into a mixed matrix. It relates to membranes designed for separation processes, where the classification emphasizes the membrane's form, structure and material properties rather than a specific application. These mixed matrix membranes are used to improve selectivity, permeability or mechanical strength in various industrial separations.
[B01D 7/00] Sublimation -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Sublimation or antisublimation in general for separation.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Cold traps; cold baffles -> B01D8/00 - Freeze-drying -> F26
[B01D 7/02] Crystallisation directly from the vapour phase -- B01D 7/02 covers the process of forming solid crystals directly from a vapour, a form of sublimation where the material bypasses the liquid stage and deposits as a crystalline solid. This technique is used to obtain pure crystals or coatings by controlling temperature, pressure and vapor composition during the crystallisation step. It falls under the broader categories of performing operations, separating and separation, specifically sublimation.
[B01D 71/00] Semi-permeable membranes for separation processes or apparatus characterised by the material -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Membranes characterised by a specific material used for the separation layer and methods of preparation specifically adapted for the respective material (e.g. a specific polymer synthesis).
[B01D 71/02] Inorganic material -- B01D 71/02 in the Cooperative Patent Classification (EPO/USPTO) pertains to semi-permeable membranes used for separation processes or apparatus, specifically those characterized by their use of inorganic materials. This classification is part of a broader hierarchy that deals with performing operations and separating techniques.
[B01D 71/021] Carbon -- B01D 71/021 relates to semi-permeable membranes or membrane apparatus in which the separating material is carbon, such as activated carbon, carbon nanotubes, graphene or carbon-based composites. These carbon membranes are used for filtration, gas separation, water desalination and other separation processes.
[B01D 71/0211] Graphene or derivates thereof -- This CPC subclass covers semi-permeable membranes used in separation processes where the membrane material is graphene or its derivatives. It resides in B01D 71/02 for membranes, and the specific code 71/0211 focuses on graphene-based materials for filtration, gas separation, or other selective permeation applications.
[B01D 71/0212] Carbon nanotubes -- The CPC subclass B01D 71/0212 covers semi-permeable membranes whose material is carbon nanotubes. It relates to separation technologies where the membrane structure exploits the high surface area and selective permeability of carbon nanotube arrays for gas, liquid or ion separation. Applications include filtration, desalination and molecular sieving.
[B01D 71/0213] Silicon -- B01D71/0213 defines silicon as the material used for semi-permeable membranes in separation apparatuses, including membranes wholly or partially composed of silicon for filtration, dialysis, gas separation or related processes. It falls under the broader categories of performing operations, separating and separation.
[B01D 71/0215] Silicon carbide; Silicon nitride; Silicon oxycarbide -- B01D 71/0215 designates semi-permeable membranes employed in separation processes that are manufactured from silicon carbide, silicon nitride or silicon oxycarbide, covering both the membrane materials themselves and apparatuses that use these ceramic-based membranes for filtration, gas-liquid or other separations.
[B01D 71/022] Metals -- B01D 71/022 covers semi-permeable membranes made of metal that are used in separation processes or apparatuses. These metallic membranes allow selective passage of substances based on size or chemical affinity while providing durability and conductivity benefits.
[B01D 71/0221] Group 4 or 5 metals -- The CPC entry B01D 71/0221 pertains to semi-permeable membranes used in separation processes whose material is a metal from group 4 or group 5 of the periodic table, such as titanium, zirconium, hafnium, vanadium, niobium or tantalum. It covers membranes or apparatus where these metals form the selective barrier for filtrating liquids or gases.
[B01D 71/0223] Group 8, 9 or 10 metals -- This subclass pertains to semi-permeable membranes or separation devices whose functional material is a metal from group 8, 9 or 10 of the periodic table, such as iron, cobalt, nickel, copper or palladium. The classification addresses the use of these metal-based membranes to achieve selective separation of components in fluid streams.
[B01D 71/02231] Palladium -- The subclass B01D 71/02231 covers semi-permeable membranes or separation apparatus that use palladium, either as a membrane constituent or as a functional coating, to achieve selective transport of substances. It applies to processes and devices where palladium's catalytic or permeation properties enhance gas, liquid or ion separation performance.
[B01D 71/02232] Nickel -- B01D 71/02232 covers semi-permeable membranes or separation apparatus in which nickel is used as the membrane material or as a principal component of the membrane structure. It falls under separating operations and relates to the use of nickel for its corrosion resistance, mechanical strength and selective permeability in filtration, dialysis or gas-separation applications.
[B01D 71/0227] comprising an intermediate layer for avoiding intermetallic diffusion -- B01D 71/0227 concerns semi-permeable membranes employed in separation processes or apparatus where the membrane incorporates an intermediate layer that prevents intermetallic diffusion between adjoining materials. This feature improves durability and selectivity of the separation system and belongs to the hierarchy of Performing Operations - Separating - Separation.
[B01D 71/024] Oxides -- This subclass covers semi-permeable membranes employed in separation processes or apparatuses where the membrane material is an oxide. It includes oxide-based membranes used for filtration, ion exchange or gas separation.
[B01D 71/025] Aluminium oxide -- Aluminium oxide is employed as the membrane material in semi-permeable separation devices, providing a chemically inert, thermally stable matrix with precisely engineered pore structures. Its high hardness and durability enable selective filtration of liquids or gases in industrial and environmental applications.
[B01D 71/027] Silicium oxide -- The CPC subclass B01D-71/027 pertains to semi-permeable membranes or separation apparatus that use silicon oxide (silica) as the membrane material. It is positioned under the hierarchy of Performing Operations → Separating → Separation.
[B01D 71/0271] Perovskites -- The code B01D 71/0271 covers the use of perovskite-based materials in semi-permeable membranes for separation processes. It includes membranes whose selective layer, support or functional additives are composed of perovskite structures, enabling ion or molecule transport with specific selectivity.
[B01D 71/028] Molecular sieves  carbon  B01D71/021 -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Membranes made of or comprising metal organic framework [MOF] as separating components.
[B01D 71/0281] Zeolites -- B01D 71/0281 covers separation processes that employ semi-permeable membranes made from or incorporating zeolites. Zeolite-based membranes provide selective filtration based on pore size and ion exchange properties, enabling efficient removal of substances in gas, liquid or mixed streams. The classification falls under performing operations → separating → separation.
[B01D 71/04] Glass -- B01D 71/04 covers the use of glass as a material in semi-permeable membranes or related separation devices. It includes glass membranes, support structures or components that provide selective permeability for filtration, dialysis or other separation processes.
[B01D 71/05] Cermet materials -- Cermet materials are composite substances that combine ceramic and metal components to form semipermeable membranes with high mechanical strength, chemical resistance and selective permeability. They are used in filtration, gas-separation and catalytic membranes where durable, precisely controlled transport of molecules is required.
[B01D 71/06] Organic material -- This subclass covers semi-permeable membranes whose separating layer is made from organic compounds such as polymers, resins or other carbon-based materials. It includes membranes used in filtration, dialysis, gas separation or liquid purification where the membrane material is organic.
[B01D 71/08] Polysaccharides -- Polysaccharide-based semi-permeable membranes used in separation processes, where the membrane material itself is a polysaccharide, fall under CPC B01D 71/08. These membranes are employed for filtration, dialysis or related operations to separate components based on size or charge.
[B01D 71/10] Cellulose -- B01D 71/10 classifies cellulose as the material used in semi-permeable membranes for separation processes, such as filtration, dialysis or other size-based or affinity-based separations. It covers apparatus and methods wherein cellulose forms the selective barrier that enables the desired separation.
[B01D 71/12] Cellulose derivatives -- The CPC subclass B01D 71/12 deals with semi-permeable membranes used in separation operations that are made from cellulose derivatives such as cellulose acetate, nitrocellulose or regenerated cellulose. It covers the material composition of the membrane and its application in processes that separate components based on selective permeability.
[B01D 71/14] Esters of organic acids -- B01D 71/14 covers semi-permeable membranes or separation apparatus in which the membrane material includes or is modified with esters of organic acids. These membranes use the chemical characteristics of the ester groups to provide selective transport, improving the efficiency of liquid or gas separations.
[B01D 71/16] Cellulose acetate -- Cellulose acetate refers to membranes made from this polymer that serve as semi-permeable barriers in separation processes such as filtration, dialysis, or gas-liquid separation. The material provides selective permeability based on size or solubility, allowing certain substances to pass while retaining others.
[B01D 71/18] Mixed esters, e.g. cellulose acetate-butyrate -- This subclass covers semi-permeable membranes whose separating material is a mixed ester, for example cellulose acetate-butyrate. Such membranes are used in filtration, dialysis, or other separation processes where the polymer's selective permeability is exploited.
[B01D 71/20] Esters of inorganic acids, e.g. cellulose nitrate -- The class B01D 71/20 includes semi-permeable membranes whose material consists of esters of inorganic acids, most commonly cellulose nitrate, used as the active component in separation or filtration processes. These membranes function by allowing selective passage of substances based on size or chemical affinity.
[B01D 71/22] Cellulose ethers -- B01D 71/22 covers cellulose ethers used as the membrane material in semi-permeable separation devices. It includes membranes formed from polymers such as methylcellulose, hydroxyethylcellulose or similar derivatives that provide selective permeability for filtration, dialysis, ion exchange or other separation processes.
[B01D 71/24] Rubbers -- This CPC subclass (B01D 71/24) covers rubbers employed as semi-permeable membranes in separation processes or apparatus, focusing on the material's composition, structure and properties that enable selective permeation for filtration, dialysis or similar operations.
[B01D 71/26] Polyalkenes -- The CPC class B01D 71/26 designates semi-permeable separation membranes whose functional material is a polyalkene polymer, such as polyethylene or polypropylene. These membranes are employed in processes like filtration, gas or liquid separation, where the polymeric film provides selective permeation based on size or solubility.

[B01D 71/72] Macromolecular compounds obtained otherwise than by reactions only involving carbon-to-carbon unsaturated bonds, not provided for in a single one of the groups -- This subclass covers macromolecular compounds produced by synthetic routes other than reactions involving only carbon-carbon unsaturated bonds and that are not placed in any other specific CPC group. It includes polymers obtained through condensation, ring-opening, or other mechanisms that do not rely solely on C=C reactions.
[B01D 71/74] Natural macromolecular material or derivatives thereof -- B01D 71/74 pertains to semi-permeable membranes or separation apparatus that are made from natural macromolecular substances such as polysaccharides, proteins, or their chemically modified derivatives. [B01D 71/78] Graft polymers -- B01D71/78 covers graft polymers that form the material of semi-permeable membranes used in separation processes. These block copolymers provide distinct phases that create selective pathways, enabling controlled passage of specific molecules while rejecting others.

[B01D 9/00] Crystallisation -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Crystallisation directly from the vapour phase -> B01D7/02 - Preservation of foods or foodstuffs, in general, e.g. pasteurising, sterilising, specially adapted for foods or foodstuff: fractionated crystallisation -> A23B2/905 - Dairy products, e.g. milk, butter, cheese; milk or cheese substitutes: Concentration by freezing out the water -> A23C1/06 - Medicinal preparations characterised by special physical form: Agglomerates; Granulates; Microbeadlets : Processes -> A61K9/1682 - Crystallisation as part of the Bayer process is also classified in -> C01F7/14 - Treatment of water, waste water or sewage by freezing -> C02F1/22 - Purification/separation of acyclic or carbocyclic compounds by crystallisation -> C07C7/14 - Production of sucrose; apparatus specially adapted therefor: Crystallisation; Crystallising apparatus; Separating crystals from mother liquors -> C13B30/00 - Investigating or analyzing materials by crystallisation -> G01N25/147
[B01D 9/0004] cooling by heat exchange  by evaporation of components of the mixture to be separated  B01D9/0013 ; refrigeration machines  F25B -- B01D 9/0004 refers to cooling operations in which heat is removed from a mixture by exchanging it with a secondary medium while one or more components of the mixture evaporate, a technique employed to promote crystallisation during separation. The subclass is linked to refrigeration machines classified under F25B.
[B01D 9/0009] by direct heat exchange with added cooling fluid -- This subclass covers crystallisation methods that use direct heat-exchange with a cooling fluid added to the system, where the fluid removes heat from the mixture to promote crystal formation. It falls under the separating operations hierarchy of the CPC.
[B01D 9/0013] by indirect heat exchange -- This subgroup covers crystallisation processes that use indirect heat exchange, where the temperature of the reaction mixture is controlled through a wall or separate heat-transfer medium rather than direct contact, such as jacketed vessels, coils, or external heat exchangers. It falls under separating operations within the broader performing operations category.
[B01D 9/0018] Evaporation of components of the mixture to be separated -- B01D 9/0018 refers to the use of evaporation to remove one or more components from a mixture for separation purposes, often as a step to concentrate the remaining material before further processing such as crystallisation. This method relies on differing volatilities to achieve the separation.
[B01D 9/0022] by reducing pressure -- B01D 9/0022 pertains to crystallisation methods that separate solid material from a liquid phase by reducing the pressure, such as vacuum or low-pressure crystallisation, where the pressure drop drives solvent evaporation and crystal formation.
[B01D 9/0027] by means of conveying fluid, e.g. spray-crystallisation  spray-drying  F26B -- The subclass B01D 9/0027 covers crystallisation methods that rely on conveying a liquid containing dissolved substances, typically by atomising the fluid into droplets and then drying or cooling them so that crystals form, as in spray-crystallisation or spray-drying. These processes are classified under separation operations.
[B01D 9/0031] by heating  B01D9/0022 ,  B01D9/0027  take precedence -- B01D 9/0031 covers crystallisation methods performed by heating, taking precedence over the earlier subclasses B01D 9/0022 and B01D 9/0027. It is nested under the hierarchy Performing Operations → Separating → Separation → Crystallisation and defines the heating-driven approach to induce solid formation from a solution.
[B01D 9/0036] Crystallisation on to a bed of product crystals; Seeding -- The code B01D 9/0036 covers crystallisation processes in which a bed of product crystals is used as a substrate for further growth, typically by adding seed crystals to initiate or control crystal formation. This method promotes uniform crystal size, improves purity and can accelerate recovery of the desired solid phase.
[B01D 9/004] Fractional crystallisation; Fractionating or rectifying columns -- B01D 9/004 covers the use of fractional crystallisation techniques and the design or operation of fractionating or rectifying columns to separate components based on differences in solubility or crystallisation behavior. It falls under the broader class of performing operations, specifically separating, separation, and crystallisation processes.
[B01D 9/0045] Washing of crystals, e.g. in wash columns -- The code B01D 9/0045 defines processes for washing crystalline products, typically carried out in wash columns, to remove residual mother liquor, solvents or impurities after crystallisation. It falls under the separation branch of performing operations.
[B01D 9/005] Selection of auxiliary, e.g. for control of crystallisation nuclei, of crystal growth, of adherence to walls; Arrangements for introduction thereof -- The subclass concerns the choice of additives or auxiliaries that influence crystallisation by controlling nucleation, directing crystal growth, or altering crystal adhesion to vessel surfaces, together with the devices or methods for introducing these substances into the crystallisation operation.
[B01D 9/0054] Use of anti-solvent -- The entry B01D 9/0054 covers crystallisation processes in which an anti-solvent is introduced to a solution to precipitate the desired compound, aiding its separation as a solid. It includes the selection, addition, and mixing conditions of the anti-solvent to control nucleation and crystal growth.
[B01D 9/0059] General arrangements of crystallisation plant, e.g. flow sheets -- This subclass falls under performing operations - separating - separation - crystallisation and covers the overall layout and organization of equipment and streams in a crystallisation plant, such as the design of flow sheets, interconnections, and material handling. It addresses how units like reactors, mixers, separators and downstream processing are arranged to achieve efficient crystal formation and recovery.
[B01D 9/0063] Control or regulation  control  per se   G05 -- B01D 9/0063 relates to methods or devices that directly control the crystallisation step, adjusting parameters such as temperature, supersaturation, agitation or seeding to regulate crystal formation, size, shape or purity. This sub-class falls under Performing Operations → Separating → Separation → Crystallisation and concerns the regulation of the crystallisation process itself.
[B01D 9/0068] Prevention of crystallisation -- This classification covers methods and agents used to inhibit or delay the formation of crystals during a process, aiming to keep substances in a dissolved or dispersed state. It includes chemical additives, temperature control, and other techniques that prevent unwanted crystallisation in industrial or laboratory operations.
[B01D 9/0072] Crystallisation in microfluidic devices -- The CPC entry B01D 9/0072 covers crystallisation processes carried out in microfluidic devices, where tiny channels and chambers guide fluid flow, mixing and nucleation at the microscale. It includes methods and apparatus that use micro-scale confinement to control crystal size, polymorphism and yield for pharmaceutical, chemical or material-science applications. This sub-class falls under Performing Operations → Separating → Separation → Crystallisation.
[B01D 9/0077] Screening for crystallisation conditions or for crystal forms -- The code B01D 9/0077 covers methods that test a range of conditions or additives to identify those that promote the formation of crystals or yield specific crystal polymorphs. It includes systematic exploration of variables such as temperature, pH, solvents, and precipitants to optimise crystallisation processes. The entry is used to classify research and development activities focused on discovering suitable crystallisation parameters or novel crystal forms.
[B01D 9/0081] Use of vibrations, e.g. ultrasound -- B01D 9/0081 concerns methods that apply mechanical vibrations, such as ultrasound, to facilitate or control crystallisation during a separation process. The vibrations may be used to promote nucleation, influence crystal growth rates, or modify particle size distribution.
[B01D 9/02] from solutions -- The CPC subgroup B01D 9/02 pertains to crystallisation performed directly from solutions, where a solute is induced to form solid crystals by cooling, evaporation, anti-solvent addition or similar techniques. It covers processes that separate the desired solid product from the surrounding liquid phase.
[B01D 9/04] concentrating solutions by removing frozen solvent therefrom -- B01D 9/04 pertains to methods for concentrating a solution by freezing it and then separating the solidified solvent, typically water, from the remaining liquid phase. The technique forms solvent crystals that are removed, leaving a more concentrated solution and is commonly employed in freeze-concentration or ice-separation processes.
[B01F] MIXING, e.g. DISSOLVING, EMULSIFYING OR DISPERSING -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Agitation and/or homogenisation of products formed by a combination of two or more components with the purpose of obtaining a homogeneous composition or homogeneous conditions in the mass of material.  Stirring of a single material with the purpose of obtaining homogeneous conditions in the mass of material.  This subclass covers the mixing, agitation and homogenisation of materials, irrespective of the application in which it is produced, whenever the device and/or method is directed to achieve said desired effect.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Mixing paints -> B44D3/06  **Glossary:** - Mixing: stirring of a single material
[B01F 2025/91] Direction of flow or arrangement of feed and discharge openings -- B01F 2025/91 covers inventions that define the direction of fluid flow by the relative positioning of feed and discharge openings, including designs that arrange these openings to achieve a specific flow pattern in pumps, mixers, reactors or similar processing equipment.
[B01F 2025/911] Axial flow -- This CPC subgroup covers axial-flow pumps, in which the fluid moves parallel to the rotating shaft driven by an impeller or propeller. It includes designs for circulation, irrigation, marine propulsion and similar applications where a straight-through flow path is required.
[B01F 2025/912] Radial flow -- B01F 2025/912 refers to devices that produce a radial flow of fluid, discharging the material outward from a central axis through a ring-shaped or multiple peripheral openings. The code is applied to spray or atomising equipment that distributes liquids, gases or powders in a circular pattern for uniform coverage.
[B01F 2025/9121] from the center to the circumference, i.e. centrifugal flow -- This subgroup covers technologies where fluid is conveyed radially outward from a central axis by centrifugal forces, such as centrifuges, vortex separators, and rotary filters. It includes designs that generate, guide or use such outward flow for separation, mixing or transport.
[B01F 2025/9122] from the circumference to the center -- Techniques for supplying or moving fluids, gases, or solid matter from the outer wall of a fermentation vessel toward its central region, such as radial injection, internal conduits or stirring, to achieve uniform conditions throughout the reactor.
[B01F 2025/913] Vortex flow, i.e. flow spiraling in a tangential direction and moving in an axial direction -- The B01F 2025/913 subclass refers to technologies that create or exploit a vortex flow, in which a fluid spirals around a central axis while simultaneously moving along that axis. It includes devices such as mixers, separators, reactors and pumps that rely on this combined tangential-axial motion to enhance mixing, separation or propulsion.
[B01F 2025/914] Tangential flow, i.e. flow spiraling in a tangential direction in a flat plane or belt-like area -- B01F 2025/914 refers to processes that create a tangential flow, where the fluid moves in a spiral path parallel to a flat or belt-like surface. This flow pattern is used to improve mixing, heat transfer, or separation in reactors, channels, or conveyance systems.
[B01F 2025/915] Reverse flow, i.e. flow changing substantially 180° in direction -- B01F 2025/915 covers devices or processes where a fluid or gas flow is redirected to move in a direction opposite its original path, typically involving a 180-degree change. It includes mechanisms such as elbows, U-bends, or reversible pumps that achieve reverse flow for mixing, pressure control, or recirculation.
[B01F 2025/916] Turbulent flow, i.e. every point of the flow moves in a random direction and intermixes -- B01F 2025/916 covers technologies and methods for creating or handling turbulent flow, where fluid particles move in random, multidirectional paths that constantly intermix. It includes apparatus and processes that generate turbulence to enhance mixing, heat transfer, or reaction rates in chemical, biochemical, or mechanical engineering applications. This subclass is used for inventions that rely on chaotic flow behavior rather than laminar or orderly movement.
[B01F 2025/917] Laminar or parallel flow, i.e. every point of the flow moves in layers which do not intermix -- The CPC subclass B01F 2025/917 covers technologies in which a fluid moves in a laminar or parallel manner, meaning each fluid layer slides past adjacent layers without intermixing. It applies to equipment, methods, and applications that rely on stable, non-turbulent flow, such as microfluidic channels, heat exchangers, and certain separation processes.
[B01F 2025/9171] Parallel flow, i.e. every point of the flow moves in parallel layers where intermixing can occur by diffusion or which do not intermix; Focusing, i.e. compressing parallel layers without intermixing them -- B01F 2025/9171 relates to devices or processes that handle fluids flowing in parallel layers, where the layers may exchange material only by diffusion and do not intermix turbulently, and to methods that compress or concentrate those parallel layers without causing mixing, such as fluid-focusing techniques used in micro-reactors or separation units.
[B01F 2025/918] Counter current flow, i.e. flows moving in opposite direction and colliding -- B01F 2025/918 refers to arrangements in spray or atomising devices where two fluid streams are directed opposite each other so that they meet and collide. This counter-current flow geometry is employed to enhance mixing, improve atomisation, or achieve specific reaction or cleaning effects within the nozzle or downstream region.
[B01F 2025/919] characterised by the disposition of the feed and discharge openings -- B01F 2025/919 covers apparatuses in the field of chemical or physical processing where the technical characterisation is based on the relative positioning of the feed and discharge openings. It includes reactors, mixers, separators or other treatment units whose performance is defined by how these inlet and outlet ports are arranged, such as opposite, adjacent, or staggered configurations, influencing flow patterns and product recovery.
[B01F 2025/9191] characterised by the arrangement of the feed openings for one or more flows, e.g. for the mainflow and the flow of an additional component -- A subclass of B01F that covers apparatus or processes in which the feed openings are configured to admit two or more separate streams, such as a primary material flow together with an auxiliary component. The focus is on the spatial arrangement of these inlet points to facilitate combined or staged feeding within the same unit.
[B01F 2025/91911] with feed openings in the center of the main flow -- The code covers apparatus for mechanically treating solids in which the material is introduced through feed openings located at the centre of the main flow path, such as central inlets of crushers, grinders or mixers that direct the feed into the core of the moving stream. This design promotes uniform processing and efficient energy use.
[B01F 2025/91912] with feed openings at the circumference of the main flow -- The CPC entry B01F 2025/91912 covers liquid-treatment apparatus in which one or more feed openings are positioned around the outer edge of the main flow channel, allowing the feed stream to enter the process stream circumferentially. This arrangement enhances contact between phases and is useful in aeration, stripping or filtration devices.
[B01F 2025/919121] with feed openings around the complete circumference of the main flow, e.g. being a perforated or porous part -- The code B01F 2025/919121 covers mixing or dispersing apparatus that features feed openings distributed around the full circumference of the principal flow path. Typical implementations include perforated or porous sleeves, rings or plates that introduce material uniformly into the moving stream to improve homogeneity.
[B01F 2025/919125] with feed openings in the center and at the circumference of the main flow -- The CPC subclass B01F 2025/919125 covers devices for handling fluids or particles where inlet ports are placed both at the centre of the main flow channel and around its outer edge. This arrangement enables simultaneous central-peripheral feeding, improving distribution and mixing efficiency in reactors, mixers or similar processing equipment.
[B01F 2025/91913] with feed openings facing each other, e.g. for creating counter flows, for creating a series of vortex flows -- B01F 2025/91913 covers devices in which two or more feed openings are positioned to face each other so that incoming streams flow oppositely, creating counter-flows or a succession of vortex flows. This arrangement promotes intense mixing, improved mass-transfer and uniform residence times within reactors, mixers, or similar process equipment.
[B01F 2025/93] Arrangements, nature or configuration of flow guiding elements -- B01F 2025/93 covers patents that focus on the design, arrangement or geometric characteristics of elements that direct the movement of fluids, gases, or particles within reactors, separators, fluidised-bed or similar process equipment. It includes baffles, vanes, plates or other flow-guiding structures whose configuration influences mixing, residence time, pressure drop or flow uniformity.
[B01F 2025/931] Flow guiding elements surrounding feed openings, e.g. jet nozzles -- The CPC subclass B01F 2025/931 covers devices that shape or direct the flow of liquids or gases around feed openings, typically by means of jet nozzles or similar flow-guiding elements. It includes arrangements that improve injection, mixing, or discharge of fluids by controlling the stream as it enters or exits the system.
[B01F 2025/932] Nature of the flow guiding elements -- The CPC subclass B01F 2025/932 groups inventions concerning the nature of flow-guiding elements in fluid-handling devices. It includes patents that disclose the material, geometry or physical configuration of components such as vanes, blades, baffles, diffusers or similar structures that control, direct or condition the movement of liquids or gases.
[B01F 2025/9321] Surface characteristics, e.g. coated or rough -- B01F 2025/9321 concerns the modification or analysis of a material's surface properties, including the application of coatings, creation of roughness or texture, and other treatments that change how the surface interacts with its environment. It covers technologies for achieving specific adhesion, wear resistance, optical, or functional characteristics through surface engineering.
[B01F 2035/35] Use of other general mechanical engineering elements in mixing devices -- B01F 2035/35 covers mixing devices that incorporate general mechanical-engineering components not otherwise defined in the classification, such as gears, cams, levers, shafts, bearings or other standard motion-transfer elements. These features are used to drive, control or improve the mixing action of the apparatus.
[B01F 2035/351] Sealings -- B01F 2035/351 covers sealings employed in chemical engineering equipment, including gaskets, O-rings, packing and similar devices that prevent leakage between fluid-containing parts of reactors, vessels, pipelines and auxiliary units.
[B01F 2035/3511] for laboratory mixers -- B01F 2035/3511 covers devices specifically designed for mixing substances in laboratory settings, such as vortex mixers, magnetic stirrers and other apparatus that agitate small volumes of liquids or suspensions. The subclass includes mechanisms, control systems and accessories that enable precise, reproducible mixing for analytical, preparative or testing purposes.
[B01F 2035/3512] Fluid sealings, e.g. using liquids or air under pressure which is leaking into the mixing receptacle -- The CPC subclass B01F 2035/3512 pertains to fluid sealing techniques that employ a pressurised liquid or gas to prevent leakage into a mixing receptacle, creating a barrier that isolates the interior during operation. It covers devices or processes that use such fluid-based seals to maintain a closed environment and protect the mixture from external contaminants.
[B01F 2035/3513] comprising a stationary member in frontal contact with a movable member -- This subclass covers devices in which a fixed element is placed to face a moving part, creating a frontal contact surface that enables separation, filtration or washing of solids and liquids. Typical examples are rotating drums, belts or plates where a stationary screen, sieve or filter element contacts the moving member to convey material while performing the separation.
[B01F 2035/352] Bearings -- The CPC subclass B01F 2035/352 covers bearings, devices that support rotating or oscillating shafts and reduce friction between moving parts. It includes ball, roller, plain, fluid-film and magnetic bearings, as well as related components such as housings, retainers and lubrication arrangements designed for load bearing and motion control.
[B01F 2035/353] Lubricating systems -- The CPC subclass B01F 2035/353 covers lubricating systems used in machines, including the design, arrangement and operation of devices that deliver, distribute or apply lubricants to moving parts. It includes apparatus such as pumps, spray nozzles, reservoirs and control mechanisms that maintain proper lubrication during operation.
[B01F 2035/98] Cooling -- B01F 2035/98 relates to the cooling stage in mechanical chemical processing, covering methods for removing heat from reaction mixtures, solids or liquids during manufacture. It includes the use of external cooling media, jackets, heat exchangers, quench streams or spray devices to lower product temperature and control thermal conditions.
[B01F 2035/99] Heating -- CPC B01F 2035/99 groups inventions that relate to heating processes or apparatuses not covered by more specific heating subclasses. It includes general methods and equipment for raising the temperature of solids, liquids or gases in industrial or laboratory settings, such as furnaces, heaters, and heat exchangers that do not fall under specialized categories like microwave, induction or combustion heating.
[B01F 21/00] Dissolving -- B01F 21/00 pertains to methods and apparatus for dissolving solid or liquid materials in a solvent, including techniques such as agitation, heating, the use of additives, or other means to promote solubilisation and produce homogeneous solutions or dispersions.
[B01F 2101/00] Indexing schemes associated with groups -- This subclass covers methods and systems for organising, coding or referencing groups of substances, particles or components used in mixing, separating or dispersing operations. It includes classification tables, index numbers, labeling schemes and other indexing tools that allow specific groups to be identified, retrieved or tracked during processing.
[B01F 2101/005] Mixing or agitating manure, dung -- B01F 2101/005 covers processes for mixing or agitating manure or dung, typically to improve homogenisation, enhance microbial activity, or facilitate handling and storage. It falls under the broader categories of performing operations, separating, and mixing, and is used to classify technologies that dissolve, emulsify or disperse solid waste in agricultural or biogas applications.
[B01F 2101/02] Mixing or agitating during harvesting or mowing, e.g. mixing with solid harvested products or particles -- B01F 2101/02 covers processes that mix or agitate material while it is being harvested or mowed, such as combining the freshly cut crop with solid particles, powders or other harvested products. The subclass falls under the broader hierarchy of performing operations, separating, and mixing (including dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing). It is used to classify equipment or methods that integrate mixing steps directly into the harvesting or mowing operation.

It falls under the broader categories of performing operations, separating and mixing, specifically focusing on preparing formulations of crop-protection chemicals.

[B01F 2101/59] Mixing reaction ingredients for fuel cells -- This CPC subclass deals with processes for mixing reaction ingredients used in fuel cell production, including dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing components to form electrolytes, catalyst layers or other cell parts. [B01F 21/02] Methods -- B01F 21/02 covers methods for dissolving solid substances in liquid media, including techniques to enhance dissolution rate such as heating, applying agitation, using auxiliary agents or particular solvent compositions, and controlling process parameters to achieve efficient solubilisation. This subclass falls under mixing operations within the broader category of separating processes.
[B01F 21/10] using driven stirrers -- The code refers to processes that employ mechanically powered stirrers to agitate a material in order to promote dissolution, emulsification or dispersion. It includes any driven mixing device used during a dissolving operation, such as impellers, turbines or rotating paddles, whether in batch or continuous reactors.
[B01F 21/15] comprising constructions for blocking or redispersing undissolved solids, e.g. sieves, separators or guiding constructions  B01F21/221  takes precedence -- This CPC subclass covers mixing or dissolving operations that include devices to trap, separate or guide solids that have not dissolved, such as sieves, separators or other guiding constructions, enabling their subsequent redispersion. It is positioned under the broader categories of performing operations, separating and mixing. More detailed provisions are found in subclass B01F21/221, which takes precedence.
[B01F 21/20] using flow mixing -- B01F 21/20 covers processes that achieve mixing, dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing by inducing flow of the materials, such as through jets, spirals, turbulence or other fluid-movement techniques. It includes equipment and methods that rely on the motion of the fluids themselves rather than mechanical agitation to create a homogeneous mixture.
[B01F 21/22] using additional holders in conduits, containers or pools for keeping the solid material in place, e.g. supports or receptacles -- B01F 21/22 covers methods of dissolving that employ extra supports, holders or receptacles inside pipes, vessels or tanks to retain solid material in place while it is mixed with a liquid. The classification includes designs such as brackets, cages or trays that keep particles from moving freely, facilitating controlled dissolution, emulsifying or dispersion.
[B01F 21/221] comprising constructions for blocking or redispersing undissolved solids -- B01F 21/221 covers apparatus or methods used in dissolving operations that include features to block, retain, or re-disperse solid particles that have not fully dissolved, ensuring more uniform mixtures and preventing agglomeration during processes such as mixing, emulsifying or dispersing.
