[B01F 27/2714] the relative position of the stator and the rotor, gap in between or gap with the walls being adjustable -- This subclass concerns mixers whose stator and rotor, or the space between the rotor and surrounding walls, can be repositioned or have an adjustable gap, allowing the clearance to be varied to control shear forces, mixing intensity, or residence time within a fixed receptacle.
[B01F 27/272] with means for moving the materials to be mixed axially between the surfaces of the rotor and the stator, e.g. the stator rotor system formed by conical or cylindrical surfaces -- B01F 27/272 relates to mixers that move the material to be mixed axially between the rotor and stator surfaces, typically using a stator-rotor arrangement with conical or cylindrical surfaces. This sub-class falls under mixing operations within the broader separating category of the Cooperative Patent Classification.
[B01F 27/2721] provided with intermeshing elements -- This subclass covers mixers that operate in a fixed receptacle using rotary stirring devices fitted with intermeshing elements such as gear-like or lobed projections that engage each other during rotation to increase shear and improve the distribution of liquids or powders. It is classified under separating operations, specifically mixing processes like dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing.
[B01F 27/2722] provided with ribs, ridges or grooves on one surface -- B01F 27/2722 pertains to rotary mixers in stationary vessels where one mixing surface is equipped with ribs, ridges or grooves to boost agitation, aiding operations such as dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing. It is classified under performing operations - separating - mixing in the CPC system.
[B01F 27/2723] the surfaces having a conical shape -- This subgroup relates to mixers that employ a rotary stirring device within a fixed receptacle whose internal surfaces are shaped conically, a geometry that aids the dispersion, dissolution or emulsification of materials during operation.
[B01F 27/2724] the relative position of the stator and the rotor, gap in between or gap with the walls being adjustable -- It covers mixers where the rotor is mounted inside a fixed stator within a stationary container, and the gap between them or between the gap and the walls can be set or varied. The classification is used for devices that adjust the clearance to control shear, mixing intensity, or residence time.
[B01F 27/276] the mixer being composed of a stator-rotor system being formed by bearing elements, e.g. roller bearings -- B01F 27/276 relates to mixers that use a rotary stirring device within a fixed vessel, where the mixing assembly consists of a stator-rotor arrangement supported by bearing elements such as roller bearings. This subclass falls under the hierarchy Performing Operations → Separating → Mixing → Mixers with rotary stirring devices.
[B01F 27/40] Mixers with rotor-rotor system, e.g. with intermeshing teeth -- B01F 27/40 covers mixers that employ a rotor-rotor arrangement, where two rotating elements with intermeshing teeth or similar profiles engage each other to convey and blend material within a fixed vessel. The design provides high shear and efficient dispersion for processes such as dissolving, emulsifying, or dispersing solids and liquids.
[B01F 27/41] with the mutually rotating surfaces facing each other -- B01F 27/41 covers mixers that employ mutually facing rotating surfaces, such as intermeshing rotors or opposed stirrers, housed within a fixed receptacle to achieve mixing, dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing of materials. The design creates shear between the opposing surfaces, enhancing mass transfer and homogenisation.
[B01F 27/411] provided with intermeshing elements -- It denotes mixers that employ rotary stirring devices within a fixed receptacle where the stirring elements mesh together, such as gear-type or intermeshing impellers, for tasks like mixing, dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing substances. The code B01F 27/411 resides under the CPC hierarchy performing operations - separating - mixing, e.g. dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing.
[B01F 27/412] provided with ribs, ridges or grooves on one surface -- Mixers that use a rotary stirring device within a fixed container and feature ribs, ridges or grooves on one interior surface. The textured surface creates turbulence and improves the distribution of solids or liquids, aiding operations such as dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing.
[B01F 27/42] with rotating surfaces next to each other, i.e. on substantially parallel axes -- The CPC subclass B01F 27/42 defines mixers that use rotary stirring devices mounted in a fixed vessel where the rotating surfaces are placed side-by-side on substantially parallel axes, allowing simultaneous agitation for processes such as dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing.
[B01F 27/421] provided with intermeshing elements -- B01F 27/421 covers mixers that employ rotary stirring devices inside a stationary vessel where the moving parts consist of intermeshing elements such as twin-screws or gear-like rotors. The intermeshing geometry creates shearing and dispersive action useful for operations like dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing, while the receptacle itself remains fixed. This subgroup falls under separating-mixing processes within the broader performing-operations class.
[B01F 27/422] provided with ribs, ridges or grooves on one surface -- This CPC subgroup concerns rotary mixers placed in stationary vessels whose interior surface is patterned with ribs, ridges or grooves to improve shear and flow for processes such as dissolution, emulsification or dispersion. The textured surface enhances mixing efficiency by creating turbulence and preventing dead zones.
[B01F 27/50] Pipe mixers, i.e. mixers wherein the materials to be mixed flow continuously through pipes, e.g. column mixers -- B01F 27/50 covers pipe mixers, where the material to be mixed moves continuously through a series of pipes or a column, achieving mixing by the flow and internal geometry of the conduit. These devices are used for processes such as dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing substances in a steady stream.
[B01F 27/55] with stirrers driven by the moving material -- This CPC subclass B01F 27/55 covers mixers that use rotary stirring devices fixed inside a stationary receptacle, where the stirring motion is supplied directly by the flow of the material being processed rather than by an external drive. It applies to equipment such as turbine or propeller agitators that are driven by the moving medium itself.
[B01F 27/60] with stirrers rotating about a horizontal or inclined axis -- B01F 27/60 identifies mixers that employ rotary stirring devices in fixed receptacles where the stirrers rotate about a horizontal or inclined axis, facilitating operations such as dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing. It lies within the hierarchy of performing operations, separating and mixing.
[B01F 27/61] about an inclined axis -- This subclass covers mixers where the rotating shaft or stirring element is mounted at an angle relative to the horizontal, allowing material to be conveyed along the inclined axis while being mixed, typically used for solids or semi-solids in a stationary container. The design enhances flow and reduces dead zones, useful for blending powders, granules or viscous liquids.
[B01F 27/62] comprising liquid feeding, e.g. spraying means -- B01F 27/62 covers mixers that have a rotary stirring device mounted in a stationary container and incorporate a means for feeding liquid, such as spray nozzles, into the material being mixed. This subclass is used for equipment where the liquid is introduced during the mixing operation to aid dissolution, emulsification or dispersion.
[B01F 27/621] the liquid being fed through the shaft of the stirrer -- B01F 27/621 covers mixers that have a rotary stirring device mounted in a stationary vessel and feed the liquid directly through the stirrer shaft, enabling reagent introduction via the rotating axis. This arrangement is used in processes such as dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing where precise delivery into the mixing zone is required.
[B01F 27/625] the receptacle being divided into compartments, e.g. with porous divisions -- This subclass concerns mixers that employ a fixed vessel equipped with a rotary stirrer, where the vessel is divided into separate compartments-often by porous partitions-to enable isolated or staged mixing, dissolution, emulsification or dispersion within each compartment.
[B01F 27/627] the receptacles being tiltable, e.g. for emptying -- B01F 27/627 classifies mixing apparatus that use a rotary stirrer within a stationary vessel whose receptacle can be tilted to aid emptying, allowing the mixed material to be discharged by angling the container rather than by separate unloading mechanisms. This subcategory falls under mixers with rotary devices in fixed receptacles within the broader mixing and separating operations.
[B01F 27/63] co-operating with deflectors or baffles fixed to the receptacle -- The code refers to mixers that employ a rotary stirring device inside a fixed vessel where the flow is enhanced by deflectors or baffles permanently attached to the receptacle, helping to disperse, dissolve or emulsify the material.
[B01F 27/65] with buckets -- This CPC subclass covers mixing apparatus where a rotary stirring element is housed in a fixed-receptacle and the material to be mixed is placed in one or more detachable buckets that rotate with the stirrer. The design enables efficient processing of powders, liquids or suspensions while allowing easy loading and discharge.
[B01F 27/70] with paddles, blades or arms -- B01F 27/70 refers to mixers that employ rotating paddles, blades or arms inside a fixed vessel to achieve mixing actions such as dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing. It is a sub-category of mixing devices used in separating operations.
[B01F 27/701] comprising two or more shafts, e.g. in consecutive mixing chambers -- The class B01F 27/701 defines mixers that employ two or more rotating shafts, often arranged in successive mixing chambers, to achieve sequential or combined mixing actions such as dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing. The shafts are fixed within a stationary vessel.
[B01F 27/702] with intermeshing paddles -- This subgroup refers to mixers that use a pair of intermeshing paddles rotating within a stationary vessel to blend, dissolve, emulsify or disperse materials. The paddles are geared together so their teeth interlock, providing shear and mixing action in a fixed receptacle.
[B01F 27/703] with stirrers rotating at different speeds -- B01F 27/703 covers mixers that have rotary stirring devices mounted in a fixed container where the stirrers rotate at different speeds. This subcategory falls under mixing operations, specifically separating processes, and applies to equipment designed to achieve variable shear or blending by using multiple impellers driven at distinct rotational rates.
[B01F 27/704] with stirrers facing each other, i.e. supported by opposite walls of the receptacle -- B01F 27/704 covers mixers that use rotary stirrers mounted on opposite walls of a fixed vessel so that the stirring elements face each other. This arrangement creates counter-rotating flow fields that improve the dispersion, dissolution or emulsification of materials within the receptacle. It is a subclass of mixers for separating and mixing operations in the CPC system.
[B01F 27/705] with stirrers rotating in opposite directions about the same axis, e.g. with a first stirrer surrounded by a tube inside a second stirrer -- B01F 27/705 covers mixers that use rotary stirring devices fixed in a single container and employ two stirrers that rotate in opposite directions about the same axis, such as a central tube stirrer surrounded by an outer stirrer, to enhance mixing, dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing processes. This configuration provides improved shear and turbulence compared with single-direction stirring, aiding efficient separation and homogenisation of liquids or suspensions.
[B01F 27/706] with all the shafts in the same receptacle -- This subclass covers mixers that have multiple rotary shafts mounted within a single fixed vessel, allowing simultaneous stirring or agitation by all shafts in the same container, typically used for processes such as dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing.
[B01F 27/707] the paddles co-operating, e.g. intermeshing, with elements on the receptacle wall -- B01F 27/707 covers mixers in which rotating paddles intermesh or otherwise cooperate with fixed elements on the receptacle wall, such as baffles or protrusions, to enhance the mixing action. The arrangement is used in stationary vessels for processes like dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing substances.
[B01F 27/708] characterised by the shape of the stirrer as a whole, i.e. of Z- or S-shape -- B01F 27/708 pertains to mixers with rotary stirring devices in fixed receptacles, specifically those characterized by the overall shape of the stirrer being Z- or S-shaped. This classification is part of the Cooperative Patent Classification system used by the EPO and USPTO for patent documentation.
[B01F 27/71] with propellers -- B01F 27/71 identifies mixers that employ propeller-type rotary stirring devices inside a fixed receptacle to carry out separating operations such as dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing. These devices belong to the broader CPC groups for performing operations, separating and mixing.
[B01F 27/711] co-operating with stationary guiding means, e.g. baffles -- This CPC subclass B01F 27/711 covers mixers with rotary stirring devices in fixed receptacles that cooperate with stationary guiding means such as baffles to direct flow, improve mixing efficiency and prevent vortex formation. It resides within the mixing sub-group of the separating category of performing operations.
[B01F 27/7111] the guiding means being tubes surrounding the propellers -- B01F 27/7111 covers mixers that use rotary stirring devices mounted in a stationary container where the propeller is enclosed or guided by tubular shrouds. The surrounding tubes direct the fluid flow and improve mixing efficiency, suitable for operations such as dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing within fixed receptacles.
[B01F 27/72] with helices or sections of helices -- Mixers that use one or more helices or partial helices as the rotating stirring element inside a stationary container, providing a continuous axial and radial flow to improve dissolution, emulsifying or dispersing of materials. The helical geometry creates a sweeping motion that efficiently mixes liquids or suspensions while the receptacle remains fixed. This design is classified under CPC B01F 27/72 for rotary stirrers with helical sections.
[B01F 27/721] with two or more helices in the same receptacle -- B01F 27/721 relates to mixers that use two or more helical stirring elements positioned together within a single fixed receptacle, generating complementary flow patterns to enhance dissolution, emulsification or dispersion of substances during mixing operations.
[B01F 27/722] the helices closely surrounded by a casing -- B01F 27/722 pertains to mixers in which one or more helically shaped stirrers are housed in a tightly fitting surrounding casing within a fixed vessel. The close clearance between the helices and the casing creates high shear forces that promote rapid dissolution, emulsification or dispersion of materials.
[B01F 27/7221] the stirrers being composed of helices and paddles on the same shaft, e.g. helically arranged ovally shaped paddles -- The subclass B01F 27/7221 covers mixers that use a single rotating shaft equipped with both helices and paddles, such as helically arranged oval-shaped paddles, to stir material within a fixed vessel. This design combines the cutting action of helices with the broader agitation of paddles for efficient mixing, dispersing, or dissolving of substances.
[B01F 27/723] the helices intermeshing to knead the mixture -- B01F 27/723 covers mixers that use intermeshing helices to knead a material within a fixed vessel. The design provides rotary stirring where the helices rotate together, creating a shearing action that mixes, dissolves, or disperses the components.
[B01F 27/724] with a single helix closely surrounded by a casing -- This subclass covers mixers that employ a single helical screw closely surrounded by a casing, mounted in a fixed receptacle to provide rotary stirring for operations such as dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing liquids.
[B01F 27/725] with two or more helices in respective separate casings, e.g. one casing inside the other -- B01F 27/725 covers mixers that employ two or more helical stirrers mounted in separate casings, for example one helix enclosed within another, to provide rotary stirring in a fixed receptacle. This subclass falls under mixing operations that involve separating, dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing substances.
[B01F 27/726] with two helices with opposite pitch on the same shaft -- B01F 27/726 designates mixers that employ a single rotating shaft fitted with two helical elements wound in opposite directions. The counter-rotating helices create a complex flow that enhances dispersion, dissolution or emulsification within a fixed vessel. This configuration is used for operations that require intensive mixing or separation.
[B01F 27/73] with rotary discs -- B01F 27/73 covers mixers that use rotary discs to stir or agitate material inside a fixed container. The discs rotate to create shear and promote mixing, suitable for processes such as dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing. This subclass falls under mixers with rotary stirring devices in fixed receptacles within the separating and mixing section of the CPC.
[B01F 27/731] with two or more parallel shafts provided with perpendicularly mounted discs, e.g. lens shaped, one against the other on each shaft and in circumferential contact with the discs on the other shafts, e.g. for cleaning -- This CPC subclass pertains to mixers that employ two or more parallel rotating shafts, each carrying discs mounted perpendicular to the shaft and shaped like lenses, positioned opposite one another so that the disc edges contact each other around the circumference. The arrangement is used in fixed receptacles, often to create cleaning action while mixing, dispersing, or emulsifying materials.
[B01F 27/74] with rotary cylinders -- B01F 27/74 covers mixers that employ one or more rotary cylinders inside a stationary container to agitate a material. The rotating cylinders create shear and turbulence that dissolve, emulsify or disperse the contents, and are used where a fixed receptacle is required for continuous or batch mixing operations.
[B01F 27/75] with stirrers having planetary motion, i.e. rotating about their own axis and about a sun axis -- B01F 27/75 covers mixers in which the stirring elements execute a planetary motion, rotating on their own axes while simultaneously revolving around a central "sun" axis, and are used in fixed receptacles for operations such as mixing, dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing.
[B01F 27/755] the stirrers being cylinders, balls or gears -- B01F 27/755 covers mixers that use rotary stirring devices fixed inside a receptacle, where the stirrers are cylinders, balls or gear-shaped elements. These mixers create shear and turbulent flow for operations such as dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing within the stationary vessel.
[B01F 27/80] with stirrers rotating about a substantially vertical axis -- This subgroup covers mixing apparatus in which a stirrer is mounted to rotate around a generally vertical axis within a stationary container. The design is used for operations such as dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing where the rotary motion is directed vertically. It falls under the broader category of mixers with rotary stirring devices in fixed receptacles.
[B01F 27/805] wherein the stirrers or the receptacles are moved in order to bring them into operative position -- B01F 27/805 covers mixing devices in which either the stirrer, the vessel, or both are repositioned to engage the rotor with the material, enabling the stirring action to commence. This includes mechanisms that shift the stirrer or the fixed receptacle into the correct operating alignment during the mixing process.
[B01F 27/806] with vertical displacement of the stirrer, e.g. in combination with means for pivoting the stirrer about a vertical axis in order to co-operate with different receptacles -- B01F 27/806 covers mixers that use a rotary stirrer fixed in a vessel but allow the stirrer to move vertically or pivot about a vertical axis so it can adapt to different vessel shapes or depths. The design enables efficient mixing while accommodating varying receptacle sizes in processes such as dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing.
[B01F 27/807] with the stirrer-head pivoting about a horizontal axis to bring it in and out of operative position, e.g. with receptacles pivoting about a horizontal axis for emptying -- The code B01F 27/807 covers mixers that use a rotary stirring device in a fixed vessel, where the stirrer-head is mounted so it can pivot about a horizontal axis to move into or out of the mixing position; the same concept applies to vessels that pivot horizontally for emptying.
[B01F 27/808] with stirrers driven from the bottom of the receptacle -- The CPC subclass B01F 27/808 covers mixers in which the rotary stirring device is mounted in a fixed receptacle and the stirrer is powered from the bottom of the vessel. This arrangement is used for operations such as dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing where bottom-driven agitation improves mixing efficiency. It falls under the hierarchy of Performing Operations → Separating → Mixing, e.g., mixing, dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing.
[B01F 27/81] the stirrers having central axial inflow and substantially radial outflow -- B01F 27/81 covers mixers that employ a rotary stirring element inside a stationary container where the material enters the stirrer along the axis and is expelled mainly in a radial direction, creating a vortex that enhances dissolution, emulsification or dispersion.
[B01F 27/811] with the inflow from one side only, e.g. stirrers placed on the bottom of the receptacle, or used as a bottom discharge pump -- This subclass covers mixing equipment that uses a rotary stirrer mounted in a stationary vessel where the material enters the vessel from a single side, typically through a bottom-placed stirrer that also acts as a discharge pump. It is used for processes such as dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing.
[B01F 27/8111] the stirrers co-operating with stationary guiding elements, e.g. surrounding stators or intermeshing stators  B01F27/812  takes precedence -- B01F 27/8111 covers mixers that use rotary stirring devices inside a fixed vessel together with stationary guiding elements such as surrounding or intermeshing stators that shape the flow. The design coordinates the moving stirrers with these fixed components to enhance mixing, and it is subordinate to B01F 27/812.
[B01F 27/812] the stirrers co-operating with surrounding stators, or with intermeshing stators, e.g. comprising slits, orifices or screens -- The B01F 27/812 subclass covers mixers in which a rotary stirrer works together with surrounding stators or intermeshing stators, such as those formed by slits, orifices or screens, within a fixed vessel. This arrangement is used for operations like dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing where the stator geometry assists the stirring action. It falls under the broader categories of performing operations, separating, and mixing.
[B01F 27/813] the stirrers co-operating with stationary guiding elements  B01F27/812  takes precedence -- This CPC subclass defines mixers where the rotary stirring element works together with fixed guiding components inside a stationary vessel. The cooperative arrangement directs flow and improves dispersion, dissolution or emulsification. It refines the broader category of mixers with rotary devices in fixed receptacles and supersedes subclass B01F27/812.
[B01F 27/82] Pan-type mixers, i.e. mixers in which the stirring elements move along the bottom of a pan-shaped receptacle -- Pan-type mixers are devices where the stirring elements travel along the bottom of a pan-shaped vessel, providing agitation for processes such as dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing. The design keeps the material contained in a fixed receptacle while the rotating blades or paddles move beneath the material to achieve uniform mixing. This subclass falls under separating operations within the broader performing operations group.
[B01F 27/83] the stirrers being additionally moved radially, or oscillating about an axis perpendicular to the stirrer axis -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The stirrer oscillates about a horizontal axis due to the way the stirrer is connected to the mixer, e.g. by springs.  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this place:
[B01F 27/84] with two or more stirrers rotating at different speeds or in opposite directions about the same axis -- B01F 27/84 covers mixers that use two or more rotary stirrers mounted in a fixed container, where the stirrers rotate at different speeds or in opposite directions around the same axis. This arrangement is used to enhance dispersion, dissolution or emulsification by creating complex flow patterns.
[B01F 27/85] with two or more stirrers on separate shafts -- This subgroup covers mixers that employ two or more stirrers each driven by separate shafts, mounted in a fixed receptacle for operations such as dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing. It is part of the broader CPC hierarchy for mixing and separating processes.
[B01F 27/851] the receptacle being subdivided in adjacent compartments -- This CPC subclass covers mixers where the container is divided into side-by-by compartments, each receiving material separately for simultaneous processing. It pertains to rotary stirring devices in fixed vessels that have internal partitions creating adjacent chambers.
[B01F 27/86] co-operating with deflectors or baffles fixed to the receptacle -- The subclass B01F 27/86 covers mixers that use a rotary stirring device inside a fixed vessel together with stationary deflectors or baffles attached to the receptacle. The baffles cooperate with the agitator to create turbulence, improve flow patterns and enhance processes such as dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing.
[B01F 27/861] the baffles being of cylindrical shape, e.g. a mixing chamber surrounding the stirrer, the baffle being displaced axially to form an interior mixing chamber -- The subclass B01F 27/861 covers mixers that use cylindrical baffles forming a mixing chamber around a rotary stirrer; the baffles are displaced axially so that an interior mixing zone is created. This design provides a fixed receptacle in which the rotating device operates, improving dispersion, dissolving, emulsifying or other mixing actions.
[B01F 27/862] the baffles being adjustable or movable  B01F27/861  takes precedence -- This subclass covers mixers that employ a rotary stirring element in a fixed vessel where the internal baffles are designed to be adjustable or movable, allowing the flow pattern and mixing action to be altered during operation. It falls under the broader categories of separating and mixing processes, and is subordinated to B01F 27/861, which takes precedence.
[B01F 27/87] the receptacle being divided into superimposed compartments -- A rotary-stirred mixer in a fixed vessel where the container is split into stacked, superimposed compartments, allowing separate but simultaneous processing of materials within a single unit. This design enables distinct zones for steps such as dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing while maintaining overall mixing action. It is classified under B01F 27/87 for mixers with compartmentalised receptacles.
[B01F 27/88] with a separate receptacle-stirrer unit that is adapted to be coupled to a drive mechanism -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Stirrers with a separate receptacle-stirrer unit, which can be connected to a drive, e.g. motor, when required.
[B01F 27/90] with paddles or arms -- The subclass B01F 27/90 covers mixers that employ rotary stirring devices in a fixed receptacle, where the stirrer consists of paddles or arms attached to a rotating shaft. It pertains to mixing operations such as dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing performed within stationary vessels.
[B01F 27/902] cooperating with intermeshing elements fixed on the receptacle walls -- B01F 27/902 covers mixers that combine a rotary stirring device with fixed intermeshing elements mounted on the walls of the receptacle. The intermeshing geometry enhances the shearing and turbulent flow for operations such as dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing liquids and solids within the vessel.
[B01F 27/9021] the elements being vertically arranged, e.g. fixed on the bottom -- The code B01F 27/9021 covers mixing devices that use a rotary stirrer inside a fixed vessel where the mixing elements are positioned vertically, such as being mounted on the bottom of the container, to promote thorough dispersion or emulsification.
[B01F 27/906] with fixed axis -- This CPC subclass covers mixers that employ a rotary stirring device mounted on a fixed axis within a stationary receptacle, where the agitator rotates while the container remains immobile. It applies to batch-type equipment used for dissolving, emulsifying, dispersing or otherwise mixing substances.
[B01F 27/91] with propellers -- B01F 27/91 covers mixers that employ propellers within a fixed vessel to create a rotary stirring action. The propeller blades rotate in a stationary receptacle, providing the mechanical energy needed for operations such as dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing substances. It falls under the broader categories of performing operations, separating, and mixing.
[B01F 27/911] forcing the material through orifices or slits, e.g. in a stationary part -- B01F 27/911 pertains to mixers with rotary stirring devices where the material is forced through orifices or slits, typically in a stationary component, enhancing mixing efficiency and material dispersion.
[B01F 27/92] with helices or screws -- The B01F 27/92 group covers mixers that use helices or screw-type rotary stirrers inside a fixed vessel to achieve separating, mixing, dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing actions. These devices rely on the helical geometry to convey material and enhance fluid movement within the receptacle.
[B01F 27/921] with helices centrally mounted in the receptacle -- B01F 27/921 designates mixers that employ helices mounted centrally in a fixed receptacle to provide rotary stirring for operations such as dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing. The classification falls under the hierarchy of performing operations, separating and mixing, and relates to equipment where the helical agitator is positioned at the centre of the vessel.
[B01F 27/9211] the helices being surrounded by a guiding tube -- B01F 27/9211 covers a mixing device in which one or more helically shaped stirrers are encased within a tubular guide that directs the material flow around the helices. The surrounding tube creates a confined channel that enhances shearing and dispersion while keeping the rotating elements in a fixed receptacle. This arrangement is used for efficient dissolution, emulsification or dispersion of liquids or suspensions.
[B01F 27/92112] combined with means for uniting flows of material taken from different parts of the receptacle -- This subgroup covers mixers that employ a rotary stirring element in a fixed vessel and include apparatus for joining material streams taken from different positions within the receptacle, improving mixing uniformity.
[B01F 27/9212] with conical helices -- B01F 27/9212 covers mixers that use conical helical agitators inside stationary vessels to carry out separating and mixing actions such as dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing. The conical helix creates combined axial and radial flow, improving material contact and homogenisation. It is a subcategory of rotary stirring devices in fixed receptacles.
[B01F 27/9213] the helices having a diameter only slightly less than the diameter of the receptacle -- This classification covers mixers that use a rotary helical stirrer whose diameter is only slightly smaller than the container it occupies, providing near-wall contact for efficient dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing. The design is employed in fixed-receptacle mixing equipment to maximize shear and homogenization.
[B01F 27/9214] with additional mixing elements other than helices; having inner and outer helices; with helices surrounding a guiding tube -- Subclass B01F 27/9214 defines mixers that employ a rotary stirrer in a fixed vessel and incorporate additional mixing elements beyond simple helices, such as paired inner and outer helices or helices that surround a guiding tube, supporting operations like dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing.
[B01F 27/922] with two or more helices, e.g. with intermeshing helices -- This subclass covers mixers that use a rotary stirring device within a stationary container and feature two or more helically shaped elements, such as intermeshing helices, to improve mixing, dissolution, emulsifying, or dispersing of materials.
[B01F 27/923] the material flowing continuously through the receptacle -- This CPC subgroup covers mixers that use a rotary stirring device in a stationary vessel where the process material flows continuously through the receptacle, enabling ongoing dissolution, emulsifying or dispersing operations.
[B01F 27/93] with rotary discs -- The B01F 27/93 subclass covers mixers that use rotary discs mounted inside a fixed receptacle to perform mixing, separating, dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing operations. It specifies devices where the disc rotation creates the desired agitation within the container. This entry falls under the CPC hierarchy of Performing Operations → Separating → Mixing, e.g. dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing.
[B01F 27/94] with rotary cylinders or cones -- B01F 27/94 covers mixers that employ rotary cylinders or conical elements to agitate material held in a stationary container. The devices are used for operations such as dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing by rotating the cylinder or cone within the fixed receptacle.
[B01F 27/941] being hollow, perforated or having special stirring elements thereon -- B01F 27/941 covers mixers whose rotary stirring devices are hollow, perforated, or equipped with specially shaped stirring elements. These features allow fluids to flow through or around the stirrer, enhancing mixing, dissolution, emulsifying or dispersing actions within a fixed receptacle.
[B01F 27/95] with stirrers having planetary motion, i.e. rotating about their own axis and about a sun axis -- The CPC subclass B01F 27/95 falls under mixing operations and designates mixers that employ stirrers with planetary motion, meaning each stirrer rotates on its own axis while simultaneously revolving around a central "sun" axis within a fixed vessel. This arrangement is used for processes such as dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing materials.
[B01F 27/951] with at least one stirrer mounted on the sun axis -- The CPC subclass B01F 27/951 covers mixers that use rotary stirring devices positioned in a fixed receptacle where at least one stirrer is mounted on a central, sun-type axis. It applies to equipment for dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing mixtures by means of a radially arranged stirrer.
[B01F 27/952] the stirrers being cylinders with their circumference in contact with the bottom of the receptacle and rotating about an axis at an angle to the sun axis, e.g. mixers of the Muller type -- B01F 27/952 classifies mixers that employ cylindrical stirrers whose outer surface contacts the bottom of a fixed receptacle while rotating about an axis inclined to the vertical, as found in Müller-type mixers. The subclass falls under mixing operations within the broader categories of separating and performing operations.
[B01F 27/953] using only helical stirrers -- This subclass covers mixing apparatus that employ only helical stirrers for rotary stirring within a stationary vessel. It includes devices where the helical element performs the agitation without additional stirring or moving parts, typically for dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing materials.
[B01F 27/96] with openwork frames or cages -- This subclass covers mixers that employ a rotary stirring device within a fixed receptacle where the stirrer is held by an openwork frame or cage, allowing material to pass through while the structure remains stable. It falls under mixing and separating operations for dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing.
[B01F 29/00] Mixers with rotating receptacles -- Mixers with rotating receptacles are devices in which the container holding the material to be processed itself rotates, creating agitation that blends, dissolves, emulsifies or disperses components. The rotation may be driven directly or via an eccentric mechanism, and the design often includes fixed or removable blades, paddles or baffles to enhance the mixing action.
[B01F 29/10] with receptacles rotated about two different axes, e.g. receptacles having planetary motion -- B01F 29/10 covers mixing devices whose receptacles rotate about two distinct axes, such as planetary mixers that combine a rotating basket with a secondary orbital motion to enhance blending, dispersion and homogenisation.
[B01F 29/15] Use of centrifuges for mixing -- B01F 29/15 relates to using centrifuges as mixers, where the rotating receptacle generates centrifugal forces to blend, dissolve, emulsify or disperse substances, fitting within the broader categories of separating and mixing operations.
[B01F 29/20] with receptacles rotating about an axis at an angle to their longitudinal axis -- B01F 29/20 covers mixing devices whose receptacles rotate about an axis that is inclined relative to the receptacle's longitudinal axis, causing the container to tumble or swivel during operation. This angular rotation enhances agitation and improves homogenisation of liquids, powders or dispersions.
[B01F 29/25] with material flowing continuously through the receptacles from inlet to discharge -- Mixers in which one or more receptacles rotate while material continuously enters, travels through the moving chamber, and exits at a discharge point. The design keeps the product in motion during processing, enabling efficient mixing, dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing without batch stopping. This subclass covers continuous-flow rotary-mixing apparatus.
[B01F 29/251] with at least one screw inside the receptacle for feeding or discharging, e.g. the axis of screw and receptacle being parallel -- The B01F 29/251 subclass defines mixers whose rotating vessel contains at least one screw that transports material for feeding or discharge, with the screw axis aligned parallel to the receptacle. This design is applied in operations such as dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing where a combined mixing and conveying action is required.
[B01F 29/252] the feed and discharge openings being at opposite ends of the receptacle -- This subclass covers mixers with a rotating receptacle where the feed opening is at one end and the discharge opening at the opposite end, allowing continuous feeding and removal of material during mixing, dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing operations. It falls under CPC group B01F 29/252 within the performing operations - separating - mixing hierarchy.
[B01F 29/253] the feed and discharge openings being at the same side of the receptacle -- B01F 29/253 covers mixers with a rotating receptacle in which both the material feed opening and the discharge opening are positioned on the same side of the vessel, allowing material to enter and exit without crossing the internal flow and enabling a more compact equipment layout.
[B01F 29/30] Mixing the contents of individual packages or containers, e.g. by rotating tins or bottles -- B01F 29/30 covers devices that mix the contents of individual packaged units, such as tins, bottles or sachets, by rotating the container itself. The classification is used for mixers whose rotating receptacle holds a single pre-filled package, enabling dissolution, emulsifying or dispersing directly inside the original container.
[B01F 29/31] the containers being supported by driving means, e.g. by rotating rollers -- B01F 29/31 covers mixers that employ rotating receptacles in which the containers are held and moved by driving means such as rotating rollers, allowing operations like dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing while the container remains supported during the mixing process.
[B01F 29/32] Containers specially adapted for coupling to rotating frames or the like -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Containers, e.g. separate tumblers or receptacles, which are specially adapted for coupling to the rotating frame of the mixer, e.g. by using an indentation on the container wall.
[B01F 29/321] of test-tubes or the like -- B01F 29/321 covers mixing apparatus where the receptacle rotates and is adapted to hold test-tubes or similar small containers. These devices are used for operations such as dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing substances in laboratory glassware. The classification falls under mixers with rotating receptacles within the broader categories of performing operations, separating, and mixing.
[B01F 29/322] of two or more containers supported for simultaneous mixing, e.g. for bottles in crates -- The entry B01F 29/322 covers rotary mixers that hold two or more containers together for simultaneous mixing, such as devices that spin bottles while they remain in crates. It falls under CPC section B01F for mixers with rotating receptacles, within the broader categories of performing operations, separating, and mixing.
[B01F 29/33] by imparting a combination of movements to two or more containers -- This subclass covers mixers in which rotating receptacles are moved together with a second motion-such as reciprocating, rocking or translating-so that two or more containers experience a combination of movements, enabling dissolution, emulsifying or dispersing of materials.
[B01F 29/331] by means of a rotary table provided with a plurality of bottle grippers at its periphery, an additional movement being imparted to the grippers -- B01F 29/331 covers mixers that use a rotary table fitted with a plurality of bottle-grippers arranged around its periphery. The configuration gives each gripper an extra movement, improving the handling and mixing of bottles during operations such as dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing.
[B01F 29/332] the bottles being submitted to a screw-motion about an axis perpendicular to the axis of the bottles and lying intermediate the ends of the bottles -- The code B01F 29/332 relates to mixers where the containers themselves rotate in a screw-like motion. In this arrangement the bottles are turned about an axis that is perpendicular to the bottle's longitudinal axis and positioned midway between the bottle ends, providing mixing through combined rotation and axial displacement.
[B01F 29/333] essentially by rotating bottles about an axis perpendicular to the bottle axis and lying outside the bottles, using a rotating drum provided with pockets for the bottles at its periphery -- B01F 29/333 covers mixers that hold bottles in pockets around the periphery of a drum which rotates about an axis perpendicular to the bottle axes, causing the bottles to turn around that external axis to perform operations such as dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing.
[B01F 29/34] Constructional details of holders for the individual packages or containers -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Package or container holders of the mixer which are shaped so as to hold the individual package or container.
[B01F 29/40] Parts or components, e.g. receptacles, feeding or discharging means  B01F29/251  takes precedence -- B01F 29/40 designates components of mixers having rotating containers, such as the receptacle itself and the mechanisms that feed material into or discharge it. It falls under the broader class of mixing operations within the CPC hierarchy. The more specific subclass B01F 29/251 is prior.
[B01F 29/401] Receptacles, e.g. provided with liners -- B01F 29/401 pertains to receptacles used in mixers with rotating containers, covering designs that may incorporate liners to contain, protect or separate the material during mixing. It falls under the broader categories of performing operations, separating, and mixing such as dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing.
[B01F 29/4011] characterised by the shape or cross-section of the receptacle, e.g. of Y-, Z -, S -, or X shape -- B01F 29/4011 identifies mixers whose rotating receptacle has a distinctive shape or cross-section, such as Y-, Z-, S- or X-type profiles. The classification falls under performing operations, separating and mixing, and covers devices where the geometry of the chamber is a defining functional element.
[B01F 29/40111] Non-cylindrical sections, e.g. elliptical or irregular -- The CPC subclass B01F 29/40111 defines mixers that employ a rotating receptacle whose shape deviates from a simple cylinder, such as elliptical or irregular cross-sections. This non-cylindrical geometry is used to modify fluid motion, shear or dispersion compared with conventional cylindrical mixers.
[B01F 29/40112] Polygonal sections, e.g. triangularor square -- The code B01F 29/40112 designates mixers whose rotating receptacles have polygonal cross-sections such as triangular or square shapes. These polygonal sections are used to enhance mixing, dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing by creating distinct flow patterns within the rotating vessel.
[B01F 29/40113] Conical, double-conicalor diabolo shapes -- The B01F 29/40113 entry classifies mixers that employ a rotating receptacle shaped as a cone, double cone or diabolo. These geometries are used to enhance mixing actions such as dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing by creating specific flow patterns within the vessel. It falls under the broader category of mixers with rotating receptacles within the mixing operations class.
[B01F 29/40114] Cubic, cubical or polyhedronical shapes -- The B01F 29/40114 entry defines mixers whose rotating receptacle is shaped as a cube, cuboid or other polyhedron. It falls under Performing Operations → Separating → Mixing (including dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing) and covers designs that use cubic or polyhedral geometries to facilitate the mixing process.
[B01F 29/40115] S shapes -- This subclass identifies mixers that use rotating receptacles with an S-shaped profile. The S-shaped geometry creates a serpentine flow path that increases shear and improves mixing efficiency in operations such as dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing.
[B01F 29/40116] Spherical shapes -- B01F 29/40116 refers to mixers with rotating receptacles that are specifically adapted for handling spherical shapes such as beads or droplets during mixing operations. The classification falls under performing operations, separating and mixing categories.
[B01F 29/40117] Toroidal shapes -- B01F 29/40117 covers mixers that use a toroidal-shaped rotating receptacle, where material circulates around a donut-like chamber to achieve dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing. The toroidal geometry creates continuous flow and shear, improving mixing efficiency in batch or continuous processes.
[B01F 29/40118] V or W shapes -- The B01F 29/40118 subclass covers mixers whose rotating receptacle is formed with V- or W-shaped interior geometries. These stepped or angular profiles promote turbulent flow and improve dispersion, dissolution or emulsification of materials during the mixing operation.
[B01F 29/40119] X shapes -- B01F 29/40119 covers mixers whose rotating receptacles are arranged in an X-shaped configuration, enabling simultaneous agitation and material flow in multiple directions during mixing, dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing operations.
[B01F 29/401195] Y or double Y shapes -- The code B01F 29/401195 covers mixers whose rotating receptacles are formed in a Y or double-Y shape. These devices use the branched geometry to enhance stirring, dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing of materials while the vessel rotates. It falls within the broader class for mixing operations under performing operations and separating.
[B01F 29/402] characterised by the relative disposition or configuration of the interior of the receptacles -- B01F 29/402 relates to mixers that have rotating receptacles whose interior is defined by the spatial arrangement or geometry of the chambers, such as their relative orientation, shape or positioning, which influences the mixing, dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing action.
[B01F 29/4021] Multi-compartment receptacles -- Multi-compartment receptacles are mixing vessels that contain two or more separate chambers within a single rotating container, allowing different liquids or powders to be kept apart until they are combined during operation. They are used in equipment that mixes, dissolves, emulsifies or disperses substances while preventing premature contact between components.
[B01F 29/4022] Configuration of the interior -- B01F 29/4022 covers the internal design and arrangement of mixers with rotating receptacles used for separating operations such as dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing. The code specifies features such as the shape, placement of baffles, blades or liners that influence fluid flow and mixing efficiency within the rotating vessel.
[B01F 29/40221] provided with baffles, plates or bars on the wall or the bottom -- The CPC subclass B01F 29/40221 covers mixers whose rotating vessel is equipped with baffles, plates or bars fixed to the wall or bottom of the receptacle. These internal elements are intended to improve the mixing action, such as dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing, by creating turbulence and preventing dead zones. This falls under the broader categories of performing operations, separating, and mixing.
[B01F 29/40222] provided with guide tubes on the wall or the bottom -- Mixers that incorporate a rotating receptacle and feature guide tubes fixed to the vessel wall or bottom to direct material flow during mixing, facilitating controlled dispersion, dissolution or emulsification. This design falls under the separating-mixing subgroup of performing-operations equipment.
[B01F 29/4023] Nature of the receptacle -- B01F 29/4023 classifies mixers with rotating receptacles based on the nature of the receptacle, such as its shape, material or construction, which affects the mixing, dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing action.
[B01F 29/40231] Surface characteristics, e.g. coated, rough -- This CPC subclass B01F 29/40231 covers mixers with rotating receptacles whose interior surfaces are specially treated - for example coated, textured or roughened - to affect the mixing, dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing of materials. It lies under Performing Operations → Separating → Mixing, e.g. dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing.
[B01F 29/403] Disposition of the rotor axis -- B01F 29/403 defines mixers that use a rotating receptacle and specifies how the rotor axis is arranged within the device. The subclass addresses the disposition of the rotor axis relative to the mixing chamber for operations such as dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing.
[B01F 29/4031] horizontal -- B01F 29/4031 covers mixers that have a rotating receptacle oriented horizontally, where the mixing action is produced by the movement of the container rather than internal agitators. These devices are used for processes such as dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing substances by tumbling the material within a horizontally-aligned chamber.
[B01F 29/4032] vertical -- B01F 29/4032 covers vertical mixers in which the mixing chamber itself rotates, often as a cylindrical or tubular receptacle that turns around a vertical axis while the material is processed. These devices are used for operations such as dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing where the rotation of the container assists in achieving uniform mixing. The classification falls under the CPC hierarchy of Performing Operations → Separating → Mixing, e.g., Dissolving, Emulsifying or Dispersing.
[B01F 29/4033] inclined -- B01F 29/4033 classifies mixers whose rotating receptacle is set at an incline rather than horizontally. The angled container uses gravity together with rotation to improve separating, dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing actions during mixing operations. This subclass falls under the broader grouping of mixing processes in the CPC system.
[B01F 29/4034] variable, e.g. tiltable during the operation -- The subclass B01F 29/4034 covers mixers that have rotating receptacles whose position can be varied-such as being tiltable-while the machine is operating, allowing the container to change orientation during mixing, dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing processes. It is part of the CPC hierarchy under Performing Operations → Separating → Mixing, e.g., dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing.
[B01F 29/4035] with a receptacle rotating around two or more axes -- B01F 29/4035 designates mixers whose receptacle rotates around two or more axes, providing complex motion for operations such as dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing within a separating process. These devices are classified under the broader categories of performing operations, separating and mixing in the Cooperative Patent Classification system.
[B01F 29/40351] having different, non-perpendicular inclinations, e.g. skew axes -- This subclass covers mixers whose rotating vessels are mounted at non-perpendicular, skewed angles relative to each other, allowing varied stirring actions. It falls under mixing devices within the performing operations and separating hierarchy of the Cooperative Patent Classification.
[B01F 29/40352] being parallel axes -- The CPC subclass B01F 29/40352 defines mixers having rotating receptacles whose shafts or axes are arranged parallel to each other, allowing simultaneous agitation of multiple streams in a parallel-axis configuration. It resides under the hierarchy of performing operations, separating, and mixing such as dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing.
[B01F 29/40353] being perpendicular axes -- B01F 29/40353 covers mixers whose rotating receptacles are driven about axes that are perpendicular to each other, enabling multidirectional flow and enhanced dispersion of liquids or solids. This arrangement is used in processes such as dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing where thorough blending is required.
[B01F 29/40354] arranged for planetary motion -- The classification covers mixers in which the mixing receptacle itself rotates on an axis while simultaneously revolving around a central point, creating planetary motion that enhances blending, dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing of materials. These devices often have a fixed outer housing and a rotating inner vessel that follows an orbital path.
[B01F 29/4036] with a plurality of rotating receptacles -- Mixers that contain several rotating receptacles within a single device, each receptacle turning relative to the others or to the housing to perform operations such as dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing. The multiple rotating containers enable simultaneous or staged mixing actions in a compact unit.
[B01F 29/40361] having axes of different, non-perpendicular inclinations -- B01F 29/40361 covers mixers with rotating receptacles driven by shafts whose axes are inclined at different, non-perpendicular angles. This arrangement creates complex flow patterns that can enhance the blending, dissolving or dispersing of liquids or powders.
[B01F 29/40362] having parallel axes -- This subgroup of B01F 29/4036 covers mixers where the rotating containers are arranged with their rotational axes parallel to each other, typically used for simultaneous processing in batch or continuous operations, enabling uniform mixing or dispersion.
[B01F 29/40363] having perpendicular axes -- In the CPC subclass B01F 29/40363, "having perpendicular axes" designates mixers whose rotating receptacle is driven by a shaft set at a right angle to the receptacle's own axis. This arrangement is used in mixing processes such as dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing, and falls under the broader category of mixing operations within the separating group.
[B01F 29/40364] being concentrically arranged -- B01F 29/40364 pertains to mixers that use rotating receptacles arranged concentrically, meaning one rotating vessel is positioned inside another to enhance mixing actions such as dissolution, emulsification or dispersion. This subclass falls under the broader group for separating and mixing operations.
[B01F 29/40365] arranged for planetary motion -- B01F 29/40365 covers mixers whose receptacles rotate while simultaneously revolving around a central axis, providing planetary motion. This arrangement combines a rotating motion of the mixing container with an orbital movement, enhancing the dispersing, dissolving or emulsifying action in batch processes.
[B01F 29/60] rotating about a horizontal or inclined axis, e.g. drum mixers -- B01F 29/60 covers mixers whose receptacle rotates about a horizontal or inclined axis, such as drum-type mixers. The classification applies to devices that achieve mixing, dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing by rotating the container rather than the agitator, and is nested under separating and mixing operations for rotating receptacles.
[B01F 29/61] comprising liquid spraying devices -- The CPC subclass B01F 29/61 pertains to mixers that employ a rotating receptacle and integrate one or more liquid-spraying devices to introduce fluid onto the material being mixed. This arrangement facilitates operations such as dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing by directly spraying liquids into the moving mass, improving homogeneity and process efficiency.
[B01F 29/62] without bars, i.e. without mixing elements -- This subclass covers mixing devices where the container itself rotates to create flow, and the design contains no internal bars, paddles or other mixing elements. Such mixers rely on the motion of the receptacle to achieve dissolution, emulsification or dispersion of materials.
[B01F 29/63] with fixed bars, i.e. stationary, or fixed on the receptacle -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Mixers with stationary bars that are optionally fixed to the receptacle.  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this place:
[B01F 29/64] with stirring devices moving in relation to the receptacle, e.g. rotating -- B01F 29/64 covers mixers that have a rotating container and a stirring element that moves relative to the container, such as a rotor or blade that turns while the receptacle spins. This arrangement improves mixing, dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing processes.
[B01F 29/80] rotating about a substantially vertical axis -- B01F 29/80 pertains to mixers in which the container itself rotates about a substantially vertical axis to achieve mixing, such as tumblers, rotary drum mixers, and other equipment where the receptacle's movement provides agitation for processes like dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing. The classification falls under mixers with rotating receptacles within the separating and mixing subgroup of performing operations.
[B01F 29/81] with stationary mixing elements -- B01F 29/81 covers mixers that have a stationary mixing component while the vessel or receptacle rotates, such as devices where blades or paddles are fixed and the container turns to achieve mixing, dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing.
[B01F 29/82] the receptacle comprising a rotary part, e.g. the bottom, and a stationary part, e.g. the wall, with optional use of a stirrer; the receptacle comprising parts moving in opposite directions -- This subclass covers mixing apparatus where the vessel is divided into a rotating section such as a bottom and a stationary section such as the wall, optionally combined with an internal stirrer. It includes designs in which the moving parts rotate in opposite directions to achieve dissolution, emulsification or dispersion.
[B01F 29/83] with rotary paddles or arms, e.g. movable out of the receptacle -- B01F 29/83 pertains to mixers that employ rotary paddles or arms, often positioned within a rotating receptacle, to facilitate separating and mixing actions such as dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing.
[B01F 29/835] Pan-type mixers, i.e. having stirrers moving along the bottom of a pan-shaped receptacle -- B01F 29/835 covers pan-type mixers that incorporate stirrers travelling along the bottom of a pan-shaped vessel, enabling operations such as separating, dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing by rotating the receptacle while the agitators move across its floor.
[B01F 29/84] with propellers -- B01F 29/84 covers mixers that incorporate propellers within a rotating container to agitate, dissolve, emulsify or disperse materials. The propeller blades rotate with the receptacle, providing shear and turbulent flow for efficient mixing. This subclass falls under mixing operations within the broader separating and performing operations group.
[B01F 29/85] with helices, e.g. rotating about an inclined axis -- B01F 29/85 covers mixing apparatus that uses helical elements rotating on an inclined axis to convey and blend material. The helices act as both transport and agitation devices, enabling processes such as dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing within a rotating receptacle. This subclass falls under mixers with rotating receptacles in the broader category of separating operations.
[B01F 29/86] with rotary discs -- B01F 29/86 designates mixers that employ one or more rotating discs as the receptacle or agitator to disperse, dissolve or emulsify substances. The rotary discs generate shear and turbulence to achieve effective mixing.
[B01F 29/87] with rotary cylinders -- B01F 29/87 covers mixers that employ one or more rotary cylinders as the primary moving element for blending, dispersing, or emulsifying materials. These devices typically have a rotating cylindrical chamber that creates shear and turbulence to achieve uniform mixing of liquids, powders, or suspensions.
[B01F 29/90] with stirrers having planetary motion -- The subclass B01F 29/90 covers mixers that have a rotating receptacle combined with stirrers that move on a planetary orbit within the chamber, providing simultaneous rotation of the container and a secondary stirring motion to improve dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing of materials.
[B01F 31/00] Mixers with shaking, oscillating, or vibrating mechanisms -- Mixers classified under B01F 31/00 belong to the broader group of separating operations and are designed to achieve mixing, dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing by means of shaking, oscillating or vibrating movements. These devices use mechanical agitation rather than rotating impellers to promote rapid and uniform distribution of components in liquids or suspensions.
[B01F 31/10] with a mixing receptacle rotating alternately in opposite directions -- B01F 31/10 covers mixers whose mixing vessel rotates alternately in opposite directions, often using shaking, oscillating or vibrating mechanisms. This subclass falls under performing operations, separating, and mixing processes such as dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing, and is used to describe equipment that enhances blend uniformity by reversing the receptacle's rotation during operation.
[B01F 31/20] Mixing the contents of independent containers, e.g. test tubes -- B01F 31/20 covers devices that mix the contents of separate containers, such as test tubes, by employing shaking, oscillating or vibrating actions. The classification pertains to equipment designed to combine liquids or solids from independent vessels without direct transfer between them.
[B01F 31/201] Holders therefor -- Holders therefor refers to components designed to retain the material being processed within mixers that use shaking, oscillating or vibrating actions. These include tubs, drums, vats or other containers and supporting structures that secure the product while it is mixed, dissolved, emulsified or dispersed. They are classified under B01F 31/201 in the CPC.
[B01F 31/202] for beverage bottles, e.g. within crates or with feeding means for the bottles -- B01F 31/202 pertains to mixers designed specifically for shaking, oscillating, or vibrating mechanisms used in the context of beverage bottles. This includes setups where bottles are mixed while contained in crates or systems that incorporate feeding mechanisms for individual bottles.
[B01F 31/22] with supporting means moving in a horizontal plane, e.g. describing an orbital path for moving the containers about an axis which intersects the receptacle axis at an angle -- B01F 31/22 defines mixers whose supporting mechanism moves in a horizontal plane, typically following an orbital path that circles an axis intersecting the vessel's axis at an angle, to aid processes such as dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing. This subclass falls under the broader hierarchy of performing operations, separating and mixing, and specifies the geometry and motion of the supporting means rather than the mixing tool itself.
[B01F 31/23] by pivoting the containers about an axis -- This subclass covers mixing apparatus where the container is pivoted around an axis to create shaking or oscillation. It includes devices that achieve dissolution, emulsification or dispersion by rotating the vessel rather than moving a rotor inside. Used in processes where gentle or intermittent mixing is needed.
[B01F 31/231] the containers being of the sandglass-type or being linked with their openings -- B01F 31/231 classifies mixing apparatus that employ shaking, oscillating or vibrating motions and whose containers are shaped like a sandglass or are joined together via their openings, enabling material to move between chambers during the mixing operation.
[B01F 31/24] the containers being submitted to a rectilinear movement -- B01F 31/24 relates to mixing devices in which the container undergoing mixing is moved in a straight-line (rectilinear) path, rather than being rotated or rocked. This class covers equipment that uses shaking, oscillating or vibrating mechanisms to translate the vessel linearly for operations such as dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing.
[B01F 31/25] the containers being submitted to a combination of movements other than within a horizontal plane, e.g. rectilinear and pivoting movement -- B01F 31/25 designates mixing processes where the containers are moved by combined motions that are not confined to a horizontal plane, such as linear and pivoting actions. It applies to equipment that shakes, oscillates or vibrates the container while also imparting rectilinear or angular displacement to enhance dissolution, emulsifying or dispersing.
[B01F 31/26] the containers being submitted to a wobbling movement -- B01F 31/26 concerns mixers that employ a wobbling motion of the containers, using shaking, oscillating or vibrating actions to achieve mixing, dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing of the material they hold. The subclass falls under mixing operations within separating processes of performing operations.
[B01F 31/265] the vibrations being caused by an unbalanced rotating member -- B01F 31/265 covers mixers in which the mixing action is produced by vibrations generated by an intentionally unbalanced rotating component. The imbalance creates oscillatory motion that promotes dissolution, emulsification or dispersion of materials. It falls under the broader categories of performing operations, separating, and mixing.
[B01F 31/27] the vibrations being caused by electromagnets -- B01F 31/27 denotes mixers in which the shaking or vibrating motion is produced by electromagnets, employing magnetic force to generate oscillatory movement of the mixing vessel or tool. These devices are used for operations such as dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing substances.
[B01F 31/275] with means for transporting test tubes to and from the stirring device -- B01F 31/275 covers laboratory mixers that employ shaking, oscillating or vibrating actions and incorporate mechanisms for moving test tubes between the stirring unit and other locations, allowing automated loading, unloading or transfer of samples during mixing operations.
[B01F 31/28] the vibrations being caused by piezoelectric elements -- This subclass covers devices that achieve mixing by generating vibrations via piezoelectric actuators, used for dissolving, emulsifying, or dispersing materials. It is located under mixers with shaking, oscillating, or vibrating mechanisms in the separating group of performing operations.
[B01F 31/29] Mixing by periodically deforming flexible tubular members through which the material is flowing -- B01F 31/29 covers mixers that achieve blending by repeatedly flexing or deforming flexible tubular members through which the material flows, using shaking, oscillating, or vibrating actions. The technique is applied to operations such as dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing substances.
[B01F 31/30] comprising a receptacle to only a part of which the shaking, oscillating, or vibrating movement is imparted -- This CPC subclass B01F 31/30 covers mixers in which the shaking, oscillating, or vibrating motion is applied to only a portion of the receptacle that holds the material. The device includes a receptacle where part of it is coupled to the motion source while the rest remains stationary, typical for processes such as dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing.
[B01F 31/31] using receptacles with deformable parts, e.g. membranes, to which a motion is imparted -- B01F 31/31 relates to mixing apparatus that use containers fitted with flexible components such as membranes or deformable parts, where motion-shaking, oscillating or vibrating-is transferred to these elements to achieve dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing of the material.
[B01F 31/311] the motion being a linear movement to one part of the receptacle -- A CPC subclass for mixers whose operation involves a linear motion directed toward a single portion of the vessel, such as shaking or vibrating devices that move material in a straight line to one part of the receptacle.
[B01F 31/312] the motion being a transversal movement to one part of the receptacle, e.g. by moving alternatively up and down the opposite edges of a closing lid to cause a pumping action -- This subclass covers mixers that produce a transverse motion on part of a receptacle, for example by alternately moving up and down opposite edges of a closing lid to create a pumping action that assists mixing, dissolution, emulsifying or dispersing.
[B01F 31/40] with an axially oscillating rotary stirrer -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Mixers having a rotating stirrer whereby simultaneously an axial motion is imparted to the stirrer axis.  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this place:
[B01F 31/401] for material flowing continuously axially therethrough -- Mixing devices that employ shaking, oscillating or vibrating actions while material moves continuously in an axial direction through the apparatus. These continuous-flow mixers are used to dissolve, emulsify or disperse substances in processes such as chemical production, food handling or pharmaceutical formulation.
[B01F 31/42] with pendulum stirrers, i.e. with stirrers suspended so as to oscillate about fixed points or axes -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Mixers having a rotating stirrer whereby simultaneously a pendulum motion is imparted to the stirrer axis.  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this place:
[B01F 31/44] with stirrers performing an oscillatory, vibratory or shaking movement -- B01F 31/44 classifies mixers in which the stirring element moves by oscillation, vibration or shaking, rather than continuous rotation. The entry applies to devices that perform separating operations such as dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing by using these rapid back-and-forth motions. It falls under the broader categories of performing operations, separating, and mixing.
[B01F 31/441] performing a rectilinear reciprocating movement -- The B01F 31/441 subclass covers mixers that generate a straight-line back-and-forth motion of the mixing element, typically by a reciprocating drive. This rectilinear reciprocating movement is employed to dissolve, emulsify or disperse materials where linear motion, rather than rotary or oscillatory action, is required.
[B01F 31/443] performing a superposed additional movement other than oscillation, vibration or shaking -- The CPC subclass B01F 31/443 covers mixers that add a superposed motion to the primary mixing action, such as a secondary translational or rotational movement, that is not simply oscillation, vibration or shaking. This additional movement is intended to improve dispersion, dissolution or emulsification beyond what standard shaking or vibrating mechanisms provide.
[B01F 31/445] performing an oscillatory movement about an axis -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Mixers having a rotating stirrer and at the same time an oscillatory movement is imparted to the stirrer axis about an axis.  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this place:
[B01F 31/449] Stirrers constructions -- B01F 31/449 covers the structural design of stirrers that use shaking, oscillating or vibrating mechanisms to mix materials, especially for processes such as dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing. It falls under the mixing subgroup of separating operations in the CPC.
[B01F 31/46] with an annular vibrating trough -- The B01F 31/46 subgroup covers mixers that employ an annular vibrating trough to aid separation or blending of materials. It falls under mixing operations that use shaking, oscillating or vibrating mechanisms to dissolve, emulsify or disperse substances, typically in processes such as dissolving or emulsifying.
[B01F 31/50] with a receptacle submitted to a combination of movements, i.e. at least one vibratory or oscillatory movement -- B01F 31/50 classifies mixers whose receptacle is subjected to a combination of movements, such as simultaneous vibration or oscillation together with another motion, to achieve mixing, dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing. It applies to equipment that uses shaking, oscillating or vibrating mechanisms to agitate the container during operation.
[B01F 31/55] the materials to be mixed being contained in a flexible bag submitted to periodical deformation -- This subclass covers mixing devices where the material to be processed is placed in a flexible bag that is repeatedly deformed by shaking, oscillating or vibrating actions, causing periodical deformation that promotes dissolution, emulsification or dispersion.
[B01F 31/56] having a vibrating receptacle provided with stirring elements, e.g. independent stirring elements -- B01F 31/56 refers to mixers that employ a vibrating vessel equipped with separate stirring elements, such as independent impellers or paddles, to achieve mixing, dissolution, emulsifying or dispersing actions. The vibration of the receptacle combined with the stirrers enhances fluid motion and particle breakdown, and the class falls under the hierarchy of performing operations, separating, and mixing.
[B01F 31/57] for material continuously moving therethrough  B01F31/29 ,  B01F31/30 ,  B01F31/401 ,  B01F31/46 ,  B01F31/84  take precedence -- B01F 31/57 defines mixing apparatuses that employ shaking, oscillating or vibrating mechanisms to treat material that passes continuously through the machine. The subclass is positioned under mixing operations and yields precedence to related subclasses B01F31/29, B01F31/30, B01F31/401, B01F31/46 and B01F31/84.
[B01F 31/60] with a vibrating receptacle -- Mixers that employ a vibrating receptacle to achieve mixing, separating, dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing of materials. The vibration of the container provides agitation without moving internal impellers, suitable for powders, liquids or pastes. Classified under CPC B01F 31/60 within the mixing sub-group of performing operations.
[B01F 31/65] the materials to be mixed being directly submitted to a pulsating movement, e.g. by means of an oscillating piston or air column -- The code B01F 31/65 covers mixers that directly subject the materials to a pulsating motion, such as that produced by an oscillating piston or an alternating air column, to achieve dissolution, emulsification or dispersion. It falls under the broader categories of performing operations, separating, and mixing, specifically where shaking, oscillating or vibrating actions are used.
[B01F 31/651] Mixing by successively aspirating a part of the mixture in a conduit, e.g. a piston, and reinjecting it through the same conduit into the receptacle -- B01F 31/651 concerns mixing methods that employ successive aspiration of a portion of the mixture in a conduit, such as by a piston, and then reinject it through the same conduit into the receptacle. This technique creates mixing by repeatedly drawing and discharging the material, often used in processes where shaking, oscillating or vibrating mechanisms are involved.
[B01F 31/70] Drives therefor, e.g. crank mechanisms -- This subclass covers mechanisms that provide power to mixers that operate by shaking, oscillating, or vibrating, such as crank drives, cam shafts, eccentric gears, or other drive arrangements that generate the required motion. It is part of the mixing group within separating operations in the CPC.
[B01F 31/80] Mixing by means of high-frequency vibrations above one kHz, e.g. ultrasonic vibrations -- B01F 31/80 covers mixing processes that employ high-frequency vibrations, generally above one kilohertz, such as ultrasonic agitation, to dissolve, emulsify or disperse materials. The technique uses shaking, oscillating or vibrating mechanisms to enhance particle contact without moving large parts.
[B01F 31/81] by vibrations generated inside a mixing device not coming from an external drive, e.g. by the flow of material causing a knife to vibrate or by vibrating nozzles -- This CPC subclass B01F 31/81 covers mixers that create vibrations inside the device, such as those generated by material flow causing a knife to vibrate or by vibrating nozzles, to achieve mixing actions like dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing. It falls under the hierarchy of performing operations, separating, mixing.
[B01F 31/82] the material being forced through a narrow vibrating slit -- B01F 31/82 covers processes in which a material is forced through a narrow vibrating slit as part of mixing operations that use shaking, oscillating or vibrating devices. It is classified under mixers with shaking, oscillating, or vibrating mechanisms in the CPC hierarchy of performing operations, separating, mixing.
[B01F 31/83] comprising a supplementary stirring element -- B01F 31/83 covers mixers that use a shaking, oscillating or vibrating motion and incorporate an additional stirring element, such as a secondary impeller, rod or blade, to enhance dissolution, emulsification or dispersion of materials.
