[B01F 35/2216] Time, i.e. duration, of at least one parameter during the operation -- B01F 35/2216 covers the specification of the time or duration of at least one operating parameter during mixing processes, such as how long a mixer runs, the residence time of a material, or the period a temperature is held. It is a subclass of accessories for mixers within the CPC hierarchy for separating and mixing operations.
[B01F 35/22161] duration of the mixing process or parts of it -- It covers specifications related to how long a mixing-operation is carried out, including timing of individual steps, total mixing time, or dwell periods, used to control product quality in processes such as dissolving, emulsifying, etc.
[B01F 35/22162] Time of feeding of at least one of the components to be mixed -- The code B01F 35/22162 refers to accessories for mixers that control when at least one component is introduced into the mixing operation. It covers devices or systems that schedule or time the feeding of materials, such as sequential or timed feed mechanisms, to influence the mixing process. This falls under the broader categories of performing operations, separating, and mixing (including dissolving, emulsifying, or dispersing).
[B01F 35/2217] Volume of at least one component to be mixed -- The CPC subclass B01F 35/2217 addresses accessories for mixers that specify or control the volume of at least one component to be mixed, enabling precise measurement or adjustment during operations such as dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing. This entry falls under performing operations, separating, and mixing.
[B01F 35/2218] Weight of at least one component to be mixed -- B01F 35/2218 addresses mixer accessories that incorporate a defined weight for at least one material to be mixed, enabling precise dosage or proportioning during mixing, dissolution, emulsifying, or dispersing operations.
[B01F 35/222] of the operation of the driving system, e.g. torque, speed or power of motors -- B01F 35/222 covers features or accessories for mixers that relate to controlling the driving system, including regulation of motor torque, speed or power. It addresses devices that monitor or adjust these parameters to optimise mixing performance.
[B01F 35/2221] the position of baffles used to modify the flow in a conduit or a container -- B01F 35/2221 covers the placement of baffles that alter fluid flow within a conduit or container, primarily used as accessories for mixers. The classification pertains to devices that improve mixing efficiency by directing streams, reducing dead zones, or facilitating dissolution, emulsification or dispersion in processes where separation and mixing operations are performed.
[B01F 35/30] Driving arrangements -- B01F 35/30 covers the drive arrangements for mixer accessories used in separating operations such as mixing, dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing. It includes shafts, gear trains, couplings, belts or other mechanisms that transmit power to the mixing implement. The code is applied to devices that provide the rotational or reciprocating motion required to operate the mixer within the broader class of performing operations.
[B01F 35/31] Couplings -- B01F 35/31 covers couplings that serve as accessories for mixers, linking rotating shafts or mixing elements to transmit torque and accommodate misalignment. The devices are classified under mixing operations within the broader categories of performing operations, separating, and mixing processes.
[B01F 35/32] Driving arrangements -- The B01F 35/32 subclass covers the driving arrangements that transmit power to mixers used for separating, mixing, dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing substances. It includes gears, belts, couplings, drive shafts, motor mounts and other mechanical components that couple a power source to the mixing elements. These accessories enable the proper rotation and operation of the mixer.
[B01F 35/32005] Type of drive -- B01F 35/32005 covers accessories for mixers that specify the type of drive used to power the mixing equipment, including electric, pneumatic, hydraulic or manual drives. It pertains to mixing operations within separating processes, focusing on drive mechanisms that affect the performance of mixers.
[B01F 35/3201] by using acoustic force, e.g. acoustically induced bubbles, acoustic windmill, acoustic scallop -- This subclass covers accessories for mixers that employ acoustic force-such as acoustically induced bubbles, acoustic windmills or acoustic scallops-to achieve mixing, separating, dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing of materials. It falls under the mixing category of the separating operations group.
[B01F 35/32015] Flow driven -- The B01F 35/32015 entry covers accessories for mixers that operate by the flow of the material being processed, using the moving liquid or suspension itself to drive or enhance mixing actions such as dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing.
[B01F 35/3202] Hand driven -- B01F 35/3202 covers hand-driven accessories for mixers used in separating operations such as mixing, dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing. It includes manually powered attachments like hand-cranked blades, paddles or stirrers that are fitted to a mixer to provide mechanical action without electric or pneumatic drive.
[B01F 35/32021] Shaking by hand a portable receptacle or stirrer for mixing -- This CPC subclass covers handheld devices that agitate a portable receptacle or stirrer by shaking to mix, disperse, dissolve or emulsify contents. It is classified as an accessory for mixers within the broader separating and performing operations categories.
[B01F 35/32025] Battery driven -- Battery-driven accessories for mixers are portable, self-contained units that supply power to mixing equipment used in separating, dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing operations. These devices enable mobility and operation where mains electricity is unavailable, expanding the flexibility of mixing processes.
[B01F 35/3203] Gas driven -- B01F 35/3203 refers to accessories for mixers that are powered by gas, such as gas-driven agitators, impellers or mixing heads used to dissolve, emulsify or disperse materials. These components use combustible or compressed gas to create the mixing action rather than electric or mechanical drives.
[B01F 35/32035] Gravity driven, e.g. by means of weights out of balance or plunger-weights moving in a cylinder -- The class covers mixer accessories that are operated by gravity, for example using an unbalanced weight or a plunger that moves within a cylinder to generate the mixing action. Such devices provide stirring, dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing without external power sources.
[B01F 35/3204] Motor driven, i.e. by means of an electric or IC motor -- The B01F 35/3204 subclass covers accessories for mixers that are powered by an electric or integrated-circuit motor. It applies to devices used in mixing, dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing operations where the motor provides the driving force for the mixing action.
[B01F 35/32045] Hydraulically driven -- Hydraulically driven accessories for mixers are components powered by hydraulic fluid to rotate or agitate materials during mixing, separating, or dispersing processes. They are used to enhance performance in operations such as dissolving, emulsifying, or dispersing mixtures in industrial settings.
[B01F 35/3205] by using optical pressure force, e.g. produced by a laser beam -- This subclass covers accessories for mixers that utilise optical pressure, such as a laser beam, to generate forces that aid or control mixing actions like dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing. It includes devices that apply light-induced pressure to move or stir materials within the mixing vessel.
[B01F 35/32055] by using solar energy -- B01F 35/32055 covers accessories for mixers that are powered or assisted by solar energy, such as solar-driven agitators, heating elements or illumination devices used to enhance dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing processes. The classification groups these solar-based components with other mixer accessories that facilitate separating operations through mixing. This entry falls under the broader categories of performing operations, separating and mixing.
[B01F 35/3206] by the rotation of the wheels during movement -- B01F 35/3206 relates to accessories for mixers that are powered by the rotation of wheels during the device's movement, enabling mixing, dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing actions while the equipment is in motion.
[B01F 35/32065] Wind driven -- This subclass relates to accessories for mixers that are powered by wind and used to perform mixing actions such as dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing in separation processes. It is classified under B01F 35/32065 within the Performing Operations - Separating - Mixing hierarchy of the CPC.
[B01F 35/321] Disposition of the drive -- The entry covers the arrangement, mounting and positioning of the drive unit in mixers, including its orientation, support structures and coupling to the mixing shaft. It pertains to accessories that determine how the drive is disposed relative to the mixing vessel and other components.
[B01F 35/3211] independent from the receptacle -- This CPC subclass covers auxiliary components used with mixers that are not integral to the mixing vessel itself. It includes detachable agitators, shafts or support structures that can be installed or removed independently of the receptacle, facilitating mixing operations such as dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing.
[B01F 35/3212] mounted on the receptacle -- The B01F 35/3212 entry covers accessories that are fixed to or integrated with the mixing receptacle itself, such as agitators, impellers, or dispersion elements that are mounted directly on the vessel wall or lid. These components facilitate dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing operations while remaining part of the container.
[B01F 35/3213] at the lower side of the axis, e.g. driving the stirrer from the bottom of a receptacle -- B01F 35/3213 covers accessories for mixers that drive the stirrer from the bottom of a receptacle, positioning the drive mechanism on the lower side of the axis. It is classified under Performing Operations → Separating → Mixing, e.g. dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing.
[B01F 35/3214] at the upper side of the axis, e.g. driving the stirrer from the top of a receptacle -- B01F 35/3214 pertains to accessories for mixers that are positioned at the upper side of the axis, such as mechanisms designed for driving the stirrer from the top of a container or vessel.
[B01F 35/3215] the driving system comprising more than one motor, e.g. having an auxiliary motor or comprising independently driven elements -- This subclass covers mixers whose propulsion includes multiple motors, such as an auxiliary motor or independently driven elements, enabling combined or separate drive of the stirring components. It is part of the mixing and separating operations hierarchy and relates to accessories for mixers.
[B01F 35/32151] Driving the stirrer axis from both ends of the axis, i.e. using at least two motors per shaft -- The entry B01F 35/32151 covers accessories for mixers in which the stirrer shaft is powered from both ends, typically by employing at least two motors attached to the same shaft to provide bidirectional drive. This arrangement is classified under mixing operations, specifically separating and mixing processes such as dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing.
[B01F 35/322] Construction of driving shafts -- B01F 35/322 covers the design and construction of driving shafts that transmit power to mixing elements in equipment used for separating, mixing, dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing. The classification applies to accessories for mixers, detailing shaft geometry, material selection, coupling methods and mounting features.
[B01F 35/323] for vertical stirrer shafts  B01F35/324  takes precedence -- B01F 35/323 covers accessories for mixers that are designed specifically for vertical stirrer shafts, such as couplings, shafts, or protective sleeves. This subclass is subordinate to B01F 35/324, which provides a more detailed definition of the same type of equipment.
[B01F 35/3231] Driving several stirrer shafts, e.g. about the same axis -- The entry B01F 35/3231 in the Cooperative Patent Classification describes accessories for mixers that drive several stirrer shafts, typically aligned about a same-axis, allowing simultaneous agitation in operations such as dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing.
[B01F 35/324] Driving independent stirrer shafts, i.e. not fitted on the container -- This subclass concerns accessories that power independent stirrer-shafts not attached to the container, typically driven by external motors or mechanisms to mix, dissolve, emulsify or disperse substances during processing.
[B01F 35/325] Driving reciprocating or oscillating stirrers -- The B01F 35/325 classification covers mechanisms and accessories that power reciprocating or oscillating stirrers used in mixing operations such as dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing. It includes drive systems that convert rotational motion into linear or back-and-forth movement to agitate materials in mixers.
[B01F 35/33] Transmissions -- B01F 35/33 covers transmission components that serve as accessories for mixers used in processes such as dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing. These transmissions convey power from the drive source to the mixing implement, enabling efficient mixing operations.
[B01F 35/331] alternately changing the speed of rotation -- B01F 35/331 concerns accessories for mixers that provide an alternating change in rotation speed during operation. Such devices are used to improve mixing, dissolution, emulsifying or dispersing by periodically varying the agitator speed.
[B01F 35/332] alternately changing the direction of rotation -- B01F 35/332 refers to accessories for mixers that periodically reverse the rotational direction of the mixing shaft. Such devices are used in operations where alternating rotation improves dissolution, emulsification or dispersion, helping to prevent buildup and enhance uniformity.
[B01F 35/333] the rotation sense being changeable, e.g. to mix or aerate, to move a fluid forward or backward or to suck or blow -- B01F 35/333 concerns mixer accessories that permit the direction of rotation to be altered so the device can both mix or aerate and also convey a fluid forward or backward, enabling suction or blowing actions. It falls under the mixing category of the CPC, within separating operations.
[B01F 35/34] Brake mechanisms -- B01F 35/34 relates to brake mechanisms that serve as accessories for mixers, providing controlled deceleration or stopping of the mixing shaft during operations such as dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing, thereby improving safety and process control.
[B01F 35/40] Mounting or supporting mixing devices or receptacles -- B01F 35/40 covers accessories for mixers that provide mounting or supporting structures for the mixing device or its receptacle, including brackets, frames, stands and other hardware used to secure mixers employed in operations such as dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing.
[B01F 35/41] Mounting or supporting stirrer shafts or stirrer units on receptacles -- This subclass covers devices that attach or support the rotating shaft or stirrer assembly of a mixer to the vessel or container in which mixing occurs. It includes brackets, holders, couplings and other fittings that secure the agitator to the receptacle. These accessories are used in mixing operations such as dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing.
[B01F 35/411] by supporting only one extremity of the shaft -- The code B01F 35/411 covers mixer accessories that secure the rotating shaft by anchoring it at a single end only, leaving the opposite extremity unsupported. Such devices typically provide a bearing, sleeve or other support at one extremity to maintain alignment while allowing free movement at the other. This arrangement is used in mixing, separating or dissolving equipment where partial shaft support is sufficient.
[B01F 35/4111] at the top of the receptacle -- This subclass covers accessories that are mounted on or placed at the upper opening of a mixing vessel, such as lids, covers, feed tubes or dispensing heads, which aid in mixing, dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing operations while preventing loss of material.
[B01F 35/4112] at the bottom of the receptacle, e.g. by studs -- The subclass B01F 35/4112 concerns accessories for mixers that are mounted at the bottom of the receptacle, for example by means of studs or similar bottom-mounted features that secure or position the accessory within the vessel.
[B01F 35/4113] at a side wall of the receptacle -- B01F 35/4113 covers accessories for mixers that are mounted on the side wall of the mixing vessel, used in operations such as separating, dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing. The element is positioned on the receptacle's lateral surface to introduce, remove or treat material during the mixing process. It is a specific sub-category of mixing equipment within the broader performing-operations hierarchy.
[B01F 35/412] by supporting both extremities of the shaft -- B01F 35/412 covers accessories for mixers that support a mixing shaft at both its ends, providing axial and radial stability during operation. This dual-end support helps reduce shaft deflection, vibration and wear, improving mixing efficiency in processes such as dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing. It is classified under performing operations, separating and mixing.
[B01F 35/4121] at the top and at the bottom of the receptacle, e.g. for performing a conical orbital movement about a vertical axis -- The classification covers accessories mounted at both the top and the bottom of a mixing receptacle that generate a conical orbital motion of the contents about a vertical axis. Such devices are used to improve mixing efficiency by creating a three-dimensional flow pattern within the vessel.
[B01F 35/4122] at the side walls of the receptacle -- This subclass covers accessories for mixers that are positioned on the side walls of a receptacle, such as stationary blades, baffles or mounts that assist in mixing, dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing. It falls within CPC B01F 35/4122 under Performing operations → Separating → Mixing → Accessories for mixers.
[B01F 35/413] by means of clamps or clamping arrangements for fixing attached stirrers or independent stirrer units -- The group covers accessories that employ clamps or clamping arrangements to secure stirrers, whether they are attached to the mixing container or mounted as independent units, ensuring the stirrers stay fixed during operation. These devices are used in mixing processes such as dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing.
[B01F 35/414] using inflatable arrangements for supporting a stirring element -- B01F 35/414 covers accessories for mixers in which an inflatable arrangement is employed to support or position a stirring element during mixing, separating, dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing processes.
[B01F 35/42] Clamping or holding arrangements for mounting receptacles on mixing devices -- B01F 35/42 covers clamping or holding mechanisms that secure receptacles such as jars or containers to mixing equipment. The class pertains to accessories that attach these receptacles to mixers, enabling stable operation during processes like dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing. It falls under the broader categories of performing operations, separating and mixing.
[B01F 35/421] having a cup-shaped or cage-type form -- This CPC subclass B01F 35/421 covers accessories for mixers that are shaped like a cup or a cage, intended for use in separating and mixing processes such as dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing. The devices are designed to hold or contain material during the operation, providing a defined volume or enclosure for efficient mixing.
[B01F 35/422] having a jaw-type or finger-type shape -- The CPC subclass B01F 35/422 covers accessories for mixers that incorporate a jaw-type or finger-type shape, used to enhance separation, dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing of materials during mixing operations. It falls under Performing Operations > Separating > Mixing (e.g. dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing) > Accessories for mixers.
[B01F 35/423] of the vertically movable, two-plates type -- The CPC subclass B01F 35/423 covers accessories for mixers that employ a vertically movable, two-plate arrangement to enhance separating, mixing, dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing operations. It refers to devices in which two plates move relative to each other along a vertical axis to create shear forces that improve material handling and processing.
[B01F 35/424] by means of an air cushion used for supporting the mixing receptacle -- Accessories for mixers that employ an air cushion to support the mixing vessel, allowing the receptacle to float or be levitated during operation, thereby reducing friction and improving mixing efficiency. This feature falls under mixing processes such as dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing within the broader category of separating operations.
[B01F 35/425] Holding arrangements for retaining loose elements of the mixing receptacle, e.g. for holding the handle of a can, while it is being shaken -- This CPC subclass covers devices that secure detachable parts of a mixing container, such as a can handle, during shaking. It includes clamps, brackets or other holding mechanisms attached to the mixer or container to prevent loss of components while operating.
[B01F 35/43] Supporting receptacles on frames or stands -- The CPC subclass B01F 35/43 covers supporting receptacles mounted on frames or stands that serve as accessories for mixers used in separating, mixing, dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing operations. It relates to devices that hold vessels or containers in mixing equipment to facilitate these processes.
[B01F 35/45] Closures or doors specially adapted for mixing receptacles -- Closures or doors specially adapted for mixing receptacles are devices such as lids, hatches or sealed doors that are designed to fit mixers' containers while allowing the mixing operation to proceed safely and efficiently. They provide secure sealing, venting or access features that prevent spillage, contamination or loss of material during processes like dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing.
[B01F 35/451] by rotating them about an axis parallel to the plane of the opening -- The subclass B01F 35/451 covers accessories for mixers that are rotated about an axis parallel to the plane of the opening, such as paddles, impellers or stirrers that operate within a vessel. These devices are used to perform mixing actions including dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing by rotating in a direction aligned with the opening's plane.
[B01F 35/452] by moving them in the plane of the opening  B01F35/454  takes precedence -- B01F 35/452 covers accessories for mixers that move the material in the plane of the opening, such as devices that convey, spread or stir the product along the opening surface. It falls under mixing operations within separating and performing operations. The more specific subclass B01F 35/454 supersedes it for particular implementations.
[B01F 35/4521] by rotating them about an axis perpendicular to the plane of the opening -- B01F 35/4521 covers accessories for mixers in which the mixing elements are rotated about an axis that is perpendicular to the plane of the opening, such as rotating blades, paddles or disks mounted on a shaft that turns vertically through the vessel's lid. This sub-class falls under performing operations → separating → mixing (e.g., dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing). It is used to classify devices that achieve mixing by vertical rotational motion.
[B01F 35/453] by moving them perpendicular to the plane of the opening -- An accessory for a mixer that introduces material into the mixing zone by moving it perpendicular to the plane of the opening, such as a chute or feed device that drops or pushes substances straight down into the agitator.
[B01F 35/4531] and moving them afterwards in another direction -- B01F 35/4531 pertains to accessories for mixers that involve changing the direction of movement after the mixing process, typically seen in mechanisms designed to enhance mixing efficiency or manage material flow.
[B01F 35/454] Moving covers on a cylindrical drum in a circular path along the drum -- This CPC subclass covers mechanisms that move protective covers along the outer surface of a rotating cylindrical drum in a circular path, serving as accessories to mixers used in separating and mixing operations such as dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing. It is classified under B01F 35/454 within the hierarchy of performing operations, separating, mixing and accessories for mixers.
[B01F 35/50] Mixing receptacles -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Receptacles specially adapted to mixing.
[B01F 35/51] characterised by their material -- B01F 35/51 pertains to accessories for mixers that are distinguished by the material of construction, such as metal, polymer, ceramic or composite components, used in mixing, dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing operations.
[B01F 35/511] provided with liners, e.g. wear resistant or flexible liners -- B01F 35/511 covers accessories for mixers that incorporate liners, such as wear-resistant or flexible liners, to protect the mixing vessel or improve the process. These liners may be made of metal, polymer, ceramic or other materials and are used in operations like dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing. The subclass falls under mixing operations within the broader category of separating and performing operations.
[B01F 35/512] characterised by surface properties, e.g. coated or rough -- B01F 35/512 covers accessories for mixers that are defined by their surface properties, such as coatings, roughness or textured finishes, which modify the interaction between the material being mixed and the equipment to improve mixing efficiency, prevent sticking or aid dispersion.
[B01F 35/513] Flexible receptacles, e.g. bags supported by rigid containers -- The CPC subclass B01F 35/513 covers flexible receptacles such as bags that are used with mixing equipment and are supported inside rigid containers. It includes designs where the pliable container holds material to be mixed, dispersed, dissolved or emulsified while being held in place by a hard shell, enabling efficient processing and handling.
[B01F 35/514] the mixing receptacle or conduit being transparent or comprising transparent parts -- B01F 35/514 covers accessories for mixers in which the mixing receptacle or conduit is made of transparent material or incorporates transparent parts, enabling the operator to visually monitor the mixing, dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing process. It falls under the mixing sub-category of separating operations within performing-operations classifications.
[B01F 35/52] Receptacles with two or more compartments -- B01F 35/52 covers receptacles that have two or more separate compartments and are intended as accessories for mixers. These containers allow ingredients to be stored or pre-measured in distinct chambers before being combined in mixing, dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing operations.
[B01F 35/522] comprising compartments keeping the materials to be mixed separated until the mixing is initiated  containers or packages for packaging two or more different materials which must be maintained separate prior to use in admixture  B65D81/32 -- The code B01F 35/522 covers accessory devices for mixers that incorporate separate compartments or containers designed to keep two or more distinct materials isolated until the mixing operation begins. It includes packaging solutions that maintain the ingredients apart prior to admixture, such as dual-chamber containers, and is cross-referenced to B65D81/32 for similar multi-compartment packaging.
[B01F 35/53] characterised by the configuration of the interior, e.g. baffles for facilitating the mixing of components -- B01F 35/53 covers accessories for mixers that are defined by the interior configuration of the mixing vessel, such as baffles, ribs or plates designed to improve the flow and promote the dissolution, emulsification or dispersion of components during operation. These features aid the mixing process by directing movement of the material and preventing dead zones.
[B01F 35/531] with baffles, plates or bars on the wall or the bottom -- The CPC code B01F 35/531 pertains to accessories for mixers that feature baffles, plates or bars mounted on the vessel wall or bottom, designed to generate turbulence, eliminate dead zones and improve the dispersion of solids or liquids during mixing operations.
[B01F 35/5311] with horizontal baffles mounted on the walls -- The CPC subclass B01F 35/5311 covers accessories for mixers that feature horizontal baffles mounted on the vessel walls, used to enhance mixing efficiency by disrupting flow and improving separation of components during operations such as dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing.
[B01F 35/5312] with vertical baffles mounted on the walls -- B01F 35/5312 covers accessories for mixers that incorporate vertical baffles fixed to the walls of the mixing vessel. The baffles create turbulence and prevent vortex formation, enhancing the efficiency of separating, dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing operations in batch or continuous mixers.
[B01F 35/532] with guide tubes on the wall or the bottom -- B01F 35/532 covers accessories for mixers that incorporate guide tubes mounted on the vessel wall or bottom to direct the flow of material during mixing. These tubes help control the circulation of liquids or suspensions, improving dispersion, dissolution or emulsification efficiency in processes such as separating or blending.
[B01F 35/54] Closely surrounding the rotating element -- B01F 35/54 covers accessories that are placed directly around the rotating element of a mixer, such as shields, jackets or housings that enclose the agitator. These components help contain the material, protect the shaft and improve mixing efficiency.
[B01F 35/55] Baffles; Flow breakers  B01F35/531  takes precedence -- B01F 35/55 covers baffles and flow-breaking devices used as accessories for mixers in processes such as dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing. These components are installed inside mixing vessels to direct fluid movement, improve turbulence and enhance separation efficiency. The more specific subgroup B01F 35/531 takes precedence for particular types of baffles.
[B01F 35/56] General build-up of the mixers -- The entry B01F 35/56 covers the overall construction of mixers used in separating and mixing processes such as dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing, focusing on the structural components, drive mechanisms, shafts, blades and other accessories that form the mixer assembly. It addresses how these parts are combined to provide a functional mixing device.
[B01F 35/561] the mixer being built-up from a plurality of modules or stacked plates comprising complete or partial elements of the mixer -- This subclass covers mixers constructed from multiple interchangeable modules or stacked plates that each contain whole or partial mixer components, enabling the device to be assembled, reconfigured, or expanded by adding or removing sections. It falls under accessories for mixers within mixing operations.
[B01F 35/562] the mixer or mixing elements being collapsible, i.e. when discharging the products -- The subclass covers mixers or mixing elements designed to collapse or fold when the product is discharged, facilitating removal or cleaning. It applies to mixing equipment where the agitator or vessel can be reduced in size or retracted after use.
[B01F 35/5621] the complete mixer being collapsible, i.e. the housing can be collapsed -- The CPC subclass B01F 35/5621 defines complete mixers whose entire housing is collapsible, allowing the unit to be folded or reduced in size for easy storage or transport. It falls under the hierarchy of performing operations, separating, mixing (including dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing), and specifically accessories for mixers.
[B01F 35/60] Safety arrangements -- Safety arrangements for mixing equipment include devices that protect operators, the apparatus and the product during operation, such as interlocks, guards, pressure-relief systems, emergency-stop mechanisms and other protective features that prevent hazards arising from over-speed, over-pressure, spillage or accidental contact. These measures are designed specifically for accessories used with mixers in processes like dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing.
[B01F 35/602] with a safety or relief valve -- B01F 35/602 covers accessories for mixers that incorporate a safety or relief valve, allowing over-pressure to be vented during operations such as dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing. The valve protects equipment and operators by releasing excess pressure, improving reliability and safety of the mixing process.
[B01F 35/605] Safety devices concerning the operation of the mixer -- The CPC subclass B01F 35/605 covers safety devices that are integrated with mixers used in separating and mixing operations such as dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing. It includes components that protect operators or prevent accidents during mixer use, for example interlocks, overload protectors, emergency stop mechanisms and shielding accessories specifically designed for mixers.
[B01F 35/6052] with locking, blocking or interlocking mechanisms for preventing operation of the actuation mechanism of the mixing device -- B01F 35/6052 covers accessories for mixers that incorporate locking, blocking or interlocking devices designed to stop the actuation mechanism from moving. These mechanisms are intended to enhance safety by preventing unintended operation of the mixing device.
[B01F 35/71] Feed mechanisms -- B01F 35/71 covers feed mechanisms, the devices that introduce raw or intermediate material into a mixer during operations such as dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing. These mechanisms include hoppers, conveyors, pumps, screw feeders and other equipment that control the rate, direction and uniformity of material entry to ensure effective mixing.
[B01F 35/711] for feeding a mixture of components, i.e. solids in liquid, solids in a gas stream -- B01F 35/711 covers accessories for mixers that feed a mixture of components, such as solids suspended in a liquid or carried by a gas stream, into the mixing chamber. It falls under the CPC hierarchy Performing Operations - Separating - Mixing, e.g. dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing.
[B01F 35/712] for feeding fluids -- This subclass covers accessories for mixers that introduce or supply fluids to the mixing zone, such as feed conduits, pour-in tubes, pumps or other devices for delivering liquids, gases or suspensions during mixing, emulsifying, dissolving or dispersing operations.
[B01F 35/713] comprising breaking packages or parts thereof, e.g. piercing or opening sealing elements between compartments or cartridges  containers or packages  per se   B65D -- B01F 35/713 pertains to accessories for mixers that involve the breaking of packages or their components, such as piercing or opening sealing elements in multi-compartment containers. This classification is specific to the mechanisms designed to integrate contents from separate compartments during mixing processes.
[B01F 35/7131] Breaking or perforating packages, containers or vials -- The subclass B01F 35/7131 covers accessories for mixers that break or perforate packages, containers or vials so the contents can be released for mixing, dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing. It falls under the hierarchy performing operations - separating - mixing.
[B01F 35/7132] the package containing one of the components dissolves when in contact with the other component of the mixture  disintegrable, dissolvable or edible packaging materials  B65D65/46 -- The entry defines mixer accessories in which the packaging of one component is engineered to dissolve, disintegrate or be edible when it contacts the other component, allowing the two substances to mix directly within the package. It is classified under CPC B01F 35/7132 for mixing accessories and linked to dissolvable or edible packaging materials in B65D65/46.
[B01F 35/7133] Opening clips which seal openings between the compartments -- Opening clips designed to close and seal openings between compartments in mixers, serving as accessories that secure internal passageways while allowing access for loading, unloading or maintenance. These components prevent leakage of materials such as liquids, powders or emulsions during operation, and are classified under the mixing and separating domain of the Cooperative Patent Classification.
[B01F 35/7134] Dissolving the seal when in contact with one of the products to be mixed, thereby bringing the compartments in communication -- The code B01F 35/7134 covers mixer accessories that use a seal which dissolves upon contact with a product being mixed, allowing separate compartments to open and communicate for the mixing process. This technique enables controlled release of ingredients, such as liquids or powders, directly within the mixer.
[B01F 35/7135] Opening the seal between the compartments by application of heat -- The CPC entry B01F 35/7135 covers devices or methods that open a seal between compartments of a mixer by applying heat, enabling transfer of material while maintaining separation during operation. It relates to accessories for mixers used in separating and mixing processes such as dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing, where a heat-activated seal is broken to join previously isolated zones.
[B01F 35/7136] Opening hooks which lock or close-off openings between compartments -- The CPC subclass B01F 35/7136 pertains to accessories for mixers that comprise opening hooks designed to lock or close off openings between compartments, enabling control of material flow during separating, mixing, dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing operations. These hooks ensure compartmental isolation while the mixer is in use.
[B01F 35/7137] Piercing, perforating or melting membranes or closures which seal the compartments -- This CPC subgroup covers devices or methods for opening sealed compartments in mixing apparatuses by piercing, perforating or locally melting the membrane or closure that separates the compartments. It includes accessories that enable a controlled breach of barriers to allow ingredient transfer or gas release during mixing operations.
[B01F 35/7138] Opening valves which close-off openings between compartments -- The CPC subclass B01F 35/7138 covers accessories for mixers that provide opening valves used to close off passages between separate compartments. It pertains to devices that control the flow of materials during mixing, separating, or dissolving operations by selectively opening or sealing internal channels. This subclass falls under the broader categories of performing operations, separating, and mixing.
[B01F 35/7139] Removing separation walls, plugs which close off the different compartments, e.g. by rotation or axially sliding -- B01F 35/7139 pertains to accessories for mixers that facilitate the removal of separation walls or plugs closing off different compartments, typically through mechanisms such as rotation or axial sliding. This classification is part of a detailed system used by the European Patent Office and United States Patent and Trademark Office to categorize inventions related to performing operations, specifically separating and mixing processes.
[B01F 35/714] for feeding predetermined amounts  B01F35/88  takes precedence -- The B01F 35/714 subclass covers accessories for mixers that automatically feed predetermined quantities of material into the mixing zone, enabling accurate dosing for operations such as dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing. These devices are tailored to provide precise metered feed rates and are treated as having priority over the more general feeding devices defined in B01F 35/88.
[B01F 35/7141] using measuring chambers moving between a loading and unloading position, e.g. reciprocating feed frames -- A mixer accessory comprising one or more measuring chambers that move back and forth between loading and unloading positions, such as reciprocating feed frames, to deliver a precisely measured amount of material into the mixing zone. This device enables controlled dosing while the mixer operates.
[B01F 35/71411] rotating or oscillating about an axis -- This subclass includes mixer accessories that create motion by rotating or oscillating about an axis, such as rotary impellers, oscillating paddles or similar devices used to improve dissolution, emulsification or dispersion during mixing operations.
[B01F 35/714111] the measuring chambers being pockets on the circumference of a drum rotating about a horizontal axis with discharging by gravity -- A measuring chamber design consisting of pockets arranged around the outer surface of a drum that rotates on a horizontal axis, allowing material to be collected in each pocket and released by gravity as the drum turns. This accessory is intended for mixers to enable controlled dosing or sampling during operation.
[B01F 35/714112] the measuring chambers being channels extending between both front faces of a rotating cylinder or disc -- This subclass covers mixers that incorporate measuring chambers formed by channels extending between the two front faces of a rotating cylinder or disc, allowing fluid to be metered or monitored as the rotor turns. It is an accessory feature for mixing equipment within the separating and mixing group.
[B01F 35/715] Feeding the components in several steps, e.g. successive steps -- This subclass of the CPC deals with devices that introduce material into a mixer in multiple, sequential batches rather than all at once. It covers accessories that control the timing or order of feed, such as multi-port inlets, controlled gates, or staged dosing mechanisms, enabling processes like stepwise dissolution, emulsification or dispersion.
[B01F 35/716] characterised by the relative arrangement of the containers for feeding or mixing the components -- A subclass of mixer accessories that is defined by the spatial relationship of the containers used to feed or combine ingredients, specifying how their arrangement relative to each other and to the mixing device influences the feeding or mixing process.
[B01F 35/7161] the containers being connected coaxially before contacting the contents -- B01F 35/7161 pertains to accessories for mixers where the containers are connected in a coaxial arrangement prior to the mixing process, ensuring that their contents come into contact. This setup is designed to facilitate efficient mixing or blending of substances within the containers.
[B01F 35/7162] A container being placed inside the other before contacting the contents -- The CPC subclass B01F 35/7162 covers containers designed to be placed inside another container before the contents are contacted, typically used as accessories for mixers in processes such as dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing. It relates to secondary vessels that hold material while the primary container provides mixing action, allowing staged addition or separation of ingredients.
[B01F 35/7163] the containers being connected in a mouth-to-mouth, end-to-end disposition, i.e. the openings are juxtaposed before contacting the contents -- A mixer accessory where two containers are joined mouth-to-mouth in an end-to-end arrangement, aligning their openings so the contents can be transferred directly between them during dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing operations. This configuration facilitates seamless mixing without intermediate handling.
[B01F 35/7164] the containers being placed in parallel before contacting the contents -- B01F 35/7164 refers to mixer accessories in which multiple containers are positioned side-by-side before their contents are brought into contact, allowing parallel loading or simultaneous processing of materials during mixing, separating or dissolving operations.
[B01F 35/717] characterised by the means for feeding the components to the mixer -- The CPC subclass B01F 35/717 covers accessories for mixers that are distinguished by the way they introduce raw materials into the mixing chamber. It includes devices such as feed hoppers, conveyors, or dosing systems designed to supply components continuously or intermittently during mixing operations. This subclass falls under the broader categories of performing operations, separating, and mixing processes.
[B01F 35/71705] using belts -- B01F 35/71705 refers to mixer accessories that employ belt mechanisms to transmit motion or torque, such as belt-driven agitators, paddles or grinding elements. It covers devices where a belt is used to couple the drive source to the mixing element, enabling flexible placement or speed control within the mixing operation. This subclass falls under mixing processes involving separating and dispersion actions.
[B01F 35/7171] using boxes, closable containers, sacks or carts -- A sub-class for mixer accessories that employ boxes, closable containers, sacks or carts to handle materials during mixing operations such as dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing, facilitating transport, storage or feeding of substances within the mixing process.
[B01F 35/71715] using buckets, cups or open containers -- The code B01F 35/71715 classifies accessories for mixers that employ buckets, cups or open containers to hold the material during mixing, dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing operations, covering detachable or interchangeable vessels used as part of the mixing apparatus.
[B01F 35/7172] using capillary forces -- The CPC entry B01F 35/7172 covers accessories for mixers that employ capillary forces to move, spread or retain liquids during mixing operations, such as devices that draw fluids into fine channels to aid dissolution, emulsification or dispersion. It applies to equipment where capillary action is used to enhance the efficiency of the mixing process.
[B01F 35/71725] using centrifugal forces -- This subclass covers accessories for mixers that employ centrifugal force to improve mixing, such as centrifugally driven agitators or devices that create radial motion to aid dissolution, emulsification or dispersion. It falls within the broader categories of performing operations, separating, mixing, and specifically addresses the use of centrifugal forces.
[B01F 35/7173] using gravity, e.g. from a hopper -- The subclass B01F 35/7173 covers accessories for mixers that introduce material by gravity, such as a hopper feeding the mixing zone. These devices are used in mixing operations like dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing, allowing material to flow into the agitator without mechanical conveyance. It falls under the broader categories of performing operations, separating and mixing.
[B01F 35/71731] using a hopper -- The code B01F 35/71731 refers to the use of a hopper as an accessory for mixers in processes that involve separating and mixing operations such as dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing. It covers the provision of a feed container that supplies material to the mixing unit, improving material handling and feed control.
[B01F 35/71735] using grippers -- The code B01F 35/71735 covers accessories for mixers that employ grippers to handle materials during mixing operations such as dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing. Grippers are used to grasp, hold or release solid or semi-solid portions, enabling precise feeding, positioning or removal within the mixing chamber. This facilitates more efficient separation and processing of components while the mixer is running.
[B01F 35/7174] using pistons, plungers or syringes -- B01F 35/7174 covers accessories for mixers that employ pistons, plungers or syringes to introduce, discharge or circulate material during mixing, separating or dissolving operations. These components enable controlled dosing, injection or removal of liquids or slurries within the mixing vessel.
[B01F 35/71745] using pneumatic pressure, overpressure, gas or air pressure in a closed receptacle or circuit system -- This subclass covers accessories for mixers that employ pneumatic or gas pressure, including over-pressure, in a sealed vessel or closed-circuit system to assist mixing, dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing materials. It is a specialized method of applying air or gas energy within a confined receptacle to enhance the performance of mixing operations.
[B01F 35/7175] using propellers -- The CPC class B01F 35/7175 covers accessories for mixers that employ propeller-type agitators to achieve separating, mixing, dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing of materials. It relates to devices where rotating propellers are used to impart shear and flow within a vessel.
[B01F 35/71755] using means for feeding components in a pulsating or intermittent manner -- This subclass relates to accessories for mixers that introduce components into the mixing zone in a pulsating or intermittent way, providing controlled dosing to enhance processes such as dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing. It falls under performing operations, separating, and mixing categories.
[B01F 35/717551] using electrical pulses -- B01F 35/717551 covers accessories for mixers that employ electrical pulses to improve mixing, dissolution, emulsification or dispersion of materials. The entry resides in the CPC hierarchy PERFORMING OPERATIONS - SEPARATING - MIXING, e.g. dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing - Accessories for mixers.
[B01F 35/7176] using pumps -- The B01F 35/7176 subclass pertains to accessories for mixers used in separating and mixing processes, specifically those that employ pumps to supply, circulate, or recirculate fluids during operations such as dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing. It covers pump-based designs that enhance the efficiency or control of the mixing action.
[B01F 35/71761] Membrane pumps -- B01F 35/71761 pertains to membrane pumps employed as accessories for mixing equipment. These pumps use a flexible or porous membrane to move liquids, facilitating operations such as dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing within a mixer. They are classified under the broader category of separating and mixing processes.
[B01F 35/717611] Peristaltic pumps -- Peristaltic pumps are positive-displacement devices that transport liquids by sequentially compressing a flexible tube, creating a continuous flow without the fluid contacting pump parts. In mixing equipment they serve as accessories that supply, recirculate or dose fluids such as solvents, reagents or emulsions, enabling precise control of feed rates during dissolution, emulsification or dispersion processes.
[B01F 35/717612] Piezoelectric pumps -- The CPC entry B01F 35/717612 covers piezoelectric pumps that are used as accessories for mixers, providing precise fluid delivery or circulation during mixing operations such as dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing. These pumps employ piezoelectric actuation to generate rapid, low-volume flow, enhancing control of ingredient addition and improving homogeneity in batch or continuous mixing processes.
[B01F 35/717613] Piston pumps -- B01F 35/717613 pertains to piston pumps used as accessories for mixers, focusing on components that facilitate the mixing process through precise fluid displacement and pressure control.
[B01F 35/717614] Venturi pumps -- Venturi pumps are fluid-moving devices that create a pressure drop by passing a liquid through a narrowed section, generating suction that can draw in or blend additional streams. In the CPC they are listed under accessories for mixers within the mixing and separating operations hierarchy, code B01F 35/717614.
[B01F 35/71765] using rakes or plain plates with raking movement -- This subclass pertains to mixer accessories that employ rakes or flat plates moving in a raking motion to agitate, blend, dissolve, emulsify or disperse materials. It covers devices that generate a sweeping action within the mixing vessel to improve uniformity of the product.
[B01F 35/7177] using rollers -- B01F 35/7177 covers the use of rollers as accessories in mixers that perform separating operations such as dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing. Rollers are employed to improve material handling, shear or contact during the mixing process.
[B01F 35/71775] using helical screws -- The entry covers accessories for mixers that employ helical screws to convey and blend materials, facilitating dissolution, emulsification or dispersion during separating operations.
[B01F 35/7178] using shovels or scoops -- B01F 35/7178 relates to accessories for mixers, specifically shovels or scoops employed to introduce, remove or handle material during mixing, separating, dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing processes. The code falls under the broader categories of performing operations, separating and mixing.
[B01F 35/71785] using slides or vibrating tables -- B01F 35/71785 pertains to accessories for mixers that employ slides or vibrating tables to aid mixing operations such as separating, dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing materials. These devices provide controlled movement or vibration to enhance contact between components during the process.
[B01F 35/7179] using sprayers, nozzles or jets -- B01F 35/7179 covers accessories for mixers that employ sprayers, nozzles or jets to introduce liquids into the material being processed. These devices are used in mixing operations such as dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing to improve distribution of reagents. The code falls under the CPC hierarchy of performing operations - separating - mixing.
[B01F 35/71791] using ink jet heads or cartridges, e.g. of the thermal bubble jet or piezoelectric type -- This entry relates to mixer accessories that employ ink-jet heads or cartridges, such as thermal-bubble or piezoelectric types, to introduce liquids or powders for purposes like dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing. It falls under the mixing subgroup of the CPC.
[B01F 35/71795] Squeezing a flexible container -- This code covers accessories that enable a mixer to squeeze a flexible container, forcing material out of the bag and into the mixing zone to aid dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing. Such devices are used to improve handling of viscous or particulate feeds and to enhance mixing efficiency.
[B01F 35/718] using vacuum, under pressure in a closed receptacle or circuit system -- B01F 35/718 covers accessories for mixers that employ vacuum or pressurised conditions within a sealed vessel or closed-circuit system to aid mixing, dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing processes. These devices use reduced or increased pressure to enhance material handling, improve dispersion and control reaction environments during mixing operations.
[B01F 35/71801] using a syphon to create a suction of a component -- This CPC subclass covers accessories for mixers that use a siphon to generate suction and draw off a component during mixing or separating operations, facilitating transfer or removal of liquids or fine particles within the mixing (dissolving, emulsifying, dispersing) process.
[B01F 35/71805] using valves, gates, orifices or openings -- This subclass covers mixer accessories that employ valves, gates, orifices or other openings to regulate material flow during mixing or separating operations such as dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing, improving control over the introduction or discharge of fluids or powders.
[B01F 35/718051] being adjustable -- The code B01F 35/718051 designates adjustable accessories for mixers used in mixing, dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing operations. It covers components such as interchangeable blades, speed-adjustable shafts or variable-geometry fittings that can be set to different positions or dimensions to modify the mixing action. These adjustable parts improve flexibility and efficiency in separation and blending processes.
[B01F 35/7181] using fans or turbines -- B01F 35/7181 concerns accessories for mixers that employ fans or turbines to create airflow or fluid motion for mixing operations, such as dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing substances. The provision of turbine-driven or fan-driven devices is intended to improve the efficiency or effectiveness of the mixing process.
[B01F 35/71815] using vibrations, e.g. standing waves or ultrasonic vibrations -- B01F 35/71815 covers accessories for mixers that employ vibrational energy, such as standing-wave or ultrasonic vibrations, to improve mixing operations like dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing. The vibration assists separation and homogenisation within the mixing process.
[B01F 35/7182] with means for feeding the material with a fractal or tree-type distribution in a surface -- This subclass covers accessories for mixers that include a feeding mechanism which distributes material over a surface in a fractal or tree-type pattern, enhancing uniform dispersion and mixing efficiency.
[B01F 35/71825] using means for feeding one phase surrounded by another phase without mixing during the feeding -- The code B01F 35/71825 designates equipment for mixing processes where one material is introduced into another without causing immediate mixing. It covers accessories that feed a phase that is surrounded by a different phase, maintaining separation during injection, typical of dissolving or emulsifying operations.
[B01F 35/75] Discharge mechanisms -- B01F 35/75 covers discharge mechanisms that serve as accessories for mixers used in separating, mixing, dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing operations. The subclass includes outlets, spray nozzles, hopper-to-container discharge devices and similar equipment designed to remove or direct processed material from the mixing zone. It is part of the broader category for performing operations in the CPC system.
[B01F 35/751] Discharging by opening a gate, e.g. using discharge paddles -- B01F 35/751 pertains to accessories for mixers that enable discharge of the processed material by opening a gate, for example using discharge paddles. This subclass falls under mixing operations, specifically separating functions, and relates to equipment that empties a mixer without interrupting the mixing process.
[B01F 35/7511] the gate carrying a stirrer acting as discharge pump -- B01F 35/7511 covers a gate component that carries a stirrer which also functions as a discharge pump, used as an accessory to mixers. The device combines mixing action with material removal, facilitating separating operations such as dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing within the mixing vessel.
[B01F 35/752] with arrangements for converting the mechanism from mixing to discharging, e.g. by either guiding a mixture back into a receptacle or discharging it -- B01F 35/752 covers accessories for mixers that incorporate a mechanism to switch from mixing to discharging, such as devices that guide the processed mixture back into a receptacle or direct it out of the mixer. This subclass falls under mixing operations, specifically for equipment that can transition between stirring and emptying the material.
[B01F 35/753] Discharging at the upper side of the receptacle, e.g. by pressurising the liquid in the receptacle or by centrifugal force -- B01F 35/753 designates mixer accessories that discharge material from the upper side of the vessel, employing methods such as pressurising the liquid inside the receptacle or using centrifugal force to expel the product. This falls under the CPC hierarchy for performing operations, separating, and mixing (e.g., dissolving, emulsifying, dispersing).
[B01F 35/754] characterised by the means for discharging the components from the mixer -- This CPC subclass covers mixers that include specific means for removing the processed material from the mixing chamber, such as outlets, augers, pneumatic or other discharge devices, used after operations like dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing. It defines the features of the discharge mechanism that separate the mixed components from the equipment.
[B01F 35/7541] using belts -- The entry B01F 35/7541 concerns accessories for mixers that use belts to transmit power or motion during mixing, dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing operations. It falls within the CPC hierarchy of performing operations, separating, mixing, and specifically addresses belt-driven mixing equipment.
[B01F 35/75415] using gravity -- B01F 35/75415 covers accessories for mixers that employ gravity to introduce or move materials during mixing operations, such as inclined troughs, gravity-fed feeders, or tilted vessels that let powders or liquids flow without mechanical transport.
[B01F 35/7542] Discharging the components by overflow -- B01F 35/7542 covers accessories for mixers that remove the processed material by allowing it to flow out over the edge of the mixing chamber. The devices typically include overflow troughs, openings or channels designed to discharge liquids, suspensions or powders without additional mechanical forcing. This falls under the CPC group for mixing and dispersing equipment, specifically the subcategory for discharging components by overflow.
[B01F 35/75425] using pistons or plungers -- The code B01F 35/75425 refers to accessories for mixers that employ pistons or plungers to aid mixing actions such as dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing. This entry resides under the broader hierarchy of performing operations, separating and mixing.
[B01F 35/754251] reciprocating in the mixing receptacle -- The code B01F 35/754251 designates accessories for mixers that create a reciprocating motion of a tool or element inside the mixing receptacle, aiding processes such as dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing. It covers devices that swing, vibrate or oscillate within the vessel to improve homogeneity.
[B01F 35/7543] using pneumatic pressure, overpressure or gas pressure in a closed receptacle or circuit system -- This CPC subclass covers mixer accessories that employ pneumatic or gas pressure in a sealed vessel or closed circuit to assist mixing operations such as dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing. The pressure can be applied as over-pressure or controlled gas flow to improve material handling within the mixer.
[B01F 35/75435] using means for discharging the mixture in a pulsating or intermittent manner -- The Cooperative Patent Classification entry B01F 35/75435 pertains to accessories for mixers, specifically addressing mechanisms that discharge the mixed substance in a pulsating or intermittent fashion. This classification is part of a detailed system used by the European and United States patent offices to organize inventions related to performing operations, separating, and mixing processes.
[B01F 35/754351] using electrical pulses -- The code B01F 35/754351 designates accessories for mixers that employ electrical pulses to enhance mixing operations such as dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing. It covers devices that apply short-duration high-voltage or low-frequency pulses to the material being mixed, improving particle breakup, heat generation or chemical activation during the process.
[B01F 35/7544] using pumps -- B01F 35/7544 designates accessories for mixers that employ pumps to move liquids, slurries or dispersions during mixing operations such as dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing. The pumps serve to feed, recirculate or discharge material, improving efficiency and control of the mixing process.
[B01F 35/75441] Venturi pumps -- Venturi pumps are accessories for mixers that employ a flow-restriction nozzle to generate a low-pressure zone, creating suction that draws additional liquids or gases into the mixing stream. This action enhances separating, mixing, dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing processes by introducing auxiliary fluid flow without moving parts.
[B01F 35/75445] using pushers -- This subclass covers accessories for mixers that employ pushers to move material within the mixing or separating chamber. The pushers can be used to feed, distribute, or discharge substances, improving mixing efficiency or aiding separation processes.
[B01F 35/7545] using slides -- This CPC subclass covers accessories for mixers that incorporate sliding components, such as sliders that adjust the position of mixing blades or containers during operation. It is used for inventions that utilize slide mechanisms to facilitate mixing, dissolution, emulsification or dispersion.
[B01F 35/75455] using a rotary discharge means, e.g. a screw beneath the receptacle  B01F35/751  takes precedence -- The B01F 35/75455 subclass covers accessories for mixers that employ a rotary discharge device, such as a screw positioned beneath the receptacle, to continuously remove the mixed material. It refines the broader B01F 35/751 group on rotary discharge means and falls under the hierarchy of performing operations, separating, and mixing (dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing).
[B01F 35/754551] using helical screws -- The entry B01F 35/754551 covers accessories for mixers that employ helical screws to transport and manipulate material during mixing, separating, dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing operations. It specifies devices in which the screw motion provides stirring, conveying or pressure effects to improve the efficiency of the mixing process.
[B01F 35/7546] using squeezing means on a deformable container -- The code B01F 35/7546 covers accessories for mixers that employ a squeezing or compression mechanism on a flexible container, such as a bag or pouch, to promote mixing, dissolution, emulsifying or dispersing of its contents. It relates to the use of deformable vessels as active mixing elements, typically by applying external pressure to force the material against the container walls. This approach is classified under performing operations, separating, mixing and mixer accessories.
[B01F 35/75465] using suction, vacuum, e.g. with a pipette -- This CPC subclass covers accessories for mixers that employ suction-vacuum techniques, such as a pipette, to draw in or remove material during mixing operations like dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing. It is classified under performing operations → separating → mixing → accessories for mixers.
[B01F 35/7547] using valves, gates, orifices or openings -- The subclass covers accessories for mixers that employ valves, gates, orifices and other openings to regulate the passage of liquids or solids during mixing operations such as dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing.
[B01F 35/75471] being adjustable -- B01F 35/75471 designates adjustable accessories for mixers used in separating operations such as dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing. The entry covers components whose size, orientation or position can be varied to modify the mixing action, allowing flexible adjustment of the equipment.
[B01F 35/75475] using fans or ventilators -- The code B01F 35/75475 refers to accessories for mixers that employ fans or ventilators to provide airflow, cooling or drying during mixing operations such as dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing. These devices are used to improve material handling, reduce temperature rise and promote uniform distribution within the mixer.
[B01F 35/7548] using tilting or pivoting means for emptying the mixing receptacle -- The subclass B01F 35/7548 covers mixing equipment that empties the mixing vessel by tilting or pivoting the receptacle, such as mixers with hinged or pivoting bowls that can dump the product after processing. It also includes any accessories that enable this tilting or pivoting action.
[B01F 35/75485] the mixing receptacle rotating in opposite directions for mixing and for discharging -- The CPC entry B01F 35/75485 covers an accessory for mixers in which the mixing receptacle itself rotates in opposite directions, enabling simultaneous mixing action and efficient discharge of the processed material. It falls under the mixing sub-group for separating operations.
[B01F 35/7549] using distributing means, e.g. manifold valves or multiple fittings for supplying the discharge components to a plurality of dispensing places -- This subclass covers mixers equipped with distribution devices such as manifold valves or multiple fittings that direct the output of the mixing unit to several dispensing points. It relates to accessories that enable simultaneous or sequential supply of mixed material to multiple outlets, improving efficiency in processes like dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing.
[B01F 35/79] Preventing lumping, or comminuting lumps, during feeding or discharging, e.g. by means of vibrations, or by scrapers -- The code B01F 35/79 covers accessories fitted to mixers that prevent the formation of lumps or break up existing lumps during material feeding or discharge, typically by applying vibration or using scraper mechanisms. These devices are employed in mixing, dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing operations to keep bulk solids flowing uniformly.
[B01F 35/80] Forming a predetermined ratio of the substances to be mixed -- This CPC subclass covers devices attached to mixers that enable the accurate metering or dosing of multiple components so that a predefined proportion is introduced during mixing. It includes proportioning tanks, dosing pumps, and control mechanisms that ensure the desired ratio of substances such as solvents, reagents or pigments.
[B01F 35/81] Forming mixtures with changing ratios or gradients -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The ratio or gradient of the amount or flow rates of two or more materials to be mixed are varied to obtain a collection of several mixtures of different concentrations.
[B01F 35/82] by adding a material to be mixed to a mixture in response to a detected feature, e.g. density, radioactivity, consumed power or colour -- This subclass covers mixer accessories that automatically introduce additional material into a mixture when a specific property of the blend-such as density, radioactivity, power consumption or colour-is detected, allowing real-time adjustment of composition.
[B01F 35/83] by controlling the ratio of two or more flows, e.g. using flow sensing or flow controlling devices -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Controlling of the ratio of two or more flows of substances to be mixed whereby the operation is a continuous process.
[B01F 35/831] using one or more pump or other dispensing mechanisms for feeding the flows in predetermined proportion, e.g. one of the pumps being driven by one of the flows  B01F35/832  takes precedence -- B01F 35/831 covers accessories for mixers that employ one or more pumps or dispensing devices to introduce separate streams in a predetermined ratio, such as when a pump is driven by one of the streams, enabling coordinated feeding of liquids for processes like dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing.
[B01F 35/8311] with means for controlling the motor driving the pumps or the other dispensing mechanisms -- This CPC entry (B01F 35/8311) covers accessories for mixers that include devices for controlling the motor that drives pumps or other dispensing mechanisms, allowing precise regulation of flow or speed during mixing operations. It is situated in the hierarchy under Performing Operations → Separating → Mixing, e.g. dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing.
[B01F 35/832] Flow control by weighing -- This subclass covers accessories for mixers that regulate material flow by measuring weight, using scales or load cells to adjust feed rates during operations such as dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing, ensuring precise dosing and consistent mixing performance.
[B01F 35/833] Flow control by valves, e.g. opening intermittently -- This CPC subclass B01F 35/833 relates to accessories for mixers that regulate the flow of liquids or gases by means of valves that can be opened intermittently, supporting operations such as dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing. It covers flow-control devices integrated into mixing equipment used in separating and mixing processes.
[B01F 35/8331] the flow of one component operating the actuator of the valve, e.g. by deforming a membrane which operates de valve actuator -- B01F 35/8331 covers accessories for mixers in which the flow of a single component is used to power a valve actuator, such as by deforming a membrane that drives the valve. The classification applies to systems that employ fluid-driven actuation to control mixing or dispensing functions.
[B01F 35/834] the flow of substances to be mixed circulating in a closed circuit, e.g. from a container through valve, driving means, metering means or dispensing means, e.g. 3-way valve, and back to the container -- The code B01F 35/834 covers closed-circuit systems that continuously circulate substances to be mixed, moving the material from a container through driving means, valves, metering or dispensing devices such as three-way valves, and back to the container. It is classified under accessories for mixers within the separating-mixing group of the CPC.
[B01F 35/88] by feeding the materials batchwise -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Feeding of the material to be mixed whereby the operation is done in batches.
[B01F 35/881] by weighing, e.g. with automatic discharge -- This subclass concerns accessories for mixers that provide weighing functions, enabling the amount of material to be measured during mixing and often incorporating automatic discharge of the measured portion.
[B01F 35/8811] the weighing being effected by material receiving containers rotating or tilting under the influence of the weight of the material in those containers -- The entry covers a weighing method in which material-receiving containers attached to a mixer rotate or tilt automatically under the influence of the material's weight, allowing the amount of material to be measured as part of the mixing operation. It is classified under mixing accessories in CPC B01F 35/8811.
[B01F 35/882] using measuring chambers, e.g. volumetric pumps, for feeding the substances -- This subclass concerns accessories that employ measured chambers such as volumetric pumps to dose and feed substances into mixers or related equipment, enabling precise control of material quantity during mixing, separating, or dispersing operations.
[B01F 35/8821] involving controlling -- B01F 35/8821 pertains to accessories for mixers that include control elements for regulating speed, torque, or mixing intensity. The code is used for devices such as variable-speed drives, feedback sensors, and automated control units that improve mixing processes like dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing. It falls under the CPC hierarchy of performing operations, separating, mixing.
[B01F 35/8822] using measuring chambers of the piston or plunger type  B01F35/8823  takes precedence -- This CPC subclass covers accessories for mixers that employ piston-or-plunger-type measuring chambers to control the volume of liquids or powders added during mixing operations such as dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing. It is nested under the broader mixing category and is superseded by the more specific subclass B01F35/8823.
[B01F 35/88221] with double acting pistons  B01F35/88222  takes precedence -- The CPC entry B01F 35/88221 covers accessories for mixers that employ double-acting pistons, enabling simultaneous compression and expansion to improve mixing efficiency. It falls under PERFORMING OPERATIONS → SEPARATING → MIXING (e.g., dissolving, emulsifying, dispersing). The more specific subclass B01F 35/88222 supersedes this code.
[B01F 35/88222] without external means for driving the piston, e.g. the piston being driven by one of the components -- The entry B01F 35/88222 covers accessories for mixers in which the piston is moved without an external drive, relying instead on the material being processed to generate the piston's motion, such as expansion, gas evolution, or other internal forces that cause the piston to travel. This subclass groups designs where the piston's operation is self-powered by one of the components mixed.
[B01F 35/8823] using diaphragms or bellows -- The code B01F 35/8823 covers accessories for mixers that employ diaphragms or bellows to aid the mixing or separating process. Such devices use flexible membranes to create pressure variations, helping to dissolve, emulsify, disperse or otherwise treat materials within the mixing vessel. It falls under the broader categories of performing operations, separating, and mixing.
[B01F 35/883] using flow rate controls for feeding the substances -- The subclass B01F 35/883 covers accessories for mixers that regulate the flow rate of substances fed into the mixing zone, typically using flow-rate control devices. It applies to mixing operations such as dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing where precise feed control improves product consistency. The classification is located under performing operations, separating, mixing, accessories for mixers.
[B01F 35/892] for solid materials, e.g. using belts, vibrations, hoppers with variable outlets or hoppers with rotating elements, e.g. screws, at their outlet  B01F35/82  -  B01F35/88  take precedence -- This subclass covers auxiliary devices attached to mixers that handle solid feeds, such as belts, vibratory feeders, and hoppers equipped with adjustable outlets or rotating screws to control discharge. It pertains to the processing of solid materials in mixing operations. It falls within B01F 35/82-35/88 which have precedence.
[B01F 35/8921] the material after falling on a, e.g. rotatable, plate being wiped from this plate by means of a scraper -- B01F 35/8921 relates to accessories for mixers where material falls onto a rotatable plate and is cleared by a scraper; it describes a wiping mechanism that removes the deposited material from the plate, a feature used in operations such as dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing.
[B01F 35/894] Measuring receptacles therefor -- B01F 35/894 covers measuring receptacles designed as accessories for mixers used in separating and mixing processes such as dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing. These receptacles enable precise measurement of liquids or powders fed into the mixing apparatus, facilitating control of composition and reaction conditions. The class falls under the broader category of performing operations, separating, and mixing.
[B01F 35/896] characterised by the build-up of the device -- This CPC subclass covers accessories for mixers that are defined by a built-up portion of the device, such as fixed or removable material accumulations that aid mixing, cleaning or product handling. Typical examples include scraper walls, built-up agitator arms or removable coating plates that form part of the mixing vessel.
[B01F 35/90] Heating or cooling systems -- B01F 35/90 covers heating or cooling systems that are incorporated into mixer accessories to control the temperature of the material being mixed. It includes jackets, external coils, coolant-circulation units and other temperature-regulation devices attached to or integrated with mixers for processes such as dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing.
[B01F 35/91] using gas or liquid injected into the material, e.g. using liquefied carbon dioxide or steam -- The subclass covers mixing apparatus in which a gas or liquid is introduced directly into the material being processed, such as liquefied carbon dioxide or steam, to aid dissolving, dispersing, or emulsifying.
