It includes accessories designed to inject the fluid while the material is being mixed, improving efficiency and product uniformity.
[B01F 35/92] for heating the outside of the receptacle, e.g. heated jackets or burners -- This subclass covers devices that apply heat to the external surface of a mixing vessel, such as heated jackets or external burners, to raise the temperature of the material being mixed. It is used in processes like dissolving, emulsifying, or dispersing where temperature control of the receptacle is required.
[B01F 35/93] arranged inside the receptacle -- B01F 35/93 covers accessories for mixers that are positioned inside the mixing receptacle, such as agitators, impellers or static elements that aid in dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing materials during a mixing operation. It is a subcategory of the mixing group within the broader performing-operations hierarchy.
[B01F 35/94] using radiation, e.g. microwaves or electromagnetic radiation -- This subclass covers accessories for mixers that employ radiation such as microwaves or other electromagnetic energy to aid mixing, dissolving, emulsifying or dispersing materials. It includes devices that integrate a radiation source with mixing equipment to provide heating or chemical activation during the operation.
[B01F 35/95] using heated or cooled stirrers -- The code covers accessories that integrate temperature-control elements with stirrers used in mixing operations, allowing the stirrer to be heated or cooled while rotating. This enables precise thermal management of materials during processes such as dissolving, emulsifying, or dispersing.
[B01J] CHEMICAL OR PHYSICAL PROCESSES, e.g. CATALYSIS OR COLLOID CHEMISTRY -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Granulation processes or devices, e.g. by dividing liquid material into drops in drums or in fluidised beds or by expressing the material through sieves, making particulate materials hydrophobic.  Chemical or physical processes or apparatus therefor concerning:  Chemical processes involving a gas, e.g. gas passing through fixed beds or fluidised beds, reacting liquid with gaseous media other than in the presence of solid particles, reacting gaseous media with gaseous media or reacting gaseous media with non-particulate solids.  Chemical processes involving a liquid, e.g. liquids passing through fixed beds or fluidised beds, reacting liquid with gaseous media, reacting liquid with liquids or reacting liquids with non-particulate solids.  Production of colloidal materials or their solutions, e.g. making microcapsules by physical drying, spraying, coacervation or polymerisation.  Sorbent or filter aid compositions comprising inorganic or organic material, sorbents specially adapted for chromatography and processes for preparing or regenerating thereof.  Catalysts:  Ion exchange processes, e.g. cation, anion or amphoteric ion-exchange; regeneration of ion-exchangers and apparatus therefor.  **Glossary:** - catalyst: any substance that increases the rate of a reaction without itself being consumed. Catalysts are commonly used in their pure form or in combination with suitable carriers. It covers also a carrier forming part of the catalyst. - chromatography: a process in which a fluid is caused to flow along a linear path comprising a sorbent, with which the fluid competes in affinity for a constituent of the fluid. The constituent is sorbed from the moving fluid by the relatively immobile sorbent and re-dissolved by a later passing portion of the fluid until an equilibrium of the sorbing-dissolving step is set up causing the constituent to concentrate in a specific volume of the sorbent and to move along the path of the fluid at a rate slower than such fluid. - fluidised particle: finely divided solid particle lifted and agitated by a stream of fluid - fluidised-bed: fluidised-solid contacting technique in which finely divided particles are lifted and agitated by a rising stream of fluid - molecular sieve: material (e.g. zeolitic, mesoporous) having cavities and channels which by their size allow some molecules to pass through, but prevent others - solid particle: particle whether catalysts, reactant or inert in solid, semi-solid or pasty state - sorbent: a material which separates a constituent from a fluid mixture containing such constituent by sorption. The action in most instances is that of selective retention (i.e. the sorbent removes only the part of the fluid mixture for which it has the greatest affinity). - zeolites: crystalline aluminosilicates with base-exchange and molecular sieve properties, having three-dimensional, microporous lattice framework structure of tetrahedral oxide units; compounds isomorphous to those of the former category, wherein the aluminium or silicon atoms in the framework are partly or wholly replaced by atoms of other elements, e.g. by gallium, germanium, phosphorus or boron
[B01J 10/00] Chemical processes in general for reacting liquid with gaseous media other than in the presence of solid particles, or apparatus specially adapted therefor -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Chemical processes in general for reacting liquid with gaseous media other than in the presence of solid particles, or apparatus specially adapted therefore ( B01J19/08 takes precedence).  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Separation, e.g. distillation, evaporation -> B01D
[B01J 10/002] carried out in foam, aerosol or bubbles -- This CPC subclass covers processes or equipment where a liquid reacts with a gaseous phase without solid particles, carried out within foams, aerosols or bubbles. It includes methods of mixing, contacting or reacting the phases in dispersed media such as froths or sprays.
[B01J 10/005] carried out at high temperatures in the presence of a molten material -- B01J 10/005 covers chemical processes in which a liquid reacts with a gaseous phase at elevated temperatures while a molten material is present, without the involvement of solid particles. The classification applies to apparatus specially adapted for such high-temperature liquid-gas reactions in a molten medium.
[B01J 10/007] in the presence of catalytically active bodies, e.g. porous plates -- B01J 10/007 covers chemical processes in which a liquid phase reacts with a gaseous phase while a catalytically active body such as a porous plate is present, but without solid particles acting as catalysts. It applies to both the reaction method and apparatus specially adapted for this type of liquid-gas interaction. This subclass falls under separating operations within chemical or physical processes.
[B01J 10/02] of the thin-film type -- B01J 10/02 covers chemical processes in which a liquid reacts with a gas in the absence of solid particles, using equipment specially adapted for thin-film operation. It includes methods and apparatus that form a continuous liquid layer through which the gaseous phase contacts the reactants, enabling efficient reaction without a solid catalyst.
[B01J 12/00] Chemical processes in general for reacting gaseous media with gaseous media -- B01J 12/00 covers general chemical processes in which a gaseous reactant is combined with another gas, such as gas-phase synthesis, combustion, or catalytic reactions, without specifying particular substances or equipment.
[B01J 12/002] carried out in the plasma state  generating or handling plasma  H05H1/00 -- B01J 12/002 covers chemical processes where gaseous reactants are combined or treated while in a plasma state, including the generation, maintenance and manipulation of plasma for such reactions. It falls under the CPC group for performing operations, separating, and chemical or physical processes such as catalysis or colloid chemistry. The entry cross-references H05H1/00 for plasma generation technologies.
[B01J 12/005] carried out at high temperatures, e.g. by pyrolysis -- B01J 12/005 relates to chemical or physical processes in which gases react with other gases at elevated temperatures, typically involving pyrolysis or other high-temperature treatment. The class covers methods that separate or transform gaseous media by heating, often to induce catalytic or colloidal reactions. It falls under the broader categories of performing operations, separating, and chemical or physical processes.
[B01J 12/007] in the presence of catalytically active bodies, e.g. porous plates -- This subclass covers gas-phase reactions where two gaseous streams are contacted in the presence of a catalytically active body such as a porous plate or other structured catalyst. It belongs to the broader group of separating operations and chemical or physical processes that employ catalysis to facilitate reactions between gases.
[B01J 12/02] for obtaining at least one reaction product which, at normal temperature, is in the solid state -- This CPC subclass B01J 12/02 covers processes in which two or more gases are reacted together to form at least one solid product that is stable under ambient conditions. It includes gas-phase synthesis, gas-solid precipitation, and related steps for isolating the solid reaction product.
[B01J 13/00] Colloid chemistry, e.g. the production of colloidal materials or their solutions, not otherwise provided for -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Methods for producing colloidal materials or their solutions, e.g. sols, gels, aerosols, aerogels as well as methods for producing microcapsules or microballoons.  **Glossary:** - Colloid: a substance microscopically dispersed evenly throughout another substance. The colloidal system, which may be solid, liquid or gaseous, has a very high stability, i.e. precipitation does not occur under the influence of gravity. - Microcapsule: a core-shell particle with a stable, non-transient shell - Microballoon: a gas-filled microcapsule - Sol: a colloidal system of solid particles in a liquid medium - Non-Newtonian sol: sol displaying a decreasing viscosity with increasing shear stress - Gel: a substantially dilute cross-linked system, which exhibits no flow when in the steady-state. A gel has the appearance of a jelly-like material. - Aerosol: a colloidal system comprising fine solid particles or liquid droplets in a gaseous medium - Aerogel: a gel in which the liquid component of the gel has been replaced with a gas - Xerogel: a solid formed from a gel by drying with unhindered shrinkage - Simple coacervation: phase separation process involving only one type of hydrophilic polymer - Complex coacervation: phase separation process involving at least two types of hydrophilic polymers - Interfacial polymerization: polymerization process where the reactants (monomers) meet at an interface where the polymerization reaction occurs and the shell material if formed - In situ polymerization: polymerization process where all reactants (monomers) are present in the same phase. Also processes comprising an initiator in the other phase are in situ polymerization processes. - After-treatment of capsule walls by coating: process in which coating of the microcapsule wall results in core-multiple shell particles - Dispersant: A non-surface active polymer or a surface active substance added to a suspension to improve the separation of particles and to prevent settling or clumping - Emulsion: Two immiscible liquids mixed with small droplets of one liquid dispersed (separated and distributed throughout the space) in the other liquid - Foam: Substance that is formed from trapped gas bubbles - Fog: Collection of liquid water droplets or ice crystals suspended in the air - Surface active agents(surfactants): Substance which have the effect of reducing the surface tension of a solvent. These substances also known as wetting agents, contain a combination of polar (hydrophilic) and non-polar (hydrophobic) parts which serve to bind oil and water together. They locate at the phase boundary between the water phase and the organic phase, or if there is no room there, they will congregate together and form micelles. - HLB: Hydrophilic-lipophilic balance of a surfactant. Measure of the degree to which it is hydrophilic or lipophilic. - Organosol: Finely divided or colloidal suspension of insoluble material in a suspending organic liquid - Thixotropic fluid: Fluid which takes a finite time to attain equilibrium viscosity when introduced to a step change in shear rate - O/W emulsion: Oil-in- water emulsion - W/O emulsion: Water-in-oil emusion - W/0/W emulsions: Water-in-oil-in-water multphase emulsions
[B01J 13/0004] Preparation of sols  by physical processes  B01J13/0086 , aerosols  B01J13/0095 -- The CPC subclass B01J 13/0004 covers methods for preparing colloidal solutions (sols) through physical means such as precipitation, drying or grinding, and includes related processes for generating aerosols. It falls under the broader categories of performing operations, separating, and chemical or physical processes like colloid chemistry. This subclass is used for inventions that produce stable colloidal dispersions without relying on chemical reactions.
[B01J 13/0008] Sols of inorganic materials in water -- B01J 13/0008 covers aqueous solutions of inorganic substances, focusing on the preparation, handling and use of such solutions in colloid chemistry. It includes processes for dispersing inorganic particles, forming sols, and related water-based formulations not covered elsewhere.
[B01J 13/0013] from a precipitate -- The B01J 13/0013 subgroup covers methods for producing colloidal materials or solutions by working directly with a precipitated solid, such as recovering, washing, redispersing or otherwise treating the precipitate to generate a stable colloid. It applies to processes where the precipitate serves as the starting material rather than alternative pathways.
[B01J 13/0017] by extraction of ions from aqueous solutions -- The subclass B01J 13/0017 covers processes that extract selected ions from aqueous solutions, typically using solvent extraction, ion-exchange or similar techniques. It falls within colloid chemistry, addressing the separation of ionic species to produce purified liquids or recover valuable metals.
[B01J 13/0021] containing a solid organic phase -- This subclass covers processes that involve a solid organic phase, such as the preparation, handling, or separation of colloidal materials or solutions containing solid organic components. It falls under performing operations, separating, and chemical or physical processes in colloid chemistry.
[B01J 13/0026] containing a liquid organic phase -- The subgroup B01J 13/0026 covers colloid-chemistry processes in which a liquid organic phase is employed, such as the preparation, modification or stabilization of colloidal particles within an organic solvent. It includes methods that use an organic liquid medium to form or treat colloidal dispersions, distinguishing these operations from those conducted in aqueous or solid phases.
[B01J 13/003] Preparation from aqueous sols -- B01J 13/003 covers methods for preparing colloidal materials or solutions by starting from aqueous sols, encompassing processes that convert water-based suspensions into new colloidal products. It lies within performing-operations, under separating and chemical or physical processes, specifically colloid chemistry.
[B01J 13/0034] Additives, e.g. in view of promoting stabilisation or peptisation -- This CPC subclass concerns additives that are introduced into colloidal materials or their solutions to improve stability or to induce peptisation, such as surfactants, polymers, inorganic ions or other stabilising agents. It applies to processes where these additives promote the formation, maintenance or controlled breakdown of colloidal particles in chemical or physical manufacturing.
[B01J 13/0039] Post treatment -- The entry B01J 13/0039 covers post-treatment operations applied to colloidal materials or their solutions after production, including steps such as stabilization, washing, drying, chemical modification or size-adjustment to obtain the required properties. It falls under separating processes within performing operations for chemical or physical treatment in colloid chemistry.
[B01J 13/0043] containing elemental metal  for medical or diagnostical purposes  A61K ,  G01N -- B01J 13/0043 covers colloidal chemistry processes that produce or handle colloidal materials or solutions not covered elsewhere, specifically those where elemental metal particles are incorporated for medical or diagnostic use, linking the pharmaceutical class A61K and the analytical class G01N.
[B01J 13/0047] containing a metal oxide -- B01J 13/0047 relates to colloid-chemical processes in which a metal oxide is present, such as the synthesis, stabilization, or modification of colloidal particles, dispersions, or solutions containing metal-oxide phases. It covers methods for producing metal-oxide colloids, incorporating them into composites, or using them as catalysts or supports.
[B01J 13/0052] Preparation of gels -- The CPC subclass B01J 13/0052 covers methods for preparing gels, including the formation of gel networks from colloidal materials through chemical or physical processes such as polymerisation, cross-linking or precipitation. It is situated under performing operations, separating, chemical or physical processes, specifically colloid chemistry.
[B01J 13/0056] containing inorganic material and water -- B01J 13/0056 pertains to colloid chemistry methods where an inorganic material is dispersed in water, including the preparation, stabilization, and manipulation of aqueous inorganic colloids. It covers processes for producing, treating, or using such water-based inorganic dispersions that are not covered by more specific subclasses.
[B01J 13/006] by precipitation, coagulation, hydrolyse coacervation -- B01J 13/006 relates to processes for making colloidal substances or their solutions by precipitation, coagulation or hydrolytic coacervation. It falls under separating operations within chemical or physical processes, particularly colloid chemistry, and covers the formation of colloidal particles from dissolved precursors through controlled precipitation or phase separation.
[B01J 13/0065] containing an organic phase -- B01J 13/0065 covers methods for preparing or utilizing colloidal materials that contain an organic liquid phase, such as dispersions or emulsions where solid particles are suspended in an organic medium. The subclass applies to separation or synthesis processes involving colloids with an organic phase that are not classified elsewhere.
[B01J 13/0069] Post treatment -- This CPC subclass deals with post-treatment operations applied to colloidal materials or their solutions after the primary formation step, including actions such as stabilization, washing, drying, or functionalisation that are not covered elsewhere. It concerns procedures that modify or condition colloids to obtain specific properties or improve performance.
[B01J 13/0073] Preparation of non-Newtonian sols, e.g. thixotropic solutions -- B01J 13/0073 covers processes for preparing colloidal sols that display non-Newtonian rheology, such as thixotropic solutions whose viscosity decreases under shear. The methods include controlling particle size, surface chemistry, and stabiliser content to achieve the desired flow behavior.
[B01J 13/0078] containing inorganic material and water -- This CPC subclass covers processes for preparing colloidal dispersions in which the dispersed phase consists of inorganic material combined with water. It includes methods such as precipitation, hydrolysis or other chemical and physical means to obtain stable inorganic-water colloids not addressed by more specific subclasses, covering aqueous inorganic nanoparticle suspensions, gels or sols.
[B01J 13/0082] containing an organic phase -- B01J 13/0082 relates to colloid-chemistry processes in which an organic phase is present, such as the preparation, handling or modification of emulsions, suspensions or solutions that contain organic solvents together with colloidal particles. The entry covers methods and apparatus for producing or treating these organic-phase colloids, including steps of mixing, stabilising, or altering the dispersed phase.
[B01J 13/0086] Preparation of sols by physical processes  colloid mills  B02C -- B01J 13/0086 covers methods for producing colloidal sols by purely physical means, such as using colloid mills or high-shear devices to disperse solid particles into a liquid medium. The entry focuses on mechanical grinding, milling or agitation techniques that create stable suspensions without chemical reactions.
[B01J 13/0091] Preparation of aerogels, e.g. xerogels -- This subclass covers processes for preparing aerogels such as xerogels, typically involving the formation of a colloidal gel followed by removal of the liquid phase to yield a highly porous solid. Techniques may include gelation, solvent exchange, supercritical drying or ambient drying to retain the three-dimensional network.
[B01J 13/0095] Preparation of aerosols -- The CPC subclass B01J 13/0095 covers methods for preparing aerosols, which are dispersions of fine particles or liquid droplets in a gas. It falls under the broader category of performing operations, separating, and chemical or physical processes such as colloid chemistry, specifically the production of colloidal materials or their solutions not otherwise covered.
[B01J 13/02] Making microcapsules or microballoons -- B01J 13/02 relates to colloid-chemical processes for producing microcapsules or microballoons, that is, tiny encapsulated particles or hollow spheres formed from colloidal materials or their solutions. It covers methods of preparing, shaping and solidifying these micro-structures, often for controlled release, protective coating or buoyancy applications.
[B01J 13/025] Applications of microcapsules not provided for in other subclasses -- B01J 13/025 covers the use of microcapsules in processes that are not covered by other specific subclasses of colloid chemistry, such as delivering active agents, protecting substances, or enabling controlled release in various chemical or physical operations. This entry falls under the hierarchy of performing operations, separating, and chemical or physical processes within the Cooperative Patent Classification system.
[B01J 13/04] by physical processes, e.g. drying, spraying -- B01J 13/04 covers the production of colloidal materials or their solutions by physical means such as drying or spraying, rather than by chemical reactions. It is located in the CPC hierarchy under performing operations, separating, chemical or physical processes, specifically colloid chemistry.
[B01J 13/043] Drying and spraying -- B01J 13/043 covers drying and spraying operations applied to colloidal materials or their solutions, where the process is not covered by more specific subclasses. It falls under performing operations, separating, and chemical or physical processes such as catalysis or colloid chemistry, dealing with the production or handling of colloids.
[B01J 13/046] combined with gelification or coagulation -- B01J 13/046 covers methods and apparatus for producing colloidal materials or their solutions together with a subsequent gelification or coagulation step, enabling the formation of solid or semi-solid colloidal gels. The classification falls under performing operations, separating, chemical or physical processes, specifically colloid chemistry.
[B01J 13/06] by phase separation -- The B01J 13/06 entry covers processes that produce colloidal materials or their solutions by using phase-separation techniques, such as precipitation, liquid-liquid extraction or other methods that separate a dispersed phase from a continuous phase. It is applied when the colloid is obtained through a phase-split step rather than by other specific chemical or physical routes. This classification falls under the broader categories of performing operations, separating, and chemical or physical processes in colloid chemistry.
[B01J 13/08] Simple coacervation, i.e. addition of highly hydrophilic material -- B01J 13/08 covers simple coacervation processes in colloid chemistry, where a highly hydrophilic material is added to a polymer or protein solution to induce phase separation and form colloidal particles or coatings. The classification addresses the preparation of colloidal suspensions, gels or precipitates by this precipitation technique, without requiring additional reagents or complex reaction steps. It is used for producing fine powders, encapsulated compounds or uniform dispersions.
[B01J 13/10] Complex coacervation, i.e. interaction of oppositely charged particles -- B01J 13/10 relates to complex coacervation, a colloidal process in which oppositely charged particles in a solution associate to form a dense polymer-rich phase, enabling encapsulation, separation and the synthesis of specialized materials.
[B01J 13/12] removing solvent from the wall-forming material solution -- The B01J 13/12 subclass relates to methods for removing solvent from solutions that contain wall-forming materials, such as colloidal particles used to produce films, gels or membranes. It includes drying, evaporation, precipitation or other separation techniques aimed at concentrating the wall-forming component.
[B01J 13/125] by evaporation of the solvent  apparatus therefor  B01J13/043 -- B01J 13/125 pertains to methods and apparatus for producing colloidal materials or their solutions through the evaporation of solvents, specifically addressing equipment designed for this process. This classification falls under the broader category of colloid chemistry within the Cooperative Patent Classification system used by the EPO and USPTO.
[B01J 13/14] Polymerisation -- B01J 13/14 covers the polymerisation of monomers in colloidal media, including the formation of polymer particles, dispersions, sols or gels, and related processes for producing colloidal polymers not classified elsewhere. It falls under the separating section of performing operations, specifically chemical or physical processes such as colloid chemistry.
[B01J 13/16] Interfacial polymerisation -- Interfacial polymerisation is a process where monomers dissolved in two immiscible liquids react at their common interface to create a polymer film, coating, or membrane. It is employed to form thin-film separation layers, encapsulate particles, or fabricate composite materials, and falls under colloid chemistry for its role in separating and shaping polymeric structures.
[B01J 13/18] In situ  polymerisation with all reactants being present in the same phase -- B01J 13/18 covers in-situ polymerisation processes where all reactants-monomers, initiators, catalysts and any additives-are present together in a single phase, typically a liquid colloidal medium. The classification applies to methods that create polymers or polymer particles directly within that phase, without transferring components between separate phases.
[B01J 13/185] in an organic phase -- This subclass, B01J 13/185, covers processes that produce, stabilize or modify colloidal particles within an organic liquid medium, such as the synthesis of organometallic nanoparticles or polymer dispersions, distinguishing them from aqueous or solid-phase methods.
[B01J 13/20] After-treatment of capsule walls, e.g. hardening -- B01J 13/20 covers processes for post-treatment of capsule walls, such as hardening or strengthening the shells of micro- or nano-capsules used in colloidal materials. The methods improve mechanical stability, control release properties, or modify surface characteristics of the encapsulated particles.
[B01J 13/203] Exchange of core-forming material by diffusion through the capsule wall -- This CPC subclass B01J 13/203 covers methods for exchanging core-forming material in colloidal capsules by diffusion through the capsule wall, used in producing encapsulated products such as pharmaceuticals or functional materials. It falls under performing operations, separating, chemical or physical processes, colloid chemistry.
[B01J 13/206] Hardening; drying -- This CPC subclass pertains to methods for hardening or drying colloidal substances, such as the removal of moisture or the setting of colloidal gels, within chemical or physical processes. It falls under performing operations, separating, and the broader field of colloid chemistry.
[B01J 13/22] Coating -- Class B01J 13/22 relates to coating processes that employ colloidal chemistry, covering the preparation and application of colloidal materials or solutions to form protective, decorative or functional layers on substrates. The subclass falls under the broader categories of performing operations, separating, and chemical or physical processes such as catalysis or colloid chemistry, and is used for inventions not otherwise classified elsewhere.
[B01J 14/00] Chemical processes in general for reacting liquids with liquids -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Chemical processes and apparatus specially adapted for reacting liquids with liquids other than in the presence of solid particles.
[B01J 14/005] in the presence of catalytically active bodies, e.g. porous plates -- B01J 14/005 includes liquid-liquid reacting processes that are carried out in the presence of a catalytically active body such as a porous plate, fixed or structured, which provides a surface for the catalyst to act while the liquids flow past or through it. The subclass covers methods where the solid catalytic element is not dispersed in the liquids but is positioned as a separate, often porous, support.
[B01J 15/00] Chemical processes in general for reacting gaseous media with non-particulate solids, e.g. sheet material -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Chemical processes and apparatus specially adapted for reacting gases with non particulate solids, e.g. sheet material
[B01J 15/005] in the presence of catalytically active bodies, e.g. porous plates -- This CPC subclass B01J 15/005 covers chemical processes in which a gaseous medium reacts with non-particulate solid materials such as sheets or porous plates that also act as catalytically active bodies. The presence of the catalytic solid, often a porous plate, facilitates the reaction and may be used for separation or conversion of the gas.
[B01J 16/00] Chemical processes in general for reacting liquids with non- particulate solids, e.g. sheet material -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Chemical processes and apparatus specially adapted for reacting liquids with non particulate solids, e.g.sheet material
[B01J 16/005] in the presence of catalytically active bodies, e.g. porous plates -- This subclass covers chemical processes in which a liquid reacts with a non-particulate solid, such as sheet material, while a catalytically active body-typically a porous plate or similar structure-is present to promote the reaction, enabling heterogeneous catalysis during separation operations.
[B01J 19/00] Chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes in general -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Particularly important is the general aspect of the processes and/or apparatus classified in this group. Processes or apparatus for specific applications should be classified in the relevant specific places for these processes or apparatus. An exception of this rule concerns apparatus used for polymerization which should be, in addition to the polymerization subclasses C08F , C08G , also classified in B01J19/00 Subclasses if specific apparatus features are very relevant.
[B01J 19/0006] Controlling or regulating processes  controlling or regulating in general  G05 -- This subclass covers methods and apparatus for governing chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes, such as catalysis or colloid chemistry, without specifying a particular process type. It falls within the separating operations area of the Performing Operations section, addressing general control or regulation of such processes.
[B01J 19/0013] Controlling the temperature of the process -- The CPC entry B01J 19/0013 covers methods and apparatus for controlling the temperature of a chemical, physical or physico-chemical process, particularly during separation operations. It includes heating, cooling, or maintaining a set temperature to optimise reaction rates, product quality or energy efficiency. This sub-group falls under the broader categories of performing operations, separating and chemical or physical processes such as catalysis or colloid chemistry.
[B01J 19/002] Avoiding undesirable reactions or side-effects, e.g. avoiding explosions, or improving the yield by suppressing side-reactions -- B01J 19/002 covers techniques for preventing unwanted reactions or side-effects during chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes, such as avoiding explosions and improving product yield by suppressing side-reactions. It falls under the hierarchy Performing Operations → Separating → Chemical or Physical Processes.
[B01J 19/0026] Avoiding carbon deposits  inhibiting incrustation in general,  C23F14/00 ,  C23F15/00 -- The B01J 19/0026 entry covers techniques for preventing carbon fouling or incrustation during separation operations that involve chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes. It includes methods for reducing or eliminating carbon deposits on equipment or in reaction streams, linking to related subclasses C23F14/00 and C23F15/00.
[B01J 19/0033] Optimalisation processes, i.e. processes with adaptive control systems  adaptive control systems  per se   G05B13/00 -- B01J 19/0033 covers optimisation processes that employ adaptive control systems to continuously adjust operating conditions in chemical, physical or physico-chemical separations. It includes the use of feedback-driven algorithms, sensors and actuators that modify parameters such as temperature, pressure or feed rates during the process, corresponding to the broader G05B13/00 class for adaptive control per se.
[B01J 19/004] Multifunctional apparatus for automatic manufacturing of various chemical products  sequential reactions  B01J19/0046 -- B01J 19/004 covers multifunctional apparatus designed for the automatic production of a range of chemical products through sequential reactions. The devices integrate mixing, reaction, separation and possibly catalytic steps in a single, continuous system, enabling efficient, automated chemical synthesis.
[B01J 19/0046] Sequential or parallel reactions, e.g. for the synthesis of polypeptides or polynucleotides; Apparatus and devices for combinatorial chemistry or for making molecular arrays  synthesis methods  per se   C40B50/00 -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Methods and devices in general for the purpose of combinatorial chemistry or the making of combinatorial arrays, e.g. biochips. This implies that a synthesis of a library is performed or is meant to be performed with a device.  It is particularly relevant to note that documents classified in this sub-group relate to general aspects of such methods and devices, such as general process steps, or the physical aspects of bio-chips.  Purely chemical or biochemical aspects, analytical aspects, as well as libraries per sé, are NOT covered by this group  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Peptide libraries -> C07K1/047 - Nucleaic acid libraries and their screening -> C12N15/1034 and subgroups - Nucleic acid analysis characterised by the use of probe arrays or probe chips. -> C12Q1/6837 - Methods for sequencing involving nucleic acid arrays, e.g. Sequencing By Hybridisation -> C12Q1/6874 - Combinatorial libraries as such -> C40B40/00 and subgroups - General methods per se for simultaneous synthesis of five or more different organic compounds -> C40B50/00
[B01J 19/0053] Details of the reactor -- B01J 19/0053 covers the specification of reactors used in chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes, including their geometry, materials, internal configurations and operating parameters. It pertains to the design and construction details essential for performing separating operations such as catalysis or colloid chemistry.
[B01J 19/006] Baffles -- Baffles are internal plates or obstacles installed in reactors, columns, or other process equipment to promote mixing, improve heat transfer and prevent short-circuiting of fluid flow, thereby enhancing the efficiency of chemical or physical separations. In the CPC they appear under B01J 19/006, within the hierarchy of performing operations, separating, and chemical or physico-chemical processes.
[B01J 19/0066] Stirrers  mixing  per se   B01F -- B01J 19/0066 covers stirrers employed to mix substances in chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes, serving to homogenise, disperse or enhance reactions. The provision includes rotary, magnetic, ultrasonic and other mixing devices that operate independently of specific reaction vessels.
[B01J 19/0073] Sealings  sealings for pressure vessels  per se   F16J15/00 -- B01J 19/0073 concerns sealings for pressure vessels, covering their design, materials and performance to ensure containment under pressure. It is placed within the broader class of chemical, physical or physico-chemical separating processes in the CPC hierarchy.
[B01J 19/008] Processes for carrying out reactions under cavitation conditions -- B01J 19/008 refers to processes that employ cavitation-generated by ultrasonic or hydrodynamic means-to carry out chemical reactions, such as sonochemical synthesis, ultrasonic-assisted polymerisation and emulsification. The subclass is placed under Performing Operations → Separating → Chemical or Physical Processes, covering the use of bubble collapse to enhance reaction rates, yields or selectivity.
[B01J 19/0086] Processes carried out with a view to control or to change the pH-value; Applications of buffer salts; Neutralisation reactions -- The CPC entry B01J 19/0086 covers processes that adjust or maintain the pH of a medium, including the use of buffer salts and neutralisation reactions, as part of chemical, physical or physico-chemical operations such as separation or catalysis. It is situated under the broader categories of performing operations, separating, and chemical or physical processes.
[B01J 19/0093] Microreactors, e.g. miniaturised or microfabricated reactors  laboratory containers with capillary fluid transport in microfabricated channels or chambers  B01L3/5027 -- B01J 19/0093 classifies microreactors, including miniaturised or microfabricated reactors and laboratory containers that use capillary fluid transport in micro-fabricated channels or chambers, for chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes. It sits under the hierarchy of Performing Operations - Separating - Chemical or Physical Processes, covering small-scale reaction equipment in the CPC system.
[B01J 19/02] Apparatus characterised by being constructed of material selected for its chemically-resistant properties -- B01J 19/02 covers apparatus used in chemical, physical or physico-chemical processing that is built from materials chosen for their chemical-resistant properties. The classification applies when the selection of corrosion-resistant or inert construction material is a defining feature of the equipment, such as reactors, mixers, filters or other process units.
[B01J 19/06] Solidifying liquids -- The B01J 19/06 class covers processes that transform liquids into solid forms, such as freezing, gelation, precipitation, crystallization or solidification of melts. It includes methods that induce solid formation by cooling, adding agents, changing solvent conditions or applying pressure, and is used for chemical, physical or physico-chemical operations that solidify liquids.
[B01J 19/08] Processes employing the direct application of electric or wave energy, or particle radiation -- B01J 19/08 covers processes that use an electric field, electromagnetic waves, or particle radiation applied directly to a material to induce chemical, physical or physico-chemical changes, such as heating, ionisation, activation or transformation. It falls under the broader categories of performing operations, separating, and chemical or physical processes, and includes methods like plasma treatment, laser-induced reactions and electron-beam processing.
[B01J 19/081] employing particle radiation or gamma-radiation -- B01J 19/081 covers the use of particle radiation or gamma-radiation to drive chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes, particularly in separation operations. It is situated under the broader categories of performing operations, separating, and chemical or physical processes such as catalysis or colloid chemistry.
[B01J 19/082] Gamma-radiation only -- B01J 19/082 covers separation or processing methods that employ only gamma-radiation as the operative energy source. It includes techniques such as radiolytic decomposition, isotope enrichment, or sterilisation where gamma photons are the sole source of radiation.
[B01J 19/084] Neutron beams only -- B01J 19/084 covers the use of neutron beams as a tool in chemical or physical operations, such as probing, analysing or inducing reactions in substances. It falls under the broader categories of performing operations, separating, and chemical or physical processes, and is applied when neutron irradiation is the primary means of effecting or monitoring the process.
[B01J 19/085] Electron beams only -- B01J 19/085 identifies processes that rely exclusively on electron beams as the energy source for chemical, physical or physico-chemical operations, such as electron-beam induced polymerisation, curing, surface modification or separation. This subclass is nested under Performing Operations - Separating - Chemical or Physical Processes and covers any method where the electron beam alone effects the desired transformation.
[B01J 19/087] employing electric or magnetic energy -- B01J 19/087 covers the use of electric or magnetic energy to drive chemical, physical or physico-chemical separating processes, such as electro- or magnetically assisted catalysis, electrophoresis, or magnetically induced phase changes.
[B01J 19/088] giving rise to electric discharges  for heating purposes  H05B7/00 ; for the production of ozone  C01B13/11 ,  H01T19/00 -- This subclass deals with processes that generate electric discharges to supply heat or to produce ozone, using arcs, sparks, corona or dielectric barrier discharges as the energy source. It covers both heating applications (H05B7/00) and ozone-generation methods (C01B13/11, H01T19/00).
[B01J 19/10] employing sonic or ultrasonic vibrations -- B01J 19/10 relates to the use of sonic or ultrasonic vibrations to facilitate chemical or physical separation operations. It covers methods and devices that apply acoustic energy to promote mixing, cavitation, emulsification, or particle dispersion during processes such as extraction, precipitation, or filtration. The subclass is part of the broader group for separating operations in the CPC.
[B01J 19/12] employing electromagnetic waves -- B01J 19/12 covers separation methods that employ electromagnetic radiation, such as microwaves, infrared, ultraviolet or other waves, to effect chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes. The subclass includes techniques where the energy of the waves drives the separation of components, assists catalysis, or modifies colloidal systems. It falls under performing operations → separating → chemical or physical processes.
[B01J 19/121] Coherent waves, e.g. laser beams  lasers  per se   H01S3/00 -- B01J 19/121 covers the use of coherent electromagnetic radiation, notably laser beams, as a tool or method in chemical, physical or physico-chemical processing and separation operations. It includes the generation, handling and application of lasers for tasks such as cutting, heating, analysing or otherwise manipulating substances in performing-operations contexts.
[B01J 19/122] Incoherent waves  gamma-radiation  B01J19/082 -- B01J 19/122 pertains to the use of incoherent waves, specifically gamma-radiation, in chemical or physical processes, as part of a broader category dealing with general chemical and physico-chemical processes. This classification is used for inventions involving radiation-induced reactions or treatments.
[B01J 19/123] Ultraviolet light -- B01J 19/123 designates methods that employ ultraviolet radiation to carry out separating operations within chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes, such as UV-induced photolysis, UV-driven precipitation or filtration. It falls under Performing Operations - Separating - Chemical or Physical Processes and includes any UV-based technique for manipulating substances.
[B01J 19/124] generated by microwave irradiation -- The class includes processes where microwave energy is applied to drive chemical or physical transformations, such as heating, synthesis, or activation of substances. It covers batch or continuous operations that rely on microwave irradiation to initiate or accelerate reactions, including the formation of nanomaterials, polymers, or other compounds.
[B01J 19/125] X-rays -- The B01J 19/125 subclass covers the use of X-rays in chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes, such as irradiation of substances to induce reactions, activation, structural analysis, or modification of materials. It groups methods where X-ray exposure is a central step in the operation.
[B01J 19/126] Microwaves -- B01J 19/126 covers the application of microwave radiation to carry out chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes, such as heating, catalysis or separation. It includes the use of microwaves to promote reactions, improve yields, or effect material transformations in batch or continuous systems.
[B01J 19/127] Sunlight; Visible light -- B01J 19/127 covers chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes that use sunlight or visible light as an energy source. It is nested under performing operations, separating, and catalytic or colloid chemistry, indicating applications such as photochemical reactions, light-driven separations and related technologies.
[B01J 19/128] Infrared light -- The CPC entry B01J 19/128 - Infrared light pertains to the use of infrared radiation in chemical or physical separation operations, such as catalysis, colloid chemistry or other physico-chemical processes. It covers methods where infrared energy is applied to facilitate or control the separation of substances, including heating, activation or selective interaction with materials.
[B01J 19/129] Radiofrequency -- B01J 19/129 covers the use of radiofrequency energy in chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes, particularly for separation operations such as heating, drying, extraction, or catalysis. The subclass includes equipment and methods that generate or apply radiofrequency fields to treat materials or drive reactions.
[B01J 19/14] Production of inert gas mixtures -- B01J 19/14 relates to processes for producing mixtures of inert gases, such as nitrogen, argon, helium or neon, by methods that may include separation, liquefaction, compression, blending or other physical-chemical operations. The subclass falls under performing operations, separating, and chemical or physical processes.
[B01J 19/16] Preventing evaporation or oxidation of non-metallic liquids by applying a floating layer, e.g. of microballoons -- The B01J 19/16 entry covers methods that protect non-metallic liquids from evaporation or oxidation by applying a floating barrier, such as a layer of micro-balloons, on the liquid surface. This technique is classified under performing operations, separating, chemical or physical processes, and relates to general chemical, physical, or physico-chemical treatments.
[B01J 19/18] Stationary reactors having moving elements inside -- A chemical or physico-chemical reactor in which the vessel itself is fixed but contains internal components that move, such as stirrers, paddles, or rotating shafts, to promote mixing, heat transfer or contact between phases. These moving elements are integral to processes like catalysis, slurry treatment or colloidal reactions.
[B01J 19/1806] resulting in a turbulent flow of the reactants, such as in centrifugal-type reactors, or having a high Reynolds-number -- B01J 19/1806 relates to chemical or physico-chemical processes in which the reactants are subjected to turbulent flow, typically achieved in centrifugal-type reactors or any system operating at a high Reynolds number, to enhance mixing and reaction rates. This sub-class falls under separating operations within the broader performing-operations hierarchy.
[B01J 19/1812] Tubular reactors -- B01J 19/1812 covers tubular reactors, which are long, tube-shaped vessels where reactants flow continuously and undergo chemical, physical or physico-chemical transformations such as catalysis, colloid chemistry or separation. The subclass focuses on the design, operation and use of these reactors in processes that perform reactions while moving fluids through the tube.
[B01J 19/1818] in series -- This CPC group covers chemical or physico-chemical processes in which unit operations are arranged in a series so that the output of one step becomes the input of the next without intermediate storage, as found in continuous-flow or cascade reactors.
[B01J 19/1825] in parallel -- The code covers methods for carrying out chemical, physical or physico-chemical operations simultaneously, i.e., performing two or more processes in parallel within a single system or apparatus. It includes parallel reactors, concurrent catalysis and separation steps, and other arrangements where reactions or treatments proceed at the same time.
[B01J 19/1831] spirally, concentrically or zigzag wound -- B01J 19/1831 covers separation operations in which the working medium is formed into a spiral, concentric or zigzag wound arrangement, such as spiral-wound membrane modules or similarly configured chromatographic beds, to improve mass transfer, packing density and flow characteristics while maintaining effective chemical or physical separation.
[B01J 19/1837] Loop-type reactors -- B01J 19/1837 designates loop-type reactors, where the reaction mixture circulates continuously in a closed loop, allowing precise control of temperature and residence time and facilitating efficient heat and mass transfer. Such reactors are employed in catalytic, polymerisation or other chemico-physical processes and fit within the broader category of separating operations in chemical processing.
[B01J 19/1843] Concentric tube -- A concentric-tube arrangement consists of an inner tube placed inside an outer tube, creating separate flow channels that allow fluids to move in opposite or the same direction. It is employed in chemical and physical processes for separating, reacting, or exchanging heat between streams while keeping the equipment compact.
[B01J 19/185] of the pulsating type -- B01J 19/185 classifies processes of the pulsating type used in chemical, physical or physico-chemical operations, especially those involving separation. It covers methods that generate periodic pressure or flow variations-such as pulsating reactors or pulsation-enhanced filtration-to improve reaction rates, mixing or phase separation.
[B01J 19/1856] placed in parallel -- B01J 19/1856 covers separation methods that are arranged to operate in parallel, allowing multiple streams to be processed simultaneously. This parallel configuration can increase throughput, improve efficiency, or enable continuous operation in chemical, physical or physico-chemical separation processes. It is a sub-category of broader performing-operations and separating techniques within the CPC system.
[B01J 19/1862] placed in series -- B01J 19/1862 covers separation methods in which the material to be treated is arranged in a series of successive stages, each stage performing a chemical or physical operation such as catalysis, adsorption, or filtration. The series configuration allows progressive removal of components or conversion of the feed as it passes from one unit to the next. This subclass falls under Performing Operations → Separating → Chemical or Physical Processes.
[B01J 19/1868] resulting in a loop-type movement -- This CPC subgroup concerns chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes that generate a loop-type movement of material, such as circulating flows in mixers, reactors or separators where the medium repeatedly follows a closed-path trajectory. It falls within the broader category of separating operations in the performing-operations hierarchy.
[B01J 19/1875] internally, i.e. the mixture circulating inside the vessel such that the upwards stream is separated physically from the downwards stream(s) -- The code B01J 19/1875 pertains to internal separation within a vessel where the circulating mixture is split so that an upward stream is physically isolated from one or more downward streams. This arrangement is used in reactors, columns or similar equipment to achieve continuous, on-line separation of phases or components without external interfaces. It falls under performing operations → separating → chemical or physical processes.
[B01J 19/1881] externally, i.e. the mixture leaving the vessel and subsequently re-entering it -- B01J 19/1881 relates to processes in which a reaction mixture or component is taken out of a vessel, treated or processed outside the vessel, and then re-introduced into the same vessel, a method often used for recycle, purification, or external catalysis within chemical or physico-chemical operations.
[B01J 19/1887] forming a thin film -- B01J 19/1887 covers processes for forming a thin film on a substrate, encompassing techniques such as vapor-phase deposition, spin-coating, dip-coating, or Langmuir-Blodgett methods that create continuous layers of material for coatings, electronics, optics or surface-treatment applications.
[B01J 19/1893] Membrane reactors  catalytic membranes  B01J35/59 ; membranes  B01D71/00 -- B01J 19/1893 covers membrane reactors that integrate catalytic membranes to combine chemical reaction and separation in a single unit. The class includes designs, operating conditions and membrane materials that enable simultaneous reaction and selective transport of reactants or products.
[B01J 19/20] in the form of helices, e.g. screw reactors -- B01J 19/20 covers chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes performed in helically shaped equipment, notably screw reactors. The classification includes designs where reactants travel along a continuous helical path to improve mixing, heat transfer or reaction kinetics.
[B01J 19/22] in the form of endless belts -- B01J 19/22 covers chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes that employ endless belts as reactors, conveyors or support surfaces, allowing continuous treatment, mixing, reaction or separation of substances while they move along the belt. The classification is used for belt-based processing equipment in industrial chemistry.
[B01J 19/24] Stationary reactors without moving elements inside -- Stationary reactors without moving elements inside are vessels used for chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes where the reaction medium is kept in place and no internal components such as stirrers, agitators or moving beds are employed. Typical designs include fixed-bed, packed-column or slurry reactors that rely on external flow or diffusion to achieve mixing and heat transfer, allowing continuous operation with a simple, low-maintenance interior.
[B01J 19/2405] provoking a turbulent flow of the reactants, such as in cyclones, or having a high Reynolds-number -- B01J 19/2405 covers methods of inducing turbulent flow of reactants, such as cyclonic mixers or other high-Reynolds-number conditions, to improve mixing and reaction rates in chemical or physical processes. It is a sub-category of separating operations within the broader performing operations class.
[B01J 19/241] of the pulsating type -- The CPC subclass B01J 19/241 concerns separation methods that employ a pulsating type of motion or action, typically applied in chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes such as catalysis or colloid chemistry. It falls under performing operations → separating → chemical or physical processes, and addresses apparatuses or procedures that use periodic pressure or flow pulses to achieve the desired separation.
[B01J 19/2415] Tubular reactors -- B01J 19/2415 covers tubular reactors, which are long‐oriented vessels used to carry out chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes such as catalysis, heat exchange or mixing while the reactants flow continuously. The classification groups these devices under separating operations within the broader category of chemical or physical processes.
[B01J 19/242] in series -- B01J 19/242 covers arrangements where separation or reaction steps are carried out consecutively in a series, such as cascade reactors, sequential filters or staged extraction units. The focus is on linking multiple chemical or physical operations so that the output of one becomes the input of the next, enabling continuous processing or staged purification.
[B01J 19/2425] in parallel -- B01J 19/2425 covers chemical or physico-chemical processes in which two or more reactions are carried out simultaneously in the same unit or apparatus, i.e., in parallel. The classification is used for methods that split or separate streams while performing parallel operations, often to improve efficiency or selectivity in catalysis, colloid chemistry or related separation processes.
[B01J 19/243] spirally, concentrically or zigzag wound -- B01J 19/243 covers methods for winding or coiling material in spiral, concentric or zig-zag patterns, such as forming rolls, coils, or layered structures of sheets, films, fibers, or electrodes. The classification applies to processes that shape or separate substances by arranging them in these tightly controlled geometric configurations.
[B01J 19/2435] Loop-type reactors -- Loop-type reactors are vessels in which the reacting medium is continuously circulated through a closed-loop conduit, often incorporating internal mixers or baffles to enhance heat and mass transfer. The loop geometry provides uniform residence-time distribution and facilitates efficient temperature control, making them suitable for catalytic, polymerization, or colloidal processes.
[B01J 19/244] Concentric tubes -- B01J 19/244 covers the use of concentric tubes in chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes, such as reactors, mixers, heat exchangers or separation devices where an inner tube is placed within an outer tube to facilitate reaction, mass transfer or temperature control. The classification applies to designs, arrangements and operating methods that exploit the annular space between the tubes.
[B01J 19/2445] placed in parallel -- The code covers arrangements where two or more reactors, columns or similar units are operated side-by-side in parallel to perform the same chemical or physical operation. Parallel placement may be used to increase production capacity, improve temperature control or provide redundancy in catalytic or colloidal processes.
[B01J 19/245] placed in series -- B01J 19/245 relates to processes or apparatus in which separation stages are arranged in series, such as a sequence of packed columns or membranes, enabling continuous treatment of a material stream. It is classified under chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes used for separating operations.
[B01J 19/2455] provoking a loop type movement of the reactants  tubular loop-type reactors  B01J19/2435 ; loop reactors having moving elements inside  B01J19/1868 -- The subclass B01J 19/2455 covers methods and apparatus that deliberately create a circulating, loop-type flow of reactants within tubular loop reactors, often using moving internal elements to improve mixing, mass transfer or reaction efficiency. It belongs to the broader category of chemical or physico-chemical processes for separating operations.
[B01J 19/246] internally, i.e. the mixture circulating inside the vessel such that the upward stream is separated physically from the downward stream(s) -- B01J 19/246 covers devices or processes in which the mixture circulates internally within a vessel and the upward moving stream is physically separated from one or more downward streams, enabling internal counter-current flow without external separators.
[B01J 19/2465] externally, i.e. the mixture leaving the vessel and subsequently re-entering it -- This subclass concerns processes in which a reaction mixture is taken out of its vessel, subjected to an external operation such as separation, treatment, or catalysis, and then fed back into the same vessel for further processing. It applies to any chemical, physical or physico-chemical treatment performed outside the primary reactor.
[B01J 19/247] Suited for forming thin films -- B01J 19/247 classifies methods, apparatus or materials that are specifically designed for forming thin films, including deposition techniques that rely on chemical or physical reactions, catalysis or colloidal processes. It falls under the broader categories of performing operations, separating and chemical or physical processes.
[B01J 19/2475] Membrane reactors -- B01J 19/2475 covers membrane reactors, devices that combine a chemical reaction vessel with a selective membrane to separate reactants, products, or catalysts during the reaction. By integrating separation and reaction steps, these reactors improve conversion efficiency, enhance selectivity, enable continuous operation, and can operate under catalytic, colloidal or other physico-chemical conditions.
[B01J 19/248] Reactors comprising multiple separated flow channels -- This subclass covers reactors that are built with two or more separate flow channels, allowing distinct streams to be processed in parallel or sequentially within a single vessel. Such designs are used to improve heat transfer, reaction control, product separation or catalyst handling in chemical and physico-chemical processes.
[B01J 19/2485] Monolithic reactors -- Monolithic reactors are single-piece, often porous, structures that serve as both catalyst support and reaction chamber, allowing fluids to flow through a continuous solid matrix. Their design improves mass and heat transfer, reduces pressure drop, and enables efficient catalytic or non-catalytic processes in chemical, physical or physico-chemical operations. This entry covers all aspects of such reactors within the broader category of separating processes.
[B01J 19/249] Plate-type reactors -- B01J 19/249 covers plate-type reactors used for performing separating operations that involve chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes such as catalysis or colloid chemistry. These reactors consist of a series of flat plates that provide large surface area for reaction and mass transfer while allowing efficient separation of phases.
[B01J 19/2495] Net-type reactors -- Net-type reactors are chemical reactors that contain a net or mesh structure through which the reaction medium flows, providing a large surface area for contact with catalysts or solid phases while allowing easy separation of solids. They are employed in catalytic, slurry or colloidal processes where efficient mass transfer and easy handling of catalyst particles are needed.
[B01J 19/26] Nozzle-type reactors, i.e. the distribution of the initial reactants within the reactor is effected by their introduction or injection through nozzles -- B01J 19/26 refers to nozzle-type reactors in which the initial reactants are introduced or injected through one or more nozzles to achieve distribution within the reactor. This subclass falls under separating processes and chemical or physical processes such as catalysis, describing equipment that uses nozzle injection to control mixing, residence time or reaction zones.
[B01J 19/28] Moving reactors, e.g. rotary drums -- B01J 19/28 covers moving reactors such as rotary drums used in chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes, including operations where the reactor itself is set in motion to improve mixing, heat transfer, or reaction kinetics. These reactors are part of the broader category of separating processes within performing operations.
[B01J 19/285] Shaking or vibrating reactors; reactions under the influence of low-frequency vibrations or pulsations  for sonic and ultrasonic vibrations  B01J19/10 -- B01J 19/285 concerns reactors that are mechanically agitated by shaking or vibrating, including processes that use low-frequency pulsations, sonic or ultrasonic vibrations to influence chemical or physico-chemical reactions. The classification falls under performing operations and separating, within the broader group of chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes.
[B01J 19/30] Loose or shaped packing elements, e.g. Raschig rings or Berl saddles, for pouring into the apparatus for mass or heat transfer -- B01J 19/30 covers loose or shaped packing elements such as Raschig rings, Berl saddles and similar devices that are poured into columns or reactors to provide surface area for mass-transfer or heat-transfer operations in chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes.
[B01J 19/305] Supporting elements therefor, e.g. grids, perforated plates -- B01J 19/305 covers supporting structures used in chemical or physical processing equipment, such as grids, perforated plates and similar elements that hold or guide reaction media during catalytic, colloidal or other separations.
[B01J 19/32] Packing elements in the form of grids or built-up elements for forming a unit or module inside the apparatus for mass or heat transfer -- B01J 19/32 covers packing elements shaped as grids or built-up structures that are assembled into a unit or module inside apparatus used for mass or heat transfer, such as reactors, columns or exchangers. The design provides a defined flow path and increased surface area to improve phase contact in separating or catalytic processes.
[B01J 19/325] Attachment devices therefor, e.g. hooks, consoles, brackets -- The CPC subclass B01J 19/325 covers attachment devices used in separation processes, such as hooks, consoles or brackets that hold or position equipment. These devices are employed in chemical, physical or physico-chemical operations where separation is performed, for example in catalysis or colloid chemistry.
[B01J 2/00] Processes or devices for granulating materials in general -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Processes or devices for granulation/agglomeration, which are applicable to (more than one) different fields of application, such as food, cosmetics, pharmaceuticals, fertilisers or for which no specific application is indicated.
[B01J 20/00] Solid sorbent compositions or filter aid compositions -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Solid inorganic or organic materials and compositions useful as adsorbent, as absorbent, as filter aid, as packing or stationary phase for preparative, analytical or investigative chromatography.  Solid sorbent materials or compositions, filter aid materials or compositions, stationary phases for chromatography, in general, characterised by their form or physical properties.  Solid sorbent materials or compositions, filter aid materials or compositions and stationary phases comprising a carrier.  Processes, in general, for preparing solid sorbents, filter aids and stationary phases for chromatography.  Regenerating or reactivating such materials or compositions, in general.  In this group "solid sorbent or filter aid" includes also materials or compositions in semi-solid, paste or gel state.
[B01J 20/02] comprising inorganic material -- B01J 20/02 relates to solid sorbent or filter-aid compositions that contain an inorganic material, used in separating operations such as catalysis, adsorption or colloid chemistry. The subclass defines mixtures where the inorganic component provides the primary sorbing or filtering function.

[B01J 20/0248] Compounds of B, Al, Ga, In, Tl  B01J20/08  takes precedence -- This classification entry pertains to solid sorbent or filter aid compositions specifically involving compounds of boron, aluminum, gallium, indium, and thallium. It is part of a broader hierarchy that deals with chemical or physical processes such as catalysis or colloid chemistry under the Cooperative Patent Classification system used by the European Patent Office and the United States Patent and Trademark Office.

[B01J 20/283] based on silica -- B01J 20/283 covers solid sorbent or filter-aid compositions that use silica as the active material. [B01J 20/284] based on alumina -- B01J 20/284 relates to solid sorbent or filter-aid compositions whose active component is alumina, employed in separation operations such as adsorption, catalysis or filtration within chemical or physical processes.
[B01J 20/285] based on polymers -- The subclass B01J 20/285 specifies solid sorbent or filter-aid compositions whose active component is a polymer or polymer-based material, such as polymeric beads, membranes or resin carriers used for adsorption or filtration in separating operations. It falls under the broader categories of separating, chemical or physical processes, and solid sorbent compositions.
[B01J 20/286] Phases chemically bonded to a substrate, e.g. to silica or to polymers -- B01J 20/286 relates to solid sorbent or filter-aid compositions where a functional phase is chemically bonded to a solid substrate such as silica or polymer particles. It includes preparation of these bonded phases and their use in separating operations that exploit catalytic or colloidal interactions.
[B01J 20/287] Non-polar phases -- B01J 20/287 covers solid sorbent or filter-aid compositions that employ non-polar phases for separation operations, such as hydrophobic polymers, silica treated with non-polar functional groups, or other inert, oil-affine materials. These compositions are used in catalytic, colloidal or other chemical and physical processes where a non-polar medium selectively adsorbs or filters target substances.
[B01J 20/288] Polar phases -- B01J 20/288 covers solid sorbent or filter-aid compositions that contain polar phases, such as materials with hydrophilic or ion-exchange sites. These sorbents are used in separation operations where chemical or physical processes-catalysis, colloid chemistry, or related actions-benefit from the selective adsorption of polar molecules or ions.
[B01J 20/289] bonded via a spacer -- The code B01J 20/289 refers to solid sorbent or filter-aid compositions in which the active particles are bonded together by a spacer material that keeps them separated and maintains porosity, improving flow and access during chemical or physical separation processes such as catalysis or colloid treatment.
[B01J 20/29] Chiral phases -- B01J 20/29 covers solid sorbent or filter-aid compositions that contain chiral phases, i.e., materials possessing handedness that can selectively interact with enantiomers during separation processes. These chiral phases are employed in catalytic, chromatographic or other chemical/physical operations to achieve enantio-selective adsorption or reaction.
[B01J 2029/062] Mixtures of different aluminosilicates -- The code B01J 2029/062 covers processes that prepare, treat or use mixtures containing two or more distinct aluminosilicate materials, such as different zeolites, clays or silicate frameworks. It includes methods for combining these solids to obtain synergistic catalytic, adsorptive or ion-exchange properties, and the resulting composite products.
[B01J 2029/081] Increasing the silica/alumina ratio; Desalumination -- B01J 2029/081 covers processes that raise the silica-to-alumina ratio of aluminosilicate materials, commonly referred to as desalumination. The methods involve removal of aluminum from the framework or surface, typically by chemical leaching, ion exchange or hydrothermal treatment, to produce high-silica zeolites or related products.
[B01J 20/291] Gel sorbents -- Gel sorbents are solid sorbent or filter-aid compositions formed as three-dimensional polymeric networks that absorb liquids or gases while retaining a fixed shape. They are employed in chemical or physical separation processes, such as catalysis and colloid chemistry, to capture, immobilize or release target substances.
[B01J 20/292] Liquid sorbents -- B01J 20/292 covers liquid sorbent materials used to trap or remove substances in separation operations, including formulations of liquid-phase sorbents and filter aids. The subclass falls under separating processes and chemical or physical processes such as catalysis or colloid chemistry, specifying compositions that function as liquid sorbents rather than solid ones.
[B01J 20/30] Processes for preparing, regenerating, or reactivating -- B01J 20/30 concerns methods for preparing, regenerating or reactivating solid sorbent or filter-aid compositions employed in separation operations, such as catalysts, ion-exchangers, adsorbents or filtration aids. It includes any chemical or physical steps that restore the material's activity or performance for reuse.
[B01J 20/3007] Moulding, shaping or extruding -- The B01J 20/3007 subclass covers the moulding, shaping or extruding of solid sorbent or filter-aid compositions, such as granular or porous materials used for separation. It pertains to chemical or physical processes that create these solids in defined forms for catalytic, adsorptive or filtration applications.
[B01J 20/3014] Kneading -- Kneading refers to the mechanical mixing or working of solid sorbent or filter-aid compositions to improve their homogeneity, particle size distribution, or binding properties, typically employed in separation processes where the treated material functions as a catalyst, adsorbent, or filtration aid.
[B01J 20/3021] Milling, crushing or grinding -- B01J 20/3021 covers the milling, crushing or grinding of solid sorbent or filter-aid compositions, detailing methods and apparatus for reducing particle size to enhance separation performance. It falls under Performing Operations → Separating → Chemical or Physical Processes, such as catalysis or colloid chemistry, and applies to both laboratory and industrial scale operations.
[B01J 20/3028] Granulating, agglomerating or aggregating -- B01J 20/3028 covers processes for granulating, agglomerating or aggregating solid sorbent or filter-aid materials, typically to improve handling, flow or separation performance. It falls under the CPC section for performing operations, separating, and chemical or physical processes such as catalysis and colloid chemistry, and describes methods that create larger, porous particles from fine powders.
[B01J 20/3035] Compressing -- This group covers the compression step in the preparation of solid sorbent or filter aid compositions, where a material is subjected to mechanical pressure to increase its density or shape before further use. The operation is classified under separating processes that involve chemical or physical actions such as catalysis or colloid chemistry.
[B01J 20/3042] Use of binding agents; addition of materials ameliorating the mechanical properties of the produced sorbent -- The CPC subclass B01J 20/3042 covers the use of binding agents and the addition of additives that improve the mechanical strength of solid sorbent or filter-aid compositions, typically in processes where sorbents are produced for separation or catalysis. It falls under performing operations, separating, chemical or physical processes such as catalysis or colloid chemistry.
[B01J 20/305] Addition of material, later completely removed, e.g. as result of heat treatment, leaching or washing, e.g. for forming pores -- The entry B01J 20/305 covers processes in which a material is added to a solid sorbent or filter-aid composition and then completely removed-by heat treatment, leaching, washing or similar means-to create pores. The temporary additive serves only to generate porosity, enhancing the final product's adsorption or filtration performance. This technique is used in chemical or physical separation operations where a porous solid is required.
[B01J 20/3057] Use of a templating or imprinting material  molecularly imprinted polymers  B01J20/268 ; filling pores of a substrate or matrix followed by the removal of the substrate or matrix -- B01J 20/3057 covers the preparation of solid sorbent or filter aid materials by filling the pores of a solid support with a templating or imprinting substance, such as molecularly imprinted polymers, and then removing the template to create selective binding sites. The process is used to produce sorbents that can separate target molecules based on shape or chemical affinity. It falls under chemical or physical processes for separating substances.
[B01J 20/3064] Addition of pore forming agents, e.g. pore inducing or porogenic agents -- The code B01J 20/3064 covers solid sorbent or filter-aid compositions that incorporate pore-forming or porogenic agents to create a defined porous structure. The addition of such agents is intended to improve the material's capacity for adsorption, filtration, or catalytic transport during chemical or physical separation processes.
[B01J 20/3071] Washing or leaching -- B01J 20/3071 covers washing or leaching operations that employ solid sorbent or filter-aid compositions to remove contaminants, extract substances, or clean solid materials. It falls under separating processes that involve chemical or physical actions such as catalysis or colloid chemistry.
[B01J 20/3078] Thermal treatment, e.g. calcining or pyrolizing -- B01J 20/3078 covers thermal treatment of solid sorbent or filter-aid compositions, such as calcining or pyrolyzing, to alter their physical or chemical characteristics. The entry lies within the CPC hierarchy for separating operations under chemical or physical processes, especially catalysis and colloid chemistry.

Techniques may involve applying liquids, polymers, metals, or other active agents onto the sorbent surface before or after its formation, and are used in separation processes, catalysis, or filtration. This subclass falls under performing operations, separating, and chemical or physical processes within the CPC system.

[B01J 2/04] in a gaseous medium -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - If combined with suspending the material in a gas, e.g. fluidised beds -> B01J2/16
[B01J 2/06] in a liquid medium -- B01J 2/06 covers granulation processes carried out in a liquid medium, where solid particles are agglomerated using a fluid phase as a binder or carrier. The classification includes methods and apparatus for wet granulation of powders, slurry preparation, and subsequent solidification of the formed granules.
[B01J 2/08] Gelation of a colloidal solution -- The CPC subclass B01J 2/08 covers processes for forming a gel from a colloidal solution, a method used to aggregate dispersed particles and convert the suspension into a solid or semi-solid mass. It falls under the broader categories of performing operations, separating, and chemical or physical processes such as catalysis and colloid chemistry, and is applied to the granulation of materials in general.
[B01J 2/10] in stationary drums or troughs, provided with kneading or mixing appliances -- This subclass covers granulation processes performed in stationary drums or troughs that are equipped with kneading or mixing appliances, focusing on chemical or physical separation operations where the material is agitated inside a fixed vessel.
