[B01J 2219/00459] Beads -- B01J 2219/00459 covers the use of beads as a solid support or media in separation processes such as chromatography, filtration, or solid-phase extraction, where the beads provide a surface for adsorption, catalysis, or size-based separation of particles or molecules. It includes bead materials, modifications and configurations used to achieve selective separation.
[B01J 2219/00461] Beads and reaction vessel together -- This CPC subclass covers arrangements in which the beads are incorporated directly into the reaction vessel, allowing the beads and the container to function as a single unit for chemical or physical processes such as catalysis, adsorption or colloidal reactions.
[B01J 2219/00463] Directed sorting -- The B01J 2219/00463 class "Directed sorting" covers techniques for separating substances by actively guiding particles to specific locations, typically using external forces such as magnetic, electric, acoustic, or flow fields. It includes processes that manipulate the motion or orientation of molecules, colloids, or larger particles to achieve selective collection, concentration or removal in chemical, physical or physico-chemical operations.
[B01J 2219/00466] in a slurry -- B01J 2219/00466 covers processes where a chemical or physical operation, such as a reaction, is carried out in a slurry - a mixture of solid particles dispersed in a liquid medium. This sub-category falls under the broader group of separating operations and chemical or physical processes, indicating that the slurry serves as the reaction medium or transport phase.
[B01J 2219/00468] by manipulation of individual beads -- Entry B01J 2219/00468 covers methods that achieve separation or other chemical/physical transformations by handling individual beads, such as bead-based chromatography, bead reactors, or bead manipulation in catalytic or colloidal processes. The classification applies to processes where discrete solid particles are individually moved, positioned or processed to effect the desired reaction or separation.
[B01J 2219/0047] Pins -- Pins are small solid members used in chemical, physical or physico-chemical operations to support, separate or retain substances, apparatus components or catalysts, thereby aiding processes such as catalysis, colloid handling and other separations.
[B01J 2219/00472] Replaceable crowns -- Replaceable crowns are detachable elements used in separation or purification equipment, such as chromatography or crystallization apparatus, that can be removed and exchanged to maintain performance and simplify cleaning. The CPC entry B01J 2219/00472 covers devices employing such crowns to improve the efficiency of chemical or physical separation processes.
[B01J 2219/00475] Sheets -- The CPC subclass B01J 2219/00475, titled "Sheets", pertains to chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes used to produce, modify, or handle sheet-like materials such as membranes, films, or laminated layers. It falls under the hierarchy of Performing Operations → Separating → Chemical or Physical Processes.
[B01J 2219/00477] Means for pressurising the reaction vessels -- B01J 2219/00477 covers devices or methods used to increase the pressure inside reaction vessels during chemical or physical processes. It falls under the separating subgroup of the performing operations class, relating to general catalytic or colloid chemistry operations.
[B01J 2219/00479] Means for mixing reactants or products in the reaction vessels -- This subclass covers devices or mechanisms used to stir, agitate, or otherwise mix chemicals within reactors, ensuring uniform reaction conditions and product homogeneity. It includes mixers, impellers, baffles and similar means for mixing reactants or products in reaction vessels.
[B01J 2219/00481] by the use of moving stirrers within the reaction vessels -- This CPC group covers chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes that employ moving stirrers inside reaction vessels. It includes methods where the stirring action is integral to the operation, such as mixing, heat or mass transfer enhancement during reactions. The classification falls under the broader categories of performing operations, separating and catalytic or colloid chemistry.
[B01J 2219/00484] by shaking, vibrating or oscillating of the reaction vessels -- B01J 2219/00484 refers to chemical or physico-chemical operations in which the reaction vessel is agitated by shaking, vibrating or oscillating to promote mixing, mass transfer or separation. Such mechanical agitation is employed in laboratory, pilot-scale or industrial reactors to enhance reaction rates, improve yields or facilitate phase separation. The code falls under the broader categories of performing operations, separating and catalytic or colloid chemistry.
[B01J 2219/00486] by sonication or ultrasonication -- B01J 2219/00486 covers separation methods that use sonication or ultrasonication, employing high-frequency sound waves to agitate, break down or extract substances as part of chemical or physical processing.
[B01J 2219/00488] by rotation of the reaction vessels -- The subclass B01J 2219/00488 covers processes that employ rotation of the reaction vessels to improve mixing, heat transfer or phase separation during chemical, physical or physico-chemical operations. It falls under Performing Operations → Separating → Chemical or Physical Processes, and includes any technique where the vessel itself is turned to aid the reaction.
[B01J 2219/0049] by centrifugation -- B01J 2219/0049 covers the use of centrifugation as a separation technique in chemical or physical processes. It falls under performing operations, separating, and chemical or physical processes such as catalysis or colloid chemistry. The code is applied to inventions that employ centrifugal forces to divide solids, liquids, or phases.
[B01J 2219/00493] by sparging or bubbling with gases -- The CPC subclass B01J 2219/00493 covers separation operations where a gas is introduced into a liquid or solid-liquid mixture, i.e., sparging or bubbling, to promote mass transfer, degassing, or removal of dissolved or suspended components.
[B01J 2219/00495] Means for heating or cooling the reaction vessels -- The CPC subclass B01J 2219/00495 covers devices and methods for heating or cooling reaction vessels used in chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes, especially within separating operations. It is located under the hierarchy Performing Operations, Separating, Chemical or Physical Processes.
[B01J 2219/00497] Features relating to the solid phase supports -- This subclass concerns the characteristics of solid-phase supports employed in separating operations such as catalysis, adsorption, immobilisation or other chemical and physical processes, including the material composition, shape, surface treatment and methods of preparing or attaching the solid support. It groups all features that define how the solid matrix participates in the overall process.
[B01J 2219/005] Beads -- The subclass B01J 2219/005 covers beads employed as solid particles in separation processes, serving as carriers, adsorbents or packing material in operations like chromatography, filtration, ion exchange and solvent extraction. It includes beads of various composition and size designed to facilitate chemical or physical separation of components.
[B01J 2219/00502] Particles of irregular geometry -- A subclass for particles that have non-regular, uneven or non-uniform shapes, such as irregular, non-spherical or angular forms. It applies to chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes where such irregularly shaped particles are produced, handled or used, especially in separation operations.
[B01J 2219/00504] Pins -- The B01J 2219/00504 subclass covers the use of pins as structural or functional elements in separation processes that involve chemical, physical or physico-chemical actions, such as supporting catalysts, fixing media, or providing spacing within columns or reactors. Pins may be metal, ceramic or polymer and are employed to enhance flow distribution, prevent channeling, or hold other components in place.
[B01J 2219/00506] with removable crowns -- Subclass B01J 2219/00506 covers separation techniques that utilize removable crown compounds, such as crown ethers, to selectively bind and extract specific ions or molecules from a mixture. The method typically involves adding the crown reagent, forming a complex, and then removing the crown phase to recover the purified component.
[B01J 2219/00509] Microcolumns -- Microcolumns are tiny packed or open-tubular columns used in chromatographic and other separation techniques, allowing rapid analysis of very small sample volumes with high efficiency. They are applied in chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes such as catalysis, colloid chemistry and micro-reactor systems.
[B01J 2219/00511] Walls of reactor vessels -- The B01J 2219/00511 entry concerns the walls of reactor vessels employed in chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes, detailing their construction, material selection and protective features that enable safe operation and assist in separation tasks within reactors. This falls under performing operations and separating in the broader hierarchy.
[B01J 2219/00513] Essentially linear supports -- Essentially linear supports are rod-like or wire-like structures that provide a continuous, narrowly shaped substrate for separation media such as gels, membranes or sorbents. They are used in processes like chromatography, electrophoresis or filtration where a linear geometry enhances flow control and particle or molecule migration.
[B01J 2219/00515] in the shape of strings -- A process for separating or treating substances where the material is arranged or formed as string-like structures, such as filaments, fibers or threads, is covered by this entry. It falls under performing operations and separating in chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes, including catalytic or colloidal methods. This classification is used for inventions that exploit the string shape to achieve the desired separation or reaction.
[B01J 2219/00518] in the shape of tapes -- B01J 2219/00518 pertains to separation methods that use materials shaped as tapes-such as tape-form adsorbents, membranes or filter elements-to isolate substances in chemical or physical processes. The tape geometry is a defining feature of the separation technique.
[B01J 2219/0052] in the shape of elongated tubes -- The CPC subclass B01J 2219/0052 covers chemical or physical processing steps that employ elongated tubular forms, such as pipes or tubes, to facilitate separation operations. It includes methods where the tube geometry is integral to the reaction, catalysis, or colloid handling, and the tube may act as reactor or conduit. This subclass falls under broader categories of performing operations and separating processes.
[B01J 2219/00522] in a multiple parallel arrangement -- B01J 2219/00522 covers chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes that are carried out in a multiple parallel arrangement, meaning several reactors or separation units operate side by side to increase capacity or efficiency. The code applies when parallelization of the process steps is a defining feature.
[B01J 2219/00524] in the shape of fiber bundles -- This CPC subclass B01J 2219/00524 covers chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes that specifically involve materials arranged as fiber bundles. It falls under the hierarchy of Performing Operations → Separating → Chemical or Physical Processes, such as catalysis or colloid chemistry, where the shape of the material is a bundle of fibers.
[B01J 2219/00527] Sheets -- B01J 2219/00527 covers processes for producing or treating sheets of material, including thin films and plate-like products, using chemical, physical or physico-chemical methods such as catalysis, colloidal techniques, or other separating operations. It is classified under performing operations, separating, and chemical or physical processes.
[B01J 2219/00529] DNA chips -- B01J 2219/00529 concerns DNA chips, also known as DNA microarrays, which are solid supports bearing immobilized nucleic acid sequences for parallel hybridization and detection of target DNA or RNA. The classification places these devices within chemical and physical processes used for separating biomolecules in analytical and diagnostic applications.
[B01J 2219/00531] essentially square -- B01J 2219/00531 falls under Performing Operations → Separating → Chemical or Physical Processes and covers separation techniques that employ an essentially square-shaped component, such as plates, membranes or channels, to effect chemical, physical or physico-chemical separation. The classification is used for devices or methods where the square geometry is a defining feature of the process.
[B01J 2219/00533] essentially rectangular -- B01J 2219/00533 designates chemical or physical separation processes that employ an essentially rectangular configuration of the reaction or separation zone. It includes methods and apparatus where the geometry of the separator, such as a rectangular conduit, tray, or chamber, is a defining feature for the operation, typically in catalytic or colloid systems.
[B01J 2219/00536] in the shape of disks -- B01J 2219/00536 covers processes that separate substances by employing disc-shaped elements, situated within the CPC hierarchy of Performing Operations, Separating, and Chemical or Physical Processes such as catalysis or colloid chemistry.
[B01J 2219/00538] in the shape of cylinders -- This CPC subgroup B01J 2219/00538 covers chemical, physical or physico-chemical separation processes that use equipment shaped as cylinders, such as columns, tubes or reactors, where the cylindrical geometry is integral to the operation. It falls under the broader categories of performing operations, separating, and chemical or physical processes including catalysis or colloid chemistry.
[B01J 2219/0054] Means for coding or tagging the apparatus or the reagents -- The B01J 2219/0054 entry concerns devices or methods that attach a code, tag or label to equipment, containers or reagents used in chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes, enabling identification, tracking or data collection throughout the operation. It falls under the broader categories of performing operations, separating and chemical or physical processes.
[B01J 2219/00542] Alphanumeric characters -- This CPC subclass (B01J 2219/00542) covers chemical, physical or physico-chemical operations in which alphanumeric characters are used for identification, labeling, coding or tracking of substances, products or process steps during performing or separating operations. It includes methods and devices that apply such characters to assist control, monitoring or documentation of the process.
[B01J 2219/00545] Colours -- B01J 2219/00545 is a CPC subclass under PERFORMING OPERATIONS → SEPARATING → CHEMICAL OR PHYSICAL PROCESSES, covering chemical, physical or physico-chemical methods for creating, modifying or applying colours, including dye synthesis, pigment preparation and related coloration processes.
[B01J 2219/00547] Bar codes -- Bar codes are applied to label samples, reagents, or equipment in chemical and physical separation processes, providing a quick way to identify and track material handling, process parameters or analytical results during operations such as chromatography, filtration or catalytic treatment.
[B01J 2219/00549] 2-dimensional -- The B01J 2219/00549 entry covers processes that generate or manipulate two-dimensional structures-such as thin films, layers, membranes or surface-bound arrays-used in catalytic, colloidal or other physico-chemical operations. It includes methods for forming, patterning, or treating these planar materials to achieve desired chemical or physical properties in separation or reaction contexts.
[B01J 2219/00551] 3-dimensional -- The subclass B01J 2219/00551 deals with chemical or physical techniques for producing or handling three-dimensional objects, including processes such as 3-D printing, extrusion, molding or other methods that shape material in three dimensions while involving separating operations.
[B01J 2219/00554] Physical means -- B01J 2219/00554 - Physical means refers to classification of processes that separate components by purely physical actions rather than chemical reactions. It includes methods such as filtration, sedimentation, centrifugation, magnetic or electric field separation, and mechanical sorting. This subclass groups devices and procedures that rely on physical forces to achieve separation.
[B01J 2219/00556] Perforations -- The CPC subclass B01J 2219/00556 covers methods and apparatus for creating perforations in materials used for separation, such as filters, membranes or reactor components. It includes mechanical, chemical or physico-chemical techniques that form holes to enhance fluid flow, mass transfer or the overall efficiency of the separating process.
[B01J 2219/00558] Cuts-out -- The CPC subclass B01J 2219/00558 "Cuts-out" covers operations in which material is removed from a solid workpiece by cutting, punching, shearing or similar mechanical separation. It is placed under chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes that involve separating a portion of a substrate, often as part of a larger manufacturing or processing sequence.
[B01J 2219/0056] Raised or sunken areas -- The B01J 2219/0056 entry covers methods for producing raised or sunken features on a material's surface, such as embossing, etching, plating, or other chemical-physical treatments that create topographical relief for functional or decorative purposes within the broader category of separating operations.
[B01J 2219/00563] Magnetic means -- Magnetic means refer to the use of magnetic fields or magnetic materials to effect separation or manipulation of particles in chemical or physical processes. This includes magnets, magnetic drums, or electromagnetic coils that attract, retain, or move ferromagnetic or paramagnetic substances during processing.
[B01J 2219/00565] Electromagnetic means -- The entry B01J 2219/00565 covers the use of electromagnetic devices or techniques in separating chemical or physical mixtures, such as magnetic, eddy-current, or electrostatic separators, as well as electromagnetic stirring or heating applied to catalytic or colloidal processes. It falls under the broader categories of performing operations, separating, and chemical or physical processes.
[B01J 2219/00567] Transponder chips -- A transponder chip designed for use in chemical or physical separation operations, providing electronic identification, data logging or wireless communication to monitor and control the process. It belongs to the CPC class B01J 2219/00567, which covers devices that facilitate separation by transmitting information about the material being treated.
[B01J 2219/00569] EEPROM memory devices -- The CPC entry B01J 2219/00569 classifies electrically erasable programmable read-only memory (EEPROM) devices, solid-state chips that retain stored information without power by using electrically controlled charge storage. Although positioned under the broad "performing operations → separating → chemical or physical processes" hierarchy, the code specifically covers the micro-fabricated memory structures and related manufacturing steps.
[B01J 2219/00572] Chemical means -- B01J 2219/00572 covers the use of chemical agents or reactions to achieve separation of substances, such as precipitation, complexation, ion exchange, solvent extraction or adsorption. It falls under performing operations → separating → chemical or physical processes, focusing on chemical means for material separation.
[B01J 2219/00574] radioactive -- The entry B01J 2219/00574 designates chemical or physical processes that employ radioactive substances or radiation to drive reactions or facilitate separations. It includes methods such as radiolysis, isotopic labeling, tracer-based monitoring and other radiochemical techniques used in general process chemistry.
[B01J 2219/00576] fluorophore -- The CPC subclass B01J 2219/00576 covers the use of fluorophores in chemical or physical processes for separating substances, such as fluorescence-based detection, labeling, or tracing in chromatography, electrophoresis, or other separation techniques that rely on the optical properties of the fluorophore.
[B01J 2219/00578] electrophoric -- The CPC subclass B01J 2219/00578, titled electrophoric, falls under Performing Operations - Separating - Chemical or Physical Processes and covers methods that use an electric field to move charged particles or molecules through a medium for separation or purification, such as electrophoresis and related electro-migration techniques.
[B01J 2219/00581] Mass -- This CPC entry B01J 2219/00581 titled "Mass" covers separation techniques that rely on differences in particle mass, such as centrifugation, sedimentation, or other mass-based physical processes used to isolate or purify substances. It falls under the broader categories of performing operations and separating, relating to chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes.
[B01J 2219/00583] Features relative to the processes being carried out -- B01J 2219/00583 covers aspects of the processes performed in chemical, physical or physico-chemical operations, particularly those related to separating and catalytic or colloidal actions. It addresses features such as reaction conditions, equipment configuration, and auxiliary steps that influence how the process is carried out.
[B01J 2219/00585] Parallel processes -- Parallel processes are methods where two or more chemical or physical operations are carried out simultaneously within a single unit or closely coupled units, allowing concurrent separation, reaction, or treatment steps. This approach can improve efficiency, reduce equipment size, and enable integrated processing such as parallel chromatography or simultaneous catalytic reactions. It falls under CPC B01J 2219/00585 in the Performing Operations > Separating > Chemical or Physical Processes hierarchy.
[B01J 2219/00587] High throughput processes -- B01J 2219/00587 covers high-throughput processes used to accelerate chemical or physical operations such as separations, catalysis, or colloid chemistry. These methods employ rapid, often automated, techniques to handle many samples or reactions in parallel, increasing productivity and data generation. The code is part of the CPC hierarchy for performing operations, separating, and chemical or physical processes.
[B01J 2219/0059] Sequential processes -- B01J 2219/0059 covers sequential processes in which one chemical, physical or physico-chemical operation follows another in a controlled order, often applied in catalysis, colloid chemistry and related separation methods. It is situated under performing operations, separating, and chemical or physical processes.
[B01J 2219/00592] Split-and-pool, mix-and-divide processes -- The split-and-pool or mix-and-divide process is a sequential method for separating components in a mixture by repeatedly dividing the sample, mixing the portions, and applying selective reactions or physical actions that create distinguishable subsets. Each cycle generates a combinatorial library of products that can be isolated based on differing properties, enabling efficient separation and analysis of complex chemical or colloidal systems. This technique is used in fields such as combinatorial chemistry, polymer synthesis, and materials screening.
[B01J 2219/00594] Gas-phase processes -- This CPC entry covers operations performed on substances in the gas phase, including chemical reactions, catalytic transformations, and physical separations such as adsorption or condensation. It falls under the hierarchy of performing operations, separating, and chemical or physical processes.
[B01J 2219/00596] Solid-phase processes -- B01J 2219/00596 covers solid-phase processes used in chemical, physical or physico-chemical operations, particularly within separating and performing operations. It includes methods where reactions, catalysis, or colloid interactions occur on or with solid materials rather than in solution. The classification groups techniques that exploit the properties of solids to achieve desired transformations or separations.
[B01J 2219/00599] Solution-phase processes -- B01J 2219/00599 covers solution-phase processes, i.e., chemical, physical or physico-chemical operations performed in a homogeneous liquid medium. It includes reactions, separations, catalysis or other transformations where reagents, catalysts and products are dissolved or suspended in solution. The class is used for patents describing any step carried out in solution.
[B01J 2219/00601] High-pressure processes -- The B01J 2219/00601 subclass covers processes that use elevated pressures to carry out chemical, physical or physico-chemical operations such as reactions, separations or crystallisations. It addresses the use of high-pressure conditions, equipment and steps that modify rates, equilibria or phase behaviour of the material.
[B01J 2219/00603] Making arrays on substantially continuous surfaces -- This CPC subclass covers methods for fabricating regular patterns or arrays such as electrodes, sensors or catalytic sites directly on large-area continuous substrates like films, wafers or belts. It includes techniques that deposit, etch, imprint or otherwise structure the surface to create a repetitive arrangement while the material remains essentially unbroken. The processes are chemical or physical in nature and are often used for separation or other operational purposes.
[B01J 2219/00605] the compounds being directly bound or immobilised to solid supports -- It covers methods where active compounds are covalently attached, adsorbed or otherwise immobilized onto solid carriers for use in catalysis, separation or analytical applications. It includes preparation of functionalized supports, binding techniques and characterization of the bound species.
[B01J 2219/00608] DNA chips -- The entry B01J 2219/00608 describes DNA chips, or micro-arrays, which are solid substrates bearing patterned, immobilised nucleic-acid probes that allow simultaneous hybridisation-based detection of many DNA sequences. These devices are employed in analytical, diagnostic and genomic screening applications and are classified under chemical or physico-chemical separating processes.
[B01J 2219/0061] The surface being organic -- A process in which the material forming the reacting or separating surface is organic, for example an organic-coated catalyst, membrane or support used in chemical, physical or physico-chemical operations such as catalysis, separation or colloid manipulation.
[B01J 2219/00612] the surface being inorganic -- It covers separation techniques where the operative surface is inorganic, such as inorganic membranes, adsorbents or solid particles used in filtration, sedimentation, centrifugation or related chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes.
[B01J 2219/00614] Delimitation of the attachment areas -- The code covers methods for defining the boundaries of zones where a catalyst, enzyme, adsorbent or other active material is fixed to a support or carrier in separation or reaction processes. It includes techniques for spatially restricting attachment to improve selectivity, efficiency or durability of the system.
[B01J 2219/00617] by chemical means -- B01J 2219/00617 covers separating operations that are carried out by chemical means, such as precipitation, complexation, or selective dissolution, rather than mechanical or physical techniques. It falls within the broader category of chemical or physico-chemical processes used for separation.
[B01J 2219/00619] using hydrophilic or hydrophobic regions -- The entry B01J 2219/00619 covers methods that employ distinct hydrophilic or hydrophobic regions to influence the behavior of liquids and particles during separation processes. It includes the design and use of surfaces, layers or zones with differing water affinity to promote selective adsorbtion, partitioning or transport in chemical, physical or physico-chemical operations. This classification falls under separating operations within broader performing operations.
[B01J 2219/00621] by physical means, e.g. trenches, raised areas -- The subclass B01J 2219/00621 covers separating operations that rely on physical structures rather than chemical agents, such as creating trenches, raised zones or other topographical features to direct or retain materials. It is part of the broader category for chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes used for separation.
[B01J 2219/00623] Immobilisation or binding -- B01J 2219/00623 covers methods for immobilising or binding substances such as catalysts, enzymes or reagents onto solid carriers or matrices to facilitate chemical or physical processes. It includes techniques for attaching, fixing or entrapping active material to improve handling, reuse, stability or separation.
[B01J 2219/00626] Covalent -- B01J 2219/00626 classifies processes that involve covalent bonding, covering methods where covalent interactions are created, broken, or modified in chemical or physico-chemical operations such as catalysis, synthesis, or colloidal transformations. It falls under the hierarchy of Performing Operations → Separating → Chemical or Physical Processes, focusing on covalent mechanisms within broader reaction and separation technologies.
[B01J 2219/00628] Ionic -- B01J 2219/00628 covers processes that exploit ionic interactions for separating or transforming substances, including ion exchange, electrophoresis and related physico-chemical methods, and falls under performing operations - separating - chemical or physical processes such as catalysis or colloid chemistry.
[B01J 2219/0063] Other, e.g. van der Waals forces, hydrogen bonding -- B01J 2219/0063 covers chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes that rely on non-covalent interactions such as van der Waals forces or hydrogen bonding, often used to influence separation, catalysis or other performing operations. It is a subclass of the broader "Separating - Chemical or physical processes, e.g. catalysis or colloid chemistry" group.
[B01J 2219/00632] Introduction of reactive groups to the surface -- B01J 2219/00632 covers processes for attaching reactive functional groups onto the surface of a material such as polymers, metals or particles to create sites that can further react, bind or catalyze other substances. The methods include chemical grafting, plasma activation, silanisation and similar surface-modification techniques used in coatings, sensors and catalysis.
[B01J 2219/00635] by reactive plasma treatment -- B01J 2219/00635 designates processes that separate substances by applying a reactive plasma, where ionized gas species chemically or physically modify and detach target components. The technique is used for cleaning, surface modification, or selective removal in chemical and physical processing.
[B01J 2219/00637] by coating it with another layer -- This CPC subgroup covers processes in which a material is coated with an additional layer to modify its surface, facilitate separation, or provide protection during chemical or physical treatment; the coating may be applied by deposition, plating, layering or similar techniques and is used in catalytic, colloidal or other physico-chemical operations.
[B01J 2219/00639] the compounds being trapped in or bound to a porous medium -- This subclass concerns processes in which chemical or physical compounds become trapped, adsorbed, absorbed or otherwise bound within a porous material, such as foams, membranes, zeolites or other structured media. It includes the design and use of porous matrices to retain catalysts, reactants, or products for purposes like separation, storage, controlled release or enhanced reaction efficiency.
[B01J 2219/00641] the porous medium being continuous, e.g. porous oxide substrates -- The CPC subclass B01J 2219/00641 covers processes that employ a continuous porous medium-such as porous oxide substrates-to facilitate separation, catalysis or other physicochemical operations. It relates to systems where the solid phase forms an uninterrupted network through which fluids or reactants pass, enabling reactions or filtration within the porous structure. This entry falls under Performing Operations → Separating → Chemical or Physical Processes.
[B01J 2219/00644] the porous medium being present in discrete locations, e.g. gel pads -- B01J 2219/00644 covers chemical or physical processes in which a porous medium is arranged in separate, localized zones such as gel pads, allowing reactions, catalysis or separations to occur within these discrete sites. The classification applies to methods where the porous structure is not continuous but positioned in distinct locations to facilitate the operation.
[B01J 2219/00646] the compounds being bound to beads immobilised on the solid supports -- B01J 2219/00646 covers methods in which target compounds are bound to beads that are immobilised on solid supports, enabling their handling, separation or catalytic use. The subclass falls under performing operations, separating, and chemical or physical processes and is used for bead-based immobilisation techniques in synthesis, analysis or catalytic applications.
[B01J 2219/00648] by the use of solid beads -- The subclass B01J 2219/00648 covers separation methods that employ solid beads as a supporting medium, such as packed-bed chromatography, bead-based extraction and bead-mediated filtration, typically used in catalytic or colloidal chemical and physical processes.
[B01J 2219/0065] by the use of liquid beads -- A process for separating substances that utilizes liquid beads as the moving or supporting phase, allowing the target components to be transferred onto or through the beads during the operation. The liquid beads serve as a dispersed medium that provides high surface area for mass-transfer or catalytic interactions, enabling efficient chemical or physical separation.
[B01J 2219/00653] the compounds being bound to electrodes embedded in or on the solid supports -- This CPC subgroup covers processes where chemical compounds are bound to electrodes that are embedded in or placed on solid supports, typically for catalytic, electro-chemical or separation applications. It resides under performing operations, separating, and chemical or physical processes such as catalysis or colloid chemistry.
[B01J 2219/00655] the compounds being bound to magnets embedded in or on the solid supports -- This subclass deals with methods where target compounds are immobilized on magnetic particles that are embedded in or attached to a solid support, allowing the bound substances to be removed by applying a magnetic field. It is used in solid-phase extraction, affinity purification and related separation techniques.
[B01J 2219/00657] One-dimensional arrays -- B01J 2219/00657 covers the use of one-dimensional arrays such as linear chains of particles, fibers, or channels in chemical or physical processes, particularly for separating substances or facilitating catalytic or colloidal reactions. It falls under performing operations, separating, and chemical/physical processes.
[B01J 2219/00659] Two-dimensional arrays -- B01J 2219/00659 covers the use of two-dimensional array formats-such as micro-plate or printed-array platforms-to carry out chemical or physical separation operations, often in high-throughput or multiplexed assays. The classification applies to methods that exploit spatially arranged reaction sites for parallel processing of samples in separation-based techniques.
[B01J 2219/00662] Two-dimensional arrays within two-dimensional arrays -- The entry B01J 2219/00662 covers separation or reaction methods that employ two-dimensional patterned structures embedded inside other two-dimensional arrays, such as layered micro-structured membranes, stacked micro-reactor grids or patterned catalyst surfaces. It relates to the design of hierarchical planar arrays used to control fluid flow, mass transfer or catalytic activity in chemical and physicochemical processes.
[B01J 2219/00664] Three-dimensional arrays -- B01J 2219/00664 covers chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes that employ three-dimensional arrays of particles, structures or supports to enhance reactions, separations or catalytic activity. It pertains to the use of spatially organised media within the broader fields of performing operations, separating and chemical or physical processes.
[B01J 2219/00666] One-dimensional arrays within three-dimensional arrays -- The CPC entry B01J 2219/00666 classifies processes that employ linear (one-dimensional) array structures situated inside three-dimensional array configurations, typically for separation, catalytic or colloidal operations. It covers the design, fabrication and use of such embedded linear arrays in reactors, filters or other chemical-physical apparatuses to enhance mass transfer, selectivity or reaction efficiency.
[B01J 2219/00668] Two-dimensional arrays within three-dimensional arrays -- The code B01J 2219/00668 covers processes that employ two-dimensional structures arranged inside three-dimensional matrices, such as patterned nanolayers or templated supports, for chemical, physical or physico-chemical operations like catalysis, separation or colloid formation. It pertains to the design and use of hierarchical arrays where the planar features are integrated within a bulk material to influence reaction pathways or material properties.
[B01J 2219/00671] Three-dimensional arrays within three-dimensional arrays -- This classification covers methods or apparatuses that create or use nested three-dimensional lattice or matrix structures, such as embedding a 3D array of particles inside another 3D array, often for separation, catalysis, or controlled reactions. It pertains to chemical or physical processes where hierarchical 3D architectures are employed to enhance mass transfer, selectivity, or functional performance.
[B01J 2219/00673] Slice arrays -- B01J 2219/00673 covers slice arrays employed in separation operations that involve chemical or physical processes such as catalytic or colloidal techniques. The classification encompasses devices that arrange multiple thin slices or plates to enhance contact between phases during reactions, filtrations, or chromatographic separations. It falls under the broader category of performing operations, separating, and general chemical or physico-chemical processes.
[B01J 2219/00675] In-situ synthesis on the substrate -- The entry B01J 2219/00675 covers processes in which a chemical compound or material is formed directly on a solid surface, such as a wafer, catalyst support or other substrate, without first preparing the product elsewhere. It includes in-situ deposition, growth, or polymerisation that creates films, coatings or nanostructures on the substrate and is classified under chemical or physico-chemical separating operations.
[B01J 2219/00677] Ex-situ synthesis followed by deposition on the substrate -- B01J 2219/00677 covers processes in which a material is first synthesized outside the target surface and then transferred or deposited onto that surface. The classification applies to ex-situ production methods before the product is applied to a substrate, typically as part of chemical or physical separation and coating operations.
[B01J 2219/0068] Means for controlling the apparatus of the process -- This subclass covers devices or systems that regulate the operation of equipment used in chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes, such as reactors, separators or catalysts. It includes sensors, controllers, feedback loops and other control mechanisms that adjust parameters like temperature, pressure, flow or composition during the process.
[B01J 2219/00682] Manual means -- The CPC subclass B01J 2219/00682 - Manual means - relates to chemical, physical or physico-chemical separation processes that are carried out by hand or other direct human action rather than by automated machinery. It falls under Performing Operations → Separating → Chemical or Physical Processes such as catalysis or colloid chemistry.
[B01J 2219/00684] Semi-automatic means -- B01J 2219/00684 "Semi-automatic means" refers to equipment that provides partial automation of separating operations within chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes, such as semi-automatic separators, conveyors, or control devices that assist but do not fully automate the removal of one phase from another. It falls under the broader categories of performing operations, separating, and chemical or physical processes like catalysis or colloid chemistry.
[B01J 2219/00686] Automatic -- The B01J 2219/00686 entry covers automatic methods for carrying out chemical or physical separation processes, such as catalyst-based or colloidal techniques, without manual intervention. It encompasses systems that control, monitor, or execute the separating operation autonomously, improving efficiency and consistency in industrial or laboratory settings.
[B01J 2219/00689] using computers -- The entry B01J 2219/00689 refers to the application of computers in chemical or physical separation processes, covering digital control, monitoring, modelling and simulation of operations such as catalysis, colloid chemistry or other physico-chemical techniques. It classifies inventions that use computer hardware or software to improve or automate the performance of these separating operations.
[B01J 2219/00691] using robots -- B01J 2219/00691 covers the use of robotic systems to carry out chemical or physical separation processes, such as automated handling, sorting, or manipulation of materials in catalytic, colloidal, or other physico-chemical operations.
[B01J 2219/00693] Means for quality control -- This CPC subclass covers devices, methods or systems used to monitor, test or otherwise assure the quality of products or processes within chemical, physical or physico-chemical operations, especially in separating or catalytic steps. It includes sensors, analytical instruments, sampling devices and control mechanisms that provide real-time data to keep product specifications within limits.
[B01J 2219/00695] Synthesis control routines, e.g. using computer programs -- The CPC entry B01J 2219/00695 covers computer-based synthesis control routines that monitor, adjust and optimise chemical or physico-chemical processes, often in real time, using software algorithms to direct reaction conditions. It falls under the "performing operations → separating → chemical or physical processes" hierarchy.
[B01J 2219/00698] Measurement and control of process parameters -- This CPC subclass covers apparatus and methods for monitoring and regulating variables such as temperature, pressure, flow rate, concentration or pH during chemical or physical separation operations, ensuring optimal performance. It includes sensors, feedback loops and control systems that maintain desired conditions in processes like catalysis, filtration, extraction or other physico-chemical separations.
[B01J 2219/007] Simulation or vitual synthesis -- B01J 2219/007 covers the use of computer-based methods to model, simulate or virtually synthesize chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes, allowing prediction of reaction outcomes, separation performance or material properties without actual laboratory experimentation. This entry falls within the hierarchy of Performing Operations → Separating → Chemical or Physical Processes. It is intended for tools that create virtual experiments to aid design, optimization and analysis of catalytic, colloidal or other process technologies.
[B01J 2219/00702] Processes involving means for analysing and characterising the products -- B01J 2219/00702 covers processes in which the products of a chemical, physical or physico-chemical reaction are examined, measured or otherwise characterised during or after the operation, using analytical equipment or techniques. It falls under separating operations and includes methods such as spectroscopy, chromatography or other analytical means applied to monitor or identify reaction outcomes.
[B01J 2219/00704] integrated with the reactor apparatus -- B01J 2219/00704 covers chemical or physico-chemical processes in which the separation step, such as catalytic or colloidal treatment, is incorporated directly into the reactor unit, allowing the reaction and subsequent isolation or conversion to occur in a single, integrated apparatus.
[B01J 2219/00707] separated from the reactor apparatus -- The CPC entry B01J 2219/00707 covers processes in which the reaction mixture or product is removed or otherwise separated from the reactor apparatus after the chemical or physico-chemical operation. It includes techniques such as decanting, draining, discharging, or any device that enables the product to be taken out of the reactor without further reaction. This subclass falls under performing operations → separating → chemical or physical processes.
[B01J 2219/00709] Type of synthesis -- B01J 2219/00709 classifies patents that describe the specific type of synthesis employed in chemical or physico-chemical processes, especially when the synthesis method is integral to a separating operation. It covers inventions where the nature of the synthesis-such as catalytic, polymeric or enzymatic routes-defines the technical contribution.

[B01J 2219/00763] Baffles -- Baffles are internal obstacles installed in reactors, separators or heat-exchangers to direct fluid flow, enhance mixing, increase residence time and improve mass- or heat-transfer efficiency. In the CPC hierarchy they fall under chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes, specifically separating operations where the baffle design influences separation performance.
[B01J 2219/00765] Baffles attached to the reactor wall -- The entry covers reactors that have baffles permanently attached to the inner wall to create turbulence, improve mixing and heat or mass transfer, and prevent flow short-circuiting during chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes such as catalysis or colloid operations.
[B01J 2219/00768] vertical -- The B01J 2219/00768 entry covers vertical separation methods, where the feed or material moves downward under gravity or a driving force toward a lower zone, often used in column or packed-bed processes to achieve chemical or physical separation. [B01J 2219/0077] inclined -- The B01J 2219/0077 entry covers separating operations that employ an inclined configuration, such as sloped plates, channels, or surfaces, to aid the removal of solids, liquids or gases in chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes. It classifies equipment or methods where the inclination improves sedimentation, flotation, filtration or other separation efficiencies within water, wastewater or similar treatment systems.

[B01J 2219/00781] Aspects relating to microreactors -- The B01J 2219/00781 entry concerns the design, operation, and use of microreactors-compact, often chip-based flow reactors that enable precise control of chemical or physico-chemical processes at very small scales. It covers aspects such as reactor geometry, mixing and heat-transfer enhancements, and integration with analytical or separation modules for improved efficiency in catalytic, colloidal, or other specialized reactions.
[B01J 2219/00783] Laminate assemblies, i.e. the reactor comprising a stack of plates -- This subclass describes laminate assemblies, specifically reactors formed by stacking plates, used to conduct chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes that involve separation or contact between reactants across the plate surfaces. The plate stack design provides efficient heat and mass transfer for operations such as catalysis or colloid chemistry.

[B01J 2219/0079] Monolith-base structure -- Monolith-base structures are single-piece porous supports, typically ceramic or polymeric, that serve as a solid matrix for catalysts or stationary phases in separation and reaction processes. Their continuous channel network provides high surface area and low pressure drop, enabling efficient flow-through catalysis, chromatography or other physico-chemical operations. This entry classifies inventions that design, manufacture or use such monolithic bases.

[B01J 2219/00795] Spiral-shaped -- The B01J 2219/00795 entry covers spiral-shaped structures or configurations employed in chemical, physical or physico-chemical separating processes, such as spirally wound membranes, coils or reactors used for catalysis, colloid treatment or other separation operations. It pertains to the use of a spiral geometry to enhance the efficiency of these processes.
[B01J 2219/00797] Concentric tubes -- Concentric tubes denotes an apparatus in which a tube is placed inside another tube so that fluids can flow in separate channels, enabling reactions, separations, heat exchange or catalysis within a single unit. The configuration is used for chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes such as catalytic or colloidal operations, allowing efficient contact between phases while maintaining isolation.
[B01J 2219/00799] Cup-shaped -- The entry code B01J 2219/00799 refers to cup-shaped chemical or physical processes. It falls under the broader category of chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes. This classification is used in the Cooperative Patent Classification system.

[B01J 2219/00858] Aspects relating to the size of the reactor -- The CPC entry B01J 2219/00858 covers any technical features that concern the size of a reactor used in chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes. [B01J 2219/0086] Dimensions of the flow channels -- The B01J 2219/0086 entry covers the specification and control of channel dimensions in flow-through equipment used for separation, such as reactors, filters, chromatography columns or other devices that rely on chemical or physical processes. It addresses how the size, shape and arrangement of these channels affect mass transport, pressure drop and overall separation efficiency.
[B01J 2219/00862] Dimensions of the reaction cavity itself -- This subclass deals with the specification and control of the geometric characteristics of the reaction cavity itself, such as its volume, shape, width, depth or internal surface area, as they affect mixing, heat removal and reaction efficiency in chemical and physical processes.
[B01J 2219/00864] Channel sizes in the nanometer range, e.g. nanoreactors -- The entry B01J 2219/00864 classifies technologies that employ channels with dimensions in the nanometer range, such as nanoreactors, for conducting chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes. [B01J 2219/00867] Microreactors placed in series, on the same or on different supports -- The code refers to configurations in which several microreactors are connected in series, either on a single substrate or on distinct supports, to perform sequential chemical or physical transformations. [B01J 2219/00869] Microreactors placed in parallel, on the same or on different supports -- A CPC sub-group for arranging several microreactors in parallel, either mounted on the same substrate or on different supports, to carry out chemical, physical or physico-chemical transformations such as catalytic or colloidal processes. [B01J 2219/00871] Modular assembly -- The code covers modular assemblies designed for separation operations that rely on chemical, physical or physico-chemical mechanisms. [B01J 2219/00873] Heat exchange -- The CPC subclass B01J 2219/00873 covers heat-exchange operations within chemical or physical processing. It includes methods and apparatus for transferring thermal energy between fluids, streams or phases to control temperature during reactions, separations or other processing steps.
[B01J 2219/00876] Insulation elements -- Insulation elements are materials or components designed to hinder heat, mass or electric transfer during separation or other chemical and physical operations, thereby improving efficiency, safety and product purity. They are employed in reactors, distillation columns, filters and similar equipment to maintain desired temperature gradients, prevent contamination and reduce energy consumption.
[B01J 2219/00878] Vacuum spaces -- The B01J 2219/00878 entry "Vacuum spaces" covers processes that create or exploit low-pressure regions to separate substances, such as vacuum filtration, distillation under reduced pressure, and evacuation of gases from reaction mixtures. It pertains to chemical, physical or physico-chemical operations where a vacuum is used to enhance separation efficiency.
[B01J 2219/0088] Peltier-type elements -- A Peltier-type element is a thermoelectric device that creates a temperature difference when an electric current passes through it, allowing direct heating or cooling without moving parts. In the CPC it falls under B01J 2219/0088, covering chemical, physical or physico-chemical separation processes that use such thermoelectric modules for temperature control. This classification groups it with other performing-operation technologies that facilitate separation by thermal means.
[B01J 2219/00882] Electromagnetic heating -- This CPC subclass covers the use of electromagnetic energy to heat materials within chemical or physical processing. It includes techniques such as induction, microwave, radio-frequency or other electromagnetic heating applied to facilitate reactions, separations or material transformations.
[B01J 2219/00885] Thin film heaters -- B01J 2219/00885 covers thin-film heaters, which are resistive heating elements formed as a very thin layer of conductive material deposited on a substrate. These devices generate heat through Joule heating for precise temperature control in chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes, often integrated into micro-reactors, sensors or laboratory equipment.
[B01J 2219/00887] Deflection means for heat or irradiation -- The subclass B01J 2219/00887 defines deflection means for heat or irradiation, i.e., devices or arrangements that redirect thermal energy or radiation to control temperature or exposure during chemical, physical or physico-chemical separating operations such as catalysis or colloid processes. It is part of the CPC hierarchy under Performing Operations → Separating → Chemical or Physical Processes.
[B01J 2219/00889] Mixing -- B01J 2219/00889 covers processes that combine two or more substances to achieve a uniform composition, including agitation, stirring, blending or other mixing techniques applied in chemical, physical or physico-chemical operations. It falls under the CPC group for separating and mixing within the broader field of chemical or physical processes.
[B01J 2219/00891] Feeding or evacuation -- B01J 2219/00891 covers methods and apparatus for introducing feedstock into a separation unit or removing the separated material, i.e., feeding or evacuation steps in chemical or physical separation processes. It relates to the handling of substances before, during, or after operations such as catalysis, adsorption, or centrifugation. The code is used to classify inventions that improve loading, discharge or transport of fluids or solids in separator systems.
[B01J 2219/00894] More than two inlets -- The subclass B01J 2219/00894 covers chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes that employ a device with more than two inlet streams, enabling the simultaneous introduction of several reactants, phases or feed streams. It is used for reactors or separators where multiple feeds are mixed or combined, often in catalytic or colloidal operations.
[B01J 2219/00896] Changing inlet or outlet cross-section, e.g. pressure-drop compensation -- This subgroup covers methods and apparatus that alter the geometric cross-section of an inlet or outlet to adjust fluid flow characteristics, such as compensating for pressure drops in reactors or separators. It includes designs that reshape passages, either dynamically or statically, to maintain desired flow rates or pressures during chemical or physical processing.
[B01J 2219/00898] Macro-to-Micro (M2M) -- The B01J 2219/00898 entry covers methods that translate macro-scale operations into micro-scale effects to achieve separation, such as using bulk chemical or physical actions to create, modify or manipulate microscopic structures or particles for catalytic, colloidal or other physico-chemical processes. It focuses on the design of processes where large-scale inputs directly control micro-level phenomena to improve separation efficiency.
[B01J 2219/009] Pulsating flow -- B01J 2219/009 covers the use of pulsating or oscillating fluid flow to improve separating operations that rely on chemical, physical or physico-chemical mechanisms. By periodically varying pressure or velocity, the technique enhances mixing, mass-transfer rates and particle transport, often increasing efficiency in catalytic, colloidal or other separation processes.
[B01J 2219/00903] Segmented flow -- Segmented flow is a method in which a fluid stream is broken into discrete segments separated by a second immiscible phase, typically a gas or liquid, enabling enhanced mixing, reaction control, heat transfer, and separation in chemical and physical processing.
[B01J 2219/00905] Separation -- The CPC entry B01J 2219/00905 covers general separation operations that use chemical, physical or physico-chemical methods to isolate or divide components of a mixture. It falls under the hierarchy of Performing Operations → Separating → Chemical or Physical Processes such as catalysis or colloid chemistry, and applies to techniques like filtration, centrifugation, adsorption and similar processes.
[B01J 2219/00907] using membranes -- B01J 2219/00907 covers the use of membrane technologies for separating substances in chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes. It falls under performing operations, separating, and chemical or physical processes such as catalysis or colloid chemistry.
[B01J 2219/00909] using filters -- This CPC subclass concerns the application of filters during chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes to effect separation of solids, liquids or colloids. It includes the choice, arrangement and operation of filter media as part of a reaction, purification or treatment step.
[B01J 2219/00912] by electrophoresis -- This subclass covers methods that separate substances using electrophoresis, where an electric field drives charged particles through a medium to achieve separation. It includes the preparation, operation and optimization of electrophoretic techniques for analytical or preparative purposes and may involve gels, capillaries or other supporting media.
[B01J 2219/00914] by dielectrophoresis -- The B01J 2219/00914 subclass covers separation techniques that employ dielectrophoresis, where non-uniform electric fields move particles according to their dielectric properties. This method is used to sort or concentrate cells, colloids, or other microscopic materials in chemical and physical processes, often within micro-fluidic or lab-on-a-chip systems.
[B01J 2219/00916] by chromatography -- This CPC subgroup covers separation methods that employ chromatography, where mixture components are differentiated by their interaction with a stationary phase and a mobile phase. It falls under performing operations, specifically separating, for chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes.
[B01J 2219/00918] by adsorption -- The CPC subclass B01J 2219/00918 pertains to separation operations that employ adsorption, where a solid surface selectively retains a component from a mixture. It is situated within the broader categories of performing operations, separating, and chemical or physical processes such as catalysis and colloid chemistry.
[B01J 2219/00921] by absorption -- This subclass covers separation techniques where a component is removed from a mixture by being taken up into another phase, such as a liquid or solid absorbent, through physical or chemical interaction. It includes gas-liquid absorption, liquid-solid adsorption and related physico-chemical methods.
[B01J 2219/00923] by surface tension -- This subgroup covers separation methods that rely on surface tension differences, using phenomena such as capillary action, droplets, or surfactant-mediated interfaces to isolate or extract components in chemical or physico-chemical processes.
[B01J 2219/00925] Irradiation -- Irradiation covers the use of electromagnetic rays, particles or other energy forms to induce chemical, physical or physico-chemical changes in substances, for purposes such as sterilisation, polymerisation, curing or material modification. It is classified under B01J 2219/00925 within the broader categories of performing operations, separating and chemical or physical processes.
[B01J 2219/00927] Particle radiation or gamma-radiation -- This subclass covers processes that use particle radiation or gamma-radiation, such as irradiation for synthesis, modification, analysis, or separation of substances within chemical, physical or physico-chemical operations. It is situated under performing operations, separating, and chemical or physical processes.
[B01J 2219/0093] Electric or magnetic energy -- B01J 2219/0093 covers the use of electric or magnetic energy to drive chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes, particularly in separation operations such as catalytic or colloidal systems. It falls under the broader categories of performing operations, separating, and chemical or physical processes.
[B01J 2219/00932] Sonic or ultrasonic vibrations -- The B01J 2219/00932 subgroup addresses the application of sonic or ultrasonic vibrations to chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes, particularly for performing operations such as separating, mixing, or enhancing reactions like catalysis or colloid chemistry.
[B01J 2219/00934] Electromagnetic waves -- The CPC code B01J 2219/00934 covers the use of electromagnetic waves-such as radio-frequency, microwave, infrared or ultraviolet radiation-to carry out separation or purification steps in chemical or physical processes, for example by heating, drying, or inducing selective interactions that facilitate the isolation of components. This falls within the broader class of performing operations and separating via chemical or physical methods.
[B01J 2219/00936] UV-radiations -- The CPC subclass B01J 2219/00936 covers the use of ultraviolet radiation as a means of effecting separation in chemical or physical processes. It includes methods where UV light stimulates reactions, alters solubility, or drives photochemical steps to isolate components, typically within catalysis, colloid chemistry or related separation operations.
[B01J 2219/00939] X-rays -- This subgroup covers the use of X-ray radiation as a means to effect or monitor separation, catalysis or other chemical or physical transformations, including irradiation, imaging and analysis of materials. It is situated under performing operations, separating, chemical or physical processes.
[B01J 2219/00941] Microwaves -- The B01J 2219/00941 subclass covers the use of microwave energy to carry out chemical, physical or physico-chemical operations, such as heating, reaction, catalysis or separation. It includes methods and apparatus that employ microwaves as the energy source for processing substances.
[B01J 2219/00943] Visible light, e.g. sunlight -- B01J 2219/00943 pertains to chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes that employ visible light, such as sunlight, to drive reactions or separations. It covers photochemical, photophysical or photocatalytic operations where the energy from the visible spectrum is used to initiate, accelerate or control the transformation of substances.
[B01J 2219/00945] Infrared light -- Infrared light refers to electromagnetic radiation with wavelengths from about 0.78 to 1000 µm used to induce or monitor chemical and physical transformations during separation operations. In this CPC subgroup it covers methods that employ infrared heating, drying, spectroscopy or other infrared-driven effects to assist in separating mixtures, catalysis or colloid processes.
[B01J 2219/00948] Radiofrequency -- The CPC subclass B01J 2219/00948 covers the application of radiofrequency energy in chemical or physical processes, particularly for separating mixtures. It includes methods that use RF heating, dielectric heating, or electromagnetic fields to facilitate separation, extraction, or purification steps.
[B01J 2219/0095] Control aspects -- The CPC entry B01J 2219/0095 covers control aspects of chemical, physical or physico-chemical separating operations, such as monitoring, regulating and automating process parameters in catalytic, colloidal or other separation systems. It includes feedback, sensor-based and computer-controlled methods that ensure stable and efficient operation.
[B01J 2219/00952] Sensing operations -- B01J 2219/00952 addresses the integration of sensors and measurement devices into separation processes, covering methods for detecting, monitoring or controlling the composition, concentration or physical parameters of a stream. These sensing operations are used to optimise chemical, physical or physico-chemical separations such as catalysis or colloid systems.
[B01J 2219/00954] Measured properties -- B01J 2219/00954 - Measured properties refers to processes that involve determining physical or chemical characteristics of substances during separation operations, such as monitoring temperature, pressure, conductivity, or composition in catalytic or colloidal systems. It falls under the broader categories of performing operations, separating, and chemical or physical processes.
[B01J 2219/00957] Compositions or concentrations -- B01J 2219/00957 covers the specific chemical mixtures, solutions, or concentrations employed in separation processes, such as solvents, adsorbents, or reagents that facilitate the physical or physico-chemical operation. It classifies patents dealing with the formulation of these substances rather than the equipment or method of separation itself.
[B01J 2219/00959] Flow -- B01J 2219/00959 covers flow phenomena relevant to separation operations, describing the movement of liquids, gases, or suspensions through equipment or media to achieve component separation. It includes design and control of flow regimes, pressure gradients, and transport mechanisms used in chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes.
[B01J 2219/00961] Temperature -- The B01J 2219/00961 subclass covers the use of temperature as a parameter in separating operations that involve chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes, such as catalytic or colloid chemistry. It includes methods where temperature is controlled, varied or monitored to achieve the separation of components.
[B01J 2219/00963] Pressure -- B01J 2219/00963 covers the use of pressure as an operating parameter in chemical or physical separation processes such as filtration, centrifugation or membrane operations. It includes methods where pressure drives the movement of fluids or particles to achieve separation and where pressure is controlled to influence reaction or transport phenomena.
[B01J 2219/00966] pH -- The code B01J 2219/00966 covers inventions that involve the use, measurement, control or adjustment of pH in chemical or physical processes. It applies to methods and apparatus where pH is a key factor in separating or transforming substances, such as in catalysis, colloid chemistry or other physico-chemical operations.
[B01J 2219/00968] Type of sensors -- This CPC subclass B01J 2219/00968 identifies the kinds of sensors employed in chemical, physical or physico-chemical separation operations, such as those used for monitoring catalysis, colloidal systems or other separation processes. It falls under Performing Operations → Separating → Chemical or Physical Processes, covering sensor technologies that support the control, measurement or detection of variables in these processes.
[B01J 2219/0097] Optical sensors -- B01J 2219/0097 relates to optical sensors used in chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes, particularly for monitoring, detecting or measuring substances, reaction conditions or other parameters by means of light-based detection.
[B01J 2219/00972] Visible light -- Processes employing visible light to induce chemical or physical changes, including photochemical reactions, photocatalysis and light-driven separation methods, where the light energy facilitates transformation of substances.

[B01J 2219/00977] Infrared light -- The code B01J 2219/00977 refers to the use of infrared radiation in chemical or physical separation processes. [B01J 2219/00979] Acoustic sensors -- B01J 2219/00979 falls under the CPC section B01J for chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes involving separation. [B01J 2219/00981] Gas sensors -- B01J 2219/00981 relates to gas sensors designed to detect, measure or monitor gaseous substances. It covers devices that convert a chemical or physical interaction with a target gas into an electrical or optical signal, including semiconductor, electrochemical, optical and catalytic sensors.
[B01J 2219/00984] Residence time -- The entry covers the measurement, control or effect of residence time - the period that reactants, intermediates or products remain in a reactor, separator or other processing unit - on chemical, physical or physico-chemical operations such as catalysis, colloid chemistry or other separation processes. It is used to optimise conversion, selectivity and equipment design.
[B01J 2219/00986] Microprocessor -- The CPC entry B01J 2219/00986 covers microprocessors that are integrated into equipment for performing chemical, physical or physico-chemical separation operations, such as controlling parameters, processing sensor data, or automating process steps. It includes devices that manage or optimise tasks like filtration, centrifugation, or catalytic reactions by providing programmable logic and real-time monitoring. This classification links the hardware component to broader separating processes within the performing-operations field.
[B01J 2219/00988] Leakage -- B01J 2219/00988 covers methods, apparatuses or measures for detecting, preventing or controlling leakage during chemical or physical separation operations, such as in reactors, columns or membrane systems. It relates to the handling of unintended fluid or gas escape within catalytic, colloidal or other physico-chemical processes.
[B01J 2219/0099] Cleaning -- B01J 2219/0099 covers chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes used to clean objects, surfaces or materials, including removal of contaminants, residues or deposits by means such as solvents, detergents, agitation, heat, ultrasonic action or other treatment methods. It encompasses general cleaning techniques not limited to specific industries or substances.
[B01J 2219/00993] Design aspects -- B01J 2219/00993 covers design aspects of equipment and processes used in chemical or physical separation operations, including the layout, geometry, and material selection of reactors, separators, and related plant components. It addresses considerations such as flow patterns, mass-transfer efficiency, and integration with catalytic or colloid systems.
[B01J 2219/00995] Mathematical modeling -- B01J 2219/00995 pertains to the application of mathematical models to describe, simulate or predict the behavior of chemical or physical separation processes, including catalysis and colloid systems. The entry covers computational methods that support the design, optimization or control of such operations.
[B01J 2219/00997] Strategical arrangements of multiple microreactor systems -- B01J2219/00997 covers the design and configuration of multiple microreactor units that operate together to achieve specific process goals. It includes strategies for arranging microreactors in series, parallel, or networked layouts to enhance reaction control, scale-up, or integration with other unit operations. The code falls under separating processes and chemical or physical processes such as catalysis and colloid chemistry.
[B01J 2219/02] Apparatus characterised by their chemically-resistant properties -- B01J 2219/02 classifies apparatus that are defined primarily by their resistance to chemical attack, corrosion or degradation. It includes reactors, vessels, pipes, filters and other equipment whose design, materials or protective coatings are selected to withstand aggressive chemicals in separation or processing operations.
[B01J 2219/0204] comprising coatings on the surfaces in direct contact with the reactive components -- B01J 2219/0204 covers processes that involve applying a coating to any surface that directly contacts reactive chemicals, such as catalysts, reagents or intermediates, in order to protect the surface, control the reaction or improve separation. [B01J 2219/0801] Controlling the process -- The entry B01J 2219/0801 covers methods and devices for regulating the conditions of a chemical, physical or physico-chemical separating operation, such as adjusting temperature, pressure, flow rate, catalyst activity or other parameters to achieve and maintain the desired process performance. It is used for process control in operations that involve catalytic, colloidal or other separation mechanisms.

The code is situated under separating processes within the broader class of chemical or physical processes.
[B01J 2219/0805] giving rise to electric discharges -- This subgroup covers processes that deliberately generate electric discharges-such as sparks, arcs, or plasma-in chemical or physical operations, often to initiate reactions, modify material surfaces, or drive synthesis. It includes methods using high-voltage breakdown in gases, liquids or solids to achieve the desired transformation.
[B01J 2219/0807] involving electrodes -- B01J 2219/0807 designates chemical or physico-chemical operations that employ electrodes, for example electrolysis, electrodeposition or electro-driven separation, as part of separating processes. [B01J 2219/0809] employing two or more electrodes -- B01J 2219/0809 covers processes that use two or more electrodes to achieve separation or transformation of substances, such as electrolysis, electrophoretic separation, or electrochemical deposition.
