[B01J 2219/0811] employing three electrodes -- B01J 2219/0811 covers chemical or physico-chemical processes that use a three-electrode arrangement, such as a working, reference, and counter electrode, to control or monitor electrochemical reactions for separation, analysis, or synthesis. The classification falls under performing operations, separating, and chemical or physical processes.
[B01J 2219/0813] employing four electrodes -- B01J 2219/0813 relates to separation operations that employ a four-electrode arrangement, such as in electro-precipitation, electrodialysis or electrophoretic devices. The four electrodes are used to generate and shape electric fields, allowing enhanced control of particle or ion migration and improved separation efficiency.
[B01J 2219/0815] involving stationary electrodes -- B01J 2219/0815 covers chemical or physico-chemical separation processes that employ stationary electrodes, for example electrodialysis, electrolytic precipitation, or membrane-based electro-separation. The subclass is nested under operations involving separating and catalytic or colloid chemistry.
[B01J 2219/0816] involving moving electrodes -- The CPC subgroup B01J 2219/0816 covers chemical or physico-chemical processes that use moving electrodes, such as electrolysis, electrodeposition or electro-separation where the electrodes are mechanically displaced relative to each other or to the reaction medium. It lies under performing operations, separating, and chemical or physical processes.
[B01J 2219/0818] Rotating electrodes -- Rotating electrodes are electrode configurations in which the electrode surface is set in motion, typically by rotation, to improve mass transport and mixing during electrochemical reactions such as plating, electrosynthesis, or analysis. This subclass falls under the broader category of chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes, specifically within separating operations that involve catalytic or colloid chemistry. It is designated by CPC code B01J 2219/0818.
[B01J 2219/082] Sliding electrodes -- The B01J 2219/082 subclass relates to processes that employ sliding electrodes, where an electrode surface moves relative to the electrolyte or a second electrode during operation. This arrangement is used to improve mass transfer or separation in electrochemical, catalytic or colloid-chemical applications.
[B01J 2219/0822] The electrode being consumed -- The subclass B01J 2219/0822 deals with chemical or physico-chemical operations in which the electrode itself is consumed during the process, for example as a sacrificial anode or in electro-deposition where material from the electrode becomes part of the product. It covers methods where electrode dissolution or erosion is an intended step of the reaction.
[B01J 2219/0824] Details relating to the shape of the electrodes -- The B01J 2219/0824 subclass covers technical information about electrode geometry used in electro-chemical separating operations. It includes design aspects such as size, shape, surface patterning and arrangement that influence the efficiency of chemical or physical processes like catalysis, electro-lysis or colloid treatment.
[B01J 2219/0826] essentially linear -- This CPC subclass covers separation techniques that specifically target substances that are essentially linear in structure, such as linear polymers or chain-like molecules. The methods may involve chemical, physical, or physico-chemical operations such as chromatography, electrophoresis, or selective precipitation designed to differentiate linear chains from branched or cyclic counterparts.
[B01J 2219/0828] Wires -- This subgroup concerns processes and apparatus that employ wires or wire meshes to achieve separation of substances, such as filtration, phase retention or catalytic support in chemical and physical operations. It covers the design, arrangement and use of wire elements to isolate, collect or pass through solid, liquid or gaseous components during treatment or synthesis.
[B01J 2219/083] cylindrical -- The CPC subclass B01J 2219/083 covers separation processes that employ cylindrical equipment or geometry, such as tubular reactors, columns, or centrifuges, where the cylindrical shape influences the chemical, physical or physico-chemical operation. It applies to methods and apparatuses that use this form to effect mixing, phase contact, or particle settlement during the separation step.
[B01J 2219/0832] essentially toroidal -- The subclass B01J 2219/0832 covers chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes performed in equipment that has an essentially toroidal shape, i.e., a doughnut-like configuration. It includes reactors, separators or other apparatus where the toroidal geometry influences flow, mixing or heat transfer, often used in catalysis, colloid chemistry or related operations.
[B01J 2219/0833] forming part of a full circle -- The B01J 2219/0833 entry covers processes that create a portion of a full circular shape, such as an arc or ring segment, for use in chemical or physical separation operations. It includes methods for shaping, assembling or treating the partial-circle as part of a larger device or reaction system.
[B01J 2219/0835] substantially flat -- The subclass B01J 2219/0835 covers separation methods that employ substantially flat configurations such as membranes, thin-layer plates, or flat filters, where the active surface is planar and the process relies on chemical or physical interactions across that surface. It includes processes like flat-sheet chromatography, flat membrane filtration, and other planar separation techniques used in catalysis or colloid chemistry.
[B01J 2219/0837] Details relating to the material of the electrodes -- B01J 2219/0837 pertains to the chemical or physical processes used for separating substances, focusing on the specifics of electrode composition and structure. It addresses how different electrode materials affect the performance of catalytic, electro-chemical or colloidal operations within these separation techniques.
[B01J 2219/0839] Carbon -- B01J 2219/0839 pertains to the use of carbon in chemical, physical, or physico-chemical processes. This classification specifically addresses aspects of carbon within broader process categories such as catalysis and colloid chemistry.
[B01J 2219/0841] Metal -- B01J 2219/0841 designates processes in which metals serve as reactants, catalysts, or products within chemical, physical or physico-chemical operations such as catalysis, colloid chemistry or related separation methods. [B01J 2219/0843] Ceramic -- B01J 2219/0843 covers processes that involve ceramic materials within the broader field of chemical, physical or physico-chemical operations, particularly those related to separating or handling ceramics in reactions, treatment, shaping or disposal. [B01J 2219/0845] Details relating to the type of discharge -- B01J 2219/0845 covers specifications concerning the nature of the discharge used in separation operations, such as electrical, pneumatic or fluid discharge, within chemical, physical or physicochemical processes. It focuses on how the discharge type influences the separation mechanism, for example in catalysis, colloid treatment or other performing operation methods.
[B01J 2219/0847] Glow discharge -- This subclass covers the use of glow discharge, a low-pressure plasma generated by an electric discharge, as a method for separating or processing materials, including surface modification, analysis and isolation of substances. It falls under CPC B01J for chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes such as catalysis and colloid chemistry.
[B01J 2219/0849] Corona pulse discharge -- Corona pulse discharge refers to a non-thermal, high-frequency electrical discharge created in a gap surrounding a pointed electrode, producing a stream of energetic electrons and ions that drive chemical or physical transformations such as oxidation, polymerisation or pollutant removal. It is employed as a pulsed power source in processes like surface treatment, gas cleaning and synthesis of nanomaterials.
[B01J 2219/085] creating magnetic fields -- B01J 2219/085 refers to methods that generate magnetic fields as part of chemical or physical operations, especially for separation or catalysis. The classification covers equipment and techniques that produce static or varying magnetic fields to influence material behavior during processing.
[B01J 2219/0852] employing permanent magnets -- This subclass covers the use of permanent magnets to drive or assist chemical, physical or physico-chemical operations, especially separation steps. It includes processes where the magnetic field of a fixed magnet influences reaction media, catalyst particles or colloids to improve performance.
[B01J 2219/0854] employing electromagnets -- B01J 2219/0854 covers separation methods that use electromagnets to manipulate or collect magnetic particles or fluids, enabling the selective removal or concentration of components in chemical or physical processes such as magnetic filtration, electromagnetic precipitation, and magnetically assisted catalysis. This subclass falls under performing operations, separating, and chemical or physical processes.
[B01J 2219/0856] employing a combination of permanent and electromagnets -- The B01J 2219/0856 subclass concerns separation techniques that utilize a combination of permanent magnets and electromagnets to generate magnetic fields. It applies to chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes where magnetic forces are used to isolate, collect or remove magnetic or magnetically responsive particles from mixtures.
[B01J 2219/0858] employing moving elements -- This CPC subclass covers processes that make use of moving elements, such as stirrers, agitators, or rotating parts, to promote chemical or physical reactions. It falls under B01J 2219 dealing with separating operations and chemical/physico-chemical processes, including catalysis and colloid chemistry.
[B01J 2219/086] Moving (electro)magnets -- The B01J 2219/086 class covers devices and methods for moving electromagnets, including the actuation of permanent or induced magnetic fields to transport, position or separate materials. It includes mechanisms that convert electrical energy into mechanical motion of magnetic components for use in separation or processing operations.
[B01J 2219/0862] employing multiple (electro)magnets -- B01J 2219/0862 relates to chemical or physical processes that employ multiple electromagnets or magnets to affect reactions or separations. It is placed under performing operations, separating, chemical or physical processes in the Cooperative Patent Classification.
[B01J 2219/0864] Three (electro)magnets -- B01J 2219/0864 concerns the use of three magnets, either permanent or electromagnetic, arranged to create a magnetic field for separating substances in chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes. The configuration is applied in operations such as magnetic separation of particles, catalyst recovery or colloid manipulation.
[B01J 2219/0866] Four (electro)magnets -- The subclass B01J 2219/0866 pertains to the use of four electromagnets or permanent magnets arranged to generate a magnetic field configuration for separating substances, particles or phases in chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes. It includes apparatus and methods that exploit the combined magnetic forces of four magnets to isolate, concentrate or direct target materials.
[B01J 2219/0867] Six or more (electro)magnets -- The subclass B01J 2219/0867 covers technologies that employ six or more electromagnets (or permanent magnets) arranged to create multiple magnetic fields for performing separation or related operations, often in chemical or physical processing equipment. It typifies apparatus where several magnetic sources act together to influence particles, fluids or gases.
[B01J 2219/0869] Feeding or evacuating the reactor -- The entry concerns methods and apparatus for introducing reactants into, or removing products from, reactors used in chemical or physical processing. It covers feeding, charging, discharge or evacuation operations essential for continuous or batch operations and is classified under separating processes within performing operations.
[B01J 2219/0871] Heating or cooling of the reactor -- B01J 2219/0871 covers the heating or cooling of reactors used in chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes such as catalysis or colloid chemistry. It relates to operations that control reactor temperature to optimize reaction performance, safety and product quality.
[B01J 2219/0873] Materials to be treated -- The subclass B01J 2219/0873 addresses the substances that are the target of a separating operation performed by chemical, physical or physico-chemical means, such as catalytic or colloidal techniques. It covers any material-solid, liquid or gas-intended to be processed or treated during the separation step.
[B01J 2219/0875] Gas -- The CPC entry B01J 2219/0875 "Gas" covers chemical, physical or physico-chemical operations in which gases are used, generated, transformed or separated, including gas-phase reactions, catalytic processes and gas handling techniques. It falls under Performing Operations → Separating → Chemical or Physical Processes.
[B01J 2219/0877] Liquid -- B01J 2219/0877 covers liquid phases used in separating operations that involve chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes such as catalysis, colloid chemistry or other transformation methods. It falls under the broader categories of performing operations, separating, and chemical or physical processes, specifying the use of liquids as the medium for these separations.
[B01J 2219/0879] Solid -- The CPC entry B01J 2219/0879 covers separation techniques that employ a solid phase. It includes processes such as filtration, sedimentation, adsorption on solid sorbents, precipitation onto solids and solid-phase extraction used to isolate or purify substances. The code falls under the broader category of chemical, physical or physico-chemical operations for separating materials.
[B01J 2219/0881] Two or more materials -- The CPC subclass B01J 2219/0881 covers chemical, physical or physico-chemical operations in which two or more materials are processed together, such as mixed-phase reactions, co-precipitation, or simultaneous treatment of multiple substances. It falls under Performing Operations → Separating → Chemical or Physical Processes. This category is used for inventions that involve combined handling or transformation of several materials in a single step.
[B01J 2219/0883] Gas-gas -- B01J 2219/0883 pertains to gas-gas interactions within chemical, physical, or physico-chemical processes. This classification covers the specific aspects of these interactions in general process contexts.
[B01J 2219/0884] Gas-liquid -- B01J 2219/0884 relates to gas-liquid contacting methods used in separation operations, covering processes such as absorption, stripping and other techniques where a gas phase interacts with a liquid phase to transfer mass or energy.
[B01J 2219/0886] Gas-solid -- B01J 2219/0886 falls under the CPC group for performing operations that separate phases, specifically chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes involving gas-solid systems. It covers methods and apparatus for treating, reacting, filtering, adsorbing or otherwise handling gases in contact with solid materials, including catalytic and colloidal interactions.
[B01J 2219/0888] Liquid-liquid -- The B01J 2219/0888 subclass covers methods and apparatus for separating components between two immiscible liquids, such as liquid-liquid extraction, partitioning or redistribution of substances from one liquid phase to another. It includes processes that exploit differences in solubility or affinity to achieve purification, concentration or recovery of desired compounds.
[B01J 2219/089] Liquid-solid -- B01J 2219/089 relates to processes and devices that separate a solid phase from a liquid phase, encompassing techniques such as filtration, sedimentation, centrifugation, flotation and other liquid-solid separation methods used in chemical, physical or physico-chemical operations.
[B01J 2219/0892] involving catalytically active material -- The CPC subclass B01J 2219/0892 pertains to separating operations that employ a catalytically active material, covering chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes in which the catalyst facilitates the separation step, such as catalytic adsorption, extraction or membrane transport.
[B01J 2219/0894] Processes carried out in the presence of a plasma -- B01J 2219/0894 covers processes in which a plasma-an ionized gas containing electrons, ions and radicals-is employed to facilitate chemical or physical transformations, such as synthesis, surface modification, or separation operations, within the broader category of chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes.
[B01J 2219/0896] Cold plasma -- Cold plasma refers to a partially ionised gas at near-ambient temperature created by an electric discharge. It is employed in chemical and physical operations such as surface cleaning, activation, coating and separation processes, exploiting reactive species without substantial heat input.
[B01J 2219/0898] Hot plasma -- Hot plasma refers to a highly ionized gas at elevated temperature that is employed to drive chemical or physical transformations, including cutting, etching, surface modification and material separation. It falls under CPC B01J 2219/0898, which covers separating operations using chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes such as catalysis or colloid chemistry.
[B01J 2219/12] Processes employing electromagnetic waves -- The CPC subclass B01J 2219/12 covers methods that use electromagnetic radiation-such as ultraviolet, infrared, microwave or radio-frequency waves-to drive chemical, physical or physico-chemical transformations, typically for separating or processing materials. It falls under performing operations, separating, and general chemical or physical processes.
[B01J 2219/1203] Incoherent waves -- Incoherent waves are wave phenomena whose phases are not uniformly correlated, often exploited in chemical and physical separation techniques such as ultrasonic or light-scattering methods. This subclass groups processes that employ such non-coherent wave energy to influence particle behavior, promote mixing, or aid in the removal of substances during catalytic, colloidal or other physico-chemical operations.
[B01J 2219/1206] Microwaves -- The entry B01J 2219/1206 refers to the use of microwave energy as a means of carrying out chemical, physical or physico-chemical operations, such as heating, activation, synthesis or separation, within processes that involve catalysis or colloidal chemistry. It falls under the broader categories of separating and chemical or physical processes.
[B01J 2219/1209] Features relating to the reactor or vessel -- This subclass falls under performing operations - separating - chemical or physical processes. It classifies inventions that concern the design, construction or functional aspects of reactors or vessels used in catalytic, colloidal or other physico-chemical reactions. Documents describing reactor geometry, materials, internal components or control features are placed here.
[B01J 2219/1212] Arrangements of the reactor or the reactors -- This code covers the design and spatial configuration of reactors or groups of reactors used in chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes. It includes concepts such as series or parallel linking, modular or staged layouts, and the integration of reactors within a plant to improve reaction control, heat management or product separation.
[B01J 2219/1215] Single reactor -- B01J 2219/1215 - Single reactor refers to processes carried out in a single vessel where chemical, physical or physico-chemical reactions, such as catalysis or colloid chemistry, are performed and separated without the need for multiple reactors. It falls under performing operations, separating, and chemical or physical processes.
[B01J 2219/1218] Multiple reactors -- B01J 2219/1218 - Multiple reactors covers the use of two or more reactors arranged in series or parallel to carry out chemical, physical or physico-chemical transformations, allowing staged or simultaneous reaction steps, better control of temperature, pressure and residence time, and integration of catalysis or separation within a single process.
[B01J 2219/1221] the reactor -- The subclass B01J 2219/1221 covers reactors used in separating operations that involve chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes such as catalysis or colloid chemistry. It includes designs and configurations of vessels where reaction and separation occur together or sequentially, focusing on the equipment itself rather than the specific reaction.
[B01J 2219/1224] Form of the reactor -- B01J 2219/1224 covers specifications of the physical form of reactors used in chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes, including the shape, geometry, internal configuration and overall arrangement of the reaction vessel. It addresses how the reactor's structure influences mixing, heat transfer, residence time and process efficiency.
[B01J 2219/1227] Reactors comprising tubes with open ends -- A reactor of the B01J series is a unit where a bundle of tubes that have open ends is employed as the reaction vessel, allowing reactants to flow through the tube interior and products to exit the open ends. Such designs are common in processes that combine chemical reaction with subsequent separation steps.
[B01J 2219/123] Vessels in the form of a cup -- B01J 2219/123 refers to cup-shaped vessels employed in chemical, physical or physico-chemical operations, especially where separating or catalytic actions are performed. The classification covers containers shaped like a cup that serve as reactors, separators or similar process equipment in laboratory or industrial settings.
[B01J 2219/1233] Closure means, such as lids, caps, seals -- This CPC subclass B01J 2219/1233 covers devices that provide a closure for vessels or reactors, such as lids, caps and seals, used in chemical or physical separation operations. It falls under performing operations, separating, chemical or physical processes.
[B01J 2219/1236] Frames for holding the lid in place -- B01J 2219/1236 relates to frames used to hold lids in place during chemical, physical or physico-chemical operations such as separation or reaction. These holding devices are classified under Performing Operations - Separating - Chemical or Physical Processes and ensure a sealed environment for safety and product integrity.
[B01J 2219/1239] Means for feeding and evacuation -- The B01J 2219/1239 subclass covers apparatus that introduces feedstock into a reaction or separation unit and withdraws product or waste. It includes feeders, pumps, conveyors, discharge devices and related arrangements used in chemical, physical or physico-chemical separating operations.
[B01J 2219/1242] Materials of construction -- B01J 2219/1242 relates to the selection and specification of materials used to construct apparatus for separating operations that involve chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes such as catalysis or colloid chemistry. It addresses how construction materials affect durability, corrosion resistance and compatibility with the operating conditions of the process.
[B01J 2219/1245] Parts of the reactor being microwave absorbing, dielectric -- B01J 2219/1245 covers reactor components that act as microwave absorbers, using dielectric materials to convert microwave energy into heat within the reaction zone. It applies to chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes where microwave heating aids separation, catalysis or other transformations.
[B01J 2219/1248] Features relating to the microwave cavity -- B01J 2219/1248 relates to the design and characteristics of the microwave cavity employed in chemical or physical separating operations, such as catalytic, colloid or other physico-chemical processes. It covers the cavity's geometry, materials, coupling methods and operating parameters that affect microwave generation and delivery to the reaction or separation medium.
[B01J 2219/1251] Support for the reaction vessel -- This CPC subclass covers structures that hold or support reaction vessels during chemical or physical processes, including racks, frames, stands, or holders designed to maintain position and stability of the vessel while enabling operations such as mixing, heating, or catalysis. It applies to any apparatus that provides mechanical support for reactors in laboratory or industrial settings.
[B01J 2219/1254] Static supports -- The CPC subclass B01J 2219/1254 covers static support structures employed in chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes, such as fixed beds, static mixers or stationary matrices that provide a non-moving framework for reactions, separations or catalysis. It includes designs where the support remains stationary while fluids or reactants pass through, enabling processes like adsorption, catalytic conversion or phase separation without mechanical movement.
[B01J 2219/1257] Rotating supports -- The CPC subclass B01J 2219/1257 covers rotating supports used in chemical or physical processing, such as drums, rollers or other moving fixtures that hold materials while they are subjected to reactions, separations or other operations. It includes designs where the support itself spins to improve mixing, heat transfer or exposure to catalysts. This subclass falls under the broader categories of performing operations, separating and chemical or physical processes.
[B01J 2219/126] in the form of a closed housing -- A process performed in a closed housing refers to chemical, physical or physico-chemical operations carried out inside a sealed vessel or enclosure, such as reactors, autoclaves or pressure-tight containers, where the environment is isolated from the surrounding atmosphere to control reaction conditions, prevent contamination or contain hazardous substances.
[B01J 2219/1263] in the form of a open housing or stand -- This subclass covers chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes carried out in apparatus where the reaction zone is an open housing or stand, such as open-air reactors, trays or support structures that do not fully enclose the material. It relates to operations involving separation, catalysis or colloid chemistry performed in such open-form equipment.
[B01J 2219/1266] Microwave deflecting parts -- The CPC subclass B01J 2219/1266 covers parts that deflect or redirect microwave radiation in chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes, such as reactors or separators that use microwave energy for heating, mixing or separation. These components are designed to control the distribution of microwaves to improve process efficiency.
[B01J 2219/1269] Microwave guides -- Microwave guides are structures that confine and direct microwave energy for use in chemical, physical or physico-chemical operations such as heating, separation or catalysis, ensuring efficient transmission of the electromagnetic field to the process zone. They are classified under CPC B01J 2219/1269, which covers wave-guide components within the broader category of separating processes in performing operations.
[B01J 2219/1272] Materials of construction -- B01J 2219/1272 designates the substances and structural components employed to build equipment used in separating operations within chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes such as catalysis or colloid chemistry. It includes metals, alloys, polymers, ceramics and composites selected for resistance to corrosion, temperature, pressure or mechanical stress in reactors, columns, filters and similar apparatus.
[B01J 2219/1275] Controlling the microwave irradiation variables -- The entry covers methods for adjusting microwave-irradiation parameters-such as power, frequency, temperature, exposure time and field distribution-to control chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes and influence reaction outcomes.
[B01J 2219/1278] Time -- B01J 2219/1278 covers the consideration, measurement, control or influence of time in chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes, such as timing of reaction steps, duration of separations, or scheduling of catalytic events. It relates to methods that explicitly incorporate time parameters to affect the outcome or efficiency of the operation.
[B01J 2219/1281] Frequency -- B01J 2219/1281 refers to processes that employ a specific frequency, such as ultrasonic, radio-frequency or microwave energy, to effect separation or transformation in chemical or physico-chemical operations. It falls under Performing Operations → Separating → Chemical or Physical Processes, covering techniques where controlled oscillations assist catalysis, colloid manipulation or other separation methods. The entry is part of the CPC system shared by the EPO and USPTO.
[B01J 2219/1284] Intensity -- B01J 2219/1284 classifies features relating to the intensity of a separating operation within chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes, such as the level of energy, temperature, pressure or other driving forces applied. It is used to index inventions that adjust or control these intensity parameters to optimise catalysis, colloid chemistry or other separation methods.
[B01J 2219/1287] Features relating to the microwave source -- B01J 2219/1287 covers aspects of the microwave source used in chemical or physical separating operations, such as power delivery, frequency control, resonant cavity design or coupling mechanisms. It falls under CPC section B01J (separating) within the broader class of chemical or physico-chemical processes.
[B01J 2219/129] Arrangements thereof -- This subclass pertains to the structural layout and configuration of equipment used for separating chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes. It covers the design and interconnection of reactors, columns, membranes, filters and related units, focusing on how they are arranged to achieve effective separation.
[B01J 2219/1293] Single source -- B01J 2219/1293 "Single source" covers processes in which a chemical or physical separation is carried out using a single source of reagent, catalyst, solvent or energy, rather than multiple distinct inputs. It is applied to catalytic, colloidal or other physico-chemical operations where the same material fulfills several functional roles within the same step. This approach streamlines the separating operation and can improve efficiency and simplicity.
[B01J 2219/1296] Multiple sources -- The B01J 2219/1296 "Multiple sources" subgroup covers chemical, physical or physico-chemical separating operations that draw on two or more material streams or origins simultaneously, such as combined feed, mixed reactors or concurrent extraction from different sources, within broader catalytic, colloidal or related processes. It pertains to performing operations that involve handling multiple sources in a single process step.
[B01J 2219/18] Details relating to the spatial orientation of the reactor -- B01J 2219/18 covers information that specifies how a reactor is oriented in space, including the direction, angle, or positioning of the vessel relative to a reference frame or other equipment. It applies to chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes such as catalysis, separation or colloid chemistry where the reactor's spatial arrangement influences performance.
[B01J 2219/182] horizontal -- B01J 2219/182 concerns apparatus or processes in which the primary working axis is oriented horizontally, such as reactors, mixers, separators or other equipment used for carrying out chemical, physical or physico-chemical operations, including catalysis and colloid chemistry. The classification applies to horizontal configurations that influence fluid flow, mixing or separation within the performing-operations domain.
[B01J 2219/185] vertical -- B01J 2219/185 refers to vertical separation processes, a sub-class of chemical, physical or physico-chemical operations that involve separating substances. It includes methods such as vertical chromatography, gravity-driven settling, or upward/downward flow reactors where the primary motion of the material is aligned with the vertical axis.
[B01J 2219/187] inclined at an angle to the horizontal or to the vertical plane -- This subclass covers separation operations in which the apparatus or flow path is inclined relative to the horizontal or vertical plane, such as inclined plates, channels, or sedimentation zones. It applies to chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes where the angle of inclination is a defining feature of the separation technique.
[B01J 2219/19] Details relating to the geometry of the reactor -- B01J 2219/19 pertains to patents that disclose the geometric characteristics of reactors used in chemical or physical processes, including shape, size, volume, internal configuration and the spatial arrangement of components that influence reaction performance.
[B01J 2219/192] polygonal -- The B01J 2219/192 subclass covers separation methods that use polygonal particles, crystals or aggregates, such as employing polygon-shaped precipitates or filter media to enhance precipitation, flotation, sedimentation, or other chemical-physical separation processes.
[B01J 2219/1921] triangular -- The CPC subclass B01J 2219/1921 covers chemical or physical processes that use a triangular configuration, such as triangular-shaped reactors, mixers or separation devices. It is situated under performing operations, separating and general physico-chemical processes.

[B01J 2219/1928] hexagonal -- The B01J 2219/1928 entry concerns separation operations that employ hexagonal structures or phases, such as hexagonal crystal lattices, membrane arrays, or catalyst arrangements, within chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes. [B01J 2219/194] round -- The CPC subclass B01J 2219/194 covers separation techniques that use round-shaped equipment such as cylindrical columns, rotors or tubular reactors, where the geometry of the apparatus is a defining feature of the process. It is classified under Performing operations - Separating - Chemical or physical processes, addressing general physico-chemical separation methods that employ a round form factor.
[B01J 2219/1941] circular or disk-shaped -- B01J 2219/1941 relates to separation processes that use a circular or disk-shaped element, such as rotating discs, plate rotors or disk centrifuges, employed in chemical, physical or physico-chemical operations.
[B01J 2219/1942] spherical -- B01J 2219/1942 covers processes for preparing or handling spherical particles, such as beads, droplets or microspheres, using chemical, physical or physico-chemical methods. It includes techniques for forming, shaping, stabilising or separating spherical entities in suspensions, emulsions or powders, often for use in catalysis, drug delivery or material synthesis.
[B01J 2219/1943] cylindrical -- The CPC entry B01J 2219/1943 pertains to chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes that employ a cylindrical configuration, such as tubular reactors, columns or vessels, for carrying out separating operations. It falls under the hierarchy Performing Operations → Separating → Chemical or physical processes (e.g., catalysis or colloid chemistry). This code is used to classify inventions that specifically utilize a cylindrical geometry to facilitate the separation of substances.
[B01J 2219/1944] spiral -- This subclass covers spiral configurations used in performing operations such as separating or catalysis, describing reactors, coils or channels shaped in a spiral to enhance mass-transfer or heat-exchange in chemical or physico-chemical processes.
[B01J 2219/1945] toroidal -- The entry B01J 2219/1945 designates toroidal-shaped equipment used in chemical or physical separation operations, such as doughnut-shaped reactors, filters or centrifuges that exploit the geometry to improve mixing, residence-time distribution or separation efficiency.
[B01J 2219/1946] conical -- The B01J 2219/1946 entry relates to chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes carried out in or involving a conical configuration, such as conical reactors, mixers, separators or other equipment with a conical shape. It falls under separating operations within the broader category of performing operations for chemical or physical processes.
[B01J 2219/1947] oval or ellipsoidal -- B01J 2219/1947 covers separation operations in which oval or ellipsoidal particles, droplets, or vessels are employed, typically within chemical or physical processes such as catalysis or colloid chemistry. The entry specifies the shape of the material or equipment used to enhance separation efficiency.
[B01J 2219/1948] ovoid or egg-shaped -- B01J 2219/1948 refers to chemical or physical separation processes in which the product is formed or conditioned into an ovoid or egg-shaped morphology, such as during crystallisation, precipitation, coating or particle formation steps that deliberately produce an egg-like geometry. This subclass falls under Performing Operations → Separating → Chemical or Physical Processes and covers methods that control shape as part of the separation operation.
[B01J 2219/24] Stationary reactors without moving elements inside -- B01J 2219/24 pertains to stationary reactors that contain no moving internal components, such as fixed-bed or packed-bed vessels used for chemical, physical or physico-chemical transformations including catalysis and separation. The class is part of the broader categories of performing operations, separating, and chemical or physical processes.
[B01J 2219/2401] Reactors comprising multiple separate flow channels -- The subclass B01J 2219/2401 refers to reactors that contain two or more distinct flow channels, allowing separate streams to pass concurrently through the same vessel. Such designs are used to improve heat and mass transfer, enable staged or simultaneous reactions, and can integrate separation steps within a single reactor for processes such as catalysis or colloid chemistry.
[B01J 2219/2402] Monolithic-type reactors -- B01J 2219/2402 relates to monolithic-type reactors employed in chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes for separating substances. These reactors consist of a single-piece porous structure that acts both as catalyst support and flow channel, allowing reactions and separation to occur within the same compact unit.
[B01J 2219/2403] Geometry of the channels -- B01J 2219/2403 covers the design and shape of the channels through which fluids or particles pass in separation or catalytic processes. It includes the dimensions, cross-sectional form and arrangement of channels that affect flow, mixing and reaction efficiency in devices such as membranes, reactors and chromatographic columns.
[B01J 2219/2404] Polygonal -- This subclass deals with separation methods that employ polygonal-shaped particles, membranes or structures to effect chemical or physical separation, such as filtration, chromatography or catalytic processes using polygonal geometry. It falls under B01J 2219, covering chemical or physico-chemical separating operations.
[B01J 2219/2406] Rectangular -- The CPC subclass B01J 2219/2406 covers rectangular equipment or components used in chemical, physical or physico-chemical separation processes, such as rectangular columns, trays, filters or reactors that implement the separating operation within the broader performing-operations domain. It applies to designs where the shape is essentially rectangular.
[B01J 2219/2407] Square
[B01J 2219/2408] Circular or ellipsoidal -- The B01J 2219/2408 subclass covers processes and apparatuses that separate particles whose shape is circular or ellipsoidal, employing methods such as centrifugation, filtration, or shape-based classification to isolate or sort these specific geometries within chemical or physical treatment operations.
[B01J 2219/2409] Heat exchange aspects -- The subclass B01J 2219/2409 covers the heat-exchange aspects of chemical, physical or physico-chemical separating processes. It includes methods and apparatus that manage temperature control, heat removal or addition during reactions, catalysis, colloidal operations, and related separation steps. This falls under Performing Operations → Separating → Chemical or physical processes.
[B01J 2219/2411] The reactant being in indirect heat exchange with a non reacting heat exchange medium -- This subclass covers processes in which a reactant is heated or cooled through indirect contact with a heat-transfer medium that does not react with the material. The medium exchanges heat with the reactant via a barrier or conduit, allowing temperature control without direct mixing. It is used in catalytic, colloidal or other physico-chemical operations where precise thermal management is required.
[B01J 2219/2412] Independent temperature control in various sections of the monolith -- This subclass covers apparatus or methods that provide separate temperature regulation for individual zones of a monolithic catalyst or reactor, allowing each section to be heated or cooled independently during chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes such as catalysis or colloid reactions.
[B01J 2219/2413] Two reactions in indirect heat exchange -- B01J 2219/2413 covers processes that couple two chemical reactions by indirect heat exchange, using a heat-transfer medium or exchanger to transfer thermal energy from one reaction zone to another without direct contact. This allows an exothermic reaction to supply heat for an endothermic reaction, improving overall energy efficiency.
[B01J 2219/2414] The same reactant stream undergoing different reactions, endothermic or exothermic -- B01J 2219/2414 covers processes in which a single feed stream of reactants is divided and subjected to multiple, possibly concurrent, reactions that may be endothermic or exothermic, allowing simultaneous synthesis or transformation within the same system.
[B01J 2219/2416] Additional heat exchange means, e.g. electric resistance heater, coils -- The subclass B01J 2219/2416 covers auxiliary heating elements incorporated into chemical or physical separation processes, such as electric-resistance heaters or coiled conductors that provide additional heat exchange to control reaction temperature or improve separation efficiency. It is part of the broader "Separating" group within "Performing operations."
[B01J 2219/2417] Direct heat exchange -- B01J 2219/2417 covers apparatus and methods that transfer heat directly between two media, typically a hot and a cold stream, without an intermediate heat-transfer fluid. The technology is used in chemical and physical separation processes such as distillation, crystallisation or drying, where efficient thermal coupling improves energy use and process control. It falls under performing operations → separating → chemical or physical processes, indicating its role in facilitating heat-driven separations.
[B01J 2219/2418] Feeding means -- This subclass covers apparatus or methods for introducing raw material, reactants, or feed streams into a chemical or physical processing unit such as a reactor, separator, or catalyst bed. It includes feeders, pumps, conveyors, and control systems that regulate flow and composition of the feed. The focus is on the means of delivering substances to the process.
[B01J 2219/2419] for the reactants -- B01J 2219/2419 pertains to methods and apparatus for treating materials with gases or vapors, specifically focusing on processes where the treatment is applied to the reactants. This classification covers preparatory steps that enhance the efficiency of chemical reactions by modifying the state or composition of the reactants before they undergo the main reaction process.
[B01J 2219/242] for the catalysts -- B01J 2219/242 concerns the use of catalysts in separation processes, including catalytic membranes, adsorbents or reactors that enable selective removal or conversion of components from mixtures. It falls under performing operations, separating, and chemical or physical processes such as catalysis.
[B01J 2219/2422] Mixing means, e.g. fins or baffles attached to the monolith or placed in the channel -- This subclass covers devices that provide agitation inside a monolithic reactor or flow channel by means of fins, baffles or similar protrusions mounted on the structure or inserted into the channel, facilitating mixing of fluids during chemical or physical separation operations. It falls under the broader categories of performing operations, separating, and chemical or physical processes.
[B01J 2219/2423] Separation means, e.g. membrane inside the reactor -- B01J 2219/2423 covers reactors that incorporate a separation element, such as a membrane, within the reaction zone to enable simultaneous reaction and product separation. The classification applies to chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes where the membrane functions as a means of filtering, retaining or extracting substances while the reaction proceeds.
[B01J 2219/2424] Wall-flow filter, e.g. adjacent cells closed alternatively at their end to force the reactant stream through the walls of the monolith -- This entry covers wall-flow filters, in which adjacent cells of a monolithic structure are alternately closed at one end so that the reactant stream is forced to pass through the walls of the monolith, enhancing contact and mass transfer. It falls under chemical or physical processes involving separation by forced flow through porous walls.
[B01J 2219/2425] Construction materials -- B01J 2219/2425 addresses chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes used to prepare or modify construction materials, including catalytic, colloidal and separation techniques. The subclass falls under performing operations for separating within the broader category of chemical or physical processes.
[B01J 2219/2427] Catalysts -- The CPC subclass B01J 2219/2427, titled Catalysts, covers substances that accelerate chemical, physical or physico-chemical reactions without being permanently changed, including catalyst compositions, supports and preparation methods. It is placed under Performing Operations, Separating, Chemical or Physical Processes, and addresses catalytic materials used across a wide range of industrial processes.
[B01J 2219/2428] Catalysts coated on the surface of the monolith channels -- The CPC entry B01J 2219/2428 covers catalysts that are applied as a coating on the internal surfaces of monolithic channel structures used in chemical or physico-chemical reactors. It falls under the hierarchy of performing operations, separating, and chemical or physical processes such as catalysis.
[B01J 2219/2429] Nanocatalysts -- B01J 2219/2429 designates nanocatalysts, which are nanoscale materials that act as catalysts to accelerate chemical reactions. The class groups these agents within separating operations of chemical or physical processes, reflecting their role in enhancing reaction rates, selectivity, and efficiency through high surface-area nano-structures.
[B01J 2219/243] Catalyst in granular form in the channels -- B01J 2219/243 covers the use of granular catalysts positioned inside the channels of a reactor or separation device, where the catalyst particles remain fixed while reactants flow through. It pertains to packed-bed or similar configurations employed in chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes such as catalysis, adsorption or colloid reactions.
[B01J 2219/2432] Monoliths having catalytic activity on its own -- Monoliths that exhibit inherent catalytic activity without added catalyst, typically porous inorganic or ceramic structures that promote reactions as fluids pass through them, are classified under separating chemical or physical processes such as catalysis and colloid chemistry. This entry covers designs where the monolithic body itself serves as the active catalyst in processes like emission control, petrochemical reforming or wastewater treatment.
[B01J 2219/2433] of the monoliths -- The CPC subgroup B01J 2219/2433 covers chemical, physical or physico-chemical operations that employ monolithic structures-such as monolithic catalysts, adsorbents or reactors-as the primary medium for separating components, facilitating reactions, or performing combined reaction-separation processes. It includes designs, preparations and uses of these solid, often porous, monoliths in industrial and laboratory settings.
[B01J 2219/2434] Metals or alloys -- B01J 2219/2434 pertains to the use of metals or alloys as materials in chemical, physical, or physico-chemical processes. This classification is part of a detailed hierarchy focusing on operations and separating techniques within the broader field of chemistry and catalysis.
[B01J 2219/2435] Steel -- The B01J 2219/2435 subclass covers chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes applied to steel, such as its separation, purification, or surface treatment. It includes methods that modify steel properties by catalysts, colloidal actions or other chemical means, typically used in recycling, refining or preparing steel for further manufacturing steps.
[B01J 2219/2437] Metal oxides -- B01J 2219/2437 covers the use of metal oxides in chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes, especially for separation operations such as catalysis, adsorption or colloidal manipulation. It includes methods where metal-oxide materials act as reagents, catalysts or supports to effect the separation or transformation of substances.
[B01J 2219/2438] Ceramics -- B01J 2219/2438 covers processes for producing ceramic materials, including the synthesis, shaping, drying, firing and related physico-chemical transformations of inorganic non-metallic solids. It addresses methods that separate, modify or combine ceramic constituents using chemical or physical means such as precipitation, sol-gel, calcination or sintering.
[B01J 2219/2439] Glass -- B01J 2219/2439 covers chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes that involve glass, such as its use as a reaction vessel, filter, membrane or support in separation or catalytic operations. The class includes methods for preparing, treating, modifying or employing glass in these processes.
[B01J 2219/244] Plastics -- The subclass B01J 2219/244 covers separation processes that employ plastics as media, carriers, or components, such as filtration, adsorption, membrane and other physico-chemical techniques using polymeric materials. It includes the design, preparation and use of plastic-based devices and structures for separating mixtures in chemical or physical operations.
[B01J 2219/2441] Other constructional details -- B01J 2219/2441 covers constructional details of equipment and apparatus used in chemical, physical or physico-chemical separating processes, encompassing aspects such as housing, support structures, sealing arrangements and other mechanical features that are not otherwise classified. It falls under the broader categories of performing operations, separating and chemical or physical processes.
[B01J 2219/2443] Assembling means of monolith modules -- B01J 2219/2443 covers the equipment, fixtures, or methods used to assemble monolithic catalyst modules, such as structured carriers or honeycomb elements, for use in chemical, physical or physico-chemical separation processes. These assembling means facilitate the construction, joining, or mounting of monoliths within reactors or separation units.
[B01J 2219/2444] Size aspects -- B01J 2219/2444 covers methods and apparatuses that address size-related aspects in chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes, such as controlling particle or droplet size, size distribution, or size-dependent separation. It is used for inventions where the dimensions of the material influence the performance of a separating operation.
[B01J 2219/2445] Sizes -- B01J 2219/2445 designates processes and equipment that separate materials according to their size, including sieving, classification, screening, and other size-based separation methods used in chemical or physical operations. It falls under the hierarchy of Performing Operations → Separating → Chemical or Physical Processes, and covers techniques that isolate particles or fractions based on dimensional criteria.
[B01J 2219/2446] Cell density -- The CPC entry B01J 2219/2446 refers to methods, apparatuses and related techniques for determining or controlling the density of cells in a suspension or culture. It covers measurement of cell concentration, monitoring of growth, and processes that adjust cell density during biotechnological or chemical operations. The class falls under chemical or physical processes related to separating operations.
[B01J 2219/2448] Additional structures inserted in the channels -- The subclass B01J 2219/2448 covers devices or methods in which extra elements-such as spacers, supports, filters or functional inserts-are placed within the flow channels of separation apparatuses used for chemical or physical processes like catalysis, chromatography or membrane filtration, to modify flow characteristics, enhance contact area or provide additional functionality.
[B01J 2219/2449] Moving elements in the monolith reactor -- This subclass covers methods for moving fluids, gases, or solid particles inside a monolith reactor, such as transporting reactants through the pores or channels of the monolith during catalytic or separation processes. It includes designs for flow-control, distribution, and conveyance of reactive species within the structured catalyst body.
[B01J 2219/245] Plate-type reactors -- Plate-type reactors are modular reaction vessels that consist of a series of flat plates forming channels through which reactants flow, providing high surface-to-volume ratios for efficient heat and mass transfer in chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes such as catalysis or colloid chemistry.
[B01J 2219/2451] Geometry of the reactor -- B01J 2219/2451 covers the design and arrangement of reactors used in chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes, focusing on the geometry that influences mixing, heat transfer and reaction efficiency. It falls under separating operations in the performing operations section.
[B01J 2219/2453] Plates arranged in parallel -- The code B01J 2219/2453 covers configurations where flat plates are positioned parallel to each other to create channels or gaps that facilitate separation, reaction, or mass-transfer operations in chemical or physical processes. Such parallel-plate arrangements are employed in filters, reactors, chromatography devices and similar systems to improve phase contact.
[B01J 2219/2454] Plates arranged concentrically -- The CPC entry B01J 2219/2454 refers to arrangements in which plates are positioned concentrically for use in chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes such as separation, catalysis or colloid operations. It falls under the hierarchy Performing Operations → Separating → Chemical or Physical Processes.
[B01J 2219/2455] Plates arranged radially -- The B01J 2219/2455 class covers apparatus in which a series of plates is positioned radially around a central axis to form a cylindrical or disc-shaped reaction zone. This configuration is used for chemical or physico-chemical separating operations, such as catalytic or colloid processes, where fluids flow through the gaps between the plates.
[B01J 2219/2456] Geometry of the plates -- B01J 2219/2456 covers the design and geometry of plates employed in performing operations such as separation, catalysis, or other chemical and physical processes. It concerns the shape, size, spacing and arrangement of plate elements that affect fluid flow, mass transfer and reaction efficiency.
[B01J 2219/2458] Flat plates, i.e. plates which are not corrugated or otherwise structured, e.g. plates with cylindrical shape -- B01J 2219/2458 in the Cooperative Patent Classification refers to flat plates used in chemical processes that do not have any corrugation or additional structuring, including those with a cylindrical shape. These components are essential in various separation and reaction technologies for their simplicity and specific surface properties.
[B01J 2219/2459] Corrugated plates -- B01J 2219/2459 relates to corrugated plates used in chemical or physical processing equipment, especially in separation operations such as plate or packed columns where the corrugation creates turbulence and larger surface area to improve mass-transfer or catalytic performance.
[B01J 2219/246] Perforated plates -- B01J 2219/246 classifies perforated plates used as structural or flow-modifying elements in chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes, such as reactors, filters, or packed columns. The code covers plates that provide fluid distribution, gas-liquid contact or support for catalytic or colloidal actions. It is a subcategory of separating operations within the broader field of chemical and physical processes.
[B01J 2219/2461] Heat exchange aspects -- The CPC subclass B01J 2219/2461 deals with heat-exchange aspects of separation operations that involve chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes such as catalysis or colloid chemistry. It covers the design, control and integration of heat-transfer equipment and methods within these separation processes, including the effects of temperature management on efficiency and product quality.
[B01J 2219/2462] the reactants being in indirect heat exchange with a non reacting heat exchange medium -- The entry B01J 2219/2462 covers processes in which reactants are heated or cooled indirectly through a separate, non-reactive heat-exchange medium rather than direct contact, allowing temperature control without the heat-transfer fluid participating in the chemical reaction. This applies to chemical, physical or physico-chemical operations where indirect thermal exchange is used to influence reaction conditions.
[B01J 2219/2464] Independent temperature control in various sections of the reactor -- The CPC subclass B01J 2219/2464 covers independent temperature control of distinct zones within a reactor, allowing separate heating or cooling of sections to optimise reaction conditions. It falls under separating operations in chemical or physical processes.
[B01J 2219/2465] Two reactions in indirect heat exchange with each other -- The B01J 2219/2465 entry covers processes where two chemical or physical reactions are thermally coupled through indirect heat exchange, allowing each reaction to supply or receive heat without direct contact. This classification pertains to separating operations that rely on such heat-mediated interaction, a subset of broader chemical, physical or physico-chemical processing.
[B01J 2219/2466] The same reactant stream undergoing different reactions, endothermic or exothermic -- This CPC subclass covers processes where a single stream of reactants is split or subjected to multiple concurrent reactions that may be either endothermic or exothermic, typically within a single reactor or in sequential steps. It includes chemical or physico-chemical operations such as catalytic transformations, heat exchange, and reaction control within the same material flow.
[B01J 2219/2467] Additional heat exchange means, e.g. electric resistance heaters, coils -- The B01J 2219/2467 subgroup concerns auxiliary heat-exchange devices, such as electric resistance heaters or coils, that are employed to add heat in chemical or physical separation operations. These elements are used to raise temperature, accelerate reactions, improve solubility or promote phase changes within the primary process.
[B01J 2219/2469] Feeding means -- B01J 2219/2469 concerns feeding means employed in processes that separate chemical or physical substances, including catalytic or colloidal operations. It covers devices or methods for introducing reactants, solvents, or other materials into reactors or separation units.
[B01J 2219/247] Feeding means for the reactants -- B01J 2219/247 covers apparatus and methods used to introduce reactants into a chemical or physico-chemical process. It includes feeders, pumps, dosing units and related control mechanisms that ensure precise delivery of liquids, gases or solids to reactors, especially in catalytic or colloid reactions. The classification is part of the CPC hierarchy under performing operations, separating, and chemical or physical processes.
[B01J 2219/2471] Feeding means for the catalyst -- This subclass covers devices or arrangements that introduce a catalyst into a reaction zone, such as feeders, dispensers, or delivery systems, used in chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes like catalysis or colloid chemistry. It includes mechanisms for controlling the amount, flow rate or distribution of the catalyst during the operation of the process.
[B01J 2219/2472] the catalyst being exchangeable on inserts other than plates, e.g. in bags -- B01J 2219/2472 covers processes where the catalyst is placed on removable inserts other than plates, such as bags, allowing the catalyst to be exchanged during operation. It belongs to the hierarchy Performing Operations - Separating - Chemical or Physical Processes, encompassing catalytic and colloid chemistry methods.
[B01J 2219/2474] Mixing means, e.g. fins or baffles attached to the plates -- The entry B01J 2219/2474 covers mixing elements such as fins or baffles that are fixed to plates to promote agitation of fluids or powders during chemical, physical or physico-chemical operations, particularly in separating or catalytic processes. It specifies the design and arrangement of these attached mixing means to enhance turbulence and uniformity within the reaction or separation zone.
[B01J 2219/2475] Separation means, e.g. membranes inside the reactor -- The B01J 2219/2475 class covers reactors that incorporate separation devices, such as membranes, directly inside the reaction zone to allow simultaneous reaction and product separation. It applies to chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes where the separation means are integral to the reactor system.
[B01J 2219/2476] Construction materials -- B01J 2219/2476 pertains to the specific category of construction materials used in chemical, physical, or physico-chemical processes. This classification is part of a broader hierarchy that deals with performing operations, separating techniques, and general processes related to catalysis or colloid chemistry.
[B01J 2219/2477] of the catalysts -- B01J 2219/2477 pertains to the specific aspects and characteristics of catalysts used in chemical, physical, or physico-chemical processes. This classification code focuses on detailing properties, preparation methods, and applications of catalysts within these broad process categories.
[B01J 2219/2479] Catalysts coated on the surface of plates or inserts -- B01J 2219/2479 refers to catalysts that are applied as thin layers on the surfaces of plates or inserts used in chemical reactors, enabling heterogeneous catalytic reactions. These coated catalysts are employed in processes such as oxidation, reduction, or polymerisation where the active material is immobilized on a solid support.
[B01J 2219/248] Nanocatalysts -- The CPC subclass B01J 2219/248 covers nanocatalysts, i.e., catalytic substances whose active components are in the nanometre size range. [B01J 2219/2481] Catalysts in granular from between plates -- The CPC subclass B01J 2219/2481 designates processes that employ granular catalyst particles confined between two plates, allowing reactions or separations to occur within the inter-plate space. It is used for chemical, physical or physico-chemical operations such as catalysis, adsorption or filtration where a thin, controlled catalyst bed is required.
[B01J 2219/2482] Catalytically active foils -- Catalytically active foils are thin metal or support sheets coated with one or more catalysts that accelerate chemical reactions while acting as a separating medium, such as in filtration, membrane reactors, or gas-liquid contactors. They combine the mechanical function of a foil with catalytic activity to enhance conversion, selectivity or efficiency in chemical or physico-chemical processes.
[B01J 2219/2483] of the plates -- B01J 2219/2483 refers to specific aspects of chemical, physical, or physico-chemical processes involving the use of plates, such as those used in catalysis or colloid chemistry. [B01J 2219/249] Plastics -- B01J 2219/249 covers chemical or physico-chemical processes used to produce, modify or treat plastics, including polymerisation, blending, compounding and subsequent separation or purification steps.
[B01J 2219/2491] Other constructional details -- B01J 2219/2491 covers various structural and design features of apparatus used in chemical or physical separation processes that are not specifically classified elsewhere, such as atypical arrangements, auxiliary components, or unique construction details of reactors, columns, filters or related equipment. [B01J 2219/2492] Assembling means -- B01J 2219/2492 relates to assembling means employed in chemical or physical separation operations, covering devices, fixtures or arrangements that bring together components, media or elements during the process. [B01J 2219/2495] the plates being assembled interchangeably or in a disposable way -- B01J 2219/2495 concerns separation operations in which the plates or support elements are designed to be interchangeable or disposable, allowing them to be quickly replaced, reconfigured or discarded after use. [B01J 2219/2497] Size aspects, i.e. concrete sizes are being mentioned in the classified document -- B01J 2219/2497 covers the consideration of concrete sizes of particles, droplets or other entities in chemical or physical separation processes such as catalysis or colloid chemistry. It falls under performing operations - separating - chemical or physical processes. The code is used when the classified document explicitly mentions size dimensions relevant to the process.

This sub-group falls under chemical or physico-chemical processes for separating mixtures.
[B01J 2219/302] Basic shape of the elements -- B01J 2219/302 covers the fundamental geometrical configuration of particles or elements used in chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes, such as spherical, rod-like, plate-like or other basic shapes that affect reaction or separation behavior. It is relevant for methods involving catalysis, colloid chemistry or other processes where shape influences performance.
[B01J 2219/30203] Saddle -- Saddle denotes a curved, saddle-shaped reaction zone or apparatus designed to improve mixing, mass-transfer or heat-transfer in chemical or physical operations such as catalytic or colloidal processes. The geometry creates distinct flow patterns that enhance contact between reacting phases.
[B01J 2219/30207] Sphere -- B01J2219/30207 covers the use of spherical particles as carriers or media in chemical or physical separation processes, including beads, microspheres and other round carriers employed for catalysis, filtration or chromatography.
[B01J 2219/30211] Egg, ovoid or ellipse -- The entry B01J 2219/30211 covers separation techniques that are tailored to particles or objects having an egg, ovoid or ellipse shape, detailing methods and equipment for isolating such items from mixtures in chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes. It is placed under the broader CPC categories of performing operations and separating.
[B01J 2219/30215] Toroid or ring -- The B01J 2219/30215 subclass covers processes that employ a toroid or ring-shaped apparatus for chemical, physical or physico-chemical operations, particularly in separation or catalytic actions. [B01J 2219/30219] Disk -- The B01J 2219/30219 subclass covers separating operations that use a disk as the working element, for example rotating-disk centrifuges, disc filters or other devices where a flat or disc-shaped component effects phase or particle separation in chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes.
[B01J 2219/30223] Cylinder -- B01J 2219/30223 designates cylinders employed in separating operations that involve chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes such as catalysis or colloid chemistry. [B01J 2219/30238] Tetrahedron -- B01J 2219/30238 covers catalytic chemical or physico-chemical processes that use tetrahedral-structured catalysts, typically tetrahedral coordination compounds or complexes, as the active species. [B01J 2219/30242] Star -- B01J 2219/30242 groups inventions that use a star-shaped arrangement or component in a chemical or physical separation operation, such as star-configured reactors, mixers, columns or membranes that create radial flow or enhanced contact to improve separating processes.
[B01J 2219/30246] Square or square-derived -- B01J 2219/30246 covers methods and apparatus that employ square or square-derived compounds, such as macrocycles or planar polymers, for separating chemical mixtures. [B01J 2219/30249] Cube -- The "Cube" entry (B01J 2219/30249) covers separation methods that employ cubic-shaped particles, matrices or structures to facilitate chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes such as catalysis, adsorption or colloidal manipulation. By using the geometry of cubes, the technique improves contact area and flow dynamics, enabling more efficient separation of substances in batch or continuous operations.
[B01J 2219/30253] Pyramid -- The code B01J 2219/30253 designates pyramid-shaped apparatuses employed in chemical or physical separation operations, typically within catalytic or colloid processes. [B01J 2219/30257] Wire -- The CPC entry B01J 2219/30257 "Wire" pertains to the use of wire as a component in chemical or physical separation operations, such as filtration media, electrodes, or support structures that aid in catalysis, colloid handling, or other physico-chemical processes. It classifies wire-based devices that facilitate the division, removal, or manipulation of substances in a process stream.
[B01J 2219/30261] twisted -- The CPC subclass B01J 2219/30261, titled "twisted," relates to chemical or physical separation processes that employ twisted elements such as helically wound tubes, twisted membranes, or other twisted geometries to enhance mixing, mass transfer, or filtration efficiency. It falls under Performing Operations → Separating → Chemical or Physical Processes, covering designs where a twisted configuration is integral to the separation method.
[B01J 2219/30265] Spiral -- B01J 2219/30265 "Spiral" covers spiral-shaped equipment used to separate mixtures by exploiting differences in density, solubility or affinity, such as spiral column chromatography or spiral liquid-liquid extraction devices. [B01J 2219/30269] Brush -- B01J 2219/30269 covers brushes employed in chemical or physical operations such as stirring, cleaning, or surface treatment during separation processes. The entry pertains to the design, material, or configuration of brushes used to facilitate mixing, particle removal, or contact enhancement in such procedures.
[B01J 2219/30273] Cross -- The code B01J 2219/30273 covers separation processes where two streams intersect or cross one another, such as cross-flow filtration, counter-current exchange, or other arrangements in which phases move in opposite or intersecting directions to achieve separation. [B01J 2219/30276] Sheet -- The CPC subclass B01J 2219/30276 falls under Performing Operations → Separating → Chemical or Physical Processes, covering general chemical, physical or physico-chemical procedures. [B01J 2219/3028] stretched -- The B01J 2219/3028 subclass covers separation techniques that employ stretching of materials such as membranes, films or fibers to create or enhance selective pathways for chemical or physical separation. It includes processes where mechanical elongation is used to modify porosity or surface structure for improved catalytic, filtration or colloidal performance.
[B01J 2219/30284] twisted -- A process that induces or utilizes a twisted configuration of a material-such as twisting fibers, ribbons, nanostructures or other elongated entities-during a chemical or physical separation operation, where the twist modifies the material's properties or aids in its isolation.
[B01J 2219/30288] folded -- The CPC entry B01J 2219/30288 folded covers chemical or physical operations in which a material is deliberately folded, such as folding of sheets, films or membranes during preparation, treatment or use. It includes methods that create a folded configuration to affect surface area, mechanical strength or functional performance, and the handling of folded structures in catalytic, colloidal or other physico-chemical processes.
[B01J 2219/30292] rolled up -- The code refers to processes where a strip, sheet or fabric used for separation is rolled up, either as a pre-formed roll for later deployment or as a rolled configuration during operation, enabling compact handling and controlled release in chemical or physical separation systems. This includes rolled-up filter media, adsorbent mats or membrane sheets employed in catalytic, colloidal or other physico-chemical separations.
[B01J 2219/30296] Other shapes -- The subgroup B01J 2219/30296 covers separating operations in chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes that produce the product in shapes other than the usual powders, granules or films, such as beads, pellets, fibers, or irregular forms, and includes the corresponding equipment and methods.
[B01J 2219/304] Composition or microstructure of the elements -- The CPC subclass B01J 2219/304 concerns the composition and microstructure of materials used in separation operations that rely on chemical, physical or physico-chemical mechanisms, including catalysts, colloids and related substances. [B01J 2219/30408] Metal -- B01J 2219/30408 pertains to the use of metals in chemical, physical, or physico-chemical processes. This classification specifically addresses the role of metals as materials or components within these processes, often involving catalysis or colloidal systems.

[B01J 2219/3045] Cork -- B01J 2219/3045 covers the use of cork as a material in separation processes, such as filtration, sorption, or as a support medium for catalysts, wherein its porous structure is exploited for chemical or physical separation tasks.

[B01J 2219/30475] comprising catalytically active material -- B01J 2219/30475 covers processes that use a catalytically active material to facilitate chemical or physical transformations, typically within separation operations such as catalysis or colloid chemistry. [B01J 2219/30483] Fibrous materials -- The B01J 2219/30483 subclass covers fibrous materials employed in separation operations that involve chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes. It includes filters, membranes, adsorbent fibres and other textile-based media used to separate or purify substances, often after catalytic or colloidal treatment. This entry falls under the broader categories of performing operations, separating, and chemical or physical processes.
[B01J 2219/30491] Foam like materials -- The CPC subclass B01J 2219/30491 covers inventions involving foam-like materials, including their preparation, treatment, or use in separation operations that rely on chemical, physical or physico-chemical actions such as catalysis or colloidal phenomena. It sits within the hierarchy of performing operations, separating, and general chemical or physical processes.
[B01J 2219/308] filling or discharging the elements into or from packed columns -- The CPC entry B01J 2219/308 covers techniques for placing or removing substances-liquids, gases, or solids-into or from packed columns used in separation operations such as chromatography, absorption or catalysis. It includes the equipment and procedural steps required for efficient filling or discharge of the column elements.
[B01J 2219/3081] Orientation of the packing elements within the column or vessel -- The entry B01J 2219/3081 covers the way packing elements-such as structured or random packings-are oriented inside a column or vessel used for separation operations. It addresses the arrangement of these internals to influence flow distribution, mass-transfer efficiency and pressure-drop characteristics.
[B01J 2219/3083] Random or dumped packing elements -- The code B01J 2219/3083 covers separation operations that employ packing elements arranged without a fixed pattern, such as randomly placed or dumped particles in columns or beds. These irregularly positioned packings are used in chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes like chromatography, absorption or distillation to enhance contact between phases.
[B01J 2219/3085] Ordered or stacked packing elements -- The CPC subclass B01J 2219/3085 covers ordered or stacked packing elements employed in chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes such as catalysis or colloid chemistry, where the elements are arranged in structured layers or arrays to facilitate separation or reaction steps. It falls under Performing Operations → Separating → Chemical or Physical Processes.
[B01J 2219/3086] Filling of the packing elements into the column or vessel, e.g. using a tube -- The subclass B01J 2219/3086 addresses methods for loading packing elements into a column or vessel, for example by feeding them through a tube, as part of separation operations that involve chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes.
[B01J 2219/3088] Emptying of the packing elements from the column or vessel, e.g. using a tube -- B01J 2219/3088 covers methods for removing the packing material from a separation column or vessel, such as by passing a tube through the packing to dislodge and extract it. It falls under performing operations → separating → chemical or physical processes, relating to the cleaning or regeneration of packed reactors and columns.
[B01J 2219/31] Size details -- B01J 2219/31 covers the specification and control of particle size in chemical or physical processes, especially those used for separating substances. It includes methods, apparatus and parameters for defining size ranges, distributions, and adjustments to achieve desired separation performance. This entry falls under the broader categories of performing operations, separating and chemical or physical processes.
[B01J 2219/312] Sizes -- B01J 2219/312 addresses techniques for measuring, controlling, or utilizing the size of particles, droplets, or other entities in chemical or physical separation processes. It includes methods for size classification, fractionation, and monitoring of size distributions in catalytic, colloidal, or related systems.
[B01J 2219/315] Two or more types of packing elements or packing elements of different sizes present in the column -- The subclass pertains to separation columns that contain two or more kinds of packing elements or packings of different sizes, employed to enhance mass-transfer efficiency, pressure drop, or flow distribution in chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes such as distillation, absorption, or chromatography.
[B01J 2219/318] Manufacturing aspects -- B01J 2219/318 refers to manufacturing aspects of chemical or physical separations, including processes such as catalysis, colloid chemistry and other physico-chemical operations.
