The code is used to classify inventions that detail the production, equipment or methods for carrying out these separation steps.
[B01J 2219/3181] Pleating -- B01J 2219/3181 covers methods for pleating, i.e., forming regular folds or corrugations in sheets, films or other substrates as part of a chemical or physical processing operation. The entry is placed under performing operations, separating, and chemical or physico-chemical processes, and is used for technologies that create pleated structures for filtration, catalyst supports or material handling.

[B01J 2219/3185] Pressing -- Pressing is a mechanical operation that applies pressure to a material in order to separate components, extract liquids, or reshape solids. [B01J 2219/3186] Sintering -- B01J 2219/3186 designates the sintering process, a chemical or physical operation in which powdered material is heated below its melting point to cause particles to bond and densify, often used to produce ceramics, metals, or composites. The technique falls under the CPC hierarchy performing operations - separating - chemical or physical processes.
[B01J 2219/3188] Extruding -- B01J 2219/3188 defines extrusion as a continuous process where a material is forced through a shaped opening to produce a product of uniform cross-section, often used for polymers, metals or composites in chemical and physical processing. [B01J 2219/32] Details relating to packing elements in the form of grids or built-up elements for forming a unit of module inside the apparatus for mass or heat transfer -- The subclass B01J 2219/32 concerns the design and specifications of packing elements that are formed as grids or built-up structures and assembled as a single module within columns, reactors or other apparatuses used for mass- or heat-transfer operations in chemical, physical or physico-chemical separation processes.
[B01J 2219/322] Basic shape of the elements -- The CPC entry B01J 2219/322 covers the fundamental geometric configuration of individual chemical elements as they appear in processes that involve separation, catalysis or colloid chemistry. It pertains to the basic shape of the elements used in chemical, physical or physico-chemical operations.
[B01J 2219/32203] Sheets -- The B01J 2219/32203 entry covers separating operations that employ sheet-type media, such as filtration membranes, adsorbent sheets or colloidal layers, to remove or isolate components from chemical or physical mixtures. It is used for processes that rely on chemical, physical or physico-chemical interactions within sheet structures.
[B01J 2219/32206] Flat sheets -- B01J 2219/32206 concerns the use of flat sheets as the separating medium in chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes. It includes methods and apparatuses where planar membranes, filters or sorbent sheets effect separation of gases, liquids or dissolved substances, such as filtration, dialysis or selective sorption.
[B01J 2219/3221] Corrugated sheets -- B01J 2219/3221 classifies processes that generate corrugated sheets through chemical, physical or physico-chemical means, falling under the broader categories of separating operations and performing operations in chemical or physical processing such as catalysis or colloid chemistry.
[B01J 2219/32213] Plurality of essentially parallel sheets -- B01J 2219/32213 covers processes that employ a plurality of essentially parallel sheets, such as stacked or layered plates, to conduct chemical, physical or physico-chemical operations like separation, reaction or heat transfer. The parallel arrangement provides a large surface area and uniform flow paths, improving efficiency in reactors, filters or similar equipment.
[B01J 2219/32217] with sheets having corrugations which intersect at an angle of 90 degrees -- This subclass covers separation methods that employ sheet-like media whose corrugated ridges intersect at right angles, creating a grid-like structure to enhance flow and filtration efficiency. The intersecting corrugations provide a defined pathway for liquids or gases, improving the physical or physico-chemical separation of components.
[B01J 2219/3222] with sheets having corrugations which intersect at an angle different from 90 degrees -- This class relates to chemical, physical or physico-chemical operations that use sheets whose corrugations intersect at angles other than right angles, such as diagonally or obliquely corrugated sheets, as part of the reaction environment or support structure. It covers processes where such angled corrugated sheets improve mixing, mass transfer or catalytic contact.
[B01J 2219/32224] characterised by the orientation of the sheet -- This CPC subclass (B01J 2219/32224) covers chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes in which the orientation of a sheet-such as a membrane, film or layered material-is a defining feature of the operation, influencing separation, catalytic or colloidal behavior. It includes methods that exploit anisotropic sheet arrangements to control mass transfer, reaction sites or filtration characteristics.
[B01J 2219/32227] Vertical orientation -- The code B01J 2219/32227 covers processes in which a vertical orientation of a material, field, or equipment is a defining feature, such as vertically aligned reactors, settling columns, or oriented fields used to aid separation or catalytic reactions. This orientation is used to enhance fluid flow, mass-transfer or catalytic efficiency in chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes.
[B01J 2219/32237] Sheets comprising apertures or perforations -- The B01J 2219/32237 entry refers to sheets that contain apertures or perforations, such as perforated plates or porous membranes, employed as filtering, supporting or separating elements in chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes. [B01J 2219/32241] Louvres -- The CPC entry B01J 2219/32241 "Louvers" covers the use of adjustable slats or louvers to control the flow of gases, liquids or vapours in chemical or physical separation operations, such as reactors, dryers, filters or catalytic units. It addresses the design, arrangement and actuation of these elements to regulate pressure drop, mixing or residence time within the process.

It falls under chemical or physico-chemical operations involving separation.

[B01J 2219/32251] Dimples, bossages, protrusions -- B01J 2219/32251 covers the incorporation of surface textures such as dimples, bossages or protrusions on separators, reactors or related equipment to modify flow, enhance mixing or improve mass-transfer efficiency during chemical or physical separation operations.

It includes information on dimensions, coatings, porosity, surface treatment and other physical or chemical characteristics that affect performance.
[B01J 2219/32258] Details relating to the extremities of the sheets, such as a change in corrugation geometry or sawtooth edges -- This subclass deals with modifications to the edges of sheets used in separating operations, focusing on changes in corrugation pattern or incorporation of saw-tooth profiles that affect handling or separation efficiency. It includes specifications of edge geometry, such as altered wave forms or serrated cuts, that influence the physical or chemical separation of sheet-like materials.
[B01J 2219/32262] Dimensions or size aspects -- The subclass B01J 2219/32262 addresses the dimensional and size-related characteristics of materials involved in separation operations, such as particle size distribution, pore dimensions, film thickness or channel geometry. It pertains to chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes where controlling these size aspects influences the efficiency or selectivity of the separation.
[B01J 2219/32265] characterised by the orientation of blocks of sheets -- This subclass covers separation processes that rely on controlling the orientation of blocks of sheets, such as aligned membranes or layered filters, to improve the efficiency of chemical, physical or physico-chemical separations. It includes methods where the direction or angle of sheet stacks is a defining feature of the operation.
[B01J 2219/32268] relating to blocks in the same horizontal level -- The Cooperative Patent Classification code B01J 2219/32268 pertains to chemical, physical, or physico-chemical processes involving blocks positioned at the same horizontal level. This classification is used by the European Patent Office and the United States Patent and Trademark Office for detailed categorization of inventions related to these specific process configurations.
[B01J 2219/32272] relating to blocks in superimposed layers -- The code covers separation methods that employ discrete block structures formed within superimposed layers of a material, such as multilayer membranes or composites, to create selective pathways or barriers for chemical or physical species. These layered blocks enable controlled transport, filtration or retention of components based on size, charge or affinity, and are applied in processes ranging from catalysis to colloidal separation.

It includes mechanical fastening, welding, adhesive bonding, or other techniques to secure the components in place within the apparatus.

[B01J 2219/3284] Pressing -- Pressing involves applying pressure to solids, semi-solids or powders to compact, shape, extrude or extract material, often as a step in chemical or physical processing such as catalyst preparation, pelletisation or solvent removal.

[B01J 2219/3287] Extruding -- Extruding is a continuous process that forces a material, such as polymer, metal or foodstuff, through a shaped die to produce a profile of uniform cross-section, often separating it from the bulk feed. It is employed in manufacturing rods, sheets, tubes and textured products, and falls under the chemical or physical separating operations of the CPC.
[B01J 2219/3288] Punching -- Punching is a mechanical separating operation in which a tool drives through a workpiece to cut out a shape or create a hole, commonly used in metalworking, plastics and other materials. [B01J 2219/33] Details relating to the packing elements in general -- B01J 2219/33 covers documentation on packing elements used in separation processes, including their design, material composition, geometry and placement in columns or reactors. [B01J 2219/3306] Dimensions or size aspects -- This subclass groups inventions that deal with the dimensional or size-related features of materials processed in separating operations, including the measurement, control, adjustment or specification of particle, droplet, bubble or other constituent sizes in chemical, physical or physico-chemical separation processes.
[B01J 2219/3313] Revamping -- Revamping refers to the regeneration or restoration of spent catalysts, adsorbents or other active materials used in separating processes, typically involving chemical, thermal or physico-chemical treatments that restore their original activity. It is classified under the broad category of performing operations, specifically separating, and deals with chemical or physical processes such as catalysis and colloid chemistry.
[B01J 2219/332] Details relating to the flow of the phases -- This subclass covers information on the movement and distribution of different phases - such as liquids, gases, or solids - within chemical or physical processes. It includes descriptions of flow patterns, phase mixing, separation, and related transport phenomena in reactors, mixers, or other equipment.
[B01J 2219/3322] Co-current flow -- Co-current flow denotes a separation technique where the material to be separated and the fluid medium travel together in the same direction, as employed in hydrocyclones and similar fluid-based separators. This arrangement provides continuous contact and improves particle or phase separation efficiency.
[B01J 2219/3325] Counter-current flow -- Counter-current flow refers to the arrangement of two streams moving in opposite directions to maximize contact and transfer of mass, heat, or momentum between them. This configuration is employed in separation, reaction and catalysis processes to improve efficiency and control of the overall operation.
[B01J 2219/3327] Cross-current flow -- B01J 2219/3327 refers to cross-current flow, a method where two streams move in opposite directions and exchange mass, heat, or momentum while remaining physically separate. It is used in chemical, physical or physico-chemical processes such as separation, extraction, and catalytic reactions where counter-flow enhances efficiency.
[B01J 2/22] by pressing in moulds or between rollers -- B01J 2/22 covers granulation processes or apparatuses that form particles by applying pressure in moulds or between rollers, creating shaped granules from powders, pastes, or slurries.
[B01J 2220/00] Aspects relating to sorbent materials -- B01J 2220/00 covers aspects relating to sorbent materials used in separating chemical or physical processes such as catalysis and colloid chemistry. [B01J 2220/40] Aspects relating to the composition of sorbent or filter aid materials -- B01J 2220/40 covers inventions that deal with the composition of sorbent or filter-aid materials used in separation operations, such as chemical or physical processes including catalysis and colloid chemistry. It includes formulations, additives, or modifications that improve the performance, stability, or selectivity of the sorbent material.
[B01J 2220/42] Materials comprising a mixture of inorganic materials -- The subclass B01J 2220/42 covers sorbent compositions that consist of mixtures of inorganic substances, such as oxides, carbides or silicates, employed in chemical or physical separation operations including catalysis, adsorption and colloid-based processes.
[B01J 2220/44] Materials comprising a mixture of organic materials -- The code B01J 2220/44 covers sorbent materials that consist of mixtures of organic components, such as blended polymers, functionalized organic particles or organic-inorganic hybrids, used in separation operations like adsorption, catalysis or colloidal processes. It includes formulations where the organic phases provide the primary sorbing or catalytic function.
[B01J 2220/445] comprising a mixture of polymers -- This CPC subgroup concerns sorbent materials that contain a mixture of two or more polymers, either physically blended, interpenetrating or co-polymerized, to achieve specific separation, catalytic or colloidal properties. The mixed-polymer sorbents are used in processes that involve chemical or physical separation steps.
[B01J 2220/46] Materials comprising a mixture of inorganic and organic materials -- B01J 2220/46 covers sorbent materials that are formed from a combination of inorganic and organic components, such as hybrid composites or coated particles, where the inorganic phase provides structural or catalytic stability and the organic phase supplies functional groups for selective adsorption. This subclass includes formulations in which the two phases are physically mixed, chemically bonded, or interpenetrated to enhance separation performance in catalytic, filtration, or ion-exchange processes.
[B01J 2220/48] Sorbents characterised by the starting material used for their preparation -- This subclass covers sorbent materials identified by the original raw material from which they are prepared, such as polymers, minerals, biomass or other precursors, and used in separation operations like adsorption, ion exchange or filtration. It is part of the chemical or physical processes for separating substances, listed under CPC code B01J 2220/48.
[B01J 2220/4806] the starting material being of inorganic character -- This subclass concerns separation techniques that employ inorganic starting materials such as metal oxides, minerals or other non-organic sorbents in chemical or physical processes like catalysis or colloidal chemistry. It addresses the use of these inorganic sorbents for adsorbing, filtering or otherwise separating target substances.
[B01J 2220/4812] the starting material being of organic character -- This subclass covers separation processes that employ sorbent materials whose starting material is organic in nature, including chemical or physical methods such as catalysis and colloid chemistry. It relates to the use of organic-based sorbents in performing operations.
[B01J 2220/4818] Natural rubber -- Natural rubber refers to a latex-derived polymer employed as a sorbent or support material in separation and catalytic processes. Its elastic, porous and chemically resistant properties make it suitable for immobilizing active phases, filtering, or adsorbing target compounds.
[B01J 2220/4825] Polysaccharides or cellulose materials, e.g. starch, chitin, sawdust, wood, straw, cotton -- B01J 2220/4825 defines sorbent materials made from polysaccharides or cellulose, including starch, chitin, sawdust, wood, straw and cotton, used in chemical or physical separation operations. [B01J 2220/4831] having been subjected to further processing, e.g. paper, cellulose pulp -- B01J 2220/4831 covers sorbent materials that have been further processed after their initial preparation, such as being formed into paper, cellulose pulp or similar shaped products, for use in separation operations. This subclass falls under chemical or physical processes involving sorbents within the broader CPC hierarchy of performing operations and separating.
[B01J 2220/4837] Lignin -- B01J 2220/4837 covers the use of lignin as a sorbent material in chemical or physical separation processes, including catalytic or colloidal applications. It falls within the CPC hierarchy of performing operations, separating, and chemical or physical processes.
[B01J 2220/4843] Algae, aquatic plants or sea vegetals, e.g. seeweeds, eelgrass -- The B01J 2220/4843 code covers sorbent materials that are derived from or incorporate algae, aquatic plants or sea vegetals such as seweeds and eelgrass, used in separating operations that employ chemical or physical processes like catalysis or colloid chemistry.
[B01J 2220/485] Plants or land vegetals, e.g. cereals, wheat, corn, rice, sphagnum, peat moss -- B01J 2220/485 covers sorbent materials derived from plants or land vegetals such as cereals, wheat, corn, rice, sphagnum and peat moss, intended for use in chemical or physical separation processes, including catalysis and colloid chemistry.
[B01J 2220/4856] Proteins, DNA -- The subclass B01J 2220/4856 concerns sorbent materials that are proteins or DNA, used in chemical or physical separation methods such as chromatography, filtration or biosorption. [B01J 2220/4862] Feathers -- This subclass covers the use of feathers as sorbent or filter media in chemical or physical separation operations, such as adsorption, catalysis, or colloid processes.

[B01J 2220/4875] the starting material being a waste, residue or of undefined composition -- This subclass covers separation operations that employ sorbent materials when the feedstock is waste, a residue, or of unknown composition, focusing on chemical or physical processes such as sorption, catalysis or colloid chemistry. It is part of the CPC hierarchy under Performing Operations, Separating, Chemical or Physical Processes, Aspects relating to sorbent materials.
[B01J 2220/4881] Residues from shells, e.g. eggshells, mollusk shells -- B01J 2220/4881 relates to the use of shell-derived residues such as eggshells or mollusk shells as sorbents or catalysts in separation, catalysis or other chemical-physical processes.
[B01J 2220/4887] Residues, wastes, e.g. garbage, municipal or industrial sludges, compost, animal manure -- The B01J 2220/4887 subclass covers processes and methods that treat or manage solid residues and wastes such as garbage, municipal or industrial sludges, compost and animal manure, with emphasis on the use of sorbent materials for separation or purification. It includes chemical or physical operations that convert, immobilise or recover components from these waste streams.
[B01J 2220/4893] Residues derived from used synthetic products, e.g. rubber from used tyres -- The code B01J 2220/4893 refers to processes that treat residues obtained from used synthetic products such as reclaimed rubber from tyres, addressing their separation, handling or reuse. It falls under performing operations for separating chemical or physical processes, specifically concerning sorbent-material aspects.
[B01J 2220/49] Materials comprising an indicator, e.g. colour indicator, pH-indicator -- B01J 2220/49 covers sorbent materials that incorporate an indicator, such as a colour or pH-sensitive compound, enabling visual detection of adsorption or reaction events during separating operations in chemical or physical processes.
[B01J 2220/50] Aspects relating to the use of sorbent or filter aid materials -- B01J 2220/50 covers inventions that focus on the use of sorbent or filter-aid substances to aid separation in chemical or physical processes, including catalysis and colloid chemistry, where the materials help capture, retain, or remove target components.
[B01J 2220/52] Sorbents specially adapted for preparative chromatography -- B01J 2220/52 covers sorbent materials that are specifically designed for use in preparative chromatography, where large-scale separation of chemical mixtures is required. These sorbents are engineered to handle high sample loads, provide selective retention, and enable efficient recovery of target compounds in industrial or laboratory purification processes.
[B01J 2220/54] Sorbents specially adapted for analytical or investigative chromatography -- B01J 2220/54 covers sorbents that are specially designed for analytical or investigative chromatography, including stationary phases, coated particles, monoliths or membranes optimized for selectivity, capacity or detection in techniques such as thin-layer, gas or liquid chromatography.
[B01J 2220/56] Use in the form of a bed -- B01J 2220/56 covers the use of sorbent materials arranged as a bed-packed, fixed or fluidised for performing separation operations such as adsorption, catalysis or ion exchange. The classification applies when the sorbent is employed in a bed form to facilitate chemical or physical processes that isolate or transform components.
[B01J 2220/58] Use in a single column -- The entry covers the use of sorbent materials in a single-column arrangement for chemical or physical separation processes, such as catalysis or colloid chemistry, where a single column performs the sorption and/or reaction step.
[B01J 2220/60] Use in several different columns -- B01J 2220/60 pertains to the use of sorbent materials in more than one column in a chemical or physical separation operation, such as multi-bed adsorption or ion-exchange processes. It covers configurations where several columns function sequentially or in parallel to enhance capacity, efficiency, or regeneration of the sorbent.
[B01J 2220/603] serially disposed columns -- The B01J 2220/603 entry covers arrangements of two or more separation columns positioned one after another so that the effluent of one column feeds directly into the next, enabling staged interaction with sorbent materials. It pertains to performing operations that separate substances through chemical or physical means such as catalysis or colloid chemistry, with a focus on the design and use of serially disposed columns.
[B01J 2220/606] parallel disposed columns -- B01J 2220/606 pertains to separation apparatus in which two or more columns are arranged side-by-side and operated in parallel, enabling simultaneous use of identical or different sorbent beds for continuous or batch processing of liquids or gases.
[B01J 2220/62] In a cartridge -- B01J 2220/62 refers to sorbent materials that are positioned inside a cartridge for use in separating mixtures by chemical or physical means such as adsorption, ion exchange or catalysis. The entry covers the design, composition and functional aspects of the cartridge-contained sorbent, including its support matrix and any modifications that enhance selectivity or capacity. It falls under the broader categories of performing operations, separating and chemical or physical processes.
[B01J 2220/64] In a syringe, pipette, e.g. tip or in a tube, e.g. test-tube or u-shape tube -- This subgroup concerns sorbent materials that are integrated into or used with syringes, pipette tips, test tubes or U-shaped tubes for separation processes, covering their preparation, application and handling within these containers for chemical or physical separations.
[B01J 2220/66] Other type of housings or containers not covered by -- It relates to sorbent-material based equipment where the housing or container does not fall under the more specific categories enumerated in the CPC, such as atypical shapes, composite structures, or multi-functional vessels used in separation processes like catalysis or colloid chemistry.
[B01J 2220/68] Superabsorbents -- Superabsorbents are polymeric sorbent materials capable of taking up and retaining large quantities of liquid relative to their own mass, often employed in hygiene products, agricultural soil conditioners and spill control. Their function involves chemical or physical absorption processes that aid separation and water management.
[B01J 2220/80] Aspects related to sorbents specially adapted for preparative, analytical or investigative chromatography -- B01J 2220/80 covers sorbent materials specially designed for use in preparative, analytical or investigative chromatography. It falls under the broader CPC hierarchy of performing operations, separating, chemical or physical processes such as catalysis or colloid chemistry. The code addresses aspects of sorbents adapted for chromatographic separation tasks.
[B01J 2220/82] Shaped bodies, e.g. monoliths, plugs, tubes, continuous beds -- B01J 2220/82 covers shaped sorbent bodies such as monoliths, plugs, tubes and continuous beds used in chemical or physical separation processes. These structures provide a fixed geometry for catalytic, ion-exchange or adsorption reactions, often engineered to improve flow distribution and mass-transfer efficiency. The subclass falls under performing operations, separating, and sorbent-material aspects of the CPC.
[B01J 2220/825] comprising a cladding or external coating -- B01J 2220/825 concerns sorbent materials that include a protective cladding or external coating. The entry covers coated sorbents used in separating operations, such as catalysis or colloid chemistry, where the coating enhances durability, selectivity or resistance to fouling. It falls under the broader categories of performing operations, separating, and chemical or physical processes.
[B01J 2220/84] Capillaries -- Capillaries are narrow tubes used as sorbent supports for separation processes, providing high surface-area channels for catalysis, chromatography or colloid interactions. The entry covers the design, materials and use of capillaries in chemical or physical separating operations.
[B01J 2220/86] Sorbents applied to inner surfaces of columns or capillaries -- This subclass covers sorbent materials that are applied as coatings to the interior surfaces of columns or capillaries used in separation processes such as chromatography. It includes any method of attaching the sorbent to the walls to form the stationary phase for chemical or physical separations.
[B01J 2229/00] Aspects of molecular sieve catalysts not covered by -- B01J 2229/00 pertains to specific characteristics or aspects of molecular sieve catalysts that are not addressed in more general classifications. This includes unique properties, preparation methods, and applications of these catalysts outside the standard scope.
[B01J 2229/10] After treatment, characterised by the effect to be obtained -- B01J 2229/10 relates to post-treatment of molecular-sieve catalysts where the modification is defined by the intended effect, such as improved activity, selectivity, stability or resistance to deactivation. The entry covers any chemical, thermal or physical step applied after synthesis that is chosen on the basis of the desired performance rather than the specific reagent or condition.
[B01J 2229/12] to alter the outside of the crystallites, e.g. selectivation -- B01J 2229/12 pertains to methods for modifying the external surfaces of molecular sieve catalysts, such as through selectivation, to enhance or alter their catalytic properties. This classification is part of a detailed hierarchy focusing on chemical and physical processes, specifically addressing aspects not covered by broader categories.
[B01J 2229/123] in order to deactivate outer surface -- This subgroup covers techniques for deliberately deactivating the external surface of molecular-sieve catalysts, for example by poisoning, coating, or heat treatment, to prevent undesired reactions while preserving internal activity. It falls under separating operations involving chemical or physical processes such as catalysis.
[B01J 2229/126] in order to reduce the pore-mouth size -- The entry covers techniques for reducing the pore-mouth diameter of molecular-sieve catalysts, typically to improve selectivity in catalytic separations. It includes modifications of catalyst structure or treatment processes that narrow the entrance openings without altering the internal pore system, fitting within the broader category of chemical or physical processes for separating substances.
[B01J 2229/14] to alter the inside of the molecular sieve channels -- This subclass deals with techniques for modifying the internal surface or geometry of molecular-sieve channels, such as ion exchange, impregnation, functionalisation or coating, to adjust their catalytic or adsorption behaviour. The methods are intended for use in separating operations and chemical or physical processes involving catalysts and are not covered by other molecular-sieve catalyst subclasses.
[B01J 2229/16] to increase the Si/Al ratio -- This subclass concerns techniques for adjusting the silicon-to-aluminum (Si/Al) ratio in molecular-sieve catalysts, a manipulation that can fine-tune catalytic activity, selectivity, and thermal stability. It applies to processes that increase the Si/Al ratio and is used when the specific catalyst modification is not covered by other more detailed categories within the chemical-process and separating operations sections.
[B01J 2229/18] to introduce other elements into or onto the molecular sieve itself -- The B01J 2229/18 subclass covers techniques for incorporating additional elements into a molecular-sieve catalyst, such as ion-exchange, impregnation, or deposition of metals, oxides or other species onto or into the sieve framework, to modify its catalytic properties. It applies to processes that alter the composition of the sieve itself rather than merely using the sieve as a support.
[B01J 2229/183] in framework positions -- The code B01J 2229/183 covers techniques that locate active components or reactants within the framework of a molecular-sieve catalyst, such as positioning catalytic sites inside the pores or channels of zeolites. It relates to separating operations that rely on chemical or physical processes, specifically aspects of molecular-sieve catalysis not addressed by other categories.
[B01J 2229/186] not in framework positions -- This CPC subclass covers aspects of molecular-sieve catalysts that fall outside the defined framework positions, including unique compositions, preparation methods, modifications or operating conditions not addressed in other subclasses. It relates to chemical or physical separation processes, particularly catalysis, where the molecular-sieve catalyst plays a distinct role.
[B01J 2229/20] to introduce other elements in the catalyst composition comprising the molecular sieve, but not specially in or on the molecular sieve itself -- This subclass covers processes in which a molecular-sieve catalyst is mixed with additional elements that become part of the overall catalyst composition, but are not specifically deposited on or within the sieve crystals themselves. It includes methods of preparing or modifying such mixed catalyst formulations for catalytic or separation operations.
[B01J 2229/22] to destroy the molecular sieve structure or part thereof -- The CPC subclass B01J 2229/22 pertains to processes that intentionally break down or damage the structure of a molecular-sieve catalyst, such as by chemical attack, thermal treatment, or mechanical stress, resulting in partial or complete destruction of the sieve framework.
[B01J 2229/24] to stabilize the molecular sieve structure -- This subgroup covers methods and additives used to maintain the structural integrity of molecular-sieve catalysts during operation or regeneration. Techniques include incorporation of binders, protective agents, or post-treatment procedures that prevent framework collapse, dealumination or pore blockage, thereby extending catalyst life.
[B01J 2229/26] to stabilize the total catalyst structure -- This subclass covers methods or additives used to maintain structural integrity of molecular-sieve catalysts during operation, ensuring the catalyst framework remains intact under reaction conditions. It falls under performing operations - separating - chemical or physical processes such as catalysis and colloid chemistry, and excludes other aspects of molecular-sieve catalysts.
[B01J 2229/30] After treatment, characterised by the means used -- This classification comprises molecular-sieve catalysts that have been modified after their initial preparation, and the entry is defined by the particular means of post-treatment such as thermal activation, ion exchange, or washing. It falls within separating operations of chemical or physical processes, focusing on the treatment step that characterizes the final catalyst.
[B01J 2229/32] Reaction with silicon compounds, e.g. TEOS, siliconfluoride -- B01J 2229/32 in the Cooperative Patent Classification (EPO/USPTO) pertains to aspects of molecular sieve catalysts involving reactions with silicon compounds, such as tetraethyl orthosilicate (TEOS) or silicon fluoride. This classification is specific to processes not covered by broader categories and focuses on the chemical interactions that modify or utilize molecular sieves in catalytic applications.
[B01J 2229/34] Reaction with organic or organometallic compounds -- The B01J 2229/34 subgroup pertains to chemical or physical processes that employ molecular-sieve catalysts to react organic or organometallic compounds. It covers catalytic transformations, such as conversions, rearrangements or coupling reactions, where the catalyst's porous structure influences activity, selectivity or product separation.
[B01J 2229/36] Steaming -- The subclass B01J 2229/36 relates to the use of steam in processes that involve molecular-sieve catalysts, covering steaming steps that are not addressed elsewhere in the classification. It falls under performing operations, separating, and chemical or physical processes such as catalysis and colloid chemistry.
[B01J 2229/37] Acid treatment -- This subclass covers processes in which acid is used to treat molecular-sieve catalysts to modify their acidity, remove impurities, or regenerate activity, typically after synthesis or deactivation. It includes steps such as contacting the sieve with mineral or organic acids, washing, and drying, applied in catalysis or separation.
[B01J 2229/38] Base treatment -- B01J 2229/38 covers procedures for treating basic components of molecular-sieve catalysts, such as regeneration, conditioning or modification of their alkaline sites, where the process does not fall under other specific molecular-sieve treatments. It includes chemical or physical base-treatment steps applied before or after catalytic operation to adjust activity, selectivity or stability.
[B01J 2229/40] Special temperature treatment, i.e. other than just for template removal -- This sub-group covers processes where molecular sieve catalysts undergo temperature treatments other than simple template removal, such as activation, regeneration or modification steps that change their physicochemical properties. It falls under separating chemical or physical processes within performing operations.
[B01J 2229/42] Addition of matrix or binder particles -- B01J 2229/42 pertains to the step of introducing matrix or binder particles into molecular-sieve catalysts during their preparation, such as mixing, impregnating or adding solid binders to improve mechanical strength, shape-holding or distribution of the active phase. The entry falls under performing operations, separating and chemical or physical processes related to catalyst formulation not covered elsewhere.
[B01J 2229/60] Synthesis on support -- B01J 2229/60 covers methods for preparing catalytic systems where active components are synthesized on a solid support, particularly molecular-sieve catalysts that are not addressed in other subclasses. It relates to performing operations that separate chemical or physical processes such as catalysis, emphasizing the preparation of supported catalysts for use in chemical reactions.
[B01J 2229/62] in or on other molecular sieves -- B01J 2229/62 relates to the use of molecular-sieve catalysts that are not otherwise specified, applied either directly on other molecular sieves or combined with them. It falls under the broader hierarchy of performing operations, separating, and chemical or physical processes such as catalysis and colloid chemistry. This subclass captures novel or auxiliary uses of alternative molecular-sieve materials in catalytic or separation applications.
[B01J 2229/64] in or on refractory materials -- The subclass B01J 2229/64 relates to the use of molecular-sieve catalysts that are situated in or applied onto refractory materials during separating operations, such as catalytic or other chemical-physical processes, where the refractory support provides heat resistance and structural stability.
[B01J 2229/66] on metal supports -- B01J 2229/66 pertains to molecular-sieve catalysts that are supported on metal substrates, covering the nature, preparation and treatment of the metal supports. The code is used for processes in which such supported sieves are employed for chemical or physical separations, including catalytic reactions.
[B01J 2231/00] Catalytic reactions performed with catalysts classified in -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The group B01J2231/00 is an indexing scheme, where specific catalytic reaction types of catalysts classified in B01J31/00 are indexed according to purpose or concept. Elements from in particular C07B , C07C , C07D , C08F and C08G are incorporated. Multiple catalytic purposes are multiply indexed.  Polymerisation reactions are only indexed in groups B01J2231/10 - B01J2231/14 if at least a dual use of the catalyst is disclosed in the application, e.g. oligomerisation and polymerisation.  Relevant NPL publications such as review articles relating to methods of using catalyst systems are also indexed in this scheme.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - catalysts used only in polymerisation reactions -> C08 , M08
[B01J 2231/005] General concepts, e.g. reviews, relating to methods of using catalyst systems, the concept being defined by a common method or theory, e.g. microwave heating or multiple stereoselectivity -- B01J 2231/005 covers general concepts such as reviews or theoretical studies on catalyst systems where a common method or principle - like microwave heating or multiple stereoselectivity - is applied to catalytic reactions. It falls under separating chemical or physical processes within performing operations.
[B01J 2231/10] Polymerisation reactions involving at least dual use catalysts, e.g. for both oligomerisation and polymerisation -- B01J 2231/10 covers catalytic processes in which a single catalyst or catalyst system is capable of both oligomerising and polymerising monomers, enabling combined production of low-molecular-weight oligomers and high-molecular-weight polymers in the same reaction environment. The classification falls within the CPC hierarchy for performing operations, separating, and chemical or physical processes such as catalysis.
[B01J 2231/12] Olefin polymerisation or copolymerisation -- B01J2231/12 relates to catalytic processes that produce polymers or copolymers from olefin monomers. It covers methods in which a catalyst, as defined in the broader B01J sub-classes, drives the polymerisation or copolymerisation of alkenes to form various polymeric materials.
[B01J 2231/122] Cationic (co)polymerisation, e.g. single-site or Ziegler-Natta type -- B01J 2231/122 refers to catalytic polymerisation where cationic species initiate polymer growth, typically using single-site or Ziegler-Natta type catalysts. The entry sits in the hierarchy performing operations, separating, chemical or physical processes and covers reactions using cationic (co)polymerisation catalysts.
[B01J 2231/125] Radical (co)polymerisation, e.g. mediators therefor -- B01J 2231/125 covers catalytic processes in which radicals are used to initiate polymerisation or copolymerisation, often with mediators that control chain growth. The entry pertains to methods and catalysts that facilitate radical-driven formation of polymer chains, including systems that combine radical generation with specific catalytic agents.
[B01J 2231/127] Anionic (co)polymerisation -- B01J 2231/127 pertains to anionic copolymerization processes, a specific type of catalytic reaction where catalysts play a crucial role in the polymerization of monomers under controlled conditions. This classification is part of the broader categories of performing operations, separating, and chemical or physical processes, focusing on catalysis.
[B01J 2231/14] Other (co) polymerisation, e.g. of lactides or epoxides -- B01J 2231/14 covers catalytic polymerisation and co-polymerisation processes that use the specified catalysts, including the polymerisation of lactides, epoxides and similar monomers. It falls under the separating chemical or physical processes section of the Cooperative Patent Classification, relating to catalytic reactions performed with classified catalysts.
[B01J 2231/20] Olefin oligomerisation or telomerisation -- The subclass B01J 2231/20 covers catalytic processes that convert olefins into oligomers or telomers, i.e., short-chain polymers formed by linking a limited number of monomer units, using catalysts specifically classified in this group. It falls under performing operations → separating → chemical or physical processes such as catalysis.
[B01J 2231/30] Addition reactions at carbon centres, i.e. to either C-C or C-X multiple bonds -- B01J 2231/30 covers catalytic addition reactions that form new bonds at carbon atoms, specifically adding reagents to carbon-carbon or carbon-heteroatom multiple bonds. It falls within the CPC hierarchy of performing operations, separating, and chemical or physical processes such as catalysis.
[B01J 2231/32] Addition reactions to C=C or C-C triple bonds -- B01J 2231/32 relates to catalytic addition reactions where a catalyst, classified within the B01J 2231 group, is used to add atoms or groups across carbon-carbon double (C=C) or triple (C≡C) bonds. The entry falls under separating processes in the broader category of chemical or physical processes, encompassing catalytic transformations that modify unsaturated hydrocarbons.
[B01J 2231/321] Hydroformylation, metalformylation, carbonylation or hydroaminomethylation -- This CPC subgroup covers catalytic reactions that add a carbonyl or aminomethyl group to unsaturated compounds, including hydroformylation (conversion of alkenes with carbon monoxide and hydrogen to aldehydes), metal-formylation, carbonylation and hydroaminomethylation, all performed with specified metal catalysts.
[B01J 2231/322] Hydrocyanation -- Hydrocyanation is a catalytic addition of hydrogen cyanide to unsaturated organic substrates, forming nitrile groups. The process typically uses metal catalysts such as nickel complexes and is employed to produce intermediates like adiponitrile. CPC B01J 2231/322 groups hydrocyanation reactions carried out with catalysts falling under subclass B01J 2231.
[B01J 2231/323] Hydrometalation, e.g. bor-, alumin-, silyl-, zirconation or analoguous reactions like carbometalation, hydrocarbation -- B01J 2231/323 covers catalytic hydrometalation processes in which a metal fragment such as boron, aluminum, silicon, zirconium or similar adds across a carbon-carbon or carbon-heteroatom double bond, including analogous carbometallation and hydrocarbation reactions performed with the specified catalysts.
[B01J 2231/324] Cyclisations via conversion of C-C multiple to single or less multiple bonds, e.g. cycloadditions -- B01J 2231/324 covers catalytic cyclisation processes in which carbon-carbon multiple bonds are converted to single or lower-order bonds, exemplified by cycloaddition reactions. The subclass falls under Performing Operations → Separating → Chemical or Physical Processes, specifically catalytic reactions using catalysts classified in the B01J series.
[B01J 2231/325] Cyclopropanations -- B01J 2231/325 covers catalytic cyclopropanation reactions, in which a catalyst - typically a metal complex or organometallic species - facilitates the formation of a three-membered cyclopropane ring from alkenes or related substrates. The class falls under performing operations, separating, chemical or physical processes such as catalysis.
[B01J 2231/326] Diels-Alder or other [4+2] cycloadditions, e.g. hetero-analogues -- B01J 2231/326 covers catalytic Diels-Alder and related [4+2] cycloaddition reactions, including those that form heterocyclic analogues, where a catalyst-often a metal complex, enzyme or organocatalyst-accelerates the cyclisation of a diene and a dienophile. The class is nested under performing operations, separating, and chemical or physical processes such as catalysis.
[B01J 2231/327] Dipolar cycloadditions -- B01J 2231/327 covers catalytic dipolar cycloaddition reactions, where a 1,3-dipole adds to a multiple bond to form a five-membered ring in the presence of a catalyst defined elsewhere in the CPC. These processes fall under separating chemical or physical operations, specifically catalytic reactions used to create cyclic compounds.
[B01J 2231/328] Cycloadditions involving more than 2 components or moieties, e.g. intra-/intermolecualar [2+2+2] or [2+2+1], e.g. Pauson-Khand type -- This CPC subclass B01J 2231/328 covers catalytic cycloaddition reactions that involve three reacting components or moieties, such as [2+2+2] or [2+2+1] processes including the Pauson-Khand reaction. It is placed in the hierarchy of performing operations, separating, chemical or physical processes, and catalytic reactions with defined catalyst types.
[B01J 2231/34] Other additions, e.g. Monsanto-type carbonylations, addition to 1,2-C=X or 1,2-C-X triplebonds, additions to 1,4-C=C-C=X or 1,4-C=-C-X triple bonds with X, e.g. O, S, NH/N -- This subclass covers catalytic addition reactions where carbonyl groups or other fragments are added to unsaturated carbon-heteroatom or carbon-carbon multiple bonds, including Monsanto-type carbonylations and similar processes involving 1,2-C=X or 1,2-C-X triple bonds and 1,4-C=C-C=X or 1,4-C≡C-X bonds with heteroatoms such as O, S or N.
[B01J 2231/341] 1,2-additions, e.g. aldol or Knoevenagel condensations -- The CPC subclass B01J 2231/341 covers catalytic reactions that effect 1,2-addition processes, such as aldol and Knoevenagel condensations, where a nucleophilic carbon adds to an electrophilic carbonyl carbon in the presence of a specified catalyst. It falls under performing operations → separating → chemical or physical processes, focusing on catalysts classified within the broader B01J group.
[B01J 2231/342] Aldol type reactions, i.e. nucleophilic addition of C-H acidic compounds, their R3Si- or metal complex analogues, to aldehydes or ketones -- B01J 2231/342 covers catalytic aldol-type reactions in which a C-H acidic compound, or its silyl- or metal-complex analogue, adds nucleophilically to an aldehyde or ketone. The class addresses processes that use a catalyst to promote this carbon-carbon bond-forming step, falling under the broader categories of performing operations, separating, and chemical or physical processes such as catalysis.
[B01J 2231/343] to prepare cyanhydrines, e.g. by adding HCN or TMSCN -- B01J 2231/343 pertains to catalytic processes for preparing cyanhydrins through the addition of hydrogen cyanide (HCN) or trimethylsilyl cyanide (TMSCN), falling under chemical and physical processes, specifically within the scope of catalysis.
[B01J 2231/344] Boronation, e.g. by adding R-B(OR)2 -- This subclass covers catalytic processes that introduce a boron-containing reagent, i.e., boronation, where a boron atom is attached to a substrate via a catalyst. It includes reactions that add R-B(OR)2 groups in transformations such as hydroboration or borylation, typically using transition-metal catalysts.
[B01J 2231/345] with organometallic complexes, e.g. by adding ZnR -- The CPC subclass B01J 2231/345 covers catalytic reactions that use organometallic complexes as catalysts, such as adding zinc-alkyl (ZnR) compounds to promote the process. It falls within the hierarchy of performing operations, separating, and chemical or physical processes, specifically addressing catalysis carried out with organometallic species.
[B01J 2231/346] Mannich type reactions, i.e. nucleophilic addition of C-H acidic compounds, their R3Si- or metal complex analogues to aldimines or ketimines -- The CPC code B01J 2231/346 covers Mannich-type reactions in which C-H-acidic compounds, or their R₃Si- or metal-complex analogues, act as nucleophiles adding to aldimines or ketimines. It falls under catalytic processes that employ classified catalysts for chemical transformations in the separating and performing operations domain.
[B01J 2231/347] via cationic intermediates, e.g. bisphenol A type processes -- B01J 2231/347 covers catalytic reactions that proceed through cationic intermediates, such as the production of bisphenol A and related compounds. It falls under separating operations within chemical or physical processes involving catalysis.
[B01J 2231/348] 1,4-additions, e.g. conjugate additions -- B01J 2231/348 pertains to catalytic reactions involving 1,4-additions, also known as conjugate additions, which are specific types of chemical reactions where an electrophile adds to the carbon-carbon double bond of a conjugated system.
[B01J 2231/349] 1,2- or 1,4-additions in combination with further or prior reactions by the same catalyst, i.e. tandem or domino reactions, e.g. hydrogenation or further addition reactions -- The B01J 2231/349 subclass covers catalytic processes in which a single catalyst effects 1,2- or 1,4-addition steps together with additional or preceding reactions, forming tandem or domino sequences such as hydrogenation followed by further addition steps. It falls under performing operations, separating, and chemical or physical processes like catalysis.
[B01J 2231/40] Substitution reactions at carbon centres, e.g. C-C or C-X, i.e. carbon-hetero atom, cross-coupling, C-H activation or ring-opening reactions -- B01J 2231/40 covers catalytic substitution reactions at carbon atoms, including carbon-carbon and carbon-heteroatom bond formation such as cross-coupling, C-H activation and ring-opening processes. The class applies to any catalyst that enables these transformations in chemical or physical processes.
[B01J 2231/42] Catalytic cross-coupling, i.e. connection of previously not connected C-atoms or C- and X-atoms without rearrangement -- This subclass covers catalytic cross-coupling reactions that join two previously separate carbon atoms or a carbon atom with another hetero-atom without rearranging the molecular framework, as exemplified by Suzuki, Heck, and related processes. It is situated within the broader hierarchy of separating operations, chemical or physical processes, and catalytic reactions.
[B01J 2231/4205] C-C cross-coupling, e.g. metal catalyzed or Friedel-Crafts type -- This CPC subclass (B01J 2231/4205) covers catalytic processes that join two carbon atoms, including metal-catalyzed C-C cross-coupling and Friedel-Crafts alkylation or acylation, used to form carbon-carbon bonds in organic synthesis.
[B01J 2231/4211] Suzuki-type, i.e. RY + R'B(OR)2, in which R, R' are optionally substituted alkyl, alkenyl, aryl, acyl and Y is the leaving group -- B01J 2231/4211 covers Suzuki-type cross-coupling reactions where an organoboron compound (R′B(OR)₂) is coupled with an organohalide or pseudohalide (RY), using a catalyst and a leaving group Y; R and R′ may be substituted alkyl, alkenyl, aryl or acyl groups. This falls under catalytic separating processes within chemical or physical operations.
[B01J 2231/4216] with R= alkyl -- B01J 2231/4216 covers catalytic reactions where the organic substituent R is an alkyl group. The processes are part of chemical or physical separation operations and are nested under the performing-operations and separating categories of the CPC.
[B01J 2231/4222] with R'= alkyl -- B01J 2231/4222 concerns catalytic reactions that employ catalysts bearing an R′ substituent that is an alkyl group, situated within the broader hierarchy of performing operations, separating, and chemical or physical processes such as catalysis. It is a Cooperative Patent Classification entry used by the EPO and USPTO.
[B01J 2231/4227] with Y= Cl -- B01J 2231/4227 designates catalytic reactions that employ catalysts containing chlorine as a component of the active site or ligand structure. The subclass is nested under performing operations - separating - chemical or physical processes such as catalysis or colloid chemistry. It captures processes where the presence of chlorine influences the catalytic activity, for example in halogen-modified metal complexes.
[B01J 2231/4233] Kumada-type, i.e. RY + R'MgZ, in which Ris optionally substituted alkyl, alkenyl, aryl, Y is the leaving group and Z is halide -- The code B01J 2231/4233 designates catalytic processes of the Kumada type, where an organomagnesium compound (R′MgZ) reacts with an organohalide (RY) in the presence of a catalyst classified under B01J 2231. In this cross-coupling, R is an alkyl, alkenyl or aryl group, Y is the leaving group attached to R, and Z is a halide. The entry falls under separating operations within chemical or physical processes.
[B01J 2231/4238] Negishi-type, i.e. RY + R'ZnZ, in which R, R' is optionally substituted alkyl, alkenyl, alkynyl, aryl, Y is the leaving group and Z is halide or R' -- B01J 2231/4238 covers Negishi-type cross-coupling reactions in which an organozinc compound R'ZnZ reacts with an organic halide or pseudohalide RY, where R and R' may be alkyl, alkenyl, alkynyl or aryl groups, optionally substituted, Y is the leaving group and Z a halide attached to zinc. The class falls under chemical or physical processes, specifically catalytic separations, within the CPC hierarchy of performing operations.
[B01J 2231/4244] with R= alkyl -- The CPC entry B01J 2231/4244 covers catalytic reactions that use catalysts whose structure includes an alkyl (R= alkyl) substituent. It falls under performing operations → separating → chemical or physical processes such as catalysis, and it specifies the use of alkyl-bearing ligands or supports in homogeneous or heterogeneous catalytic systems.
[B01J 2231/425] with R'= alkyl -- B01J 2231/425 relates to catalytic reactions that use a catalyst whose substituent R′ is an alkyl group. It falls under performing operations, separating, chemical or physical processes such as catalysis, and specifically covers processes where the alkyl-substituted catalyst is employed.
[B01J 2231/4255] Stille-type, i.e. RY + R'3SnR'', in which R is alkenyl, aryl, R' is alkyl and R'' is alkenyl or aryl -- B01J 2231/4255 covers catalytic reactions that use organotin compounds in a Stille-type coupling, where an alkenyl or aryl group (R) is transferred from a tris-alkyl-tin reagent (R′₃SnR″) to a substrate (RY); R is alkenyl or aryl, R′ is alkyl and R″ is alkenyl or aryl.
[B01J 2231/4261] Heck-type, i.e. RY + C=C, in which R is aryl -- B01J 2231/4261 covers catalytic Heck-type coupling reactions in which the R substituent is an aryl group, forming a C-C bond between an aryl halide (RY) and an alkene (C=C). This entry falls under chemical or physical processes, specifically separating operations that involve catalysis.
[B01J 2231/4266] Sonogashira-type, i.e. RY + HC-CR' triple bonds, in which R=aryl, alkenyl, alkyl and R'=H, alkyl or aryl -- The CPC subclass B01J 2231/4266 covers catalytic reactions of the Sonogashira type in which an organohalide (R-Y) is coupled with a terminal alkyne (H-C≡C-R′) using a metal catalyst. R may be aryl, alkenyl or alkyl, and R′ can be hydrogen, alkyl or aryl, producing substituted alkynes.
[B01J 2231/4272] via enolates or aza-analogues, added as such or made in-situ, e.g. ArY + R2C=C(OM)Z -> ArR2C-C(O)Z, in which R is H or alkyl, M is Na, K or SiMe3, Y is the leaving group, Z is Ar or OR' and R' is alkyl -- B01J 2231/4272 refers to catalytic processes that employ enolates or their aza-analogues, either introduced as such or generated in-situ, to form carbon-carbon or carbon-oxygen bonds. Typical reactions follow the pattern ArY + R₂C=C(OM)Z → ArR₂C-C(O)Z, where R may be hydrogen or an alkyl group, M is Na, K or SiMe₃, Y is a leaving group, Z is an aryl or alkoxy group and R′ is alkyl. This subclass falls under separating chemical or physical processes within performing operations.
[B01J 2231/4277] C-X Cross-coupling, e.g. nucleophilic aromatic amination, alkoxylation or analogues -- This CPC subclass covers catalytic reactions that form C-X bonds by cross-coupling, such as nucleophilic aromatic amination, alkoxylation and related transformations using the specified catalysts.
[B01J 2231/4283] using N nucleophiles, e.g. Buchwald-Hartwig amination -- B01J 2231/4283 covers catalytic reactions in which nitrogen nucleophiles are employed, exemplified by Buchwald-Hartwig amination, and falls under the broader categories of separating processes and chemical or physical processes such as catalysis. It specifies the use of catalysts classified for nitrogen-based nucleophilic coupling.
[B01J 2231/4288] using O nucleophiles, e.g. alcohols, carboxylates, esters -- B01J 2231/4288 covers catalytic reactions in which oxygen-containing nucleophiles-such as alcohols, carboxylates or esters-are employed with classified catalysts. The entry is situated under Performing Operations → Separating → Chemical or Physical Processes, focusing on the use of O-nucleophiles in catalysis.
[B01J 2231/4294] using S nucleophiles, e.g. thiols -- B01J 2231/4294 covers catalytic processes in which sulfur-containing nucleophiles, especially thiols, react with electrophilic substrates using catalysts designed for S-nucleophile reactions; it applies to chemical or physical operations, including separation steps, where such catalysis is central.
[B01J 2231/44] Allylic alkylation, amination, alkoxylation or analogues -- B01J 2231/44 covers catalytic reactions that modify allylic compounds by introducing alkyl, amino or alkoxy groups, using catalysts classified in the B01J 2231 subgroup for allylic functionalisation.
[B01J 2231/46] C-H or C-C activation -- The CPC subclass B01J 2231/46 covers catalytic reactions in which a catalyst enables activation of carbon-hydrogen or carbon-carbon bonds, facilitating their cleavage or functionalisation. It falls under the broader categories of performing operations, separating, and chemical or physical processes such as catalysis and colloid chemistry.
[B01J 2231/48] Ring-opening reactions -- B01J 2231/48 designates catalytic processes in which a cyclic molecule's ring is opened, typically by a chemical or physical catalyst classified in the broader B01J group, to form linear or open-chain products. This subclass includes procedures that exploit catalytic activation, bond cleavage and rearrangement to convert rings into functional intermediates or final compounds.
[B01J 2231/482] asymmetric reactions, e.g. kinetic resolution of racemates -- B01J 2231/482 covers catalytic processes that employ chiral catalysts to achieve enantioselective outcomes, such as kinetic resolution of racemic mixtures, enabling preferential formation or consumption of one enantiomer. It falls under separating chemical or physical processes within the broader performing-operations category.
[B01J 2231/485] kinetic resolution of epoxide racemates -- This subclass concerns catalytic processes that achieve kinetic resolution of racemic epoxides, employing chiral catalysts to selectively transform one enantiomer while leaving the other unchanged, thereby enriching the desired enantiomer.
[B01J 2231/487] by hydrolysis -- B01J 2231/487 covers catalytic reactions in which the desired transformation is achieved by hydrolysis, i.e., the cleavage of chemical bonds through reaction with water, using a catalyst defined in the parent group B01J 2231. It applies to methods and apparatuses where hydrolytic catalysts facilitate the separation or conversion of substances in chemical processing.
[B01J 2231/49] Esterification or transesterification -- The CPC subclass B01J 2231/49 covers catalytic reactions that use catalysts classified in group 2231, specifically those carrying out esterification or transesterification processes. It falls under the hierarchy of performing operations, separating, and chemical or physical processes such as catalysis.
[B01J 2231/50] Redistribution or isomerisation reactions of C-C, C=C or C-C triple bonds -- The CPC class B01J 2231/50 covers catalytic processes that rearrange carbon-carbon single, double or triple bonds, including redistribution and isomerisation reactions carried out with designated catalysts. It is situated under performing operations, separating, and chemical or physical processes, catalytic reactions.
[B01J 2231/52] Isomerisation reactions -- This CPC subclass B01J 2231/52 relates to catalytic isomerisation processes carried out with catalysts belonging to the B01J 2231 group, i.e., metal or alloy particles supported on solid carriers. It covers methods that rearrange the molecular structure of a compound to produce a different isomer without changing its molecular formula, commonly used in petrochemical refining and fine-chemical synthesis.
[B01J 2231/54] Metathesis reactions, e.g. olefin metathesis -- The subclass B01J 2231/54 covers catalytic processes that perform metathesis reactions, such as olefin metathesis, using designated catalysts. It includes methods, apparatus and catalyst compositions for effecting the exchange of molecular fragments between unsaturated compounds within the broader category of separating operations in chemical or physical processing.
[B01J 2231/543] alkene metathesis -- B01J 2231/543 covers catalytic reactions in which alkene metathesis is carried out using catalysts that belong to the specified subclass. The classification falls under the CPC hierarchy Performing Operations - Separating - Chemical or Physical Processes, Catalytic reactions performed with catalysts classified in. It relates to processes that rearrange carbon-carbon double bonds of alkenes to form new olefins.
[B01J 2231/546] alkyne metathesis -- Alkyne metathesis is a catalytic process that rearranges carbon-carbon triple bonds by breaking and reforming them to generate new alkyne products. The reaction is carried out with specific homogeneous or heterogeneous catalysts that mediate the exchange of alkyne fragments, enabling synthesis of valuable organic compounds and polymer precursors.
[B01J 2231/60] Reduction reactions, e.g. hydrogenation -- B01J 2231/60 pertains to catalytic reduction reactions such as hydrogenation, where a substrate is reduced by adding hydrogen or another reducing agent in the presence of a catalyst belonging to the specified catalyst groups. It includes processes for converting unsaturated organic compounds, oxides, or nitrogen-containing species to more reduced forms, typically using metal or alloy catalysts. The subclass falls under Performing Operations → Separating → Chemical or Physical Processes, e.g. Catalysis or Colloid Chemistry.
[B01J 2231/62] Reductions in general of inorganic substrates, e.g. formal hydrogenation, e.g. of N -- B01J 2231/62 covers catalytic reduction processes applied to inorganic substrates, such as formal hydrogenation of nitrogen-containing compounds. The code is used for inventions that employ specific catalysts to achieve these reductions, fitting within the broader categories of performing operations, separating, and chemical or physical processes like catalysis. It provides a detailed classification for patents describing any inorganic reduction reaction that proceeds via a catalytic mechanism.
[B01J 2231/625] of CO -- B01J 2231/625 refers to catalytic reactions involving carbon monoxide (CO) as a reactant, specifically within the context of chemical processes that utilize catalysts. This classification is part of the Cooperative Patent Classification system used by the European Patent Office and the United States Patent and Trademark Office for organizing patent documents.
[B01J 2231/64] Reductions in general of organic substrates, e.g. hydride reductions or hydrogenations -- B01J 2231/64 pertains to catalytic reduction processes that convert organic substrates to less oxidized forms, including hydride reductions and hydrogenations, performed with suitable catalysts within the broader categories of performing operations, separating and chemical or physical processes.
[B01J 2231/641] Hydrogenation of organic substrates, i.e. H -- B01J 2231/641 covers catalytic hydrogenation of organic substrates, where hydrogen is added to compounds such as alkenes, alkynes, carbonyls or aromatics using solid, liquid or heterogeneous catalysts. The class falls under performing operations, separating, chemical or physical processes, specifically catalytic reactions.
[B01J 2231/643] of R2C=O or R2C=NR (R= C, H) -- This subclass covers catalytic processes where the substrate is a carbonyl compound R2C=O or an imine R2C=NR with R being carbon or hydrogen. It falls under separating chemical or physical processes, specifically catalytic reactions using catalysts classified in B01J 2231, and is used to classify methods that employ such substrates in catalytic transformations.
[B01J 2231/645] of C=C or C-C triple bonds -- This subclass concerns catalytic reactions in which the catalyst operates on carbon-carbon double or triple bonds, such as hydrogenation, polymerisation, coupling or other transformations of C=C or C≡C groups. It is situated under performing operations, separating, chemical or physical processes and belongs to the B01J 2231 series for catalysts acting on such unsaturated bonds.
[B01J 2231/646] of aromatic or heteroaromatic rings -- This subclass covers catalytic reactions that act on aromatic or hetero-aromatic ring structures using the catalysts defined in the B01J group, including processes such as hydrogenation, oxidation, functionalisation or ring-opening of these cyclic compounds.
[B01J 2231/648] Fischer-Tropsch-type reactions -- B01J 2231/648 defines catalytic processes that convert a mixture of carbon monoxide and hydrogen (synthesis gas) into hydrocarbons by Fischer-Tropsch-type reactions, typically using iron, cobalt or other metal catalysts. The subclass covers the reaction conditions, catalyst preparation, gas-phase synthesis steps and subsequent separation of the liquid or solid hydrocarbon products.
[B01J 2231/70] Oxidation reactions, e.g. epoxidation, (di)hydroxylation, dehydrogenation and analogues -- B01J 2231/70 relates to catalytic oxidation processes, including epoxidation, (di)hydroxylation, dehydrogenation and comparable reactions carried out with specified catalysts. It sits within the hierarchy of performing operations → separating → chemical or physical processes → catalytic reactions with classified catalysts, and focuses on oxidation steps that transform substrates using catalytic agents.
[B01J 2231/72] Epoxidation -- B01J 2231/72 pertains to catalytic epoxidation processes, describing methods that produce epoxides by reacting substrates with an oxidising agent in the presence of catalysts such as metal oxides, peroxides or enzyme-like systems. The entry sits within the hierarchy of performing operations, separating, chemical or physical processes, and specifically deals with catalytic reactions.
[B01J 2231/74] Aziridination -- Aziridination under CPC B01J 2231/74 covers catalytic processes that form three-membered nitrogen heterocycles by adding nitrene equivalents to carbon-carbon double bonds using metal-based catalysts. The entry includes methods where the catalyst belongs to the specified subclass and the reaction yields aziridine products.
[B01J 2231/76] Dehydrogenation -- B01J 2231/76 covers catalytic dehydrogenation reactions in which a hydrogen atom is removed from a substrate using catalysts classified in the B01J series. The class falls under performing operations, separating, and chemical or physical processes such as catalysis. It includes methods and apparatus for dehydrogenating organic compounds with heterogeneous or homogeneous catalysts.
[B01J 2231/763] of -CH-XH (X= O, NH/N, S) to -C=X or -CX triple bond species -- This CPC entry covers catalytic transformations in which a carbon bearing a hydrogen and a heteroatom group (X = O, NH, N, S) is converted into a carbon-heteroatom double bond or a carbon-heteroatom triple bond. Such reactions include dehydrogenative coupling, oxidative dehydrogenation, and related processes where the catalyst facilitates formation of C=O, C=N, C=S, C≡O, C≡N, or C≡S linkages. The classification is used for processes in organic synthesis and fine-chemical production.
[B01J 2231/766] of -CH-CH- or -C=C- to -C=C- or -C-C- triple bond species -- B01J 2231/766 pertains to catalytic reactions involving the conversion of carbon-carbon double bonds to carbon-carbon triple bonds, focusing on specific chemical transformations within the broader field of catalysis and colloid chemistry.
[B01J 2235/00] Indexing scheme associated with group -- Groups B01J2235/00 , B01J2235/05 , B01J2235/10 , B01J2235/15 and B01J2235/30 are incomplete pending reclassification of documents from groups B01J35/00 , B01J35/30 , B01J35/31 , B01J35/393 and B01J35/50 . [B01J 2235/10] Infrared [IR] -- This subgroup covers the use of infrared radiation as an energy source or analytical tool in separation operations such as chromatography, distillation or extraction. It includes processes where IR heating, detection or monitoring assists in separating chemical mixtures, often in catalytic or colloidal contexts.

It is classified under performing operations, separating, and chemical or physical processes such as catalysis or colloid chemistry.
[B01J 2/24] Obtaining flakes by scraping a solid layer from a surface -- This subclass covers methods and apparatus that produce flake-shaped particles by physically scraping a continuous solid layer from a substrate surface. The technique is used in granulation processes to generate thin, flat fragments for further processing or use. It falls under separating operations within chemical or physical processes.
[B01J 2/26] on endless conveyor belts -- The B01J 2/26 subgroup covers granulation processes or equipment in which the material is conveyed and processed on an endless conveyor belt, allowing continuous feeding, mixing and size reduction or agglomeration. It specifies the use of a moving belt as the primary transport and reaction surface during granulation.
[B01J 2/28] using special binding agents -- B01J 2/28 relates to granulation processes that employ special binding agents to promote particle cohesion, enabling the formation of granules during chemical or physical operations such as catalysis or colloid chemistry. The entry covers devices and methods that add or generate these binders during the separation and granulating steps.
[B01J 2/30] using agents to prevent the granules sticking together -- The B01J 2/30 entry defines processes for granulating materials that employ anti-binding agents to keep the granules from adhering to each other. It covers any chemical or physical method that adds substances-such as surfactants, lubricants, or coatings-to prevent granule agglomeration during production. This falls under the broader categories of performing operations, separating, and chemical or physical processes.

[B01J 23/94] of catalysts comprising metals, oxides or hydroxides of the iron group metals or copper -- The classification B01J 23/94 lies within performing operations, separating, chemical or physical processes and covers patents that disclose catalysts comprising iron-group metals or copper, together with their oxides or hydroxides, that are not otherwise listed. These catalysts are employed in various catalytic, separation or colloid- chemistry applications.
[B01J 23/96] of catalysts comprising metals, oxides or hydroxides of the noble metals -- B01J 23/96 pertains to catalytic substances that consist of noble metals, their oxides or hydroxides, used in separation operations within chemical or physical processes. The subclass captures formulations not specifically enumerated in other groups of the B01J series. It falls under the hierarchy of Performing Operations - Separating - Chemical or Physical Processes, e.g., catalysis.
[B01J 25/00] Catalysts of the Raney type -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Catalysts of the Raney type, i.e. catalysts prepared by leaching of an alloy to produce porous structure, such as by dissolving aluminium from alloy using a base such as NaOH.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Electrocatalysts, electrodes -> H01M
[B01J 25/02] Raney nickel -- The CPC class B01J 25/02 pertains to Raney nickel, a porous, high-surface-area alloy used as a heterogeneous catalyst in chemical reactions such as hydrogenation, hydrodesulfurization and other reduction processes. It includes the preparation of the activated metal by leaching, its formulation, and its application in separating or transforming substances within catalytic or colloid chemistry.
[B01J 25/04] Regeneration or reactivation -- The B01J 25/04 subclass covers processes for restoring the activity of Raney-type catalysts after they have been deactivated. It includes chemical, thermal or mechanical treatments that reactivate the catalyst surface, allowing it to be reused in further catalytic reactions.
