[B01J 2531/00] Additional information regarding catalytic systems classified in -- **Definition:** This place covers:  In this indexing scheme additional compositional aspects of the coordination complexes contained within group B01J31/00 are indexed, e.g. polynuclearity, ligand type, metal bonding mode(s) and the specifically disclosed central metal(s) therein.  Furthermore additional information is indexed regarding any special solvent (systems) used for any catalyst system of the group B01J31/00 .  General concepts, e.g. as presented in review articles, relating to catalyst systems and methods of making them, the concept being defined by a common material or method/theory are indexed in B01J2531/001 - B01J2531/008 .  Indexing codes B01J2531/0202 - B01J2531/0222 specify the presence and type of polynuclearity, as well as linkage type of the metals involved among themselves.  Indexing codes B01J2531/0225 - B01J2531/0283 characterise the complexes on the basis of bond-type (linkage-type) between central metal(s), thereby specifying the structural geometry of the complexes, while classification entries B01J31/16 - B01J31/24 are purely compositional subdivisions.  Indexing codes B01J2531/0286 - B01J2531/0297 broadly characterise ligands by their function.  Indexing codes B01J2531/10 - B01J2531/847 are used to index the individual (central) metals in the (polynuclear) complexes.  Special solvent (systems) used with any catalyst system of the group B01J31/00 are indexed in B01J2531/90 - B01J2531/985 .  **Glossary:** - Ionic liquid: an organic salt in the liquid state at the reaction temperature employed, e.g. ≤ 100 °C or ≤ 20 °C ("room-temperature ionic liquid") - Bi- or polynuclear complex: a coordination complex comprising two or more same or different metal atoms, without M-M bonds; e.g. Cp(Lx)Zr-imidazole-Zr(Lx)Cp, [R 2 PCp-Fe-CpPR 2 ]Rh(L) n , [(salen)Co(III)]SbF 6 , Co(II)(salen)Fe(II)Cl 2 . - Bimetallic complex: a discrete coord. complex comprising one or more units of two metals, same or different, with metal-metal bonds but no all-metal (M)n rings, e.g. Cr 2 (OAc) 4 , [(Mo 2 ) 4 (MeOPhNCNPhOMe) 8 (Ph(COO) 2 ) 4 ], [Ph 2 P-X-PPh 2 ]Pt(Cl)SnCl 3 , [Cp(CO) 2 Mn=] 2 Pb. - Metal cluster: a coordination complex with 3 to abt. 1000 same or different metal atoms and further comprising M-M bonds to provide (M)n rings, i.e. size range from atomic to colloid dimensions; e.g. Rh x (CO) y , [R 3 P=N=PR 3 ] + [Ru 3 lr(CO) 13 ] - , [Rh 3 (DIPAMP) 3 (μ 3 -OMe) 2 ]BF 4 , Pt 4 (OAc) 8 , "Pd(OAc) 2 " = Pd 3 (OAc) 6 . - Pincer-type complexes: Example (with metal-carbon bond): - Tripodal ligand: Example (tris(pyrazolyl)borate skeleton): - Corrole: Example: - Porphyrin ring system: Example:
[B01J 2531/001] General concepts, e.g. reviews, relating to catalyst systems and methods of making them, the concept being defined by a common material or method/theory -- When indexing in this group, only the focus is indexed in B01J2531/004 - B01J2531/007 and only if groups with closely related members are concerned, e.g. N-heterocyclic carbenes ( B01J2531/004 ), Pd-complexes ( B01J2531/005 ), added halide ( B01J2531/007 ). Otherwise the main code B01J2531/002 is used.
[B01J 2531/002] Materials -- The subclass B01J 2531/002 Materials pertains to substances employed in catalytic systems for separation operations, such as catalysts, supports, membranes or sorbents that facilitate chemical or physical processes like catalysis or colloid chemistry. It includes any material designed to enhance or enable the selective separation of components in a reaction mixture.
[B01J 2531/004] Ligands -- The CPC entry B01J 2531/004 "Ligands" covers the use of ligands as components of catalytic systems in chemical or physical processes, particularly those employed in separating operations. It pertains to the design, selection, or modification of ligands that enhance catalyst performance, stability, or selectivity in reactions such as catalysis, colloid chemistry, or related separation techniques.
[B01J 2531/005] Catalytic metals -- B01J 2531/005 - Catalytic metals refers to metal-based catalysts employed in chemical or physical separation processes, such as catalytic reactors, adsorbents, or colloidal systems. It includes metals that accelerate reactions while remaining unchanged, enabling efficient separation of mixtures or transformation of substrates.
[B01J 2531/007] Promoter-type Additives -- The CPC entry B01J 2531/007 covers promoter-type additives used in catalytic systems, i.e., substances that enhance the activity, selectivity or stability of a catalyst without themselves being the primary catalyst. These additives are employed in chemical or physical processes such as separations, catalysis or colloid chemistry and are classified under performing operations, separating, and catalytic processes.
[B01J 2531/008] Methods or theories -- B01J 2531/008 covers methods and theories related to catalytic systems employed in separation processes, encompassing experimental techniques, modelling, kinetic analysis, catalyst design and process simulation used to understand or improve catalytic performance in chemical or physical separations.
[B01J 2531/02] Compositional aspects of complexes used, e.g. polynuclearity -- The B01J 2531/02 subclass covers the composition of catalytic complexes, emphasizing structural features such as polynuclearity and ligand makeup, and how these aspects influence the performance of separation processes within chemical or physical catalysis.
[B01J 2531/0202] Polynuclearity -- B01J 2531/0202 covers catalytic systems that contain two or more metal atoms linked in a single molecule or cluster, known as polynuclear catalysts. These polynuclear species are used in separation or chemical-physical processes such as catalysis or colloid chemistry, where multiple metal centers cooperate to enhance activity, selectivity or stability.
[B01J 2531/0205] Bi- or polynuclear complexes, i.e. comprising two or more metal coordination centres, without metal-metal bonds, e.g. Cp(Lx)Zr-imidazole-Zr(Lx)Cp -- Entry B01J 2531/0205 pertains to catalytic systems that consist of bi- or polynuclear complexes with two or more metal coordination centres that are not linked by metal-metal bonds. It includes examples such as cyclopentadienyl-ligated zirconium units bridged by imidazole ligands, which find use in chemical or physical processes like catalysis.
[B01J 2531/0208] Bimetallic complexes, i.e. comprising one or more units of two metals, with metal-metal bonds but no all-metal (M)n rings, e.g. Cr -- This CPC subclass covers bimetallic complexes that contain two different metal atoms bonded directly to each other, but do not form extended all-metal rings; an example is a chromium-based heterobimetallic structure.
[B01J 2531/0211] Metal clusters, i.e. complexes comprising 3 to about 1000 metal atoms with metal-metal bonds to provide one or more all-metal (M)n rings, e.g. Rh -- B01J 2531/0211 denotes metal clusters-complexes that contain three to roughly one thousand metal atoms linked by metal-metal bonds and often forming all-metal rings such as (M)n structures. These clusters are classified under chemical or physical processes for separating operations, reflecting their use as catalytic systems in reactions like hydrogenation, oxidation or other transformations.
[B01J 2531/0213] Complexes without C-metal linkages -- This CPC entry concerns catalytic systems that employ complex compounds in which the carbon atoms are not directly bonded to a metal centre. Such metal-free C-linkages are used in chemical or physical separation processes, including catalysis and colloid chemistry.
[B01J 2531/0216] Bi- or polynuclear complexes, i.e. comprising two or more metal coordination centres, without metal-metal bonds, e.g. Cp(Lx)Zr-imidazole-Zr(Lx)Cp -- B01J 2531/0216 covers bi- or polynuclear coordination complexes that contain two or more metal centers but lack direct metal-metal bonds, such as Cp(Lx)Zr-imidazole-Zr(Lx)Cp. These compounds are used in catalytic or separating processes within chemical or physical operations. The class resides under the hierarchy of performing operations, separating, and chemical or physical processes.
[B01J 2531/0219] Bimetallic complexes, i.e. comprising one or more units of two metals, with metal-metal bonds but no all-metal (M)n rings, e.g. Cr -- This CPC subclass covers bimetallic coordination compounds that contain direct metal-metal bonds between two different metals, excluding all-metal ring structures, and is relevant to catalytic and separation processes within chemical or physical operations.
[B01J 2531/0222] Metal clusters, i.e. complexes comprising 3 to about 1000 metal atoms with metal-metal bonds to provide one or more all-metal (M)n rings, e.g. Rh -- This CPC subclass (B01J 2531/0222) covers metal clusters, which are complexes of three to roughly a thousand metal atoms connected by metal-metal bonds to form one or more all-metal rings, such as rhodium clusters. These materials are treated as catalytic systems in processes that involve separating chemical or physical reactions, and they fall under the broader category of performing operations for separation.
[B01J 2531/0225] Complexes comprising pentahapto-cyclopentadienyl analogues -- The CPC subclass B01J 2531/0225 covers catalytic systems that contain complexes where a metal is coordinated by pentahapto-cyclopentadienyl analogues, i.e., five-atom haptic ligands similar to cyclopentadienyl. These organometallic complexes are used in chemical or physical processes such as separation, catalysis, or colloid chemistry.
[B01J 2531/0227] Carbollide ligands, i.e. [nido-CnB(11-n)H11](4-n)- in which n is 1-3 -- B01J 2531/0227 covers carbollide ligands of the formula [nido-CₙB₁₁₋ₙH₁₁]⁴⁻ where n = 1-3, used to coordinate metal centers in catalytic systems. These ligands are part of chemical or physical processes such as catalysis, fitting within the broader categories of performing operations, separating, and chemical or physical processes. They enable the design of specialized catalysts for a range of reactions.
[B01J 2531/023] Phospholyl ligands, i.e. [CnP(5-n)Rn]- in which n is 0-4 and R is H or hydrocarbyl, or analogous condensed ring systems -- The subclass B01J 2531/023 covers phospholyl ligands of formula [CnP(5-n)Rn]- where n is 0-4 and R is hydrogen or a hydrocarbyl group, as well as analogous condensed-ring systems, used in catalytic or separating chemical processes.
[B01J 2531/0233] Aza-Cp ligands, i.e. [CnN(5-n)Rn]- in which n is 0-4 and R is H or hydrocarbyl, or analogous condensed ring systems -- The CPC subclass B01J 2531/0233 concerns catalytic systems that use aza-cyclopentadienyl (aza-Cp) ligands of the formula [CnN(5-n)Rn] where n ranges from 0 to 4 and R is hydrogen or a hydrocarbyl group, or analogous condensed-ring structures. These ligands are employed in chemical or physical operations such as separations and catalysis, fitting under Performing Operations - Separating - Chemical or Physical Processes.
[B01J 2531/0236] Azaborolyl ligands, e.g. 1,2-azaborolyl -- Entry B01J 2531/0236 covers azaborolyl ligands such as 1,2-azaborolyl, which are used as coordinating entities in catalytic systems. These ligands fall under the subclass for chemical or physical processes involving separation and performing operations, particularly in catalysis and colloid chemistry. The classification is part of the CPC system shared by the EPO and USPTO.
[B01J 2531/0238] Complexes comprising multidentate ligands, i.e. more than 2 ionic or coordinative bonds from the central metal to the ligand, the latter having at least two donor atoms, e.g. N, O, S, P -- B01J 2531/0238 covers metal complexes that contain multidentate ligands, meaning each ligand forms three or more ionic or coordinative bonds with the central metal atom and provides at least two donor atoms such as nitrogen, oxygen, sulfur or phosphorus. These structures are classified under the CPC hierarchy for performing operations, separating, and chemical or physical processes like catalysis. The code is used in both the EPO and USPTO patent databases.
[B01J 2531/0241] Rigid ligands, e.g. extended sp2-carbon frameworks or geminal di- or trisubstitution -- B01J 2531/0241 pertains to rigid ligands employed in catalytic systems, including extended sp2-carbon frameworks and geminal di- or trisubstituted structures. These ligands maintain a fixed geometry that can improve selectivity, activity and stability of catalysts, fitting within the separating chemical or physical processes branch of the CPC.
[B01J 2531/0244] Pincer-type complexes, i.e. consisting of a tridentate skeleton bound to a metal, e.g. by one to three metal-carbon sigma-bonds -- B01J 2531/0244 covers pincer-type complexes, which are tridentate ligands that bind a metal through one to three metal-carbon sigma bonds and are employed as catalytic systems in chemical or physical processes such as catalysis and colloid chemistry.
[B01J 2531/0247] Tripodal ligands, e.g. comprising the tris(pyrazolyl)borate skeleton, "tpz", neutral analogues thereof by CH/BH exchange or anionic analogues of the latter by exchange of one of the pyrazolyl groups for an anionic complexing group such as carboxylate or -R-Cp -- The code B01J 2531/0247 relates to catalytic systems that employ tripodal ligands built on a tris-pyrazolyl-borate framework. It includes neutral ligands altered by CH/BH exchange and anionic versions where a pyrazolyl group is replaced by a carboxylate or other anionic complexing group, used to bind metals in catalytic or colloidal processes.
[B01J 2531/025] Ligands with a porphyrin ring system or analogues thereof, e.g. phthalocyanines, corroles -- B01J 2531/025 covers ligands that contain a porphyrin macro-ring or related analogues such as phthalocyanines and corroles, which are used as catalytic components in chemical or physical separation processes. The entry falls under the hierarchy of performing operations, separating, and catalytic or colloid chemistry.
[B01J 2531/0252] Salen ligands or analogues, e.g. derived from ethylenediamine and salicylaldehyde -- B01J 2531/0252 classifies salen-type ligands and their analogues, such as compounds derived from ethylenediamine and salicylaldehyde, which serve as chelating agents or catalyst components in chemical processes. These substances are used in catalytic or separation operations, fitting within the broader CPC hierarchy of performing operations, separating, and chemical or physical processes.
[B01J 2531/0255] Ligands comprising the N2S2 or N2P2 donor atom set, e.g. diiminodithiolates or diiminodiphosphines with complete pi-conjugation between all donor centres -- The B01J 2531/0255 entry covers ligands that contain an N₂S₂ or N₂P₂ donor set, such as diiminodithiolates and diiminodiphosphines, where the donor atoms are linked by a fully conjugated π-system. These ligands are used in catalytic or other chemical/physical separating processes within the broader performing-operations classification.
[B01J 2531/0258] Flexible ligands, e.g. mainly sp3-carbon framework as exemplified by the "tedicyp" ligand, i.e. cis-cis-cis-1,2,3,4-tetrakis(diphenylphosphinomethyl)cyclopentane -- The CPC entry B01J 2531/0258 treats flexible ligands formed on an sp³-carbon backbone, exemplified by the "tedicyp" ligand-a cyclopentane bearing four diphenylphosphinomethyl groups in a cis-cis-cis configuration. Such ligands are used to modify catalytic systems in chemical and physical separation processes.
[B01J 2531/0261] Complexes comprising ligands with non-tetrahedral chirality -- This CPC subgroup pertains to catalytic complexes whose ligands display chiral elements that are not based on a tetrahedral stereocenter, such as axial, planar or helical configurations. The classification is used for processes that involve separation or transformation in catalysis and related chemical or physical operations.
[B01J 2531/0263] Planar chiral ligands, e.g. derived from donor-substituted paracyclophanes and metallocenes or from substituted arenes -- The B01J 2531/0263 subclass covers planar-chiral ligands employed in catalytic systems, specifically those originating from donor-substituted paracyclophanes, metallocenes or substituted arenes. These ligands are designed to induce chirality in reactions such as asymmetric hydrogenation, hydroformylation or cross-coupling, and are classified under performing operations, separating, chemical or physical processes like catalysis.
[B01J 2531/0266] Axially chiral or atropisomeric ligands, e.g. bulky biaryls such as donor-substituted binaphthalenes, e.g. "BINAP" or "BINOL" -- The CPC code B01J 2531/0266 falls under Performing Operations → Separating → Chemical or Physical Processes, specifically catalytic systems, and designates axially chiral or atropisomeric ligands such as bulky biaryl compounds. Typical examples include donor-substituted binaphthalenes like BINAP or BINOL, which are used to induce stereoselectivity in catalytic reactions.
[B01J 2531/0269] Complexes comprising ligands derived from the natural chiral pool or otherwise having a characteristic structure or geometry -- This CPC subclass B01J 2531/0269 falls under Performing Operations - Separating - Chemical or Physical Processes and concerns catalytic complexes whose ligands are derived from the natural chiral pool or are designed with a distinctive structural or geometric arrangement, enabling selective reactions or separations in catalysis and related processes.
[B01J 2531/0272] derived from carbohydrates, including e.g. tartrates or DIOP -- B01J 2531/0272 covers catalytic systems that are derived from carbohydrates such as tartrates or DIOP. These catalysts are used in chemical or physical processes for separating substances within the performing operations class. The entry pertains to carbohydrate-based ligands or catalysts employed in catalytic separations.
[B01J 2531/0275] derived from amino acids -- This subclass covers catalytic systems that are derived from or based on amino acids and employed in separation operations such as extraction, precipitation, or membrane filtration. It is situated under performing operations → separating → chemical or physical processes, for example catalysis.
[B01J 2531/0277] derived from fullerenes and analogues, e.g. buckybowls or Cp5Cp -- B01J 2531/0277 covers catalytic systems that incorporate carbon-based structures drawn from fullerenes and related analogues such as buckybowls or pentacyclopentadienyl (Cp₅Cp) units. These materials are used in chemical or physical processes, including separation operations, where their unique electronic and steric properties enhance catalytic activity or selectivity. The entry falls under the broader categories of performing operations, separating, and chemical or physical processes.
[B01J 2531/028] comprising affinity tags, e.g. for recovery -- This subclass concerns catalytic systems that include affinity tags to allow selective binding to a solid support, facilitating straightforward recovery and reuse of the catalyst. It is situated under separating operations within chemical or physical processes in the Performing Operations hierarchy.
[B01J 2531/0283] The bonding to the affinity counterpart occurring via hydrogen bonding -- B01J 2531/0283 covers separation techniques that exploit hydrogen-bond interactions between an analyte and an affinity ligand, enabling selective binding and release of target molecules. The subclass falls under chemical or physical processes related to catalysis and colloid chemistry within the broader categories of performing operations and separating.
[B01J 2531/0286] Complexes comprising ligands or other components characterized by their function -- The CPC entry B01J 2531/0286 falls under Performing Operations → Separating → Chemical or Physical Processes such as catalysis or colloid chemistry. It covers complexes that contain ligands or other components defined primarily by their functional role in catalytic systems, rather than by their specific chemical structure.
[B01J 2531/0288] Sterically demanding or shielding ligands -- Sterically demanding or shielding ligands are bulky organic or organometallic molecules that surround a metal centre in a catalyst, limiting access of substrates and thus influencing reaction rates and selectivity. By providing steric protection they can prevent undesirable side reactions, enhance catalyst stability, and enable control of regio- or enantio-selectivity in chemical transformations.
[B01J 2531/0291] Ligands adapted to form modular catalysts, e.g. self-associating building blocks as exemplified in the patent document EP-A-1 479 439 -- This CPC subclass B01J 2531/0291 covers ligands designed to assemble into modular catalytic systems, such as self-associating building blocks that can be combined to form tailored catalysts. The classification falls under performing operations, separating, and chemical or physical processes like catalysis, and is illustrated by EP-A-1479439.
[B01J 2531/0294] "Non-innocent" or "non-spectator" ligands, i.e. ligands described as, or evidently, taking part in the catalytic reaction beyond merely stabilizing the central metal as spectator or ancilliary ligands, e.g. by electron transfer to or from the central metal or by intra-/intermolecular chemical reactions, e.g. disulfide coupling, H-abstraction -- B01J 2531/0294 covers catalytic systems that use non-innocent or non-spectator ligands, which actively take part in the reaction by transferring electrons or undergoing chemical transformations such as disulfide coupling or hydrogen abstraction, rather than merely stabilising the central metal.
[B01J 2531/0297] Non-coordinating anions -- B01J 2531/0297 falls under CPC section B01J for chemical or physical processes, specifically within the performing-operations hierarchy of separating and catalysis. The entry "Non-coordinating anions" covers anions that do not bind to the active metal centre of a catalyst, thereby preserving its reactivity and selectivity in homogeneous or heterogeneous catalytic systems. This classification is used for inventions that employ such anions to control or enhance catalytic performance.
[B01J 2531/10] Complexes comprising metals of Group I (IA or IB) as the central metal -- B01J 2531/10 covers catalytic complexes whose central atom is a Group I metal, including alkali and coinage metals, used in chemical or physical processes such as separation, catalysis, or colloid chemistry. These complexes may serve as active sites or promoters in reactions that involve performing operations or separating mixtures.

[B01J 2540/00] Compositional aspects of coordination complexes or ligands in catalyst systems -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Additional information regarding catalytic systems classified in B01J31/00 and not covered by B01J2531/00 .  In this group further compositional aspects of coordination complexes and/or ligands classified in B01J31/16 - B01J31/24 are indexed. Only such aspects are provided for and are intended to be indexed, which provide additional information regarding the complexes and/or ligands classified in B01J31/16 - B01J31/24 and indexed in B01J2531/00 , e.g. non-coordinating substituents on the ligand described as essential, and which are explicitly mentioned in the claims or the worked examples.  The primary subdivision is according to heteroatoms in such substituents, cf. indexing codes B01J2540/10 (oxygen), B01J2540/20 (halogen), B01J2540/30 (sulfur), B01J2540/40 (nitrogen), B01J2540/50 (phosphorus). The respective lower groups define these heteroatom groups further.  A further primary subdivision is according to generic statements of function of substituent groups on the ligand, cf. B01J2540/60 . These functions must be presented as essential in the application, either in the description or the claims. The focus here is more on the groups as such, whereas the codes in B01J2531/00 , e.g. B01J2531/028 or B01J2531/0291 are used for the overall ligand. In case of doubt the codes of B01J2531/00 should be used.
[B01J 2540/10] Non-coordinating groups comprising only oxygen beside carbon or hydrogen -- B01J 2540/10 covers catalyst systems in which the ligands or coordination complexes contain non-coordinating substituents that are composed solely of oxygen atoms attached to carbon or hydrogen. These groups do not bind to the metal centre and are defined by the absence of heteroatoms such as nitrogen, phosphorus or sulfur. The subclass aids in distinguishing catalyst compositions that rely on purely oxide-based ancillary groups.
[B01J 2540/12] Carboxylic acid groups -- B01J 2540/12 covers catalyst systems in which the coordination complex or its ligands contain carboxylic-acid functional groups, detailing how these groups affect the composition and performance of the catalyst during chemical or physical separation processes such as catalysis or colloid chemistry.
[B01J 2540/20] Non-coordinating groups comprising halogens -- The CPC subgroup B01J 2540/20 relates to catalyst systems in which halogen-containing groups are present on ligands or in the surrounding matrix but do not coordinate to the metal centre. It covers the use of such non-coordinating halogen groups to modify the behaviour of coordination complexes in chemical or physical separating processes such as catalysis.
[B01J 2540/22] comprising fluorine, e.g. trifluoroacetate -- B01J 2540/22 covers catalyst systems in which the coordination complex or its ligands contain fluorine, such as compounds derived from trifluoroacetate. The entry addresses the compositional aspects of these fluorinated ligands within chemical or physical separation processes. It falls under the broader categories of performing operations, separating, and chemical or physical processes like catalysis.

[B01J 2540/30] Non-coordinating groups comprising sulfur -- B01J 2540/30 covers catalyst systems where sulfur-containing groups are present but do not bind to the metal centre, acting as non-coordinating moieties. It falls under separating operations in chemical or physical processes, specifically addressing the compositional aspects of ligands or coordination complexes that include inert sulfur groups. This subclass helps identify formulations where sulfur improves stability or activity without direct metal coordination.

[B01J 29/046] Chromiasilicates; Aluminochromosilicates  B01J29/005  takes precedence -- B01J 29/046 covers catalysts that are chromiasilicates or aluminochromosilicates, which are molecular-sieve materials containing chromium incorporated into a silicate framework. The broader subclass B01J29/005 for silicate catalysts with other metals has priority over this specific group.
[B01J 29/047] Germanosilicates; Aluminogermanosilicates  B01J29/005  takes precedence -- The subclass B01J 29/047 designates catalysts that consist of germanosilicate or aluminogermanosilicate molecular sieves, employed in separating operations or catalytic colloid chemistry. The class falls under performing operations, separating, and chemical or physical processes like catalysis.

These materials are employed in chemical or physical processes such as catalysis and separation, where the metal component provides active sites within the porous sieve structure.

It is located under Performing Operations - Separating - Chemical or Physical Processes, e.g. catalysis or colloid chemistry.
