[B01J 29/7484] TON-type, e.g. Theta-1, ISI-1, KZ-2, NU-10 or ZSM-22 -- This subclass covers molecular-sieve catalysts that belong to the TON family, such as Theta-1, ISI-1, KZ-2, NU-10 or ZSM-22, which are employed in catalytic or colloidal processes for chemical or physical separations. It includes the structure, preparation and use of these zeolitic materials as active catalytic phases.
[B01J 29/7492] MTT-type, e.g. ZSM-23, KZ-1, ISI-4 or EU-13 -- The entry B01J 29/7492 covers catalysts that are molecular sieves of the MTT structural type, including examples such as ZSM-23, KZ-1, ISI-4 and EU-13. These materials are used in chemical or physical processes for separating substances, often in catalytic reactions.
[B01J 29/76] Iron group metals or copper -- B01J 29/76 covers catalysts that incorporate molecular sieves where the active component is an iron-group metal or copper. These catalysts are used in processes such as oxidation, reduction, or other chemical transformations where the sieve provides support or shape selectivity. The classification falls under separating chemical or physical processes in performing operations.
[B01J 29/7607] A-type -- The subclass B01J 29/7607 covers catalysts that incorporate molecular sieves, specifically A-type sieves, used in chemical or physical processes such as catalysis or colloid chemistry. These materials serve to promote reactions or separations by providing defined pore structures that enhance selectivity and activity.
[B01J 29/7615] Zeolite Beta -- The CPC entry B01J 29/7615 is classified under performing operations, separating, chemical or physical processes and concerns catalysts that incorporate molecular-sieve materials, specifically zeolite Beta. Zeolite Beta is a crystalline aluminosilicate with a large pore system, used as a solid-acid catalyst in reactions such as hydrocarbon cracking, alkylation and isomerisation.
[B01J 29/7623] MAZ-type, e.g. Mazzite, Omega, ZSM-4 or LZ-202 -- B01J 29/7623 covers catalysts that incorporate MAZ-type molecular sieves-zeolitic materials such as Mazzite, Omega, ZSM-4 or LZ-202-used to facilitate separating operations in chemical or physical processes, often as heterogeneous catalysts for reactions like catalysis or adsorption.
[B01J 29/763] CHA-type, e.g. Chabazite, LZ-218 -- B01J 29/763 designates catalysts that incorporate molecular sieves of the CHA type, such as chabazite or LZ-218. These porous materials are used in chemical or physical processes, including catalytic reactions, where their framework provides active sites for improved performance.
[B01J 29/7638] EMT-type, e.g. EMC-2, ECR-30, CSZ-1, ZSM-3 or ZSM-20 -- B01J 29/7638 relates to catalysts that contain molecular sieves of the EMT structural type, such as EMC-2, ECR-30, CSZ-1, ZSM-3 or ZSM-20, and are employed in chemical or physical processes-including catalytic reactions and separation operations-where the sieve's porous framework provides active sites or shape-selective effects.
[B01J 29/7646] EUO-type, e.g. EU-1, TPZ-3 or ZSM-50 -- B01J 29/7646 covers catalytic materials that contain molecular-sieve zeolites of the EUO family, such as EU-1, TPZ-3 or ZSM-50, used in chemical or physical processes for separating components. The subclass is situated under performing operations, separating, and catalytic or colloid chemistry, and specifies zeolite-based catalysts for selective reaction pathways.
[B01J 29/7653] MFS-type, e.g. ZSM-57 -- MFS-type molecular sieves such as ZSM-57 are synthetic zeolitic catalysts characterised by a medium-pore framework and a specific channel system that facilitates shape-selective reactions, especially in hydrocarbon conversion and separation processes. They are employed to increase reaction rates and selectivity in catalytic cracking, alkylation, isomerisation and related chemical or physical operations where precise molecular sieving is required.
[B01J 29/7661] MRE-type, e.g. ZSM-48 -- B01J 29/7661 covers catalysts that are molecular-sieve materials of the MRE structural type, exemplified by ZSM-48. These zeolitic catalysts are used to facilitate chemical reactions or separations, particularly in processes such as catalysis, adsorption, and ion exchange within the broader category of separating operations.
[B01J 29/7669] MTW-type, e.g. ZSM-12, NU-13, TPZ-12 or Theta-3 -- The entry B01J 29/7669 covers catalytic compositions that contain molecular-sieve crystals of the MTW structural type, such as ZSM-12, NU-13, TPZ-12 or Theta-3. These sieves are used as heterogeneous catalysts in chemical or physical processes, including separation and transformation reactions.
[B01J 29/7676] MWW-type, e.g. MCM-22, ERB-1, ITQ-1, PSH-3 or SSZ-25 -- This classification entry pertains to catalysts that include molecular sieves of the MWW type, such as MCM-22, ERB-1, ITQ-1, PSH-3, and SSZ-25, which are used in chemical processes for their unique structural properties.
[B01J 29/7684] TON-type, e.g. Theta-1, ISI-1, KZ-2, NU-10 or ZSM-22 -- B01J 29/7684 classifies catalysts that incorporate TON-type molecular sieves such as Theta-1, ISI-1, KZ-2, NU-10 or ZSM-22, which are employed in separation-related catalytic processes.
[B01J 29/7692] MTT-type, e.g. ZSM-23, KZ-1, ISI-4 or EU-13 -- B01J 29/7692 covers catalytic processes that employ molecular sieves of the MTT structural type, such as ZSM-23, KZ-1, ISI-4 or EU-13, as active components. These sieves act as solid acid or shape-selective catalysts in chemical transformations, especially in separation and conversion reactions within the broader category of separating and chemical/physical processes.
[B01J 29/78] containing arsenic, antimony, bismuth, vanadium, niobium, tantalum, polonium, chromium, molybdenum, tungsten, manganese, technetium or rhenium -- The subclass B01J 29/78 covers molecular-sieve catalysts that incorporate one or more of the elements arsenic, antimony, bismuth, vanadium, niobium, tantalum, polonium, chromium, molybdenum, tungsten, manganese, technetium or rhenium in their structure or active sites. It is applied to chemical or physical processes such as catalysis or separation where these element-containing sieves serve as the functional material.
[B01J 29/7807] A-type -- The B01J 29/7807 subclass defines A-type catalysts that incorporate molecular sieves as the active component in chemical or physical operations such as catalysis, adsorption or ion-exchange. These catalysts are used in processes that involve separating substances or facilitating reactions within the broader performing-operations and separating categories.
[B01J 29/7815] Zeolite Beta -- B01J 29/7815 is a CPC entry classified under Performing Operations, Separating, Chemical or Physical Processes such as Catalysis or Colloid Chemistry, and it concerns catalysts comprising molecular sieves. The entry specifically covers the zeolite Beta crystalline aluminosilicate used as a catalyst in various chemical reactions.
[B01J 29/7823] MAZ-type, e.g. Mazzite, Omega, ZSM-4 or LZ-202 -- B01J 29/7823 covers catalysts made from MAZ-type molecular sieves such as Mazzite, Omega, ZSM-4 or LZ-202. These porous inorganic materials are used in catalytic or separating operations that rely on their uniform pore structures to facilitate reactions or physical separations.
[B01J 29/783] CHA-type, e.g. Chabazite, LZ-218 -- B01J 29/783 covers catalysts that are molecular sieves of the CHA (chabazite) structural type, such as chabazite or LZ-218. These materials are used in catalytic processes, including separation, catalysis and colloid chemistry, where their uniform pore structure provides shape-selective activity.
[B01J 29/7838] EMT-type, e.g. EMC-2, ECR-30, CSZ-1, ZSM-3 or ZSM-20 -- The CPC entry B01J 29/7838 pertains to catalysts that incorporate EMT-type molecular sieves-examples include EMC-2, ECR-30, CSZ-1, ZSM-3 and ZSM-20-and are employed in chemical or physical processes such as catalysis or colloid chemistry.
[B01J 29/7846] EUO-type, e.g. EU-1, TPZ-3 or ZSM-50 -- B01J 29/7846 covers catalysts made from molecular sieves of the EUO structural type, such as EU-1, TPZ-3 or ZSM-50, employed in catalytic or separating operations. These materials belong to the class of porous zeolitic frameworks used to accelerate chemical reactions or facilitate separation processes.
[B01J 29/7853] MFS-type, e.g. ZSM-57 -- B01J 29/7853 covers catalysts that are molecular sieves of the MFS structural type, exemplified by ZSM-57, used in chemical or physical processes such as catalytic separations or reactions. These materials combine the pore architecture of MFS zeolites with catalytic activity for improved performance in industrial processes.
[B01J 29/7861] MRE-type, e.g. ZSM-48 -- B01J 29/7861 covers catalysts that are molecular-sieve structures of the MRE type, exemplified by ZSM-48, employed in chemical or physical separating processes such as catalysis. These materials operate by providing uniform pores that facilitate selective adsorption and reaction of molecules.
[B01J 29/7869] MTW-type, e.g. ZSM-12, NU-13, TPZ-12 or Theta-3 -- B01J 29/7869 covers catalysts that contain molecular-sieve crystals with an MTW framework, exemplified by materials such as ZSM-12, NU-13, TPZ-12 or Theta-3. These sieves are used in catalytic or colloidal processes where the porous structure provides shape-selective activity.
[B01J 29/7876] MWW-type, e.g. MCM-22, ERB-1, ITQ-1, PSH-3 or SSZ-25 -- B01J 29/7876 relates to catalysts that contain molecular sieves with the MWW framework type, exemplified by materials such as MCM-22, ERB-1, ITQ-1, PSH-3 or SSZ-25. These sieves are employed in catalytic or separation operations, particularly in chemical or physical processes including catalysis and colloid chemistry.
[B01J 29/7884] TON-type, e.g. Theta-1, ISI-1, KZ-2, NU-10 or ZSM-22 -- The B01J 29/7884 entry classifies catalysts that contain TON-type molecular sieves, such as Theta-1, ISI-1, KZ-2, NU-10 or ZSM-22, which are employed in chemical or physical processes like catalysis and separation. It falls under the hierarchy of performing operations, separating, and chemical or physical processes, and pertains specifically to catalyst compositions based on these zeolitic structures.
[B01J 29/7892] MTT-type, e.g. ZSM-23, KZ-1, ISI-4 or EU-13 -- B01J 29/7892 covers catalysts that contain molecular sieves of the MTT-type, such as ZSM-23, KZ-1, ISI-4 or EU-13, used in chemical or physical processes including catalysis and separation. These zeolite catalysts have a distinctive pore system that improves activity and selectivity in reactions such as cracking, isomerization and adsorption.
[B01J 29/80] Mixtures of different zeolites -- B01J 29/80 relates to mixtures of different zeolite molecular sieves employed as catalysts or adsorbents in chemical or physical processes such as separation, catalysis, or ion exchange, exploiting complementary pore structures or acidities to enhance reaction or adsorption performance.
[B01J 29/82] Phosphates -- B01J 29/82 covers the use of phosphate compounds as catalysts that incorporate molecular sieves in separation processes such as catalysis or colloid chemistry. The class is part of the broader group of performing operations and separating chemical or physical processes.
[B01J 29/83] Aluminophosphates [APO compounds] -- B01J 29/83 covers aluminophosphate (APO) molecular-sieve materials used as catalysts in separation, catalytic or colloidal processes. The subclass includes porous inorganic compounds whose framework contains aluminum, phosphorus and oxygen, providing defined pore structures that facilitate catalytic activity, adsorption or molecular-sieving functions.
[B01J 29/84] Aluminophosphates containing other elements, e.g. metals, boron -- B01J 29/84 covers catalytic molecular sieves based on aluminophosphate frameworks that incorporate additional elements such as metals or boron. These mixed-element aluminophosphates are used to enhance catalytic activity or selectivity in separation and chemical/physical processes, including reactions like catalysis and adsorption.
[B01J 29/85] Silicoaluminophosphates [SAPO compounds] -- B01J 29/85 covers silicoaluminophosphates, known as SAPO compounds, used as molecular-sieve catalysts in chemical reactions such as acid catalysis, hydrocracking and selective oxidation. The classification applies to processes where SAPO materials serve as the active phase for separating reactants or products, often in the presence of other catalysts.
[B01J 29/86] Borosilicates -- B01J 29/86 covers borosilicate materials used as catalysts that incorporate molecular-sieve structures. These catalysts fall within the broader class of chemical or physical processes for separating substances, specifically under performing operations that involve catalysis or colloid chemistry.
[B01J 29/87] Gallosilicates -- B01J 29/87 refers to gallosilicates, which are silicate structures incorporating gallium that function as molecular-sieve catalysts. These materials are employed in separation operations such as catalysis, adsorption and ion exchange, exploiting their porous framework to promote chemical or physical processes.
[B01J 29/88] Ferrosilicates -- B01J 29/88 covers ferrosilicate compounds employed as catalysts, particularly molecular sieves, in processes that separate substances through chemical or physical means such as catalysis or colloid chemistry. These materials offer high surface area and ion-exchange properties, enabling efficient catalytic or sorptive actions in industrial separations.
[B01J 29/89] Silicates, aluminosilicates or borosilicates of titanium, zirconium or hafnium -- The CPC subclass B01J 29/89 relates to catalysts that are silicates, aluminosilicates or borosilicates containing titanium, zirconium or hafnium. These materials are molecular-sieve type catalysts used in chemical or physical processes such as catalysis or colloid chemistry, falling under the broader categories of performing operations, separating, and chemical or physical processes.
[B01J 29/90] Regeneration or reactivation -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Regeneration or reactivation of catalysts comprising molecular sieves  **Glossary:** - Regeneration, Reactivation: a substance microscopically dispersed evenly throughout another substance. The colloidal system, which may be solid, liquid or gaseous, has a very high stability, i.e. precipitation does not occur under the influence of gravity. - Microcapsule: a core-shell particle with a stable, non-transient shell
[B01J 3/00] Processes of utilising sub-atmospheric or super-atmospheric pressure to effect chemical or physical change of matter -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Pressure vessels for containing or storing compressed, liquefied or solidified gases -> F17C
[B01J 3/002] Component parts of these vessels not mentioned in  B01J3/004 ,  B01J3/006 ,  B01J3/02  -  B01J3/08 ; Measures taken in conjunction with the process to be carried out, e.g. safety measures -- B01J 3/002 covers components of reactors and pressure vessels used in sub- or super-atmospheric processes that are not specified in the related sub-groups B01J3/004, B01J3/006, B01J3/02-08, and includes any safety or auxiliary measures employed together with the process.
[B01J 3/004] Sight-glasses therefor  see  also  G02B -- The Cooperative Patent Classification code B01J 3/004 pertains to sight-glasses used in processes that involve sub-atmospheric or super-atmospheric pressures for chemical or physical changes, with additional reference to the optics classification G02B.
[B01J 3/006] Processes utilising sub-atmospheric pressure; Apparatus therefor -- B01J 3/006 covers processes that employ vacuum or high pressure to bring about chemical or physical transformations of substances, and includes the equipment designed for such pressure-based operations. It falls under separating, chemical or physical processes within the Performing Operations section of the CPC.
[B01J 3/008] Processes carried out under supercritical conditions -- The entry B01J 3/008 covers processes that employ supercritical fluids-materials kept at temperatures and pressures above their critical point-to induce chemical or physical changes in matter. By operating under supercritical conditions, the fluid has both gas-like diffusivity and liquid-like solvating power, enabling efficient reactions, separations, extractions and polymerisations that would be difficult under ordinary pressures.
[B01J 3/02] Feed or outlet devices therefor -- B01J 3/02 covers devices that feed material into or discharge it from reactors that operate at pressures below or above atmospheric, such as pumps, valves, nozzles, and venting assemblies. These components are used in chemical or physical processes, including catalysis and colloid chemistry, where sub- or super-atmospheric pressure is employed to drive reactions or separations.
[B01J 3/03] Pressure vessels, or vacuum vessels, having closure members or seals specially adapted therefor -- The B01J 3/03 subclass covers pressure or vacuum vessels that are equipped with specially designed closure members or seals to maintain the required sub-atmospheric or super-atmospheric conditions during chemical or physical processes. These vessels are used where precise containment of gases or liquids is essential for reactions such as catalysis, colloid chemistry, or other high-pressure or high-vacuum operations.
[B01J 3/04] Pressure vessels, e.g. autoclaves -- This classification concerns pressure vessels such as autoclaves employed to create sub-atmospheric or super-atmospheric conditions for chemical or physical transformations. It falls under B01J 3/04 within the CPC hierarchy of Performing Operations → Separating → Chemical or Physical Processes.
[B01J 3/042] in the form of a tube -- B01J 3/042 relates to processes that employ sub-atmospheric or super-atmospheric pressure to induce chemical or physical changes when the reaction medium is confined within a tube, falling under separating operations in the chemical or physical processes category.
[B01J 3/044] in the form of a loop -- The CPC entry B01J 3/044 covers processes that employ sub-atmospheric or super-atmospheric pressure in a closed-loop configuration, where the material circulates continuously through a reactor or pipe loop to achieve a chemical or physical transformation. Typical applications include loop-type reactors for catalysis, polymerisation, or gas-liquid contacting under controlled pressure.
[B01J 3/046] Pressure-balanced vessels -- This CPC entry covers pressure-balanced vessels used in processes that employ sub-atmospheric or super-atmospheric pressure to induce chemical or physical changes. These vessels maintain a pressure differential while allowing reaction mixtures to be processed, improving safety and efficiency in catalytic or colloidal operations.
[B01J 3/048] Multiwall, strip or filament wound vessels  for pressurised gas vessels  F17C1/06 ; for making them  B29 -- The CPC entry B01J 3/048 covers processes for producing multi-wall, strip-wound or filament-wound vessels intended to contain pressurised gases, encompassing the fabrication methods defined in B29 and relating to the design class F17C1/06 for such pressure vessels.
[B01J 3/06] Processes using ultra-high pressure, e.g. for the formation of diamonds -- B01J 3/06 covers processes that employ ultra-high pressure, typically above several gigapascals, to induce chemical or physical transformations such as the synthesis of diamonds or other superhard materials. The methods rely on sub- or super-atmospheric pressure conditions to change matter's structure, often in combination with catalysts or high temperatures. This subclass falls under separating operations within chemical and physical processes.
[B01J 3/062] characterised by the composition of the materials to be processed -- B01J 3/062 covers separating processes that employ sub-atmospheric or super-atmospheric pressure to induce a chemical or physical change, where the defining characteristic is the specific composition of the material being processed. The entry is relevant to operations such as catalysis or colloid chemistry in which pressure conditions are exploited and the feedstock's makeup determines the method's application.
[B01J 3/065] Presses for the formation of diamonds or boronitrides -- B01J 3/065 covers presses that generate the high pressures required to synthesize diamonds or boronitrides, typically using hydraulic or mechanical systems to create sub-atmospheric or super-atmospheric conditions for crystal growth. The technology is employed in high-pressure, high-temperature (HPHT) processes for producing these ultra-hard materials.
[B01J 3/067] Presses using a plurality of pressing members working in different directions -- B01J 3/067 pertains to presses that employ several pressing members that act in different directions to shape, compact or otherwise treat material, often under sub- or super-atmospheric pressure conditions. This subclass falls within the broader category of chemical or physical processes involving pressure-based operations.
[B01J 3/08] Application of shock waves for chemical reactions or for modifying the crystal structure of substances -- B01J 3/08 covers processes that employ shock waves - generated by rapid pressure changes such as explosions or implosions - to initiate chemical reactions or to alter the crystal lattice of materials.
[B01J 31/00] Catalysts comprising hydrides, coordination complexes or organic compounds -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The meaning of the term "catalyst" within this group encompasses catalyst systems, i.e. additives essential to the catalytic function of such systems are also included in this group, e.g. co-catalysts, specific reagents, solvents or solvent mixtures.  **Glossary:** - bimetallic complex: a discrete coordination complex comprising one or more units of two metals, same or different, with metal-metal bonds but no all-metal (M)n rings, e.g. Cr 2 (OAc) 4 , [(Mo 2 ) 4 (MeOPhNCNPhOMe) 8 (Ph(COO) 2 ) 4 ], [Ph 2 P-X-PPh 2 ]Pt(Cl)SnCl 3 , [Cp(CO) 2 Mn=] 2 Pb - bi- or polynuclear complex: a coordination complex comprising two or more same or different metal atoms, without M-M bonds; e.g. Cp(Lx)Zr-imidazole-Zr(Lx)Cp, [R 2 PCp-Fe-CpPR 2 ]Rh(L) n , [(salen)Co(III)]SbF 6 , Co(II)(salen)Fe(II)Cl 2 - carbenes (carbyne): a metal coordination complex comprising a formally divalent (trivalent) ligand with a neutral carbon atom bonded to the metal via two (three) unshared electrons, i.e. a formal derivative of the hypothetical ligand CR 2 (CR); the electrons may be paired or not - catalyst: covers also a carrier forming part of the catalyst, specific additives and co-catalysts - coordination complexes: all donor-acceptor compounds or complex ions comprising organic or inorganic, anionic or neutral Lewis basic ligands, attached to a Lewis acid central metal or metal ion through one or several complexing donor atoms with at least one lone-pair of electrons, e.g. N, O, S, P, to provide at least a sigma-bond; typically the maximum number of same or different ligands according to the coordination number, spatial requirements of the ligand and electronic configuration of the metal is bound in a predictable geometry; complexes of neutral, cationic or anionic hydrocarbon ligands with delocalised charge and/or bonding site, e.g. Pd-olefin complexes or metallocenes, are also included - ionic liquid: an organic salt in the liquid state at the reaction temperature employed, e.g. less than or equal to 100°C or less than or equal to 20°C ("room-temperature ionic liquid") - metal cluster: a coordination complex with 3 to about 1000 same or different metal atoms and further comprising M-M bonds to provide (M)n rings, i.e. size range from atomic to colloid dimensions; e.g. Rh x (CO) y , [R 3 P=N=PR 3 ] + [Ru 3 lr(CO) 13 ] - , [Rh 3 (DIPAMP) 3 (μ 3 -OMe) 2 ]BF 4 , Pt 4 (OAc) 8 , "Pd(OAc) 2 " = Pd 3 (OAc) 6 - metal-organic framework: crystalline compounds consisting of metal ions or clusters coordinated to often rigid organic molecules as linkers to form one-, two-, or three-dimensional structures; the linkers are often dicarboxylates, the metals often Zn, Cu, Fe or Al - N-Heterocyclic carbene: a saturated or unsaturated heterocyclic compound comprising a neutral carbon atom with a lone electron pair (in the typical case of singlet or "nucleophilic" carbenes) adjacent to at least one nitrogen atom - oligomer: compound with more than two identical repeating units connected to one another and typically 500 less than M less than 10000 g/mol; grouped with the respective polymers - organic complexes: all coordination complexes comprising organic ligands - organic compound: a compound in which carbon is bonded to - a second carbon; - at least one atom of hydrogen or halogen; or - nitrogen by a single or double bond; except cyanic acid (HOCN), cyanogen (NCCN), cyanamide (H 2 NCN), cyanogen halide (HalCN), hydrocyanic acid (HCN), isocyanic acid (HNCO), fulminic acid (HCNO) and metal carbides (MCCM) - organic ligand: a carbon-containing ligand bonded to a central metal in which said carbon is bonded to - a second carbon;- at least one atom of hydrogen or halogen; or - nitrogen by a single or double bond; except cyanic acid (HO-C≡N), cyanogen (N≡C-C≡N), cyanamide (H 2 N-C≡N), cyanogen halide (Hal-C≡N), hydrocyanic acid (HC≡N), isocyanic acid (HN=C=O), fulminic acid (HC≡N-O), carbides (C≡C) and the respective anionic ligands derived by deprotonation (O-C≡N, N=C=N, C≡N, N=C=O, C≡N-O) - organometallic complexes: all coordination complexes comprising a M-C bond, e.g. metal carbonyls; also includes complexes which are not strictly organometallic per se, e.g. comprising only N, O, S and/or P coordinated ligands, but are described as involving, or known to involve, organometallic intermediates and/or transition states during use, e.g. Group 8-10 metal complexes for a variety of catalytic reactions or steps thereof, such as oxidative addition, e.g. hydrogenation, carbonylation or epoxidation - organometallic compound: all organic compound wherein a metal or metalloid atom is bonded directly to a carbon fragment, the latter being formally anionic, no further neutral ligands being coordinated to the metal and the compound requiring no further cations for charge balance; e.g. M(CR 3 ) n with M=main group metal, n=valency of metal and R=H or hydrocarbyl - polymer: a macromolecular substance (typically M>10000 g/mol) comprising repeating units made up of one or several kinds of atoms or groups of atoms, which are identically connected to one another
[B01J 31/003] containing enzymes -- It designates chemical or physical processes, especially separating operations, that employ enzymes as catalytic agents. The subclass falls under catalysts comprising hydrides, coordination complexes or organic compounds and focuses on biocatalysis.
[B01J 31/006] comprising organic radicals, e.g. TEMPO -- The CPC subclass B01J 31/006 covers catalyst formulations that contain organic radicals, such as the nitroxyl radical TEMPO, either alone or in combination with other components. These radical-based catalysts are employed in chemical or physical processes, including oxidation-reduction reactions, where the radical functionality aids in substrate activation or electron transfer.
[B01J 31/02] containing organic compounds or metal hydrides -- **Definition:** This place covers:  B01J31/02 - B01J31/0274 : Catalysts comprising simple organic compounds as components of a catalyst system (e.g. solvent or additive) or catalysts per se (e.g. sulfonic acids), which can contain the heteroatoms (i.e. elements other than carbon or hydrogen) defined in B01J31/0201 - B01J31/0255 or other heteroatoms ( B01J31/0272 ), with the exception of metal atoms as heteroatoms (see B01J31/12 ).  B01J31/0277 - B01J31/0298 : Catalysts comprising ionic liquids, as components in catalyst systems or catalysts per se, the ionic liquid compounds being used in the molten state at the respective reaction temperature. Compounds likewise being organic onium salts but only used in the solid or dissolved state in multiphase mixtures (e.g. phase-transfer catalysts) and/or with melting points at temperatures above those normally used in organic synthesis (i.e. > 200 °C) would not be classified here but rather in the catalyst compound subgroups of B01J31/02 - B01J31/0274 .  B01J31/04 : Catalysts comprising carboxylic acids or their salts, with the exception of multi-metal carboxylate complexes like "Pd(II)acetate", i.e. Pd 3 (OAc) 6 or "Cr(II)acetate", i.e. Cr 2 (OAc) 4 and ionic liquids with carboxylate counter-anions (see B01J31/0277 ).  B01J31/06 - B01J31/10 : Catalysts comprising polymers and/or oligomers without metal-carbon bonds, including (sulfonated) ion-exchange resins.  B01J31/12 - B01J31/146 : Catalysts comprising organometallic compounds, including organometallic polymers and metal hydrides.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Catalysts comprising coordination complexes -> B01J31/16 - B01J31/24 - Multi-metal carboxylate complexes with bridging ligands -> B01J31/2226  **Glossary:** - Organic compound: a compound in which carbon is bonded to - a second carbon;- at least one atom of hydrogen or halogen; or - nitrogen by a single or double bond;except cyanic acid (HOCN), cyanogen (NCCN), cyanamide (H2NCN), cyanogen halide (HalCN), hydrocyanic acid (HCN), isocyanic acid (HNCO), fulminic acid (HCNO) and metal carbides (MCCM). - Metal amide: M + [NR 2 ] - - Metal guanidide: M + [RNC(NR2)NR] - and isomers - Onium salt: salts of cations derived by the protonation of mononuclear parent hydrides of a pnictogen (Grp. 15), chalcogen (Grp. 16), or halogen (Grp. 17), their hydrogen substitution derivatives R, typ. organic radicals or halogens, e.g. tetramethylammonium, and further derivatives having polyvalent additions, e.g. iminium, nitrilium. - Ionic liquid: an organic, typ. onium salt in the liquid state at the reaction temperature employed, e.g. ≤ 100 °C or ≤ 20 °C ("room-temperature ionic liquid")
[B01J 31/0201] Oxygen-containing compounds -- B01J 31/0201 covers oxygen-containing compounds that act as catalysts - such as hydrides, coordination complexes or organic molecules - in separating operations and related chemical or physical processes.
[B01J 31/0202] Alcohols or phenols -- B01J 31/0202 designates catalytic processes in which alcohols or phenols act as the active species, either as simple hydrides, coordination complexes or organic compounds, within separation or other chemical-physical operations. The subclass falls under the broader group concerning catalytic and colloidal chemistry for separating substances.
[B01J 31/0204] Ethers -- B01J 31/0204 covers ether compounds employed in catalytic systems, typically as ligands, solvents or promoter molecules in processes that use hydride-based, coordination-complex or organic catalysts. The class includes simple and substituted ethers that facilitate reactions such as hydrogenation, hydroformylation or other catalytic transformations.
[B01J 31/0205] comprising carbonyl groups or oxygen-containing derivatives, e.g. acetals, ketals, cyclic peroxides -- This CPC subclass defines catalysts that incorporate carbonyl groups or oxygen-containing derivatives such as acetals, ketals and cyclic peroxides, employed in chemical or physical processes like catalysis and separation. It is located under B01J 31/02 within the hierarchy of performing operations, separating, and catalysts comprising hydrides, coordination complexes or organic compounds.
[B01J 31/0207] Aldehydes or acetals -- B01J 31/0207 covers catalytic processes that employ hydrides, coordination-complex or organic catalysts to transform, separate or treat aldehydes or acetals, such as hydrogenation, oxidation or protective-group reactions involving these carbonyl compounds.
[B01J 31/0208] Ketones or ketals -- The B01J 31/0208 subclass covers separating and other chemical or physical operations that employ ketones or ketals as catalysts, such as hydride-containing, coordination-complex or organic catalytic systems, within the broader category of performing operations. It specifically addresses the use of these carbonyl-based compounds in processes like catalysis, extraction, or phase-transfer operations.
[B01J 31/0209] Esters of carboxylic or carbonic acids -- The entry covers catalytic processes that involve esters of carboxylic or carbonic acids, where the catalyst is a hydride, coordination complex or organic compound. It relates to chemical or physical operations, such as separation or purification steps, that use these esters as intermediates, solvents or reagents.
[B01J 31/0211] with a metal-oxygen link -- B01J 31/0211 covers catalysts that contain a metal-oxygen bond, including hydrides, coordination complexes and organic compounds in which the active site features a metal linked directly to oxygen. These catalysts are used in chemical and physical processes such as separation, catalysis and colloid chemistry, falling under the broader category of performing operations and separating.
[B01J 31/0212] Alkoxylates -- The CPC subclass B01J 31/0212 relates to alkoxylate compounds used as catalysts, typically organic molecules in which an alkoxy group is bound to a metal centre or other catalytic entity. These catalysts, which may be hydride-based, coordination complexes or purely organic, are employed in chemical or physical processes such as polymerisation, oxidation or other transforming reactions.
[B01J 31/0214] Aryloxylates, e.g. phenolates -- B01J 31/0214 covers catalyst materials that contain aryloxylate groups such as phenolates, used in chemical or physical separating processes including catalysis. These organic compounds function as hydride or coordination-complex components to facilitate reactions.
[B01J 31/0215] Sulfur-containing compounds -- B01J 31/0215 covers sulfur-containing compounds that function as catalysts - such as hydrides, coordination complexes or organic species - in separation operations involving chemical or physical processes like catalysis or colloid chemistry. These substances are used to facilitate the selective removal or isolation of target materials.
[B01J 31/0217] Mercaptans or thiols -- The CPC entry B01J 31/0217 covers the use of mercaptans or thiols as catalytic agents, including hydride-based, coordination-complex or organic catalyst formulations, in chemical or physical processes such as separation operations. It falls under the broader categories of performing operations, separating, and chemical or physical processes.
[B01J 31/0218] Sulfides -- B01J 31/0218 designates sulfide substances that function as catalysts, either as simple hydrides, coordination complexes or organic compounds, in chemical or physical processes such as catalysis, separation or colloid chemistry. The class falls under the CPC hierarchy of Performing Operations - Separating - Chemical or Physical Processes. It includes inorganic and organometallic sulfide catalysts employed to accelerate reactions or facilitate phase separations.
[B01J 31/022] Disulfides -- B01J 31/022 relates to disulfide substances employed as catalysts-hydrides, coordination complexes or organic-based compounds-in chemical or physical processes such as catalytic reactions and separation operations.
[B01J 31/0221] Polysulfides -- The CPC entry B01J 31/0221 covers polysulfide compounds used as catalysts in chemical or physical processes, such as separating operations that involve catalysis or colloid chemistry. It pertains specifically to catalysts formed from polysulfides, including those combined with hydrides, coordination complexes or organic molecules.
[B01J 31/0222] comprising sulfonyl groups -- B01J 31/0222 covers catalysts that contain sulfonyl groups and are used in separating operations such as catalysis or colloid-based processes. These catalysts may be hydrides, coordination complexes or organic compounds bearing sulfonyl functionalities.
[B01J 31/0224] being perfluorinated, i.e. comprising at least one perfluorinated moiety as substructure in case of polyfunctional compounds -- B01J 31/0224 falls under the CPC subclass for catalysts used in chemical or physical processes, specifically separating operations involving hydride, coordination-complex or organic catalysts. This sub-group covers catalysts that contain at least one perfluorinated moiety as a structural element, including polyfunctional compounds. The perfluorinated groups give the catalysts high chemical stability and distinct lipophobic characteristics.
[B01J 31/0225] comprising sulfonic acid groups or the corresponding salts -- B01J 31/0225 designates catalysts that contain sulfonic acid groups or their corresponding salts, typically employed in chemical or physical separation processes such as catalysis or colloid chemistry. These organic-based catalyst materials fall under the broader category of hydride, coordination-complex or organic-compound catalysts.
[B01J 31/0227] being perfluorinated, i.e. comprising at least one perfluorinated moiety as substructure in case of polyfunctional compounds -- The CPC entry B01J 31/0227 covers catalysts that contain one or more perfluorinated groups, meaning at least one perfluorinated moiety is present in polyfunctional compounds used in catalytic or separating processes. It falls under the hierarchy of Performing Operations → Separating → Chemical or Physical Processes such as catalysis, where the perfluorinated structure imparts specific chemical properties.
[B01J 31/0228] with a metal-sulfur link, e.g. mercaptides -- B01J 31/0228 covers catalysts that contain a metal-sulfur bond, such as mercaptide complexes, used in chemical or physical processes including catalysis and separation operations. These catalysts may be hydrides, coordination complexes, or organic compounds featuring the metal-sulfur linkage.
[B01J 31/0229] also containing elements or functional groups covered by  B01J31/0201  -  B01J31/0214 -- B01J 31/0229 covers catalysts that contain hydride ligands, coordination-complex structures or organic compounds and also include elements or functional groups defined in the earlier sub-groups B01J31/0201 through B01J31/0214. These materials are used in chemical or physical separation processes such as catalysis or colloid chemistry. The classification falls under the CPC hierarchy Performing operations - separating - chemical or physical processes.
[B01J 31/0231] Halogen-containing compounds -- B01J31/0231 designates halogen-containing compounds that function as catalysts, such as hydrides, coordination complexes or organic molecules, in chemical or physical processes including catalysis, colloid chemistry and separation operations. The class groups substances where the presence of fluorine, chlorine, bromine or iodine is essential to the catalytic action.
[B01J 31/0232] also containing elements or functional groups covered by  B01J31/0201  -  B01J31/0228   perfluorinated sulfonyl compounds or moieties  B01J31/0224 ; perfluorosulfonic acids  B01J31/0227 -- B01J 31/0232 classifies catalysts that contain hydrides, coordination complexes or organic compounds and additionally incorporate perfluorinated sulfonyl groups or perfluorosulfonic acid moieties, as defined in the related sub-codes. These materials are used in catalytic or separation processes within chemical or physical operations.
[B01J 31/0234] Nitrogen-, phosphorus-, arsenic- or antimony-containing compounds -- The CPC entry B01J 31/0234 covers catalysts that contain nitrogen, phosphorus, arsenic or antimony, including hydrides, coordination complexes and organic compounds, used in performing operations such as separating, or in chemical or physical processes like catalysis and colloid chemistry.
[B01J 31/0235] Nitrogen containing compounds -- The B01J 31/0235 subclass covers catalysts that contain nitrogen, including metal-hydride complexes, coordination compounds and organic nitrogen-based substances, used in chemical or physical separation operations such as catalytic or colloidal processes.
[B01J 31/0237] Amines -- The B01J 31/0237 subgroup covers catalytic systems in which amine compounds are employed as ligands or active components of catalysts based on hydrides, coordination complexes or organic molecules, primarily for separating chemical or physical mixtures such as extraction, adsorption or selective transport processes.
[B01J 31/0238] with a primary amino group -- B01J 31/0238 covers catalysts that contain a primary amino group, whether as part of hydride-bearing compounds, coordination complexes or purely organic molecules, and are employed in catalytic or separation operations such as reactions, hydride transfer or colloidal processes. It focuses on the presence of an -NH₂ substituent as a defining structural feature of the catalyst.
[B01J 31/0239] Quaternary ammonium compounds -- B01J 31/0239 classifies quaternary ammonium compounds employed as catalysts in separation operations, particularly in chemical or physical processes like ion-exchange or phase-transfer reactions. The subclass falls under the broader categories of performing operations, separating, and catalysts comprising hydrides, coordination complexes or organic compounds.
[B01J 31/0241] Imines or enamines -- B01J 31/0241 covers catalytic separation processes in which imine or enamine compounds, or their derivatives, are employed as the active catalyst, often in conjunction with hydride, coordination-complex or organic catalysts, to facilitate chemical or physical separation steps such as extraction, precipitation or membrane filtration.
[B01J 31/0242] Enamines -- Enamines are organic compounds featuring a carbon-carbon double bond adjacent to a nitrogen atom, which act as nucleophilic catalysts or intermediates in a variety of organic transformations such as Michael additions, aldol condensations and asymmetric syntheses. In the CPC they fall under catalysts comprising organic compounds within the chemical or physical processes category.
[B01J 31/0244] with nitrogen contained as ring member in aromatic compounds or moieties, e.g. pyridine -- This subclass covers catalysts that include a nitrogen atom as part of a heterocyclic aromatic ring, such as pyridine, incorporated into hydride, coordination complex, or organic catalyst formulations. These catalysts are employed in chemical or physical processes, including separation operations, within the broader category of catalytic processes.
[B01J 31/0245] being derivatives of carboxylic or carbonic acids -- The subgroup B01J 31/0245 covers catalysts-hydrides, coordination complexes or organic compounds-that are derivatives of carboxylic or carbonic acids and are used in separating operations within chemical or physical processes such as catalysis.
[B01J 31/0247] Imides, amides or imidates (R-C=NR(OR)) -- B01J 31/0247 covers catalysts that contain imides, amides or imidates (structures of the form R-C=NR(OR)), which are used in chemical or physical processes such as separation or other performing operations. These organic compounds may act as hydride donors, coordination complexes or other catalytic agents to facilitate reactions like catalysis, colloid chemistry or related transformations.
[B01J 31/0248] Nitriles -- The subclass B01J 31/0248 covers catalysts that contain nitrile groups, including metal-hydride, coordination-complex or organic nitrile compounds used in chemical or physical processes such as catalysis, separation or colloid chemistry. It is nested under Performing Operations → Separating → Chemical or Physical Processes.
[B01J 31/0249] Ureas (R 2 N-C(=O)-NR 2 ) -- B01J 31/0249 addresses catalytic agents that contain urea structures (R₂N-C(=O)-NR₂), used in chemical or physical processes such as catalysis or colloid chemistry. The entry falls under performing operations, separating, and chemical or physical processes, and includes hydride, coordination-complex or organic-compound catalysts derived from urea.
[B01J 31/0251] Guanidides (R 2 N-C(=NR)-NR 2 ) -- The CPC entry B01J 31/0251 falls under Performing operations → Separating → Chemical or physical processes and defines catalysts that are guanidides, i.e., compounds with the R₂N-C(=NR)-NR₂ structure. These guanidide-based catalysts may be employed as hydrides, coordination complexes or organic catalysts in various chemical reactions.
[B01J 31/0252] with a metal-nitrogen link, e.g. metal amides, metal guanidides -- This subclass covers catalysts that contain a direct metal-nitrogen bond, such as metal amides or metal guanidides, used in chemical or physical processes including catalysis and colloid chemistry. These materials may be hydrides, coordination complexes or organic compounds featuring the metal-nitrogen linkage.
[B01J 31/0254] on mineral substrates -- B01J 31/0254 relates to separation processes that employ catalysts such as hydrides, coordination complexes or organic compounds when the material being treated is a mineral substrate. It belongs to the CPC hierarchy under Performing Operations → Separating → Chemical or Physical Processes, e.g., catalysis or colloid chemistry.
[B01J 31/0255] Phosphorus containing compounds -- B01J 31/0255 designates catalysts that incorporate phosphorus, including phosphide hydrides, phosphorus-based coordination complexes, or organic phosphorus compounds, which are employed in chemical or physical operations such as catalysis, separation or colloidal processes. It resides in the hierarchy of performing operations - separating - chemical or physical processes.
[B01J 31/0257] Phosphorus acids or phosphorus acid esters -- The CPC subclass B01J 31/0257 addresses the use of phosphorus acids and phosphorus-acid esters as catalysts in chemical or physical processes, particularly those involved in separating operations. It falls within the broader categories of performing operations, separating, and catalytic or colloid chemistry, and includes hydride-containing, coordination-complex or organic-based catalyst systems.
[B01J 31/0258] Phosphoric acid mono-, di- or triesters ((RO)(R'O)2P=O), i.e. R= C, R'= C, H -- The CPC subclass B01J 31/0258 refers to catalysts that are phosphoric-acid mono-, di- or tri-esters, where the phosphorus atom bears a double-bonded oxygen and is bonded to organic substituents (R and R′ are carbon-based groups or hydrogen). These ester compounds are employed in chemical or physical processes, particularly as catalysts in separations and related operations.
[B01J 31/0259] comprising phosphorous acid (-ester) groups ((RO)P(OR')2) or the isomeric phosphonic acid (-ester) groups (R(R'O)2P=O), i.e. R= C, R'= C, H -- B01J 31/0259 covers catalysts that contain phosphorus-acid or phosphonic-acid ester groups, where the phosphorus is bound to carbon or hydrogen substituents such as (RO)P(OR′)₂ or R(R′O)₂P=O. These compounds are employed in catalytic processes for separating mixtures, including reactions that involve hydrides or coordination complexes. The entry falls under performing operations, separating, chemical or physical processes.
[B01J 31/0261] comprising phosphonous acid (-ester) groups (RP(OR')2) or the isomeric phosphinic acid (-ester) groups (R2(R'O)P=O), i.e. R= C, R'= C, H -- B01J 31/0261 classifies catalysts that contain phosphonous-acid ester groups RP(OR′)₂ or the isomeric phosphinic-acid ester groups R₂(R′O)P=O, where the substituents R and R′ are carbon-based or hydrogen. These substances are used in chemical or physical processes such as catalysis or separation operations.
[B01J 31/0262] comprising phosphinous acid (-ester) groups (R2P(OR')) or the isomeric phosphine oxide groups (R3P=O) , i.e. R= C, R'= C, H -- B01J 31/0262 defines catalysts that incorporate phosphinous acid ester groups (R2P(OR')) or the related phosphine oxide groups (R3P=O) where the substituents R and R' are carbon atoms or hydrogen. These catalysts fall under chemical or physical separation processes, including catalysis and colloid chemistry, and may be hydrides, coordination complexes, or organic compounds.
[B01J 31/0264] Phosphorus acid amides -- Phosphorus acid amides are nitrogen-substituted phosphorus compounds that function as catalysts or catalyst precursors in chemical and physical processes, particularly in reactions involving hydride transfer, hydrogenation, polymerisation or other transformations mediated by coordination complexes. They fall within the CPC subclass for catalysts comprising hydrides, coordination complexes or organic compounds.
[B01J 31/0265] Phosphazenes, oligomers thereof or the corresponding phosphazenium salts  polyphosphazenes  per se   C07F9/067 -- B01J 31/0265 in the Cooperative Patent Classification system covers phosphazenes, their oligomers, and corresponding phosphazenium salts, including polyphosphazenes, specifically for use as catalysts. This entry is part of a broader category dealing with chemical processes and catalysis.
[B01J 31/0267] Phosphines or phosphonium compounds, i.e. phosphorus bonded to at least one carbon atom, including e.g. sp2-hybridised phosphorus compounds such as phosphabenzene, the other atoms bonded to phosphorus being either carbon or hydrogen -- The CPC subclass B01J 31/0267 covers phosphines and phosphonium compounds, in which phosphorus is bonded to at least one carbon atom, including sp²-hybridised species such as phosphabenzene, with any remaining phosphorus bonds to carbon or hydrogen. These organophosphorus entities are listed as catalytic materials for chemical or physical processes, particularly in the context of separating operations.
[B01J 31/0268] Phosphonium compounds, i.e. phosphine with an additional hydrogen or carbon atom bonded to phosphorous so as to result in a formal positive charge on phosphorous -- The subclass B01J 31/0268 covers phosphonium compounds, in which phosphorus bears a formal positive charge because it is bonded to an extra hydrogen or carbon atom. These organophosphonium salts and related hydride complexes are used as catalysts in chemical or physical processes such as hydrogenation, polymerisation and other transformations involving separation or reaction steps.
[B01J 31/0269] on mineral substrates -- This subclass deals with catalytic separation methods that use hydride, coordination-complex or organic catalysts applied directly to mineral substrates such as ores, rocks or clays, typically for leaching, oxidation or reduction processes. It is nested under performing operations, separating, chemical or physical processes.
[B01J 31/0271] also containing elements or functional groups covered by  B01J31/0201  -  B01J31/0231 -- B01J 31/0271 classifies catalysts used in separation operations that consist of hydrides, coordination complexes or organic compounds and that also incorporate the elements or functional groups defined in sub-groups B01J31/0201 through B01J31/0231. The entry falls under the CPC hierarchy PERFORMING OPERATIONS - SEPARATING - CHEMICAL OR PHYSICAL PROCESSES, such as catalysis or colloid chemistry.
[B01J 31/0272] containing elements other than those covered by  B01J31/0201  -  B01J31/0255 -- B01J31/0272 covers catalysts that include elements not listed in the earlier sub-groups B01J31/0201 through B01J31/0255, such as hydride-based catalysts, coordination complexes or organic compounds containing other heteroatoms. These catalysts are used in separating chemical or physical processes, including catalysis and colloid chemistry. The classification falls within the Performing Operations → Separating → Chemical or Physical Processes hierarchy.
[B01J 31/0274] containing silicon  ligands in coordination complexes  B01J31/1608 -- B01J 31/0274 classifies catalysts that are coordination complexes bearing silicon-containing ligands, used in chemical or physical processes such as catalysis and separation. It is placed under performing operations, separating, chemical or physical processes and includes hydride, coordination-complex or organic-compound catalysts.
[B01J 31/0275] also containing elements or functional groups covered by  B01J31/0201  -  B01J31/0269 -- The sub-class B01J 31/0275 deals with catalytic substances that contain hydride ligands, coordination-complex structures or organic compounds and that also incorporate the particular elements or functional groups defined in the preceding range B01J31/0201-B01J31/0269. It is intended for catalysts employed in separating operations and other chemical or physical processes.
[B01J 31/0277] comprising ionic liquids, as components in catalyst systems or catalysts  per se , the ionic liquid compounds being used in the molten state at the respective reaction temperature -- The code B01J 31/0277 concerns catalysts that incorporate ionic liquids, either as components of a catalyst system or as the catalyst itself, with the ionic liquid employed in its molten state at the reaction temperature. It is situated in the hierarchy of chemical or physical processes, specifically separating operations involving hydride, coordination-complex or organic catalysts.
[B01J 31/0278] containing nitrogen as cationic centre -- B01J 31/0278 pertains to catalysts that contain nitrogen atoms acting as cationic centres, including hydride-bearing complexes, coordination compounds and organic catalysts where the nitrogen carries a positive charge. These materials are employed in chemical or physical processes such as catalysis or separation.
[B01J 31/0279] the cationic portion being acyclic or nitrogen being a substituent on a ring -- This CPC subclass B01J 31/0279 covers catalytic systems in which the cationic component is an acyclic species or where a nitrogen atom serves as a substituent on a ring. Such catalysts are used in separation operations involving chemical or physical processes such as catalysis.
[B01J 31/0281] the nitrogen being a ring member -- The code B01J 31/0281 designates catalytic substances in which a nitrogen atom forms part of a ring structure of the ligand coordinated to the metal centre. Such nitrogen-containing heterocyclic complexes are used in various chemical transformations, including hydrogenation and related processes.
[B01J 31/0282] of an aliphatic ring, e.g. morpholinium -- The B01J 31/0282 subclass pertains to catalytic compounds that incorporate an aliphatic ring, exemplified by morpholinium moieties. It covers catalysts-whether hydrides, coordination complexes or organic molecules-where the active site includes such saturated heterocyclic structures, used in chemical or physical catalytic processes.
[B01J 31/0284] of an aromatic ring, e.g. pyridinium -- B01J 31/0284 covers catalysts that incorporate an aromatic ring, for example pyridinium groups, within hydride, coordination-complex or organic-compound catalysts employed in chemical or physical processes such as catalysis and colloid chemistry. This subclass sits under Performing Operations → Separating → Chemical or Physical Processes in the CPC system.
[B01J 31/0285] also containing elements or functional groups covered by  B01J31/0201  -  B01J31/0274 -- The subclass B01J 31/0285 covers catalysts that contain hydride ligands, coordination-complex structures, or organic compounds, and may also include the specific elements or functional groups defined in the related sub-classes B01J31/0201 through B01J31/0274. It pertains to chemical or physical processes such as catalysis within the broader domain of separating operations.
[B01J 31/0287] containing atoms other than nitrogen as cationic centre -- This CPC subclass (B01J 31/0287) covers catalysts that have a cationic centre formed by atoms other than nitrogen, such as metals or other elements, within the broader category of chemical or physical processes involving separating operations. It pertains to hydride-based, coordination-complex or organic catalysts used in reactions like catalysis or colloid chemistry.
[B01J 31/0288] Phosphorus -- The subclass B01J 31/0288 covers catalysts that contain phosphorus, such as phosphorus hydrides, phosphorus-based coordination complexes and organic phosphorus compounds, which are employed in chemical or physical processes like catalysis or colloid chemistry.
[B01J 31/0289] Sulfur -- The B01J 31/0289 entry covers catalysts that contain sulfur, including hydrides, coordination complexes or organic sulfur compounds, used in chemical or physical processes such as catalysis or colloid chemistry. It falls under the broader categories of performing operations, separating, and chemical or physical processes, and pertains to the role of sulfur-based catalysts in facilitating reactions and separations.
[B01J 31/0291] also containing elements or functional groups covered by  B01J31/0201  -  B01J31/0274 -- The CPC class B01J 31/0291 covers catalysts that are hydrides, coordination complexes or organic compounds used in separating operations that fall under chemical or physical processes such as catalysis or colloid chemistry. The entry specifies that the catalysts also include elements or functional groups defined in subclasses B01J31/0201 through B01J31/0274.
[B01J 31/0292] immobilised on a substrate -- This subclass (B01J 31/0292) concerns catalytic agents-hydrides, coordination complexes or organic compounds-that are attached to a solid support or other substrate for use in chemical or physical separation processes. Immobilising the catalyst facilitates its recovery, reuse and stability during performing-operation steps such as catalysis or colloid chemistry.
[B01J 31/0294] by polar or ionic interaction with the substrate, e.g. glass -- B01J 31/0294 covers catalysts-such as hydrides, coordination complexes or organic compounds-that act on a substrate through polar or ionic interactions, for example when the substrate is glass. It falls under the hierarchy of performing operations, separating, and chemical or physical processes.
[B01J 31/0295] by covalent attachment to the substrate, e.g. silica -- The CPC entry B01J 31/0295 covers catalysts made from hydrides, coordination complexes or organic compounds that are covalently bonded to a solid substrate such as silica. These anchored catalysts are employed in separation or other chemical-physical processes, for example in catalytic or colloid-chemistry applications, under the broader category of separating operations.
[B01J 31/0297] the substrate being a soluble polymer, dendrimer or oligomer of characteristic microstructure of groups  B01J31/061  -  B01J31/068 -- B01J 31/0297 covers catalytic processes in which the substrate is a soluble polymer, dendrimer or oligomer possessing a defined microstructure of functional groups, and the catalyst is a hydride, coordination complex or organic compound. It falls under the hierarchy of Performing Operations - Separating - Chemical or Physical Processes such as catalysis, and narrows further to the class for catalysts involving polymeric or dendritic substrates.
[B01J 31/0298] the ionic liquids being characterised by the counter-anions -- B01J 31/0298 classifies ionic liquids whose catalytic function is defined by the nature of their counter-anions, covering hydride, coordination-complex or organic-compound based systems used in chemical or physical separation processes such as catalysis or colloid chemistry. It focuses on how the anionic component influences performance, stability and selectivity in these operations.
[B01J 31/04] containing carboxylic acids or their salts -- B01J 31/04 covers catalysts-hydrides, coordination complexes or organic compounds-that incorporate carboxylic acids or their salts. These catalytic materials are employed in chemical or physical processes such as separation, catalysis or colloid chemistry, where the acidic functional group contributes to the catalyst's activity or stability.
[B01J 31/06] containing polymers -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Catalysts containing polymers and/or oligomers without metal-carbon bonds, including (sulfonated) ion-exchange resins.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - (Sulfonated) Ion-exchange resins -> B01J31/08 ( B01J31/10 ) - Organometallic polymers -> B01J31/123 - Polymer-bound organometallic complexes -> B01J31/165 - Coordination polymers -> B01J31/1691  **Glossary:** - Polymer: a macromolecular substance (typically M>10000 g/mol) comprising repeating units made up of one or several kinds of atoms or groups of atoms, which are identically connected to one another. - Oligomer: compound with more than two identical repeating units connected to one another and typically 500 less than M less than 10000 g/mol; grouped with the respective polymers
[B01J 31/061] Chiral polymers -- Chiral polymers are polymeric substances that possess a defined handedness and are employed as catalysts or support materials in asymmetric synthesis, separations and related chemical processes. This CPC entry (B01J 31/061) covers such polymeric catalysts, including those derived from hydrides, coordination complexes or organic compounds, within the broader category of chemical or physical processes for separating substances.
[B01J 31/062] Polymeric amino acids -- B01J 31/062 designates polymeric amino acids employed as catalytic agents, typically in the form of hydride-containing, coordination-complex or organic-based polymers, for use in chemical or physical separation processes such as catalysis or colloid chemistry. The class falls under the hierarchy Performing operations, Separating, Chemical or physical processes.
[B01J 31/063] Polymers comprising a characteristic microstructure -- B01J 31/063 in the Cooperative Patent Classification system refers to catalysts that include polymers with a specific microstructure, focusing on those containing hydrides, coordination complexes, or organic compounds. This classification is part of a broader category dealing with chemical and physical processes, particularly catalysis.
[B01J 31/064] Dendrimers -- B01J 31/064 covers dendrimers, which are highly branched, monodisperse macromolecules built from a central core and repeating layered units (generations). The classification includes their use as catalysts-often hydride-containing, coordinated, or organic-based-within chemical or physical processes such as catalysis, colloid chemistry, or other separations.
[B01J 31/065] Cyclodextrins -- Cyclodextrins are cyclic oligosaccharides made from glucose units that form a toroidal cavity able to host guest molecules, enabling them to act as supramolecular catalysts and solubilising agents in chemical and physical processes such as catalysis, separation and colloid chemistry.
[B01J 31/066] Calixarenes and hetero-analogues, e.g. thiacalixarenes -- The entry B01J 31/066 covers calixarenes and hetero-analogues such as thiacalixarenes when employed as catalysts or functional agents in separating operations that involve chemical or physical processes, including catalysis with hydrides, coordination complexes or organic compounds. It addresses their role in facilitating or enhancing reactions and separations through host-guest interactions or surface binding.
[B01J 31/067] Molecularly imprinted polymers  catalytic antibodies  C12N9/0002 -- B01J31/067 covers catalysts that are molecularly imprinted polymers or catalytic antibodies-polymeric materials engineered with specific binding sites that mimic enzyme activity to speed up chemical reactions. These catalysts are employed in processes such as catalysis, separation or synthesis and fall under chemical or physical processes.
[B01J 31/068] Polyalkylene glycols -- B01J 31/068 covers polyalkylene glycols used as catalytic agents or media in chemical and physical separation processes. These water-soluble polymers serve as ligands, stabilizers or solvent-like components for hydride or coordination-complex catalysts, facilitating reactions such as hydrogenation, polymerisation or colloid formation.
[B01J 31/069] Hybrid organic-inorganic polymers, e.g. silica derivatized with organic groups  nitrogen containing groups on mineral substrates  B01J31/0254 ; organometallic polymers  B01J31/123 ; coordination complexes immobilised on an inorganic support  B01J31/1616 ; coordination polymers, e.g. metal-organic frameworks  B01J31/1691 -- B01J 31/069 covers hybrid organic-inorganic polymers where inorganic supports such as silica or mineral substrates are functionalised with organic or nitrogen-containing groups, including organometallic polymers, immobilised coordination complexes and coordination polymers such as metal-organic frameworks.
[B01J 31/08] Ion-exchange resins -- The CPC subclass B01J 31/08 covers ion-exchange resins used as catalysts, including those based on hydrides, coordination complexes or organic compounds. It falls under performing operations, separating, and chemical or physical processes such as catalysis and colloid chemistry, describing resin materials that facilitate ion exchange in chemical reactions.
[B01J 31/10] sulfonated -- B01J 31/10 refers to sulfonated catalysts, meaning hydrides, coordination complexes or organic compounds that have sulfonic-acid groups attached, and are employed in chemical or physical processes such as catalysis and separation operations.
[B01J 31/12] containing organo-metallic compounds or metal hydrides -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Monomeric and polymeric organometallic compounds, the metals, with the exception of arsenic and antimony, including those defined in the notes under the title of section C , and furthermore include the metalloid element boron as "metal".  In the case of organometallic polymers the metalloid elements silicon, arsenic, antimony, selenium and tellurium are furthermore covered under the term "metal".  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Organometallic monomeric compounds of antimony, arsenic -> B01J31/0234 - Organic monomeric compounds of silicon, selenium, tellurium -> B01J31/0272 - Dendrimers -> B01J31/064 - Calixarenes and hetero-analogues, e.g. thiacalixarenes -> B01J31/066 - Molecularly imprinted polymers -> B01J31/067 - (Sulfonated) Ion-exchange resins -> B01J31/08 , B01J31/10 - Polymer-bound organometallic complexes -> B01J31/165 - Coordination polymers -> B01J31/1691 - Catalysts for the preparation of polysiloxanes, e.g. Karstedt catalysts -> C08G77/08  **Glossary:** - Organometallic compounds: all organic compounds wherein a metal or metalloid atom is bonded directly to a carbon fragment, the latter being formally anionic, no further neutral ligands being coordinated to the metal and the compound requiring no further cations for charge balance; e.g. M(CR 3 ) n with M= main group metal, n= valency of metal and R= H or hydrocarbyl.
[B01J 31/121] Metal hydrides -- B01J 31/121 covers metal-hydride catalysts, i.e., compounds where a metal is combined with hydrogen in a hydride form, used to promote reactions such as hydrogenation, reduction or hydrogen-storage processes. The subgroup falls within CPC B01J for catalytic operations involving hydrides, coordination complexes or organic compounds applied in separating and transformation steps.
[B01J 31/122] Metal aryl or alkyl compounds -- B01J 31/122 concerns metal compounds in which the metal is bound to aryl or alkyl groups, typically used as catalysts or catalyst precursors in chemical reactions such as hydrogenation, polymerisation or coupling. These substances include hydride-containing complexes, coordination compounds and organometallic reagents employed to promote or accelerate processes within the separating and catalytic sections of the CPC.
[B01J 31/123] Organometallic polymers, e.g. comprising C-Si bonds in the main chain or in subunits grafted to the main chain  B01J31/064 ,  B01J31/066 ,  B01J31/067 ,  B01J31/08  and  B01J31/10  take precedence; polymer-bound organometallic complexes  B01J31/165 ; coordination polymers  B01J31/1691 ; catalysts for the preparation of polysiloxanes, e.g. Karstedt catalysts  C08G77/08 -- B01J 31/123 in the Cooperative Patent Classification covers organometallic polymers with specific chemical bonds, such as C-Si bonds in their main chain or grafted subunits, while noting precedence for related entries that detail more specific aspects of these materials.
[B01J 31/124] Silicones or siloxanes or comprising such units -- This CPC subgroup covers catalysts that contain silicone or siloxane structures, either as the main material or as functional units attached to other compounds. Such catalysts are employed in chemical or physical processes, especially separation operations, where the silicon-oxygen backbone provides thermal stability and unique surface properties. They may be hydride-based, coordination complexes or organic-modified silicone materials.
[B01J 31/125] Cyclic siloxanes -- B01J 31/125 relates to cyclic siloxane compounds employed as catalysts in chemical or physical processes, including separations and reactions such as catalysis. The subclass groups siloxane-based catalyst systems that may contain hydride groups, coordination complexes or organic ligands, and is placed under the broader categories of performing operations, separating and chemical or physical processes.
[B01J 31/126] the siloxanes or siloxane units, cyclic or not, comprising an additional Si-H bond, e.g. polyhydromethylsiloxane [PHMS] -- B01J 31/126 covers catalysts that include siloxane or siloxane units-cyclic or linear-bearing an additional Si-H bond, exemplified by polyhydromethylsiloxane (PHMS). These materials are used in chemical or physical processes such as catalysis, falling under the CPC hierarchy of Performing Operations → Separating → Chemical or Physical Processes, e.g., catalysis or colloid chemistry.
[B01J 31/127] the siloxane units, e.g. silsesquioxane units, being grafted onto other polymers or inorganic supports, e.g. via an organic linker -- The subclass covers processes in which siloxane units, for example silsesquioxane fragments, are chemically grafted onto polymers or inorganic supports via an organic linker, producing hybrid materials that function as catalysts or functional supports. It belongs to the B01J group dealing with catalysts based on hydrides, coordination complexes or organic compounds within separating chemical or physical operations.
[B01J 31/128] Mixtures of organometallic compounds -- B01J 31/128 relates to mixtures of organometallic compounds that serve as catalysts, including hydrides, coordination complexes or organic-based catalysts, employed in chemical or physical processes such as catalysis or colloid chemistry. It is placed under the separating operations of the performing operations hierarchy.
[B01J 31/14] of aluminium or boron -- B01J 31/14 covers catalysts that contain aluminium or boron, including hydrides, coordination complexes or organic compounds of these elements. These catalysts are used in chemical or physical processes such as catalytic reactions, colloid chemistry or separations within the broader performing-operations and separating categories.
[B01J 31/143] of aluminium -- This CPC subgroup concerns catalysts that contain aluminium, such as aluminium hydrides, aluminium-based coordination complexes or organo-aluminium compounds, employed in chemical or physical processes like catalysis, polymerisation or colloid chemistry. It falls under performing operations, separating, chemical or physical processes, focusing on aluminium-based catalytic materials.
[B01J 31/146] of boron -- B01J 31/146 pertains to catalysts that include boron-based compounds, specifically within the context of hydrides, coordination complexes, or organic substances used in chemical processes. This classification is part of a detailed system for organizing patent information by the European Patent Office and United States Patent and Trademark Office.
[B01J 31/16] containing coordination complexes -- **Definition:** This place covers:  B01J31/16 - B01J31/2495 : Catalysts containing coordination complexes with inorganic or organic ligands, including organometallic complexes on an inorganic or polymer support, coordination polymers and metal-organic frameworks, as well as phosphines.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - (Poly)Oxoacid (poly)salts -> B01J31/02 , B01J31/10 - Other compounds comprising only anionic organo-nitrogen, organo-oxygen or organo-sulfur fragments with a metal bonded to these heteroatoms, e.g. Ti(OR)4 -> B01J31/02 - B01J31/0254 - Compounds comprising only alkyl groups bonded to a metal -> B01J31/12  **Glossary:** - Coordination complexes: all donor-acceptor compounds or complex ions comprising organic or inorganic, anionic or neutral Lewis basic ligands, attached to a Lewis acid central metal or metal ion through one or several complexing donor atoms with at least one lone-pair of electrons, e.g. N, O, S, P, to provide at least a sigma-bond; typically the maximum number of same or different ligands according to the coordination number, spatial requirements of the ligand and electronic configuration of the metal is bound in a predictable geometry;complexes of neutral, cationic or anionic hydrocarbon ligands with delocalised charge and/or bonding site, e.g. Pd-olefin complexes or metallocenes, are also included. - Organometallic complexes: all coordination complexes comprising a M-C bond, e.g. metal carbonyls; included are furthermore complexes which are not strictly organometallic per se, e.g. comprising only N, O, S and/or P coordinated ligands, but are described as involving, or known to involve, organometallic intermediates and/or transition states during use, e.g. Group 8-10 metal complexes for a variety of catalytic reactions or steps thereof, such as oxidative addition, e.g. of ArX, hydrogenation, carbonylation, epoxidation. - Metal-organic framework: crystalline compounds consisting of metal ions or clusters coordinated to often rigid organic molecules as linkers to form one-, two-, or three-dimensional structures; see e.g. reviews: - O. Yaghi et al., Nature 423 (2003) 705-714 (XP2392829),- M.J. Rosseinsky, Micropor. Mesopor. Mat. 73 (2004) 15-30 (XP4522127); the linkers are often dicarboxylates, the metals often Zn, Cu, Fe or Al. - Chelating or multidentate ligands: a ligand having more than one donor (Lewis basic) centre, the centres not being on contiguous atoms and being lone electron pairs, with each of these centres connecting to a common metal centre; the prefix κ denotes these ligands, the superscript index quantifies the number of bonding atoms of the ligand involved (κ 2 : bidentate ligand, usually omitted if no other coordinating entities are present)
[B01J 31/1608] the ligands containing silicon -- B01J 31/1608 covers catalysts that incorporate ligands containing silicon atoms, used in chemical or physical separation processes such as catalysis, hydride transfer or colloid chemistry. The subclass falls under the hierarchy performing operations, separating, chemical or physical processes, focusing on silicon-based ligands in coordination complexes or organometallic catalysts.

This classification is part of the broader category dealing with chemical or physical processes, specifically focusing on catalysis.

These immobilised catalytic species are used in separation operations and chemical or physical processes such as catalysis and colloid chemistry.

It falls under performing operations, separating, chemical or physical processes, specifically catalysts comprising hydrides, coordination complexes or organic compounds.

It classifies the synthesis and use of such silicon-linked catalytic systems within chemical or physical process operations.

[B01J 31/1658] immobilised by covalent linkages, i.e. pendant complexes with optional linking groups, e.g. on Wang or Merrifield resins -- B01J 31/1658 relates to catalysts that are immobilised through covalent linkages to solid supports, typically as pendant complexes with optional linking groups attached to polymer resins such as Wang or Merrifield beads. The entry covers hydride, coordination-complex or organic catalysts fixed in this way for use in chemical or physical processes.

[B01J 31/1675] the linkage being to an organometallic polymer covered by groups  B01J31/123  -  B01J31/127 , e.g. polyhydrosiloxanes -- B01J31/1675 concerns catalysts that are hydrides, coordination complexes or organic compounds whose active sites are covalently linked to an organometallic polymer, such as polyhydrosiloxanes, falling under separating chemical or physical processes within the performing operations hierarchy.
[B01J 31/1683] the linkage being to a soluble polymer, e.g. PEG or dendrimer, i.e. molecular weight enlarged complexes -- This code B01J31/1683 covers catalysts that are linked to soluble polymers such as polyethylene glycol or dendrimers, creating larger molecular-weight complexes. [B01J 31/22] Organic complexes -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Catalysts comprising coordination complexes comprising organic ligands, including polynuclear carboxylate complexes such as "Pd(II)acetate", i.e. Pd 3 (OAc) 6 , "Pt(II)acetate", i.e. Pt 4 (OAc) 8 or "Cr(II)acetate", i.e. Cr 2 (OAc) 4 .  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Complexes of organic silicon ligands -> B01J31/1608 - Organic coordination complexes immobilised on an inorganic support -> B01J31/1616 - Organic coordination complexes immobilised on a polymer support -> B01J31/165 - Coordination polymers, e.g. metal-organic frameworks (MOF) -> B01J31/1691 - Complexes containing nitrogen, phosphorus, arsenic or antimony as complexing atoms in organic ligands, if no oxygen as additional complexing atom is present, as in the case of e.g. the phosphite / phosphonate tautomer pair -> B01J31/18 - Complexes of organic phosphines -> B01J31/24  **Glossary:** - Organic ligand: a carbon-containing ligand bonded to a central metal in which said carbon is bonded to - a second carbon;- at least one atom of hydrogen or halogen; or - nitrogen by a single or double bond;except cyanic acid (HO-C≡N), cyanogen (N≡C-C≡N), cyanamide (H 2 N-C≡N), cyanogen halide (Hal-C≡N), hydrocyanic acid (HC≡N), isocyanic acid (HN=C=O), fulminic acid (HC≡N-O), carbides (C≡C) and the respective anionic ligands derived by deprotonation (O-C≡N, N=C=N, C≡N, N=C=O, C≡N-O). - Bridging ligand: a ligand connecting two or more metal centres in an at least binuclear complex, the metal atoms being directly bonded to another or not; the prefix μ denotes these ligands, the superscript index quantifies the number of metal centres connected (the simplest case, 2, may be omitted) - Chelating or multidentate ligands: a ligand having more than one donor (Lewis basic) centre, the centres not being on contiguous atoms and being lone electron pairs, with each of these centres connecting to a common metal centre; the prefix κ denotes these ligands, the superscript index quantifies the number of bonding atoms of the ligand involved (κ 2 : bidentate ligand, usually omitted if no other coordinating entities are present) - Polyhapto ligands: a ligand having a group of contiguous atoms, typ. a π-electron system, which are coordinated to a common central atom; the prefix η denotes these ligands, the superscript index quantifies the number of contiguous bonding atoms of the ligand involved (η 5 : pentahapto ligand, e.g. Cp) - Thiocarbamates: ligands of either one of the following isomeric structures: - N-Heterocyclic carbene: a saturated or unsaturated heterocyclic compound comprising a neutral carbon atom with a lone electron pair (in the typical case of singlet or "nucleophilic" carbenes) adjacent to at least one nitrogen atom.
