It is nested under crushing, pulverising or disintegrating in general.
[B02C 13/08] and acting as a fan -- B02C 13/08 pertains to crushing or pulverising machinery that employs rotary beater elements and also operates as a fan, using the airflow to move, dry or transport the disintegrated material.
[B02C 13/09] and throwing the material against an anvil or impact plate -- The B02C 13/09 subclass refers to processes that disintegrate material by using a mill with rotary beater elements and then propelling the material against an anvil or impact plate to achieve crushing or pulverising. It is a subcategory of general crushing, pulverising, or disintegrating operations.
[B02C 13/095] with an adjustable anvil or impact plate -- B02C 13/095 covers crushing, pulverising or disintegrating processes that employ mills with rotary beater elements and feature an adjustable anvil or impact plate, allowing the operator to vary the gap or impact force and thus control particle size. This subclass falls under the general category of disintegrating by mills having rotary beater elements within the broader crushing and pulverising hierarchy.
[B02C 13/10] with horizontal rotor shaft and axial flow -- The subclass B02C 13/10 covers crushing and pulverising mills that employ a horizontal rotor shaft with material flowing axially through the machine. It specifies disintegrating devices in which the rotor is oriented horizontally and the feed is conveyed along the axis of rotation, typical of certain hammer or impact mills used for general size reduction.
[B02C 13/12] with vortex chamber -- This subclass covers disintegration processes performed in mills that employ rotary beater elements combined with a vortex chamber. The vortex chamber creates a swirling flow that enhances the grinding or pulverising action, improving particle size reduction and homogenisation. It is used for general crushing, pulverising, or disintegrating operations in which the chamber assists the rotary beater's action.
[B02C 13/13] with horizontal rotor shaft and combined with sifting devices, e.g. for making powdered fuel -- The code B02C 13/13 covers rotary mills that have a horizontal rotor shaft and incorporate built-in sifting or screening devices, enabling simultaneous grinding and classification of material. These machines are used for producing fine powders such as powdered fuel by crushing, pulverising, or disintegrating the feed while separating the desired particle size.
[B02C 13/14] with vertical rotor shaft, e.g. combined with sifting devices -- This subgroup concerns machines that use a vertical rotor shaft equipped with rotary beater elements to crush, pulverise or disintegrate material, often together with a sifting or screening device. It falls under the general class for crushing, pulverising and disintegrating with rotary-beater mills.
[B02C 13/16] with beaters hinged to the rotor -- The CPC subclass B02C 13/16 covers crushing, pulverising or disintegrating processes performed in mills equipped with rotary beater elements, where the beaters are hinged to the rotor, allowing flexing or retraction during operation to enhance grinding efficiency and reduce wear.
[B02C 13/18] with beaters rigidly connected to the rotor -- The CPC subclass B02C 13/18 covers crushing, pulverising or disintegrating operations performed in rotary mills where the beaters are permanently fixed to the rotor rather than being removable or loosely attached. It applies to general mill designs that utilise rigidly connected beaters to break down bulk material.
[B02C 13/1807] the material to be crushed being thrown against an anvil or impact plate  with horizontal axis  B02C13/09 ; centrifugal acceleration of material through radially extending channels  B02C19/0025 ; centrifugal acceleration of material by means of an open top rotor  B02C19/0031 -- B02C 13/1807 pertains to disintegrating materials using mills with rotary beater elements, specifically where the material is thrown against an anvil or impact plate. This process often involves horizontal axis machinery and utilizes centrifugal force for acceleration through radially extending channels or via an open-top rotor.
[B02C 13/1814] by means of beater or impeller elements fixed on top of a disc type rotor -- The B02C 13/1814 subclass covers disintegration processes that employ mills with rotary beater or impeller units mounted on the upper side of a disc-type rotor. It specifies that the beater elements are fixed to the disc and used to crush or pulverise material during milling.
[B02C 13/1821] the beater or impeller elements being rotatably fixed around their own axis -- B02C 13/1821 relates to crushing or pulverising equipment in which the beater or impeller elements are mounted to rotate about their own axis while the mill itself may also rotate, a configuration used to break down solid particles in rotary mills.
[B02C 13/1828] with dead bed protected beater or impeller elements -- This subclass covers machines that pulverise or disintegrate materials with rotary beater or impeller elements that run over a dead (inactive) bed, the beater surfaces being shielded from direct contact with the work material to reduce wear while still delivering impact forces.
[B02C 13/1835] by means of beater or impeller elements fixed in between an upper and lower rotor disc -- This subclass of the CPC covers crushing, pulverising, or disintegrating operations that use a beater or impeller element fixed between an upper and lower rotor disc, the discs rotating to shear and break material. It applies to mills employing rotary beater elements for disintegration.
[B02C 13/1842] with dead bed protected beater or impeller elements -- This CPC subgroup covers crushing and pulverising processes where rotary beater or impeller elements operate over a dead bed that shields them from direct contact with the material. The protection reduces wear and contamination, making the mill suitable for fine grinding of powders, slurries, or other feedstocks.
[B02C 13/185] Construction or shape of anvil or impact plate -- The subclass B02C 13/185 concerns the construction and shape of anvils or impact plates used in mills that employ rotary-beater elements for crushing, pulverising, or otherwise disintegrating material. It addresses design features such as geometry, size, material and mounting that influence impact energy transfer.
[B02C 13/20] with two or more co-operating rotors -- This subclass covers mills that use two or more rotating beater elements that work together to crush, pulverise or otherwise disintegrate material. The cooperating rotors create intersecting shear and impact zones, improving size-reduction efficiency for bulk solids.
[B02C 13/205] arranged concentrically -- The CPC entry B02C 13/205 covers mills that use rotary beater elements positioned concentrically around a central axis to achieve disintegration of material. In this arrangement the beater elements rotate in a circular pattern, providing uniform impact and shear forces for crushing or pulverising the feedstock.
[B02C 13/22] with intermeshing pins -- The B02C 13/22 subclass covers crushing or pulverising mills that use intermeshing pins as the rotary beater elements. In these devices the pins on a rotating component engage with matching pins on a stationary part to disintegrate material through repeated impact and shear. This arrangement is applied to mills designed for general disintegration of bulk solids.
[B02C 13/24] arranged around a vertical axis -- This subclass covers machines that employ rotary beater elements arranged around a vertical axis to crush, pulverise or otherwise disintegrate bulk material. The vertical-axis configuration is typical of vertical roller or similar mills used for grinding ores, minerals, grains or other solids.
[B02C 13/26] Details -- B02C 13/26 pertains to the detailed aspects of disintegrating materials using mills equipped with rotating beater elements, focusing on specific components and operational features that enhance efficiency and functionality in crushing or pulverizing processes.
[B02C 13/28] Shape or construction of beater elements -- B02C 13/28 addresses the design and geometry of rotary beater components employed in mills for disintegrating material. It focuses on the shape, arrangement and construction of beater elements that affect grinding efficiency and the size of the final product.
[B02C 13/2804] the beater elements being rigidly connected to the rotor -- The subclass B02C 13/2804 concerns disintegrating mills in which the rotary beater elements are rigidly attached to the rotor, so the beaters cannot pivot or be driven separately. It covers crushing or pulverising processes using such fixed beaters that rotate together with the shaft.
[B02C 13/282] Shape or inner surface of mill-housings -- This subclass covers technical features of the shape or interior surface of housings for rotary-beater mills used in crushing or pulverising materials. It includes design aspects that affect flow, wear and the overall efficiency of the mill.
[B02C 13/284] Built-in screens -- The entry concerns crushing or pulverizing apparatus that use rotary-beater mills incorporating built-in screens within the mill chamber to separate material or control product size. The screens are integral to the mill, positioned inside the crushing zone, enabling on-line classification, preventing oversize particles, and improving overall efficiency.
[B02C 13/286] Feeding or discharge -- The subclass B02C 13/286 covers the feeding and discharge arrangements for mills that use rotary beater elements to crush, pulverise or otherwise disintegrate material. It includes devices such as hoppers, conveyors, feed chutes and discharge mechanisms that introduce raw material into, or remove product from, these rotary-beater mills.
[B02C 13/288] Ventilating, or influencing air circulation -- The B02C 13/288 subclass covers features that ventilate or otherwise control airflow within mills that use rotary beater elements for crushing, pulverising, or disintegrating material. It includes designs that enhance air movement to aid cooling, dust removal, or material transport during the grinding process.
[B02C 13/30] Driving mechanisms -- This subclass covers the means of powering mills that use rotary beater elements for crushing or pulverising materials. It includes gear trains, belt drives, hydraulic or pneumatic drives, and other mechanical arrangements that transmit rotational motion to the beater, focusing on the drive system rather than the mill structure itself.
[B02C 13/31] Safety devices or measures -- The B02C 13/31 subclass covers safety devices or measures applied to mills that use rotary beater elements for crushing, pulverising, or disintegrating materials. It addresses protective mechanisms such as emergency stops, interlocks, shielding, and overload preventers designed to reduce hazards and ensure safe operation of these specific disintegration machines.
[B02C 15/00] Disintegrating by milling members in the form of rollers or balls co-operating with rings or discs -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Mills with rollers or balls co-operating with stationary or rotating rings arranged according to the titles of the sub-groups; many for coal or ore.
[B02C 15/001] Air flow directing means positioned on the periphery of the horizontally rotating milling surface -- B02C 15/001 covers arrangements that direct a stream of air along the edge of a horizontally rotating milling surface, such as a roller or ball mill, to improve material transport, cooling or separation during crushing, pulverising or disintegrating. The air-flow means are positioned on the periphery to enhance efficiency and control of the particle size reduction operation.
[B02C 15/003] Shape or construction of discs or rings -- The subclass B02C 15/003 covers the shape and construction of discs or rings that cooperate with rollers or balls in milling processes to crush, pulverise, or otherwise disintegrate material. It is part of the broader group on crushing, pulverising, or disintegrating in general.
[B02C 15/004] Shape or construction of rollers or balls -- This subclass defines the shape and construction features of rollers or balls that work together with rings or discs in milling devices for crushing, pulverising or disintegrating materials. It falls under the broader category of crushing, pulverising and disintegrating operations.
[B02C 15/005] Rollers or balls of composite construction -- The subclass B02C 15/005 defines milling media-rollers or balls-manufactured from composite materials, meaning they consist of two or more constituent substances engineered for enhanced durability, wear resistance or other functional attributes. These composite rollers or balls are used in crushing, pulverising or disintegrating processes where they cooperate with rings or discs to mill the material.
[B02C 15/006] Ring or disc drive gear arrangement -- B02C 15/006 covers milling or disintegrating apparatus in which rollers or balls are powered by a gear arrangement built into a rotating ring or disc. The ring-or-disc drive transmits motion to the milling members, coordinating their movement for efficient crushing or pulverising.
[B02C 15/007] Mills with rollers pressed against a rotary horizontal disc  with pendularly mounted rollers  B02C15/04 -- This subclass covers milling machines where rollers are pressed against a rotating horizontal disc, the rollers being mounted on pendular supports that allow them to swing while grinding or pulverising material.
[B02C 15/02] Centrifugal pendulum-type mills -- B02C 15/02 covers centrifugal pendulum-type mills, where grinding media such as rollers or balls swing on pendulum arms and are forced against rings or discs to achieve crushing and pulverizing of materials. The design uses centrifugal forces to enhance impact and shear, suitable for fine grinding in mineral processing or material recycling.
[B02C 15/04] Mills with pressed pendularly-mounted rollers, e.g. spring pressed -- B02C 15/04 covers milling devices that employ rollers mounted on a pendular, spring-pressed arrangement to crush or pulverise material. The rollers swing like a pendulum and are forced against the workpiece by a spring or similar bias, providing intermittent rolling contact for disintegration.
[B02C 15/045] pressed against the interior of a ring rotating in a vertical plane -- This subclass covers milling or grinding apparatus where the material is forced against the inner surface of a ring that rotates in a vertical plane, with rollers or balls acting in cooperation with the ring or an associated disc to achieve crushing, pulverising, or disintegration.
[B02C 15/06] Mills with rollers forced against the interior of a rotary ring, e.g. under spring action -- This subclass covers mills where rollers are pressed against the inner surface of a rotating ring, typically by springs, to grind or pulverize material. The configuration forces the material between the rollers and the ring, providing efficient disintegration.
[B02C 15/08] Mills with balls or rollers centrifugally forced against the inner surface of a ring, the balls or rollers of which are driven by a centrally arranged member -- Mills where balls or rollers are forced outward by centrifugal action against the inner surface of a ring, the ring being driven by a centrally arranged member, are used for crushing, pulverising or disintegrating bulk materials. The configuration enables continuous grinding as the rotating members repeatedly impact the workpiece.
[B02C 15/10] Mills with balls or rollers centrifugally forced against the inner surface of a ring, the balls or rollers of which are driven by other means than a centrally-arranged member -- The code covers mills that use balls or rollers pressed centrifugally against the inner surface of a ring to crush or pulverise material, where the rotating motion of the balls or rollers is produced by a drive other than a centrally-located shaft. It falls under the broader class for crushing, pulverising and disintegrating operations.
[B02C 15/12] Mills with at least two discs and interposed balls or rollers mounted like ball or roller bearings -- This subclass covers mills that contain two or more rotating discs separated by a series of balls or rollers that act as bearing elements, allowing the material to be milled while the interposed elements roll or crush between the discs. The configuration is used for disintegrating solids by combined disc and bearing action.
[B02C 15/123] with rings and interposed rollers -- This subclass covers crushing or pulverising processes in which the milling members are rollers or balls that work together with rings or discs to achieve disintegration. The configuration involves rings surrounding or interspersed between the rollers, enabling continuous grinding of the material as it passes through the milling zone. It is a specific arrangement within the general category of crushing, pulverising, or disintegrating operations.
[B02C 15/14] Edge runners, e.g. Chile mills -- Edge runners such as Chile mills are milling devices in which rollers or balls rotate close to a surrounding ring or disc, the material being disintegrated by the shearing action between the moving members and the stationary surface.
[B02C 15/16] with milling members essentially having different peripheral speeds and in the form of a hollow cylinder or cone and an internal roller or cone -- This subclass (B02C 15/16) relates to crushing, pulverising or disintegrating apparatus in which milling members-such as rollers or balls-operate at different peripheral speeds and are configured as a hollow cylinder or cone together with an internal roller or cone to achieve the milling action.
[B02C 17/00] Disintegrating by tumbling mills, i.e. mills having a container charged with the material to be disintegrated with or without special disintegrating members such as pebbles or balls -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Mills having a rotating, perforated or un-perforated or a fixed container, charged with the material to be disintegrated, with or without special disintegrating members such as pebbles or balls, and/or with or without special stirring means; feeding and discharging devices with the associated sorting or separating arrangements.  Shape, material of grinding media, e.g. balls, pebbles, rods.
[B02C 17/002] with rotary cutting or beating elements -- B02C 17/002 relates to tumbling mills that incorporate rotary cutting or beating components such as knives, hammers or beaters mounted on a rotating shaft to disintegrate material. These elements actively shear or strike the load inside the mill, supplementing the conventional impact of balls or pebbles to achieve finer crushing, pulverising or general disintegration.
[B02C 17/005] the charge being turned over by magnetic forces -- This subclass covers tumbling mills in which the material to be broken is turned over by magnetic forces rather than by gravity or mechanical rotation. The magnetic field causes the charged contents, such as pebbles or balls, to move and grind, providing a method of disintegration that relies on magnetically induced motion.
[B02C 17/007] specially adapted for disintegrating refuse -- The B02C 17/007 subclass includes tumbling-mill type disintegrators that use a charged container, optionally with pebbles or balls, to break down material. It specifically covers machines specially adapted for disintegrating refuse in crushing, pulverising, or general disintegration operations.
[B02C 17/02] with perforated container -- B02C 17/02 covers tumbling mills that disintegrate material in a container equipped with perforations, allowing processed material to pass through while the mill may contain pebbles, balls or other disintegrating members, and relates to crushing, pulverising or disintegrating operations in general.
[B02C 17/04] with unperforated container -- B02C 17/04 covers tumbling-mill disintegration where the material is placed in a closed, unperforated container that rotates to tumble the charge, optionally with grinding media such as balls or pebbles, to crush or pulverise the contents. This subcategory falls under general crushing, pulverising or disintegrating operations.
[B02C 17/06] with several compartments -- The subclass B02C 17/06 refers to tumbling mills that contain several compartments and are used for disintegrating material, optionally with grinding media such as pebbles or balls.
[B02C 17/07] in radial arrangement -- B02C 17/07 covers crushing, pulverising or disintegrating operations carried out in tumbling mills where the material and any grinding media are arranged radially within the rotating container, with or without special members such as pebbles or balls. This subgroup pertains to general-purpose disintegration using the radial configuration of the mill.
[B02C 17/08] with containers performing a planetary movement -- This subclass covers tumbling mills in which the grinding vessel itself follows a planetary trajectory, rotating around its own axis while simultaneously revolving around a central axis. The planetary motion enhances the impact and shear forces on the charge, improving disintegration of solids. It applies to devices that may use balls, pebbles or other grinding media inside the moving container.
[B02C 17/10] with one or a few disintegrating members arranged in the container -- The subclass B02C 17/10 covers tumbling mills that employ only one or a few disintegrating members, such as a small number of balls or pebbles, arranged within the container to crush, pulverise or otherwise disintegrate the charged material. It is a narrow group within the broader category of crushing and pulverising operations.
[B02C 17/14] Mills in which the charge to be ground is turned over by movements of the container other than by rotating, e.g. by swinging, vibrating, tilting -- The B02C 17/14 class covers tumbling mills that break down material by turning the charge through movements of the container other than continuous rotation, such as swinging, vibrating or tilting. These mills may use the container alone or contain auxiliary disintegrating members like pebbles or balls to aid the crushing process.
[B02C 17/16] Mills in which a fixed container houses stirring means tumbling the charge -- B02C 17/16 covers mills that have a stationary container in which the material is disintegrated by internal stirring elements that tumble the charge. The classification applies to tumbling-type mills such as ball or pebble mills where the grinding media move within a fixed vessel to crush, pulverise, or otherwise break down the material.
[B02C 17/161] Arrangements for separating milling media and ground material -- The CPC code B02C 17/161 covers devices that separate the grinding media-such as balls or pebbles-from the pulverised product in tumbling mills, enabling the media to be recirculated while the ground material is removed. This arrangement is used in mills where the material is disintegrated by tumbling in a container.
[B02C 17/163] Stirring means -- B02C 17/163 relates to the stirring mechanisms employed in tumbling mills used for disintegrating material. It covers devices that agitate the mill's contents, with or without grinding media such as pebbles or balls, to enhance crushing and pulverising.
[B02C 17/166] of the annular gap type -- This subclass relates to tumbling mills that disintegrate material in an annular gap formed between a rotating inner cylinder and a stationary outer cylinder, with or without grinding media such as balls or pebbles. The annular-gap design enables continuous feeding of the material and removal of the crushed or pulverised product.
[B02C 17/168] with a basket media milling device arranged in or on the container, involving therein a circulatory flow of the material to be milled -- The CPC code B02C 17/168 refers to crushing or pulverising where a basket-media milling device is arranged in or on the container, with a circulatory flow of the material to be milled.
[B02C 17/18] Details -- B02C 17/18 pertains to specific details of tumbling mills used for disintegration, including those with containers charged with materials and optional disintegrating elements like pebbles or balls. This classification covers the design and operational aspects that enhance the efficiency and effectiveness of these mills in crushing or pulverizing processes.
[B02C 17/1805] Monitoring devices for tumbling mills -- The entry B02C 17/1805 covers devices that monitor the operation of tumbling mills used for crushing, pulverising or disintegrating material. It includes sensors, measurement units and control systems that assess parameters such as mill speed, load, temperature, vibration, power consumption or material flow to optimise performance and safety.
[B02C 17/181] Bearings specially adapted for tumbling mills -- Bearings specially adapted for tumbling mills are designed to support the rotating drum of a mill that disintegrates material by tumbling, often with pebbles or balls. They must withstand high impact forces, variable loads and continuous rotation while maintaining precise alignment of the mill's shaft.
[B02C 17/1815] Cooling or heating devices -- This CPC subclass concerns auxiliary temperature-control equipment for tumbling mills that disintegrate materials. It includes devices that heat or cool the mill container or its charge to improve grinding efficiency or protect temperature-sensitive substances.
[B02C 17/182] Lids -- This subclass concerns lids for tumbling mills used in crushing, pulverising or disintegrating operations, covering their design, structure and sealing features that retain the material charge and any grinding media while the mill rotates.
[B02C 17/1825] Lifting devices  lifting devices associated with the lining for containers  B02C17/22 -- This CPC subclass concerns lifting devices that form part of the lining or support structure of containers used in tumbling mills, where the container holds material to be crushed or pulverized. It includes mechanisms that raise or adjust the container wall, lining, or internal components to facilitate loading, unloading, or operation of the mill.
[B02C 17/183] Feeding or discharging devices -- B02C 17/183 covers feeding or discharging devices for tumbling mills that break down material by rotating a container with or without added grinding media such as pebbles or balls. It relates to general crushing, pulverising and disintegrating operations using tumbling action.
[B02C 17/1835] Discharging devices combined with sorting or separating of material  B02C17/186  takes precedence -- This CPC subclass covers apparatus that discharge material from tumbling mills while simultaneously sorting or separating the material, such as sieves, cyclones or classifiers integrated with the discharge system. It applies to crushing, pulverising and disintegrating operations where the mill container may be charged with balls or pebbles.
[B02C 17/184] with separator arranged in discharge path of crushing zone -- This CPC subclass concerns tumbling mills for crushing, pulverising or otherwise disintegrating material where a separator is placed in the discharge path of the crushing zone, enabling the separation of finer particles from coarser ones as they leave the mill.
[B02C 17/1845] with return of oversize material to crushing zone -- B02C 17/1845 relates to crushing or pulverising operations in tumbling mills where material that remains oversized after an initial pass is fed back into the crushing zone for further size reduction, often using pebbles or balls inside the mill. The code applies to both general and specific methods of returning oversized particles to improve overall disintegration efficiency.
[B02C 17/185] with more than one separator -- The B02C 17/185 subclass covers crushing, pulverising or disintegrating operations performed in tumbling mills that contain a container filled with the material to be broken down, optionally using disintegrating members such as pebbles or balls, and that employ more than one separator to classify or separate the product during the process. This falls under the broader categories of performing operations and crushing, pulverising or disintegrating in general.
[B02C 17/1855] with separator defining termination of crushing zone, e.g. screen denying egress of oversize material -- The code B02C 17/1855 refers to tumbling-mill crushing where a separator, such as a screen, is positioned to define the end of the crushing zone and prevent oversized particles from exiting the mill. This arrangement controls product size and improves efficiency.
[B02C 17/186] Adding fluid, other than for crushing by fluid energy -- The subclass B02C 17/186 covers processes in tumbling mills where a fluid is introduced into the mill for purposes other than fluid-energy crushing, such as aiding in the disintegration of material while the mill rotates with or without grinding media.
[B02C 17/1865] after crushing -- The B02C 17/1865 subclass refers to operations performed after an initial crushing step, specifically disintegrating the material in tumbling mills. These mills contain a chamber filled with the crushed material and may include grinding media such as pebbles or balls to further reduce particle size.
[B02C 17/187] with recirculation of material to crushing zone -- This subgroup refers to tumbling-mill disintegration in which the processed material is continuously fed back into the crushing zone for further size reduction. The recirculation arrangement allows repeated impact and attrition within the same mill, improving efficiency for ores, minerals or other bulk solids.
[B02C 17/1875] passing gas through crushing zone -- The CPC subclass B02C 17/1875 covers processes in which a gas is introduced or forced through the crushing zone of a tumbling mill, such as a mill charged with material and optionally pebbles or balls, to aid disintegration. The gas flow may assist particle transport, cooling, or oxidation during crushing, pulverising, or grinding operations.
[B02C 17/188] characterised by point of gas entry or exit or by gas flow path -- B02C 17/188 relates to tumbling or rotary mills employed for disintegrating materials, distinguished by the location where gas is introduced or removed, or by the specific route the gas follows within the mill. The point of gas entry, exit, or flow path defines the mill's design and operational characteristics.
[B02C 17/1885] the applied gas acting to effect material separation  B02C17/1895  takes precedence -- The B02C 17/1885 subclass covers tumbling-mill disintegration in which a gas is applied to aid the separation of the material, for example by using air or other gases to fluidise or transport particles during crushing or pulverising. It deals with the role of the gas in the separation process, and any more specific provisions in B02C 17/1895 supersede it.
[B02C 17/189] with return of oversize material to crushing zone  B02C17/1895  takes precedence -- This subclass deals with tumbling-mill disintegrators in which the material to be reduced is placed in a rotating container, often with pebbles or balls, and any particles that remain oversized are fed back into the crushing zone for further size reduction.
[B02C 17/1895] gas being recirculated to crushing zone -- B02C 17/1895 covers tumbling mills where gas is recirculated to the crushing zone, aiding material transport, temperature control or combustion during crushing, pulverising or disintegrating operations.
[B02C 17/20] Disintegrating members -- B02C 17/20 relates to the disintegrating members employed in tumbling mills, where a rotating container holds the material to be broken down together with impact elements such as balls, pebbles or similar pieces that crush, pulverise or otherwise disintegrate the charge.
[B02C 17/205] Adding disintegrating members to the tumbling mill -- This code covers tumbling mills in which the grinding chamber is equipped with additional disintegrating members such as balls, pebbles or other media that aid the crushing or pulverising of the charge. The added media rotate with the mill and increase impact and attrition forces to improve material breakdown.
[B02C 17/22] Lining for containers -- Lining for containers refers to protective or functional coatings applied to the interior surfaces of tumbling-mill vessels where material is crushed or pulverized. These linings guard the vessel against wear, corrosion and contamination and can be made of rubber, polymer, ceramic or metal alloys, sometimes incorporating beads or other disintegrating members.
[B02C 17/225] using rubber or elastomeric material -- This subclass concerns tumbling-mill disintegration where rubber or other elastomeric components are employed, such as rubber liners, pebbles, balls or similar members, to aid the crushing, pulverising or grinding of the charged material. It covers the use of these flexible, resilient materials as part of the mill's internal wear-resistant or disintegrating elements.
[B02C 17/24] Driving mechanisms -- B02C 17/24 concerns the driving mechanisms of tumbling mills used for crushing, pulverising or disintegrating materials, covering the transmission elements such as shafts, gears, belts, chains, motors and other devices that provide rotational motion to the mill container or its internal disintegrating members.
[B02C 18/00] Disintegrating by knives or other cutting or tearing members which chop material into fragments -- **Definition:** This place covers:
[B02C 18/0007] specially adapted for disintegrating documents -- B02C 18/0007 covers machines and methods specially adapted to disintegrate documents, using knives, cutters or tearing members that chop paper into small fragments. It falls under crushing, pulverising or disintegrating in general, focusing on equipment designed for secure document shredding.
[B02C 18/0076] with cutting or tearing members fixed on endless flexible members  without cutting or tearing members  B02C19/0006 -- This classification entry, B02C 18/0076, pertains to disintegrating machinery that uses knives or other cutting or tearing members mounted on an endless flexible member, such as a chain or belt, for chopping material into fragments. It excludes configurations where the cutting or tearing members are not fixed in this manner.
[B02C 18/0084] specially adapted for disintegrating garbage, waste or sewage -- B02C 18/0084 covers equipment specially adapted for disintegrating garbage, waste or sewage by means of knives, blades or other cutting or tearing members that chop the material into fragments. Such devices are designed for the initial size reduction of solid waste streams before further processing or disposal.
[B02C 18/0092] for waste water or for garbage -- The B02C 18/0092 class covers devices or methods that fragment solid waste, such as sewage sludge or municipal garbage, using knives, blades or other cutting or tearing members that chop the material into pieces. It is a subcategory of crushing, pulverising or disintegrating operations specifically intended for waste-water treatment or garbage processing.
[B02C 18/02] with reciprocating knives -- A process or device that disintegrates material by chopping it with knives that move back-and-forth (reciprocating). The reciprocating motion repeatedly drives the cutting edges through the feedstock to break it into fragments, often used in shredders, mixers or comminution equipment.
[B02C 18/04] Details -- B02C 18/04 pertains to the detailed aspects of disintegrating materials using knives or other cutting and tearing members that fragment the material. This classification covers specific components, mechanisms, and operational features of such equipment.
[B02C 18/06] with rotating knives -- B02C 18/06 covers apparatus or methods that disintegrate material by using rotating knives to cut, tear or chop the feed into fragments. The classification applies to crushing or pulverising operations where the cutting members rotate about an axis to achieve size reduction.
[B02C 18/062] with rotor elements extending axially in close radial proximity of a concentrically arranged slotted or perforated ring -- This subclass covers crushing or disintegrating devices in which rotor elements run axially and are positioned very close to a concentric slotted or perforated ring. The narrow radial gap between the rotating blades and the ring creates a cutting action that chops material into fragments.
[B02C 18/065] within rotatable bowls, e.g. meat cutters -- B02C 18/065 covers machines that use a rotatable bowl equipped with knives or similar cutting members to chop material into fragments, for example meat-cutting devices. The subclass is part of the broader group of crushing, pulverising and disintegrating operations that employ cutting or tearing elements.
[B02C 18/067] Tub-grinders -- Tub-grinders are rotating drum devices equipped with cutting or tearing members that shred material into smaller pieces, typically used for solid waste, agricultural residues, or recyclable streams. They fall under the crushing, pulverising, and disintegrating group, specifically addressing disintegration by knives or similar cutters that chop material into fragments.
[B02C 18/08] within vertical containers -- B02C 18/08 covers crushing or pulverising processes that use knives, blades or similar cutting members to tear material into fragments while the material is confined inside a vertical container, such as a tall tank or hopper, enabling efficient disintegration in a controlled, upward-flow environment.
[B02C 18/083] with a disc rotor having generally radially extending slots or openings bordered with cutting knives -- B02C 18/083 covers crushing and pulverising equipment that uses a disc rotor with radially extending slots or openings lined with cutting knives, where the knives slice or tear material into fragments as the disc rotates.
[B02C 18/086] specially adapted for disintegrating plastics, e.g. cinematographic films  for plastic bottles  B02C19/0093 , disintegrating plastics  B29B17/00 -- This subclass covers machines specially adapted to disintegrate plastics, such as cinematographic film or plastic bottle material, by means of knives, cutters or tearing members that chop the polymer into fragments. The equipment is designed for cutting, shredding or tearing sheets and films rather than general crushing or pulverising.
[B02C 18/10] with drive arranged above container -- This subclass deals with machines that use knives, blades or similar cutting members to break down material into fragments, where the power source or drive mechanism is positioned above the processing container. It falls under crushing, pulverising, or disintegrating in general.
[B02C 18/12] with drive arranged below container -- B02C 18/12 relates to machines that disintegrate material with knives or other cutting members while the drive mechanism is positioned below the container holding the material. It falls under general crushing, pulverising or disintegrating operations where the chopping action is produced by a lower-mounted drive.
[B02C 18/14] within horizontal containers -- The B02C 18/14 subclass concerns crushing, pulverising or disintegrating processes performed inside horizontally oriented containers. It specifically covers disintegration by knives, blades or other cutting or tearing members that chop the material into fragments while the material remains within a horizontal vessel. This subclass falls under the broader categories of performing operations, crushing, pulverising, or disintegrating, and crushing, pulverising, or disintegrating in general.
[B02C 18/141] with axial flow -- A device in which knives, rotors or similar cutting members chop material while the workpiece moves parallel to the cutting axis, producing fragments through axial flow. This arrangement falls under crushing, pulverising and disintegrating operations that use cutting or tearing members to fragment material. It is a specific sub-category of general axial-flow disintegration.
[B02C 18/142] with two or more inter-engaging rotatable cutter assemblies -- B02C 18/142 covers crushing and pulverising processes that use two or more inter-engaging rotatable cutter assemblies to cut, tear or chop material into fragments. The rotating cutters mesh with each other to increase shear and impact forces, improving disintegration efficiency in operations such as shredding, grinding or size reduction.
[B02C 18/143] with a disc rotor having generally radially extending slots or openings bordered with cutting knives -- The CPC subclass B02C 18/143 covers crushing, pulverising or disintegrating devices that employ a disc rotor with radially extending slots or openings whose edges are fitted with cutting knives. The knives cut or tear material as the disc rotates, producing fragments or a fine powder. This subclass falls under the broader category of disintegrating by knives or other cutting members.
[B02C 18/144] with axially elongated knives -- This subclass concerns crushing or pulverising processes that employ knives elongated along their axis to cut and tear material into fragments. The axially elongated knives rotate or move longitudinally, providing a slicing action that disintegrates the feedstock within shredders, grinders or similar equipment.
[B02C 18/145] with knives spaced axially and circumferentially on the periphery of a cylindrical rotor unit -- The B02C 18/145 subclass covers crushing, pulverising or disintegrating operations that use knives mounted around a cylindrical rotor. The knives are arranged both axially and around the rotor's circumference, cutting or tearing material as the rotor turns to produce fragments. This method falls under the broader category of disintegrating by cutting members.
[B02C 18/146] with a rotor comprising a plurality of axially contiguous disc-like segments each having at least one radially extending cutting element -- This subclass covers crushing equipment where the rotor is built from several axially contiguous disc-like segments, each segment carrying at least one radially extending cutting element that slices material into fragments.
[B02C 18/148] specially adapted for disintegrating plastics, e.g. cinematographic films  for plastic bottles  B02C19/0093 , disintegrating plastics  B29B17/00 -- B02C 18/148 covers equipment that uses knives or other cutting or tearing members to fragment plastics, such as cinematographic film or bottle material, for subsequent processing. The classification includes specially adapted machines that chop plastic sheets or films into smaller pieces, facilitating recycling or further treatment. It falls under crushing, pulverising or disintegrating operations in the CPC hierarchy.
[B02C 18/16] Details -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Details in relation to the shredder housing, jam prevention, safety, lubrication of the knives and the like.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Disintegrating by knives or other cutting or tearing members which chop materials into fragments, specially adapted for disintegrating documents -> B02C18/0007
[B02C 18/18] Knives -- B02C 18/18 covers the use of knives or similar cutting members to disintegrate material by chopping it into smaller fragments. It falls under the broader class of crushing, pulverising, or disintegrating operations, specifically addressing methods that employ cutting or tearing actions rather than impact or shear. This classification is used for technologies where knives are the primary means of breaking down substances.
[B02C 18/182] Disc-shaped knives -- B02C 18/182 pertains to disc-shaped knives used in crushing, pulverising or disintegrating processes, where material is cut, torn or chopped into fragments by rotating or reciprocating disc members.
[B02C 18/184] with peripherally arranged demountable cutting tips or elements -- This subclass covers crushing or pulverizing machines that use knives or cutting members arranged around the periphery, with demountable cutting tips or elements that can be removed and replaced. It pertains to operations that disintegrate material by peripheral cutting arrangements.
[B02C 18/186] Axially elongated knives -- The B02C 18/186 subclass covers axially elongated knives that function as cutting or tearing members to chop material into fragments during crushing, pulverising or disintegrating operations. These knives are oriented along their length to slice or shear the feedstock, enabling efficient size reduction.
[B02C 18/20] Sickle-shaped knives -- B02C 18/20 relates to sickle-shaped knives that cut or tear material into fragments during crushing or pulverising operations. The curved, sickle-like cutting edge is used to chop material as a specific type of cutting member within the broader category of disintegrating by knives. This subclass falls under crushing, pulverising, or disintegrating in general.
[B02C 18/22] Feed or discharge means -- B02C 18/22 covers the feed or discharge means associated with crushing, pulverising or disintegrating devices that use knives or other cutting members. It includes chutes, conveyors, hoppers or other structures that supply material to the cutting zone or remove the fragments after processing. These elements are part of the broader class dealing with general crushing and disintegration operations.
[B02C 18/2216] Discharge means -- B02C 18/2216 defines the discharge means used in crushing or disintegrating processes that employ knives or other cutting members to chop material into fragments. It covers devices and mechanisms that remove the chopped product from the processing zone, such as conveyors, hoppers, augers, or air-blowing systems, ensuring continuous operation of the disintegration equipment.
[B02C 18/2225] Feed means -- This subclass concerns feed mechanisms that supply material to crushing or pulverising units that break the workpiece with knives, blades or other cutting members. It includes hoppers, conveyors, feed rollers and similar devices that deliver the material to the cutting zone for continuous disintegration into fragments.
[B02C 18/2233] of ram or pusher type -- This subclass concerns machines that crush or pulverise material by means of knives, blades or other cutting members that slice the feed into fragments, specifically using a ram or pusher mechanism to drive the workpiece against the cutters. It falls under the broader group for crushing, pulverising, or disintegrating operations.
[B02C 18/2241] of conveyor belt type  B02C18/225  takes precedence -- B02C 18/2241 pertains to disintegrating materials using knives or other cutting members on a conveyor belt system, with B02C 18/225 taking precedence for more specific applications.
[B02C 18/225] of conveyor belt and cooperating roller type -- The B02C 18/225 entry covers disintegrating devices that use a conveyor belt and a cooperating roller as cutting members to chop material into fragments. It falls under crushing, pulverising and disintegrating operations where knives or similar tearing elements are employed, typically in a continuous feed system that integrates the belt-roller arrangement to fragment the workpiece.
[B02C 18/2258] of screw type -- The subclass B02C 18/2258 covers screw-type machines that disintegrate material by cutting, tearing or chopping with rotating knives or similar members. It is applied to crushing, pulverising or general disintegration processes where the material is broken into fragments by a screw-driven cutting action.
[B02C 18/2266] of revolving drum type -- The B02C 18/2266 subclass covers crushing, pulverising or disintegrating processes that use knives, blades or other cutting or tearing members to chop material into fragments, specifically employing a revolving-drum arrangement where the drum rotates while the cutting elements slice the load. This configuration is typical in waste-recycling, material-reduction and comminution equipment that requires continuous, rotary shredding.
[B02C 18/2275] using a rotating arm -- The subclass B02C 18/2275 covers machines that disintegrate material by knives or other cutting or tearing members mounted on a rotating arm, which chops the feed into fragments as part of a crushing or pulverising operation. It falls under the general category of crushing, pulverising and disintegrating processes.
[B02C 18/2283] using rollers  B02C18/225  takes precedence -- B02C 18/2283 pertains to disintegration operations in which rollers act as cutting or tearing members to chop material into fragments, forming part of the broader crushing, pulverising and disintegrating class. It is a narrower subclass under B02C 18/225, which has precedence in the hierarchy.
[B02C 18/2291] Feed chute arrangements -- B02C 18/2291 covers the design and layout of feed chutes that deliver material to crushing or pulverising devices that use knives, blades or other cutting members to tear and fragment the feed. The classification addresses the geometry, positioning and guiding features of the chute that ensure consistent feed flow and efficient material disintegration.
[B02C 18/24] Drives -- B02C 18/24 classifies the power-transmission mechanisms that drive cutting or tearing members used to chop material into fragments in crushing or pulverising processes. It includes shafts, gears, belts, pulleys, motors and other drive devices that provide the mechanical energy to the knives or similar disintegrating tools. This subgroup covers the arrangement and operation of such drives within the broader context of material disintegration.
[B02C 18/26] with knives which both reciprocate and rotate -- This subgroup covers crushing or disintegrating machines that use knives which move both back-and-forth (reciprocate) and spin (rotate) to cut material into fragments. The combined motion enhances chopping efficiency for a variety of solid feedstocks.
[B02C 18/28] with spiked cylinders -- This subclass concerns crushing or pulverising machines that employ cylinders bearing spikes or protruding members to cut, tear and fragment material. The spiked cylinders act as rotating knives, breaking bulk solids into smaller pieces in processes such as recycling, waste treatment or mineral processing. It falls under the broader category of disintegrating by cutting or tearing members.
[B02C 18/30] Mincing machines with perforated discs and feeding worms -- This subclass covers mincing machines that employ a perforated rotating disc combined with a worm-type feeder to draw material into the disc where it is chopped into small fragments by the disc and cutting members. It relates to crushing and disintegrating operations that use knives or other cutting elements to break down material.
[B02C 18/301] with horizontal axis -- B02C 18/301 relates to crushing, pulverising or disintegrating equipment that uses knives or other cutting members to chop material into fragments while the cutting action rotates about a horizontal axis. The devices are designed for general disintegration of bulk solids using a horizontal-axis chopping mechanism.
[B02C 18/302] with a knife-perforated disc unit -- The B02C 18/302 classification covers crushing or pulverising machines that use a rotating disc perforated with knives to cut and tear material into fragments. The knife-perforated disc acts as a cutting element, shearing the feed as it passes through the openings, and is used for general disintegration operations.
[B02C 18/304] with several axially aligned knife-perforated disc units -- The subclass B02C 18/304 covers machines that disintegrate material by cutting with several axially aligned disc units, each disc being perforated with knives or similar cutting members. It pertains to general crushing, pulverising or disintegrating operations where the material is chopped into fragments by a series of knife-perforated discs arranged along a common axis.
[B02C 18/305] Details -- B02C 18/305 pertains to the detailed aspects of disintegrating materials using knives or other cutting and tearing members, focusing on the mechanisms that chop material into fragments. This classification is part of a broader category dealing with general methods of crushing, pulverising, or disintegrating.
[B02C 18/32] with sharpening devices -- B02C 18/32 covers apparatus that disintegrates material by knives, blades or other cutting members that chop it into fragments, specifically when the device includes a sharpening mechanism to maintain cutting efficiency. This sub-class falls under crushing, pulverising and disintegrating operations in general.
[B02C 18/34] with means for cleaning the perforated discs -- This subclass covers machines that crush, pulverise or disintegrate material using knives or other cutting members that chop the material into fragments, where the perforated cutting discs are provided with a cleaning mechanism to remove debris and keep the discs operating efficiently.
[B02C 18/36] Knives or perforated discs -- This subgroup covers devices that use rotating knives or perforated discs to chop bulk material into smaller fragments, typically within crushers or pulverizers. The cutting members shear, slice or tear the feedstock rather than applying pure compression, and are employed in mineral processing, recycling and food applications.
[B02C 18/362] Knives -- B02C 18/362 is placed under crushing, pulverising and disintegrating in general and refers to knives or similar cutting members that chop material into fragments. It covers devices where blades slice, tear or cut to achieve disintegration, such as food processors, waste shredders or industrial cutters.
[B02C 18/365] Perforated discs -- The B02C 18/365 subclass covers perforated discs employed in machines that disintegrate material by cutting, tearing or chopping with knives or similar members. These discs are typically perforated to facilitate the breakup of bulk into smaller fragments during crushing or pulverising operations.
[B02C 18/38] Drives -- B02C 18/38 defines the drive units that power knives or other cutting members used to chop material into fragments in general crushing, pulverising, or disintegrating operations. It includes the mechanical, hydraulic or electrical transmission systems-motors, gearings, belts, couplings and related components-that provide the rotational or reciprocating motion required for these chopping devices.
[B02C 19/00] Other disintegrating devices or methods -- **Definition:** This place covers:  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Rotors with beater elements -> B02C13/09 , B02C13/14
[B02C 19/0006] Crushing by endless flexible members  with cutting or tearing members  B02C18/0076 -- This classification code, B02C 19/0006, pertains to methods and devices for crushing materials using endless flexible members equipped with cutting or tearing elements, a specific technique within the broader category of disintegrating processes. It is part of the Cooperative Patent Classification system used by the European Patent Office and the United States Patent and Trademark Office.
[B02C 19/0012] Devices for disintegrating materials by collision of these materials against a breaking surface or breaking body and/or by friction between the material particles (also for grain) -- B02C 19/0012 covers devices that break down materials by forcing the material to strike a hard surface or moving body, or by generating friction between the particles themselves, a principle also applied to grain processing. These tools belong to the broader class of crushing, pulverising and disintegrating operations.
[B02C 19/0018] using a rotor accelerating the materials centrifugally against a circumferential breaking surface  rotors with beater elements  B02C13/09 ,  B02C13/1807 -- B02C 19/0018 covers crushing or pulverising processes in which a rotor throws the material centrifugally against a surrounding breaking surface, optionally using rotors fitted with beater elements, to fragment the feedstock by impact. This method is classified under the general disintegrating devices category.
[B02C 19/0025] by means of a rotor with radially extending channels -- This subclass covers crushing or pulverising equipment that uses a rotor equipped with radially-extending channels. The channels convey material outward, providing shear and impact forces that break down the feed. It applies to general disintegrating devices employing this rotor design.
[B02C 19/0031] by means of an open top rotor -- B02C 19/0031 covers crushing or pulverising operations that employ an open-top rotor, where material is introduced from above and broken down as the rotor spins. It falls under the CPC hierarchy of Performing Operations → Crushing, Pulverising, or Disintegrating → Crushing, Pulverising, or Disintegrating in General → Other Disintegrating Devices or Methods. This subgroup includes apparatuses such as open-top grinding or mixing machines that use a rotor to achieve size reduction.
[B02C 19/0037] with concentrically arranged open top rotors -- This CPC subgroup covers crushing, pulverising or other disintegrating devices that employ two or more rotors arranged concentrically with an open top, the rotors rotating relative to each other to generate shear, impact or compression forces on the material. It is used for machines where the open-top rotor configuration enables continuous feeding and efficient size reduction.
[B02C 19/0043] the materials to be pulverised being projected against a breaking surface or breaking body by a pressurised fluid  jet mills  B02C19/06 -- This classification entry pertains to methods and devices for disintegrating materials by projecting them against a breaking surface using a pressurised fluid, specifically covering jet mills.
[B02C 19/005] the materials to be pulverised being disintegrated by collision of, or friction between, the material particles  jet mills  B02C19/06 -- B02C 19/005 covers processes where the material to be pulverised is broken down by mutual collisions or friction among the particles themselves, as typified by jet mill operations. It falls under crushing, pulverising, or disintegrating in general within the performing operations class.
[B02C 19/0056] specially adapted for specific materials not otherwise provided for -- The CPC subclass B02C 19/0056 covers crushing, pulverising or disintegrating devices and methods that are specially adapted for particular materials for which no other specific provision exists, falling under the broader group for general crushing, pulverising or disintegrating operations.
[B02C 19/0062] specially adapted for shredding scrap metal, e.g. automobile bodies -- B02C 19/0062 designates devices or methods specially adapted for shredding scrap metal, such as automobile bodies, within the broader class of crushing, pulverising, or disintegrating operations. It covers equipment engineered to break down metallic waste into smaller fragments for recycling or disposal.
[B02C 19/0068] specially adapted for breaking-up fluorescent tubes -- This subgroup of B02C covers devices or methods designed specifically to break fluorescent tubes, typically for safe disposal or recycling. It includes specially adapted crushing, pulverising or disintegrating tools that handle the glass and mercury-containing components of such tubes.
[B02C 19/0075] specially adapted for disintegrating medical waste  sterilisation of refuse  A61L11/00 ; disposal of medical waste  B09B3/00 -- This classification code, B02C 19/0075, pertains to devices or methods specifically adapted for disintegrating and sterilizing medical waste, linking to related processes in refuse sterilization and medical waste disposal.
[B02C 19/0081] specially adapted for breaking-up bottles -- The B02C 19/0081 entry covers devices or methods that are specially adapted for breaking up bottles, such as glass-bottle crushers, hammers or specialized feed mechanisms that fragment bottles for recycling. It falls under the broader class of crushing, pulverising, or disintegrating operations aimed at reducing solid waste to smaller pieces.
[B02C 19/0087] for glass bottles -- B02C 19/0087 covers devices or methods for crushing, pulverising or otherwise disintegrating glass bottles, typically for recycling or waste handling. The subclass deals with equipment designed to break whole bottles into fragments suitable for further processing.
[B02C 19/0093] for plastic bottles -- The CPC subclass B02C 19/0093 refers to devices or methods used to crush, shred or otherwise disintegrate plastic bottles, typically to reduce their size for recycling, waste handling or transport. It encompasses machinery that compresses, grinds, or breaks down bottle bodies and caps into smaller fragments. This entry falls under the broader categories of performing operations and crushing, pulverising, or disintegrating in general.
[B02C 19/06] Jet mills -- B02C 19/06, Jet mills, describes devices that achieve crushing, pulverising or disintegrating by using high-velocity jets of fluid to impact material, producing fine particles without mechanical contact. Jet mills are employed in industrial processes for grinding powders, pigments, pharmaceuticals and other substances, providing precise particle-size control and low contamination.
[B02C 19/061] of the cylindrical type  B02C19/068  takes precedence -- B02C 19/061 refers to disintegrating devices or methods of the cylindrical type within the broader category of crushing, pulverising, or disintegrating in general. This classification is part of the Cooperative Patent Classification system used by the EPO and USPTO.
[B02C 19/063] of the toroidal type  B02C19/068  takes precedence -- B02C 19/063 refers to disintegrating devices or methods of the toroidal type, a specific category under crushing, pulverising, or disintegrating in general. However, B02C 19/068 takes precedence when applicable.
[B02C 19/065] of the opposed-jet type  B02C19/068  takes precedence -- B02C 19/065 refers to disintegrating devices or methods of the opposed-jet type, where jets of fluid or gas are directed against each other to break apart materials. This classification is part of a broader hierarchy dealing with crushing and pulverising in general. However, B02C 19/068 takes precedence when applicable.
[B02C 19/066] of the jet-anvil type  B02C19/068  takes precedence -- B02C 19/066 refers to disintegrating devices or methods of the jet-anvil type, where a high-velocity fluid stream strikes a solid surface to break apart materials. This classification is specific and part of a broader hierarchy dealing with crushing and pulverising in general.
[B02C 19/068] of the fluidised-bed type -- B02C 19/068 covers crushing or pulverising processes that employ a fluidised-bed arrangement, in which a gas stream suspends the material to create a fluid-like state that facilitates its break-up. The method typically uses the turbulent motion of the fluidised particles to disintegrate solids without direct mechanical impact. It is classified under other disintegrating devices or methods in the broader crushing and pulverising group.
[B02C 19/08] Pestle and mortar -- B02C 19/08 classifies pestles and mortars, the manual devices used to crush, grind or pulverize solid materials. It covers variations in shape, size, material and auxiliary features for mixing or disintegrating substances in laboratory, culinary or industrial settings.
[B02C 19/10] Mills in which a friction block is towed along the surface of a cylindrical or annular member -- A friction-driven mill in which a block or pad is pulled along the surface of a rotating cylinder or annular member to crush or pulverise material passing between them. This arrangement provides continuous grinding action and is commonly employed for fine or medium size reduction of bulk solids.
[B02C 19/11] High-speed drum mills -- B02C 19/11 pertains to high-speed drum mills used for crushing, pulverising or otherwise disintegrating materials. The devices feature a rapidly rotating drum fitted with internal grinding elements that reduce particle size through impact and shearing forces. This subclass captures mills operating at elevated rotational speeds for fine comminution.
[B02C 19/16] Mills provided with vibrators -- This subgroup covers milling machines that incorporate vibratory mechanisms to assist the grinding or pulverising process, improving material flow, reducing clogging and enhancing efficiency. The vibrating aid can be attached to the mill housing, shaft or grinding chamber and is used in general crushing, pulverising or disintegrating operations.
[B02C 19/18] Use of auxiliary physical effects, e.g. ultrasonic waves or irradiation, for disintegrating -- B02C 19/18 relates to methods and devices that employ auxiliary physical effects-such as ultrasonic waves, electromagnetic radiation, or other energy forms-to assist in crushing, pulverising, or otherwise disintegrating materials. It is a sub-category of general disintegrating operations within the broader class of performing operations.
[B02C 19/186] Use of cold or heat for disintegrating  B02C4/44 ,  B02C7/17 ,  B02C11/08  take precedence -- B02C 19/186 covers the use of temperature-either cooling or heating-to assist crushing, pulverising or other disintegrating operations. The classification applies when thermal treatment is employed to soften, fracture or otherwise facilitate the break-up of solid materials before or during mechanical reduction. It includes methods where heat or cold is integral to the disintegration device or process.
[B02C 19/20] Disintegrating by grating -- The CPC subclass B02C 19/20 covers methods and devices that break down solid material by forcing it through a grate, screen, or similar perforated surface, causing the material to be sliced or shredded into smaller particles. It pertains to disintegrating operations that use a grating action rather than impact or compression.
[B02C 19/22] Crushing mills with screw-shaped crushing means -- This subclass covers crushing mills that employ one or more screw-shaped elements to apply shear and compressive forces to break down bulk material. The screws may be arranged in helical rows, rotors or augers, and are used for grinding, pulverising or size reduction of solids in continuous or batch operations. The classification is used for machines where the primary crushing action is provided by the screw geometry rather than conventional hammers or rollers.
[B02C 2/00] Crushing or disintegrating by gyratory or cone crushers -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Crushers with a gyrating conical head and a bowl assembly surrounding the head; normally the head is driven.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Cone crushers with non-coaxial discs with intersecting axes -> B02C7/005
[B02C 2002/002] the bowl being a driven element for providing a crushing effect -- B02C 2002/002 covers crushing or grinding apparatus in which the bowl functions as the driven element that creates the crushing action. The bowl is powered to rotate, oscillate or vibrate, directly applying force to the material to be reduced in size. This arrangement is typical of certain impact or roller crushers used for solid bulk processing.
[B02C 2/005] Lining -- The B02C 2/005 "Lining" subclass covers protective wear components such as liners, mantles or plates installed on gyratory or cone crushers to shield the crusher shell and crushing surfaces from abrasion and impact during the crushing or disintegrating of material. These linings are replaceable parts that extend crusher life and maintain efficient operation.
[B02C 2/007] Feeding devices -- Feeding devices are components that introduce material into gyratory or cone crushers, ensuring a controlled and continuous flow of feed. They may include spouts, conveyors, vibrating feeders, or loading hoppers designed to regulate feed rate, distribution, and size before crushing.
[B02C 2013/145] with fast rotating vanes generating vortexes effecting material on material impact -- This subclass covers machines or apparatuses that use rapidly rotating vanes to produce vortexes that strike a workpiece, providing impact or agitation for processes such as grinding, milling, mixing or surface treatment.
[B02C 2013/1857] rotating coaxially around the rotor shaft -- A device that rotates in a coaxial manner around the shaft of a rotor, typically forming a concentric sleeve, disc or cage that drives, supports or balances the rotor while sharing the same axis of rotation. This arrangement is used in machines such as turbines, compressors and other rotating equipment to transmit torque, provide clearance or enhance dynamic stability.
[B02C 2013/1864] rotatable around its own axis -- This subgroup relates to devices or components that can rotate about their own longitudinal axis, such as rotating shafts, rotors, or spindle assemblies used in machining, forming or processing. It includes mechanisms that provide continuous or indexed rotation for material handling or tool operation.
[B02C 2013/1871] vertically adjustable -- B02C 2013/1871 covers drilling or boring apparatus that includes a mechanism for vertical adjustment of the drill or its support. The classification is applied to rigs, columns, or tool holders that can be raised or lowered to change the drilling depth or position while maintaining alignment, such as vertically adjustable drill stems, mast sections, or boring heads.
[B02C 2013/1878] radially adjustable -- A radially adjustable device in the B02C crushing-grinding group, where the grinding or crushing element can be moved outward or inward relative to the axis, allowing the spacing between the working surface and the material to be varied for precise control of the comminution process.
[B02C 2013/1885] of dead bed type -- B02C 2013/1885 covers apparatus that uses a dead-bed type reactor, meaning a fixed packed bed of catalyst or inert material through which a fluid passes for chemical treatment, cleaning or conversion. The stationary bed creates a reaction zone without moving parts, typical in gas absorption, liquid-phase reactions or filtration.
[B02C 2013/1892] cooled or heated -- B02C 2013/1892 denotes processes in which a solid mineral, metal or alloy surface is intentionally cooled or heated as part of its treatment. The temperature change may be applied before, during or after exposure to gases, vapours or plasma to modify surface properties such as hardness, stress or reactivity.
[B02C 2013/2808] the beater elements are attached to disks mounted on a shaft -- This CPC subgroup classifies machines in which the beater elements are fixed to disks that are mounted on a rotating shaft, typically used for crushing, mixing or cleaning solid-materials in processes such as waste treatment, food processing or mineral grinding.
[B02C 2013/2812] the beater elements are attached to a hollow cylindrical rotor -- The CPC subclass B02C 2013/2812 covers machines where the beater or grinding elements are fixed to a hollow cylindrical rotor, allowing material to be processed as it passes through or around the rotating shaft. Such designs are employed in mixers, mills or grinders in which the hollow rotor permits flow of liquids or gases while the beater elements perform the working action.
[B02C 2013/2816] of chain, rope or cable type -- B02C 2013/2816 refers to powder-metal manufacturing processes in which a chain, rope or cable is employed to convey, feed, or support the material during production. It covers equipment and methods that use such flexible members for handling metal powders or compacted parts.
[B02C 2013/2825] with fastening means for fixing lining members to the inner surface of mill-housings -- B02C 2013/2825 covers grinding or milling apparatus in which the interior lining of the mill housing is attached by dedicated fastening devices. It includes designs where liners, wear plates or protective elements are secured directly to the inner surface using bolts, clamps, brackets or other mechanical means, enabling easy replacement and improved wear resistance.
[B02C 2013/28609] Discharge means -- B02C 2013/28609 relates to devices or arrangements that discharge contents from containers, tanks, reactors or similar vessels, covering outlets, valves, spouts and other mechanisms that enable controlled release of liquids, gases or solids.
[B02C 2013/28618] Feeding means -- B02C 2013/28618 falls under the "Feeding means" subgroup of the Cooperative Patent Classification for chemical or physical processing. It covers devices and mechanisms that introduce solid, liquid or gaseous substances into reactors, mixers, furnaces, or similar process equipment, ensuring controlled flow, metering or distribution of the feed material during a production operation.
[B02C 2013/28627] of ram or pusher type -- The code B02C 2013/28627 falls within the class for crushing, grinding or pulverising machines and covers devices that use a ram or pusher mechanism to force material against a work-piece, die or crushing surface. Such apparatus typically employ a reciprocating or continuously moving ram to apply high pressure, enabling efficient size reduction, compaction or shaping of bulk solids. This subgroup distinguishes ram-type systems from other crushing or grinding arrangements.
[B02C 2013/28636] of conveyor belt type -- B02C 2013/28636 covers inventions that use a conveyor-belt arrangement for mechanically cleaning, treating or removing material from a surface, with the workpiece transported on a moving belt while undergoing abrasive or other cleaning actions. It falls under CPC group B02C, which addresses mechanical cleaning and decontamination methods.
[B02C 2013/28645] of conveyor belt and cooperating roller type -- This CPC subclass covers conveyor systems in which a belt works together with a cooperating roller, describing arrangements where the roller guides, supports, or drives the belt. It includes designs for tensioning, drive mechanisms and support features of belt-roller combinations used in material handling and processing.
[B02C 2013/28654] of screw type -- The CPC sub-group B02C 2013/28654 designates apparatus for crushing, grinding, mixing, stirring or agitating that employ a screw-type element. Such devices use a rotating helical screw to convey and blend solid, semi-solid or liquid media in a chamber, often for compounding, extrusion or homogenisation.
[B02C 2013/28663] using rollers -- B02C 2013/28663 covers milling or planing processes that remove material by means of rollers rather than traditional cutting tools. The rollers, which may be cylindrical or conical, shape, smooth or finish workpieces in metal or non-metal processing, and the code applies to machines and methods that rely on this rolling action for material removal.
[B02C 2013/28672] Feed chute arrangements -- The CPC subclass B02C 2013/28672 covers inventions that deal with the design, positioning and integration of feed chutes used to introduce solid, liquid or granular material into a reactor, furnace, grinder, mixer or similar processing unit. It includes features such as chute geometry, guides, seals, loading mechanisms and arrangements that improve material flow, reduce spillage and protect the equipment during feeding.
[B02C 2013/28681] Feed distributor plate for vertical mill -- The feed distributor plate for a vertical mill is a component positioned above the grinding zone that spreads incoming material uniformly across the mill's interior, preventing channeling and improving grinding efficiency. It typically features adjustable openings or slots that can be set to control flow rate and distribution pattern, ensuring consistent feed to the grinding media and aiding product quality.
[B02C 2013/2869] Arrangements of feed and discharge means in relation to each other -- This CPC subgroup covers inventions that describe how the inlet (feed) and outlet (discharge) components of a processing apparatus are positioned relative to each other. It includes configurations that optimise flow, mixing, or separation in reactors, filters, or treatment plants. The focus is on the spatial arrangement rather than the specific mechanical details of the feed or discharge devices themselves.
[B02C 2013/29] devices for manipulating beater elements -- B02C 2013/29 covers devices designed to adjust, move, or control beater elements within machines that process solid or liquid materials, such as mixers, crushers, or dispersers. The classification includes mechanisms that change the position, speed, or orientation of the beaters to improve mixing efficiency or product quality.
[B02C 2015/002] combined with a classifier -- B02C 2015/002 covers processes or apparatuses that separate solid particles from a liquid or bulk solid mixture while simultaneously classifying the particles by size, density or other properties. The entry applies to integrated systems where the separation step is combined with a classifier such as a sieve, hydrocyclone or screening device.
[B02C 2015/008] Roller drive arrangements -- B02C 2015/008 covers arrangements for driving rollers in machines such as conveyors, presses, and printing devices. It includes mechanisms that transmit power to one or more rollers, using gears, belts, chains, cams, hydraulic or pneumatic motors, or direct coupling, and addresses configurations that control roller speed, torque, and synchronization. The classification applies to the structural and functional aspects of the drive system rather than the rollers themselves.
[B02C 2015/126] of the plural stage type -- B02C 2015/126 pertains to metal-working apparatus that incorporates a plural-stage configuration, where two or more processing stations are arranged in sequence to perform successive operations on the workpiece within a single machine.
[B02C 2015/143] each runner pivot carrying more than one runner -- The CPC subclass B02C 2015/143 refers to devices in which a single pivot or axle carries two or more runners (rollers) arranged side by side. This arrangement is typical in conveyor or transport mechanisms where multiple rollers are mounted on a common rotating support to guide or move articles.
[B02C 2015/146] Step-shaped runners -- Step-shaped runners are specially profiled channels in moulds or casting dies that have a series of stepped cross-sections to regulate the flow of molten material and reduce turbulence. They are employed in metal casting and plastic injection processes to improve fill uniformity and solidification control. The CPC subgroup B02C 2015/146 groups inventions concerning such runner designs.
[B02C 2017/065] with several compartments in the form of multiwell blocks -- The subclass concerns centrifugation or sedimentation apparatus that includes a plurality of separate chambers arranged as a multiwell block, enabling simultaneous processing of many individual samples in distinct compartments.
[B02C 2017/165] with stirring means comprising more than one agitator -- The CPC subclass B02C 2017/165 relates to mixing or stirring operations that employ more than one agitator within the same vessel or system. It covers apparatuses and methods where multiple stirring elements are used simultaneously to enhance homogenisation, heat transfer or reaction control. This subclass is applied to both batch and continuous processes in chemical and related industries.
