[B02C 2018/0015] for disintegrating CDs, DVDs and/or credit cards -- B02C 2018/0015 covers methods and apparatuses for destroying or disintegrating optical media such as CDs and DVDs, and plastic cards like credit cards, using processes such as shredding, grinding, crushing or chemical dissolution to prevent data recovery.
[B02C 2018/0023] Switching devices -- The CPC subgroup B02C 2018/0023 covers switching devices that are incorporated in chemical or catalytic process equipment to open or close electrical circuits, including relays, contactors, solid-state switches and related control components used to regulate power to electrodes, heaters or other process units.
[B02C 2018/003] Removing clips, pins or staples before disintegrating -- The CPC subclass B02C 2018/003 covers methods that first detach fastening elements such as clips, pins or staples from a material before it is broken down or disintegrated, typically in recycling or waste-processing operations. It relates to preparatory steps that ensure efficient separation of the target material after the removal of these holding devices.
[B02C 2018/0038] Motor drives -- B02C 2018/0038 covers motor drives used to power and control the motion of machining equipment, including electric, hydraulic or pneumatic drive units, speed-control and power-supply arrangements, and associated control electronics.
[B02C 2018/0046] Shape or construction of frames, housings or casings -- B02C 2018/0046 classifies inventions that relate to the shape or construction of frames, housings or casings for machines, devices or equipment. It covers design features of structural enclosures, protective shells and supporting frameworks that define the overall geometry, mounting arrangements or integration of components within a mechanical system.
[B02C 2018/0053] hand-operated -- B02C 2018/0053 is a Cooperative Patent Classification entry that falls under the broader B02C group for chemical or physical processes and their apparatus. This specific code is used to classify hand-operated devices, such as manually powered tools or implements, that apply or control a chemical or physical operation. It helps patent examiners identify inventions that rely on human effort rather than motor-driven mechanisms.
[B02C 2018/0061] with compacting devices for the disintegrated material -- B02C 2018/0061 covers machines that integrate a crushing, grinding or other disintegration unit with a compacting device, allowing the material that has just been broken down to be compacted directly into briquettes, pellets or similar forms. It includes arrangements where the compacting stage follows immediately after the disintegrating stage within the same apparatus.
[B02C 2018/0069] with stripping devices -- The CPC subclass B02C 2018/0069 falls under the broader group B02C covering separation and purification installations that use filters or membranes. This specific entry is limited to installations that include a stripping device, i.e., a mechanism that removes accumulated solid, liquid or sludge from the filter surface or from a moving belt, typically by mechanical, pneumatic or hydraulic action.
[B02C 2018/147] of the plural stage type -- B02C 2018/147 classifies chemical reactors that comprise several stirred stages arranged in series, each stage functioning as an individual mixing zone to achieve staged reaction or conversion. It is applied to processes where the material passes through multiple mixed vessels to control reaction progress or heat-transfer characteristics.
[B02C 2018/162] Shape or inner surface of shredder-housings -- B02C covers machines for shredding, crushing and grinding. The subgroup 2018/162 pertains to the shape or inner surface of shredder housings, describing geometric features, textures or coatings applied to the interior of the housing that affect material flow, wear resistance and overall shredding efficiency.
[B02C 2018/164] Prevention of jamming and/or overload -- The CPC subclass B02C 2018/164 covers methods, apparatuses or control systems that prevent jamming or overload in machines used for shaping, forming, crushing or otherwise processing materials. It includes sensors, feedback loops, relief mechanisms or operational strategies that detect excess load or blockage and intervene to maintain continuous operation.
[B02C 2018/166] Lubricating the knives of the cutting mechanisms -- The code refers to technologies that apply lubrication to the knives of cutting mechanisms, such as shears, slicers or industrial cutters, in order to reduce friction, wear and heat generation and to improve cutting performance and blade life.
[B02C 2018/168] User safety devices or measures in shredders -- The CPC subclass B02C 2018/168 covers safety features incorporated in shredding machines, such as interlock mechanisms, protective guards, automatic shut-off controls and other devices or procedures that prevent user injury while the shredder is operating. It includes designs that detect obstruction, limit access to moving parts and provide emergency stopping functions to enhance operator protection.
[B02C 2018/188] Stationary counter-knives;  Mountings thereof -- B02C 2018/188 relates to stationary counter-knives and the structures that hold them in place. The classification covers counter-knives that remain fixed while a moving knife cuts material, and includes the various brackets, frames or other mountings designed to secure the counter-knife in cutting devices such as slicers, textile cutters, or food processors.
[B02C 2018/2208] for weblike material -- The CPC entry B02C 2018/2208 covers methods for producing or processing web-like carbon or inorganic materials, such as mats, webs, or mesh structures, often employed as filters, reinforcement layers, or electrode components. It includes steps for forming, treating, or shaping these porous, sheet-like products.
[B02C 2018/307] Cooling arrangements in mincing machines -- The CPC subclass B02C 2018/307 covers cooling arrangements for mincing machines, detailing how temperature-control systems such as water sprays, air streams or refrigerant circuits are integrated into equipment that shreds or grinds material. It includes designs that prevent overheating of cutting elements, maintain product quality and improve operational safety during continuous mincing processes.
[B02C 2018/308] with separating devices for hard material, e.g. bone -- This CPC subgroup covers processes or apparatuses that shape or treat hard materials such as bone using separating devices, for example cutting, grinding or milling tools that incorporate mechanisms to separate the material during removal.
[B02C 2018/367] Resiliently mounted knives or discs -- This subclass covers crushers, grinders or similar machines in which the cutting elements - knives, blades or discs - are fixed to a flexible or resilient mounting, such as a spring, elastomer or other shock-absorbing support. The mounting allows the elements to flex, retract or absorb impact, extending service life and improving safety.
[B02C 2019/183] Crushing by discharge of high electrical energy -- B02C covers technologies for crushing solid materials, and the sub-group 2019/183 specifies crushing that is achieved by releasing a high-energy electrical discharge onto the material. This method uses a rapid arc or spark to generate shock waves or plasma, fragmenting the workpiece without mechanical impact.
[B02C 2/02] eccentrically moved -- The B02C 2/02 subclass covers crushing or disintegrating operations performed with gyratory or cone crushers in which the crushing elements are driven by an eccentric motion. The eccentric movement creates a rotating crushing chamber that compresses material between a mantle and a bowl, allowing continuous size reduction of ores, rocks, or other solid feedstocks.
[B02C 2021/023] for disintegrating material on the surface of the ground -- This CPC subgroup covers methods and apparatuses for breaking up or loosening material that lies on the ground surface, such as soil, sediment or debris, by mechanical, hydraulic or other disruptive actions to facilitate subsequent processing, mixing or removal.
[B02C 2023/165] Screen denying egress of oversize material -- B02C 2023/165 relates to a screen that blocks the exit of oversized particles while permitting smaller material to pass, typically employed in processing lines to separate or retain large fragments during crushing, screening or recycling operations. The device may be adjustable or fixed and is intended to improve product size distribution and protect downstream equipment.
[B02C 2/04] with vertical axis -- The subclass B02C 2/04 covers crushing or disintegration operations that employ gyratory or cone crushers whose rotating shaft is oriented vertically. It includes processes where material is fed into a cone-shaped chamber and fragmented by compression as the crusher turns around a vertical axis.
[B02C 2/042] Moved by an eccentric weight -- This subclass covers gyratory or cone crushing machines in which the crushing element is driven by an eccentric weight that generates a reciprocal or oscillating motion to break material. The eccentric mass creates the required movement without a separate drive shaft, simplifying the mechanism for crushing or pulverising applications.
[B02C 2/045] and with bowl adjusting or controlling mechanisms  B02C2/042 ,  B02C2/06  take precedence -- B02C 2/045 pertains to gyratory or cone crushing machines that include mechanisms for adjusting or controlling the position of the crushing bowl. Such bowl-adjusting features allow the gap to be varied, improving product size, throughput and wear compensation. The code is applied to patents describing these bowl-adjustment or control arrangements in crushers.
[B02C 2/047] and with head adjusting or controlling mechanisms  B02C2/042 ,  B02C2/06  take precedence -- This subclass pertains to gyratory or cone crushers equipped with mechanisms that adjust or control the crushing head, such as hydraulic or mechanical devices that vary the head position or clearance. It refines the broader category of general crushing and disintegrating operations, and is superseded by the more specific groups B02C2/042 and B02C2/06.
[B02C 2/06] and with top bearing -- This subclass covers gyratory or cone crushers in which the crushing head is supported by a top bearing. The top bearing holds the rotating mantle, allowing the crushing chamber to operate with reduced vibration and improved alignment. It pertains to general crushing, pulverising, or disintegrating processes.
[B02C 2/08] with horizontal axis -- This subclass includes crushing or pulverising operations carried out by gyratory or cone crushers that operate with a horizontal axis, where material is reduced by compressive action between a rotating conical mantle and a stationary concave.
[B02C 2/10] concentrically moved -- The subclass covers crushing or pulverising in gyratory or cone crushers where the material is reduced by a component that moves concentrically with the crushing chamber, such as a mantle that advances radially toward or away from a fixed concave. This includes any size-reduction mechanism that relies on concentric motion of the crushing elements.
[B02C 21/00] Disintegrating plant with or without drying of the material -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Disintegrating plants normally with at least two disintegration devices; transportable disintegration plants may be equipped solely with one crusher or disintegration device.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Milling plants specially adapted for grain -> B02C9/04
[B02C 21/002] using a combination of a roller mill and a drum mill -- The B02C 21/002 subclass deals with methods that disintegrate plant matter, optionally after drying, by employing both a roller mill and a drum mill in sequence or combination. This approach falls under crushing, pulverising and disintegrating operations in the CPC hierarchy.
[B02C 21/005] the roller mill having cooperating rollers -- The entry B02C 21/005 covers roller mills that use two or more cooperating rollers to crush, pulverise or otherwise disintegrate material, especially plant matter, with or without concurrent drying. The rollers work in tandem to apply shear and compressive forces, producing a fine, uniform product suited for feed, food, or industrial processing. This subclass falls under the broader category of general crushing and pulverising operations.
[B02C 21/007] using a combination of two or more drum or tube mills -- The subclass B02C 21/007 covers processes that use two or more drum or tube mills operating in combination to crush, pulverise or otherwise disintegrate plant material, optionally with drying, as part of general plant-material disintegration operations.
[B02C 21/02] Transportable disintegrating plant -- The code B02C 21/02 covers mobile apparatus for breaking down plant material, optionally drying it, used in processes such as feed production or bio-fuel preparation. It resides within the performing-operations hierarchy under crushing, pulverising, or disintegrating, specifically disintegrating plant with or without drying.
[B02C 21/026] self-propelled -- The code B02C 21/026 covers self-propelled equipment used for crushing, pulverising or otherwise disintegrating plant material, optionally including drying steps. These machines move under their own power rather than being stationary or towed, and are employed in agricultural or processing contexts.
[B02C 2201/00] Codes relating to disintegrating devices adapted for specific materials -- The subclass B02C 2201/00 covers codes that describe disintegrating devices specially adapted for particular types of material. It falls under crushing, pulverising or disintegrating in general, focusing on equipment designed to break down specific substances such as ores, plastics or organic matter.
[B02C 2201/02] for reinforced concrete -- B02C 2201/02 covers methods and apparatus for crushing, pulverising or otherwise disintegrating reinforced concrete, focusing on devices adapted to break down the composite material that includes steel reinforcement. The classification includes machines, tools and processes designed to fragment or reduce reinforced concrete structures for demolition, recycling or waste treatment.
[B02C 2201/04] for used tyres -- This subclass covers devices or methods specifically adapted to crush, pulverise or otherwise break down used tyres, facilitating their recycling or material recovery. It falls under the broader category of performing operations that involve crushing, pulverising or disintegrating materials, and is intended for applications where the feedstock is waste tyre material.
[B02C 2201/06] for garbage, waste or sewage -- The CPC subclass B02C 2201/06 covers crushing, pulverising or disintegrating devices that are specifically adapted for processing garbage, waste or sewage. It includes machines designed to break down solid refuse or sewage sludge into smaller particles for further treatment or disposal. This subclass falls under the broader hierarchy of performing operations, crushing, pulverising, or disintegrating in general.
[B02C 2201/063] for waste water or sewage -- The B02C 2201/063 subclass covers crushing, pulverising or disintegrating devices that are specifically adapted for treating waste water or sewage. It includes mechanical shredders, grinders or macerators designed to break down solid waste and sludge in wastewater streams, facilitating subsequent treatment or disposal. This subclass falls under the broader category of general crushing and disintegrating operations.
[B02C 2201/066] for garden waste -- The CPC subclass B02C 2201/066 covers machines or processes that crush, pulverise or otherwise break down garden waste such as grass, leaves, twigs and similar plant material, usually for composting, mulching or disposal. It falls under the broader group for crushing, pulverising or disintegrating in general.
[B02C 2210/00] Codes relating to different types of disintegrating devices -- B02C 2210/00 groups the CPC codes that designate the various types of disintegrating devices used for crushing, pulverising or otherwise breaking down solid materials. It encompasses classifications for equipment such as hammer mills, impact crushers, shredder-type units and other mechanisms that achieve material disintegration through mechanical action.
[B02C 2210/01] Indication of wear on beaters, knives, rollers, anvils, linings and the like -- B02C 2210/01 relates to the detection or signalling of wear on the working parts of crushing, pulverising or disintegrating equipment, such as beaters, knives, rollers, anvils and linings. It covers wear-indicating devices, markers or sensors that alert operators when these components have deteriorated and may need replacement.
[B02C 2210/02] Features for generally used wear parts on beaters, knives, rollers, anvils, linings and the like -- The code B02C 2210/02 covers design features of wear components that are commonly fitted to crushing and pulverising equipment such as beaters, knives, rollers, anvils, and linings. It includes general specifications for these parts that protect or replace surfaces subject to abrasion during disintegration processes.
[B02C 23/00] Auxiliary methods or auxiliary devices or accessories specially adapted for crushing or disintegrating not provided for in preceding groups or not specially adapted to apparatus covered by a single preceding group -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Feeding devices, safety devices, disintegrating additives, separating or sorting of material associated with crushing or disintegrating, adding fluid, liquids or gases which are not intended for crushing or disintegrating.
[B02C 23/02] Feeding devices -- B02C 23/02 relates to auxiliary feeding devices used with crushing, pulverising or disintegrating machines. It covers methods or accessories specially adapted to introduce material into the primary crusher or grinder when such feeding means are not defined in earlier groups. The focus is on devices that ensure continuous, controlled supply of the workpiece.
[B02C 23/04] Safety devices -- This CPC subclass covers safety devices for crushing, pulverising or disintegrating equipment. It includes auxiliary methods, devices or accessories that protect against hazards such as overload, impact or unintended release of material, which are not addressed in earlier groups.
[B02C 23/06] Selection or use of additives to aid disintegrating -- B02C 23/06 relates to the selection and use of additives that facilitate crushing, pulverising or disintegrating processes. It covers auxiliary chemicals, agents or substances applied to improve break-up efficiency and is grouped with other special-purpose aids not covered by the preceding categories.
[B02C 23/08] Separating or sorting of material, associated with crushing or disintegrating -- B02C 23/08 refers to auxiliary methods, devices or accessories specially adapted for separating or sorting material in conjunction with crushing or disintegrating processes, where such equipment is not covered by earlier groups. It addresses equipment that assists in handling the product stream after size-reduction operations.
[B02C 23/10] with separator arranged in discharge path of crushing or disintegrating zone -- This group covers crushing or pulverising machines that incorporate a separator positioned in the material discharge path from the crushing or disintegrating zone. The separator is used to classify, size-separate or remove fines downstream of the primary crushing action, and is considered an auxiliary device not covered by earlier groups.
[B02C 23/12] with return of oversize material to crushing or disintegrating zone -- B02C 23/12 covers auxiliary methods or devices that return oversized material back to the crushing or disintegrating zone, allowing continuous size reduction. It applies to systems where material rejected by screening is re-introduced to improve the efficiency of crushing or pulverising operations.
[B02C 23/14] with more than one separator -- B02C 23/14 covers auxiliary methods, devices or accessories for crushing, pulverising or disintegrating that use more than one separator to divide the material during the operation. It applies when the separators are not covered by earlier groups and are not integral to a single apparatus.
[B02C 23/16] with separator defining termination of crushing or disintegrating zone, e.g. screen denying egress of oversize material -- The B02C 23/16 subclass covers auxiliary devices for crushing or disintegrating apparatus that provide a separator defining the end of the crushing zone, such as screens or grates that prevent oversized material from leaving the crusher. These devices are not specified in earlier groups and are specially adapted to terminate the crushing or disintegrating process.
[B02C 23/18] Adding fluid, other than for crushing or disintegrating by fluid energy -- This group covers auxiliary processes or equipment that introduce a fluid to a crushing or disintegrating operation when the fluid is not employed as the primary means of size reduction. The fluid may be added for cooling, lubrication, dust suppression or material transport, but it does not provide the crushing energy itself.
[B02C 23/20] after crushing or disintegrating -- B02C 23/20 covers operations applied after a material has been crushed or pulverized, such as classification, separation, cleaning, drying, conditioning or other post-crushing treatments that are not part of the primary crushing apparatus.
[B02C 23/22] with recirculation of material to crushing or disintegrating zone -- The CPC subgroup B02C 23/22 refers to auxiliary equipment or methods that return already crushed or pulverised material back into the crushing or disintegrating zone for further size reduction, enabling repeated processing within the same operation. It covers recirculation arrangements that are not specified in earlier groups and are specially adapted to improve the efficiency of crushing or disintegrating apparatus.
[B02C 23/24] Passing gas through crushing or disintegrating zone -- This subclass covers auxiliary methods or devices that introduce a gas stream into the zone where material is being crushed, pulverised or otherwise disintegrated, to aid the operation. The gas may be used for cooling, transport, reaction or to improve particle size distribution, and the provision is not covered by the more specific preceding groups.
[B02C 23/26] characterised by point of gas entry or exit or by gas flow path -- B02C 23/26 is a CPC subgroup for crushing or disintegrating processes that includes auxiliary methods or devices not covered elsewhere, where the key feature is the location of gas entry or exit or the specific gas flow path within the apparatus. Such features affect the efficiency of pneumatic or fluid-assisted comminution.
[B02C 23/28] gas moving means being integral with, or attached to, crushing or disintegrating element -- This subgroup covers crushing or disintegrating equipment that incorporates, or has attached to it, a gas-moving device such as a blower, air-jet, or gas-conveying element used to transport material, assist agitation, or remove fines directly at the crushing zone. It applies to integrated or directly coupled gas-driven means not classified elsewhere.
[B02C 23/30] the applied gas acting to effect material separation -- B02C 23/30 covers auxiliary techniques in crushing or disintegrating where a gas is directed onto the material to assist separation, such as pneumatic or jet streams used to blow, lift, or segregate particles during processing.
[B02C 23/32] with return of oversize material to crushing or disintegrating zone -- This subgroup deals with auxiliary arrangements that recycle material which remains oversized back into the crushing or disintegrating zone for further size reduction. It includes recirculating conveyors, screen-based feedback loops and other devices that keep large particles in the crushing circuit.
[B02C 23/34] gas being recirculated to crushing or disintegrating zone -- B02C 23/34 pertains to auxiliary arrangements in which a gas stream is recirculated back into the crushing or disintegrating zone of a pulverising apparatus. The recirculated gas may be used for cooling, conveying material, improving efficiency, or modifying the reaction environment, and is not covered by more specific groups.
[B02C 23/36] the crushing or disintegrating zone being submerged in liquid -- This subgroup covers crushing or disintegrating operations in which the zone where the material is broken is immersed in a liquid, for example wet grinding or slurry-based comminution. It applies to equipment or processes that perform the crushing or disintegration while the workpiece remains submerged, distinguishing it from dry-zone methods.
[B02C 23/38] in apparatus having multiple crushing or disintegrating zones -- B02C 23/38 pertains to crushing or disintegrating equipment that incorporates more than one crushing or disintegrating zone, allowing staged size reduction or different processing conditions within a single machine. It falls under the auxiliary methods or devices group for crushing, where the multiple zones are not otherwise covered by earlier groups.
[B02C 23/40] with more than one means for adding fluid to the material being crushed or disintegrated -- B02C 23/40 covers crushing or disintegrating apparatus that employs two or more separate fluid-addition devices to introduce liquids, gases or sprays into the material during processing. The multiple injection points are used to improve grinding efficiency, cooling or chemical action. This subclass captures designs not covered by single-fluid-addition groups.
[B02C 25/00] Control arrangements specially adapted for crushing or disintegrating -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Overall control arrangements and methods.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Jaw clearance or overload control for jaw crushers -> B02C1/025 - Crushing or disintegrating by gyratory or cone crushers moved by an eccentric weight -> B02C2/042 - Crushing or disintegrating by gyratory or cone crushers eccentrically moved with vertical axis and with bowl adjusting or controlling mechanisms -> B02C2/045 - Adjusting, applying pressure to or controlling the distance between milling members in roller mills -> B02C4/32 - Driving mechanisms; Roller speed control in roller mills -> B02C4/42 - Controlling the distance between discs in disc mills -> B02C7/14 - Controlling the distance between discs in disc mills adapted for grain -> B02C7/186 - Monitoring devices for tumbling mills -> B02C17/1805
[B02C 4/00] Crushing or disintegrating by roller mills -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Roller mills with two or more rollers; with a roller co-operating with a stationary member like a plate, a bar a grid or grating or co-operating with a conveyor.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Roller mills with milling members in the form of rollers or balls co-operating with rings or discs -> B02C15/00
[B02C 4/02] with two or more rollers -- The CPC subgroup B02C 4/02 covers crushing or pulverising processes that employ roller mills with two or more rollers to reduce material size. It is a specific subclass of general roller-mill crushing within the broader category of performing operations, crushing, pulverising, or disintegrating. This code is applied to patents describing equipment or methods where material passes between multiple rollers for size reduction.
[B02C 4/04] specially adapted for milling paste-like material, e.g. paint, chocolate, colloids -- B02C 4/04 in the Cooperative Patent Classification covers roller-mill arrangements that are specially adapted for processing paste-like substances such as paints, chocolate, or colloidal mixtures. The subclass falls under the hierarchy of performing operations, crushing, pulverising or disintegrating, and specifically addresses mills designed to handle highly viscous or semi-solid feeds.
[B02C 4/06] specially adapted for milling grain -- The subclass B02C 4/06 covers crushing or disintegrating apparatus that is specially adapted for milling grain, primarily roller mills fitted with features such as adjustable gaps, husk-separating mechanisms, or grain-specific feeding arrangements to optimise size reduction and product quality. It falls under the broader categories of performing operations, crushing/pulverising, and crushing/pulverising in general.
[B02C 4/08] with co-operating corrugated or toothed crushing-rollers -- B02C 4/08 covers crushing or disintegrating processes that use co-operating rollers whose surfaces are corrugated or fitted with teeth, allowing material to be sheared and reduced between the rollers. Such roller-mill arrangements are employed for fine grinding, size reduction or pulverisation of bulk solids.
[B02C 4/10] with a roller co-operating with a stationary member -- The CPC subclass B02C 4/10 covers crushing or pulverising processes that use a roller acting against a fixed or stationary component, such as a roller mill where material is compressed between a rotating roller and a stationary surface. It is situated within the broader group for crushing, pulverising or disintegrating operations, specifically under the general category for roller-mill type equipment.
[B02C 4/12] in the form of a plate -- B02C 4/12 relates to crushing or pulverising operations performed with roller mills where the material to be reduced is presented in the form of a plate. It includes apparatus that feed a flat plate between rotating rollers to achieve size reduction or disintegration.
[B02C 4/14] specially adapted for milling paste-like material, e.g. paint, chocolate, colloids -- B02C 4/14 covers roller-mill crushing or disintegration equipment specially designed for processing paste-like substances, such as paints, chocolate, or colloidal mixtures. The classification addresses mills that can handle high-viscosity, semi-solid feeds, providing uniform comminution without excessive shear or heat buildup.
[B02C 4/16] specially adapted for milling grain -- The B02C 4/16 subclass refers to roller mills that are specially adapted for grinding grain. It falls under crushing, pulverising or disintegrating operations, specifically those using roller mills to break down cereal grains into meal or flour.
[B02C 4/18] in the form of a bar -- B02C 4/18 refers to crushing or disintegrating operations performed with roller mills where the workpiece is shaped as a bar. It includes methods that feed, roll, or grind bar-like material between rotating rollers to achieve size reduction or pulverisation.
[B02C 4/20] wherein the roller is corrugated or toothed -- B02C 4/20 relates to crushing or pulverising operations using roller mills where at least one roller has a corrugated or toothed surface. The projections increase shear and impact forces, improving grip on the material and enhancing its disintegration.
[B02C 4/22] specially adapted for milling paste-like material, e.g. paint, chocolate, colloids -- Devices or roller mills specifically designed to crush or pulverise paste-like materials, enabling efficient processing of substances such as paint, chocolate or colloidal mixtures. The adaptation often includes sealed housings, temperature control or specialised surface features to maintain consistency during milling.
[B02C 4/24] specially adapted for milling grain -- This subgroup includes roller-mill machines that have been specially modified or fitted for grain milling, featuring grain-specific grinding surfaces, adjustable gaps, or mechanisms to handle husks and other grain parts, enabling efficient crushing or pulverising while achieving the desired particle size.
[B02C 4/26] in the form of a grid or grating -- This subclass addresses roller-mill crushing where the material is forced through a grid or grating formed by the rollers, enabling crushing, pulverising or disintegrating of the feed. It falls under the general crushing operations category in the CPC hierarchy.
[B02C 4/28] Details -- B02C 4/28 pertains to the detailed aspects of crushing or disintegrating materials using roller mills, focusing on specific components and mechanisms that enhance efficiency and performance in these processes.
[B02C 4/283] Lateral sealing shields -- B02C 4/283 relates to lateral sealing shields fitted on roller mills used for crushing, pulverising or disintegrating material. These shields close the side gaps between the rollers and the surrounding housing to prevent product leakage, improve milling efficiency and protect the equipment from wear.
[B02C 4/286] Feeding devices -- B02C 4/286 covers feeding devices used in the crushing, pulverising or disintegrating of materials by roller mills. These devices introduce, regulate or pre-condition the raw material before it reaches the rollers, ensuring a steady and uniform feed to improve milling efficiency and product consistency.
[B02C 4/30] Shape or construction of rollers -- B02C 4/30 covers the design and geometric features of rollers used in roller mills for crushing, pulverising or otherwise disintegrating materials. It addresses aspects such as roller cross-section, surface texture, peripheral shape and internal construction that affect grinding efficiency and product consistency.
[B02C 4/305] Wear resistant rollers -- B02C 4/305 pertains to roller mills that employ wear-resistant rollers for crushing, pulverising or disintegrating materials. The classification covers rollers made of or coated with hard, abrasion-resistant materials such as carbide, ceramics or hardened steel, intended to extend service life and maintain consistent grinding performance.
[B02C 4/32] Adjusting, applying pressure to, or controlling the distance between, milling members -- B02C 4/32 pertains to the means for adjusting, applying pressure to, or controlling the gap between milling members in roller-type crushing or pulverising equipment. It includes mechanisms such as springs, hydraulic or pneumatic actuators, and manual shims that regulate the force and distance between rollers to achieve the desired particle size.
[B02C 4/34] in mills wherein a roller co-operates with a stationary member -- B02C 4/34 covers crushing or pulverising operations performed in mills where a rotating roller works together with a stationary component such as a bed plate or table. The arrangement forces material between the moving roller and the fixed surface to achieve size reduction, commonly used in grain, mineral or plastic processing.
[B02C 4/36] in mills specially adapted for paste-like materials -- This subclass covers crushing or pulverising performed in roller mills that are specially designed for paste-like or highly viscous materials, such as dough, rubber compounds, or food pastes, where the material is fed and processed in a semi-solid state using rollers or similar elements to achieve size reduction.
[B02C 4/38] in grain mills -- This CPC subclass concerns the crushing or disintegrating of grain by means of roller mills. It falls under the general category of crushing, pulverising and disintegrating operations and specifically addresses the process used in grain-mill equipment to reduce grain to flour or meal.
[B02C 4/40] Detachers, e.g. scrapers -- B02C 4/40 covers detachers such as scrapers used in crushing or pulverising operations, especially in roller mills, to remove material adhering to the rollers or mill surfaces. These devices are designed to continuously or intermittently scrape off product, improving feed and discharge efficiency.
[B02C 4/42] Driving mechanisms -- B02C 4/42 covers the drive systems that transmit power to rollers in mills used for crushing, pulverising or disintegrating materials. It includes gears, belts, chains, hydraulic, pneumatic or other mechanisms and couplings that provide the rotational motion required for the rollers.
[B02C 4/423] with vibrating or oscillating mechanisms -- A sub-category of roller-mill crushing that employs vibrating or oscillating mechanisms to assist the breakage of material. The cyclic motion of the rollers or the workpiece enhances feed handling, energy transfer and particle size reduction. This entry falls under crushing, pulverising and disintegrating in general.
[B02C 4/426] Torque counterbalancing mechanisms -- B02C 4/426 pertains to torque-counterbalancing mechanisms used in roller-mill crushers and pulverisers. These devices offset the rotational forces generated during grinding to stabilise the mill, reduce wear on bearings and improve energy efficiency. The classification covers springs, flywheels, hydraulic or pneumatic systems that automatically adjust to maintain balanced torque.
[B02C 4/44] Cooling or heating rollers or bars -- The sub-group B02C 4/44 covers roller-mill crushing equipment in which the rollers or bars are temperature-controlled, either cooled or heated, to improve material flow, prevent sticking, or aid in size reduction. It includes methods for integrating heating elements, circulating fluids, or other thermal management systems directly into the crushing rollers. This helps maintain consistent performance with heat-sensitive or thermally-reactive feedstock.
[B02C 7/00] Crushing or disintegrating by disc mills -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Disc mills used to grind, fiberize, pulverize, granulate or refine a feedstock which is fed between opposing discs or plates. The discs may be grooved, serrated or spiked.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Apparatus specially adapted for manufacture or treatment of cocoa or cocoa products -> A23G1/04
[B02C 7/005] Crushers with non-coaxial toothed discs with intersecting axes -- This group includes crushers that employ two toothed discs whose axes are not aligned but intersect, forming a non-coaxial arrangement used to crush or pulverize material. The discs rotate relative to each other to achieve size reduction.
[B02C 7/02] with coaxial discs -- The B02C 7/02 subclass covers crushing or pulverising processes that employ disc mills whose rotating discs share a common axis. In this arrangement the coaxial discs grind material by shearing and impact as they rotate against each other.
[B02C 7/04] with concentric circles of intermeshing teeth -- B02C 7/04 covers disc mills that employ concentric circles of intermeshing teeth to crush or pulverise material. The teeth are arranged in multiple rings that mesh with each other, providing shearing and impact forces as the disc rotates. This design is used for fine grinding of minerals, chemicals, or waste.
[B02C 7/06] with horizontal axis -- B02C 7/06 covers disc mills in which the rotating disc is mounted on a horizontal axis, causing the material to be sheared and pulverised as it passes between the disc and a stationary surface. This arrangement is used for general crushing, pulverising or disintegrating operations.
[B02C 7/08] with vertical axis -- This subclass defines disc-mill crushing or disintegrating apparatus whose rotor spins around a vertical axis, applying shear and impact forces to break material. It is part of the broader group for general crushing, pulverising, or disintegrating operations, specifically covering vertical-axis disc mills.
[B02C 7/10] with eccentric discs -- It covers processes where material is reduced by rotating discs that are offset eccentrically, creating a shearing and compressive action that crushes or pulverises the feed. Such disc mills are employed in mineral processing, chemical production and recycling operations.
[B02C 7/11] Details -- B02C 7/11 pertains to the detailed aspects of crushing or disintegrating materials using disc mills, focusing on specific components and mechanisms that enhance efficiency and performance in these processes.
[B02C 7/12] Shape or construction of discs -- B02C 7/12 covers the design features of the rotating discs used in disc-mill crushers and pulverisers. It includes the geometry, profile, rim, hub and material of the discs that affect grinding efficiency and wear resistance. The subclass falls under performing operations, specifically crushing or disintegrating by disc mills.
[B02C 7/13] for grain mills -- This CPC subclass covers methods and apparatus for crushing or pulverising grain using disc-mill technology, including the arrangement of rotating discs that shear and reduce particle size. It falls under general crushing-pulverising operations.
[B02C 7/14] Adjusting, applying pressure to, or controlling distance between, discs -- B02C 7/14 covers mechanisms that adjust, apply pressure to, or control the distance between discs in disc-mill crushers. It includes devices for setting disc spacing, pressure and alignment to achieve the desired crushing or pulverising effect, and falls under the broader category of crushing, pulverising, or disintegrating in general.
[B02C 7/16] Driving mechanisms -- B02C 7/16 relates to the drive systems that power disc mills used for crushing, pulverising or disintegrating materials. It covers the various mechanical, hydraulic or electric arrangements that transmit power to the rotating disc, including direct shafts, gears, belts, clutches and speed-control devices.
[B02C 7/17] Cooling or heating of discs -- B02C 7/17 relates to methods and apparatus for controlling the temperature of the rotating discs in disc mills, encompassing cooling or heating techniques used to improve grinding efficiency, prevent overheating, or maintain desired material properties.
[B02C 7/175] Disc mills specially adapted for paste-like material, e.g. paint, chocolate, colloids -- B02C 7/175 covers disc mills that are specifically designed for processing paste-like substances such as paints, chocolate, or colloidal mixtures. These mills employ rotating disks to crush, pulverise, or disintegrate the material, fitting within the broader class of crushing and pulverising operations.
[B02C 7/18] Disc mills specially adapted for grain -- Disc mills specially adapted for grain are machines that use rotating discs to crush, pulverise, or disintegrate grain into finer particles. They are designed to handle bulk grain feedstocks, providing controlled particle size reduction while minimizing heat and product damage. This type of equipment falls under CPC subclass B02C 7/18, which covers disc-mill technologies for general crushing and pulverising operations.
[B02C 7/182] with horizontal axis -- The code B02C 7/182 designates disc-mill machines whose grinding or crushing discs rotate about a horizontal axis to pulverise, crush or otherwise disintegrate material. It covers the specific arrangement of the disc and feed system where the axis of rotation lies horizontally, distinguishing these mills from vertical-axis designs.
[B02C 7/184] with vertical axis -- B02C 7/184 covers crushing or pulverising operations performed with disc mills that have a vertical axis of rotation, where material is fed onto or into a rotating disc and is reduced by the action of the disc's surface. This subclass falls under the broader groups for crushing, pulverising or disintegrating processes.
[B02C 7/186] Adjusting, applying pressure to, or controlling distance between, discs -- The B02C 7/186 subclass concerns disc-mill crushing operations in which the spacing, pressure or relative position of the rotating and stationary discs is adjusted or controlled to achieve the desired size reduction or material flow. It addresses mechanisms for setting and maintaining disc distance during grinding.
[B02C 7/188] Driving mechanisms -- The B02C 7/188 subgroup details the driving mechanisms used in disc-mill equipment for crushing, pulverising or disintegrating materials. It covers all types of power transmission and motor arrangements that rotate the disc, including direct, belt, gear and hydraulic drives, as applied to general disc-mill operations.
[B02C 9/00] Other milling methods or mills specially adapted for grain -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Methods or mills, systems, plants or sequences of operations specially adapted for grain with the meaning of cereal grain.
[B02C 9/02] Cutting or splitting grain -- The B02C 9/02 class covers cutting-splitting of grain as a milling operation. It includes methods and equipment that separate grain kernels by cutting, slicing or cleaving rather than grinding, such as mills specially adapted for this purpose.
[B02C 9/04] Systems or sequences of operations -- B02C 9/04 pertains to systems or sequences of operations used in grain milling that employ alternative milling methods or mills specially adapted for grain, within the broader category of crushing, pulverising and disintegrating processes. It addresses the arrangement and coordination of such equipment to achieve efficient grain reduction.
[B03] SEPARATION OF SOLID MATERIALS USING LIQUIDS OR USING PNEUMATIC TABLES OR JIGS -- This CPC subclass B03 covers processes that separate solid particles from mixtures by employing liquids-such as flotation, washing or sedimentation-or by using pneumatic tables or jigs, where airflow or vibration assists separation based on differences in density, size or other physical properties, and is commonly applied in mineral processing and related industries.
[B03B] SEPARATING SOLID MATERIALS USING LIQUIDS OR USING PNEUMATIC TABLES OR JIGS -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Apparatus for separating or classifying or washing granular powdered or lumpy solid materials of different physical properties such as size, shape or specific gravity.  **Glossary:** - jig: an apparatus for cleaning or separating solid particles (ore or coal) by agitation in a fluid, e.g. water, where the layer or bed of particles are supported on a perforated plate or screen is subjected to an alternating rising and falling (pulsating) flow of the fluid - vanner: an apparatus for cleaning or separating solid particles (ore or coal) by an inclined belt - sluice: an apparatus for cleaning or separating solid particles (ore or coal) by an inclined trough with riffles at the bottom to retain the heavy minerals - strake: an apparatus for cleaning or separating solid particles (ore or coal) by a finely riffled sluice in which fabrics or corrugated rubber forms pockets to trap heavy mineral - heavy media: liquids or suspensions of particles in water used for sink-float separation
[B03B 1/00] Conditioning for facilitating separation by altering physical properties of the matter to be treated -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Pre-treatment, like heating, cooling, exposing to radiation, adding additives, varying physical properties, of the material prior to physical separation carried out by methods and devices of this subclass.
[B03B 1/02] Preparatory heating -- The B03B 1/02 entry defines heating a material before it is subjected to liquid-based or pneumatic solid-separation processes, with the purpose of altering its physical properties-such as viscosity, surface tension or moisture content-to make the subsequent separation more efficient. This preparatory heating is considered a conditioning step that facilitates the removal of solids by improving flow, dispersion or adhesion characteristics.
[B03B 1/04] by additives -- B03B 1/04 covers the use of chemical additives to condition solid materials before separation by liquids, pneumatic tables or jigs. Additives such as flocculants, dispersants, surfactants or other agents are employed to alter particle size, surface tension or charge, thereby improving settling, flotation or segregation efficiency.
[B03B 1/06] by varying ambient atmospheric pressure -- This subclass covers processes that condition a material for solid-liquid or pneumatic separation by changing the surrounding atmospheric pressure, thereby altering the material's physical properties to aid separation. It is a subcategory of separating solid materials using liquids or pneumatic tables or jigs within the broader performing-operations field.
[B03B 11/00] Feed or discharge devices integral with washing or wet-separating equipment -- **Definition:** This place covers:  All kinds of feeding and discharging arrangements, like rotary feeder, scraper, screw or lifting wheel discharger for washing or wet separating apparatus.
[B03B 13/00] Control arrangements specially adapted for wet-separating apparatus or for dressing plant, using physical effects -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Control arrangements adapted for flotation plants as classified in B03D .
[B03B 13/005] Methods or arrangements for controlling the physical properties of heavy media, e.g. density, concentration or viscosity -- The code B03B 13/005 covers techniques and devices for adjusting the density, concentration or viscosity of heavy media used in wet separation processes, such as in mineral dressing or other solid-liquid separation plants. It includes control systems that regulate these properties to optimise the separation efficiency.
[B03B 13/02] using optical effects -- B03B 13/02 covers control arrangements for wet-separating or mineral-dressing equipment that employ optical phenomena, such as light detection, imaging or visual feedback, to monitor and regulate the separation process.
[B03B 13/04] using electrical or electromagnetic effects -- The subclass B03B 13/04 covers control arrangements that employ electrical or electromagnetic effects to regulate wet-separating equipment or dressing plants, such as hydrocyclones, jigging tables or other liquid-based separators, enabling precise adjustment of flow, vibration, or magnetic fields during the separation of solid particles.
[B03B 13/06] using absorption or reflection of radioactive emanation -- This subclass covers control arrangements for wet-separating or dressing equipment that employ the absorption or reflection of radioactive emanations to monitor or regulate the separation process. It applies to systems using liquid or pneumatic methods where the detection of radioactive radiation provides feedback on material presence or concentration.
[B03B 2005/405] using horizontal currents -- B03B 2005/405 covers technologies that create or exploit horizontal fluid currents to achieve mixing, stirring or aeration of liquids or gases, such as horizontal impellers, jets or flow-inducing devices used in chemical, food or water-treatment processes.
[B03B 2009/066] the refuse being batteries -- B03B 2009/066 covers the handling of waste that consists of batteries, including collection, storage, transport and treatment methods such as neutralisation, leaching or recycling to recover metals and reduce environmental impact.
[B03B 2009/067] the refuse being carpets -- B03B 2009/067 covers the handling and treatment of carpet waste, including collection, sorting, recycling, shredding, incineration, or disposal of used carpets and similar textile floor coverings. It addresses processes and systems designed specifically for the management of this type of refuse.
[B03B 2009/068] Specific treatment of shredder light fraction -- The subclass B03B 2009/068 covers processes that specifically treat the light fraction obtained from shredding operations, such as the removal of contaminants, separation of metals, or further size reduction to prepare the material for recycling or disposal. It pertains to handling streams that consist mainly of low-density, non-metallic particles separated after initial shredding.
[B03B 2011/002] Rotary feeding devices -- B03B 2011/002 covers rotary feeding devices that move material using rotating elements such as augers, screws or rotary drums. They are employed in processes like grinding, mixing or bulk-solid handling and may include mechanisms to control feed rate, direction and separation.
[B03B 2011/004] Lifting wheel dischargers -- The code covers devices where a rotating lifting wheel transports bulk material and the discharge is achieved by lifting-wheel discharge mechanisms such as diverters, gates or chutes that remove material from the wheel circumference, used in drying, cleaning, separation or transport processes. It includes designs where the discharge occurs at a fixed or variable position relative to the wheel and may involve gravity or pneumatic assistance.
[B03B 2011/006] Scraper dischargers -- B03B 2011/006 covers devices that remove collected material from a surface by a scraping action and then discharge it, typically used in dust-collection or particulate-handling systems. The scraper discharger's motion clears the accumulated solids and transfers them to a hopper, conveyor or waste stream for further processing or disposal.
[B03B 2011/008] Screw dischargers -- A screw discharger is a device that uses a rotating screw or auger to convey bulk material from a hopper or storage vessel to a downstream process, providing controlled feeding of powders, granules or other particulate products. The class includes various screw configurations, drive arrangements and protective housings for use in industries such as food, chemicals, plastics and pharmaceuticals.
[B03B 4/00] Separating by pneumatic tables or by pneumatic jigs -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Separating or classifying materials on stationary, swinging, shaking, rotary or belt-like tables and using a continuous or pulsating gas current.
[B03B 4/005] the currents being pulsating, e.g. pneumatic jigs; combination of continuous and pulsating currents -- B03B 4/005 deals with separating solid materials on pneumatic tables or jigs when the driving air flow is pulsating rather than continuous, or when a mix of continuous and pulsating currents is applied. The subclass captures the effect of pulsation on particle transport and separation.
[B03B 4/02] using swinging or shaking tables -- The CPC subclass B03B 4/02 falls under the hierarchy "Performing operations → Separation of solid materials using liquids or using pneumatic tables or jigs → Separating solid materials using liquids or using pneumatic tables or jigs → Separating by pneumatic tables or by pneumatic jigs." It specifically covers processes that employ swinging or shaking tables-often called pneumatic tables or jigs-to separate solid particles in a liquid medium, commonly used in mineral and ore processing.
[B03B 4/04] using rotary tables or tables formed by travelling belts -- The subclass B03B 4/04 covers separation of solid particles by means of pneumatic tables or jigs that employ rotating tables or continuous belt-type surfaces to convey material while an air stream segregates particles by size, density or shape.
[B03B 4/06] using fixed and inclined tables -- The CPC subclass B03B 4/06 refers to separation operations that employ fixed or inclined pneumatic tables or jigs, using airflow to move and classify solid particles. These methods are common in mineral processing, recycling and bulk material handling where size, density or shape determines product streams.
[B03B 4/065] having inclined portions -- B03B 4/065 covers pneumatic tables or jigs that incorporate inclined sections to aid the separation of solid particles. The inclined portions guide material flow by gravity, improving stratification and discharge efficiency during pneumatic sorting or washing operations. This subclass falls under the broader category of separating solid materials using liquids or pneumatic devices.
[B03B 5/00] Washing granular, powdered or lumpy materials -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Washing in order to separate fine particles, especially organic particles from coarse particles. Apparatus for classifying solids by wet methods.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Separating by pneumatic tables or by pneumatic jigs -> B03B4/00
[B03B 5/02] using shaken, pulsated or stirred beds as the principal means of separation -- B03B 5/02 covers separation processes where the primary mechanism is a shaken, pulsated or stirred bed of solid particles, typically employed to wash granular, powdered or lumpy materials using liquids or pneumatic action. It falls under the CPC hierarchy of performing operations → separation of solid materials using liquids or pneumatic tables or jigs → separating solid materials using such methods.
[B03B 5/04] on shaking tables -- B03B 5/04 covers the use of shaking tables for washing granular, powdered or lumpy solid materials. The method employs a vibrating inclined platform together with a liquid stream to separate particles by density or size, commonly applied in mineral beneficiation and ore cleaning.
[B03B 5/06] Constructional details of shaking tables, e.g. riffling -- B03B 5/06 concerns the constructional details of shaking tables, including features such as riffling, that are employed in washing or separating granular, powdered or lumpy solid materials by means of liquids or pneumatic action. It falls under the broader categories of performing operations, separation of solid materials using liquids or pneumatic tables or jigs, and specifically addresses the design elements of these shaking devices.
[B03B 5/08] on vanners -- The B03B 5/08 entry covers washing and separating granular, powdered or lumpy solids by means of vanners, which are inclined, often vibrating decks that transport material while a liquid film loosens and carries away finer particles. These machines are widely used in mineral processing to clean ore and recover valuable fractions.
[B03B 5/10] on jigs -- The B03B 5/10 subclass covers washing processes that employ jigs-devices that use a perforated or slotted surface and a pulsating liquid flow to agitate, separate, and clean granular, powdered or lumpy solids. The method typically combines mechanical agitation with liquid movement to remove contaminants while the material remains on the jig during operation.
[B03B 5/12] using pulses generated mechanically in fluid -- B03B5/12 concerns washing solid particles by introducing mechanically generated pulses into the cleaning fluid to agitate the material and detach contaminants, enhancing removal efficiency. The pulses are created by devices such as pistons, diaphragms or impact hammers that produce pressure waves in the liquid during the washing operation.
[B03B 5/14] Plunger jigs -- The B03B 5/14 classification covers the use of plunger jigs for washing granular, powdered or lumpy materials, where the material is held in a jig and pressed or moved through a liquid to remove contaminants or separate solid components.
[B03B 5/16] Diaphragm jigs -- Diaphragm jigs are devices that use a flexible membrane to generate pulsating water flow or pneumatic action, separating solid particles from liquids during washing of granular, powdered or lumpy materials. They provide vigorous agitation and classification while allowing the material to pass through openings, enhancing cleaning efficiency.
[B03B 5/18] Moving-sieve jigs -- Moving-sieve jigs are devices that combine a reciprocating or vibrating sieve with a fluid-driven jigging action to wash and separate granular, powdered or lumpy materials. The moving screen allows finer particles to pass while coarser or denser items are retained and lifted by pulsating water or air currents, enabling simultaneous cleaning and classification in mineral or ore processing.
[B03B 5/20] using pulses generated by air injection -- B03B 5/20 relates to washing granular, powdered or lumpy solid materials by means of pulses generated by air injection into the liquid medium, creating agitation that improves cleaning and separation. The technique is often applied with pneumatic tables or jigs that deliver rapid air bursts to enhance removal of contaminants.
[B03B 5/22] using pulses generated by liquid injection -- B03B 5/22 covers washing of granular, powdered or lumpy materials by applying pulses generated through liquid injection during separation operations. It falls under the CPC hierarchy for performing operations, specifically the separation of solid materials using liquids or pneumatic tables or jigs.
[B03B 5/24] Constructional details of jigs, e.g. pulse control devices -- B03B 5/24 covers the design features of jigs used in liquid or pneumatic washing of granular, powdered or lumpy solids, including mechanisms that control the timing or intensity of the washing pulse. It addresses the construction of pulse control devices and related structural elements that improve separation efficiency.
[B03B 5/26] in sluices -- The B03B 5/26 subclass concerns washing of granular, powdered or lumpy solids by conveying them through a sluice, a channel where a flowing liquid removes contaminants and separates particles. It falls under operations that separate solid materials with liquids, specifically the washing step using a sluice apparatus.
[B03B 5/28] by sink-float separation -- B03B 5/28 covers processes that separate granular, powdered or lumpy solid materials by exploiting density differences, typically immersing the mixture in a liquid where heavier particles sink and lighter ones float, enabling washing or cleaning of the separated fractions.
[B03B 5/30] using heavy liquids or suspensions -- The CPC subclass B03B 5/30 covers washing of granular, powdered or lumpy solids by means of heavy liquids or suspensions, where dense liquids are used to separate and clean the material. It falls under the broader category of separating solid materials with liquids or pneumatic tables or jigs.
[B03B 5/32] using centrifugal force -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Apparatus and methods using centrifugal force for separation, e.g. by utilizing centrifuges or hydrocyclones (cf. B03B5/34 ), not necessarily using special heavy liquids or suspensions.
[B03B 5/34] Applications of hydrocyclones -- B03B 5/34 pertains to the use of hydrocyclones for processing solid particles, such as washing, classifying or concentrating granular, powdered or lumpy materials by creating a cyclonic vortex in a cylindrical chamber. The entry includes any apparatus or method that exploits this liquid-driven cyclone action to separate or clean solids.
[B03B 5/36] Devices therefor, other than using centrifugal force -- B03B 5/36 covers equipment for washing granular, powdered or lumpy solids with liquids or pneumatic action, where the cleaning does not rely on centrifugal force. The devices are used to separate solid material from contaminants in processes such as mineral processing, food handling or powder recycling, and fall under the broader class of performing operations for solid-material separation using liquids or pneumatic tables or jigs.
[B03B 5/38] of conical receptacle type -- The code B03B 5/38 covers washing processes for granular, powdered or lumpy solids in which the material is held in a conical-shaped receptacle. The conical design promotes fluid flow and sedimentation, improving removal of contaminants by liquids or pneumatic action. It is a sub-category of separation operations that use liquids or pneumatic jigs.
[B03B 5/40] of trough type -- The B03B 5/40 subclass covers washing operations for granular, powdered or lumpy solids that are performed in a trough-type apparatus. It pertains to processes where a liquid bath or spray flows through a channel or trough, allowing the material to be agitated and cleaned while moving along the trough. This falls under the broader category of separating solid materials using liquids or pneumatic devices.
[B03B 5/42] of drum or lifting wheel type -- The B03B 5/42 subclass refers to washing processes for granular, powdered or lumpy substances that employ a drum or lifting-wheel type equipment, typically involving tumbling or rotation to achieve cleaning with liquids or pneumatic action. It falls under the broader group of separating solid materials using liquids or pneumatic tables or jigs. This classification is used for patents describing such drum-based washing or cleaning operations.
[B03B 5/44] Application of particular media therefor -- B03B 5/44 relates to the use of specific washing media in processes that clean granular, powdered or lumpy substances by means of liquids, pneumatic tables or jigs. It includes methods where particular media such as surfactants, solvents or abrasive particles are applied to enhance separation or removal of contaminants.
[B03B 5/442] composition of heavy media -- The CPC class B03B 5/442 covers formulations of heavy-media liquids or slurries employed in washing and density-based separation of granular, powdered or lumpy solids, such as mineral concentrates or recyclables. These compositions are designed to have a higher specific gravity than the target particles, allowing lighter material to float while heavier material sinks, facilitating removal of contaminants or size-based classification.
[B03B 5/445] composition of dry heavy media -- The subclass covers formulations of dry heavy media used in washing granular, powdered or lumpy materials, where the media's density enables sink-float separation of particles. These compositions are typically made from metals, oxides or synthetic particles and are employed with liquid-based or pneumatic separation equipment.
[B03B 5/447] recovery of heavy media -- This subclass covers methods for retrieving and re-conditioning the dense liquid or solid media used in gravity-based separation of solid particles such as iron ore or coal after the washing step. Techniques may involve separating the media from the product stream, cleaning it, and recirculating it for reuse.
[B03B 5/46] using dry heavy media -- B03B 5/46 describes washing or cleaning granular, powdered or lumpy solids by passing them through a dry heavy-media stream, such as sand or metal beads, which transports the particles while separating contaminants. The heavy media acts as a carrier in a pneumatic or jig-type separator, eliminating the need for liquids.
[B03B 5/48] by mechanical classifiers -- This group covers the use of mechanical classifiers-rotating drums, rakes, screens or screw conveyors-to separate granular, powdered or lumpy substances based on size, shape or density without employing liquids, pneumatic tables or jigs. The equipment relies on forced motion and sieving or stratification to achieve the separation.
[B03B 5/50] Rake classifiers -- B03B 5/50 covers rake classifiers, devices that continuously separate and wash granular, powdered or lumpy solid materials by transporting them over a moving rake or conveyor while a liquid spray removes fines and contaminants. They are employed in mineral processing, recycling and chemical industries for efficient solid-liquid separation.
[B03B 5/52] Spiral classifiers -- Spiral classifiers are machines that employ a helical trough through which a slurry or wash liquid flows, allowing particles to be separated by size or density while the material is washed. As the liquid moves upward along the spiral, heavier or larger particles settle and are removed, and finer or lighter particles are carried away, making the device suitable for washing granular, powdered or lumpy substances.
[B03B 5/54] Drag classifiers -- Drag classifiers are machines that separate solid particles by moving them through a liquid or across a surface, creating differential drag forces that cause finer material to stay suspended while coarser particles settle or are removed. This technique is employed for washing granular, powdered or lumpy substances, and is classified under CPC code B03B 5/54.
[B03B 5/56] Drum classifiers -- B03B 5/56 refers to drum classifiers, devices that continuously wash, separate or classify granular, powdered or lumpy solid materials by rotating them in a cylindrical drum while a liquid or pneumatic flow removes fines or unwanted particles. The drum's motion and internal baffles promote agitation and contact with the washing medium, enabling efficient size-based separation in processes such as mineral processing, recycling or food production.
[B03B 5/58] Bowl classifiers -- Bowl classifiers are rotating or oscillating vessels that separate solid particles suspended in a liquid, typically used to wash granular, powdered or lumpy materials. The process relies on differential settling and centrifugal forces to move finer particles to the periphery while coarser material remains near the centre, allowing clean separation and removal of contaminants. This equipment falls under CPC B03B 5/58 within the broader category of separation of solid materials using liquids.
[B03B 5/60] by non-mechanical classifiers, e.g. slime tanks -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Hydraulic classifiers -> B03B5/62 - Water impulse classifiers -> B03B5/68
[B03B 5/62] by hydraulic classifiers, e.g. of launder, tank, spiral or helical chute concentrator type -- The CPC subclass B03B 5/62 covers washing of granular, powdered or lumpy materials using hydraulic classifiers such as launders, tanks, spiral or helical chute concentrators. It falls under the performing-operations group for separating solid materials with liquids or pneumatic devices.
[B03B 5/623] Upward current classifiers -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Hydraulic classifiers where the hindered settling is accomplished by an upwardly directed fluid flow.
[B03B 5/626] Helical separators -- Helical separators are rotating, screw-type devices that convey solid particles through a liquid bath while imparting a helical motion to promote thorough washing and removal of fines. The helical flight channels lift and tumble the material, allowing fresh liquid to contact each particle and facilitating separation of contaminants. They are typically used in mineral processing, chemical, and food industries for washing granular, powdered or lumpy feed.
[B03B 5/64] of the free settling type -- B03B 5/64 covers washing of granular, powdered or lumpy solids by means of a free-settling process, where the material is introduced into a liquid and allowed to separate by gravity without mechanical agitation. The classification includes equipment and methods that rely on natural settling to remove contaminants, commonly used in mineral processing, food cleaning and similar industries.
[B03B 5/66] of the hindered settling type -- B03B 5/66 covers washing processes for granular, powdered or lumpy materials that employ a hindered-settling approach, where the solid particles remain in suspension while the liquid is filtered or decanted. This method falls under separation of solids using liquids, specifically within the broader category of performing operations for solid-material separation.
[B03B 5/68] by water impulse -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Hydraulic classifiers -> B03B5/62
[B03B 5/70] on tables or strakes -- The code B03B 5/70 covers washing processes for granular, powdered or lumpy solids that are held on a table or strake while a liquid is applied, often with vibration or pneumatic assistance. It relates to separating solid material by using a liquid medium in a horizontal or inclined surface arrangement to remove contaminants or residues. This sub-category falls under performing operations that separate solids with liquids or pneumatic tables.
[B03B 5/72] which are movable -- The entry B03B 5/72 covers washing processes for solid particulate materials that can be moved, such as granular, powdered or lumpy substances, using liquids, pneumatic tables, or jigging equipment. It includes methods where the material is displaced during washing, for example on vibrating tables, conveyors, or rotating drums, to improve cleaning efficiency.
[B03B 5/74] Revolving tables -- B03B 5/74 covers the use of revolving tables as part of washing or cleaning equipment for granular, powdered or lumpy solid materials. The tables rotate to provide uniform agitation while the material is contacted with a liquid or pneumatic medium, improving separation or cleaning efficiency.
[B03B 7/00] Combinations of wet processes or apparatus with other processes or apparatus, e.g. for dressing ores or garbage -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Wet processes or apparatus in combination with other types of separating processes or apparatus, particularly for dressing/processing ores/minerals, mainly mechanical treatment of crude ores to separate and concentrate the valuable constituents.
[B03B 9/00] General arrangement of separating plant, e.g. flow sheets -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Plants with or without flow sheet involving different, more than one, apparatus for separating, screening, classifying or crushing, not necessarily of the wet or pneumatic table type, specially adapted for the materials specified in the sub-groups. If there are multiple separation techniques involved, the classification occurs also in the subgroups for the specific separation technique (e.g. in B03B5/18 if a moving-sieve jig is involved and if special technical features of this device are claimed).
[B03B 9/005] specially adapted for coal -- B03B 9/005 designates plant arrangements and flow-sheet configurations that are specially adapted for the separation of coal, covering such features as coal-specific crushers, screens, hydrocyclones and other liquid- or pneumatic-based equipment integrated to efficiently wash, classify and de-water coal.
[B03B 9/02] specially adapted for oil-sand, oil-chalk, oil-shales, ozokerite, bitumen, or the like -- This subclass covers the general arrangement of separation plants designed to process oil-sand, oil-chalk, oil-shale, ozokerite, bitumen and similar oily solids, specifying flow-sheet layouts and equipment adaptations for handling these materials.
[B03B 9/04] specially adapted for furnace residues, smeltings, or foundry slags -- This subclass covers configurations of equipment for separating solid materials with liquids or pneumatic means that are specifically designed for handling furnace residues, smeltings or foundry slags. It includes flow-sheet arrangements of the separating plant tailored to these high-temperature or metallurgical waste streams.
[B03B 9/06] specially adapted for refuse -- The subclass B03B 9/06 covers apparatus and plant arrangements for separating solid waste that are specially adapted for processing refuse, using liquids, pneumatic tables or jig devices. It includes general flow-sheet layouts and other configurations designed to handle garbage or similar heterogeneous solid streams. This falls under the broader category of performing operations for separating solid materials with liquids or pneumatic means.
[B03B 9/061] the refuse being industrial -- The subclass B03B 9/061 deals with flow-sheet arrangements of separating plants that treat industrial refuse, detailing how solid waste is isolated using liquid media or pneumatic tables, jigs, and related equipment. It covers the overall layout of systems such as sedimentation, flotation or jigging circuits designed for the processing of industrial waste streams.
[B03B 9/062] the refuse being glass -- This subclass addresses the configuration of separation plants where the material to be processed is glass, covering flow-sheet designs and equipment arrangements that separate glass particles from other solids by means of liquid media, pneumatic tables or jigging devices.
[B03B 9/063] the refuse being concrete slurry -- B03B 9/063 pertains to the overall layout of equipment used to separate concrete slurry waste, describing flow-sheet arrangements for extracting solid particles from the liquid phase by means of hydraulic or pneumatic separation methods. It falls under the CPC group for performing operations involving separation of solid materials using liquids, tables or jigs.
[B03B 9/065] the refuse being building rubble -- B03B 9/065 covers methods and apparatus for separating solid waste that consists of building rubble, such as demolition debris, using liquid media, pneumatic tables or jigging techniques. It includes the design of flow-sheet arrangements and plant configurations specifically adapted to handle and separate construction-site refuse. This subclass is situated under the broader category of separating solid materials with liquids or pneumatic devices.
[B03C] MAGNETIC OR ELECTROSTATIC SEPARATION OF SOLID MATERIALS FROM SOLID MATERIALS OR FLUIDS -- **Definition:** This place covers:  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Separating isotopes -> B01D59/00 - Separating solid materials using liquids or using pneumatic tables or jigs -> B03B - Separating solids from solids by sieving, screening, sifting or by using gas currents; Separating by other dry methods applicable to bulk material, e.g. loose articles fit to be handled like bulk material -> B07B  **Glossary:** - high-voltage: voltage of 1000V (RMS) or more for alternating current and 1500V or more for direct current
[B03C 1/00] Magnetic separation -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Separation of particles out of a fluid or a stream of particles using magnetic effects.
[B03C 1/002] High gradient magnetic separation  acting directly on the substance being separated  B03C1/025 ; acting on the medium containing the substance being separated  B03C1/32 -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Magnetic separation methods that use high gradient magnetic fields.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Magnetic separation device that uses a high gradient magnetic field acting directly on the substance being separated -> B03C1/025 - High gradient magnetic separation acting on the medium -> B03C1/32
[B03C 1/005] Pretreatment specially adapted for magnetic separation -- This subclass covers processes and devices that condition or modify a material before it undergoes magnetic separation, including steps such as grinding, de-watering, adding magnetic additives, or surface treatment to improve magnetic response. The pretreatment is specifically designed to enhance the efficiency or selectivity of the subsequent magnetic separation operation.
[B03C 1/01] by addition of magnetic adjuvants -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Pretreatment specially adapted for magnetic separation by addition of magnetic adjuvants that do not cause an advanced chemical reaction with the particles to be separated.
[B03C 1/015] by chemical treatment imparting magnetic properties to the material to be separated, e.g. roasting, reduction, oxidation -- It covers magnetic separation methods in which the material is first rendered magnetic by a chemical treatment-such as roasting, reduction or oxidation-so that it can be attracted and removed by a magnetic field.
[B03C 1/02] acting directly on the substance being separated -- B03C 1/02 covers magnetic or electrostatic separation methods in which the separating field is applied directly to the material being separated, rather than to a carrier medium; the field acts on the particles themselves to effect separation of solids from solids or fluids.
[B03C 1/021] Separation using Meissner effect, i.e. deflection of superconductive particles in a magnetic field -- B03C 1/021 pertains to the magnetic separation process that utilizes the Meissner effect, where superconductive particles are deflected within a magnetic field, enabling their separation from other materials or fluids. This method leverages the unique properties of superconductors to achieve precise material separation.
[B03C 1/023] Separation using Lorentz force, i.e. deflection of electrically charged particles in a magnetic field -- B03C 1/023 covers methods that separate solid particles by applying a magnetic field that creates a Lorentz force on electrically charged particles, causing their deflection and subsequent collection. The technique exploits the interaction between the particle's charge and the magnetic field to achieve separation from other solids or fluids.
