[B03D 1/1456] Feed mechanisms for the slurry -- B03D 1/1456 covers the devices and methods that introduce a slurry into a flotation circuit, regulating the rate, distribution and aeration of the feed to ensure uniform contact with the flotation media. These feed mechanisms may include pumps, nozzles, troughs or other apparatus that control slurry delivery to the separation cell.
[B03D 1/1462] Discharge mechanisms for the froth -- B03D 1/1462 deals with methods and apparatus for removing the froth that forms during mineral flotation, such as skimmers, overflow weirs, spray or mechanical discharge devices that separate foam from the liquid phase. The aim is to recover concentrate while preventing foam loss and improving overall recovery efficiency.
[B03D 1/1468] Discharge mechanisms for the sediments -- The B03D 1/1468 entry covers the design and operation of mechanisms that remove or discharge sediment from flotation tanks, such as sluices, pumps, or overflow systems. It falls under the CPC group for separating solid materials with liquids, specifically the flotation subcategory.
[B03D 1/1475] Flotation tanks having means for discharging the pulp, e.g. as a bleed stream -- This subclass covers flotation tanks equipped with devices that allow pulp to be removed during operation, typically through a bleed stream that continuously or intermittently discharges material to control concentration and improve separation efficiency.
[B03D 1/1481] with a plurality of parallel plates -- B03D 1/1481 covers flotation devices that employ several parallel plates to enhance the separation of solid particles from liquids. The plates create multiple flotation zones where air bubbles rise, attaching to particles and lifting them for collection. This arrangement improves efficiency compared with single-plate or open-tank configurations.
[B03D 1/1487] Means for cleaning or maintenance -- Means for cleaning or maintenance (B03D 1/1487) covers devices, tools, or procedures used to keep flotation equipment, such as cells, tanks, or related structures, in proper working condition. This includes systems for removing residues, scaling, or fouling that can impair the separation of solid particles by flotation, as well as components that facilitate routine upkeep and repair of the apparatus.
[B03D 1/1493] with means for establishing a specified flow pattern -- This subclass covers flotation processes in which the apparatus includes devices that create a predetermined flow pattern of the liquid medium-such as baffles, weirs, or flow-directing inserts-to enhance the separation of solid particles from the slurry. It falls under the broader category of separating solid materials using liquids, specifically the flotation technique.
[B03D 1/16] with impellers -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Mixing gases or vapours with liquids -> B01F23/20
[B03D 1/18] without air supply -- B03D 1/18 covers flotation methods that separate solid particles from liquids without introducing air, using mechanical agitation, magnetic, electrostatic or chemical means to induce the particles to rise. The subclass is part of the broader category for separating solids with liquids, specifically under flotation processes that do not rely on an external gas supply.
[B03D 1/20] with internal air pumps -- B03D 1/20 covers flotation methods that employ internal air-pump devices to introduce bubbles directly into the slurry, enhancing the attachment of hydrophobic particles for separation. The internal pumps generate a controlled dispersion of air, improving recovery efficiency in mineral processing operations.
[B03D 1/22] with external blowers -- B03D 1/22 relates to flotation processes in which the aeration of the slurry is provided by external blowers that supply air or other gases to the cell, tank or jig, rather than by internal agitators or spargers. This subclass covers equipment and methods that use blower-driven gas streams to create bubbles for separating solid particles.
[B03D 1/24] Pneumatic -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Mixing gases or vapours with liquids -> B01F23/20
[B03D 1/242] Nozzles for injecting gas into the flotation tank -- This subclass covers the design and use of nozzles that introduce gas into flotation tanks during the separation of solid materials with liquids. It includes the shape, arrangement and operating method of the gas-injecting devices that improve particle recovery in flotation processes.
[B03D 1/245] Injecting gas through perforated or porous area -- B03D 1/245 covers flotation methods in which gas is introduced into a liquid through a perforated plate, porous surface or similar diffuser to generate bubbles that attach to solid particles for separation. The entry includes designs of the gas-injection area, operating parameters and means of controlling bubble size and distribution.
[B03D 1/247] Mixing gas and slurry in a device separate from the flotation tank, i.e. reactor-separator type -- The B03D 1/247 subclass covers processes where gas is introduced into a slurry in a separate reactor-separator unit rather than directly in the flotation tank, enabling the formation of fine bubbles that attach to particles before the mixture is transferred to the flotation zone for separation. This arrangement improves control of bubble size and distribution, enhancing recovery of valuable solids from the slurry.
[B03D 1/26] Air lift machines -- Air lift machines are devices that inject compressed air into a liquid to generate an upward-moving fluid stream that transports solid particles, aiding in the separation of solids from liquids in flotation processes. They are employed in mineral processing and other industries to concentrate ores, improve slurry handling, and recover valuable particles without mechanical agitation. This technology falls under the CPC class B03D 1/26, a subcategory of separation of solid materials using liquids or pneumatic means.
[B03D 2201/00] Specified effects produced by the flotation agents -- B03D 2201/00 classifies the specific actions and outcomes generated by agents used in flotation, including how they modify surface properties, promote bubble attachment, and influence selectivity or recovery of target particles. It addresses the intended chemical or physical effects that the reagents impart during the separation of solids with liquids.
[B03D 2201/002] Coagulants and Flocculants -- B03D 2201/002 covers coagulant and flocculant chemicals used in flotation to promote the aggregation of fine particles, thereby improving their separation from liquids. These agents alter particle surface properties so that they clump into larger aggregates that can be more readily recovered.
[B03D 2201/005] Dispersants -- B03D 2201/005 classifies chemical dispersants employed in froth-flotation processes. These agents alter the surface properties of mineral particles to prevent agglomeration and aid their release from bubbles, improving mineral separation efficiency. The code covers formulations and uses of dispersants that affect the flotation behaviour of solids in liquid media.
[B03D 2201/007] Modifying reagents for adjusting pH or conductivity -- The B03D 2201/007 entry covers flotation processes in which reagents are added to control the pH or the electrical conductivity of the slurry, thereby influencing the surface properties of particles and the efficiency of their separation. It includes acids, bases, salts and other chemicals used to adjust solution conditions for optimal flotation performance.
[B03D 2201/02] Collectors -- The CPC entry B03D 2201/02, titled "Collectors," covers chemical agents used in flotation to promote the attachment of solid particles to gas bubbles, thereby enhancing their separation from the surrounding liquid. These agents modify surface properties to increase hydrophobicity and are essential for efficient mineral recovery.
[B03D 2201/04] Frothers -- The entry B03D 2201/04 designates frothers, substances added in flotation processes to create and stabilize a fine bubble layer that aids the separation of solid particles from liquids. Frothers modify surface tension, improve bubble formation, and enhance the efficiency of the flotation step within mineral processing.
[B03D 2201/06] Depressants -- B03D 2201/06 covers depressants used in flotation processes, which are chemicals that selectively inhibit the attachment of unwanted mineral particles to air bubbles, thereby preventing their rise to the surface. This subclass falls under the broader category of separating solid materials with liquids, specifically within the flotation branch of the Cooperative Patent Classification system.
[B03D 2203/00] Specified materials treated by the flotation agents -- This subclass covers the treatment of particular solid materials with flotation agents, specifying the kinds of ores, minerals or other solids that are subjected to the flotation process. It classifies operations where the material's nature is a defining factor in the choice or action of the chemical agents used to separate it by flotation.
[B03D 2203/001] Agricultural products, food, biogas, algae -- B03D 2203/001 pertains to the treatment of agricultural products, food, biogas, and algae using flotation agents in separation processes. This classification is part of a detailed system for patent documentation, focusing on specific materials within the broader category of solid-liquid separation techniques.
[B03D 2203/003] Biotechnological applications, e.g. separation or purification of enzymes, hormones, vitamins, viruses -- The code B03D 2203/003 covers flotation techniques applied to biotechnological processes, specifically the separation or purification of biologically derived substances such as enzymes, hormones, vitamins and viruses. It includes the use of flotation agents to treat these materials so they can be recovered from liquid media, enabling efficient isolation in pharmaceutical and industrial biotechnology.
[B03D 2203/005] Fine and commodity chemicals -- B03D 2203/005 covers flotation processes used to separate fine and commodity chemicals from mixtures by employing flotation agents. It belongs to the CPC hierarchy for performing operations, specifically the separation of solid materials using liquids.
[B03D 2203/006] Oil well fluids, oil sands, bitumen -- This CPC subclass covers the use of flotation agents to separate oil-well fluids, oil sands or bitumen from other solids. It falls under performing operations → separation of solid materials using liquids → flotation, and specifies those particular hydrocarbon-based materials as the subject of the flotation treatment.
[B03D 2203/008] Water purification, e.g. for process water recycling -- The subclass B03D 2203/008 covers the use of flotation techniques to purify water, particularly for recycling process water, by treating specified solid contaminants with flotation agents that cause them to separate from the liquid phase. It falls under the broader category of separating solid materials with liquids or pneumatic methods.
[B03D 2203/02] Ores -- The CPC subclass B03D 2203/02 concerns the flotation treatment of ores, describing the use of flotation agents to separate valuable mineral particles from waste material. It includes methods and apparatus that employ liquid media and reagents to render target ore particles hydrophobic for separation.
[B03D 2203/025] Precious metal ores -- B03D 2203/025 pertains to the treatment of precious metal ores using flotation agents in the separation process. This classification is part of a detailed system for categorizing patent information, specifically focusing on the operational techniques used in mineral processing.
[B03D 2203/04] Non-sulfide ores -- This subclass covers flotation processes applied to non-sulfide mineral ores, such as oxides, carbonates, silicates and other non-sulphide materials, where reagents are used to separate valuable particles from gangue in a liquid medium. It focuses on the specific treatment of these ores by flotation agents to achieve solid-liquid separation.
[B03D 2203/06] Phosphate ores -- B03D 2203/06 in the Cooperative Patent Classification system, used by the EPO and USPTO, specifies the treatment of phosphate ores through flotation processes. This entry is part of a broader hierarchy that deals with separation techniques for solid materials using liquids or pneumatic methods.
[B03D 2203/08] Coal ores, fly ash or soot -- CPC subclass B03D 2203/08 pertains to flotation processes in which coal ores, fly ash or soot are the specific solid materials being treated with flotation agents for separation. It falls under Performing Operations → Separation of solid materials using liquids or pneumatic tables or jigs → Flotation → Specified materials treated by the flotation agents.
[B03D 2203/10] Potassium ores -- The CPC subclass B03D 2203/10 refers to flotation processes applied to potassium ores, where flotation agents are used to separate potassium-bearing minerals from the surrounding waste rock. It is part of the broader category of performing operations that separate solid materials with liquids or pneumatic tables or jigs. This entry specifically addresses the treatment of potassium-containing materials by flotation.
[B03D 3/00] Differential sedimentation -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Differential sedimentation is used for separating a mixture of particulate material into its fractions (for a definition of differential sedimentation, refer to the glossary of terms).  Differential sedimentation type separation apparatus or methods that may include coagulation or flocculation.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Separation of suspended particles from liquids by sedimentation -> B01D21/00 - Sink-float classifiers -> B03B5/28 - Hydraulic classifiers -> B03B5/62  **Glossary:** - Coagulation: Formation of aggregates of particles, the coagula, induced by substances for reducing interparticle repulsion forces, such as an electrolyte. - Flocculation: Formation of aggregates of particles, the flocs or "flakes", induced by a bridging action of large molecules, such as modified starch or polymers. - Agglomeration: Formation of aggregates of particles, the agglomerates, induced by a bridging liquid such as oil. - Differential sedimentation: Differential sedimentation exploits the different sedimentation velocities of the particles belonging to different fractions of a mixture of particles, for separating the mixture into its fractions. When introducing a mixture of particles into a medium, e.g. a liquid, the fraction of particles showing a high sedimentation velocity will settle in a first, bottom layer whilst the fractions of particles showing a low sedimentation velocity will settle on top of that first layer.The liquid in differential sedimentation is used as a separation aid. The objective of this process is thus particle classification as opposed to the process of separating liquid from particles by sedimentation.The driving force for sedimentation is usually gravity or centrifugal force. - Differential centrifugal: sedimentation
[B03D 3/02] Coagulation -- This classification covers methods where particles in a slurry are induced to aggregate (coagulate) to enhance differential sedimentation during flotation, typically using chemical additives or pH adjustment to promote floc formation and improve separation efficiency. It is applied in mineral processing and water treatment to accelerate the settling of fine particles.
[B03D 3/04] assisted by vibrations -- B03D 3/04 pertains to flotation processes in which the separation of solid particles by differential sedimentation is enhanced by applying vibrations. The vibrations may be mechanical, acoustic, or ultrasonic and are used to improve particle dispersion, prevent agglomeration, or increase settling rates during liquid-based or pneumatic separation.
[B03D 3/06] Flocculation -- Flocculation refers to the induced aggregation of fine particles in a liquid to form larger flocs that settle more readily, aiding the separation of solid materials by flotation and differential sedimentation. In this CPC subclass, the process is applied within flotation systems where the flocs improve recovery of valuable minerals or contaminants.
[B04] CENTRIFUGAL APPARATUS OR MACHINES FOR CARRYING-OUT PHYSICAL OR CHEMICAL PROCESSES -- B04 covers devices that use centrifugal force to separate, mix, or otherwise treat materials in physical or chemical processes. It includes centrifuges, separators, and related machinery designed to spin liquids, gases, or solids to achieve separation, agitation, or reaction.
[B04B] CENTRIFUGES -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Centrifuges that are intended for separating, drying, extracting, purifying or other ways of treating mixtures composed of matter in the following physical states:  Such centrifuges may comprise centrifuges with rotary bowls provided with solid jackets for separating predominantly liquid mixtures with or without solid particles; centrifuges with rotary bowls in which solid particles or bodies become separated by centrifugal force and simultaneously sifted or filtered; or other centrifuges including radial chamber apparatus such as butyrometers or counter-current apparatus.  Elements or accessories of centrifuges such as casings, lids, rotary bowls, drives, gearing, suspending or balancing of rotary bowls, feeding, charging or discharging of bowls, control arrangements, heating, cooling or suppressing the formation of foam.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - High-speed drum mills -> B02C19/11 - Cyclone separators -> B04C - Centrifugal casting machines -> B22D - Domestic spin dryers -> D06F
[B04B 1/00] Types of centrifuges -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Centrifuges with rotary bowls with or without separating walls
[B04B 1/02] without inserted separating walls -- B04B 1/02 covers centrifuges that operate without inserted separating walls, meaning the rotating chamber is open or contains only a simple, uninterrupted space for the material to be separated. This subclass includes designs that rely on the centrifugal force alone rather than internal baffles or partitions to divide the flow. It is used for both batch and continuous processes where wall-free separation is advantageous.
[B04B 1/04] with inserted separating walls -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Documents with claims related to the separating walls and/or the construction of the discharging outlets of the lighter fluid(s)  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Documents with claims related to the discharging outlets for the solids or solids/heavier fluid mixture and the mechanism -> B04B1/10 - B04B1/18
[B04B 1/06] of cylindrical shape -- B04B 1/06 designates centrifuges whose rotating component is cylindrical, typically a tube or drum that spins around its longitudinal axis to generate centrifugal forces for separating solids, liquids or gases. This shape is common in laboratory and industrial equipment where a uniform radial field is needed to achieve efficient particle sedimentation or phase separation.
[B04B 1/08] of conical shape -- B04B 1/08 designates centrifuges that have a conical shape, where the rotor or bowl narrows toward one end to create a tapered chamber for separating liquids or solids. The conical geometry enhances sedimentation by directing heavier material toward the tip while lighter material remains near the wider opening, improving efficiency in batch or continuous processes.
[B04B 1/10] with discharging outlets in the plane of the maximum diameter of the bowl -- The B04B 1/10 subclass defines centrifuges whose discharge ports are located in the plane of the bowl's maximum diameter, allowing material to be expelled outward from the central region at the widest point of the rotating container. This arrangement facilitates continuous removal of separated phases while maintaining balance and efficiency in the centrifugal process.
[B04B 11/00] Feeding, charging, or discharging bowls -- **Definition:** This place covers:  All aspects of feeding, distributing and discharging arrangements in centrifuge bowls.
[B04B 11/02] Continuous feeding or discharging -- B04B 11/02 covers methods for continuously feeding material into or discharging material from the bowls of centrifuges, enabling a steady flow of the processed substance rather than batch loading. It applies to centrifugal machines used in physical or chemical processes where uninterrupted material handling improves efficiency.
[B04B 11/04] Periodical feeding or discharging -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The handling e.g. loading/unloading of sample carriers like tubes or tube racks in close relation with the centrifuge .
[B04B 11/043] Load indication with or without control arrangements -- B04B 11/043 relates to centrifuge bowls and includes devices that detect or display the amount of material present, optionally linked to control systems that adjust operation based on the load. Such load-indication mechanisms help ensure proper feeding, charging, or discharge and can trigger automatic adjustments to speed or timing.
[B04B 11/05] Base discharge -- B04B 11/05 covers features for removing material from the base of centrifuge bowls, including designs that enable solids or liquids to be discharged after a separation run. It relates to the feeding, charging and especially the base-discharge mechanisms used in centrifugal apparatus.
[B04B 11/06] Arrangement of distributors or collectors in centrifuges -- B04B 11/06 covers the design and placement of distributors or collectors within centrifuges that handle feeding, charging, or discharging of the bowl. It pertains to how material streams are directed or collected during the centrifugation process, ensuring efficient flow and separation. This subclass falls under the broader categories of performing operations, centrifugal apparatus for physical or chemical processes, and centrifuges.
[B04B 11/08] Skimmers or scrapers for discharging -- B04B 11/08 pertains to skimmers or scrapers designed to discharge material from the bowl of a centrifugal apparatus such as a centrifuge. It falls under feeding, charging, or discharging devices used in centrifuges for physical or chemical processing.
[B04B 11/082] Skimmers for discharging liquid -- A skimmer for discharging liquid is a component used with centrifugal bowls to remove liquid that collects on the surface during operation. It typically consists of a blade or perforated plate that contacts the liquid layer and guides it out of the bowl, facilitating continuous separation in processes such as decanting, washing or extraction.
[B04B 1/12] with continuous discharge -- B04B 1/12 covers centrifuges that discharge material continuously while the machine is running, rather than stopping for batch removal. These devices are used in processes where a steady flow of solids, liquids or slurries must be separated on an ongoing basis, often in chemical, pharmaceutical or industrial applications. The classification falls under performing operations, centrifugal apparatus, and types of centrifuges.
[B04B 1/14] with periodical discharge -- B04B 1/14 pertains to centrifuges that incorporate a periodic discharge system, whereby the separated solid or liquid phase is expelled at regular intervals rather than continuously. Such devices may use rotating or oscillating valves, hatches, or timed openings to empty the collection chamber during operation.
[B04B 1/16] with discharging outlets controlled by the rotational speed of the bowl -- A subclass of centrifuges in which the discharge outlet is regulated according to the bowl's rotational speed, allowing the flow of material to be automatically adjusted as the speed changes. This speed-controlled outlet mechanism is used to improve separation efficiency and product handling.
[B04B 1/18] controlled by the centrifugal force of an auxiliary liquid -- B04B 1/18 covers centrifuges in which the rotation or movement of the sample is regulated by the centrifugal force generated by an additional liquid that surrounds or contacts the material being processed. The auxiliary liquid transmits the force, allowing precise control of separation or mixing without direct mechanical drive of the workpiece.
[B04B 1/20] discharging solid particles from the bowl by a conveying screw coaxial with the bowl axis and rotating relatively to the bowl -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Liquid and solids outlets for screw centrifuges  Documents with claims related to disc stacks in a screw centrifuge  Distributers for feeding the material into the bowl of a screw centrifuges
[B04B 1/2008] with an abrasion-resistant conveyor or drum -- B04B 1/2008 refers to centrifuges equipped with a conveyor or drum that is specifically abrasion-resistant, enabling the device to handle and transport harsh, gritty feedstocks without rapid wear. Such designs are typical in mineral processing, recycling or chemical plants where continuous removal of solid particles is required.
[B04B 1/2016] Driving control or mechanisms; Arrangement of transmission gearing -- B04B 1/2016 relates to the driving control and transmission mechanisms used in centrifuges, covering the arrangement of gears, couplings, belts or other power-transfer components that regulate rotor speed and torque. It includes designs for speed control devices, gear trains and their integration with the centrifuge drive system.
[B04B 13/00] Control arrangements specially designed for centrifuges -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Control arrangements specially adapted for centrifuges.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Control arrangements for feed, charge or discharge -> B04B11/00
[B04B 13/003] Rotor identification systems -- Rotor identification systems are control mechanisms that automatically recognize the type of rotor installed in a centrifuge, linking it to the machine's control software to set appropriate speed, balance and safety parameters. They use RFID tags, barcodes or mechanical coding to ensure only compatible rotors are operated, preventing misuse or damage.
[B04B 15/00] Other accessories for centrifuges -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Auxiliary equipment for centrifuges like cooling means, cleaning means, venting means and the like
[B04B 15/02] for cooling, heating, or heat insulating -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Cooling of bearings: -> B04B9/12
[B04B 15/04] for suppressing the formation of foam -- This subclass covers accessories fitted to centrifuges that prevent or reduce foam during operation, such as antifoam dispensers, degassing ports or surfactant delivery devices. By suppressing foam formation, these items improve separation efficiency and product quality in centrifugal processes.
[B04B 15/06] for cleaning bowls, filters, sieves, inserts, or the like -- The code B04B 15/06 covers accessories designed to clean the internal components of centrifuges, such as bowls, filters, sieves or inserts. These devices are used to remove residues and maintain hygiene and performance of the rotating equipment.
[B04B 15/08] for ventilating or producing a vacuum in the centrifuge -- This subclass includes accessories that provide airflow or generate a vacuum within a centrifuge, such as venting ports, vacuum pumps or related devices that aid separation efficiency. It is grouped under other accessories for centrifuges in the CPC hierarchy.
[B04B 15/10] for forming a filtering layer in the rotary bowl -- B04B 15/10 covers accessories for centrifuges that create a filtering layer on the inner surface of a rotary bowl, facilitating separation of solids and liquids during operation. The feature typically involves a film or deposit that enhances filtration efficiency in centrifugal processes.
[B04B 15/12] for drying or washing the separated solid particles -- The subclass B04B 15/12 covers accessories attached to centrifuges that are used to dry or wash the solid particles after they have been separated. It includes devices such as spray or mist nozzles, washing chambers, drying drums, or any apparatus that applies fluid, heat or air to the solids while they remain on the centrifuge. These items are intended to improve the cleanliness or moisture content of the recovered material.
[B04B 2001/2025] with drive comprising a planetary gear -- This CPC subclass refers to propeller assemblies whose power-transmission system incorporates a planetary gear train, allowing speed reduction or torque increase between the drive source and the propeller. It is used in marine, aeronautical or other rotating-propulsion applications where compact, efficient gearing is required.
[B04B 2001/2033] with feed accelerator inside the conveying screw -- This subclass refers to screw-type conveying devices that contain a feed-accelerator mechanism built into the interior of the screw flight to increase the rate at which material is introduced into the conveyor. The accelerator may be a tapered or helical section or other internal guide that speeds up the feed before it is transported, improving handling efficiency for bulk or granular products.
[B04B 2001/2041] with baffles, plates, vanes or discs attached to the conveying screw -- The Cooperative Patent Classification entry B04B 2001/2041 pertains to centrifuges and similar apparatus, specifically detailing designs that incorporate baffles, plates, vanes, or discs attached to the conveying screw to enhance material handling and separation efficiency.
[B04B 2001/205] with special construction of screw thread, e.g. segments, height -- The CPC subclass B04B 2001/205 relates to rotary screw-type pumps or compressors whose impeller features a non-standard screw-thread geometry, such as segmented or variable-height threads. These special thread constructions are used to modify the flow or pressure characteristics of the device, often improving efficiency or allowing tailored performance across operating ranges.
[B04B 2001/2058] with ribbon-type screw conveyor -- B04B 2001/2058 refers to rotary or screw pumps that incorporate a ribbon-type screw conveyor, using a flat helical ribbon to move bulk material or slurry through the pump housing and provide feeding or conveying action as part of the pumping process.
[B04B 2001/2066] with additional disc stacks -- B04B 2001/2066 covers stirring or mixing apparatus equipped with one or more additional disc stacks, which are used to modify flow patterns and improve homogenisation of liquids or pastes in industrial processing.
[B04B 2001/2075] with means for recovering the energy of the outflowing liquid -- B04B 2001/2075 relates to pumps that include means for recovering the energy of the outflowing liquid, such as turbines, pressure-recovery vanes or other devices that convert the fluid's kinetic or pressure energy back into useful work.
[B04B 2001/2083] Configuration of liquid outlets -- The CPC subclass B04B 2001/2083 pertains to the design and arrangement of liquid outlets in devices that move or press liquids, such as pumps, sprayers, or dispensing apparatuses. It includes specific configurations of nozzles, ports, or openings that control the direction, flow rate, or spray pattern of the discharged fluid.
[B04B 2001/2091] Configuration of solids outlets -- This subgroup addresses the structural design of the outlet through which solid particles are discharged from a spray or dispensing device, covering the shape, orientation and any features that control the flow pattern or distribution of the solids.
[B04B 2005/0435] with adapters for centrifuge tubes or bags -- The subclass B04B 2005/0435 covers centrifuge apparatus that incorporate adapters designed to hold standard centrifuge tubes or flexible bags, enabling stable and balanced loading during operation. These adapters provide secure positioning and sealing of the containers within the rotor.
[B04B 2005/045] having annular separation channels -- The CPC subgroup B04B 2005/045 pertains to rotary centrifugal separators that incorporate annular separation channels, i.e., concentric grooves or passages arranged around the rotor axis through which the feed moves radially outward for particle or phase separation.
[B04B 2005/0457] having three-dimensional spirally wound separation channels -- This subclass covers apparatuses that separate fluids by means of channels wound in a three-dimensional spiral shape, creating a continuous helical flow path within a compact volume. The design is used in filters, membranes, heat-exchange or similar devices to improve separation efficiency, and includes any implementation where the separation channels are arranged in a spatially spiraled geometry.
[B04B 2005/0464] with hollow or massive core in centrifuge bowl -- B04B 2005/0464 designates centrifuge bowls that contain either a hollow core or a solid, massive core. The core alters the internal flow dynamics or structural strength of the bowl to achieve particular separation or processing effects, and it is a subclass of B04B 2005 for centrifuges with specially constructed bowls.
[B04B 2005/0471] with additional elutriation separation of different particles -- B04B 2005/0471 covers spray- or atomising-type equipment that incorporates an additional elutriation step to separate particles of different size or density, typically by allowing lighter particles to be carried upward by the gas stream while heavier ones fall out. This subclass is used in processes such as spray drying, granulation or coating where downstream particle classification improves product uniformity.
[B04B 2005/0478] with filters in the separation chamber -- B04B 2005/0478 concerns separation devices that incorporate one or more filters positioned inside the separation chamber to retain solid particles while permitting fluids to pass, typically employed in filtration, centrifugation or sedimentation processes.
[B04B 2005/0485] with a displaceable piston in the centrifuge chamber -- The sub-group B04B 2005/0485 refers to centrifuges whose rotating chamber includes a piston that can be displaced during operation. The movable piston adjusts the internal volume or creates a pressure differential to enhance separation of solids from liquids, and it may be used for loading, unloading or compensating for material expansion.
[B04B 2005/0492] with fluid conveying umbilicus between stationary and rotary centrifuge parts -- This CPC subgroup covers centrifuges that incorporate a fluid-conveying umbilicus linking the stationary and rotating parts, enabling liquid to pass across the rotary interface without leakage. The design typically uses seals, rotary unions or similar devices to maintain continuous flow while the rotor spins, facilitating processes such as continuous separation or feed-discharge in high-speed centrifugation.
[B04B 2005/105] being a grinding mill -- B04B 2005/105 relates to machines that crush or pulverise material by means of a rotating grinding mill. It includes designs where material is fed into a rotating drum or cylinder containing grinding media, and the mill may be powered mechanically or by other means.
[B04B 2005/125] the rotors comprising separating walls -- The CPC subclass B04B 2005/125 covers rotors in centrifuges that incorporate internal separating walls to divide the rotor chamber into distinct zones. These walls enable simultaneous processing of multiple streams or improve separation efficiency by isolating different phases during rotation.
[B04B 2007/005] Retaining arms for gripping the stationary part of a centrifuge bowl or hold the bowl itself -- B04B 2007/005 pertains to the components that secure a centrifuge bowl in place, specifically retaining arms or similar devices that grip the stationary part of the bowl or hold the bowl itself during operation. This subclass covers the means of clamping or supporting the bowl to ensure stable rotation and effective separation.
[B04B 2007/025] Lids for laboratory centrifuge rotors -- The code refers to accessories that close or seal the rotors of laboratory centrifuges. Such lids are designed to contain samples during high-speed rotation, protect the operator from accidental release, and may include features for balancing or temperature control. They are typically made from plastics, metals or composites and are fitted to standard rotor designs.
[B04B 2007/065] Devices and measures in the event of rotor fracturing, e.g. lines of weakness, stress regions -- The code refers to safety and control features designed for rotors that may fracture, including the incorporation of intentional lines of weakness, stress-relief zones, or other measures that limit crack propagation and prevent catastrophic failure. Such devices aim to guide breakage in a predictable manner, protect adjoining components, and maintain overall system stability after a rotor break.
[B04B 2009/085] Locking means between drive shaft and rotor -- The subclass B04B 2009/085 relates to mechanical devices that lock a drive shaft to the rotor of a pump, compressor or similar rotating machine, preventing relative motion between them. It includes interlocking elements such as splines, keys, friction clutches or other securing means that ensure the shaft drives the rotor in a fixed, non-slipping manner.
[B04B 2009/143] by weight compensation with liquids -- This CPC subclass covers devices and methods that balance or compensate variable loads by adding, removing or shifting liquids, such as hydraulic counterweights in elevators, lifts, rotating platforms or similar mechanisms where the liquid's mass offsets the weight of the payload.
[B04B 2011/046] Loading, unloading, manipulating sample containers -- The CPC subclass B04B 2011/046 covers apparatuses and methods for loading, unloading and handling sample containers such as vials, test tubes, cuvettes or micro-plates. It includes mechanisms that position, transfer, rotate, seal or otherwise manipulate these containers during processes like testing, analysis or preparation, often in automated laboratory or industrial settings.
[B04B 2011/084] with cables for cake removal -- B04B 2011/084 covers pump arrangements that include cables or cord-like elements used to dislodge and extract solid build-up, often called cake, from pump interiors or associated conduits. The cables are typically moved mechanically or by fluid pressure to scrape or pull the deposit away, improving pump efficiency and reducing maintenance.
[B04B 2011/086] with a plurality of scraper blades -- This CPC class covers machines that employ several scraper blades arranged to remove material from a surface, typically in a rotating, reciprocating or fixed configuration. It includes industrial cleaning, coating removal, and material-handling equipment that uses multiple blades to increase coverage or efficiency.
[B04B 2011/088] with angularly and axially offset scrapers -- B04B 2011/088 relates to cleaning machines that employ scrapers positioned at an angle or displaced along the axis relative to each other, enabling more efficient removal of material from surfaces.
[B04B 2013/006] Interface detection or monitoring of separated components -- The subclass covers methods and apparatus for detecting or monitoring the interface between separated components in a separation process, such as liquid-liquid, solid-liquid or gas-liquid boundaries, using sensors, optical, acoustic or other techniques. It is part of CPC group B04B, which relates to separating, purifying, extracting and concentrating processes.
[B04B 3/00] Centrifuges with rotary bowls in which solid particles or bodies become separated by centrifugal force and simultaneous sifting or filtering -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Any type of centrifuge having a screen, sieve or mesh associated with the bowl for the separation of solids from the liquid, having different types of mechanism for discharging the solid particles.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Drying solid material by using centrifuges -> F26B5/08
[B04B 3/02] discharging solid particles from the bowl by means coaxial with the bowl axis and moving to and fro, i.e. push-type centrifuges -- B04B 3/02 covers push-type centrifuges where solid material is expelled from the rotating bowl through a coaxial discharge mechanism that moves back and forth along the bowl axis. This arrangement enables continuous removal of separated particles while the bowl continues to rotate.
[B04B 3/025] with a reversible filtering device -- This subclass covers rotary-bowl centrifuges that separate solid particles by centrifugal force while simultaneously sifting or filtering, and that incorporate a filtering device which can be reversed to restore the bowl for normal operation.
[B04B 3/04] discharging solid particles from the bowl by a conveying screw coaxial with the bowl axis and rotating relatively to the bowl -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - The sifting or filtering part of the bowl -> B04B1/20 - Aspects relating to details not relating to -> B04B1/2008 , B04B1/2016
[B04B 3/06] discharging solid particles by vibrating the bowl -- This subclass covers centrifuges with rotating bowls that separate solids by centrifugal force and concurrently sift or filter them, where the emptied solids are removed by vibrating the bowl. It falls under the broader categories of performing operations, centrifugal apparatus, and centrifuges.
[B04B 3/08] discharging solid particles by bowl walls in the form of endless bands -- B04B 3/08 covers rotary-bowl centrifuges that remove solid particles by directing them against the bowl wall so they form continuous bands that slide out of the machine. The discharge method is used in devices that combine centrifugal separation with simultaneous sifting or filtering.
[B04B 5/00] Other centrifuges -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Centrifugal separators for fluid circulation systems. Planetary centrifuges. Centrifuges having radially arranged separation chambers with or without separate fluid receptacles, like centrifuges comprising test tubes, flexible receptacles, flat or spatial spiral chambers etc..  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Centrifugal cuvette type rotors -> G01N21/07
[B04B 5/005] Centrifugal separators or filters for fluid circulation systems, e.g. for lubricant oil circulation systems -- This CPC subclass B04B 5/005 covers centrifugal devices that function as separators or filters within fluid circulation loops, especially in lubricant-oil systems. It includes machines that use rotation to remove contaminants from the circulating fluid, helping maintain clean and efficient operation.
[B04B 5/02] Centrifuges consisting of a plurality of separate bowls rotating round an axis situated between the bowls -- B04B5/02 covers centrifuges built from several separate bowls that rotate about an axis located between the bowls. The multiple-bowl arrangement is used to improve throughput or separation efficiency in physical or chemical processing.
[B04B 5/04] Radial chamber apparatus for separating predominantly liquid mixtures, e.g. butyrometers -- B04B 5/04 classifies radial-chamber centrifuge devices that separate predominantly liquid mixtures, such as butyrometers, by introducing the feed into a rotating chamber where centrifugal forces create distinct zones for component separation. These apparatuses are a subset of other centrifuges used for physical or chemical processing.
[B04B 5/0407] for liquids contained in receptacles  B04B5/0442  takes precedence -- B04B 5/0407 designates centrifuges that operate on liquids remaining inside their own containers, where the receptacle is spun or mounted within the machine to effect separation. This subclass resides under other centrifuges in the CPC hierarchy for centrifugal devices used in physical or chemical processes, and it is overridden by the finer subcategory B04B5/0442.
[B04B 5/0414] comprising test tubes -- B04B 5/0414 refers to centrifuges that incorporate test tubes as the containers for the material to be processed, typically for small-scale or laboratory applications. It is classified under other centrifuges within the broader group of centrifugal apparatus used for physical or chemical processes.
[B04B 5/0421] pivotably mounted -- B04B 5/0421 designates centrifuges whose drum or rotor is pivotably mounted, allowing the spinning element to swing about a pivot for easier loading, unloading or cleaning. It is a sub-group of "other centrifuges" within the broader class of centrifugal apparatus used for physical or chemical processes.
[B04B 5/0428] with flexible receptacles -- The code refers to centrifuge machines whose rotating drum or basket holds a flexible receptacle such as a bag, pouch or film that can deform during operation, allowing easy loading, unloading and containment of liquids or solids. These devices are used in processes where a sealed, adaptable container is preferred to rigid drums.
[B04B 5/0442] with means for adding or withdrawing liquid substances during the centrifugation, e.g. continuous centrifugation -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Centrifuges for blood separation and blood washing including the 2-omega type (see e.g. US4221322) and rotary sealing type (see e.g. WO8000227) centrifuges.  Aspects of elutriation, tubings, interface detection, rotary sealings in connection with these centrifuges.
[B04B 5/06] Centrifugal counter-current apparatus -- Centrifugal counter-current apparatus are machines that separate components by spinning a mixture while the feed and the discharge streams move in opposite directions within the rotor. The design creates a continuous counter-flow that enhances separation of liquids, solids, or immiscible phases, and is commonly used in decanter and disc-stack centrifuges.
[B04B 5/08] Centrifuges for separating predominantly gaseous mixtures -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Centrifuges for separating different gases from each other, as well as separating solid or liquid particles from gases are classified in this group, see also B01D45/12 and B01D45/14 .
[B04B 5/10] Centrifuges combined with other apparatus, e.g. electrostatic separators -- This CPC subclass covers centrifuges that are integrated with additional equipment such as electrostatic separators, enabling simultaneous physical and electrostatic separation in a single unit. It falls under the "other centrifuges" group within the broader category of centrifugal apparatus for physical or chemical processes.
[B04B 5/12] Centrifuges in which rotors other than bowls generate centrifugal effects in stationary containers -- This subclass covers centrifuges whose rotating element is not a bowl-such as disc, drum or other rotor types-that create centrifugal forces acting on material held in a fixed container. The container remains stationary while the rotor spins, enabling separation processes where the centrifugal effect is generated by the rotor rather than the container itself.
[B04B 7/00] Elements -- **Definition:** This place covers:  E.g. details relating to chucks (see e.g. US3785549), gripping arms (see e.g. US3837633), sealings.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Rotary seals enclosing an aperture in a bowl body through which a feeding/discharging member is inserted (see e.g. WO8000227)and rotating seals carried on the processing chamber between ports in the chamber and the non-rotating portions of the system (see e.g. US4636193) -> B04B5/0442 - Seals in conjunction with bearings -> B04B9/12
[B04B 7/02] Casings -- This subclass defines the protective shells, liners or other enclosing structures that form the outer part of a centrifuge, surrounding the rotating elements and the material being processed. The casings are designed to contain the working space, withstand mechanical and chemical stresses, and provide sealing and safety for the centrifuge's operation.
[B04B 7/04] Casings facilitating discharge -- The B04B 7/04 subclass relates to centrifuge casings that incorporate features to aid the removal of processed material, such as discharge ports, removable liners, flanges or other mechanisms that facilitate emptying the vessel after operation. It covers structural elements designed specifically for efficient discharge.
[B04B 7/06] Safety devices -- **Definition:** This place covers:  E.g. door and lid locking devices, breaking point arrangements, leak detection systems  Safety measures in case of rotor fracturing
[B04B 7/08] Rotary bowls -- B04B 7/08 covers rotary bowls, the rotating containers or barrels used in centrifuges to generate centrifugal forces that separate liquid-solid or liquid-liquid mixtures. The classification includes designs where the bowl itself spins around a central axis, enabling continuous or batch processing in laboratory and industrial applications.
[B04B 7/085] fibre- or metal-reinforced -- The B04B 7/085 subclass pertains to elements of centrifuges-such as rotors, bowls, housings or other structural parts-that are reinforced with fibre or metal materials to enhance strength, rigidity, or resistance to wear during physical or chemical processing.
[B04B 7/10] Bowls for shaping solids -- The classification B04B 7/10 covers bowls that are incorporated into a centrifuge and are shaped to form solid material during operation, such as forming pellets or compacted shapes. These bowls are treated as elements of centrifugal apparatus used for physical or chemical processing.
[B04B 7/12] Inserts, e.g. armouring plates -- The code covers components placed inside centrifuge bowls or rotors to protect or strengthen them, such as protective plates or armour that line the interior. These inserts may be removable or fixed and are used to extend equipment life or adapt the machine to particular processing conditions.
[B04B 7/14] for separating walls of conical shape -- The subclass B04B 7/14 covers centrifuge designs that incorporate conical-shaped separating walls to improve the separation of materials during rotation. It falls under the broader group of centrifugal apparatus used for physical or chemical processing.
[B04B 7/16] Sieves or filters -- The entry B04B 7/16 covers sieves or filters that are incorporated as elements of a centrifuge, used to retain or separate solid particles from liquids during operation. It includes screens, meshes, or filter media positioned within the rotor or discharge area of the machine.
[B04B 7/18] formed or coated with sieving or filtering elements -- B04B 7/18 covers centrifuge components whose surfaces are formed or coated with sieving or filtering elements, such as perforated, mesh, or porous layers integrated into the rotor, bowl or container. This design enables simultaneous separation and retention of particles during the centrifugal process.
[B04B 9/00] Drives specially designed for centrifuges -- **Definition:** This place covers:  All kind of centrifuge drives, transmission gearings, control of the drives and the suspension and balancing of the rotary bowls.
[B04B 9/02] Electric motor drives -- B04B 9/02 covers electric motor drives specially designed for centrifuges, including the motor and associated speed-control, power-transmission and mounting arrangements that provide the rotational energy needed for centrifugation processes.
[B04B 9/04] Direct drive -- B04B 9/04 classifies centrifuges in which the motor is coupled directly to the rotor or drive shaft, eliminating intermediate transmissions such as belts, gears or pulleys. The direct drive arrangement provides efficient power transfer and a simpler mechanical design.
[B04B 9/06] Fluid drive -- Fluid drives for centrifuges use a fluid medium-typically hydraulic oil or compressed air-to transmit power from a motor to the rotor, enabling smooth torque delivery and precise speed control. These systems are specially engineered for centrifugal equipment, allowing rapid acceleration, variable speeds, and reduced mechanical vibration compared with direct mechanical couplings.
[B04B 9/08] Arrangement or disposition of transmission gearing -- The B04B 9/08 subclass covers how transmission gears are arranged or positioned within centrifuge drive systems. It includes the spatial layout, alignment and mounting of gear sets that transfer power from the motor to the rotating rotor, focusing on the mechanical design of the gearing assembly used in centrifugal apparatus.
[B04B 9/10] Control of the drive -- **Definition:** This place covers:  E.g. speed limiting arrangements
[B04B 9/12] Suspending rotary bowls -- **Definition:** This place covers:  E.g. sealing, damping, lubricating, cooling of bearings
[B04B 9/14] Balancing rotary bowls ; -- The subclass deals with techniques and devices for balancing the rotating bowls of centrifuges, ensuring even mass distribution to minimise vibration and improve performance. It includes mechanisms, adjustments and control systems specifically designed for bowl balancing.
[B04B 9/146] Imbalance detection devices -- The B04B 9/146 subclass covers devices that sense or monitor imbalance in rotating components of centrifuges, particularly within specially designed drives. Such equipment detects uneven mass distribution during operation and typically triggers alarms or corrective actions to protect the machine and ensure safe processing.
[B04C] APPARATUS USING FREE VORTEX FLOW, e.g. CYCLONES -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Apparatus for separating or like treating (e.g. drying, extracting, purifying) in which centrifugal or centripetal effects are generated by free vortex flow, otherwise than by rotary bowls, rotors or curved passages. The free vortex flow generated may follow a flat spiral, have an unchanged or constant axial direction, or have a reversible axial direction.  The mixtures treated are composed of the following physical states of matter:  Accessories for such devices e.g. safety or control devices.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Combinations of cyclones with filters, for separating particles from gases or vapours -> B01D50/00 - Combinations of cyclones with electrostatic precipitating arrangements -> B03C3/14 - Cyclonic type combustion apparatus using fluent fuel -> F23C3/00 - Vortex burners e.g. for cyclone-type combustion apparatus -> F23D1/00  **Glossary:** - Vortex finder: overflow duct, which is the discharging outlet for the lighter fluid phase.
[B04C 1/00] Apparatus in which the main direction of flow follows a flat spiral -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Flat cyclones or vortex chambers and cyclones where the inlet extends substantially over the whole height of the vortex chamber.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Arrangements with curved passages -> B01D45/16
[B04C 11/00] Accessories, e.g. safety or control devices, not otherwise provided for -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Control arrangements per se, regulators, valves in inlet and/or overflow ducting.
[B04C 2003/003] Shapes or dimensions of vortex chambers -- The subclass B04C 2003/003 relates to design features that define the shape or dimensional parameters of vortex chambers used in cyclonic separators, reactors and similar devices. It covers patents specifying geometric configurations such as conical, cylindrical or multi-stage chambers and the ratios of diameters, heights or inlet angles that influence vortex formation and particle-separation performance.
[B04C 2003/006] Construction of elements by which the vortex flow is generated or degenerated -- The B04C 2003/006 subclass covers the design and arrangement of components that create or disrupt a vortex flow, such as vanes, rings, or slots used in turbines, pumps, mixers and similar fluid-handling equipment. It includes structural features that intentionally generate swirling motion or diminish it to control fluid dynamics.
[B04C 2005/133] Adjustable vortex finder -- Adjustable vortex finder is a component of a cyclone separator whose throat or inlet can be shifted to change the position of the vortex core, enabling optimisation of particle-fluid separation under varying flow conditions. The device usually consists of a movable plate, sleeve or rod that can be positioned radially or axially to adjust the vortex flow path.
[B04C 2005/136] Baffles in the vortex finder -- B04C 2005/136 covers devices that add baffles inside the vortex finder of a cyclone separator or similar fluid-handling equipment. The baffles are intended to disrupt or straighten the swirling flow, reducing vortex strength and improving separation efficiency and discharge stability. This sub-class falls under the broader B04C group for centrifugal pumps and related apparatus.
[B04C 2009/001] with means for electrostatic separation -- B04C 2009/001 covers apparatuses for separating solid or liquid particles that include features for electrostatic separation, such as electrodes, charged plates or fields that impart opposite charges to particles to enable their collection or removal. It applies to devices where electrostatic forces are the primary means of distinguishing and extracting the target material from a mixture.
[B04C 2009/002] with external filters -- Covers pumps that include external filters to clean the fluid being moved, typically used in hydraulic or liquid handling systems. The classification applies to designs where the filtration element is located outside the pump housing rather than integrated internally.
[B04C 2009/004] with internal filters, in the cyclone chamber or in the vortex finder -- The B04C 2009/004 entry covers cyclone separators that incorporate internal filtering elements, either placed within the main cyclone chamber or inside the vortex finder, to enhance particle removal and improve separation efficiency.
[B04C 2009/005] with external rotors, e.g. impeller, ventilator, fan, blower, pump -- The CPC subclass B04C 2009/005 covers rotary machines that have an external rotor, meaning the rotating element is positioned outside a stationary housing. It includes devices such as impellers, ventilators, fans, blowers and pumps that operate on this external-rotor principle.
[B04C 2009/007] with internal rotors, e.g. impeller, ventilator, fan, blower, pump -- B04C covers rotary machines that generate flow with an internal rotor, such as an impeller, ventilator, fan, blower or pump. The subgroup 2009/007 specifically relates to devices where the rotor is situated inside the housing and imparts motion to the fluid, typically used for circulating air or liquids in industrial and domestic applications.
[B04C 2009/008] with injection or suction of gas or liquid into the cyclone -- This CPC subgroup covers cyclone separators in which gas or liquid is deliberately injected or drawn out during operation, a technique used to modify the flow pattern, enhance collection efficiency, or recover specific phases of the material being processed.
[B04C 3/00] Apparatus in which the axial direction of the vortex remains unchanged -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Cyclones where the direction of flow does not change, the fluid enters the cyclone on an upstream side and the separated phases are discharged on the downstream side.
[B04C 3/02] with heating or cooling, e.g. quenching, means -- B04C 3/02 covers centrifugal apparatus that uses a free vortex flow in which the axis of the vortex remains constant and includes integrated heating or cooling, such as quenching, to control the temperature of the material during processing.
[B04C 3/04] Multiple arrangement thereof -- B04C 3/04 covers centrifugal apparatus that uses a free vortex flow such as cyclones where the axial direction of the vortex stays unchanged, and specifically the provision of two or more such vortex-generating units arranged together within a single machine or system to increase capacity or improve separation.
[B04C 3/06] Construction of inlets or outlets to the vortex chamber -- B04C 3/06 covers the design and arrangement of inlet and outlet passages for vortex chambers such as cyclones, focusing on how the flow enters and leaves the rotating vortex without altering its axial direction. It includes features of the feed nozzle, discharge ports, and any guiding or diffusing elements that affect pressure drop, separation efficiency, or flow stability.
[B04C 5/00] Apparatus in which the axial direction of the vortex is reversed -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Cyclones where the axial direction of the lighter fluid is reversed. The direction of flow does change, the fluid enters the cyclone on an upstream side and the separated heavier fluid is discharged on the downstream side, the lighter fluid travels back to the upstream side of the cyclone.
[B04C 5/02] Construction of inlets by which the vortex flow is generated -- B04C 5/02 covers the construction of inlets that generate a vortex flow, typically used in centrifugal apparatus such as cyclones where the axial direction of the vortex is reversed.
[B04C 5/04] Tangential inlets -- Tangential inlets are inlet arrangements for centrifugal devices that use free-vortex flow, such as cyclones, where the feed enters the chamber at a point tangent to the circular cross-section. This configuration imparts a swirling motion to the fluid, establishing a vortex that may have its axial direction reversed for improved separation or mixing. The design is applied in machines that carry out physical or chemical processes using centrifugal forces.
[B04C 5/06] Axial inlets -- B04C 5/06 covers apparatus that uses free vortex flow with the axial direction of the vortex reversed, such as cyclones that have an axial inlet feeding the fluid directly into the vortex core.
[B04C 5/08] Vortex chamber constructions -- B04C 5/08 covers vortex chamber constructions where the vortex flow direction along the axis is opposite to that of conventional designs, such as reversed-flow cyclones used for separation or mixing. These devices employ a free vortex flow and are classified under centrifugal apparatus for physical or chemical processes.
[B04C 5/081] Shapes or dimensions -- The CPC subclass B04C 5/081 relates to the geometric shape and dimensional features of cyclone-type centrifugal devices in which the axial direction of the free vortex flow is reversed. It covers design variations such as the profile of the inlet, vortex chamber, and outlet sections that affect flow behavior and separation efficiency.
[B04C 5/085] with wear-resisting arrangements -- B04C 5/085 covers vortex-flow apparatus such as cyclones that reverse the axial direction of the vortex and incorporate wear-resisting features to protect internal components from abrasive wear.
[B04C 5/087] with flexible gas-tight walls -- B04C 5/087 covers cyclone-type centrifugal apparatus whose vortex flows opposite to the usual axial direction and which incorporates flexible, gas-tight walls to contain the process stream. The flexible liners allow the equipment to accommodate pressure variations while maintaining a sealed environment during separation or reaction operations.
[B04C 5/10] with perforated walls -- B04C 5/10 covers cyclone-type apparatus in which the vortex rotates opposite to the usual axial direction and the separating chamber walls are perforated, allowing feed or product streams to pass through the wall openings for improved separation efficiency.
[B04C 5/103] Bodies or members, e.g. bulkheads, guides, in the vortex chamber -- B04C 5/103 pertains to structural elements situated within a vortex chamber of a centrifugal device, such as bulkheads, guides, or similar members that shape, support or direct the rotating flow. These components are used in apparatuses where the vortex's axial direction is reversed to enhance separation or mixing processes.
[B04C 5/107] Cores -- B04C 5/107 covers the core component of vortex-flow machines whose axial vortex direction is reversed. The core is the central element placed within the cylindrical chamber, shaping the flow and providing structural support for the reversed vortex, often used in cyclonic separators and similar apparatus.
[B04C 5/12] Construction of the overflow ducting, e.g. diffusing or spiral exits -- B04C 5/12 concerns the design of overflow ducts in vortex-flow apparatus such as cyclones, covering diffusing or spiral exit configurations that manage the outflow of the swirling gas or liquid stream, often with reversed axial vortex direction.
[B04C 5/13] formed as a vortex finder and extending into the vortex chamber -- B04C 5/13 covers centrifugal devices such as cyclones that use a vortex finder formed and extending into the vortex chamber to reverse the axial direction of the vortex flow, thereby altering the vortex path for improved separation or processing efficiency.
[B04C 5/14] Construction of the underflow ducting -- B04C 5/14 relates to the design and construction of the underflow ducting in cyclonic separators that reverse the axial vortex direction, detailing the structural features that guide the separated material out through the bottom outlet.
[B04C 5/15] with swinging flaps or revolving sluices -- Apparatus that creates a free-vortex flow, such as a cyclone whose vortex axis runs opposite to the feed direction, and that uses movable elements like swinging flaps or rotating sluices to periodically open and close the flow path, thereby controlling particle separation or discharge.
[B04C 5/16] with variable-size outlets from the underflow ducting -- The B04C 5/16 subclass refers to centrifugal apparatus that employ a free vortex flow, such as cyclones, where the axial direction of the vortex is reversed and the underflow duct includes outlets whose size can be varied to adjust the discharge characteristics.
[B04C 5/18] with auxiliary fluid assisting discharge -- B04C 5/18 covers centrifugal devices that employ a free-vortex flow, such as cyclones, in which the vortex direction is reversed and an auxiliary fluid is introduced to help discharge the separated material. The auxiliary stream assists the removal of particles or liquid from the vortex core, improving separation efficiency.
[B04C 5/181] Bulkheads or central bodies in the discharge opening -- This subclass concerns vortex-flow devices such as cyclones that incorporate a bulkhead or central body positioned in the discharge opening. The internal element alters the flow pattern at the outlet, aiding particle separation, pressure control or wear reduction.
[B04C 5/185] Dust collectors -- These devices collect dust and other particulate matter by generating a free vortex whose axial flow is reversed, causing particles to migrate outward and be trapped while the cleaned gas exits upward. The arrangement is typical of cyclone dust collectors.
[B04C 5/187] forming an integral part of the vortex chamber -- This subcategory covers designs where a component is manufactured as a single piece within the vortex chamber of a cyclone or similar free-vortex apparatus. It includes methods of molding, casting, or machining the chamber wall and internal features so that they form a continuous, integral structure rather than being assembled from separate parts. The aim is to improve durability and flow efficiency.
[B04C 5/20] with heating or cooling, e.g. quenching, means -- The B04C 5/20 subclass covers vortex-flow apparatus in which the axial direction of the vortex is reversed and that incorporate heating, cooling or quenching means. It applies to cyclones and similar machines where temperature control is integrated into the separation process.
[B04C 5/22] with cleaning means -- This CPC subclass B04C 5/22 covers vortex-reversing cyclone apparatus that includes cleaning means. The cleaning features may be internal scrapers, water-spray systems, or removable liners designed to dislodge and remove the separated material while the device continues to operate.
[B04C 5/23] using liquids -- Subclass B04C 5/23 covers apparatus that creates a free vortex flow-such as cyclones-in which the axial direction of the vortex is reversed and the medium being processed is a liquid. It includes devices that use this reversed vortex to separate, mix, or otherwise treat liquids.
[B04C 5/24] Multiple arrangement thereof -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Combinations of reverse flow cyclones of series flow and parallel flow where the features of the interactive connection are important.
[B04C 5/26] for series flow -- B04C 5/26 covers cyclone-type apparatus that creates a free vortex whose axial direction is reversed to handle a series flow, allowing the incoming stream to be split into successive axial sections within the same vessel. This design improves separation efficiency by repeatedly reorienting the vortex as the material progresses through the device.
[B04C 5/28] for parallel flow -- B04C 5/28 refers to cyclone-type apparatus that creates a free vortex whose axial direction is opposite the normal flow, and where the inlet and outlet streams run parallel to each other. It is used for separating particles or liquids by centrifugal forces in a reversed-vortex configuration. This subclass falls under centrifugal machines for physical or chemical processes.
[B04C 5/30] Recirculation constructions in or with cyclones which accomplish a partial recirculation of the medium, e.g. by means of conduits -- B04C 5/30 covers cyclonic apparatus that incorporates recirculation structures to return part of the processed medium back into the vortex, typically by means of internal conduits or similar pathways, enabling partial reuse of the flow within devices that employ a free-vortex, reversed-axial flow pattern.
[B04C 7/00] Apparatus not provided for in group -- **Definition:** This place covers:  All cases where the combination of different types of cyclones like a through flow type cyclone ( B04C3/00 and sub-groups) and a reverse flow type cyclone ( B04C5/00 and sub-groups) is claimed. Details relating to the one or the other type are classified in the relevant groups of the specific cyclone.
[B04C 9/00] Combinations with other devices, e.g. fans, -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Combinations of cyclones with expansion chambers, diffusors, water locks or sieves.  Combinations of cyclones with internally or externally arranged rotors like fans, ventilators, blowers, impellers, pumps.  Combinations of cyclones with internally or externally arranged filters.  Combinations of cyclones with electrostatic separation equipment.  Cyclones with injection or suction of liquid or gas into the cyclone chamber.
[B05] SPRAYING OR ATOMISING IN GENERAL -- B05 covers technologies for creating fine dispersions of liquids, powders or gases by spraying or atomising, including the methods, devices and systems used to generate controlled sprays, mists or aerosols for applications such as coating, cleaning, cooling, combustion and agricultural treatment. It encompasses both the mechanical principles of atomisation and the practical arrangements for delivering the dispersed material.
[B05B] SPRAYING APPARATUS -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Apparatus for the release or projection of drops or droplets into the atmosphere or into a chamber to form a mist or the like. The materials to be projected may be suspended in a stream of gas or vapour.  Apparatus as above, for the release or projection of streams or sprays of other fluent materials, e.g. particulate material entrained in a gas, into the atmosphere or into a chamber.  Nozzles or guns for the release of gas only, e.g. air or blowing guns or nozzles.  Projection of droplets of coating material out of numerous closely spaced nozzles by using an inkjet technology to form plain, i.e. not patterned, coating.  Examples of such apparatus, and subcomponents thereof covered by this subclass, include:  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Spray-mixers with nozzles -> B01F25/72 - Processes for applying liquids or other fluent material to surfaces by spraying -> B05D  **Glossary:** - coating: the applied material. A coating may be a solidified layer originally applied as a liquid (e.g. dried paint) or a layer of material which, once applied, remains in a liquid or semi-liquid state (e.g. lubricant). - cock: a valve as defined in group F16K5/00 , i.e. a cut-off apparatus having at least one of the sealing faces shaped as a more or less complete surface of a solid of revolution, the opening and closing movement being predominantly rotary. The Figure illustrates body, plug, port and stem: - gate valve or sliding valve: a valve as defined in group F16K3/00 , i.e. a cut-off apparatus with closure members having a sliding movement along the seat for opening and closing. The Figure illustrates a closed valve position and an opened valve position with body, gate, bonnet, packing gland, yoke, handwheel and stem: - lift valve: a valve as defined in group F16K1/00 , i.e. a cut-off apparatus with closure members having at least a component of their opening and closing motion perpendicular to the closing faces. The Figure illustrates body, inlet, outlet, seat, plug (disk) and bonnet: - liquid or fluent: designates materials that can flow, e.g. liquids, including solutions, dispersions, suspensions, semi-liquids, pastes, melts or particulate materials - particulate materials: solid materials in the form of very small pieces, e.g. powders, granules, short fibres or chips
[B05B 1/00] Nozzles, spray heads or other outlets, with or without auxiliary devices such as valves, heating means -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Spraying or sprinkling apparatus with moving outlet elements or moving deflecting elements -> B05B3/00 - Electrostatic spraying apparatus; Spraying apparatus with means for charging the spray electrically; Apparatus for spraying liquids or other fluent materials by other electric means -> B05B5/00 - Spraying apparatus for discharge of liquids or other fluent materials from two or more sources, e.g. of liquid and air, of powder and gas -> B05B7/00 - Devices for applying liquids or other fluent materials to surfaces by contact -> B05C - Nozzles for ink-jet printing mechanisms -> B41J2/135 - Nozzles for liquid-dispensing, e.g. in vehicle service stations -> B67D7/42

[B05B 1/005] Nozzles or other outlets specially adapted for discharging one or more gases -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative examples of subject matter classified in this place:  1. [B05B 1/02] designed to produce a jet, spray, or other discharge of particular shape or nature, e.g. in single drops, -- It covers spray or jet devices whose nozzles are shaped or configured to generate a specific discharge pattern, such as single droplets, defined streams, or particular spray geometries. These designs may include specialized outlet geometries, internal flow paths or auxiliary elements to achieve the desired shape.
[B05B 1/04] in flat form, e.g. fan-like, sheet-like -- A B05B 1/04 spray-apparatus feature is a flat, fan- or sheet-like nozzle or outlet that produces a broad, planar spray. It includes flat-form spray heads, diaphragms or plates that emit a thin sheet of liquid or mist, often with auxiliary devices such as valves or heaters. This design is used where a wide, uniform coverage area is required.
[B05B 1/042] Outlets having two planes of symmetry perpendicular to each other, one of them defining the plane of the jet  B05B1/044 ,  B05B1/046  take precedence -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this place:  1a.  1b. [B05B 1/044] Slits, e.g. narrow openings defined by two straight and parallel lips; Elongated outlets for producing very wide discharges, e.g. fluid curtains  B05B1/046  takes precedence -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this place:  The Figure illustrates two straight and parallel lips defining an elongated slotted outlet (536).  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Outlets formed, e.g. cut, in the circumference of tubular or spherical elements -> B05B1/046
[B05B 1/046] Outlets formed, e.g. cut, in the circumference of tubular or spherical elements -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this place:  The Figure illustrates an outlet (12) that is formed by a cut in the circumference of a spherical element (8).
[B05B 1/048] having a flow conduit with, immediately behind the outlet orifice, an elongated cross section, e.g. of oval or elliptic form, of which the major axis is perpendicular to the plane of the jet -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this place:  The Figure illustrates a conduit (20) having an ellipse shaped cross-section, wherein the major axis of the conduit (20) is perpendicular to that of the plane of the jet.
[B05B 1/06] in annular, tubular or hollow conical form -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative examples of subject matter classified in this place:  1.  Figure 1 illustrates a spray jet (102) being shaped as a hollow conical form, which includes a dry zone line above and a typical spray zone line below, both divided into a power zone (converged toward the vertical line 113) nearest to the spray jet (101, 103 on the left) and a coverage zone (diverged toward the right side) beyond the power zone.  2.
