[B05B 1/08] of pulsating nature, e.g. delivering liquid in successive separate quantities -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this place:  The Figure illustrates fluidic oscillators.
[B05B 1/083] the pulsating mechanism comprising movable parts -- B05B 1/083 classifies spray-apparatus components in which the pulsating action is produced by movable parts, such as reciprocating or oscillating elements that modulate the flow of fluid through nozzles, spray heads or other outlets, optionally combined with valves, heating or other auxiliary devices.
[B05B 1/085] rotated by the liquid or other fluent material discharged, e.g. liquid rotated turbines -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this place:  The Figure illustrates a liquid rotated turbine (2) being rotated by the fluid being discharged, thus producing a pulsating discharge.
[B05B 1/0852] the liquid or other fluent material actuating several pulsating mechanisms -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this place:  1a.  1b.  Figure 1a illustrates a shower head (15) comprising three cup-shaped discharge caps (160). Figure 1b illustrates a cap (160) comprising a turbine (128) creating a pulsating discharge.
[B05B 1/0854] the pulsating mechanism being provided with means for preventing rotation -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this place:  1a.  1b.  Figure 1a illustrates a shower head (15) comprising three cup-shaped discharge caps (160). Figure 1b illustrates each cap (160) comprising a turbine (128) creating a pulsating discharge. Rotation of the turbine (128) can be prevented by a stop (197).
[B05B 1/0856] the pulsating mechanism comprising movable balls -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this place:  The Figure illustrates how water flowing through openings (70) rotates balls (56) around annular channels (54) to successively cover and uncover openings (64), thereby causing the water to exit from these openings in the form of pulsating jets.
[B05B 1/086] with a resiliently deformable element, e.g. sleeve -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this place:  1a.  1b.  Figure 1a illustrates the expansion of a tube (24) enabling discharge through a channel (38). As the substance rushes out through the channel (38), the tube (24) collapses and returns to a configuration similar to that shown in Figure 1b. As this occurs, the seal between the tube (24) and an O-ring (44) once again becomes effective in Figure 1b, and the cycle will repeat, thus creating a pulsating discharge.
[B05B 1/10] in the form of a fine jet, e.g. for use in wind-screen washers -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative examples of subject matter classified in this place:  1a.  Figure 1a illustrates nozzles forming an array of fine jets.  1b.  Figure 1b illustrates a nozzle array positioned onto a car windshield.  2.  Figure 2 illustrates a nozzle (14) forming a single fine jet (20).
[B05B 11/00] Single-unit hand-held apparatus in which flow of contents is produced by the muscular force of the operator at the moment of use -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Single-unit hand-held apparatus comprising a container and a dispensing nozzle attached thereto, in which flow of contents is produced by the muscular force of the operator at the moment of use.  **Glossary:** - Content: liquid or other fluid material. - Pump: A device comprising a chamber into which the material is first sucked from the container through an inlet, then delivered out of the chamber, e.g. sprayed, through an outlet. Inlet and outlet can be equipped with valves. - Lift valve: A valve as defined in main group F16K1/00 , i.e. a cut-off device with closure members having at least a component of their opening and closing motion perpendicular to the closing faces. - Gate valve or sliding valve: A valve as defined in main group F16K3/00 , i.e. cut-off device with closing members having a sliding movement along the seat for opening and closing.
[B05B 11/0002] incorporating means for heating or cooling, e.g. the material to be sprayed -- A spray device classified under B05B 11/0002 is a single-unit hand-held apparatus whose flow is generated by the operator's muscular effort, and it includes elements that heat or cool the material being sprayed, such as built-in heaters, coolers or temperature-control components integrated into the spray system.
[B05B 11/0005] Components or details  of single units wherein the flow is effected by a pump  B05B11/1042 -- This CPC subclass covers the components or detailed features of single-unit, hand-held spraying devices whose flow of material is generated by a pump operated by the user, such as pump mechanisms, valves, and related parts that control discharge.
[B05B 11/0008] Sealing or attachment arrangements between sprayer and container  between pump and container  B05B11/1043 -- This classification entry, B05B 11/0008, pertains to the design of sealing or attachment mechanisms in single-unit hand-held spraying apparatus where the operator's muscular force generates the flow. It specifically addresses connections between the sprayer and container, as well as between the pump and container.
[B05B 11/001] Snap-on-twist-off type connections -- The B05B 11/001 subclass covers single-unit handheld spraying devices that rely on the operator's muscular force to discharge the product, and that use snap-on twist-off type connections for attaching or sealing the spray head or nozzle. This applies to portable, manually-actuated spray apparatus.
[B05B 11/0013] Attachment arrangements comprising means cooperating with the inner surface of the container -- B05B 11/0013 relates to attachment arrangements for hand-held spray devices in which the flow is generated by the operator's muscle force. The subclass covers means that cooperate with the inner surface of the container, such as internal guides, seals or valves, to control or enhance the ejection of the product during use.
[B05B 11/0027] Means for neutralising the actuation of the sprayer  pump locking means  B05B11/1059 ; Means for preventing access to the sprayer actuation means -- The classification code B05B 11/0027 pertains to single-unit hand-held spraying apparatus where the flow is generated by the operator's muscular force, specifically addressing mechanisms that neutralize or lock the pump actuation and prevent unauthorized access to the actuation means.
[B05B 11/0029] Valves not actuated by pressure  B05B11/0032 ,  B05B11/0094  take precedence; automatically opened during actuation of a spray pump  B05B11/1053 -- This CPC subclass covers single-unit hand-held spray devices where the valve is opened by the operator's muscular force rather than by pressure, and where the valve is not actuated by pressure. It includes valves that automatically open during actuation of a spray pump, with precedence given to sub-subclasses B05B11/0032, B05B11/0094 and B05B11/1053.
[B05B 11/0032] Manually actuated means located downstream the discharge nozzle for closing or covering it, e.g. shutters  automatically removed during actuation of a spray pump  B05B11/1053 -- It covers manually operated components placed downstream of the discharge nozzle in single-unit hand-held spray devices, used to close or cover the nozzle, such as shutters that open automatically when the pump is actuated.
[B05B 11/0035] Pen-like sprayers -- B05B 11/0035 pen-like sprayers are single-unit hand-held devices that release a flow of contents by the operator's muscular force at the moment of use. They belong to the spraying or atomising group and require no separate pump or power source.
[B05B 11/0037] Containers  for several components  B05B11/0078 -- This classification entry, B05B 11/0037, pertains to single-unit hand-held spraying apparatus where the flow is generated by the operator's muscular force at the moment of use, specifically addressing containers designed for holding several components as detailed in B05B11/0078.
[B05B 11/0038] Inner container disposed in an outer shell or outer casing -- The code B05B 11/0038 designates a hand-held spraying device in which the material is held in an inner container that is placed inside an outer shell or casing. The inner container is activated by the operator's muscular force, releasing the contents as a spray without a separate pump.
[B05B 11/0039] associated with means for compensating the pressure difference between the ambient pressure and the pressure inside the container, e.g. pressure relief means -- Class B05B 11/0039 covers hand-held spray devices that include features to balance the pressure difference between the surrounding air and the container, such as pressure-relief or venting mechanisms. These means prevent excessive build-up or drop in pressure during operation, ensuring consistent flow when the user actuates the nozzle by muscular force.
[B05B 11/0041] compensating underpressure without contact of the fluid remaining in the container with the atmospheric air  B05B11/026  and  B05B11/028  take precedence -- This CPC subclass B05B 11/0041 covers single-unit hand-held spray devices that rely on the operator's muscular effort to discharge the product and that incorporate a method for compensating the under-pressure inside the container without letting the remaining fluid contact atmospheric air. The more specific subclasses B05B 11/026 and B05B 11/028 have priority over this entry.
[B05B 11/00411] the means being an inert gas -- A single-unit, hand-held sprayer that relies on the operator's muscular force to discharge its contents, classified under spraying or atomising in general. The entry specifies that the dispensing medium is supplied by an inert gas.
[B05B 11/0044] compensating underpressure by ingress of atmospheric air into the container, i.e. with venting means  venting means for deformable containers  B05B11/047 -- The entry B05B 11/0044, within the Cooperative Patent Classification for spraying or atomising, covers single-unit hand-held spray devices that rely on the operator's muscular force and include venting means to let atmospheric air enter the container, compensating the underpressure created during use. It specifically addresses deformable containers equipped with such venting features.
[B05B 11/00442] the means being actuated by the difference between the atmospheric pressure and the pressure inside the container -- B05B 11/00442 covers hand-held spray devices where the operator's muscle force creates the flow, and the dispensing mechanism is triggered by the pressure difference between ambient air and the container's interior. It specifies the actuating means that exploit this pressure differential to release the product.
[B05B 11/00444] with provision for filtering or cleaning the air flow drawn into the container -- This subclass covers handheld spray devices that are powered solely by the operator's muscle force and include a mechanism to filter or clean the air drawn into the container before atomisation. It falls under B05B 11/00444 within the CPC hierarchy for spraying apparatus.
[B05B 11/00446] the means being located at the bottom of the container or of an enclosure surrounding the container -- This subgroup pertains to single-unit hand-held spray devices powered by the user's muscle effort, specifically the features positioned at the bottom of the product container or its surrounding enclosure that initiate or control the discharge of the material. It falls under the broader classes of performing operations, spraying or atomising in general, and spraying apparatus.
[B05B 11/0054] Cartridges, i.e. containers specially designed for easy attachment to or easy removal from the rest of the sprayer  attachment arrangements between pump and container  B05B11/1043 -- This CPC subclass covers cartridges, i.e. containers engineered for quick and easy attachment to or detachment from hand-held spray devices that are operated by the user's muscular force. It includes the arrangements that connect the pump to the container and related coupling mechanisms.
[B05B 11/0056] with an additional opening for filling or refilling -- B05B 11/0056 covers single-unit hand-held spraying devices that rely on the operator's muscular force to produce flow and include an additional opening for filling or refilling the container.
[B05B 11/0059] allowing operation in any orientation, e.g. for discharge in inverted position -- The CPC subclass B05B 11/0059 covers single-unit hand-held spraying devices powered by the operator's muscle that can function in any orientation, including upside-down discharge, allowing the spray to be emitted regardless of how the apparatus is held.
[B05B 11/0062] Outlet valves actuated by the pressure of the fluid to be sprayed  fluid-actuated pump outlet valve arrangements  B05B11/1016 ,  B05B11/1022 ,  B05B11/1033 ,  B05B11/1036 ,  B05B11/104 ,  B05B11/1064 ,  B05B11/1097 -- B05B 11/0062 covers hand-held spray devices whose outlet valve is opened by the pressure of the fluid being sprayed, using fluid-actuated pump valve arrangements such as those listed in sub-groups 1016, 1022, 1033, 1036, 104, 1064 and 1097. These valves rely on the fluid's own pressure to control flow, eliminating the need for external actuation.
[B05B 11/0064] Lift valves  B05B11/007  takes precedence -- B05B 11/0064 covers single-unit hand-held spraying devices that rely on the operator's muscular force to generate flow, such as pump-action or squeeze bottles. The design includes a lift valve mechanism, but the more specific class B05B 11/007 on lift valves takes precedence. This falls under the broader categories of performing operations, spraying or atomising in general, and spray apparatus.
[B05B 11/0067] having a valve seat located downstream the valve element -- This CPC subclass covers single-unit hand-held spray devices in which the valve seat is positioned downstream of the valve element, so the flow is controlled after the valve passes the seat. It applies to manually operated dispensers that rely on the operator's muscular force to create the spray.
[B05B 11/007] being opened by deformation of a sealing element made of resiliently deformable material, e.g. flaps, skirts, duck-bill valves -- A single-unit hand-held spray device that releases its contents by the operator's muscle force and is opened through deformation of a resilient sealing element such as a flap, skirt or duck-bill valve, classified under B05B 11/007. This entry belongs to the spraying apparatus subgroup within the broader categories of spraying or atomising in general and performing operations.
[B05B 11/0072] A valve member forming part of an outlet opening -- The subclass B05B 11/0072 covers valve components that make up or form the outlet opening of single-unit hand-held spray devices, where the operator's muscular force generates the flow of material. It pertains to the part of the apparatus that controls the discharge of the sprayed or atomised content.
[B05B 11/0075] Two outlet valves being placed in a delivery conduit, one downstream the other -- A subclass of hand-held spray devices that rely on the operator's muscle force, where the delivery conduit contains two outlet valves arranged in series, one downstream of the other, to control the flow of the sprayed material. This arrangement is used in single-unit atomisers within the broader spray-apparatus group.
[B05B 11/0078] Arrangements for separately storing several components  arrangements for pumping several liquids or other fluent materials from several containers  B05B11/1081 -- The code B05B 11/0078 covers hand-held spray devices that rely on the operator's muscular effort to create flow, where the formulation is divided into two or more separate containers and the apparatus includes mechanisms for storing each component individually and pumping them together at the point of use. It pertains to multi-component arrangements within single-unit spray units.
[B05B 11/0081] and for mixing the components in a common container as a mixture ready for use before discharging the latter -- A single-unit hand-held spray apparatus that generates flow by the operator's muscular effort at the moment of use and is designed to mix the components in a single container into a ready-to-use mixture before discharge.
[B05B 11/0083] one of the components being in powder form -- It designates a single-unit hand-held spray device in which the operator's muscular force creates the flow, and where at least one of the sprayed components is supplied as a powder that is combined with a liquid at the point of use.
[B05B 11/0086] Arrangements for allowing spraying and pouring -- The entry covers designs that let a single-hand-held spray device, operated by the user's muscle power, switch between or simultaneously provide a spray discharge and a pour flow, typically through specialised caps, nozzle arrangements or valve mechanisms that control the direction and shape of the expelled material.
[B05B 11/0089] Dispensing tubes -- Dispensing tubes are single-unit hand-held spray devices that deliver their contents by the operator's muscular force at the moment of use, without the need for external pressurisation or batteries. They belong to the CPC subclass for spraying apparatus.
[B05B 11/0091] movable, e.g. articulated on the sprayer -- The code B05B 11/0091 designates hand-held spray devices whose nozzle or spray head can be moved or articulated relative to the main body, enabling the operator to aim the spray. Such devices are powered by the user's muscular force at the moment of use.
[B05B 11/0094] movement of the dispensing tube controlling a valve -- This subclass concerns hand-held spray devices in which the operator's muscular effort pushes the dispensing tube, and the tube's movement directly operates a valve that controls product release. It applies to units where flow is generated at the moment of use by the tube's motion.
[B05B 11/0097] Means for filling or refilling the sprayer  through additional openings in the container  B05B11/0056 -- This classification entry pertains to hand-held spraying apparatus where the operator's muscular force generates the flow of contents, specifically addressing mechanisms for filling or refilling the sprayer via additional openings in the container.
[B05B 11/01] characterised by the means producing the flow -- A manually operated, single-unit hand-held sprayer that relies on the operator's muscular force to generate the discharge of its contents, with the flow-producing mechanism defining the classification. This covers devices such as squeeze bottles or pump sprays where the user creates the spray at the moment of use.
[B05B 11/02] Membranes or pistons acting on the contents inside the container, e.g. follower pistons -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Membranes or pistons which are in contact with the contents inside the container.  **Glossary:** - Follower: an element, e.g. piston, that is in contact with the material to be dispensed throughout the dispensing process.
[B05B 11/025] with stepwise advancement of the piston, e.g. for spraying a predetermined quantity of content -- This subclass covers hand-held spray devices where the piston moves in discrete steps, allowing a set amount of liquid or other material to be discharged each actuation. The stepwise piston advancement is typically achieved mechanically to deliver a predetermined quantity per spray.
[B05B 11/026] Membranes separating the content remaining in the container from the atmospheric air to compensate underpressure inside the container -- B05B 11/026 covers hand-held spray devices that incorporate a membrane separating the product remaining in the container from outside air, allowing air to flow in to equalize the pressure drop created during discharge. The membrane prevents contaminant ingress while maintaining the suction needed for the operator-driven spray.
[B05B 11/027] inverted during outflow of content -- The classification code B05B 11/027 pertains to single-unit hand-held spraying apparatus where the flow is generated by the operator's muscular force, specifically designed for use in an inverted position to ensure the outflow of contents.
[B05B 11/028] Pistons separating the content remaining in the container from the atmospheric air to compensate underpressure inside the container -- The code B05B 11/028 relates to pistons built into single-hand spray devices that separate the remaining product from atmospheric air, forming a compensating chamber that equalises the pressure drop as the contents are expelled. This arrangement keeps the flow consistent while preventing external air from entering the container.
[B05B 11/029] located on top of the remaining content -- B05B 11/029 covers single-unit hand-held spraying devices where the operator's muscular force drives the flow at the moment of use, with the actuator positioned on top of the remaining material.
[B05B 11/04] Deformable containers producing the flow, e.g. squeeze bottles -- A single-unit hand-held spray device that relies on the operator's muscular force to deform a flexible container-such as a squeeze bottle-and push the contents through a nozzle, creating a spray or atomised flow. This type of apparatus falls under spraying or atomising in general, specifically the class of spray apparatus that uses deformable containers to generate the discharge.
[B05B 11/041] designed for spraying particulate material  B05B11/045  takes precedence -- This CPC class covers hand-held spray devices that rely on the operator's muscle power to expel a particulate payload, such as powders or granules, at the moment of use; it is a subcategory of spray apparatus and is superseded by B05B11/045.
[B05B 11/042] the spray being effected by a gas or vapour flow in the nozzle, spray head, outlet or dip tube -- B05B 11/042 covers hand-held spray devices where the discharge is produced by a gas or vapour stream that passes through the nozzle, spray head, outlet or dip tube, assisting the liquid atomisation while the operator provides the mechanical force. The classification applies to single-unit apparatus that rely on muscular effort at the moment of use.
[B05B 11/043] designed for spraying a liquid  B05B11/046  takes precedence -- Classification B05B 11/043 refers to single-unit hand-held spray devices where the operator's muscular effort directly forces the liquid out at the moment of use. These manual apparatuses belong to the broader group of spraying or atomising equipment and are overridden by the more specific subclass B05B 11/046 for liquid-spraying designs.
[B05B 11/045] designed for spraying particulate material  B05B11/046  takes precedence -- B05B 11/045 covers single-unit hand-held spraying devices in which the operator's muscular force generates the flow of material at the moment of use, and the apparatus is intended for spraying particulate material. The more specific subclass B05B 11/046 supersedes this entry.
[B05B 11/046] the gas or vapour flow coming from a source where the gas or vapour is not in contact with the liquid or other fluent material to be sprayed, e.g. from a compressive bulb, an air pump or an enclosure surrounding the container -- B05B 11/046 relates to hand-held spray devices in which the propelling gas or vapour that pushes the liquid out never contacts the liquid, for example when a compressive bulb, an air pump or an enclosure surrounding the container supplies the driving stream. The operation relies on the operator's muscular force at the moment of use and falls under the spraying apparatus subgroup of performing-operations.
[B05B 11/047] characterised by the outlet or venting means  B05B11/041  and  B05B11/042  take precedence -- This subclass covers single-unit hand-held spray devices whose flow is generated by the muscular force of the operator and whose outlet or venting means provide the distinguishing feature, subject to the earlier definitions of B05B11/041 and B05B11/042.
[B05B 11/048] characterised by the container, e.g. this latter being surrounded by an enclosure, or the means for deforming it  B05B11/041 ,  B05B11/046  take precedence -- The code B05B 11/048 covers hand-held spray devices whose operation is defined by the design of the container, such as a container enclosed within a housing or equipped with a mechanism that deforms the container to release its contents. It refines earlier subclasses B05B 11/041 and B05B 11/046, which must be considered first when classifying such apparatuses.
[B05B 11/06] Gas or vapour producing the flow, e.g. from a compressible bulb or air pump -- This subclass covers single-unit hand-held spray devices whose discharge is driven by gas or vapour generated from a compressible bulb, air pump or similar source, providing the propelling force for the flow of the material.
[B05B 11/061] characterised by the means producing the gas or vapour pressure -- A single-hand-held sprayer in which the discharge pressure is created by a gas- or vapour-producing mechanism, such as a compressed-gas cartridge or a volatile propellant, rather than by the operator's manual pumping. The device relies on this internal pressurising means to atomise the contents when the trigger is actuated.
[B05B 11/062] designed for spraying particulate material -- This subclass covers single-unit hand-held spray devices that rely on the operator's muscle power to discharge material, specifically when the device is intended to atomize or spray particulate substances. It falls under the broader categories of performing operations, spraying or atomising in general, and spray apparatus.
[B05B 11/064] the particulate material being stored in several discrete quantities delivered one at a time -- B05B 11/064 classifies a single-unit hand-held spraying device in which the material to be atomised is kept in a series of separate containers or compartments and each dose is released individually by the operator's muscle force.
[B05B 11/065] the particulate material being separated from a main storage in discrete quantities delivered one at a time -- This subclass deals with hand-held spray devices that release the material from a main reservoir in separate doses, each dose being delivered individually when the operator applies muscular force. It covers apparatus in which particulate material is separated from its storage in discrete quantities and emitted one at a time.
[B05B 11/067] the particulate material being separated from the main storage by a dosing device -- B05B 11/067 refers to single-unit hand-held spraying devices in which the operator's muscular force drives the flow, and where the particulate material to be sprayed is withdrawn from a main reservoir by a dosing mechanism before atomisation.
[B05B 11/068] comprising a liquid-absorbent material -- This subclass covers hand-held spray devices that rely on the operator's squeeze to expel liquid and include a liquid-absorbent material such as a sponge or wick to hold the fluid before release. The absorbent element stores the liquid and allows controlled atomisation when the device is used.
[B05B 11/10] Pump arrangements for transferring the contents from the container to a pump chamber by a sucking effect and forcing the contents out through the dispensing nozzle -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Pumps drawing the contents from the container into a pump chamber inlet during a suction stroke and forcing the contents out of a pump chamber outlet into the dispensing nozzle during a dispensing stroke.
[B05B 11/1001] Piston pumps  B05B11/1087 ,  B05B11/1088 ,  B05B11/109  take precedence -- This classification code, B05B 11/1001, pertains to single-unit hand-held spraying apparatus that use piston pumps operated by the user's muscular force, with specific subcategories for different pump mechanisms taking precedence.
[B05B 11/1002] the direction of the pressure stroke being substantially perpendicular to the major axis of the container  B05B11/1009 ,  B05B11/1015  take precedence -- It covers hand-held spray devices where the pressure stroke is directed substantially perpendicular to the major axis of the container. The classification pertains to the orientation of the stroke in muscular-force operated sprayers and takes precedence over B05B11/1009 and B05B11/1015.
[B05B 11/1004] comprising a movable cylinder and a stationary piston -- B05B 11/1004 pertains to single-unit hand-held spray apparatus that rely on the operator's muscular effort to produce flow, using a movable cylinder that slides against a stationary piston to dispense the contents.
[B05B 11/1005] with means for adjusting or modifying pump stroke -- B05B 11/1005 refers to a single-unit hand-held spray device in which the operator's muscular force drives the flow, and the apparatus includes a mechanism for adjusting or modifying the pump stroke length or pressure, allowing variable delivery of the contents.
[B05B 11/1007] by adjusting or modifying the pump end-of-sucking-stroke position -- This subclass concerns single-unit hand-held spray devices whose flow is generated by the operator's muscle power and whose liquid-drawing amount is controlled by adjusting or modifying the pump end-of-sucking-stroke position, allowing the user to vary how much product is drawn before discharge.
[B05B 11/1008] by adjusting or modifying the pump end-of-dispensing-stroke position -- This CPC subclass B05B 11/1008 covers single-unit hand-held spray devices that rely on the operator's muscular force, specifically those that adjust or modify the pump's end-of-dispensing-stroke position to control the spray output.
[B05B 11/1009] actuated by a lever -- A single-unit handheld spray device that relies on the operator's muscle power, where a lever is pressed or pulled to generate the flow of the material being atomised. The lever action drives a piston or compresses a container, creating the pressure needed for spraying at the moment of use. This design is classified under CPC B05B 11/1009 for lever-actuated spraying apparatus.
[B05B 11/1011] without substantial movement of the nozzle in the direction of the pressure stroke -- B05B 11/1011 covers single-unit hand-held spray devices that rely on the operator's muscular force to discharge the material and where the nozzle does not undergo substantial movement in the direction of the pressure stroke. This subclass falls under spraying apparatus that operate without significant nozzle displacement during use.
[B05B 11/1012] the pump chamber being arranged substantially coaxially to the neck of the container  B05B11/1011  takes precedence -- This subclass concerns hand-held spray devices whose pump chamber is placed essentially on the same axis as the container neck, allowing the product to be driven directly from the container into the spray mechanism. It narrows the broader category of spray apparatus with coaxial pump chambers.
[B05B 11/1014] the pump chamber being arranged substantially coaxially to the container -- B05B 11/1014 covers hand-held spraying devices in which the pump chamber is positioned essentially along the same axis as the container, so the pumping mechanism aligns coaxially with the product reservoir. This arrangement allows the operator's muscular force to drive the pump directly in a single-unit unitary apparatus.
[B05B 11/1015] actuated without substantial movement of the nozzle in the direction of the pressure stroke  B05B11/1011  takes precedence -- A single-unit hand-held spraying device that generates flow by the operator's muscle force, where the actuation does not require the nozzle to move appreciably in the direction of the pressure stroke. This arrangement refines the broader class for spray apparatus without substantial nozzle displacement during the pressure stroke, as defined in B05B 11/1011.
[B05B 11/1016] the outlet valve having a valve seat located downstream a movable valve element controlled by a pressure actuated controlling element  B05B11/1022 ,  B05B11/1023  take precedence -- B05B 11/1016 covers hand-held spraying devices where the operator's muscle force drives the flow, specifically focusing on outlet valves that have a downstream valve seat and a movable valve element operated by a pressure-actuated control; the more specific sub-classes B05B 11/1022 and B05B 11/1023 take precedence.
[B05B 11/1018] and the controlling element cooperating with means for opening or closing the inlet valve  B05B11/1019  takes precedence -- This classification entry, B05B 11/1018, pertains to single-unit hand-held spraying apparatus where the flow is generated by the operator's muscular force, specifically addressing designs where a controlling element works with mechanisms for opening or closing the inlet valve. However, it notes that the more specific code B05B 11/1019 takes precedence when applicable.
[B05B 11/1019] the inlet valve moving concurrently with the controlling element during whole pressure and aspiration strokes, e.g. a cage for an inlet valve ball being part of the controlling element -- This CPC subclass covers single-hand-held spray devices where the inlet valve is linked to the control element and moves together throughout the pressure and aspiration strokes, such as a cage that carries the inlet-valve ball as part of the trigger mechanism.
[B05B 11/1021] having an outlet valve which is a gate valve  B05B11/1023 ,  B05B11/1038  take precedence -- This CPC subclass describes a single-unit hand-held spray device in which the material is expelled by the operator's muscular force at the moment of use and the outlet is controlled by a gate valve.
[B05B 11/1022] actuated by pressure -- B05B 11/1022 covers single-unit hand-held spraying devices where the flow of the material is created by the operator's muscular force at the moment of use, the apparatus being actuated directly by the pressure generated during operation.
[B05B 11/1023] having an outlet valve opened by deformation or displacement of the piston relative to its actuating stem -- This subclass covers single-unit hand-held spray devices where the operator's muscular force drives a piston, and the outlet valve opens by deformation or axial displacement of the piston relative to its actuating stem, releasing the contents as a spray.
[B05B 11/1025] a spring urging the outlet valve in its closed position  B05B11/1026  takes precedence -- A single-unit hand-held spray device that relies on the operator's muscle power to dispense its contents, equipped with a spring that continuously pushes the outlet valve toward the closed position so that the valve must be manually opened for each spray. This feature is defined in CPC code B05B 11/1025, which is superseded by the more specific subclass B05B 11/1026.
[B05B 11/1026] the piston being deformable and its deformation allowing opening of the outlet -- The subclass covers single-unit hand-held spray devices whose piston is made of a deformable material; when the user applies muscular force the piston flexes or compresses, and that deformation itself opens the outlet, allowing the product to be emitted without a separate valve.
[B05B 11/1028] Pumps having a pumping chamber with a deformable wall  B05B11/1087  take precedence -- B05B 11/1028 covers hand-held spraying devices in which a pump with a deformable chamber creates the flow of material by the operator's muscular effort at the moment of use. The pump's chamber flexes to draw and discharge the product, and the subclass is subordinate to B05B11/1087, which has priority.
[B05B 11/1029] actuated by a lever -- A single-unit hand-held spraying device in which the operator's muscular force generates the flow of material at the moment of use, and the discharge is started or stopped by moving a lever. This type of apparatus falls under the CPC group B05B 11/1029 for lever-actuated spray units.
[B05B 11/103] without substantial movement of the nozzle in the direction of the pressure stroke -- B05B 11/103 refers to hand-held spray devices that rely on the operator's muscular force to discharge the product, where the nozzle remains essentially stationary relative to the pressure stroke and does not undergo substantial movement during actuation. The design is a single-unit apparatus in which the flow is generated at the moment of use without the nozzle moving forward with the pressure.
[B05B 11/1032] actuated without substantial movement of the nozzle in the direction of the pressure stroke  B05B11/103  takes precedence -- This CPC sub-class (B05B 11/1032) covers single-unit hand-held spray devices in which the operator's muscular effort creates the flow and the nozzle remains essentially stationary during the pressure stroke. It falls under Performing Operations → Spraying or Atomising in General → Spraying Apparatus, and refines the broader group B05B 11/103.
[B05B 11/1033] the deformable wall, the inlet and outlet valve elements being integrally formed, e.g. moulded -- A single-unit hand-held sprayer that creates flow by the operator's muscular effort uses a deformable wall and inlet/outlet valve elements that are integrally formed, for example by molding. This construction provides a compact, sealed mechanism without separate valve parts.
[B05B 11/1035] the pumping chamber being a bellow -- This subcategory covers hand-held spray devices whose pump chamber is a bellows that the operator compresses to expel the fluid. It pertains to manually actuated spraying apparatus with a bellows pumping chamber.
[B05B 11/1036] the outlet valve being opened in the direction opposite to the fluid flow downstream the outlet valve by the pressure acting on a valve controlling element -- The entry covers spray devices in which the outlet valve is opened opposite the direction of fluid flow, the valve being actuated by pressure acting on a controlling element rather than by the operator's push. This principle is applied in single-unit, hand-held sprayers where the fluid is forced through the valve by the pressure generated in the system.
[B05B 11/1038] Pressure accumulation pumps, i.e. pumps comprising a pressure accumulation chamber -- The entry B05B 11/1038 defines pressure-accumulation pumps, which incorporate a chamber that stores fluid under pressure before release. These pumps are used in single-unit, hand-held spray devices where the operator's muscular force generates the flow at the moment of use, and they belong to the broader class of spraying apparatus within performing operations.
[B05B 11/1039] the outlet valve being mechanically opened after a defined accumulation stroke -- This subclass covers single-unit hand-held spraying devices that are powered by the operator's muscular force and where the outlet valve is opened mechanically only after a defined accumulation stroke. The valve activation occurs after the pump or piston has moved a set distance, providing a controlled release of the product.
[B05B 11/104] the outlet valve being opened by pressure after a defined accumulation stroke -- B05B 11/104 covers single-unit hand-held spray devices whose outlet valve opens by pressure after a preset accumulation stroke, meaning the operator's muscular force builds up fluid before release. The classification is part of the CPC hierarchy under Performing Operations → Spraying or Atomising in General → Spraying Apparatus.
[B05B 11/1042] Components or details -- This subclass covers the individual parts and specific details of single-unit hand-held spray devices that rely on the operator's muscular force to generate flow, such as nozzles, pumps, valves, triggers and ergonomic grips.
[B05B 11/1043] Sealing or attachment arrangements between pump and container  sealing arrangements around pump actuating stem  B05B11/105 -- B05B 11/1043 covers sealing or attachment arrangements between a pump and its container, focusing on seals around the pump actuating stem in single-unit handheld spraying devices where the flow is generated by the operator's muscular force.
[B05B 11/1045] the pump being preassembled as an independent unit before being mounted on the container  B05B11/1047 ,  B05B11/1049  take precedence -- This classification code, B05B 11/1045, pertains to single-unit hand-held spraying apparatus where the flow is generated by the operator's muscular force, specifically focusing on designs where the pump is preassembled as an independent unit before attachment to the container. However, more specific codes like B05B 11/1047 and B05B 11/1049 take precedence when applicable.
[B05B 11/1046] the pump chamber being arranged substantially coaxially to the neck of the container  B05B11/1049  takes precedence -- This classification entry, B05B 11/1046, pertains to single-unit hand-held spraying apparatus where the operator's muscular force generates the flow, specifically detailing designs with a pump chamber aligned coaxially to the container's neck. However, it notes that entry B05B 11/1049 takes precedence in cases of overlap.
[B05B 11/1047] the pump being preassembled as an independent unit before being mounted on the container -- In the CPC hierarchy of performing operations, spraying or atomising in general, this subcategory (B05B 11/1047) covers spray devices where the pump is preassembled as an independent unit before being mounted on the container, typically a hand-held apparatus operated by the user's muscular force at the moment of use.
[B05B 11/1049] Attachment arrangements comprising a deformable or resilient ferrule clamped or locked onto the neck of the container by displacing, e.g. sliding, a sleeve surrounding the ferrule -- The entry defines spray devices that secure a deformable or resilient ferrule to the neck of a container by moving a surrounding sleeve, such that the ferrule is clamped or locked in place, typically by sliding the sleeve.
[B05B 11/105] Sealing arrangements around pump actuating stem -- This subclass covers sealing arrangements that encircle the pump-actuating stem of hand-held spraying devices, preventing fluid leakage and protecting the stem from contamination. It includes gaskets, O-rings, labyrinth seals and other sealing means integrated into the pump assembly.
[B05B 11/1052] Actuation means  locking means therefor  B05B11/1059 ; the dispensing stroke being affected by the stored energy of a spring  B05B11/109 -- This classification code, B05B 11/1052, pertains to single-unit hand-held spraying apparatus where the flow is generated by the operator's muscular force, specifically detailing actuation mechanisms that include locking features and those utilizing a spring's stored energy for dispensing.
[B05B 11/1053] combined with means, other than pressure, for automatically opening a valve during actuation; combined with means for automatically removing closures or covers from the discharge nozzle during actuation -- A hand-held spray device that the user powers by muscular effort, equipped with an automatic mechanism that opens the valve and removes any closure or cover from the discharge nozzle each time the unit is actuated. This combines manual operation with self-actuated valve and nozzle-clearance features.
[B05B 11/1054] the valve being located upstream of an outlet valve -- B05B 11/1054 covers hand-held, single-unit spray devices that rely on the operator's muscular effort, where a valve is placed before the outlet valve to control the flow of material. The upstream valve regulates pressurisation or discharge before the material reaches the final outlet valve.
[B05B 11/1056] comprising rotatable or articulated levers  B05B11/1053  takes precedence; lever actuated piston pumps  B05B11/1009 ; lever actuated pumps with deformable chamber  B05B11/1029 -- This CPC subclass B05B 11/1056 covers single-unit hand-held spray devices that generate flow by the operator's muscular effort at the moment of use and incorporate rotatable or articulated levers. It follows the broader groups for lever-actuated piston pumps (B05B 11/1009) and lever-actuated pumps with a deformable chamber (B05B 11/1029), and is nested under spraying apparatus in the performing operations section.
[B05B 11/1057] Triggers, i.e. actuation means consisting of a single lever having one end rotating or pivoting around an axis or a hinge fixedly attached to the container, and another end directly actuated by the user -- The entry B05B 11/1057 covers triggers used in hand-held spraying devices, defined as a single lever that pivots or rotates around a fixed axis or hinge attached to the container, with the opposite end directly pulled or pressed by the user to dispense the material.
[B05B 11/1059] Means for locking a pump or its actuation means in a fixed position  B05B11/1091  takes precedence -- This subclass covers devices that lock the pump or its actuation means in a fixed position in hand-held, manually powered spray apparatus, preventing movement during use. It applies to single-unit dispensers where the operator's muscle force drives the flow. Where a more specific feature is defined in subclass B05B 11/1091, that subclass has priority.
[B05B 11/106] in a retracted position, e.g. in an end-of-dispensing-stroke position -- This code covers single-unit hand-held spray devices whose dispensing mechanism is held in a retracted or end-of-stroke position when not in use, so the flow of contents is produced only by the operator's muscular force at the moment of actuation.
[B05B 11/1061] Pump priming means -- The B05B 11/1061 subclass refers to pump-priming mechanisms in single-unit, hand-held spraying devices whose discharge is generated by the operator's muscular effort at the moment of use. It covers features that prepare the pump for operation, ensuring fluid can be expelled when the user presses or squeezes the unit.
[B05B 11/1063] Air exhausted from the pump chamber being discharged into the container during priming -- This subclass covers hand-held spray devices where, during the priming stroke, air that leaves the pump chamber is routed back into the container, affecting the mixing or pressure build-up. It applies to apparatus that rely on the operator's muscular force to generate flow.
[B05B 11/1064] Pump inlet and outlet valve elements integrally formed of a deformable material  pump chambers having a deformable wall integrally formed with inlet and outlet valve elements  B05B11/1033 -- A single-unit hand-held spray device in which the pump chamber walls and both the inlet and outlet valve elements are formed together from a deformable material, such as an elastomer, so that the operator's muscle pressure deforms the chamber to open and close the valves and discharge the product.
[B05B 11/1066] Pump inlet valves  B05B11/1018 ,  B05B11/1019 ,  B05B11/1064  take precedence -- These subclasses cover the inlet valves of pumps used in single-hand-held spray devices where the fluid is expelled by the operator's muscular action. The categories B05B11/1018, B05B11/1019 and B05B11/1064 define specific valve types and are given priority over the broader B05B11/1066 grouping.
[B05B 11/1067] actuated by pressure -- This CPC subgroup covers hand-held spray devices that dispense their contents by the operator's direct muscular pressure applied at the moment of use, rather than by a separate pump or compressed gas.
[B05B 11/1069] the valve being made of a resiliently deformable material or being urged in a closed position by a spring -- This CPC subclass (B05B 11/1069) covers single-unit hand-held spray devices where the valve is made of a flexible material that deforms to open, or is kept closed by a spring. It pertains to spraying or atomising apparatus whose flow is generated by the operator's muscular force.
[B05B 11/107] Gate valves; Sliding valves -- The CPC subclass B05B 11/107 pertains to single-unit hand-held spray devices that rely on the operator's muscular effort to generate flow, and that employ gate or sliding valves to start, stop, or regulate the discharge of the material. This covers portable atomising apparatus where the valve moves linearly to open or close the spray passage.
[B05B 11/1071] Two inlet valves being placed in a supply conduit one upstream of the other -- This class pertains to a single-unit hand-held spray device powered by the operator's muscular force, where the supply conduit contains two inlet valves arranged one upstream of the other to control the flow of the material being sprayed.
[B05B 11/1073] Springs -- The subclass B05B 11/1073 covers springs incorporated in single-unit hand-held spray devices where the operator's muscular force initiates the flow of material. These springs may store energy to return a piston, maintain pressure, or assist in loading the spray chamber during each actuation.
[B05B 11/1074] located outside pump chambers -- This subclass deals with single-unit hand-held spray devices that rely on the operator's muscular force to create the flow of material, with the pump chamber positioned outside the main body of the apparatus. It falls under the broader categories of performing operations, spraying or atomising in general, and spraying apparatus.
[B05B 11/1076] Traction springs, e.g. stretchable sleeve -- B05B 11/1076 covers traction springs such as stretchable sleeves that store elastic energy and release it to drive the pumping action of a hand-held spraying device, where the operator's muscular effort initiates flow. These springs provide the mechanical force needed to pressurise and dispense the contents during spraying.
[B05B 11/1077] characterised by a particular shape or material  B05B11/1076  takes precedence -- The Cooperative Patent Classification entry B05B 11/1077 pertains to single-unit hand-held spraying apparatus where the flow is generated by the operator's muscular force, specifically focusing on designs characterized by a particular shape or material. However, this classification takes precedence only when it does not conflict with B05B 11/1076.
[B05B 11/1078] Vacuum chambers acting like springs -- The subclass covers hand-held spray devices that incorporate a flexible vacuum chamber acting like a spring; when the operator squeezes the chamber a negative pressure is generated, drawing the material in and then expelling it as a spray. This arrangement supplies the flow of contents without auxiliary pumps or compressors.
[B05B 11/108] Means for counting the number of dispensing strokes -- A mechanism incorporated in single-unit hand-held spray devices that records each actuation of the valve, allowing the user or manufacturer to track how many dispensing strokes have occurred. Such counting means may be mechanical gears, click counters or electronic sensors and are used for dosage control, maintenance scheduling or usage monitoring.
[B05B 11/1081] Arrangements for pumping several liquids or other fluent materials from several containers, e.g. for mixing them at the moment of pumping -- Arrangements for pumping two or more liquids or other fluent materials from separate containers, typically to mix them at the moment of dispensing, as found in hand-held spray devices that combine ingredients during use.
[B05B 11/1083] in adjustable proportion -- This subclass covers single-unit, hand-held spray devices that dispense their contents by the operator's muscular force and include a mechanism for varying the mixing ratio of the components, allowing the proportion of liquids to be adjusted while spraying.
[B05B 11/1084] each liquid or other fluent material being pumped by a separate pump -- The CPC subclass B05B 11/1084 covers single-unit hand-held spraying devices in which each liquid or other fluent material is pumped by a separate pump, the flow being generated by the operator's muscular force at the moment of use. It falls under spraying apparatus within the broader hierarchy of performing operations and spraying or atomising in general.
[B05B 11/1085] the pumps being coaxial -- Coaxial pumps in a single-unit hand-held spray device are positioned on the same axis so that the operator's muscular pressure directly drives the pump mechanism, delivering the contents in a controlled spray. This arrangement integrates the pump and nozzle into a compact unit, enabling immediate flow at the moment of use.
[B05B 11/1087] Combination of liquid and air pumps -- A single-unit, hand-held spray device that relies on the operator's muscular force at the moment of use and contains both a liquid pump and an air pump working together to generate the spray. This combination of liquid and air pumps is integral to the apparatus, which falls under the "spraying or atomising in general - spraying apparatus" subclass.
[B05B 11/1088] the pump being a double-acting pump -- This subclass concerns single-unit hand-held spray devices whose pump is double-acting, delivering the product on both the forward and return strokes under the operator's muscular force. It falls under the broader category of spraying apparatus in the CPC hierarchy.
[B05B 11/109] the dispensing stroke being affected by the stored energy of a spring  B05B11/1088  takes precedence -- This classification code, B05B 11/109, pertains to single-unit hand-held spraying apparatus where the flow is generated by the operator's muscular force, specifically utilizing the stored energy of a spring for the dispensing stroke. However, it notes that the more specific code B05B 11/1088 takes precedence when applicable.
[B05B 11/1091] being first hold in a loaded state by locking means or the like, then released  B05B11/1092  takes precedence -- B05B 11/1091 covers single-unit hand-held spray devices that are loaded and held in a ready-to-use position by a locking mechanism, which is then released to allow the operator's muscular force to dispense the contents. The classification applies when the lock secures the product before the spray is actuated, and it is superseded by B05B 11/1092 for more specific variants.
[B05B 11/1092] automatically released from a loaded state at the end of the loading stroke -- The code refers to a single-unit hand-held spray device in which the operator's muscular force supplies the flow, and the mechanism automatically switches from a loaded condition to a release position when the loading stroke is completed. This automatic release occurs at the end of the stroke without additional actuation.
[B05B 11/1094] having inlet or outlet valves not being actuated by pressure or having no inlet or outlet valve -- This subclass covers hand-held spray devices that rely on the operator's muscle power and feature either valves that are not pressure-actuated or lack inlet or outlet valves altogether. It pertains to portable, single-unit sprayers where the fluid is expelled without pneumatic or spring-loaded valve mechanisms.
[B05B 11/1095] with movable suction side -- B05B 11/1095 covers single-unit hand-held spray devices that rely on the operator's muscular force to create flow and include a suction side that can be moved relative to the nozzle, allowing the device to draw liquid into the spray chamber during use.
[B05B 11/1097] with means for sucking back the liquid or other fluent material in the nozzle after a dispensing stroke -- This subclass covers hand-held spray devices that rely on the operator's squeeze to expel product and include a mechanism that draws any remaining liquid back into the nozzle after each spray.
[B05B 11/1098] Air being permanently entrapped or sucked into the liquid pump chamber -- B05B 11/1098 pertains to single-unit hand-held spray devices in which air is either permanently trapped or actively drawn into the liquid pump chamber to assist atomisation during the operator's manual pumping action.

[B05B 1/16] having selectively- effective outlets -- B05B 1/16 covers spray apparatus whose outlets-such as nozzles, spray heads, or similar devices-can be selectively activated, adjusted, or shut off to control the flow or pattern of the spray. The classification includes arrangements with valves, heating elements or other auxiliary means that enable selective effectiveness of individual outlets.

[B05B 1/1681] with a selecting mechanism comprising a gate valve, sliding valve or cock and a lift valve -- This subclass covers spray heads or nozzle outlets that incorporate a selection mechanism formed by a gate, sliding or cock valve together with a lift valve, enabling controlled initiation and cessation of spray flow. It is used to regulate fluid discharge in spraying or atomising devices.
[B05B 1/169] having three or more selectively effective outlets -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this place:  The Figure illustrates a spray head having three or more selectively effective outlets (161).
[B05B 1/18] Roses -- Group B05B1/18 is impacted by reclassification into groups B05B1/188 , B05B1/1881 , B05B1/1882 , B05B1/1884 , B05B1/1886 , B05B1/1887 , B05B1/189 , B05B1/1892 , B05B1/1894 , B05B1/1896 and B05B1/192 . [B05B 1/182] characterised by their outlet openings or elements; Mounting arrangements therefor -- Groups B05B1/182 , B05B1/1821 , B05B1/1822 , B05B1/1823 , B05B1/1824 and B05B1/1825 are incomplete pending reclassification of documents from group B05B1/185 . All groups listed in this Warning should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[B05B 1/1821] the outlet openings arranged in plural groups or rows -- This subclass deals with spray heads whose outlet openings are arranged in several groups or rows rather than a single line, enabling patterned or broader coverage. It includes designs where multiple rows of nozzles are positioned to deliver fluid simultaneously for applications like painting, agricultural spraying, or humidification.

[B05B 1/192] combined with features functioning other than as a support or as a portion of the fluid handling elements, e.g. audio systems, speakers or lights -- Group B05B1/192 is incomplete pending reclassification of documents from group B05B1/18 . Groups B05B1/18 and B05B1/192 should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[B05B 1/20] Perforated pipes or troughs, e.g. spray booms -- This subclass concerns perforated pipes, troughs or comparable conduits used as spray booms to discharge fluid through a series of openings, forming a spray or mist. The designs may incorporate auxiliary features such as valves or heating elements, but the perforated element itself serves as the primary outlet.
[B05B 12/00] Arrangements for controlling delivery -- B05B 12/00 covers devices and mechanisms that regulate the amount, rate, or timing of fluid discharge from a spray or atomising apparatus. It includes valves, pistons, pressure-control systems and electronic controllers that adjust delivery to achieve precise coverage, reduce waste, or match application requirements. This subclass groups the various means by which spray output is modulated during operation.
[B05B 12/002] Manually-actuated controlling means, e.g. push buttons, levers or triggers  for single units  B05B11/00 -- The subclass B05B 12/002 deals with manually-actuated control devices, such as push buttons, levers or triggers, that operate a single spray unit. It falls under spraying apparatus and specifically addresses arrangements for controlling the delivery of spray or atomising material.
[B05B 12/0022] associated with means for restricting their movement -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Means to restrict, limit the actuation by the user by limiting the movement of the controlling means.  Such restricting means are:  Biasing means such as springs are not considered as means for restricting the movement.  If there is only one locking or stopping position, classification is made in B05B12/0024 and subgroup.  Example of a locking means, with several positions:  Example of a stopping means, with several positions:
[B05B 12/0024] to a single position -- B05B 12/0024 relates to spray apparatus whose delivery is directed to a single position. It includes designs where the nozzle or atomiser is fixed so that the spray is emitted at one predetermined point, typically used for targeted coating, cleaning or treatment operations. The subclass covers mechanisms for maintaining that fixed delivery orientation.
[B05B 12/0026] to inhibit delivery -- The subclass B05B 12/0026 covers features of spraying or atomising devices that deliberately inhibit or stop the discharge of the spray. It includes valves, throttles, safety interlocks or other control mechanisms that prevent unwanted delivery, providing precise dosing or protection against accidental release.
[B05B 12/004] comprising sensors for monitoring the delivery, e.g. by displaying the sensed value or generating an alarm  B05B12/08  takes precedence; registering or indicating the condition or the working of machines or other apparatus in general  G07C3/00 -- B05B 12/004 relates to spray or atomising apparatus that includes sensors for monitoring the delivery of material, with the measured values displayed to the operator or used to trigger an alarm, ensuring accurate dosing and safe operation.
[B05B 12/006] Pressure or flow rate sensors -- B05B 12/006 covers devices that monitor the pressure or flow rate of liquids or gases in spraying systems. These sensors provide feedback for controlling the amount of material delivered by spray apparatus and are used to regulate performance in agricultural, industrial, or consumer applications.
[B05B 12/008] integrated in or attached to a discharge apparatus, e.g. a spray gun -- This subclass covers spray devices where the control arrangement is built into or attached to the discharge unit, such as a spray gun, providing immediate regulation of flow, pressure or atomisation directly at the outlet.
[B05B 1/202] comprising inserted outlet elements -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this place:  The Figure illustrates outlets (14) comprising inserted elements (16) along an elongated tubular body (12).
[B05B 12/02] for controlling time, or sequence, of delivery -- B05B 12/02 covers spray apparatus that include means for regulating the timing or order of fluid discharge. It encompasses control devices such as timing circuits, sequencers and valves that adjust the duration, frequency or sequence of delivery.
[B05B 12/04] for sequential operation or multiple outlets -- Group B05B12/04 is impacted by reclassification into group B05B13/002 . Groups B05B12/04 and B05B13/002 should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[B05B 1/205] characterised by the longitudinal shape of the elongated body -- B05B 1/205 covers spray nozzles, heads or other outlets whose elongated body is defined by its longitudinal shape, such as cylindrical, conical or other profiles, rather than by external features like valves or heating elements. The shape is a key factor in controlling fluid flow and spray characteristics.
[B05B 12/06] for effecting pulsating flow -- This subgroup pertains to spray devices that incorporate mechanisms to produce a pulsating or intermittent flow of the conveyed medium. It includes valves, diaphragms or other actuators that periodically vary the discharge pressure or volume, thereby generating a pulsed spray pattern for improved atomisation or material handling.
[B05B 1/207] the elongated body being a closed loop -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative examples of subject matter classified in this place:  1.  Figure 1 illustrates a first arrangement (10) of several nozzles (40) along a closed loop (31).  2.  Figure 2 illustrates a second arrangement (130) of several openings (132) along a closed loop (138).
[B05B 12/08] responsive to condition of liquid or other fluent material discharged, of ambient medium or of target -- This subclass covers spray devices whose delivery is adjusted automatically based on feedback from the sprayed fluid, surrounding environment, or the surface being treated. It includes sensors and control mechanisms that modify flow rate, pressure or droplet size in response to measured conditions.
[B05B 12/081] responsive to the weight of a reservoir or container for liquid or other fluent material; responsive to level or volume of liquid or other fluent material in a reservoir or container -- B05B 12/081 covers spray devices that adjust their operation according to the weight of the liquid container or to the level or volume of fluid in the reservoir. This includes mechanisms that sense the amount of material and modulate flow or atomisation accordingly, ensuring consistent performance as the supply depletes.
[B05B 12/082] responsive to a condition of the discharged jet or spray, e.g. to jet shape, spray pattern or droplet size -- B05B 12/082 covers spray or atomising devices that automatically modify their operation in response to a detected condition of the discharged jet or spray, such as changes in jet shape, spray pattern or droplet size. The system adjusts delivery parameters to maintain the desired performance of the spray.
[B05B 12/084] responsive to condition of liquid or other fluent material already sprayed on the target, e.g. coating thickness, weight or pattern -- The B05B 12/084 subclass covers spray-applicator arrangements that adjust delivery based on the state of material already deposited on the target, such as coating thickness, weight, or pattern. It includes sensors, feedback loops, and control mechanisms that modify spray parameters in real time to achieve desired coverage.
[B05B 12/085] responsive to flow or pressure of liquid or other fluent material to be discharged  B05B1/3006 ,  B05B1/323  take precedence; spray pistols designed to control volume of flow with the controlling means being fluid actuated  B05B7/1254 -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Nozzles, spray heads or other outlets designed to control volume of flow, the controlling element being actuated by the pressure of the fluid to be sprayed -> B05B1/3006 - Nozzles, spray heads or other outlets designed to control volume of flow in which a valve member forms part of the outlet opening, the valve member being actuated by the pressure of the fluid to be sprayed -> B05B1/323 - Spraying apparatus for discharge of liquids or other fluent materials from two or more sources, e.g. of liquid and air, of powder and gas -> B05B7/1254
[B05B 12/087] Flow or presssure regulators, i.e. non-electric unitary devices comprising a sensing element, e.g. a piston or a membrane, and a controlling element, e.g. a valve -- B05B 12/087 covers non-electric flow or pressure regulators used in spraying devices, comprising a single integrated unit with a sensing element such as a piston or membrane that detects pressure changes and a controlling element like a valve that adjusts the flow accordingly.
[B05B 12/088] the sensing element being a flexible member, e.g. membrane, diaphragm, bellows -- A spraying apparatus that includes a delivery-control arrangement wherein the sensing element is a flexible member such as a membrane, diaphragm or bellows, used to detect pressure or flow changes and adjust the spray output accordingly.
[B05B 12/10] responsive to temperature or viscosity of liquid or other fluent material discharged -- This subclass concerns spray devices that modify the flow or atomisation according to the temperature or viscosity of the liquid being discharged. It includes sensors or control mechanisms that detect these properties and adjust pressure, nozzle opening, or other parameters to maintain consistent spray performance.
[B05B 12/12] responsive to conditions of ambient medium or target, e.g. humidity, temperature -- B05B 12/12 relates to spray or atomising equipment that includes control arrangements which modify the delivery of the spray in response to ambient or target conditions such as humidity or temperature, using sensors or feedback mechanisms to adjust flow, droplet size or timing.
[B05B 12/122] responsive to presence or shape of target  B05B12/124  takes precedence -- B05B 12/122 covers spraying devices that adjust the delivery of fluid in response to the detected presence or shape of the target, such as sensors that trigger or modulate spray flow. The subclass is superseded by B05B12/124, which provides a more specific arrangement for target-responsive control.
[B05B 12/124] responsive to distance between spray apparatus and target -- This subclass of B05B 12 covers spray devices that automatically modify their delivery parameters according to the measured distance between the nozzle and the target, using sensors, pressure adjustments, or variable-orifice designs to change droplet size, flow rate or spray pattern. Such distance-responsive controls are employed in painting, pesticide application and fire-fighting systems.
[B05B 12/126] responsive to target velocity, e.g. to relative velocity between spray apparatus and target  B05B9/06  takes precedence -- B05B12/126 concerns spray devices whose delivery is adjusted according to the motion of the target, such as the relative speed between the nozzle and the surface being treated. The control system varies flow, pressure or atomisation to maintain effectiveness despite target velocity. This sub-group is governed by the broader class B05B9/06.
[B05B 12/14] for supplying a selected one of a plurality of liquids or other fluent materials to a single spray outlet -- B05B 12/14 covers spray apparatus that can feed a chosen one of several liquids or fluent materials to a single spray outlet, providing a mechanism for selecting among multiple supply streams within the same spray device.
[B05B 12/1409] the selection means being part of the discharge apparatus, e.g. part of the spray gun -- B05B 12/1409 concerns spray apparatus in which the means for selecting the spray type, flow rate or pattern are incorporated into the discharge unit itself, for example a spray gun that contains an integrated selector valve or nozzle.
[B05B 12/1418] for supplying several liquids or other fluent materials in selected proportions to a single spray outlet  controlling ratio of two or more flows of fluid  G05D11/02 -- The Cooperative Patent Classification entry B05B 12/1418 pertains to arrangements for controlling the delivery of several liquids or other fluent materials in selected proportions to a single spray outlet, specifically addressing the control mechanism that regulates the ratio of two or more fluid flows.
[B05B 12/1427] a condition of a first liquid or other fluent material in a first supply line controlling a condition of a second one in a second supply line -- This subclass pertains to spray devices in which a condition such as pressure, flow rate or temperature of a fluid in a first supply line is used to control a corresponding condition of a fluid in a second supply line, enabling coordinated delivery and atomisation. It includes mechanisms that sense the first line's state and adjust the second line accordingly.
[B05B 12/1436] the controlling condition of the first liquid or other fluent material in the first supply line being its flow rate or its pressure -- This CPC sub-group concerns devices that regulate the flow rate or pressure of the first liquid or other fluent material in the first supply line of a spraying apparatus. It is situated under spraying-apparatus arrangements for controlling delivery, within the broader categories of performing operations and spraying or atomising in general.
[B05B 12/1445] pumping means for the liquids or other fluent materials being mechanically linked, e.g. leader-follower pumps -- B05B 12/1445 covers pumping arrangements in spray devices where the pumps are mechanically linked, such as leader-follower systems that coordinate the movement of multiple pumps to ensure a steady, controlled flow of liquids or other fluent materials during atomisation. This subclass refines the broader category of spraying apparatus with delivery-control mechanisms.
[B05B 12/1454] separate units comprising both a material container and a spray device permanently connected thereto being removably attached to a part of the spray apparatus, e.g. to a robot arm -- The entry covers modular spray units in which a material container and its spray device are permanently joined as a single component that can be detached and re-attached to a part of the spray apparatus, such as a robot arm, allowing quick replacement or exchange of the entire dispensing module.
[B05B 12/1463] separate containers for different materials to be sprayed being moved from a first location, e.g. a filling station, where they are fluidically disconnected from the spraying apparatus, to a second location, generally close to the spraying apparatus, where they are fluidically connected to the latter  B05B12/1454  takes precedence -- The Cooperative Patent Classification code B05B 12/1463 pertains to spray apparatus with arrangements for controlling delivery, specifically involving separate containers for different materials that move from a filling station where they are disconnected to a location near the spraying apparatus where they connect. This classification is subordinate to B05B 12/1454 in precedence.
[B05B 12/1472] separate supply lines supplying different materials to separate outlets of the spraying apparatus  B05B12/1454  takes precedence -- Group B05B12/1472 is impacted by reclassification into group B05B13/002 . Groups B05B12/1472 and B05B13/002 should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[B05B 12/1481] comprising pigs, i.e. movable elements sealingly received in supply pipes, for separating different fluids, e.g. liquid coating materials from solvent or air  cleaning pipes with pigs  B08B9/0557 , pigs  per se   F16L55/26 -- The subclass covers spray apparatus that employ removable sealing elements, called pigs, positioned inside supply pipes to separate or clean different fluids during operation, such as isolating liquid coating material from solvents or air. It includes arrangements for controlling delivery by inserting these movable pigs, enabling fluid segregation and pipe cleaning within spraying systems.
[B05B 12/149] characterised by colour change manifolds or valves therefor  B05B12/1409  takes precedence -- This subclass covers spray devices whose manifolds or valves change colour to indicate flow or operating condition, giving a visual cue for controlling liquid delivery. It is part of the broader category of spraying or atomising apparatus with delivery-control arrangements.
[B05B 12/16] for controlling the spray area -- The Cooperative Patent Classification code B05B 12/16 pertains to arrangements for controlling the spray area in spraying apparatus. This classification is used by the European Patent Office and the United States Patent and Trademark Office to categorize inventions that involve mechanisms or methods for adjusting the coverage or spread of a spray.
[B05B 12/18] using fluids, e.g. gas streams -- B05B 12/18 relates to spraying apparatus that employ fluids such as gas streams to regulate the delivery of the sprayed material. It includes arrangements where a carrier gas or other fluid is used to convey, atomise or control the flow of a liquid or solid feed in spray devices. This subclass falls under spraying or atomising in general, focusing on fluid-based delivery mechanisms.
[B05B 1/22] Spouts -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this place:  1a.
