[B05B 12/20] Masking elements, i.e. elements defining uncoated areas on an object to be coated -- B05B 12/20 pertains to masking elements employed in spraying or atomising processes, specifically devices or components that define areas of an object that must remain uncoated. The classification includes removable or fixed masks, frames, sleeves or similar structures used to protect selected surfaces during the application of liquids, powders or gases.
[B05B 12/22] movable relative to the spray area -- The B05B 12/22 subclass includes spray apparatus whose spray nozzle or delivery system can be moved relative to the target area, such as by pivoting, sliding, rotating or otherwise adjusting its position during operation. It covers mechanisms that allow the operator to vary the spray angle, distance or coverage while spraying or atomising.
[B05B 12/24] made at least partly of flexible material, e.g. sheets of paper or fabric -- The subclass B05B 12/24 refers to spraying or atomising apparatus where the delivery system includes flexible elements-such as paper, fabric or similar sheets-to regulate or direct the spray flow. These flexible components form part of the arrangement that controls the amount, pattern or timing of the material being dispensed.
[B05B 12/26] for masking cavities -- The B05B 12/26 subclass pertains to spray-apparatus arrangements that incorporate means for masking cavities in the workpiece or dispensing system. It covers features that limit or prevent spray from entering recessed or hollow areas during atomisation, ensuring selective coating or protecting unwanted zones. This subclass falls under spraying or atomising in general, specifically the control of delivery.
[B05B 12/265] between a door and a post, e.g. foam strips -- The CPC entry B05B 12/265 covers spraying or atomising devices that are positioned in the gap between a door and an adjoining post, such as using foam strips or similar sealing elements to control the delivery of the spray. This arrangement belongs to the broader class of spraying apparatus with delivery-control features.
[B05B 12/28] for defining uncoated areas that are not enclosed within coated areas or -- B05B 12/28 covers spray-applicator arrangements that create or delineate uncoated regions that are not completely surrounded by coated material, such as masks, shields or patterned delivery controls that selectively prevent spray from reaching designated areas on a workpiece.
[B05B 12/29] with adjustable size -- B05B 12/29 covers spray or atomising devices that include mechanisms for adjusting the size of the spray droplets or the spray pattern. The classification applies to apparatuses in which the delivery of the spray can be varied, for example by changing nozzle dimensions, or by using variable-orifice or adjustable-flow components, to control the size of the emitted spray.
[B05B 12/30] specially adapted for vehicle wheels -- B05B 12/30 covers spray or atomising apparatus that are specially adapted for vehicle wheels, focusing on arrangements that control the delivery of the spray onto the wheel or tyre. It includes devices integrated with or mounted on wheels to direct fluid, powder or other media in a regulated manner.
[B05B 12/32] Shielding elements, i.e. elements preventing overspray from reaching areas other than the object to be sprayed -- B05B 12/32 covers shielding elements incorporated in spraying apparatus to prevent overspray from contacting surfaces other than the intended target. These devices, such as guards, baffles or curtains, are positioned to contain the spray jet and protect surrounding areas during the application of liquids or powders.
[B05B 12/34] movable relative to the spray area -- This subclass covers spray apparatus in which the spray head or nozzle can be moved relative to the area being sprayed, providing adjustable positioning or direction during operation. It includes mechanisms and arrangements that allow the user to control the delivery of the spray by shifting the nozzle with respect to the target surface.
[B05B 12/36] Side shields, i.e. shields extending in a direction substantially parallel to the spray jet -- B05B 12/36 covers side shields that extend substantially parallel to the spray jet in spraying or atomising devices. These shields are arranged to control the delivery of the spray, preventing lateral dispersion and protecting surrounding areas or equipment.
[B05B 1/24] incorporating means for heating the liquid or other fluent material, e.g. electrically -- The B05B 1/24 subclass covers spray nozzles, spray heads or other outlets that incorporate heating means for the liquid or other fluent material, such as electrically powered heaters, so that the fluid is warmed directly at the point of atomisation. It applies to apparatus where the heating function is integrated into the dispensing device.
[B05B 12/40] Devices for making a normally hidden area accessible for the spray material -- Devices that enable spray material to reach areas that are normally hidden or difficult to access, such as recessed cavities, channels or interior surfaces, by using extensions, probes, flexible conduits or other mechanisms that guide the spray flow into those concealed spaces.
[B05B 1/26] with means for mechanically breaking-up or deflecting the jet after discharge, e.g. with fixed deflectors -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this place:  The Figure illustrates two impinging jets (S1) and (S2).
[B05B 1/262] with fixed deflectors -- B05B 1/262 covers spray apparatus nozzles or heads that incorporate fixed deflectors to shape or redirect the spray flow, typically integrated into the nozzle body without moving parts. These fixed deflectors modify jet direction or distribution for improved coverage or atomisation.

This entry is part of a broader category that includes machines and plants not covered by more specific groups, focusing on the application of liquids or other fluent materials onto object surfaces through spraying.

[B05B 13/02] Means for supporting work -- B05B 13/02 covers devices that hold or support the workpiece during spraying operations, such as frames, clamps, pallets or other structures that position objects for uniform coating. The supports may be fixed or adjustable and are considered part of the spraying apparatus rather than a separate piece of equipment.
[B05B 13/0207] the work being an elongated body, e.g. wire or pipe  B05B13/0436 ,  B05B13/0463  take precedence -- The CPC subclass B05B 13/0207 designates spray machines or plants that apply liquids to elongated works such as wires or pipes, falling under "performing operations - spraying or atomising - spraying apparatus." Sub-groups B05B 13/0436 and B05B 13/0463 have priority over this entry.
[B05B 13/0214] the liquid or other fluent material being applied to the whole periphery of the cross section of the elongated body -- This subclass covers spray devices that deliver a liquid or other fluent material uniformly around the entire circumference of an elongated object, applying the coating to the whole periphery of the cross-section, such as coating rods, tubes or similar shapes in a continuous, 360-degree manner.
[B05B 13/0221] characterised by the means for moving or conveying the objects or other work, e.g. conveyor belts  B05B13/0207  takes precedence; conveyors in general  B65G -- B05B13/0221 covers spraying machines where the workpiece is moved or conveyed by mechanisms such as conveyor belts during the application of liquids, distinguishing them from other spray apparatus. This subclass follows B05B13/0207 on conveyors in general and applies when the conveying means is integral to the spraying system.
[B05B 13/0228] the movement of the objects being rotative  B05B13/0242  takes precedence -- This CPC subclass deals with spraying or atomising machines that apply liquids to surfaces by rotating the object being treated rather than moving the spray nozzle. It covers apparatus where the workpiece itself is moved in a rotative-movement, and it supersedes the broader B05B13/0242 group.
[B05B 13/0235] the movement of the objects being a combination of rotation and linear displacement  B05B13/0242  takes precedence -- B05B13/0235 classifies spraying devices in which the target objects are moved by a simultaneous rotary and linear displacement, enabling coverage by a combined motion path. This subclass applies to apparatus not otherwise allocated, but is superseded by the more specific B05B13/0242.
[B05B 13/0242] the objects being individually presented to the spray heads by a rotating element, e.g. turntable -- The subclass pertains to spraying apparatus where each workpiece is presented individually to one or more spray heads by a rotating element such as a turntable, enabling sequential coating of objects that are fed one-by-one.
[B05B 13/025] the objects or work being present in bulk -- B05B 13/025 pertains to spraying machines that treat a mass of items or workpieces present together rather than single objects. It covers apparatus that applies liquids or other fluent materials to bulk-loaded substrates in operations such as coating, cleaning or drying.
[B05B 13/0257] in a moving container, e.g. a rotatable foraminous drum -- The sub-group B05B 13/0257 covers spraying devices where the liquid or fluid to be applied is placed in a moving container, such as a rotatable perforated drum, that transports the material while it is atomised through the drum openings onto the target surface.
[B05B 1/3026] Gate valves; Sliding valves; Cocks  B05B1/326  takes precedence -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this place:  1a.  Figure 1a illustrates a cock in an open position (80), which acts as the controlling element to control the flow of fluid upward to an outlet (16).  1b.  Figure 1b illustrates the cock (80) in a closed position.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Nozzles, spray heads or other outlets designed to control volume of flow, in which a valve member forms part of the outlet opening and in which the valve is a gate valve, a sliding valve or a cock -> B05B1/326
[B05B 13/0264] Overhead conveying means, i.e. the object or other work being suspended from the conveying means; Details thereof, e.g. hanging hooks -- The subclass covers devices that suspend the workpiece from an overhead conveying system used in spray applications, such as hanging hooks, carriers or other attachment mechanisms that hold the object while it is moved through the spray zone.
[B05B 13/0271] the object or work standing still during the spraying operation -- This subclass, B05B 13/0271, concerns spraying or atomising operations in which the target object or workpiece remains stationary while the spray device moves to apply liquids or other fluent materials. It applies to machines or plants designed for such fixed-workpiece spraying, distinguishing them from arrangements where the object is conveyed or moved during application.
[B05B 13/0278] Arrangement or mounting of spray heads  B05B13/0207  takes precedence -- The Cooperative Patent Classification code B05B 13/0278 pertains to the arrangement or mounting of spray heads in machines or plants used for applying liquids or other fluent materials to surfaces by spraying, with specific emphasis on configurations not covered by more detailed subcategories. This classification is part of a broader hierarchy dealing with spraying apparatus and operations.
[B05B 13/0285] Stands for supporting individual articles to be sprayed, e.g. doors, vehicle body parts -- The B05B 13/0285 entry covers stands that support individual articles while they are being sprayed, such as doors or vehicle body parts, providing a stable platform for the workpiece during the application of liquids or other fluent materials. This classification falls under spraying apparatus within the broader categories of performing operations and spraying or atomising in general.
[B05B 13/0292] devices for holding several workpieces to be sprayed in a spaced relationship, e.g. vehicle doors spacers -- The B05B 13/0292 subclass covers devices that hold multiple workpieces at a defined spacing-relationship while they are sprayed, such as spacers for vehicle doors. These devices are used in spraying apparatus to ensure even coating and prevent contact between parts.
[B05B 1/3033] the control being effected by relative coaxial longitudinal movement of the controlling element and the spray head  B05B1/3026  takes precedence -- This subclass of B05B concerns spray apparatus-specifically nozzles, spray heads or other outlets-where the spray is regulated by moving the controlling element and the spray head longitudinally in a coaxial relationship. It refines the broader B05B1/3026 category.
[B05B 1/304] the controlling element being a lift valve -- B05B 1/304 relates to spray or atomising devices whose flow is regulated by a lift valve incorporated in the nozzle or spray head. The lift valve opens by pressure or mechanical displacement to control the discharge of fluid, often used when precise metering or automatic shut-off is required.
[B05B 13/04] the spray heads being moved during operation -- B05B 13/04 covers spray devices whose heads are displaced while dispensing, including reciprocating, oscillating, rotating or otherwise movable nozzles that change position during operation to improve coverage or control. The movement may be relative to the workpiece or the apparatus itself and is used in coating, painting, cleaning and similar processes.
[B05B 13/0405] with reciprocating or oscillating spray heads  B05B13/0436 ,  B05B13/0442 ,  B05B13/0447 ,  B05B13/0468  take precedence -- A spray apparatus that applies liquids or other fluent materials to surfaces using spray heads that move back-and-forth or oscillate during operation, falling under the B05B 13/0405 subgroup of machines for spraying where the heads reciprocate or oscillate. This class covers devices not otherwise listed in more specific groups.
[B05B 13/041] with spray heads reciprocating along a straight line -- The subgroup B05B 13/041 relates to spray apparatus whose spray heads move back and forth along a straight line while dispensing liquid or other fluent material, providing a linear reciprocating motion for uniform application on a workpiece. It is a technical specialization within the broader class of machines for spraying or atomising.
[B05B 13/0415] the angular position of the spray heads relative to the straight line being modified during the reciprocating movement -- B05B 13/0415 covers spray devices in which the angular orientation of the spray heads is altered relative to a reference line while the heads move in a reciprocating manner. The classification addresses mechanisms that adjust nozzle angle during the back-and-forth motion to affect spray pattern or coverage.
[B05B 13/0421] with rotating spray heads -- This subgroup covers machines or plants that apply liquids or other fluent materials to surfaces by spraying using spray heads that rotate during operation. The rotating heads create a broader or more uniform coverage and are defined when the device does not fall within any more specific groups of the spraying apparatus class.
[B05B 13/0426] with spray heads moved along a closed path  B05B13/0421  takes precedence -- This subclass covers machines that apply liquids by spraying using heads that travel continuously along a closed trajectory, such as circular or oval tracks, rather than fixed positions. The moving spray heads enable uniform coating of objects passing through or mounted on the path. It belongs to the B05B 13 series for spraying apparatus, subclass 0426, and is superseded by the broader 0421 group.
[B05B 13/0431] with spray heads moved by robots or articulated arms, e.g. for applying liquid or other fluent material to three-dimensional [3D] surfaces -- Group B05B13/0431 is incomplete pending reclassification of documents from group B05B13/0457 . Group B05B13/0431 is also impacted by reclassification into groups B05B13/002 and B05B13/0433 . All groups listed in this Warning should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[B05B 13/0433] the work being vehicle components, e.g. vehicle bodies -- Group B05B13/0433 is incomplete pending reclassification of documents from groups B05B13/0431 and B05B13/0452 . Groups B05B13/0431 , B05B13/0452 and B05B13/0433 should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[B05B 13/0436] Installations or apparatus for applying liquid or other fluent material to elongated bodies, e.g. light poles, pipes  B05B13/0442 ,  B05B13/0463  take precedence -- This subclass covers installations or devices that apply liquids or other fluent materials by spraying onto elongated objects such as light poles, pipes or similar long bodies, where the arrangement is not specified in any other more detailed groups. It belongs to the spraying apparatus category within the performing-operations section.
[B05B 13/0442] Installation or apparatus for applying liquid or other fluent material to separate articles rotated during spraying operation -- This subclass covers installations or devices that apply liquids or other fluent materials to individual items that are rotated while being sprayed, such as rotating drums, mandrels or turntables, enabling uniform coating of each separate article during the spraying process.
[B05B 13/0447] Installation or apparatus for applying liquid or other fluent material to conveyed separate articles  B05B13/0442  takes precedence -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Installation or apparatus for applying liquid or other fluent material to separate articles rotated during spraying operations -> B05B13/0442
[B05B 13/0452] the objects being vehicle components, e.g. vehicle bodies -- Group B05B13/0452 is impacted by reclassification into groups B05B13/002 and B05B13/0433 . Groups B05B13/0452 , B05B13/002 and B05B13/0433 should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[B05B 13/0457] specially designed for applying liquid or other fluent material to three-dimensional [3D] surfaces of the work, e.g. by using several moving spray heads  B05B13/0431 ,  B05B13/0452  take precedence -- Group B05B13/0457 is impacted by reclassification into group B05B13/0431 . Groups B05B13/0457 and B05B13/0431 should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[B05B 1/3046] the valve element, e.g. a needle, co-operating with a valve seat located downstream of the valve element and its actuating means, generally in the proximity of the outlet orifice  B05B1/308  takes precedence -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this place:  The Figure illustrates a spray gun with a "valve seat" located downstream of a movable valve element (4).  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Nozzles, spray heads or other outlets designed to control volume of flow, in which the control is effected by relative coaxial longitudinal movement of the controlling element and the spray head, and in which the controlling element comprises both a lift valve and a deflector -> B05B1/308
[B05B 13/0463] Installation or apparatus for applying liquid or other fluent material to moving work of indefinite length -- This subclass covers installations or devices that spray or otherwise apply liquids or fluent substances onto work that moves continuously and has no defined end, such as webs, belts, or sheets in coating, painting or printing lines. The equipment typically includes nozzles, delivery pumps and guiding mechanisms that operate over an indefinite length of material.
[B05B 13/0468] with reciprocating or oscillating spray heads -- This subclass covers spray apparatus in which the spray head moves back-and-forth or swings side-to-side during operation, providing a reciprocating or oscillating spray pattern. Such devices are used to apply liquids or other fluid materials to surfaces and are not covered by more specific groups within the B05B hierarchy.
[B05B 13/0473] with spray heads reciprocating along a straight line -- A spray apparatus in which the spray heads move back and forth along a straight line, rather than rotating or moving other ways. The classification covers machines that apply liquids or other fluent materials by linearly reciprocating nozzles, typically used for coating or cleaning operations.
[B05B 13/0478] the angular position of the spray heads relative to the straight line being modified during the reciprocating movement -- It relates to spraying apparatus where the angular position of the spray heads relative to a reference straight line is actively altered during the reciprocating movement of the heads, allowing the spray pattern to be varied dynamically.
[B05B 13/0484] with spray heads having a circular motion, e.g. being attached to a rotating supporting element  B05B13/0468  takes precedence -- A spray apparatus whose heads are set into a circular motion, such as by being mounted on a rotating support, is covered by CPC code B05B 13/0484. This sub-group falls under "spraying or atomising in general" and is superseded by the broader group B05B 13/0468.
[B05B 13/0489] around the moving work -- This subgroup covers spray apparatus where the spray discharge zone is arranged to surround or follow a workpiece that moves relative to the machine, such as rotating, orbital or wrap-around sprays that apply liquid to all sides of a moving object.
[B05B 13/0494] with spray heads being moved along a closed path  B05B13/0484  takes precedence -- This subclass covers machines that apply liquids by spraying using heads that travel continuously along a closed path such as a loop, ring or circular track, rather than being fixed or moving back-and-forth. The moving heads may be mounted on belts, chains or rotating arms and are used for uniform coating of surfaces. The preceding group B05B13/0484, concerning heads moving in a closed circuit, has priority over this entry.
[B05B 1/3053] the actuating means being a solenoid -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this place:  The Figure illustrates a solenoid (25) that controls the movement of a lift valve element (20) for controlling the volume of flow through the outlet towards (22), wherein the valve seat is located downstream of a movable lift valve element (20).
[B05B 1/306] the actuating means being a fluid -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this place:  The Figure illustrates an intake duct (28) for a pressurized control fluid flow (F2), e.g. compressed air, which controls the movement of lift valve element (240) for controlling the volume of flow (F1) through outlet (18), wherein a valve seat (20) is located downstream of a movable valve element (240).
[B05B 13/06] specially designed for treating the inside of hollow bodies -- A spray apparatus configured to deliver liquids inside hollow objects such as tubes, containers or cavities for coating, cleaning or other treatment. It incorporates inlet/outlet ports, internal nozzles or rotating heads that direct the spray flow through the interior to achieve uniform coverage.
[B05B 13/0609] the hollow bodies being automatically fed to, or removed from, the machine -- This subgroup deals with spray machines that automatically feed hollow containers-like cans, bottles or other empty bodies-into the apparatus and automatically remove them after coating. It includes mechanisms for loading, positioning and discharging the bodies without manual handling.
[B05B 13/0618] only a part of the inside of the hollow bodies being treated -- B05B 13/0618 covers spray or atomising machines that treat only a portion of the interior of hollow bodies, such as containers, pipes or tubes, rather than the whole cavity. It applies to devices designed to direct liquid or other fluent material onto a limited internal area of the workpiece.
[B05B 13/0627] Arrangements of nozzles or spray heads specially adapted for treating the inside of hollow bodies  B05B13/0645  takes precedence -- This CPC subclass B05B 13/0627 covers specific configurations of nozzles or spray heads that are designed to treat the interior surfaces of hollow objects. The designs focus on delivering spray or atomized fluid inside tubes, vessels or similar cavities. The later subclass B05B 13/0645 supersedes this group for more detailed arrangements.
[B05B 13/0636] by means of rotatable spray heads or nozzles -- A spray apparatus that applies liquids to a workpiece using rotatable spray heads or nozzles, in which the nozzle assembly rotates during operation to create a uniform spray pattern. The classification covers machines that incorporate a drive mechanism for the rotation and feed the fluid to the moving nozzles, distinguishing them from fixed-nozzle spray systems.
[B05B 13/0645] the hollow bodies being rotated during treatment operation  B05B13/0618  takes precedence -- B05B 13/0645 refers to spray apparatus in which hollow bodies such as drums, tubes or similar containers are rotated while the sprayed material is applied to them, covering the treatment of the rotating vessel itself. This subgroup is subordinate to B05B 13/0618, which takes precedence.
[B05B 13/0654] and a treating nozzles being translated through the hollow bodies in a direction essentially parallel to the rotational axis  B05B13/0681  takes precedence -- This classification entry, B05B 13/0654, pertains to machines or plants that apply liquids or other fluent materials by spraying onto surfaces of objects, specifically involving treating nozzles translated through hollow bodies parallel to the rotational axis. However, it notes that a more specific code, B05B 13/0681, takes precedence when applicable.
[B05B 1/3066] the valve element being at least partially hollow and liquid passing through it when the valve is opened -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this place:  The Figure illustrates a spray device with a valve seat (22) located downstream of movable valve element (90), wherein needle (90) comprises an internal channel (94).
[B05B 13/0663] and the hollow bodies being translated in a direction parallel to the rotational axis  B05B13/0681  takes precedence -- This classification code pertains to machines or plants that apply liquids or other fluent materials by spraying onto surfaces of objects, specifically where the hollow bodies are translated parallel to their rotational axis. However, a more specific code (B05B13/0681) takes precedence when applicable.
[B05B 13/0672] and the inclination or the distance of a treating nozzle being modified relative to the rotation axis, e.g. for treating irregular internal surfaces -- The Cooperative Patent Classification code B05B 13/0672 pertains to machines or plants that apply liquids or other fluent materials by spraying, specifically focusing on designs where the inclination or distance of a treating nozzle is adjusted relative to the rotation axis, such as for treating irregular internal surfaces.
[B05B 13/0681] the hollow bodies comprising a closed end to be treated  B05B13/0672  takes precedence -- This subclass covers spraying apparatus that treat hollow bodies having a closed end, such as cleaning, coating, or filling of the interior. It falls under the broader group for machines applying liquids by spraying and is subordinate to B05B13/0672, which takes precedence.
[B05B 13/069] the hollow bodies having a closed end -- B05B 13/069 covers spraying apparatus that incorporates hollow bodies with a closed end, such as closed-ended nozzles or bulbs that form part of the spray generation or distribution system. These components are used to direct or atomise liquids while the closed end prevents direct fluid escape, contributing to a controlled spray pattern.
[B05B 1/3073] the controlling element being a deflector acting as a valve in co-operation with the outlet orifice -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this place:  The Figure illustrates a deflector on a valve body (80), which acts as both a valve and a deflector.
[B05B 1/308] the controlling element comprising both a lift valve and a deflector separated from the lift valve -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this place:  The Figure illustrates a movable controlling element (18) comprising a lift valve (26) and a deflector (29) separated from the lift valve (26), wherein the deflector (29) itself does not act as a valve.
[B05B 1/3086] the controlling element being a grooved body, which is movable in the outlet orifice -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this place:  1a.  Figure 1a illustrates three or more valve bodies (30).  1b.  Figure 1b illustrates a valve body (30) which is provided with grooves (33).  1c.  Figure 1c illustrates the movement of each valve body (30) within a corresponding outlet orifice (42) that controls the amount of water flowing out of the outlet orifice (42).
[B05B 1/3093] Recirculation valves, i.e. the valve element opens a passage to the nozzle and simultaneously closes at least partially a return passage the feeding means -- The CPC entry B05B 1/3093 covers recirculation valves used in spray or atomising devices, where the valve element simultaneously opens a feed passage to the nozzle and partially closes a return passage, thereby regulating the flow of material to the outlet. It falls under the hierarchy of Performing Operations → Spraying or Atomising in General → Spraying Apparatus.
[B05B 1/32] in which a valve member forms part of the outlet opening -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this place:  1a. [B05B 1/323] the valve member being actuated by the pressure of the fluid to be sprayed  single unit outlet valves actuated by the pressure of the fluid to be sprayed  B05B11/0062 -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this place:  1a. [B05B 1/326] Gate valves; Sliding valves; Cocks -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this place:  The Figure illustrates a rotatable outer sleeve (9) closing hole (8), in which the outer sleeve (9) has a first hole (10) that can be located in alignment with a second hole (8) in an inner member (1) by turning the rotatable outer sleeve (9) suitably.
[B05B 1/34] designed to influence the nature of flow of the liquid or other fluent material, e.g. to produce swirl -- B05B 1/34 covers spray-or-atomising nozzles, heads or outlets that incorporate features to modify the fluid flow, such as internal vanes, ribs or rotating elements, in order to create a swirl, turbulence or other flow patterns that improve atomisation.
[B05B 1/3402] to avoid or reduce turbulence, e.g. with fluid flow straightening means -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this place:  1a.  1b.  1c.  Figures 1a, 1b and 1c illustrate fins (20, 30) acting as flow straightening means to reduce turbulence.
[B05B 1/3405] to produce swirl -- This subclass covers spray nozzles or heads that create a rotational, or swirling, flow of the liquid before it exits the outlet, typically by using guide vanes, chambers or other geometry to impart angular momentum, thereby enhancing atomisation or directing the spray pattern.
[B05B 1/341] before discharging the liquid or other fluent material, e.g. in a swirl chamber upstream the spray outlet -- This subclass concerns devices or arrangements that treat the liquid or other fluent material before it leaves the spray outlet, such as swirl chambers, mixing zones or auxiliary elements placed upstream to impart rotation, homogenise or otherwise condition the flow prior to discharge.
[B05B 1/3415] with swirl imparting inserts upstream of the swirl chamber -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this place:  The Figure illustrates an insert inside of a tube (28) that imparts swirl upstream of a swirl chamber.
[B05B 1/3421] with channels emerging substantially tangentially in the swirl chamber -- B05B 1/3421 covers spray nozzles whose fluid supply channels enter the swirl chamber at a substantially tangential angle, creating a swirling motion that improves atomization. Such designs may include auxiliary devices like valves or heating elements.
[B05B 1/3426] the channels emerging in the swirl chamber perpendicularly to the outlet axis  B05B1/3436  takes precedence -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this place:  1a.  1b.  Figures 1a and 1b illustrate channels (16) emerging in a swirl chamber (15) perpendicularly to an axis "a" in Figure 1b.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Channels that are formed at the interface of cooperating elements and the interface is a plane perpendicular to the outlet axis -> B05B1/3436
[B05B 1/3431] the channels being formed at the interface of cooperating elements, e.g. by means of grooves -- This subgroup covers spray nozzles in which flow channels are created at the contact surfaces of moving parts, such as interlocking grooves that form passageways when the elements are assembled. The design uses the interface of cooperating components to define the fluid path, often improving precision or reducing clogging.
[B05B 1/3436] the interface being a plane perpendicular to the outlet axis -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this place:  1a.
