[B05B 1/3442] the interface being a cone having the same axis as the outlet -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this place:  1a.  1b.  Figures 1a and 1b illustrate channels (126) formed at the conical interface between outer member (106) and inner member (102).
[B05B 1/3447] the interface being a cylinder having the same axis as the outlet -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this place:  The Figure illustrates channels (52) formed at the cylinder interface between housing (46) and plug (49) that shares the same axis as the outlet (end of 48).
[B05B 1/3452] the cooperating elements being movable, e.g. adjustable relative to one another -- This subclass concerns spray apparatus whose cooperating elements can be moved relative to each other, such as adjustable nozzles, spray heads or outlet sections. The movable parts allow the spray pattern, flow rate or heating to be varied during operation.
[B05B 1/3457] in response to liquid pressure -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this place:  1a.  1b.  Figures 1a and 1b illustrate a cooperative element (having grooves 50) being movable in response to fluid pressure.
[B05B 1/3463] the channels extending outwardly, e.g. radially from the inside to the outside -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this place:  The Figure illustrates channels (24) extending from inside (30) to the outside of a swirl plate for imparting a swirling motion within swirl chambers (14).
[B05B 1/3468] with means for controlling the flow of liquid entering or leaving the swirl chamber  B05B1/3452  takes precedence -- The subclass B05B 1/3468 covers spray nozzles or heads that incorporate devices for regulating the liquid flow into or out of the swirl chamber, such as adjustable valves or throttling elements. These flow-control features are used to modify spray pattern, droplet size or discharge rate and represent a specific refinement of the broader class B05B 1/3452.
[B05B 1/3473] in response to liquid pressure -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this place:  The Figure illustrates a controlling means (valve 19), which controls a fluid that is entering or leaving a swirl chamber (24a) in response to the fluid (via fluid moving valve 19).
[B05B 1/3478] the liquid flowing at least two different courses before reaching the swirl chamber -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this place:  The Figure illustrates a first path (T1) and a second path (T2) reaching swirl chamber (SC).
[B05B 1/3484] with a return channel extending from the swirl chamber by-passing the swirl imparting means -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this place:  The Figure illustrates channels (172, 174) that bypass the swirl imparting means (248, 238) from a swirl chamber (132) back to a supply flow (178) toward the right side and the return flow (174). Further aspects include: fuel delivered to furnace toward the right side beyond the fuel outlet (150), atomizer spray plate (130), retaining nut (110) and backplate (170).
[B05B 1/3489] Nozzles having concentric outlets -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this place:  The Figure illustrates a spray nozzle having three concentric outlets (O1, O2, O3) dispensing a swirling discharge from vanes (21, 22).
[B05B 1/3494] the discharge outlet being not on the axis of the swirl chamber -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this place:  The Figure illustrates an outlet (1) on a different axis than that of a swirl chamber (12).
[B05B 1/36] Outlets for discharging by overflow -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Nozzles, spray heads or other outlets for discharging by overflow. For example, an outlet having a trough, open tank or holder type fluid handling means, which depends for its distributing function upon accumulated fluid running over a top or an edge of a retaining wall or through a depression or notch in a wall.  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this place:  1a.  1b.  Figures 1a and 1b illustrate an outlet (19) discharging by overflow of water (from 21).
[B05B 14/00] Arrangements for collecting, re-using or eliminating excess spraying material -- B05B 14/00 covers devices that are incorporated into spray or atomising equipment to capture, recycle or dispose of surplus material generated during operation. It includes collection chambers, filters, separators, recirculation loops and venting systems that prevent waste, reduce environmental impact and improve efficiency of spraying processes.
[B05B 14/10] the excess material being particulate -- This subclass concerns spray apparatus that include features for handling excess spray material when it is in particulate form, such as filters, cyclones, collection chambers or other means that capture, recycle or dispose of the overspray particles.
[B05B 14/20] from moving belts, e.g. filtering belts or conveying belts -- This subclass covers spraying systems that incorporate moving belts, such as filter or conveyor belts, to capture, transport, and possibly reuse or dispose of overspray or excess material. The belts act as a continuous medium for separating the sprayed product from the air and directing it away from the work area.
[B05B 14/30] comprising enclosures close to, or in contact with, the object to be sprayed and surrounding or confining the discharged spray or jet but not the object to be sprayed -- This subclass deals with spray apparatuses that include enclosures placed close to or in contact with the workpiece, which surround or contain the spray or jet and any excess material without enclosing the object itself, facilitating collection, reuse or elimination of surplus spray.
[B05B 14/40] for use in spray booths -- B05B 14/40 covers arrangements within spray booths that collect, recycle or eliminate excess spraying material such as overspray, drips or vapors. The entry includes filtration, recovery or disposal systems designed to keep the booth environment clean and reduce waste.
[B05B 14/41] by cleaning the walls of the booth -- The B05B 14/41 subclass covers devices or methods that clean the walls of a spray booth to manage excess spray material. It includes arrangements that collect, reuse or eliminate overspray by removing it from the booth surfaces, thereby improving material recovery and hygiene. This falls under spraying apparatus within the broader category of performing operations.
[B05B 14/412] wherein the walls of the booth is perforated or porous walls and the walls are cleaned of or prevented from being contacted with excess material by a flow of fluid, e.g. air or water, directed into the booth -- The B05B 14/412 subclass covers spray booths whose walls are perforated or made of porous material, and where a directed flow of fluid-such as air or water-carries away or blocks excess spray from contacting the walls, thereby collecting, re-using, or eliminating the surplus material.
[B05B 14/42] using electrostatic means -- This CPC subclass B05B 14/42 deals with spray apparatus that uses electrostatic means to collect, reuse, or eliminate excess spray material. It covers arrangements that apply electrostatic charging or attraction to control overspray, improve deposition efficiency, and reduce waste.
[B05B 14/43] by filtering the air charged with excess material -- This CPC subclass concerns spray devices that incorporate a system for filtering the airstream that carries excess material, allowing the collection, reuse or removal of that material. It falls under the broader categories of performing operations, spraying or atomising in general, and spray apparatus.
[B05B 14/435] with means for cleaning the filters by gas flow, e.g. blasts of air -- This subclass covers spray devices equipped with a mechanism that cleans the filters by forcing a gas, typically air, through them in bursts. The gas flow removes collected spray residues, allowing the filter to be regenerated without disassembly.
[B05B 14/437] with means for introducing solid material into the air charged with excess material for preventing clogging of the filter -- B05B 14/437 covers spray devices that incorporate a system to feed solid particles into the airstream carrying surplus spray, thereby preventing filter blockage. The solid material mixes with the excess liquid or aerosol, facilitating its removal or reuse and keeping the filtering element clear.
[B05B 14/44] using walls specially adapted for promoting separation of the excess material from the air, e.g. baffle plates -- It covers devices that incorporate specially shaped walls or baffle plates within a spraying system to separate and collect excess spray material from the airstream, enhancing material recovery or reducing emissions. The walls are designed to promote separation of overspray from the carrier air before it leaves the apparatus.
[B05B 14/45] using cyclone separators -- B05B 14/45 covers spraying apparatus that incorporate cyclone separators to handle surplus spray liquid. The cyclone creates a centrifugal airflow that forces droplets to the outer wall, allowing the collected material to be removed, reused, or otherwise eliminated, thereby improving efficiency and reducing waste.
[B05B 14/46] by washing the air charged with excess material -- B05B 14/46 designates spray equipment incorporating means to collect, reuse or eliminate excess spray by washing the airstream that carries the surplus material. This subcategory falls under spraying or atomising in general, specifically focusing on apparatuses that treat the spray-laden air to recover or remove the surplus.
[B05B 14/462] and separating the excess material from the washing liquid, e.g. for recovery -- This subclass covers features in spray devices that incorporate mechanisms to capture overspray or excess liquid, separate it from the washing fluid and permit its reuse or disposal, such as separators, filters or collection chambers integrated into the apparatus.
[B05B 14/463] by means of ultrafiltration -- The B05B 14/463 subclass covers spray-applying equipment that incorporates ultrafiltration to separate and recover excess liquid from the spray stream. By passing the overspray through a membrane filter, the system removes suspended particles, allowing the filtrate to be reused or safely discarded.
[B05B 14/465] using substantially vertical liquid curtains or wetted walls behind the object to be sprayed -- The entry B05B 14/465 covers spray-painting or coating systems that employ substantially vertical liquid curtains or wetted walls positioned behind the target object. These curtains capture excess spray material, allowing it to be collected, reused, or eliminated, thereby improving efficiency and reducing waste.
[B05B 14/468] with scrubbing means arranged below the booth floor -- B05B 14/468 covers spraying apparatus that incorporates a scrubbing system positioned beneath the booth floor to capture, treat and possibly recycle overspray or excess material. The arrangement removes contaminants from the airflow before discharge, improving environmental control and material efficiency.
[B05B 14/469] wherein the washing material is the spraying material -- The code B05B 14/469 covers spray devices in which the material being sprayed also serves as the washing medium, so the apparatus integrates the application and cleaning functions by reusing or eliminating excess spray fluid during operation.
[B05B 14/48] specially adapted for particulate material -- This subclass covers spray devices that include means to capture or reclaim the overspray when the material being atomized is in particulate form, such as powders or granules. It falls under spraying or atomising in general, specifically spray apparatus with arrangements for collecting, re-using or eliminating excess material.
[B05B 14/49] specially adapted for solvents -- B05B 14/49 covers spraying apparatus that are specially adapted for use with solvents, including features that collect, reuse or dispose of excess spraying material to minimise waste and improve safety during solvent-based atomising operations.
[B05B 15/00] Details of spraying plant or spraying apparatus not otherwise provided for -- The subclass B05B 15/00 covers specific features of spraying plants or apparatus that are not covered in any other subclass, such as particular arrangements, feed mechanisms, auxiliary components or distinctive configurations of a sprayer. It applies to devices for atomising or spraying liquids, gases or powders where the details of the plant are otherwise uncategorised.
[B05B 15/14] Arrangements for preventing or controlling structural damage to spraying apparatus or its outlets, e.g. for breaking at desired places -- This subclass covers features incorporated in spray devices that either guard against unintended structural failure or deliberately allow controlled rupture of the apparatus or its nozzles, such as breakaway couplings or sacrificial sections.
[B05B 15/16] for preventing non-intended contact between spray heads or nozzles and foreign bodies, e.g. nozzle guards -- This subclass covers features that keep spray heads or nozzles from non-intended contact with foreign objects, such as guards, shields, or other protective elements that prevent the outlet from being struck or blocked. It is used to avoid clogging, damage, or contamination by physically separating the spray tip from unwanted material.
[B05B 15/18] for improving resistance to wear, e.g. inserts or coatings -- B05B 15/18 covers features of spraying equipment designed to increase wear-resistance, such as the use of protective inserts or surface coatings on components that are subject to abrasion.
[B05B 15/20] Arrangements for agitating the material to be sprayed, e.g. for stirring, mixing or homogenising -- B05B 15/20 covers designs within spraying equipment that incorporate means for agitating the material to be sprayed, such as stirring, mixing or homogenising mechanisms, to ensure uniform distribution before atomisation. This sub-category falls under spraying apparatus in the broader field of performing operations and spraying or atomising in general.
[B05B 15/25] using moving elements, e.g. rotating blades -- This subclass covers spray devices that create or direct a spray by means of moving parts such as rotating blades, impellers or fans. It includes apparatus where the motion of these elements atomises the fluid, rather than static nozzles, and any details not covered by other more specific categories.
[B05B 15/30] Dip tubes -- The B05B 15/30 subclass covers dip tubes, which are elongated members that extend into a liquid reservoir or tank to draw the fluid upward into a spray or atomising device. It pertains to details of spraying plants or apparatus where the tube's design, positioning or connection is not otherwise specified in broader categories.
[B05B 15/33] Weighted -- B05B 15/33 covers spraying or atomising equipment that incorporates a weighting system, such as a ballast or counterweight, to stabilise the nozzle, adjust flow or improve spray distribution. The classification applies to any weighted spraying apparatus whose specific design features are not otherwise covered by more detailed subclasses.
[B05B 15/37] with decorative elements -- The B05B 15/37 subclass concerns spraying or atomising equipment that includes decorative elements such as ornamental shapes, patterns, colours or finishes, where the functional details of the device are not otherwise specified in the classification. It is a narrow category within spraying apparatus under the broader groups for performing operations and spraying in general.
[B05B 15/40] Filters located upstream of the spraying outlets -- B05B 15/40 covers the use of filters positioned before the spray outlets in spraying equipment, aimed at removing particulates or contamination from the fluid prior to atomisation. The classification applies to any arrangement where filtration is integrated upstream of nozzles, regardless of the specific filter type or spray system.
[B05B 15/50] Arrangements for cleaning -- B05B 15/50 covers arrangements for cleaning spraying equipment, including built-in devices that flush, wash or otherwise remove residues from nozzles, hoses or chambers. The code applies to self-cleaning mechanisms, cleaning cycles and auxiliary components that maintain performance of spray apparatus.
[B05B 15/52] for removal of clogging particles -- The subclass covers spray equipment that incorporates means for clearing clogs, such as removing particles that block nozzles, filters or delivery lines. It includes cleaning, back-flushing or mechanical removal features that are not described in other more specific subclasses.
[B05B 15/522] using cleaning elements penetrating the discharge openings -- B05B 15/522 relates to spray apparatus in which cleaning components are introduced into the discharge openings of the nozzle or sprayer to remove deposits, blockages or residues. The classification covers methods and devices that insert brushes, rods, wires or other cleaning elements through the spray orifice for in-situ maintenance, ensuring consistent atomisation and preventing clogging.
[B05B 15/5223] the cleaning element, e.g. a needle, and the discharge opening being movable relative to each other in a direction substantially parallel to the flow of liquid or other fluent material through said opening -- This subclass covers spraying devices in which a cleaning element such as a needle and the discharge opening can move relative to each other in a direction substantially parallel to the flow of liquid or other fluent material through the opening, enabling in-situ cleaning of the nozzle.
[B05B 15/5225] the cleaning element being located upstream of the discharge opening or being actuated upstream therefrom -- The code B05B 15/5225 covers spraying apparatus in which a cleaning element is positioned before the discharge opening or is operated from a point upstream of that opening, ensuring the spray path is cleared before liquid is emitted. This subgroup falls under spraying or atomising in general, specifically detailing cleaning arrangements that are not covered by other more specific provisions.
[B05B 15/525] by increasing the cross section of the discharge openings -- This CPC subclass covers spraying devices that enlarge the flow by widening the discharge openings, thereby altering the spray pattern or amount delivered. It pertains to apparatus that increase the cross-section of the spray orifices, often to improve coverage, reduce pressure, or adjust droplet size.
[B05B 15/528] by resilient deformation of the nozzle -- This subclass covers spray devices that generate a mist by flexing or elastically deforming the nozzle during operation, so that the nozzle shape changes to create the atomising action. It includes designs where the deformation is achieved by pressure, spring bias or inherent material elasticity.
[B05B 15/531] using backflow -- This subclass concerns spraying devices that employ backflow of the medium to aid or control atomisation, for example using a reverse flow through a nozzle or conduit to draw additional material into the spray or to regulate pressure. It includes any arrangement where the fluid or gas returns partially toward the spray source as part of the operation.
[B05B 15/534] by reversing the nozzle relative to the supply conduit -- This subclass covers spray apparatus where the nozzle is oriented opposite the supply conduit, causing the fluid to reverse direction before atomisation. Reversing the nozzle alters flow dynamics, pressure recovery, and droplet formation, and is used in specialized spraying systems.
[B05B 15/55] using cleaning fluids -- B05B 15/55 covers spraying devices that apply cleaning fluids, where the particular construction of the apparatus is not detailed in any other more specific subclass. It includes various pump, nozzle and distribution mechanisms designed to atomise liquids for cleaning purposes.
[B05B 15/555] discharged by cleaning nozzles -- B05B 15/555 covers spraying devices whose discharge is obtained by a cleaning action on the nozzle, typically where the flow of liquid is induced when the nozzle is cleared of deposits. This subcategory falls under spraying apparatus in the broader group of spraying or atomising operations.
[B05B 15/557] the cleaning fluid being a mixture of gas and liquid -- B05B 15/557 covers spraying apparatus that use a cleaning fluid composed of both gas and liquid phases, such as foams, aerosols or mist mixtures, for the purpose of cleaning surfaces or equipment. The entry applies when the fluid's gas-liquid composition is a defining feature of the device.
[B05B 15/58] preventing deposits, drying-out or blockage by recirculating the fluid to be sprayed from upstream of the discharge opening back to the supplying means -- The B05B 15/58 subclass covers spray devices that incorporate a recirculation system to return fluid from upstream of the nozzle back to the supply, thereby preventing deposits, drying or blockage of the discharge opening. This feature is used to keep the spraying plant clear and maintain consistent operation.
[B05B 15/60] Arrangements for mounting, supporting or holding spraying apparatus -- This subclass covers designs and configurations that secure spraying devices to a structure, including brackets, frames or holders that position the spray equipment for operation, and details of how the apparatus is attached or supported.
[B05B 15/62] Arrangements for supporting spraying apparatus, e.g. suction cups -- The subclass B05B 15/62 concerns arrangements for supporting spraying apparatus, such as suction cups or other mounting devices, that hold the equipment in place while it operates.
[B05B 15/622] ground-penetrating -- The B05B 15/622 subclass covers spraying apparatus that penetrates the ground to deliver liquids or gases below the surface, such as equipment for injecting pesticides, fertilizers, seed treatments or remediation agents directly into soil. It includes designs and details of the ground-penetrating elements, delivery mechanisms and control features not otherwise classified in more specific spray-equipment categories.
[B05B 15/625] designed to be placed on the ground -- The B05B 15/625 subclass covers spraying or atomising equipment that is intended to be installed directly on the ground rather than mounted on a vehicle, pole or handheld frame. It includes ground-based spray rigs, stationary nozzles, spreaders and other apparatus whose primary support structure is fixed to the ground, with no further detail on the spraying mechanism.
[B05B 15/628] of variable length -- The Cooperative Patent Classification entry B05B 15/628 pertains to details of spraying plant or apparatus that are not otherwise categorized, specifically addressing components or systems of variable length. This classification is used for inventions involving adjustable parts in spraying equipment.
[B05B 15/63] Handgrips -- The subclass B05B 15/63 covers handgrips that are part of spraying devices, describing their form, attachment, adjustment and ergonomic features for safely holding and controlling the spray apparatus during operation.
[B05B 15/65] Mounting arrangements for fluid connection of the spraying apparatus or its outlets to flow conduits -- B05B 15/65 covers mounting arrangements that connect the fluid lines of a spraying device or its outlets to supply or discharge conduits. It includes fittings, brackets, hoses and other structures that secure the spray apparatus to fluid sources, but does not cover other aspects of the spray system.
[B05B 15/652] whereby the jet can be oriented -- This subclass covers spraying devices whose jet can be oriented, meaning the direction of the spray can be adjusted or pivoted to target a specific area. It applies to apparatus with mechanisms for steering, rotating or otherwise directing the liquid jet during operation.
[B05B 15/654] using universal joints -- B05B 15/654 covers the incorporation of universal joints in spray devices to transmit rotary motion between misaligned shafts, allowing flexible positioning of nozzles or pumps. The use of such joints facilitates angular adjustment and vibration reduction in spraying plants where direct coupling is impractical.
[B05B 15/656] whereby the flow conduit length is changeable -- B05B 15/656 covers spraying devices in which the length of the liquid flow conduit can be varied, allowing the operator to adjust the distance between the pump and the spray nozzle or manipulate spray pressure and pattern. This feature is used to adapt the apparatus to different operating conditions or target areas. The classification falls under spraying apparatus - within the broader category of performing operations.
[B05B 15/658] the spraying apparatus or its outlet axis being perpendicular to the flow conduit -- B05B 15/658 covers spray devices whose discharge outlet is arranged at a right-angle to the supply conduit, meaning the nozzle or pipe terminus is perpendicular to the flow. This configuration produces a lateral spray pattern useful in painting, cooling, pesticide application and similar processes.
[B05B 15/68] Arrangements for adjusting the position of spray heads -- This classification covers devices that allow the spray heads of a spraying apparatus to be moved or angled to achieve desired coverage, including mechanisms for pivoting, shifting or tilting the nozzles. It falls under arrangements for adjusting the position of spray heads within the broader group of spraying apparatus.
[B05B 15/70] Arrangements for moving spray heads automatically to or from the working position -- The B05B 15/70 subclass concerns mechanisms that automatically shift spray heads between their operational and idle positions. It falls under the broader categories of performing operations, spraying or atomising in general, and spraying apparatus, and addresses arrangements for moving the heads without manual intervention.
[B05B 15/72] using hydraulic or pneumatic means -- It covers spraying devices that use hydraulic or pneumatic power to generate the spray, detailing the construction and operation of such apparatus when not specified elsewhere. The entry falls under spraying or atomising in general, specifically within the spraying apparatus subclass.
[B05B 15/74] driven by the discharged fluid -- B05B 15/74 covers spraying equipment whose operation is powered directly by the fluid that is discharged, so the flow of the spray itself drives mechanisms such as pumps, nozzles or moving parts. This class is used for devices where the propelling fluid provides the motive force rather than an external motor.
[B05B 15/80] Arrangements in which the spray area is not enclosed, e.g. spray tables -- The code B05B 15/80 covers spray apparatus in which the spray zone is open rather than enclosed, such as spray tables where material is projected onto a work surface without a surrounding chamber. It pertains to arrangements that allow free-air dispersion of the spray, used in painting, coating or cleaning operations where an open area is required.
[B05B 16/00] Spray booths -- Spray booths are enclosed workspaces designed for applying liquids or powders to objects by spraying, while containing overspray and fumes within the enclosure. They incorporate ventilation, filtration and sometimes heating or cooling to protect operators, maintain product quality and meet environmental or safety regulations.
[B05B 16/20] Arrangements for spraying in combination with other operations, e.g. drying -- The B05B 16/20 subclass covers apparatus that integrates spraying with additional processes, such as drying, within a single system. It includes configurations like spray booths where the sprayed material is immediately treated by another operation to improve efficiency or product quality. This combination of functions is designed to streamline manufacturing steps that involve applying a liquid or powder and then rapidly curing or drying it.
[B05B 16/25] for both automatic and manual spraying -- The B05B 16/25 subclass includes spray booths that are equipped to handle both automatic and manual spraying operations. These enclosures provide controlled airflow, filtration and lighting to contain overspray while allowing flexible access for hand-held or mechanized spray devices in painting, coating and similar processes.
[B05B 16/40] Construction elements specially adapted therefor, e.g. floors, walls or ceilings -- B05B 16/40 pertains to construction elements specially adapted for spray booths, including floors, walls or ceilings that are designed to contain, control or withstand spray emissions and facilitate cleaning. It falls under the hierarchy of performing operations, spraying or atomising in general, and spray apparatus.
[B05B 16/405] Partly or totally cylindrical walls; Round floors -- The subclass B05B 16/405 covers spray-booth enclosures whose walls are at least partly cylindrical and may have a round floor, providing a circular or semi-circular interior shape that helps contain overspray and promote uniform airflow. It applies to designs where the booth's geometry is fully or partially round, optimizing space usage and airflow patterns for painting, coating or other atomising operations.
[B05B 16/60] Ventilation arrangements specially adapted therefor -- B05B 16/60 relates to ventilation arrangements specially adapted for spray booths, covering the design of airflow paths, exhaust and filtration systems that control overspray, fumes and particles to ensure operator safety and product quality.
[B05B 16/80] Movable spray booths -- B05B 16/80 pertains to movable spray booths, which are portable enclosures designed to contain and exhaust mist generated during spraying or atomising processes. These booths can be relocated or transported to different work stations and typically incorporate ventilation and filtration systems to protect workers and the surrounding environment.
[B05B 16/90] comprising conveying means for moving objects or other work to be sprayed in and out of the booth, e.g. through the booth -- A spray-booth arrangement that includes conveying means to transport objects or workpieces into and out of the booth for coating, allowing continuous feeding and removal during the spraying process.
[B05B 16/95] the objects or other work to be sprayed lying on, or being held above the conveying means, i.e. not hanging from the conveying means -- B05B 16/95 relates to spray-booth arrangements in which the objects or workpieces to be coated are positioned on a conveyor or supported above it, rather than being hung from the conveying system, covering fixtures and methods for holding items during spraying operations.
[B05B 17/00] Apparatus for spraying or atomising liquids or other fluent materials, not covered by the preceding groups -- B05B 17/00 covers devices that spray or atomise liquids, pastes or other fluid materials when they do not fit into any of the more specific groups listed earlier in the hierarchy. It includes general-purpose or unconventional spray mechanisms that fall outside the defined sub-categories for spraying apparatus.
[B05B 17/04] operating with special methods -- The B05B 17/04 subgroup includes spray or atomising apparatus that use special or unconventional operating methods, such as pulsed, ultrasonic, electro-static, or other distinctive techniques. It covers devices that fall outside the more general spray-equipment groups defined in the hierarchy.
[B05B 17/06] using ultrasonic vibrations -- Apparatus for spraying or atomising liquids that employ ultrasonic vibrations to generate a fine mist, using high-frequency sound waves to break the liquid into droplets without conventional nozzles. This method falls under CPC B05B 17/06 within the broader group for spraying apparatus.
[B05B 17/0607] generated by electrical means, e.g. piezoelectric transducers -- Apparatus for spraying or atomising liquids that uses an electrical drive such as a piezoelectric transducer to create the spray. The device typically vibrates a nozzle or membrane to form fine droplets without moving mechanical parts.
[B05B 17/0615] spray being produced at the free surface of the liquid or other fluent material in a container and subjected to the vibrations -- B05B 17/0615 covers spraying devices that create a spray at the free surface of a liquid or other fluent material contained in a vessel, using vibration to atomise the material. It falls under the broader categories of performing operations, spraying or atomising in general, and spraying apparatus.
[B05B 17/0623] coupled with a vibrating horn -- Apparatus for spraying or atomising liquids in which a vibrating horn is integrated to produce the atomising action, typically by imparting high-frequency oscillations to the fluid stream. The horn may be coupled to a nozzle or other delivery element to generate fine droplets for coating, cooling or dispensing applications. This subgroup covers devices not classified in more specific preceding groups.
[B05B 17/063] having an internal channel for supplying the liquid or other fluent material -- B05B 17/063 covers spraying or atomising devices that incorporate an internal channel through which the liquid or other fluent material is delivered to the spray outlet, distinguishing them from designs that feed the material externally. This subclass falls under spraying apparatus within the broader category of performing operations and spraying or atomising in general.
[B05B 17/0638] spray being produced by discharging the liquid or other fluent material through a plate comprising a plurality of orifices -- This subclass covers devices that produce a spray by forcing a liquid or other fluent material through a plate containing multiple orifices, creating a pattern of jets or droplets. The plate may be perforated or fitted with nozzles and is used in applications such as coating, cooling or atomising.
[B05B 17/0646] Vibrating plates, i.e. plates being directly subjected to the vibrations, e.g. having a piezoelectric transducer attached thereto -- Vibrating plates are spray or atomising devices in which a plate is made to oscillate, often by a piezoelectric transducer, to generate a fine mist of liquid or other fluid. The vibration directly drives the formation of droplets, allowing precise control of spray characteristics without moving nozzles.
[B05B 17/0653] Details -- B05B 17/0653 pertains to specific details of apparatus for spraying or atomising liquids or other fluent materials, excluding those categorized in prior groups. This classification focuses on particular components or aspects that enhance functionality or efficiency in the spraying process.
[B05B 17/0661] Transducer materials -- This subclass covers the substances and components that form the transducer in spray or atomising devices, including piezoelectric ceramics, magnetostrictive alloys and other materials that convert electrical energy into the mechanical motion needed to generate a spray. It falls under spray apparatus not otherwise classified.
[B05B 17/0669] Excitation frequencies -- The code covers spray or atomising devices whose operation relies on a defined excitation frequency to generate the fluid jet, such as ultrasonic or piezo-electric emitters where the frequency of vibration determines droplet formation, size or spray pattern. It includes any apparatus that specifies or varies these frequencies for liquid or other fluent material atomisation.
[B05B 17/0676] Feeding means -- Feeding means are components that supply liquids or other fluent materials to a spray or atomising device, ensuring continuous flow from a source to the nozzle or atomiser. They include pumps, pressure vessels, conduits and control valves that are not covered by more specific groups within the spraying apparatus classification.
[B05B 17/0684] Wicks or the like -- Wicks or the like are components that deliver liquid to a spray or atomising device by capillary action, including wicks, porous plugs, saturated fibers or similar structures that draw fluid from a reservoir to the discharge zone for aerosol formation.
[B05B 17/0692] generated by a fluid  B05B17/0607  takes precedence -- This CPC subclass covers spraying or atomising apparatus in which the spray is generated by a fluid rather than by mechanical means such as a pump or nozzle. It applies when the device does not fall under any more-specific group and is subordinated to B05B 17/0607, which takes precedence.
[B05B 17/08] Fountains -- B05B 17/08 covers fountain devices that project, spray or circulate water for decorative or ornamental purposes. The classification includes mechanisms that create artistic water displays, such as tiered jets, cascading streams or rotating sprays, which are not included in more specific spray-apparatus groups.
[B05B 17/085] designed to produce sheets or curtains of liquid, e.g. water walls -- The B05B 17/085 group covers spray or atomising devices specifically engineered to generate continuous sheets or curtains of liquid, such as water-wall installations. These apparatus create planar flows of fluid for cooling, cleaning, visual effects, or fire-suppression purposes.
[B05B 3/00] Spraying or sprinkling apparatus with moving outlet elements or moving deflecting elements -- The subclass B05B 3/00 covers spraying or sprinkling devices in which the outlet openings or deflecting elements move, such as rotating or oscillating nozzles, pivoting plates or adjustable vanes that alter the direction or pattern of the spray.
[B05B 3/001] incorporating means for heating or cooling, e.g. the material to be sprayed -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this place:  The Figure illustrates shaping air (10) used as cooling air passing in ducts (20) to cool a rotor (13) and a stator (12).
[B05B 3/002] comprising a moving member supported by a fluid cushion -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this place:  The Figure illustrates an annular interface (7) in between a housing (C) and shaft (A) sized so as to minimize leakage while still allowing rotation of the shaft (A) with a slight cushion of liquid. Passages (20) enable liquid to enter chamber (21). Liquid pressure upon surface (23) creates a thrust force capable of countering the input force applied by the liquid onto an inlet end (6).
[B05B 3/003] with braking means, e.g. friction rings designed to provide a substantially constant revolution speed -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this place:  The Figure illustrates a frustoconical brake pad (1030) being part of a brake disposed in deflector (1008), which maintains rotation of the deflector (1008) at a relatively constant speed irrespective of flow-rate, fluid pressure or temperature. The brake includes brake pad (1030) sandwiched in between a friction disk (1028, above brake pad 1030) and seal retainer (1032, below brake pad 1030). Friction disk (1028) is held relatively stationary by shaft (1020), while seal retainer (1032) rotates with the deflector (1008). During operation of nozzle (1000), seal retainer (1032) is urged upwardly against brake pad (1030), which results in a variable frictional resistance that maintains a relatively constant rotational speed of deflector (1008), irrespective of the rate of fluid flow, fluid pressure or operating temperature.
[B05B 3/005] using viscous dissipation, e.g. a rotor movable in a chamber filled with oil -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this place:  The Figure illustrates how rotation of deflector (102) will be slowed by the viscous shearing of fluid (110) in between stator (124 on central shaft 112) and the deflector wall forming a chamber (108).
[B05B 3/006] using induced currents; using magnetic means -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this place:  The Figure illustrates magnets (5) being used to dampen rotation of distributor (2).
[B05B 3/007] with friction clutch means -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this place:  1a.  1b.  1c.  Figures 1a, 1b and 1c illustrate a sprinkler (100). The water flow through sprinkler (100) is adjusted by aligning spaces or apertures (130A) formed by throttle plate (130) with apertures (124B) in drive plate (124). Therefore, increasing alignment of apertures (130A, 124B) increases the flow out of sprinkler (100), while decreasing alignment of apertures (130A, 124B) decreases the flow. Throttle plate (130) is located below drive plate (124) and includes centre aperture (130B that engages with mating lower end (116A) of flow adjustment shaft (116). In this respect, rotating flow adjustment shaft (116) also rotates throttle plate (130) relative to drive plate (124). Throttle plate (130) is frictionally engaged to the bottom of drive plate (124), rotating throttle plate (130) with drive plate (124). Additionally, the flow of water through sprinkler (100) may cause slight movement and pressure of throttle plate (130) upwards against drive plate (124), further increasing friction. The frictional or clutching force between throttle plate (130) and drive plate (124) is such that it can be overcome when the user adjusts flow adjustment member (112) and therefore the flow of sprinkler (100).
[B05B 3/008] comprising a wobbling or nutating element, e.g. rotating about an axis describing a cone during spraying  B05B3/043  takes precedence -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this place:  The Figure illustrates a water deflection plate (28) carried by a starter sleeve (44) and a hanger tube (26) for wobbling or nutating motion.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Rotor nozzles -> B05B3/043
[B05B 3/02] with rotating elements -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this place:  The Figure illustrates an electric motor (8) connected to a reduction gear (6) that is in mesh with a tooth wheel (20). Operation of the motor (8) rotates part (22) and nozzle (12).  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Electric spraying discharge apparatus characterised by having rotary outlet or deflecting elements -> B05B5/04
[B05B 3/0202] being deflecting elements  the liquid driven rotor being a deflecting rotating element  B05B3/0426 ; discharging over substantially the whole periphery of the rotating member  B05B3/10 -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this place:  The Figure illustrates a deflecting rotating element (27).  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Spraying or sprinkling apparatus with moving outlet elements or moving deflecting elements driven by the liquid or other fluent material discharged, comprising a liquid driven rotor, actuated downstream of the outlet elements, and the liquid driven rotor being a deflecting rotating element -> B05B3/0426 - Spraying or sprinkling apparatus with moving outlet elements or moving deflecting elements discharging over substantially the whole periphery of the rotating member -> B05B3/10
[B05B 3/0204] being a ventilator or fan -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this place:  The Figure illustrates a fan assembly (14) having blades (54) that deflect a stream (32).
[B05B 3/021] with means for regulating the jet relative to the horizontal angular position of the nozzle, e.g. for spraying non-circular areas by changing the elevation of the nozzle or by varying the nozzle flow-rate  when the rotating elements are rotated by the liquid or other fluent material discharged  B05B3/0453 -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative examples of subject matter classified in this place:  1a.  1b.  Figures 1a and 1b illustrate rotating jet arms (102a, 102b) of a sprinkler adjusting an angle of inclination (104) of nozzles (106), which is inversely proportional to the area of coverage of sprinkler (100), the rotation being driven by reaction of the water discharged nozzles (106) on the respective nozzle body.  2.  Figure 2 illustrates the angular position of the outlet element (20) that allows for the rotating impact sprinkler to spray noncircular areas.  3.  Figure 3 illustrates a percussive sprinkler (A) having a main body (30) that is rotated with vertical variation, a sprinkler head driving rod (50) shifting vertically along a circumferential path on a driving rod retaining surface (21, shaped for vertical variation during rotation) of an adjusting ring, so that the sprinkler head (33) is driven to swing vertically with changes of the discharge angle.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Spraying or sprinkling apparatus with moving outlet elements or moving deflecting elements driven by the liquid or other fluent material discharged, comprising a liquid driven rotor with automatic means for regulating the discharged jet relative to the angular position of the outlet elements or to the direction of rotation of the outlet elements -> B05B3/0453
[B05B 3/025] Rotational joints -- The B05B 3/025 subclass covers spray or sprinkling devices that incorporate rotational joints to provide movement of outlet or deflecting elements. These joints allow the nozzle or spray arm to rotate, enabling adjustable spray patterns or directions while maintaining a sealed fluid path during operation. This design is used in equipment where precise, variable atomisation is required.
[B05B 3/026] the fluid passing axially from one joint element to another -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative examples of subject matter classified in this place:  1.  Figure 1 illustrates a rotational joint between a fixed tube (5) that contains a rotary bearing on the end connected to rotating tube (310).  2.  Figure 2 illustrates fluid passing from (42') into (42) to be sprayed (18), passing axially from the bottom member to that of the nozzle member (14), both elements being jointed to one another via bearing sleeve (40) so that the spray (18) sweeps over a surface due to rotation about central axis (26).
[B05B 3/027] with radial fluid passages -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this place:  The Figure illustrates rotational joints of arms (102a, 102b) having inner radial fluid passages extending from the central fluid passage (120) to the outlets of holes (106).
[B05B 3/028] the rotation being orbital  comprising liquid driven rotors wherein the movement of the outlet elements are a combination of two movements, one being rotational  B05B3/0444 ; when the rotating elements are rotated by jet reaction of the liquid or other fluent material discharged  B05B3/066 -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this place:  The Figure illustrates a nozzle (25) rotating about both axes (X) and (Z).  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Spraying or sprinkling apparatus with rotating outlet elements driven by the liquid or other fluent material discharged, the movement of the outlet elements being a combination of two movements, one being rotational -> B05B3/0444 - Spraying or sprinkling apparatus with rotating elements, driven by the liquid or other fluent material discharged by jet reaction, the movement of the outlet elements being a combination of two movements, one being rotational -> B05B3/066
[B05B 3/04] driven by the liquid or other fluent material discharged, e.g. the liquid actuating a motor before passing to the outlet -- Group B05B3/04 is impacted by reclassification into groups B05B3/0417 and B05B1/085 . All groups listed in this Warning should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[B05B 3/0412] comprising a liquid driven piston motor -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative examples of subject matter classified in this place:  1.  Figure 1 illustrates a sprinkler device (10) having a helical track (106) and a tracking element (112) coupled between a reciprocating piston (66) and a rotating head or nozzle (98) to convert the reciprocal linear motion of the piston to rotation motion of the head or nozzle.  2.  Figure 2 illustrates rotating nozzles (7) actuated by piston (6).
[B05B 3/0417] comprising a liquid driven rotor, e.g. a turbine -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this place:  The Figure illustrates nozzles (5) rotated by rotor (4) via gears (61) and (62,72), wherein rotor (4) is driven by water.
[B05B 3/0419] the liquid or other fluent material powering several rotors -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this place:  The Figure illustrates several rotors (1) driven by water, wherein each rotor (1) actuates the rotation of a corresponding nozzle (2).
[B05B 3/0421] provided with means for preventing rotation -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this place:  1a.  1b.  Figures 1a and 1b illustrate a stop (410) to disable rotation of rotor (380), wherein rotation of rotor (380, in Figure 1b) drives rotation of nozzle (113, in Figure 1a).
[B05B 3/0423] the rotor axis not being parallel to the rotation axis of the moving outlet elements, e.g. being perpendicular thereto -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative examples of subject matter classified in this place:  The Figure illustrates rotation of a rotor (42) not being parallel to the rotation axis of an outlet element (22a, 22b, 24).
[B05B 3/0425] actuated downstream of the outlet elements -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this place:  The Figure illustrates a rotor (8) actuated downstream of nozzle (4) outlet, wherein the rotor (8) actuates rotation of the nozzle (4).
[B05B 3/0426] the liquid driven rotor being a deflecting rotating element -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this place:  The Figure illustrates a liquid driven rotor (2) that is a deflecting rotating element.
[B05B 3/0429] the rotating outlet elements being directly attached to the rotor or being an integral part thereof -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this place:  The Figure illustrates a rotor (31) directly attached on the outside of a nozzle (16) supply.
[B05B 3/043] Rotor nozzles -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Nozzles consisting of an element having an upstream part rotated by the liquid flow and a downstream part connected to the apparatus by a universal joint.  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this place:  The Figure illustrates a rotor nozzle having an upstream part rotated inside a housing (11) and a downstream part connected to universal joint (23).
[B05B 3/0432] the rotation of the outlet elements being reversible  B05B3/0444  takes precedence -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this place:  1a.  1b.  Figures 1a and 1b illustrate rotation of a rotor (380) that causes oscillation of a nozzle (113).  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Spraying or sprinkling apparatus with rotating outlet elements driven by the liquid or other fluent material discharged, the movement of the outlet elements being a combination of two movements, one being rotational -> B05B3/0444
[B05B 3/0435] by reversing the direction of rotation for the rotor -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this place:  1a.  1b.  Figures 1a and 1b illustrate that when water reaches an impeller (5) through inlet (313), the water flow drives the impeller (5) in one rotational direction and when water reaches the impeller (5) through inlet (314) the water flow drives the impeller (5) in the other rotational direction.

[B05B 3/1007] characterised by the rotating member  B05B3/105  takes precedence -- A spraying or sprinkling device that incorporates moving outlet or deflecting elements, such as rotating nozzles, discs or plates, to alter the direction, pattern or distribution of the discharged fluid. The motion of these components creates a variable spray, improving coverage or adapting to different application needs. This falls under the CPC hierarchy of Performing Operations → Spraying or Atomising in General → Spraying Apparatus.

[B05B 3/1071] with two rotating members rotating at different speeds -- A spray or atomising device that employs two rotating members, such as drums, discs or rings, which turn at different speeds to alter the motion of the outlet or deflecting elements. The speed difference creates variable flow or direction of the spray, allowing finer control of droplet size, distribution or pattern.

The classification covers any means-mechanical, electronic or hydraulic-used to monitor or adjust the speed of the moving outlet elements.

[B05B 3/18] with elements moving in a straight line, e.g. along a track -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this place:  The Figure illustrates a mobile sprinkler (10) moving along a track (H).  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Watering arrangements making use of movable installations on wheels or the like -> A01G25/09
[B05B 5/00] Electrostatic spraying apparatus -- B05B 5/00 covers devices that apply a charge to a liquid or powder stream to improve deposition, using electrostatic forces to atomize or direct the spray. These apparatuses typically include a charging electrode, a discharge electrode and a spray nozzle, and are used in painting, coating and agricultural spraying to increase efficiency and reduce waste.
[B05B 5/001] incorporating means for heating or cooling, e.g. the material to be sprayed -- This subcategory covers electrostatic spray devices that integrate heating or cooling mechanisms to condition the material being sprayed, such as built-in heaters, chillers or other temperature-control units.
[B05B 5/002] comprising means for neutralising the spray of charged droplets or particules -- In the CPC this subclass is placed under spraying apparatus in the broader group for spraying or atomising in general. It covers electrostatic spray devices that incorporate means such as ionisers or opposite-charge emitters to neutralise the charge of sprayed droplets or particles, improving deposition efficiency.
[B05B 5/003] by mixing two sprays of opposite polarity -- This subclass covers electrostatic spray devices that generate a charged mist by combining two separate atomising streams, each carrying opposite electrical polarity. Mixing the oppositely charged sprays imparts a net charge to the droplets, enhancing attraction to the target and improving coating efficiency.
[B05B 5/004] by alternating the polarity of the spray -- This subclass covers electrostatic spraying devices that obtain atomisation or deposition by periodically reversing the electric charge of the droplets, typically using a switching power supply or alternating voltage to improve coating uniformity and reduce repulsion. Alternating the polarity attracts oppositely charged particles to the target surface and is used in paint, coating and powder deposition processes.
[B05B 5/005] the high voltage supplied to an electrostatic spraying apparatus being adjustable during spraying operation, e.g. for modifying spray width, droplet size -- B05B 5/005 covers electrostatic sprayers in which the high-voltage supply can be varied while the device is operating, allowing the user to change the spray width or droplet size on the fly. This adjustable-voltage feature is part of the broader category of spraying apparatus within the performing-operations and spraying-or-atomising hierarchy.
[B05B 5/006] the adjustement of high voltage is responsive to a condition, e.g. a condition of material discharged, of ambient medium or of target -- In electrostatic spraying devices this subclass concerns systems that adjust the high-voltage supply in response to a condition such as the properties of the material being discharged, the surrounding medium, or the target surface, thereby controlling the spray characteristics.
[B05B 5/007] the high voltage supplied to an electrostatic spraying apparatus during spraying operation being periodical or in time, e.g. sinusoidal -- This subgroup covers electrostatic spraying apparatus in which the high-voltage charge applied to the liquid is varied periodically, for example with sinusoidal or pulsed waveforms, during the spraying operation. Modulating the voltage in time is used to influence droplet formation, deposition patterns and charge balance.
[B05B 5/008] with periodical change of polarity -- Electrostatic spraying devices that periodically reverse the electrical polarity of the charging electrode or nozzle, allowing the charge on the spray to alternate during operation. This periodic polarity change improves droplet dispersion, reduces charge buildup on surfaces and enhances uniform coating or deposition.
[B05B 5/025] Discharge apparatus, e.g. electrostatic spray guns -- B05B 5/025 pertains to discharge apparatus such as electrostatic spray-guns that impart an electric charge to a liquid or powder stream for enhanced deposition on a target. The devices typically include a charging electrode, power supply and nozzle to create a charged spray for coating, painting or surface-treatment.
[B05B 5/0255] spraying and depositing by electrostatic forces only -- This subclass covers devices that atomize a material and direct the droplets onto a target solely using electrostatic charge, without mechanical acceleration. The apparatus generates charge on the spray particles, creating an electric field that attracts them to the workpiece, enabling uniform coating.
[B05B 5/03] characterised by the use of gas -- Electrostatic spraying apparatus that employ a gas to generate or transport the charged spray. The gas may be used to atomize the material, to carry the droplets, or to assist in creating the electric field that charges the particles. This subclass includes devices where the gas is integral to the electrostatic spraying process.
[B05B 5/032] for spraying particulate materials -- This subclass covers devices that apply electrostatic forces to disperse solid particles as a spray, typically using a high-voltage electrode to charge the material before it leaves the nozzle. The charged particles are attracted to an oppositely charged target, improving deposition efficiency and reducing overspray.
[B05B 5/035] characterised by gasless spraying -- The B05B 5/035 subclass covers electrostatic spraying devices that atomise and apply liquids without a carrier gas, using an electric charge to create a fine spray. These gas-free systems are employed for applying paints, coatings or other liquids where precise, efficient deposition is required.
[B05B 5/04] characterised by having rotary outlet or deflecting elements -- This subclass covers electrostatic spray devices that incorporate a rotary outlet or rotating deflecting components to disperse the charged material. The rotating element aids atomisation and directs the spray pattern, enhancing coverage and efficiency.
[B05B 5/0403] characterised by the rotating member -- The B05B 5/0403 subgroup covers electrostatic spraying apparatus that incorporate a rotating member, such as a spinning electrode, drum or disc, which both atomises the liquid and imparts an electrostatic charge to the droplets. This design creates a continuous, uniform spray for coating, painting or treating surfaces, and the rotation may also aid in directing the charged spray toward the target.
[B05B 5/0407] with a spraying edge, e.g. like a cup or a bell -- This CPC subgroup covers electrostatic spray devices that incorporate a defined spraying edge, such as cup-shaped or bell-shaped edges, to shape or direct the charged spray. The edge helps achieve uniform atomisation and precise placement of the aerosol.
[B05B 5/0411] with individual passages at its periphery -- This subclass concerns electrostatic spray devices that have separate channels or passages arranged around the outer edge of the atomizing head or nozzle, allowing the charging of the spray after it exits through peripheral ducts. These passages typically conduct charge to the fluid droplets as they flow outward, improving deposition efficiency.
[B05B 5/0415] Driving means; Parts thereof, e.g. turbine, shaft, bearings -- B05B 5/0415 covers the power-transmission components of electrostatic spraying devices, including the mechanisms that drive the spray operation such as turbines, shafts and bearings. These elements are classified as the driving means and their associated parts that generate and convey motion to the spray head. It falls under the CPC hierarchy for spraying apparatus within the broader group of performing operations.
[B05B 5/0418] designed for spraying particulate material -- Electrostatic spraying apparatus designed specifically for dispersing particulate material, such as powders or granules, by charging the particles and using electrostatic forces to direct them toward a target surface. This subclass covers devices that generate, charge, and atomise solid particles for coating, coating, or material-application processes.
[B05B 5/0422] comprising means for controlling speed of rotation -- The class B05B 5/0422 pertains to electrostatic spray devices that incorporate mechanisms for adjusting or regulating the rotational speed of the spray head or atomising component. By controlling rotation speed, the apparatus can influence droplet formation, distribution and charge characteristics, thereby affecting spray performance and application efficiency.
[B05B 5/0426] Means for supplying shaping gas -- This subclass covers devices that supply the shaping gas used to form the spray cone in electrostatic spraying equipment, such as gas nozzles, ducts or manifolds that deliver air or other gases to influence droplet trajectory and coating pattern.
[B05B 5/043] using induction-charging -- B05B 5/043 covers electrostatic spray devices that generate charge on the liquid droplets by induction-charging, i.e., using an electric field to induce charge without direct contact. The method is applied in spray guns and similar apparatus to improve atomisation and deposition efficiency. It is a subclass of spraying apparatus within the CPC.
[B05B 5/047] using tribo-charging -- Electrostatic spraying apparatus that generate a charge on droplets by tribo-charging, i.e., by frictional contact between the liquid and a solid surface or moving parts. The subclass covers designs in which the triboelectric effect provides the electrostatic charge for atomisation, including related feed, electrode and grounding arrangements.Electrostatic spraying apparatus that generate a charge on droplets by tribo-charging, i.e., by frictional contact between the liquid and a solid surface or moving parts. The subclass covers designs in which the triboelectric effect provides the electrostatic charge for atomisation, including related feed, electrode and grounding arrangements.
[B05B 5/053] Arrangements for supplying power, e.g. charging power -- The B05B 5/053 classifies the electrical power-supply arrangements used in electrostatic spraying devices, including circuits and components that provide or charge the high-voltage source for atomisation. It covers methods of delivering charging power to the spray nozzle, electrode charging circuits, and related control circuitry that enable the generation of an electrostatically charged spray.
[B05B 5/0531] Power generators -- The subclass B05B 5/0531 covers the power-generation units that provide the electrical energy needed for electrostatic spraying apparatus. It includes generators, transformers, power supplies and other devices that produce the high voltage required to charge the spray droplets, enabling the atomising process.
[B05B 5/0532] driven by a gas turbine -- The CPC subclass B05B 5/0532 designates electrostatic spraying apparatus whose operation is powered by a gas turbine. Such devices employ the turbine's output to generate the airflow and, where needed, the energy to charge the droplets for electrostatic atomisation.
[B05B 5/0533] Electrodes specially adapted therefor; Arrangements of electrodes -- The subclass B05B 5/0533 pertains to electrostatic spray devices that incorporate electrodes specially designed or configured for charging the liquid, such as particular shapes, materials or surface treatments, and to the specific arrangements of those electrodes within the apparatus to optimise atomisation and spray direction.
[B05B 5/0535] at least two electrodes having different potentials being held on the discharge apparatus, one of them being a charging electrode of the corona type located in the spray or close to it, and another being of the non-corona type located outside of the path for the material -- B05B 5/0535 covers electrostatic spraying devices that employ at least two electrodes at different potentials: a corona-type charging electrode placed in the spray zone or nearby, and a non-corona electrode positioned outside the material's trajectory, enabling charge induction without direct contact.
[B05B 5/0536] Dimensional characteristics of electrodes, e.g. diameter or radius of curvature of a needle-like corona electrode -- This subclass of the Cooperative Patent Classification covers the dimensional properties of electrodes used in electrostatic spraying devices, such as the diameter or radius of curvature of a needle-like corona electrode. It falls under spraying apparatus within the broader category of spraying or atomising in general and performing operations.
[B05B 5/0537] comprising a charge return path between the target and the spraying apparatus which is not the "true" earth, i.e. using a direct charge return path like a wire or the like, e.g. "floating earth" -- B05B 5/0537 concerns electrostatic spraying apparatus that return the charge to the target through a dedicated conductive path rather than the true earth, such as a wire or other "floating earth" arrangement.
[B05B 5/0538] the operator being part of a charge return path between target and apparatus -- This CPC subclass covers electrostatic spraying devices where the operator forms part of the charge-return path between the target and the apparatus, allowing the charge to flow through the operator. It resides under performing operations, spraying or atomising in general, and spraying apparatus.
[B05B 5/057] Arrangements for discharging liquids or other fluent material without using a gun or nozzle -- B05B 5/057 covers configurations that release liquids or other fluids without a conventional gun or nozzle, typically employing electrostatic charging to atomise the material. The arrangements may use electrodes, high-voltage fields or similar means to create a fine spray for coating, printing or cleaning processes.
[B05B 5/06] using electric arc -- B05B 5/06 covers electrostatic spraying devices that create the charge on the spray particles by means of an electric arc. The arc ionises the liquid as it exits the nozzle, allowing the droplets to be attracted to a charged target for efficient coating or deposition. This subclass falls under spraying or atomising in general, specifically within spray apparatus.
[B05B 5/08] Plant for applying liquids or other fluent materials to objects -- B05B 5/08 covers plants designed to apply liquids, pastes, or other fluent substances onto objects, typically using spraying or atomising techniques. The classification includes systems such as electrostatic sprayers where the material is charged to improve deposition on the target surface.
[B05B 5/081] specially adapted for treating particulate materials -- B05B 5/081 designates electrostatic spraying apparatus that are specially adapted for processing particulate materials such as powders, granules or dust. The devices employ a charging system to impart an electrostatic charge to the particles before they are atomised, enhancing their control and uniformity during application.
[B05B 5/082] characterised by means for supporting, holding or conveying the objects -- An electrostatic spray apparatus that incorporates devices such as trays, conveyors, brackets or other fixtures for supporting, holding or transporting the workpieces during coating. These supporting means ensure the objects remain in the correct position and orientation while the electrostatic spray is applied.
[B05B 5/084] the objects lying on, or being supported above conveying means, e.g. conveyor belts -- B05B 5/084 classifies electrostatic spraying apparatus elements that handle objects positioned on or above a conveying system such as a conveyor belt. It covers devices and mechanisms for supporting, feeding, or transporting workpieces while they receive a sprayed coating, ensuring consistent application during the spraying operation. This sub-group falls under the broader categories of performing operations, spraying or atomising in general, and spraying apparatus.
[B05B 5/085] the plant being provided on a vehicle -- The classification code B05B 5/085 pertains to electrostatic spraying apparatus specifically designed for use on vehicles, where the plant or equipment is mounted. This includes systems integrated into agricultural sprayers, paint spray trucks, and other mobile applications requiring precise material application.
[B05B 5/087] Arrangements of electrodes, e.g. of charging, shielding, collecting electrodes  B05B5/12 ,  B05B5/14  take precedence; arrangements of electrodes on the discharge apparatus  B05B5/0533 -- Electrostatic spraying apparatus equipped with specific electrode configurations for charging, shielding, or collecting the spray, or with electrodes mounted on the discharge unit. The classification covers designs where the placement and geometry of charging, shielding or collecting electrodes are defined, taking precedence over broader electrode-arrangement groups such as B05B5/12 and B05B5/14.
[B05B 5/088] for creating electric field curtains -- B05B 5/088 covers electrostatic spray devices that generate a continuous electric field, or "curtain," to charge droplets as they leave the nozzle, enhancing their trajectory and deposition efficiency. The field curtain is produced by electrodes arranged to blanket the spray zone, allowing better control of particle motion and reducing overspray.
[B05B 5/10] Arrangements for supplying power, e.g. charging power -- The classification covers the means for delivering the electrical energy that charges the spray, including power supplies, transformers, high-voltage generators, connectors and control circuitry incorporated in electrostatic spraying equipment. It addresses the arrangement of these components to provide the required charging power.
[B05B 5/12] specially adapted for coating the interior of hollow bodies -- Electrostatic spraying apparatus specially adapted for coating the interior of hollow bodies uses an electrically charged spray to deposit material uniformly inside tubes, pipes or other enclosed sections, often for corrosion-resistant or functional coatings in industrial, automotive or aerospace applications.
[B05B 5/14] specially adapted for coating continuously moving elongated bodies, e.g. wires, strips, pipes -- Electrostatic spraying apparatus specially adapted for continuously coating elongated bodies such as wires, strips or pipes, wherein the device includes mechanisms to feed the material continuously through the spray zone and deposit a uniform electrostatically charged coating onto its surface.
[B05B 5/16] Arrangements for supplying liquids or other fluent material -- The code covers designs for delivering liquids or other fluent material to a spray device, including pumps, reservoirs, channels, feed lines and related pressure-regulation or metering elements that ensure a controlled flow to the nozzle. It also encompasses arrangements that distribute the fluid to one or many spray heads, particularly in electrostatic spraying systems.
[B05B 5/1608] the liquid or other fluent material being electrically conductive -- B05B 5/1608 covers electrostatic spraying devices in which the sprayed fluid itself is electrically conductive, allowing the liquid to acquire charge directly rather than relying on external charging of droplets. This classification applies to systems that use conductive inks, paints, polymers or other fluent materials to achieve electrophoretic atomisation for uniform coating or deposition.
[B05B 5/1616] and the arrangement comprising means for insulating a grounded material source from high voltage applied to the material -- This subclass covers electrostatic spraying devices that incorporate an arrangement to electrically isolate a grounded material source from the high voltage applied to the material, preventing current leakage and ensuring safe operation. It is located in CPC group B05B 5/1616 under spraying apparatus.
[B05B 5/1625] the insulating means comprising an intermediate container alternately connected to the grounded material source for filling, and then disconnected and electrically insulated therefrom -- An electrostatic spray device includes an insulating system that uses an intermediate container which is temporarily linked to the grounded material supply to receive the coating liquid, then is detached and kept electrically isolated from the source during operation. This arrangement prevents charge leakage and improves spray stability.
[B05B 5/1633] the arrangement comprising several supply lines arranged in parallel, each comprising such an intermediate container -- The B05B 5/1633 subclass covers electrostatic spraying apparatus that use multiple parallel supply lines, each incorporating its own intermediate container to hold the coating material before delivery to the spray head. This arrangement allows simultaneous feeding of several streams, improving flow consistency and enabling more uniform atomisation in the spraying process.
[B05B 5/1641] an additional container being provided downstream the intermediate container -- A feature of electrostatic spraying apparatus in which an additional container is positioned downstream of the intermediate container, allowing further storage, mixing or feeding of material just before atomisation. This arrangement can provide precise control of the spray composition or enable sequential addition of components during operation.
[B05B 5/165] by dividing the material into discrete quantities, e.g. droplets -- B05B 5/165 covers electrostatic spraying devices that create a spray by breaking the coating material into separate droplets before charging them. The subclass includes nozzle designs, atomising chambers or vibration techniques that produce discrete quantities of liquid for more uniform deposition.
[B05B 5/1658] Details -- B05B 5/1658 pertains to specific details of electrostatic spraying apparatus, focusing on components and mechanisms that enhance the efficiency and precision of the spraying process. This classification covers aspects such as electrode designs, power supply systems, and control mechanisms.
[B05B 5/1666] Voltage blocking valves, e.g. with axially separable coupling elements -- Voltage blocking valves are components used in electrostatic spraying equipment to interrupt the high-voltage supply when required, preventing current flow through the spray nozzle. They often feature axially separable coupling elements that allow quick disconnection and maintenance while preserving the integrity of the high-voltage circuit.
[B05B 5/1675] the supply means comprising a piston, e.g. a piston pump -- B05B 5/1675 covers the fuel-or-material supply components of electrostatic spraying devices that utilise a piston mechanism, such as a piston pump, to deliver coating material to the atomiser. The claim focuses on the piston-based delivery system within the broader category of spraying apparatus.
[B05B 5/1683] specially adapted for particulate materials -- This subgroup covers electrostatic spraying devices that are modified to handle particulate feedstocks such as powders, granules or fine solids. It includes features like specialised hoppers, vibratory or pneumatic feeders and charging electrodes that ensure uniform particle dispersion and effective atomisation.
[B05B 5/1691] Apparatus to be carried on or by a person or with a container fixed to the discharge device -- Electrostatic spraying apparatus designed to be worn or carried by a user, or to have its liquid container directly attached to the spray nozzle. Such devices integrate the discharge unit and the supply tank, enabling handheld or body-mounted operation for applying paints, coatings, or pesticides with electrostatically charged droplets.
[B05B 7/00] Spraying apparatus for discharge of liquids or other fluent materials from two or more sources, e.g. of liquid and air, of powder and gas -- This class covers devices that combine two or more fluid streams, such as liquid and air or powder and gas, to produce a spray or atomised discharge. The apparatus mixes the separate sources before ejection, enabling controlled atomisation for applications like painting, cleaning, or coating.
[B05B 7/0006] Spraying by means of explosions -- The B05B 7/0006 subclass covers spraying techniques that rely on explosive forces to atomise liquids, powders or gases. It includes devices where a detonation, rapid combustion or shock wave created by an explosive charge is used to break up a fluid stream and eject a fine spray, such as pyrotechnic dispensers, internal-combustion-driven sprayers and other explosion-based atomisers.
[B05B 7/0012] Apparatus for achieving spraying before discharge from the apparatus -- A spraying apparatus that creates a spray or atomisation within the device itself, before the mixed liquids, gases or powders leave the nozzle, typically using two or more separate sources such as liquid and air. This pre-discharge atomising arrangement is classified under B05B 7/0012.
[B05B 7/0018] with devices for making foam -- B05B 7/0018 covers spraying devices that combine two or more streams, such as liquid with air or powder with gas, and include mechanisms that convert the mixture into foam. The classification applies to apparatus designed to produce a foamed spray for applications like cleaning, coating, or firefighting.
[B05B 7/0025] with a compressed gas supply -- The subclass B05B 7/0025 covers spraying devices that combine two or more feeds-such as liquid with air or powder with gas-and use a compressed-gas source to atomise the mixture, delivering the product as a spray or mist. This includes any apparatus where the compressed gas is integral to the discharge mechanism.
