[B05B 7/0031] with disturbing means promoting mixing, e.g. balls, crowns -- The subclass refers to spraying or atomising devices that combine two or more streams, such as liquid with air or powder with gas, and employ disturbing elements like balls or crowns to promote mixing and improve the homogeneity of the discharged material.
[B05B 7/0037] including sieves, porous members or the like -- B05B 7/0037 covers spraying devices that employ two or more fluid sources and incorporate sieves, porous members or similar structures to filter, break up or atomize the discharge. These components help control droplet size or remove solid particles during the spray.
[B05B 7/0043] including a plurality of individual elements, e.g. needles, baffles, rotatable blades -- This subclass covers spraying apparatus that discharges liquids or other fluent materials from two or more sources and includes designs that incorporate a plurality of individual elements such as needles, baffles or rotatable blades, falling under spraying or atomising in general.
[B05B 7/005] wherein ambient air is aspirated by a liquid flow -- A spraying apparatus that creates a spray by drawing ambient air into the device through the kinetic energy of a flowing liquid, mixing the liquid and aspirated air to produce a fine atomised jet. This method combines two fluid streams - liquid and air - for efficient discharge.
[B05B 7/0056] with disturbing means promoting mixing, e.g. balls, crowns -- The B05B 7/0056 subclass covers spraying devices that draw fluids from two or more sources and include disturbing elements, such as balls or crowns, to promote mixing of the liquids or other fluent materials during discharge. This falls under the broader categories of performing operations, spraying or atomising in general, and spraying apparatus.
[B05B 7/0062] including sieves, porous members or the like -- B05B 7/0062 covers spray devices that discharge liquids or other fluids from two or more sources, such as mixing liquid with air or powder with gas, and that incorporate sieves, porous members or similar structures to filter, atomise or regulate the flow.
[B05B 7/0068] including a plurality of individual elements, e.g. needles, baffles, rotatable blades -- B05B 7/0068 covers spraying devices that use several discrete components such as needles, baffles or rotating blades to generate and direct a spray from multiple material streams. The arrangement of these elements creates mixing or atomisation of liquids, powders or gases for applications like coating, fumigation or aerosol delivery.
[B05B 7/0075] Nozzle arrangements in gas streams -- B05B 7/0075 covers the design and positioning of nozzles within gas streams in spraying devices, where liquid, powder or other fluent materials are discharged from multiple sources such as liquid-air or powder-gas mixtures. It addresses how the nozzles interact with the carrier gas to achieve atomisation or fine spray.
[B05B 7/0081] Apparatus supplied with low pressure gas, e.g. "hvlp"-guns; air supplied by a fan -- This subclass covers spraying devices that receive their driving gas at low pressure, such as high-volume low-pressure (HVLP) guns, where the air is delivered by a fan rather than a compressor. The classification includes apparatus that combine a low-pressure gas source with a liquid or powder feed for atomisation.
[B05B 7/0087] Atmospheric air being sucked by a gas stream, generally flowing through a venturi, at a location upstream or inside the spraying apparatus -- A spraying device that draws atmospheric air into the main gas stream, typically via a venturi, with the air intake positioned upstream of or within the apparatus. This arrangement mixes the drawn air with the discharge material to improve atomisation.
[B05B 7/0093] At least a part of the apparatus, e.g. a container, being provided with means, e.g. wheels or casters for allowing its displacement relative to the ground -- B05B 7/0093 covers spraying apparatus where a component such as a container is equipped with mobility aids like wheels or casters, allowing the device to be moved relative to the ground. It applies to systems that discharge liquids, powders or gases from two or more sources.
[B05B 7/02] Spray pistols -- B05B 7/02 covers spray pistols, a type of spraying apparatus that discharges liquids, powders or other fluent materials from two or more sources such as a liquid combined with air or a powder with gas. The classification is situated under performing operations, spraying or atomising in general, and spraying apparatus.
[B05B 7/025] Nozzles having elongated outlets, e.g. slots, for the material to be sprayed -- The B05B 7/025 subclass covers spraying apparatus that use nozzles with elongated openings such as slots, allowing a continuous sheet or ribbon of spray material to be emitted instead of a circular jet. These designs are used when a uniform, linear distribution of liquid, powder, or gas is required, often improving coverage and reducing overspray.
[B05B 7/04] with arrangements for mixing liquids or other fluent materials before discharge -- Spraying apparatus that combines two or more fluid streams-such as liquid with air or powder with gas-before they are discharged, using internal arrangements to mix the liquids or other fluent materials prior to atomisation. This class covers devices where the mixing of the separate sources occurs within the spray system itself.
[B05B 7/0408] with arrangements for mixing two or more liquids -- B05B 7/0408 covers spraying devices that combine two or more liquids before discharge, such as mixers that feed a liquid stream together with air or another fluid to produce a homogeneous spray.
[B05B 7/0416] with arrangements for mixing one gas and one liquid -- This subclass of the CPC concerns spraying devices that blend a gas and a liquid before they are emitted. It includes apparatuses where a single gas stream is mixed with a liquid stream to produce an atomised spray, such as air-liquid nozzles.
[B05B 7/0425] without any source of compressed gas, e.g. the air being sucked by the pressurised liquid -- B05B 7/0425 refers to spraying apparatus that mixes two or more streams, such as liquid and air or powder and gas, but operates without an external source of compressed gas; the air is drawn in by the pressurised liquid itself.
[B05B 7/0433] with one inner conduit of gas surrounded by an external conduit of liquid upstream the mixing chamber -- This subclass concerns spray devices that convey a gas through an inner conduit which is surrounded upstream by an external conduit carrying a liquid, so that the gas is mixed with the liquid before reaching the mixing chamber. The arrangement enables simultaneous discharge of the two streams, typically to improve atomisation or to deliver a combined spray of liquid and gas.
[B05B 7/0441] with one inner conduit of liquid surrounded by an external conduit of gas upstream the mixing chamber -- This CPC subgroup covers spray devices that deliver liquid through a single inner conduit that is completely surrounded by an external gas conduit before the mixing chamber, allowing the liquid and gas streams to combine for atomisation. It falls under B05B 7/04 for spraying apparatus using two or more sources, specifically the subclass for one inner liquid conduit encased by a gas conduit.
[B05B 7/045] the gas and liquid flows being parallel just upstream the mixing chamber  B05B7/0458 ,  B05B7/0466  take precedence -- This subclass concerns spray devices that discharge liquids together with a gas flowing in parallel just upstream of the mixing chamber, a configuration used to enhance atomisation when two or more streams (e.g., liquid and air or powder and gas) are combined. It narrows the broader category of multi-source spraying apparatus and is overridden by the more specific sub-subclasses B05B 7/0458 and B05B 7/0466.
[B05B 7/0458] the gas and liquid flows being perpendicular just upstream the mixing chamber -- The B05B 7/0458 entry covers spraying apparatus in which a gas stream and a liquid stream intersect at right angles just before entering the mixing chamber, creating a perpendicular flow arrangement that aids atomisation of the liquid. This subclass falls under spraying or atomising in general, specifically devices that discharge liquids or other fluent materials from multiple sources.
[B05B 7/0466] with means for deflecting the central liquid flow towards the peripheral gas flow -- This CPC subclass covers spraying devices that combine at least two streams, such as liquid and gas, where the design includes features that direct the central liquid jet outward toward the surrounding airflow, improving atomization and mixing.
[B05B 7/0475] with means for deflecting the peripheral gas flow towards the central liquid flow  B05B7/0458  takes precedence -- This classification covers spraying devices that combine at least two streams, such as a liquid and a gas, and include features that redirect the outer gas flow inward toward the central liquid jet to improve atomisation. The design enhances mixing and droplet formation by using deflection elements that guide the peripheral gas toward the liquid core.
[B05B 7/0483] with gas and liquid jets intersecting in the mixing chamber -- This subclass covers spray devices that discharge both gas and liquid through separate nozzles so that the jets intersect within a mixing chamber, creating a combined spray of the two fluids. It pertains to apparatus where the interaction of the gas and liquid streams occurs before the mixture exits the nozzle.
[B05B 7/0491] the liquid and the gas being mixed at least twice along the flow path of the liquid -- B05B 7/0491 covers spraying apparatus where the liquid and gas are mixed at least twice along the liquid's flow path, ensuring repeated blending before discharge. It falls under the subclass of devices that discharge liquids or other fluent materials from two or more sources.
[B05B 7/06] with one outlet orifice surrounding another approximately in the same plane -- Spraying apparatus that discharges liquids or other fluent materials from two or more sources, such as a liquid and an air stream or a powder and a gas, using a configuration where one outlet orifice is positioned around another in roughly the same plane. This design allows simultaneous or mixed ejection of the substances for improved atomisation or coating performance.
[B05B 7/061] with several liquid outlets discharging one or several liquids -- B05B 7/061 covers spraying apparatus that uses two or more liquid outlets to discharge one or more liquids, often together with air or gas, enabling simultaneous or mixed spraying from separate sources. It falls under spraying or atomising in general, performing operations.
[B05B 7/062] with only one liquid outlet and at least one gas outlet -- This subclass covers spray devices that have a single liquid discharge port together with at least one gas discharge port, enabling atomisation of the liquid by the gas stream. Such apparatus are used where a liquid and a gas, typically air, are mixed at the nozzle to form a spray.
[B05B 7/063] one fluid being sucked by the other -- B05B 7/063 covers spraying devices that generate a spray by drawing one fluid into a stream of another, such as using a high-velocity gas to entrain and disperse a liquid, or using a liquid jet to suck in air or powder for atomisation.
[B05B 7/064] the liquid being sucked by the gas -- This subclass covers spraying apparatus where a gas stream draws the liquid from a separate source into the discharge nozzle, mixing the two to produce an atomised spray. It applies to devices that use suction of liquid by gas, such as air-liquid or powder-gas systems.
[B05B 7/065] an inner gas outlet being surrounded by an annular adjacent liquid outlet -- This CPC subgroup covers spraying devices in which a central gas outlet is encircled by an annular liquid outlet, enabling simultaneous discharge of gas and liquid from the same nozzle. The arrangement is used to mix air (or another gas) with a fluid for atomisation or spraying.
[B05B 7/066] with an inner liquid outlet surrounded by at least one annular gas outlet -- B05B 7/066 covers spray devices that have a central liquid nozzle surrounded by one or more annular gas outlets, allowing simultaneous discharge of liquid and gas streams to atomise the liquid. The arrangement is used to improve mixing and droplet formation in applications such as painting, coating, or agricultural spraying.
[B05B 7/067] the liquid outlet being annular -- The entry B05B 7/067 refers to spraying devices in which the liquid is discharged through an annular, or ring-shaped, outlet rather than a conventional round nozzle. This configuration is used in apparatus that combine liquids with gases such as air or powder, enabling a uniform spray pattern from multiple sources.
[B05B 7/068] the annular gas outlet being supplied by a gas conduit having an axially concave curved internal surface just upstream said outlet -- This subclass covers spraying devices where an annular gas outlet is supplied by a conduit whose interior surface upstream of the outlet is axially concave, shaping the gas flow before it mixes with the liquid or powder. It falls under B05B 7/068 within the CPC hierarchy for spraying apparatus using two or more sources.
[B05B 7/08] with separate outlet orifices, e.g. to form parallel jets , to form intersecting jets -- The B05B 7/08 subclass covers spraying devices that discharge liquids, powders or gases from two or more separate sources through distinct outlet orifices, creating parallel or intersecting jets. Such apparatuses are designed to mix the streams after ejection, allowing controlled jet patterns for applications like coating, cooling or atomisation. This classification falls under the broader categories of performing operations, spraying or atomising in general, and spraying apparatus.
[B05B 7/0807] to form intersecting jets -- This subclass relates to spraying devices that generate intersecting jets of two or more fluids, such as liquid with air or powder with gas, to improve atomisation. The intersecting jet arrangement is used to produce finer droplets or more uniform sprays.
[B05B 7/0815] with at least one gas jet intersecting a jet constituted by a liquid or a mixture containing a liquid for controlling the shape of the latter -- This subclass covers spray devices in which at least one gas jet intersects a liquid or liquid-containing jet to control the shape or atomisation of the liquid stream, typically using two or more sources such as liquid and air.
[B05B 7/0823] comprising a rotatable spray pattern adjusting plate controlling the flow rate of the spray shaping gas jets -- The B05B 7/0823 subclass covers spraying devices that discharge liquids or other fluent materials from at least two sources, such as a liquid together with a gas, and incorporate a rotatable plate used to adjust the spray pattern while controlling the flow rate of the shaping-gas jets.
[B05B 7/083] comprising rotatable spray shaping gas jet outlets -- B05B 7/083 covers spraying devices that use two or more material streams, such as liquid and air, and include rotatable outlets for shaping the gas jet. The rotatable elements allow adjustment of spray pattern and direction, improving atomisation control in applications ranging from coating to cooling.
[B05B 7/0838] comprising a single means controlling simultaneously the flow rates of shaping and spraying gas jets -- The CPC entry B05B 7/0838 defines spray apparatus that discharges liquids, powders or gases from two or more sources and includes a single mechanism that simultaneously controls the flow rates of the shaping-gas and the spraying-gas jets, ensuring coordinated delivery of the material.
[B05B 7/0846] with jets being only jets constituted by a liquid or a mixture containing a liquid -- A spray apparatus that combines two or more sources-for example liquid with air or powder with gas-where every ejected jet is formed solely from a liquid or a liquid-containing mixture. This subclass covers devices that use only liquid jets to atomise or discharge the material.
[B05B 7/0853] with one single gas jet and several jets constituted by a liquid or a mixture containing a liquid  B05B7/0815  takes precedence -- B05B 7/0853 covers spraying apparatus that employ one gas jet together with several jets of a liquid or a liquid-containing mixture, using the gas stream to atomise the multiple liquid streams. It is situated under the broader categories of performing operations, spraying or atomising in general, and spraying apparatus. The subclass B05B 7/0815, which deals with a single liquid jet and a gas jet, takes precedence over this entry.
[B05B 7/0861] with one single jet constituted by a liquid or a mixture containing a liquid and several gas jets  B05B7/0815  takes precedence -- This classification entry pertains to spraying apparatus designed for discharging liquids or mixtures of liquid and gas from multiple sources, specifically configurations where a single jet is formed from a liquid or a liquid mixture alongside several gas jets. The code indicates that this setup is distinct but related to other multi-source spray systems, with a specific note that a different classification takes precedence when the apparatus primarily features a single liquid jet accompanied by multiple gas jets.
[B05B 7/0869] the liquid or other fluent material being sucked or aspirated from an outlet orifice by another fluid, e.g. a gas, coming from another outlet orifice -- B05B 7/0869 refers to spraying apparatus in which a liquid or other fluent material is drawn into the spray stream by a second fluid, such as a gas, entering through a separate outlet, so that suction or aspiration from one orifice is created by the flow from another.
[B05B 7/0876] to form parallel jets constituted by a liquid or a mixture containing a liquid  B05B7/0884 ,  B05B7/0892  take precedence -- B05B 7/0876 falls under the Performing Operations > Spraying or Atomising in General > Spraying Apparatus hierarchy and covers spraying devices that discharge liquids or liquid-containing mixtures from two or more sources, such as a liquid combined with air or a powder mixed with gas. The entry specifically relates to apparatus designed to produce parallel jets of the liquid or liquid mixture, and it takes precedence over the narrower sub-entries B05B 7/0884 and B05B 7/0892.
[B05B 7/0884] the outlet orifices for jets constituted by a liquid or a mixture containing a liquid being aligned -- This subclass deals with spraying devices whose discharge openings or nozzles that generate liquid or liquid-containing jets are arranged in a defined alignment, such as in rows, arrays or patterns, to control the distribution of the spray.
[B05B 7/0892] the outlet orifices for jets constituted by a liquid or a mixture containing a liquid being disposed on a circle -- This CPC subclass covers spray devices whose discharge nozzles are arranged in a circular pattern, each orifice delivering a liquid or liquid-containing jet. It falls under spraying apparatus for discharge of liquids or other fluent materials from two or more sources within the broader performing-operations hierarchy.
[B05B 7/10] producing a swirling discharge -- B05B 7/10 covers spray devices that generate a swirling or vortex discharge of liquids, gases, powders or combinations thereof, often using rotating nozzles, swirl chambers or tangential inlets to improve atomisation, droplet uniformity and coverage. The swirl action mixes the material streams before they exit the nozzle.
[B05B 7/12] designed to control volume of flow, e.g. with adjustable passages -- B05B 7/12 relates to spray apparatus that draws liquid or other fluent material from two or more sources and incorporates means for regulating the amount of each component delivered, such as adjustable passages or throttling elements. These features allow the user to set the flow volume of the materials in the atomising stream.
[B05B 7/1209] the controlling means for each liquid or other fluent material being manual and interdependent -- A spray apparatus that delivers two or more fluids such as liquid and air, where the manual control devices for each fluid are linked so that adjusting one automatically influences the other. The interdependent manual controls allow coordinated regulation of flow rates during spraying.
[B05B 7/1218] With means for adjusting or modifying the action of the controlling means -- B05B 7/1218 relates to spray apparatus that use two or more fluid sources, such as liquid-air or powder-gas combinations, and incorporate features that allow the operator to adjust or modify the behaviour of the controlling means, for example through variable nozzles, adjustable valves or electronic regulators.
[B05B 7/1227] Non linear relationship between the controlling means displacement and the valve element displacement -- B05B 7/1227 covers spray devices that use a valve whose displacement does not follow a linear proportion to the movement of the controlling mechanism, such as cams, levers or pneumatic pistons. The non-linear relationship is employed to fine-tune flow rates or atomisation characteristics when liquids, gases or powders are mixed from two or more sources.
[B05B 7/1236] with three or more interdependent valves -- The CPC class B05B 7/1236 covers spraying devices that combine two or more separate sources-such as liquid with air or powder with gas-to atomise a fluid, and that incorporate three or more valves whose operation is interdependent, meaning the opening or closing of one valve directly affects the others.
[B05B 7/1245] A gas valve being opened before a liquid valve -- The invention relates to a spray gun that supplies both a pressurised gas and a liquid to a mixing chamber, with the gas valve actuated to open before the liquid valve. This sequencing improves atomisation and prevents liquid back-flow when the nozzle is idle.
[B05B 7/1254] the controlling means being fluid actuated -- Fluid-actuated control mechanisms that regulate the discharge in spray devices drawing from two or more sources, such as liquid and air or powder and gas, are covered. The entry includes pneumatic or hydraulic actuators that adjust flow rates, mixing ratios or nozzle operation to manage the spray output.
[B05B 7/1263] pneumatically actuated -- B05B 7/1263 refers to spraying devices that discharge liquids, powders or other fluent materials from at least two sources, such as a liquid combined with air, and are driven by pneumatic actuation. The subclass sits within the CPC hierarchy of performing operations, spraying or atomising in general, and spraying apparatus.
[B05B 7/1272] actuated by gas involved in spraying, i.e. exiting the nozzle, e.g. as a spraying or jet shaping gas -- B05B 7/1272 covers spraying devices where the spray is driven or shaped by a gas that exits the nozzle, such as a jet-forming or carrier gas. The gas may be introduced together with the liquid or powder and provides the primary momentum for atomisation.
[B05B 7/1281] Serial arrangement, i.e. a single gas stream acting on the controlling means first and flowing downstream thereof to the nozzle -- The B05B 7/1281 entry covers spray apparatus that uses a single gas stream to first act on a controlling means-such as a valve, mixing chamber or distributor-and then flows downstream to the nozzle, allowing simultaneous discharge of two or more sources, for example liquid and air or powder and gas.
[B05B 7/129] Hand guns comprising a gas valve located at the bottom of the handle  B05B7/0087  takes precedence -- The CPC subclass B05B 7/129 covers handheld spray guns that have a gas valve positioned at the bottom of the handle, enabling simultaneous discharge of fluids or other fluent materials from two or more sources such as liquid with air or powder with gas; this subclass is subordinate to B05B 7/0087, which has priority.
[B05B 7/14] designed for spraying particulate materials -- B05B 7/14 refers to spraying apparatus specially designed for projecting particulate materials such as powders, often in combination with a gas or liquid stream. The devices typically employ two or more feed sources to atomise the particles for uniform distribution onto a target surface.
[B05B 7/1404] Arrangements for supplying particulate material -- B05B 7/1404 covers the feeding mechanisms that deliver particulate material to a spraying device that uses two or more sources, such as a powder combined with a gas or liquid. The classification concerns arrangements that control the supply, metering and mixing of the particles before they are atomised or discharged.
[B05B 7/1409] specially adapted for short fibres or chips  B05B7/145  takes precedence -- This subclass covers spray devices that combine two or more streams such as liquid with air or powder with gas and are specially designed to handle short fibers or chips, providing mechanisms to prevent clogging and ensure uniform atomization. It is superseded by the more specific B05B7/145.
[B05B 7/1413] Apparatus to be carried on or by a person, e.g. by hand; Apparatus comprising a container fixed to the discharge device -- B05B 7/1413 covers portable spraying devices that are carried or held by a person, where the material container is directly attached to the discharge mechanism. The subclass includes hand-held or body-mounted apparatus that combine the supply vessel and the nozzle for liquids, powders or gases in a single unit.
[B05B 7/1418] comprising means for supplying an additional liquid -- It covers spray apparatus that discharges liquids or other fluent materials from at least two sources and includes means for supplying an additional liquid. Such devices typically combine a primary stream with a secondary liquid to modify spray pattern, droplet size or delivery characteristics.
[B05B 7/1422] the means for supplying particulate material comprising moving mechanical means, e.g. to impart vibration -- This class pertains to spray apparatus that discharge liquids or other fluent materials from two or more sources, such as liquid and air, and specifically addresses the means for supplying particulate material by moving mechanical devices, for example vibratory feeders that impart vibration to the particles.
[B05B 7/1427] Apparatus to be carried on the back of the user -- B05B 7/1427 covers spraying apparatus that combines two or more sources, such as liquid and air or powder and gas, and is specifically designed to be worn on the user's back, allowing portable, hands-free delivery of the mixed discharge.
[B05B 7/1431] comprising means for supplying an additional liquid  B05B7/1418  takes precedence -- This CPC subclass B05B 7/1431 covers spraying apparatus that discharges liquids or other fluent materials from at least two sources and includes means for supplying an additional liquid; it falls under B05B 7/1418, which takes precedence.
[B05B 7/1436] to a container where the particulate material and the additional liquid are brought together  mixing in general  B01F -- The subclass B05B 7/1436 covers spraying apparatus in which a particulate material and an additional liquid are introduced into a container where they are mixed before discharge, enabling combined powder-liquid atomisation for applications such as coating or humidification.
[B05B 7/144] the means for supplying particulate material comprising moving mechanical means  B05B7/1422 ,  B05B7/1459  take precedence -- B05B 7/144 covers the mechanisms that feed particulate material into a spraying device by means of moving mechanical components, such as conveyors, screw feeders or agitators, used when liquids or gases are simultaneously discharged. It falls under spraying apparatus that combines two or more sources and is subordinate to the broader categories B05B 7/1422 and B05B 7/1459.
[B05B 7/1445] involving vibrations  B05B7/145  takes precedence -- The B05B 7/1445 class covers spray devices that discharge liquids or other fluent materials from two or more sources-such as liquid with air or powder with gas-and employ vibratory action to aid atomisation or improve the spray formation. It falls under the broader categories of performing operations, spraying or atomising in general, and spray apparatus, and is superseded by the more specific B05B 7/145 subclass.
[B05B 7/145] specially adapted for short fibres or chips -- It covers spraying devices designed to handle short fibers or chips, mixing them with a carrier fluid for uniform discharge. These apparatus are adapted to prevent clogging and ensure consistent atomization of solid particulates with liquids or gases.
[B05B 7/1454] comprising means for supplying collected oversprayed particulate material  spray booth with arrangements for collecting oversprayed material  B05B14/40 -- B05B 7/1454 covers spraying devices that incorporate systems to recirculate or re-supply material that has been oversprayed, such as a spray-booth equipped with collection chambers and feeding mechanisms that return the collected particles to the spray stream.
[B05B 7/1459] comprising a chamber, inlet and outlet valves upstream and downstream the chamber and means for alternately sucking particulate material into and removing particulate material from the chamber through the valves  conveying material in bulk by using a combination of gas pressure and suction  B65G53/28 ; pumps in general  F04B ; apparatus for repeatedly measuring and separating a predetermined volume of fluent solid material from a supply or container  G01F11/00 -- This classification code, B05B 7/1459, pertains to spraying apparatus designed for discharging materials from multiple sources, featuring a chamber with inlet and outlet valves. It involves mechanisms that alternately suck particulate material into the chamber and remove it using gas pressure and suction, integrating aspects of conveying bulk materials, pump operations, and precise volume measurement and separation.
[B05B 7/1463] the means for supplying particulate material comprising a gas inlet for pressurising or avoiding depressurisation of a powder container -- This subclass covers spray devices that combine two or more sources, such as air and liquid, where the feed system includes a gas inlet that pressurises the powder container or prevents its depressurisation, ensuring stable delivery of particulate material.
[B05B 7/1468] the means for supplying particulate material comprising a recirculation loop -- B05B 7/1468 covers means for supplying particulate material that incorporate a recirculation loop within spraying apparatus designed to discharge liquids, powders or other fluent substances from two or more sources. The loop recirculates the solid or powder feed to maintain a steady supply and enhance atomisation efficiency.
[B05B 7/1472] Powder extracted from a powder container in a direction substantially opposite to gravity by a suction device dipped into the powder -- A dispensing system that withdraws powder from a container in a direction opposite to gravity by immersing a suction device into the bulk material, allowing the powder to be drawn upward for mixing or spraying with a second fluid such as air or gas. This approach enables precise atomisation of solid particles together with a carrier medium.
[B05B 7/1477] means for supplying to several spray apparatus -- The code B05B 7/1477 covers devices that provide material to multiple spray units at once, enabling simultaneous operation of two or more spray apparatus such as liquid-air or powder-gas mixers. These means coordinate the flow from a shared source to each nozzle or gun, improving efficiency and uniformity in multi-spray systems.
[B05B 7/1481] Spray pistols or apparatus for discharging particulate material -- The B05B 7/1481 subclass covers spray pistols and similar devices that discharge particulate material, such as powders or granules, by mixing it with a second medium like air or gas. These apparatuses use two or more sources-typically a solid or liquid feed and a propelling fluid-to atomise or project the particles for applications ranging from cleaning to coating.
[B05B 7/1486] for spraying particulate material in dry state -- The B05B 7/1486 subclass covers spraying apparatus designed to discharge dry particulate material, such as powders, from two or more separate sources. It includes devices that combine the particulate stream with a carrier gas or air to form a spray, enabling controlled atomisation of solids in a dry state.
[B05B 7/149] with separate inlets for a particulate material and a liquid to be sprayed -- A spraying apparatus that combines two distinct streams-a particulate material and a liquid-by feeding each through its own inlet before discharge. The design allows separate control of the powder and liquid flow rates, enabling precise mixing and atomisation for applications such as coating, dusting, or fire-suppression.
[B05B 7/1495] and with separate outlets for the particulate material and the liquid -- The subclass B05B 7/1495 covers spray devices that discharge a liquid and a solid particulate from distinct outlets. Each material is supplied through its own channel and nozzle, so the two streams emerge separately and only combine in the spray plume, enabling controlled mixing of liquid and solid particles.
[B05B 7/16] incorporating means for heating the material to be sprayed -- B05B 7/16 falls under the CPC group for spraying apparatus that combines two or more sources, specifically covering devices that integrate heating elements to warm the material being atomised. It is a subcategory of B05B 7, which deals with spraying apparatus using multiple feeds such as liquid and air or powder and gas, within the broader class of performing operations and general spraying.
[B05B 7/1606] the spraying of the material involving the use of an atomising fluid, e.g. air  B05B7/168 ,  B05B7/1686 ,  B05B7/20 ,  B05B7/22  take precedence -- The code B05B 7/1606 covers spraying devices that discharge liquids or other fluent materials from two or more sources, such as a liquid together with an atomising fluid like air. It pertains to apparatus that create an atomised spray by mixing the primary material with a secondary gas or fluid to achieve fine droplets, and it takes precedence over related sub-entries such as B05B7/168, B05B7/1686, B05B7/20 and B05B7/22.
[B05B 7/1613] comprising means for heating the atomising fluid before mixing with the material to be sprayed -- The B05B 7/1613 subclass covers spray devices that include a heating element for the atomising fluid-such as air or gas-to raise its temperature before it is mixed with the material to be sprayed, improving atomisation efficiency and droplet control.
[B05B 7/162] and heat being transferred from the atomising fluid to the material to be sprayed -- The subclass B05B 7/162 relates to spraying apparatus that discharge liquids or other fluent materials from two or more sources-such as a liquid with air or a powder with gas-where heat is transferred from the atomising fluid to the material being sprayed during the atomisation process.
[B05B 7/1626] at the moment of mixing -- The classification code B05B 7/1626 pertains to spraying apparatus designed for the discharge of liquids or other fluent materials from multiple sources, specifically focusing on the process where these materials are mixed at the moment of discharge. This ensures a precise and controlled combination of substances as they exit the device.
[B05B 7/1633] and heat being transferred from the material to be sprayed to the atomising fluid -- This CPC subclass covers spraying apparatus where heat is transferred from the material being sprayed to the atomising fluid, such as using heated liquid or gas streams to assist atomisation. It falls under B05B 7/1633 within the hierarchy of performing operations, spraying or atomising in general, and spraying apparatus.
[B05B 7/164] the material to be sprayed and the atomising fluid being heated by independent sources of heat, without transfer of heat between atomising fluid and material to be sprayed -- This subclass concerns spraying devices that discharge a liquid, powder or other fluent material from at least two separate sources, where the material to be sprayed and the atomising fluid are each heated by distinct heating sources and no heat is transferred between them. The heating of each stream is independent, allowing separate temperature control for the feed and the atomising medium.
[B05B 7/1646] the material to be sprayed and the atomising fluid being heated by the same source of heat, without transfer of heat between atomising fluid and material to be sprayed -- This subcategory covers spraying devices that heat both the feed material and the atomising fluid using a single heat source, while preventing direct thermal exchange between the two streams. It applies to systems where liquid, powder or other fluids are atomised after being independently heated, such as dual-heater spray nozzles.
[B05B 7/1653] the source of heat being a heat conductive fluid -- This subclass covers spraying devices in which the heating source is a heat-conductive fluid, such as hot water, oil or other liquid, that transfers thermal energy to the material being atomised, aiding the discharge of liquids, powders or gas mixtures.
[B05B 7/166] the material to be sprayed being heated in a container  B05B7/208  takes precedence -- A spraying apparatus that combines two or more feed streams-such as liquid with air or powder with gas-and heats the material to be sprayed inside a container before it is atomised, enabling controlled discharge of hot fluids or particles. This falls under CPC code B05B 7/166, which is subordinate to B05B 7/208.
[B05B 7/1666] fixed to the discharge device -- This subclass covers spraying apparatus where the component that combines two-or-more fluid streams, such as liquid with air or powder with gas, is permanently attached to the discharge device, forming an integral part of the nozzle rather than a removable or adjustable element.
[B05B 7/1673] heat being transferred to the material to be sprayed by a heat transfer conductive fluid  B05B7/162 ,  B05B7/1653  take precedence -- A spraying apparatus that combines two or more sources, such as liquid and air or powder and gas, and uses a heat-transfer conductive fluid to convey heat to the material being sprayed. It is classified under CPC B05B 7/1673, with the more specific subclasses B05B 7/162 and B05B 7/1653 taking precedence.
[B05B 7/168] with means for heating or cooling after mixing  B05B7/201 ,  B05B7/22  take precedence -- This subclass concerns spray or atomising devices that discharge liquids, powders or gases from two or more sources and then condition the combined stream by heating or cooling before it leaves the apparatus. It falls under the hierarchy of performing operations, spraying or atomising in general, and spraying apparatus.
[B05B 7/1686] involving vaporisation of the material to be sprayed or of an atomising-fluid-generating product -- B05B 7/1686 covers spray apparatus that create a spray by vaporising either the material to be discharged or the fluid that forms the atomising medium. Such devices typically combine two or more streams, for example liquid with air or powder with gas, to produce a fine mist or spray.
[B05B 7/1693] with means for heating the material to be sprayed or an atomizing fluid in a supply hose or the like -- B05B 7/1693 refers to spraying apparatus that incorporate means for heating either the material to be sprayed or the atomizing fluid within a supply hose or similar conduit, enabling temperature-controlled atomisation.
[B05B 7/18] the material having originally the shape of a wire, rod or the like -- This subclass covers spraying devices that discharge liquids or other fluents from multiple sources, where the material to be sprayed is initially shaped as a wire, rod, filament or similar solid form before being atomised. The configuration typically involves converting the solid feed into a spray stream by means such as heating, melting, or pressurising with a gas. It is used for processes like powder coating, metal deposition or aerosol generation from solid feedstock.
[B05B 7/20] by flame or combustion -- B05B 7/20 covers spraying devices that create a spray by applying flame or combustion to the material, using the heat of the flame to atomize liquids, powders, or gas-liquid mixtures from multiple sources.
[B05B 7/201] downstream of the nozzle -- B05B 7/201 covers parts or actions located downstream of the nozzle in spraying apparatus, such as mixing chambers, flow-control elements, or devices that modify, shape or treat the spray after it leaves the nozzle.
[B05B 7/203] the material to be sprayed having originally the shape of a wire, rod or the like -- The CPC subclass B05B 7/203 covers spraying devices that feed material that is initially in the form of a wire, rod or similar solid shape, which is then atomised with a second fluid such as air or gas. It falls under spraying apparatus that combine at least two sources, typically a material stream and a carrier gas.
[B05B 7/205] the material to be sprayed being originally a particulate material -- B05B 7/205 covers spray devices that handle materials that are initially in particulate form, such as powders, granules or solid particles, before they are dispersed as a spray. The apparatus includes means to feed, fluidise and atomise the particles, often mixing them with a carrier gas or liquid.
[B05B 7/206] in a container fixed to the discharge device -- This subclass concerns spray devices whose supply container is permanently attached to the discharge unit, so the liquid, powder or gas is fed directly from a fixed tank or bottle integrated with the nozzle. It includes arrangements where the container forms part of the atomising head, allowing simultaneous or sequential release with a second fluid such as air.
[B05B 7/208] the material to be sprayed being heated in a container -- The B05B 7/208 subclass covers spraying devices in which the material to be atomised is heated inside a container before discharge. It applies to apparatuses that combine heating with the mixed-source ejection of liquids, powders or gases, extending the general spraying apparatus category.
[B05B 7/22] electrically , e.g. by arc -- B05B 7/22 covers spraying devices that employ electrical energy, such as an electric arc, to atomise or mix two or more fluid streams for discharge. The classification applies to apparatus that combine liquids, powders or gases and rely on an electrical discharge to create a fine spray.
[B05B 7/222] using an arc -- B05B 7/222 refers to spraying devices that create the spray by means of an electric arc, using the arc's heat or plasma to atomise liquids, powders or gases discharged from two or more sources. This technique is applied in equipment where the arc assists the formation of a fine spray.
[B05B 7/224] the material having originally the shape of a wire, rod or the like -- B05B 7/224 pertains to spraying apparatus designed for discharging materials from two or more sources, specifically where one of the materials is in the form of a wire, rod, or similar shapes before being processed and sprayed. This classification covers devices that transform such solid forms into a sprayable state alongside another material, like a liquid or gas.
[B05B 7/226] the material being originally a particulate material -- B05B 7/226 covers spraying apparatus that discharge materials originally in particulate form, such as powders or granules, often combined with a carrier gas or liquid for atomisation. The subclass includes devices that mix solid particles with air, gas, or liquid streams to create a spray.
[B05B 7/228] using electromagnetic radiation, e.g. laser -- B05B 7/228 classifies spraying apparatus that employ electromagnetic radiation-such as laser beams-to assist the discharge or atomisation of liquids, powders or other fluent materials from multiple sources. The radiation may be used to heat, vaporise or otherwise energise the feedstock, improving spray formation, direction or droplet size control.
[B05B 7/24] with means, e.g. a container, for supplying liquid or other fluent material to a discharge device -- B05B 7/24 relates to spraying apparatus that includes a provision, such as a container or reservoir, for feeding liquid, powder or other fluent material to the nozzle or discharge unit. The supply means may be integrated with the spray head to allow simultaneous or sequential delivery from multiple sources.
[B05B 7/2402] Apparatus to be carried on or by a person, e.g. by hand; Apparatus comprising containers fixed to the discharge device  B05B7/0012  takes precedence -- The subclass B05B 7/2402 covers portable spraying devices that are intended to be carried or hand-operated, including systems where one or more fluid or gas containers are fixed directly to the discharge head. It pertains to compact apparatuses designed for personal use, such as handheld spray guns with integrated supply tanks.
[B05B 7/2405] using an atomising fluid as carrying fluid for feeding, e.g. by suction or pressure, a carried liquid from the container to the nozzle  B05B7/2459  -  B05B7/247  take precedence -- B05B 7/2405 covers spraying devices that use an atomising fluid such as air or gas as a carrier to draw a second liquid from its container and deliver it to the nozzle, typically by suction or pressure. This subclass falls under spraying or atomising in general within the broader class of performing operations.
[B05B 7/2408] characterised by the container or its attachment means to the spray apparatus -- The subclass B05B 7/2408 covers spray devices that are defined by the design of the container or the means by which the container is attached to the apparatus, including integrated or detachable tanks for liquids, powders or gases and the coupling mechanisms that connect them to the spray nozzle.
[B05B 7/241] the container being pressurised -- B05B 7/241 covers spraying devices in which the reservoir or container holding the liquid, powder or other fluent material is deliberately pressurised to promote discharge. The pressurisation may be achieved by gas, compressed air or mechanical means and is used in apparatus that combine two or more sources, such as liquid and air, to create a spray. This sub-class falls under spraying apparatus for discharge of liquids or other fluent materials.
[B05B 7/2413] with means for changing the position or the orientation of the container relative to the spray apparatus -- This subclass covers spray devices that discharge liquids, powders or gases from multiple sources and includes mechanisms that let the container be repositioned or reoriented relative to the spray head. It applies to apparatus where the container's angle or placement can be adjusted during operation.
[B05B 7/2416] characterised by the means for producing or supplying the atomising fluid, e.g. air hoses, air pumps, gas containers, compressors, fans, ventilators, their drives -- B05B 7/2416 covers spraying apparatus whose distinguishing feature is the means for producing or supplying the atomising fluid, including air hoses, air pumps, gas containers, compressors, fans, ventilators and their drives.
[B05B 7/2418] Air pumps actuated by the operator, e.g. manually actuated -- Air-pump spraying devices in which the air source is driven directly by the operator, for example with a hand-operated pump, belong to this subclass. The pumps supply the pressurised air that mixes with the liquid or powder to create the spray. This classification covers apparatus where the operator's manual action provides the airflow for atomisation.
[B05B 7/2421] Gas containers -- B05B 7/2421 covers containers that store or supply gas used in spraying apparatus that combine two or more streams, such as air or other gases with liquids, powders or other fluids. The classification includes pressure vessels, gas bottles and similar devices that feed gas to the spray system.
[B05B 7/2424] the carried liquid and the main stream of atomising fluid being brought together downstream of the container before discharge  B05B7/2435  takes precedence -- The B05B 7/2424 class covers spray devices that combine the liquid to be sprayed with the principal atomising stream only after the liquid leaves its container, mixing them downstream before ejection. This arrangement differs from earlier mixing points and is superseded by the more specific B05B 7/2435 subclass.
[B05B 7/2427] and a secondary stream of atomising fluid being brought together in the container or putting the carried liquid under pressure in the container -- The classification covers spray devices that introduce a secondary atomising fluid, such as air or gas, into the same container as the primary liquid, mixing the streams inside the vessel, or that pressurise the carried liquid within the container to create the spray. These arrangements use two or more sources to generate the discharge.
[B05B 7/2429] the carried liquid and the main stream of atomising fluid being brought together after discharge  B05B7/2435  takes precedence -- B05B 7/2429 covers spraying devices that discharge a liquid or other fluent material from one source and an atomising fluid from a separate source, with the two streams meeting only after they have left the nozzle. The classification is subordinate to B05B 7/2435, which has priority.
[B05B 7/2432] and a secondary stream of atomising fluid being brought together in the container or putting the carried liquid under pressure in the container -- B05B 7/2432 covers spraying devices that combine a secondary atomising fluid with the primary liquid inside the container, or that pressurise the liquid within the container before discharge, enabling the formation of a fine spray from two or more sources. This subclass falls under spraying apparatus that uses dual streams, such as liquid-air or powder-gas mixtures.
[B05B 7/2435] the carried liquid and the main stream of atomising fluid being brought together by parallel conduits placed one inside the other -- The CPC subclass B05B 7/2435 covers spraying apparatus where a carried liquid and the main stream of atomising fluid are merged by parallel conduits arranged one inside the other, allowing the two streams to be discharged together.
[B05B 7/2437] and a secondary stream of atomising fluid being brought together in the container or putting the carried fluid under pressure in the container -- This subclass covers spray devices that combine a primary fluid with a secondary atomising fluid inside the same container, either by mixing the streams within the vessel or by pressurising the carried fluid to generate the spray. It applies to apparatus for discharging liquids, powders, gases or mixtures from two or more sources.
[B05B 7/244] using carrying liquid for feeding, e.g. by suction, pressure or dissolution, a carried liquid from the container to the nozzle  B05B7/2459  -  B05B7/247  take precedence -- B05B 7/244 covers spraying devices that combine two or more feeds, such as liquid with air or powder with gas, where a carrying liquid transports a second liquid from its container to the nozzle using suction, pressure or dissolution. The subclass falls under spraying apparatus in the broader performing operations hierarchy.
[B05B 7/2443] the carried liquid and the main stream of carrying liquid being brought together downstream of the container before discharge  B05B7/2454  takes precedence -- The CPC subclass B05B 7/2443 covers spraying devices in which a liquid carried in a container is combined with the main stream of the carrying fluid after they leave the container, so the mixing occurs downstream of the container before discharge. This arrangement is distinguished from the later subclass B05B 7/2454, which has priority over it.
[B05B 7/2445] and a secondary stream of carrying liquid being brought together in the container or putting the carried liquid under pressure in the container -- The subclass covers spraying devices that mix a secondary liquid serving as a carrier with the primary material inside the container, or that pressurise the carrier liquid within the container before discharge. It relates to equipment that discharges liquids or other fluent substances from multiple sources, such as liquid-air or powder-gas combinations, by combining streams in the container.
[B05B 7/2448] the carried liquid and the main stream of carrying liquid being brought together after discharge  B05B7/2454  takes precedence -- Spraying apparatus that discharges liquids or other fluent materials from two or more sources, such as a liquid carrier and an air stream, and where the carried liquid and the main stream of the carrying fluid are combined only after leaving the nozzle. This subclass follows B05B 7/2454, which has priority.
[B05B 7/2451] and a secondary stream of carrying liquid being brought together in the container or putting the carried liquid in the container -- This subclass covers spraying devices in which a secondary liquid stream, serving as a carrier, is combined with the primary stream inside the container or is introduced into the container containing the carried liquid. The arrangement enables mixing of liquids or fluids before discharge, often used for atomising mixtures of liquid and gas.
[B05B 7/2454] the carried liquid and the main stream of carrying liquid being brought together by parallel conduits, one conduit being in the other -- This subclass covers spray devices that combine the liquid to be discharged with its carrying stream by using parallel conduits arranged coaxially, one conduit situated inside the other, so the two streams merge before atomisation. It falls under the broader group for spraying or atomising apparatus that use multiple fluid sources.
[B05B 7/2456] and a secondary stream of carrying liquid being brought together in the container or putting the carried liquid under pressure in the container -- This CPC subclass covers spray devices that combine a secondary liquid stream with a primary spray medium inside the container, either by mixing the liquids within the vessel or by pressurising the secondary liquid in the container before discharge. It falls under performing operations, spraying or atomising in general, and spraying apparatus.
[B05B 7/2459] a liquid being fed by capillarity from the container to the nozzle -- This subclass covers spraying apparatus where a liquid is drawn by capillary action from its container to the nozzle, enabling mixed discharge of fluid and air or other gas, typically in devices that combine two or more sources.
[B05B 7/2462] using a carrying liquid flowing through the container for dissolving a block of solid material -- The code B05B 7/2462 designates spraying devices that dissolve a solid block by passing a carrying liquid through the container before discharge. It applies to apparatuses where the liquid both transports the solid and forms part of the spray, such as in dual-source atomisers mixing dissolved solids with air or gas.
[B05B 7/2464] a liquid being fed by mechanical pumping from the container to the nozzle -- B05B 7/2464 relates to spraying apparatus in which a liquid is conveyed from its container to the nozzle by a mechanical pump, often to be mixed with a gas stream for atomisation. This subclass falls under performing operations, spraying or atomising in general, and spraying apparatus.
[B05B 7/2467] a liquid being fed by a pressure generated in the container, which is not produced by a carrying fluid -- The classification code B05B 7/2467 pertains to spraying apparatus designed for discharging liquids or other fluent materials from multiple sources, specifically where the liquid is propelled by pressure generated within its container, and this pressure is not created by a carrying fluid.
[B05B 7/247] a liquid being fed by gravity only from the container to the nozzle  B05B7/2478  takes precedence -- This classification code pertains to spraying apparatus designed for discharging liquids or other fluent materials from multiple sources, specifically where the liquid is fed by gravity alone from the container to the nozzle. A more specific subcategory exists for cases involving additional features, indicating a hierarchical preference in classification.
[B05B 7/2472] comprising several containers -- This subclass refers to spray devices that combine the outputs of two or more separate containers, such as a liquid reservoir and an air source or a powder hopper and a gas source, to form the spray. It covers arrangements where the multiple containers are integral to the apparatus and the streams are mixed at the nozzle or immediately before discharge.
[B05B 7/2475] comprising a container carried on the back of the user -- A spray device whose reservoir is mounted on the user's back, allowing the wearer to carry the supply while operating the apparatus. The back-borne container can hold liquids, powders, gases or mixtures that are discharged, often combined with air or another fluid, for portable spraying in various applications.
[B05B 7/2478] Gun with a container which, in normal use, is located above the gun -- B05B 7/2478 pertains to spraying guns in which the supply container is positioned above the gun during normal operation, facilitating the combined discharge of liquids, gases or powders from separate sources. The design is used in devices that mix a fluid and a propellant such as air to atomise the material. It falls under the CPC subclass for spraying apparatus employing two or more feed streams.
[B05B 7/2481] with a flexible container for liquid or other fluent material -- This CPC subclass covers spraying apparatus that uses a flexible container to hold liquid or other fluent material, enabling discharge from multiple sources such as liquid and air or powder and gas. It falls under B05B 7/2481 within the hierarchy.
[B05B 7/2483] the supplying means involving no pressure or aspiration, e.g. means involving gravity or capillarity  B05B7/2459 ,  B05B7/247  take precedence -- The B05B 7/2483 subclass covers supplying means for spray or atomising devices that operate without external pressure or aspiration, such as systems that rely on gravity flow or capillary action to feed liquid, powder, gas or other fluent materials from multiple sources.
[B05B 7/2486] with means for supplying liquid or other fluent material to several discharge devices -- This subclass covers spraying devices that incorporate a distribution system capable of feeding the same liquid or other fluid to multiple nozzles or discharge units simultaneously. The design enables a single supply line to serve several spray heads, often used in applications requiring uniform coverage or concurrent operation of multiple jets.
[B05B 7/2489] an atomising fluid, e.g. a gas, being supplied to the discharge device  B05B7/2402 ,  B05B7/2483 ,  B05B7/262  take precedence -- B05B 7/2489 covers spraying apparatus that discharges liquids or other fluent materials from two or more sources, where an atomising fluid such as a gas is supplied to the discharge device to create the spray. It falls under the hierarchy of performing operations → spraying or atomising in general → spraying apparatus, and is subordinate to the broader subclasses B05B 7/2402, B05B 7/2483 and B05B 7/262.
[B05B 7/2491] characterised by the means for producing or supplying the atomising fluid, e.g. air hoses, air pumps, gas containers, compressors, fans, ventilators, their drives -- The Cooperative Patent Classification entry B05B 7/2491 pertains to spraying apparatus designed for discharging materials from multiple sources, focusing on components that produce or supply the atomising fluid, such as air hoses, pumps, gas containers, compressors, and ventilators, including their drives.
[B05B 7/2494] a liquid being supplied from a pressurized or compressible container to the discharge device -- This subclass covers spraying or atomising devices in which the liquid feed is provided directly from a pressurised or compressible container to the discharge nozzle or mixing chamber. It pertains to apparatus that combine the stored pressurised liquid with air, gas or other medium for spray generation.
[B05B 7/2497] several liquids from different sources being supplied to the discharge device -- This subclass covers spray devices that receive two or more liquids from separate sources and deliver them together to a discharge nozzle, often in combination with a gas or air stream. It describes apparatus designed to mix or atomise multiple fluids simultaneously during spraying.
[B05B 7/26] Apparatus in which liquids or other fluent materials from different sources are brought together before entering the discharge device -- B05B 7/26 covers spraying devices that mix liquids, powders, gases or other fluent materials from two or more separate sources before the mixture reaches the nozzle or discharge unit, enabling the combined stream to be atomised or sprayed in a single operation. This includes configurations that blend, for example, a liquid with air or a powder with a carrier gas within the apparatus.
[B05B 7/262] a liquid and a gas being brought together before entering the discharge device -- This subclass covers spray devices that combine a liquid and a gas upstream of the nozzle, forming a premixed stream that is then discharged. The arrangement may involve mixing chambers, injectors or venturis that unite the two phases before atomisation. It is used for applications such as coating, cooling or humidifying.
[B05B 7/265] the liquid being fed by gravity, or sucked into the gas -- The B05B 7/265 subclass covers spraying devices that introduce the liquid into the gas stream either by gravity feed or by suction (aspiration) into the gas, enabling atomisation of the combined fluid. It applies to apparatus where the liquid source is not actively pumped but drawn or fed passively as part of the spray generation process.
[B05B 7/267] the liquid and the gas being both under pressure -- B05B 7/267 covers spraying apparatus that discharges a liquid and a gas at the same time, with both fluids supplied under pressure, as part of the subclass for multi-source spraying devices.
[B05B 7/28] in which one liquid or other fluent material is fed or drawn through an orifice into a stream of a carrying fluid -- B05B 7/28 covers spray devices in which a liquid or other fluent material is introduced through an orifice into a flowing carrier fluid such as air or gas, creating a mixed jet for atomisation or coating. The classification applies to apparatus that feed or draw the secondary material into the primary stream to generate the spray.
[B05B 7/30] the first liquid or other fluent material being fed by gravity, or sucked into the carrying fluid -- This subcategory of spraying apparatus covers configurations where the primary liquid or other fluent material is introduced into the spray stream by gravity feed or is drawn into the carrier fluid by suction. It applies to devices that combine two or more sources, such as liquid with air, within the nozzle to create a spray.
[B05B 7/32] the fed liquid or other fluent material being under pressure -- B05B 7/32 covers spraying devices in which the liquid or other fluent material is forced out under pressure, typically by a pump, compressor or gas-driven system, to form a spray jet from two or more sources such as liquid and air or powder and gas. This subclass falls under spraying apparatus in the broader category of performing operations.
[B05B 9/00] Spraying apparatus for discharge of liquids or other fluent material, without essentially mixing with gas or vapour -- B05B 9/00 covers spraying devices that discharge liquids or other fluid materials directly, without substantially mixing them with a gas or vapour. It includes apparatuses such as nozzles, sprayers and atomisers that rely on pressure, gravity or mechanical action to form a spray, rather than employing air-assisted or pneumatic atomisation. The class is used for equipment that delivers a liquid stream in the form of droplets for applications like coating, cleaning or irrigation.
[B05B 9/002] incorporating means for heating or cooling, e.g. the material to be sprayed -- B05B 9/002 covers spray apparatus that incorporate means for heating or cooling the material to be sprayed, allowing temperature control of the liquid or other fluent substance during discharge. This subclass falls under performing operations → spraying or atomising in general → spraying apparatus.
[B05B 9/005] the liquid or other fluent material being a fluid close to a change of phase -- This subclass covers spray devices that discharge liquids or other fluent substances that are near a phase change, such as superheated or near-boiling fluids, without substantial mixing with a gas or vapor. It applies to apparatuses that rely on the material's proximity to boiling or condensation to form the spray.
[B05B 9/007] At least a part of the apparatus, e.g. a container, being provided with means, e.g. wheels, for allowing its displacement relative to the ground -- This subclass covers spray apparatus that includes mobility means, such as wheels attached to the container or other parts, allowing the device to be displaced relative to the ground while dispensing liquids or other fluent material without mixing with gas.
[B05B 9/01] Spray pistols, -- Spray pistols are handheld devices that discharge liquids or other fluent materials as a spray without mixing them with gas or vapor, using a pump or pressurised chamber to atomise the fluid for tasks such as cleaning, painting, or pest control.
[B05B 9/03] characterised by means for supplying liquid or other fluent material -- B05B 9/03 classifies spraying apparatus that are distinguished by the inclusion of means for supplying the liquid or other fluent material to the discharge zone, such as pumps, feeders, reservoirs or conduits. The provision of a dedicated supply system is the defining feature of this subgroup.
[B05B 9/035] to several spraying apparatus  B05B9/0423  takes precedence -- B05B 9/035 covers spraying apparatus that discharge liquids or other fluent materials without essentially mixing them with a gas or vapour, specifically designs involving several simultaneous spray units. This subclass falls under spraying or atomising in general and is superseded by the more specific B05B 9/0423.
[B05B 9/04] with pressurised or compressible container -- B05B 9/04 covers spray devices that use a pressurised or compressible container to store the liquid or other fluent material, providing the driving pressure for discharge. These apparatuses dispense the product directly without intentional mixing with a gas or vapour, and fall under the broader categories of performing operations, spraying or atomising in general, and spraying apparatus.
[B05B 9/0403] with pumps for liquids or other fluent material  B05B9/043  takes precedence -- The classification code B05B 9/0403 pertains to spraying apparatus designed for the discharge of liquids or other fluent materials without significant mixing with gas or vapor, specifically featuring pumps. This entry is part of a broader hierarchy that includes performing operations and general spraying or atomizing processes.
[B05B 9/0406] with several pumps -- A spraying apparatus that discharges liquids or other fluent material without significant mixing with gas, and incorporates two or more pumps to provide the spray. This configuration allows independent or combined pumping actions to regulate flow, pressure, or delivery sequence, improving control and performance of the spray. It falls under the CPC group B05B 9/0406 for multi-pump spray devices.
[B05B 9/0409] the pumps being driven by a hydraulic or a pneumatic fluid -- This subclass covers spray apparatus whose liquid-moving pumps are driven by a hydraulic or pneumatic fluid instead of mechanical or electric power. It includes any pump powered by pressurised fluid used to discharge liquids or other fluent material in the spray device.
[B05B 9/0413] with reciprocating pumps, e.g. membrane pump, piston pump, bellow pump  B05B9/0409  takes precedence -- Spraying apparatus that discharges liquids or other fluent material using a reciprocating pump such as a membrane, piston or bellow type, without substantial mixing with gas or vapour. This falls under the CPC subclass B05B 9/0413, which is a narrower division of B05B 9/0409.
[B05B 9/0416] with pumps comprising rotating pumping parts, e.g. gear pump, centrifugal pump, screw-type pump  B05B9/042  takes precedence -- A spraying apparatus that discharges liquids or other fluent material without mixing with gas, employing rotating pumping parts such as gear, centrifugal or screw-type pumps.
[B05B 9/042] with peristaltic pumps -- B05B 9/042 includes spraying devices that employ peristaltic pumps to move liquids or other fluent materials through a nozzle, delivering the fluid without essential mixing with gas or vapour. The rotating rollers compress a flexible hose, generating a continuous flow that is then atomised by the apparatus.
[B05B 9/0423] for supplying liquid or other fluent material to several spraying apparatus -- A B05B 9/0423 device provides a common feed line or manifold that distributes liquid or other fluent material to multiple spray heads or nozzles, enabling simultaneous operation of several spraying units without additional mixing with gas or vapour. This arrangement simplifies fluid supply and ensures consistent delivery across the connected apparatus.
[B05B 9/0426] with a pump attached to the spray gun or discharge device  single-units hand-held apparatus in which the flow is effected by a pump  B05B11/10 -- The CPC subclass B05B 9/0426, nested under Performing Operations → Spraying or Atomising in General → Spraying Apparatus, pertains to spray guns or discharge devices that incorporate an attached pump, such as single-unit hand-held units where the pump provides the liquid flow, and corresponds to B05B11/10.
[B05B 9/043] having pump readily separable from container -- This CPC subclass B05B 9/043 covers spraying apparatus where the pump is readily separable from the liquid container, allowing easy removal for cleaning, replacement or refilling. It falls under spraying or atomising in general and pertains to devices that discharge liquids without essential mixing with gas or vapour.
[B05B 9/047] supply being effected by follower in container, e.g. membrane or floating piston -- The B05B 9/047 entry covers spraying devices where the liquid is delivered by a follower within the container, such as a flexible membrane or a floating piston, that pushes the material toward the spray nozzle without mixing it with gas. This arrangement is classified under spraying or atomising in general, specifically under spraying apparatus for discharge of liquids or other fluent material.
[B05B 9/06] the delivery being related to the movement of a vehicle, e.g. the pump being driven by a vehicle wheel -- This CPC subclass B05B 9/06 pertains to spraying devices whose liquid delivery is driven by the motion of a vehicle, for example a pump that is powered directly by a wheel or other moving part of the vehicle. It falls under the hierarchy of performing operations, spraying or atomising in general, and spraying apparatus.
[B05B 9/08] Apparatus to be carried on or by a person, e.g. of knapsack type -- B05B 9/08 covers portable spraying devices meant to be worn or carried by a person, such as knapsack sprayers, that discharge liquids or other fluent materials without substantially mixing them with gas or vapour. These apparatuses are used for manual spraying in agricultural, horticultural or similar fields.
[B05B 9/0805] comprising a pressurised or compressible container for liquid or other fluent material  B05B9/085  takes precedence -- B05B 9/0805 covers spraying or atomising devices that discharge a liquid or other fluent material without substantially mixing it with a gas or vapour, and that incorporate a pressurised or compressible container to hold the material. This subclass falls under PERFORMING OPERATIONS → SPRAYING OR ATOMISING IN GENERAL → SPRAYING APPARATUS, and is superseded by the more specific B05B 9/085.
[B05B 9/0811] comprising air supplying means actuated by the operator to pressurise or compress the container -- A spraying device that discharges liquids or other fluent materials without mixing them with gas, and includes air-supplying means that the operator actuates to pressurise or compress the container, enabling the spray.
[B05B 9/0816] the air supplying means being a manually actuated air pump -- This subclass refers to spray devices that discharge liquids or other fluent materials without essential mixing with gas, and where the air-supplying means is a manually operated pump.
[B05B 9/0822] a discharge device being fixed to the container -- The code B05B 9/0822 designates a spray apparatus in which the discharge device is fixed directly to the container holding the liquid or other fluent material. The device releases the product without substantial mixing with gas or vapour, serving general spraying or atomising functions.
[B05B 9/0827] the air pump being actuated by shaking -- B05B 9/0827 covers spray devices that discharge liquids or other fluent material without mixing with gas, in which the air pump is powered by manual shaking. The shaking motion compresses a pump chamber, drawing in air that is then released to atomise the liquid when the nozzle is opened. This subclass falls under spraying apparatus in the broader performing-operations hierarchy.
[B05B 9/0833] comprising a compressed gas container, e.g. a nitrogen cartridge -- B05B 9/0833 covers spraying devices that discharge liquids or other fluent materials without essential mixing with gas or vapour and that incorporate a compressed-gas container such as a nitrogen cartridge to provide the propelling pressure. This subclass falls under spraying apparatus in the broader category of performing operations.
[B05B 9/0838] supply being effected by follower in container, e.g. membrane or floating piston, or by deformation of container -- B05B 9/0838 classifies spraying devices whose material is fed from the container by a follower mechanism rather than a pump, using elements such as a flexible membrane, a floating piston or deformation of the container walls to push the liquid toward the spray nozzle.
[B05B 9/0844] the container being pressurised or compressed by a gas generated by a chemical reaction -- This CPC subclass covers spraying devices in which the liquid reservoir is pressurised by a gas produced through a chemical reaction, rather than by an external compressor. It applies to apparatus that discharge liquids or other fluent materials without mixing with a separate gas stream, falling under the broader categories of performing operations, spraying or atomising, and spraying apparatus.
[B05B 9/085] with a liquid pump -- A spraying apparatus that discharges liquids or other fluent material without substantially mixing them with gas or vapour, and that incorporates a liquid pump to generate the flow. This falls under the broader categories of performing operations, spraying or atomising in general, and spraying apparatus.
[B05B 9/0855] the pump being motor-driven  B05B9/0866 ,  B05B9/0872  take precedence -- This classification entry, B05B 9/0855, pertains to spraying apparatus for discharging liquids or other fluent materials without mixing with gas or vapor, specifically where the pump is motor-driven. However, more specific subclasses such as B05B 9/0866 and B05B 9/0872 take precedence when applicable.
[B05B 9/0861] the motor being electric -- This subclass covers spraying devices in which the driving motor is electric, indicating that the pump or agitator that creates the spray pressure is powered by an electric motor rather than by pneumatic, hydraulic, or manual means. It applies to any spray apparatus that relies on electricity to operate its fluid-moving components.
[B05B 9/0866] the pump being a gear, centrifugal or screw-type pump -- B05B 9/0866 designates spraying devices whose feed pump is a gear, centrifugal or screw-type pump, used to deliver liquids or other fluent material without essential gas mixing. The classification lies under spraying apparatus within the broader performing operations category.
[B05B 9/0872] the pump being a peristaltic pump -- A peristaltic pump incorporated in a spraying apparatus that discharges liquids or other fluent material without substantial mixing with gas or vapour. The pump moves fluid through a flexible tube by compressing it with rotating rollers, providing a smooth, pulsation-free flow suitable for precise spray applications.
[B05B 9/0877] the pump being of pressure-accumulation type or being connected to a pressure accumulation chamber -- B05B 9/0877 classifies spraying apparatus that discharges liquids or other fluent material using a pump of the pressure-accumulation type or one connected to a pressure-accumulation chamber, falling under the broader categories of performing operations, spraying or atomising in general, and spraying apparatus.
[B05B 9/0883] having a discharge device fixed to the container -- The classification concerns spray devices whose discharge nozzle or any other release element is permanently attached to the container holding the fluid, so it cannot be removed as a separate part. This arrangement is typical of aerosol cans, pump bottles and similar dispensers.
[B05B 9/0888] Carrying means for knapsack sprayers -- A B05B 9/0888 knapsack-sprayer carrying means is a device that supports, secures or transports a backpack-type liquid sprayer, enabling the user to move the apparatus while keeping the sprayer's weight balanced and its operation ready. It is classified under spraying apparatus that discharges liquids without mixing with gas.
[B05B 9/0894] Gun with a container which, in normal use, is located above the gun -- A spraying gun whose supply container is positioned above the gun in normal operation, allowing the liquid or other fluent material to flow downward into the gun for discharge without mixing with a gas or vapour. This arrangement is typical for handheld spray devices that rely on gravity or a pump to feed the material from the elevated tank into the nozzle.
[B05C] APPARATUS FOR APPLYING FLUENT MATERIALS TO SURFACES, IN GENERAL -- **Definition:** This place covers:  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Brushes -> A46B - Implements for writing and drawing -> B43K  **Glossary:** - Coating: The applied material. A coating may be a solidified layer originally applied as a liquid (e.g. dried paint) or a layer of material which, once applied, remains in a liquid or semi-liquid state (e.g. lubricant). - Fluidised-bed technique: A technique used to cause dry particulate material to behave like a fluid. This is commonly achieved by the introduction of a pressurised fluid into the material and promotes a high degree of contact between the fluid and material. - Liquid or fluent: designates materials which can flow, e.g. liquids, including solutions, dispersions and suspensions, as well as semi-liquids, pastes, melts and particulate materials. - Particulate materials: Solid materials in the form of very small pieces, e.g. powders, granules, short fibres or chips.
