[B05C 1/00] Apparatus for applying liquids or other fluent materials to surfaces -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Apparatus for applying liquids or other fluent materials to a surface by contact of the member carrying the fluid with the work surface, e.g. by using brushes, pads or rollers
[B05C 1/003] incorporating means for heating or cooling the liquid or other fluent material  B05C11/1042  takes precedence -- B05C 1/003 covers apparatus for applying liquids or other fluent materials to surfaces that includes means for heating or cooling the material during application. Such devices may incorporate heating elements, cooling coils or temperature-control systems to adjust the material temperature as it is sprayed or atomised. The more specific subclass B05C11/1042, which details heating or cooling, takes precedence over this broader group.
[B05C 1/006] for applying liquid or other fluent material to the edges of essentially flat articles -- This CPC subclass B05C 1/006 covers devices that apply liquid or other fluent material specifically to the edges of essentially flat articles. It falls under apparatus for applying fluent materials to surfaces, within the broader categories of spraying or atomising and performing operations.
[B05C 1/02] for applying liquid or other fluent material to separate articles -- This CPC subclass B05C 1/02 covers apparatus designed to apply liquids or other fluent substances onto separate articles, often using spray or atomising methods, and is used for coating, cleaning or treating distinct objects.
[B05C 1/022] to the outer surface of hollow articles  B05C1/027  takes precedence -- B05C 1/022 covers apparatus that apply liquids or other fluent materials to the outer surfaces of hollow objects such as tubes, bottles or containers. This includes devices like spray nozzles, brushes or rollers designed to coat the exterior of such articles. The more specific sub-class B05C 1/027, dealing with particular methods or mechanisms, takes precedence over this broader category.
[B05C 1/025] to flat rectangular articles, e.g. flat sheets  B05C1/027  takes precedence -- This CPC subclass covers devices that apply liquids, powders, or other fluid substances onto flat rectangular items such as sheets or plates. The apparatus is designed to uniformly treat the surface of these planar objects for processes like coating, cleaning, or printing. It falls under B05C 1/025, with B05C 1/027 as a more specific subcategory.
[B05C 1/027] only at particular parts of the articles  at particular parts of work of indefinite length  B05C1/16 -- B05C 1/027 pertains to apparatus for applying liquids or other fluent materials to surfaces, specifically designed to apply material only at particular parts of articles or workpieces, including those of indefinite length. This classification is part of the broader category of spraying or atomising in general and falls under the Cooperative Patent Classification system used by the EPO and USPTO.
[B05C 1/04] for applying liquid or other fluent material to work of indefinite length -- B05C 1/04 covers devices that deposit liquids or other fluent substances onto a continuously extending workpiece such as webs, belts, or moving substrates. These apparatuses are used in processes like coating, printing, or cleaning where the material is applied while the work moves indefinitely.
[B05C 1/06] by rubbing contact, e.g. by brushes, by pads -- B05C 1/06 covers apparatus that apply liquids or other fluent substances to a surface by rubbing contact, such as brushes, pads or similar elements that spread the material through direct mechanical contact rather than by spraying or atomising.
[B05C 1/08] using a roller -- This subclass covers devices that apply liquids or other fluent materials to a surface by means of a roller. It includes rollers that spread or deposit the material as they rotate over the target area and falls within the broader group of apparatus for applying fluent materials in spraying or atomising operations.
[B05C 1/0804] the material being applied without contact with the roller -- B05C 1/0804 covers devices that apply liquids or other fluent substances to a surface without the material touching a roller, using methods such as airflow, pressure or vibration to project the material onto the target area. This sub-group falls under apparatus for applying fluent materials in general.
[B05C 1/0808] Details thereof, e.g. surface characteristics -- B05C 1/0808 covers the specification of surface-related features of devices that apply liquids or other fluent substances to a substrate, including the texture, roughness, wettability or other characteristics of the surface that affect application performance.
[B05C 1/0813] characterised by means for supplying liquid or other fluent material to the roller -- B05C 1/0813 refers to devices for applying liquids or other fluent materials in which the roller receives the material through a dedicated supply means, such as a pipe, channel or spray nozzle, allowing continuous coating or treatment of a surface. This feature distinguishes the apparatus from other rollers that are manually or indirectly fed. It falls under the broader group for spraying or atomising in general.
[B05C 1/0817] characterised by means for removing partially liquid or other fluent material from the roller, e.g. scrapers  coating roller co-operating with dosing rollers  B05C1/0834 -- This subclass concerns apparatus that includes a device, such as a scraper, for removing partially liquid or other fluent material from a coating roller, particularly when the coating roller operates together with dosing rollers.
[B05C 1/0821] characterised by driving means for rollers or work -- B05C 1/0821 covers apparatus for applying liquids or other fluent materials to surfaces that incorporate a driving mechanism for the rollers or for the workpiece itself. The driving means power the motion of the rollers or the material-bearing element, enabling continuous or intermittent application during spraying or atomising operations.
[B05C 1/0826] the work being a web or sheets  B05C1/0804 ,  B05C1/0808 ,  B05C1/0813 ,  B05C1/0817 ,  B05C1/0821 ,  B05C1/10 ,  B05C1/12  take precedence -- B05C 1/0826 pertains to apparatus for applying liquids or other fluent materials onto webs or sheets, with specific focus on the material form and application process. This classification is part of a broader hierarchy dealing with spraying or atomising in general and includes considerations for various types of work surfaces and methods.
[B05C 1/083] being passed between the coating roller and one or more backing rollers -- B05C 1/083 covers apparatus where the coating medium is conveyed between a primary coating roller and one or more backing rollers that support, tension or guide the material, enabling uniform application of liquids or other fluent substances to a surface.
[B05C 1/0834] the coating roller co-operating with other rollers, e.g. dosing, transfer rollers -- This subclass covers coating rollers that operate in conjunction with additional rollers such as dosing or transfer rollers to apply liquids or other fluent materials onto a surface. It relates to apparatus for applying such materials, where the rollers cooperate to control film thickness, distribution, or feed of the coating.
[B05C 1/0839] the work being unsupported at the line of contact between the coating roller and the work -- The subclass deals with coating or spraying apparatus in which the material to be coated is not supported at the nip line where a roller contacts the workpiece. Such unsupported work at the line of contact enables uniform application of liquids or other fluent media on surfaces without backing support.
[B05C 1/0843] the work being backed up by gas jet means for pushing the material in contact with the coating roller -- This subclass covers coating devices where a gas jet backs up the workpiece, creating a pressure flow that pushes the liquid or fluent material into contact with a moving coating roller for uniform application. It pertains to apparatuses that use gas-assisted delivery in spray or atomising systems for surface treatment.
[B05C 1/0847] the circumferential speed of the coating roller and the work speed having same direction but different value -- This classification covers devices where the coating roller rotates at a circumferential speed that differs from the linear speed of the workpiece, even though both move in the same direction. It relates to mechanisms that adjust roller and substrate speeds to control film thickness or application quality in liquid coating operations.
[B05C 1/0852] the circumferential speed of the coating roller being higher than the work speed -- This subclass concerns coating machines where the roller carrying the liquid or other fluent material rotates at a higher circumferential speed than the workpiece moves, creating a differential-speed transfer of the coating. The arrangement is used to improve film thickness control and uniformity in spraying or atomising applications.
[B05C 1/0856] Reverse coating rollers -- Reverse coating rollers are cylindrical tools that receive coating material on one side and transfer it to a substrate from the opposite side, enabling uniform thin films in printing, laminating or other coating processes. They are classified under apparatus for applying fluent materials to surfaces.
[B05C 1/086] a pool of coating material being formed between a roller, e.g. a dosing roller and an element cooperating therewith -- The B05C 1/086 subclass concerns devices that create a film of coating material between a dosing roller and a cooperating element, forming a pool of liquid that is transferred to a surface during operation. This arrangement is used in apparatus for applying liquids or other fluent substances, typically within broader spraying or atomising equipment.
[B05C 1/0865] the cooperating element being a roller, e.g. a coating roller -- B05C 1/0865 covers devices that apply liquids or other fluent substances to a surface where the moving element that works together with the spray or flow is a roller, such as a coating roller. These apparatuses are used in spraying or atomising operations to distribute material evenly across surfaces.
[B05C 1/0869] the work contacting the pool -- This subgroup covers devices where the material being sprayed or atomised is applied to a work that is part of a pool, such as the pool surface, walls, or water body itself. It includes apparatus designed to treat, clean, or coat pool surfaces by directing liquids or fluent substances onto them.
[B05C 1/0873] Controlling means responsive to conditions of the liquid or other fluent material, of the ambient medium, of the roller or of the work -- Class B05C 1/0873 covers apparatus for applying liquids or other fluent materials to surfaces that include controlling means which respond to conditions of the material, the ambient medium, the roller, or the workpiece.
[B05C 1/0878] responsive to the pressure applied between two rollers, e.g. between the coating roller and a backing roller or between the coating roller and a dosing roller -- The subclass B05C 1/0878 covers apparatus that controls the amount of liquid or other fluent material applied to a surface by responding to the pressure between two rollers, for example between a coating roller and a backing roller or between a coating roller and a dosing roller, adjusting flow according to that pressure.
[B05C 1/0882] responsive to the distance between two rollers, e.g. between the coating roller and a backing roller -- The B05C 1/0882 subclass covers apparatus that automatically adjust or react to the spacing between two rollers, such as a coating roller and a backing roller, to control the flow of liquids or other fluent substances onto a surface during spraying or coating operations. This enables precise regulation of film thickness and uniformity in processes like printing, painting, or laminating.
[B05C 1/0886] responsive to the condition of the work -- B05C 1/0886 covers apparatus that applies liquids or other fluent substances to a surface and automatically adapts its operation according to the condition of the workpiece, such as thickness, texture, or temperature. The devices may include sensors, feedback controls or variable-rate mechanisms that modify spray or flow in response to real-time measurements.
[B05C 1/0891] responsive to the speed of moving of the work -- A B05C 1/0891 device is an apparatus for applying liquids or other fluent materials to surfaces that automatically adjusts its delivery or spray parameters in response to the speed at which the workpiece moves, ensuring consistent coverage regardless of line speed.
[B05C 1/0895] responsive to the thickness of the weight of material applied to the work -- This subclass covers apparatus that automatically adjust the delivery of liquids or other fluent materials based on the measured thickness or weight of the coating already applied, enabling precise control of layer buildup on a workpiece.
[B05C 1/10] the liquid or other fluent material being supplied from inside the roller -- B05C 1/10 covers devices that apply liquids or other fluent substances to a surface by feeding the material from inside a roller, such as rollers with internal reservoirs that discharge the fluid onto the workpiece during rotation. This subclass falls under apparatus for applying fluent materials in general, within the broader categories of spraying or atomising.
[B05C 11/00] Component parts, details or accessories not specifically provided for in groups -- **Definition:** This place covers:
[B05C 11/02] Apparatus for spreading or distributing liquids or other fluent materials already applied to a surface -- **Definition:** This place covers:  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Hand tools for removing partially or for spreading or redistributing applied fluent materials, e.g. colour touchers -> B05C17/10
[B05C 11/021] Apparatus for spreading or distributing liquids or other fluent materials already applied to the surface of an elongated body, e.g. a wire, a tube -- B05C 11/021 relates to apparatus that spreads or distributes liquids or other fluent materials already deposited on the surface of an elongated body such as a wire or tube. The devices are designed to move along the length of the item and evenly redistribute the coating, often using rollers, wipers or similar mechanisms, and fall under the broader group for spraying or atomising and apparatus for applying fluent materials to surfaces.
[B05C 11/023] Apparatus for spreading or distributing liquids or other fluent materials already applied to a surface  B05C11/021 ,  B05C11/04  take precedence -- B05C 11/023 pertains to apparatus designed for the spreading or distribution of liquids and other fluent materials that have already been applied to a surface, with specific emphasis on components not covered in more detailed categories. This classification is part of the broader context of spraying and atomising technology, focusing on operational mechanisms and accessories used post-application.
[B05C 11/025] with an essentially cylindrical body, e.g. roll or rod -- B05C 11/025 covers devices for applying fluent materials to surfaces that incorporate an essentially cylindrical body such as a roll or rod, used to transfer liquids, pastes, powders, or gases in a spraying or atomising operation.
[B05C 11/026] with an elongated body renewable by feeding it across the surface -- This subclass covers devices where an elongated body, such as a tube or pipe that supplies the material, is moved or fed continuously across the target surface while the material is applied, allowing continual renewal of the dispensing length. It relates to spray or atomising apparatus where the feed mechanism traverses the workpiece.
[B05C 11/028] with a body having a large flat spreading or distributing surface -- B05C 11/028 covers apparatus for applying fluent materials that incorporate a body with a large flat surface used to spread or distribute the material over a target area, such as rollers, spreaders or plate-like extensions attached to spray devices. The provision applies to component parts, details or accessories that are not otherwise listed in more specific groups.
[B05C 11/04] with blades -- This subgroup covers apparatus that atomises or sprays by using blades to fragment a fluid as it passes, including rotating or stationary blade arrangements that assist the discharge of material onto a surface. It falls under the broader class of devices for applying fluent materials in general.
[B05C 11/041] characterised by means for positioning, loading, or deforming the blades -- B05C 11/041 covers devices for spraying or atomising where the rotating or vibrating blades are equipped with means to position, load or deform them, such as adjustable holders, tensioning systems or shaping elements, enabling precise control of the blade orientation and movement during operation.
[B05C 11/042] allowing local positioning, loading or deforming along the blades -- This subclass covers features in spray or atomising devices where the blades can be individually positioned, loaded or deformed to control local spray characteristics, allowing precise adjustment of fluid delivery across the surface. It includes mechanisms that modify blade spacing, angle or shape during operation to vary the distribution of the applied material.
[B05C 11/044] characterised by means for holding the blades -- The entry B05C 11/044 covers components for spray or atomising devices that are specifically designed to hold the blades used to form the spray. It includes brackets, clamps, or other holder structures that secure rotary or vibratory blades within the apparatus, and is classified under component parts and accessories not otherwise provided for in other groups.
[B05C 11/045] characterised by the blades themselves -- This subgroup concerns spray or atomising machines and lists component parts, details or accessories whose defining feature is the blades themselves, such as their shape, geometry, edge or mounting. It covers blade designs that affect the formation of the fluent spray and is used when the blades are the primary characteristic of the apparatus.
[B05C 11/047] provided with end dams -- B05C 11/047 pertains to apparatus for applying fluent materials to surfaces that are equipped with end dams - barriers at the ends of feed channels or nozzles that block back-flow and prevent material leakage, thereby improving control and reducing waste.
[B05C 11/048] Scrapers, i.e. metering blades having their edge oriented in the upstream direction in order to provide a reverse angle of attack -- B05C 11/048 covers scrapers or metering blades whose cutting edge faces upstream, creating a reverse angle of attack that shifts the material flow back toward the inlet. These components are used in spraying or atomising devices to control the delivery of fluent substances onto surfaces, and are classified as accessories not otherwise specified in the apparatus group.
[B05C 11/06] with a blast of gas or vapour -- This subclass covers component parts, details and accessories of apparatus for applying fluent materials that use a blast of gas or vapour to convey the material onto a surface, where the specific element is not provided for in any other more specific group.
[B05C 11/08] Spreading liquid or other fluent material by manipulating the work, e.g. tilting -- This subgroup covers devices or components that spread liquids or other fluent substances by moving or tilting the workpiece rather than using dedicated spray heads, such as mechanisms that rely on gravity or surface inclination to distribute the material. It is part of the broader group for spraying or atomising and apparatus for applying fluent materials.
[B05C 11/10] Storage, supply or control of liquid or other fluent material -- B05C 11/10 pertains to the storage, supply, or control mechanisms for liquids or other fluent materials used in apparatus for applying these materials to surfaces. This classification covers components that manage the flow and storage but are not detailed in more specific groups within the same class.
[B05C 11/1002] Means for controlling supply, i.e. flow or pressure, of liquid or other fluent material to the applying apparatus, e.g. valves -- This subgroup concerns devices that regulate the flow or pressure of liquids or other fluent materials supplied to spraying or atomising equipment, such as valves and similar control mechanisms. It includes any means for adjusting the supply to the applying apparatus, without specifying particular component designs.
[B05C 11/1005] responsive to condition of liquid or other fluent material already applied to the surface, e.g. coating thickness, weight or pattern -- B05C 11/1005 classifies apparatus for applying fluent or powdery substances to a surface that adjusts its operation in response to the condition of material already deposited, such as coating thickness, weight, or pattern, enabling feedback-controlled spraying or atomising.
[B05C 11/1007] responsive to condition of liquid or other fluent material  B05C11/1005  takes precedence -- B05C 11/1007 covers apparatus for applying fluent materials to surfaces that adjust their operation in response to a condition of the liquid or other fluent material, such as viscosity, pressure, temperature or flow rate. The device includes sensors or control mechanisms that modify spray or atomisation parameters automatically, improving precision or efficiency. It builds on the broader group B05C 11/1005, which deals with general spraying or atomising equipment.
[B05C 11/101] responsive to weight of a container for liquid or other fluent material; responsive to level of liquid or other fluent material in a container -- B05C 11/101 covers spray or atomising devices that modify their operation in response to the weight or level of the liquid or other fluent material in the container, such as weight-sensing or level-sensing controls that adjust flow or pressure automatically.
[B05C 11/1013] responsive to flow or pressure of liquid or other fluent material  B05C11/101  takes precedence; control of flow in general  G05D7/00 ; control of fluid pressure in general  G05D16/00 -- B05C 11/1013 pertains to component parts or accessories for apparatus that apply fluent materials to surfaces, specifically those responsive to the flow or pressure of the liquid or material. This classification takes precedence over more general control mechanisms and is closely related to broader categories on flow and fluid pressure control.
[B05C 11/1015] responsive to a conditions of ambient medium or target, e.g. humidity, temperature  B05C11/1005  takes precedence ; responsive to position or movement of the coating head relative to the target -- B05C 11/1015 covers apparatus for applying fluent materials that automatically adjust the spraying or atomising operation in response to ambient conditions such as humidity or temperature, or to the position or movement of the coating head relative to the target, extending the broader group B05C 11/1005.
[B05C 11/1018] responsive to distance of target -- B05C 11/1018 classifies devices for applying fluent materials-such as sprays, atomisers, or powders-whose operation adjusts automatically according to the distance between the nozzle and the target surface. The entry covers mechanisms, sensors or control systems that vary flow, pressure or spray pattern as the target moves closer or farther away.
[B05C 11/1021] responsive to presence or shape of target  B05C11/1018  takes precedence -- B05C 11/1021 covers components, details or accessories of spraying or atomising devices that adjust the delivery of a fluent material in response to the presence or shape of the target surface. It includes sensors, actuators or control mechanisms that modify spray pattern, flow rate or nozzle orientation when the target is detected, and is placed under apparatus for applying fluent materials to surfaces in the hierarchy of performing operations - spraying or atomising in general.
[B05C 11/1023] responsive to velocity of target, e.g. to web advancement rate -- B05C 11/1023 covers components of spray or atomising apparatus that automatically adjust their operation according to the speed of the target surface, such as mechanisms that vary flow or nozzle movement in line with a moving web's advancement rate.
[B05C 11/1026] Valves  in general  F16K ;  B05C5/0225  takes precedence -- B05C 11/1026 designates valve components used in general spraying or atomising equipment, covering the structure and function of valves that regulate the flow of fluent materials onto surfaces. It is a catch-all for valve details not otherwise specified, with precedence given to related groups in F16K and B05C5/0225.
[B05C 11/1028] Lift valves -- Lift valves are components that control the upward movement of a valve seat to open or close the flow of liquid or gas in spraying or atomising equipment. They are used in apparatus for applying fluent materials to surfaces, allowing precise regulation of discharge during operation. This subclass covers the design and function of such valves in general spray devices.
[B05C 11/1031] Gate valves; Sliding valves -- B05C 11/1031 covers gate and sliding valve components used in spray or atomising equipment, such as valve bodies, stems, seats and related accessories that regulate the flow of fluid to the applicator. It applies to parts not otherwise specified in the main groups of the apparatus for applying fluent materials to surfaces.
[B05C 11/1034] specially designed for conducting intermittent application of small quantities, e.g. drops, of coating material  B05C5/0225 ,  B05C11/1026  take precedence -- B05C 11/1034 pertains to apparatus components for applying fluent materials in small, intermittent quantities, such as drops, to surfaces. This classification is specific to parts not covered under more detailed categories and is used when the application method involves precise, controlled dispensing of coating material.
[B05C 11/1036] Means for supplying a selected one of a plurality of liquids or other fluent materials, or several in selected proportions, to the applying apparatus -- B05C 11/1036 covers devices that select and deliver one of several liquids or other fluent materials, or mixtures in defined ratios, to spraying or atomising equipment. It pertains to component parts, details or accessories for apparatuses that apply fluids to surfaces.
[B05C 11/1039] Recovery of excess liquid or other fluent material; Controlling means therefor -- The code relates to devices that recover surplus liquid or other fluid during spraying or atomising processes and includes mechanisms that control that recovery. It covers component parts, details or accessories not otherwise classified.
[B05C 11/1042] provided with means for heating or cooling the liquid or other fluent material in the supplying means upstream of the applying apparatus -- B05C 11/1042 covers spraying or atomising devices whose supply system includes integrated heating or cooling elements that condition the liquid or other fluent material before it reaches the applying nozzle or other dispensing part. The feature may be built into the pump, tank, or feed line and is intended to control temperature for optimal atomisation or coating performance. This sub-group falls under apparatus for applying fluent materials to surfaces.
[B05C 11/1044] Apparatus or installations for supplying liquid or other fluent material to several applying apparatus or several dispensing outlets, e.g. to several extrusion nozzles -- The subclass covers apparatus or installations that feed a liquid or other fluent material to multiple applying devices or dispensing outlets at once, for example systems that supply several extrusion nozzles simultaneously. It is part of the spraying or atomising group for equipment that applies fluent materials to surfaces.
[B05C 11/1047] Apparatus or installations for supplying liquid or other fluent material comprising a buffer container or an accumulator between the supply source and the applicator -- B05C 11/1047 covers devices that feed a liquid or other fluent material to a spray or atomising applicator through an intermediate buffer tank or accumulator, which smooths flow and stores material between the source and the nozzle. This sub-group falls under spraying or atomising in general and pertains to general-purpose apparatus for applying fluent substances to surfaces.
[B05C 11/105] by capillary action, e.g. using wicks -- B05C 11/105 covers parts of spray or atomising devices that convey fluids to a surface by capillary action, for example using wicks. It includes any component, detail or accessory that relies on capillary forces to transport the liquid and is not specifically defined in other groups.
[B05C 11/11] Vats or other containers for liquids or other fluent materials -- B05C 11/11 covers vats, tanks and similar containers designed to hold liquids or other fluent substances for use in spraying or atomising operations. The subclass includes the construction, shape, materials and accessories of such vessels when they serve as reservoirs for the fluid to be applied.
[B05C 11/115] Sealing means for work inlet or outlet -- This subclass covers sealing devices that close or protect the material inlet or outlet of spray or atomising equipment used for applying fluent substances to surfaces. It includes gaskets, O-rings, caps, and other accessories that prevent leakage or contamination of the feed line while the apparatus is in operation.
[B05C 1/12] the work being fed round the roller -- In this CPC subclass the workpiece is conveyed around a rotating roller that transfers or distributes a liquid or other fluent material onto its surface, as in coating, printing or similar processes. The classification covers mechanisms where the substrate is fed past the roller rather than the fluid delivery system itself.
[B05C 1/14] using a travelling band -- The B05C 1/14 subclass relates to devices that apply liquids or other fluent materials to a surface by moving the material on a travelling band such as a belt or chain. It is a sub-category of apparatus for applying fluent materials to surfaces within the broader spraying or atomising group.
[B05C 1/16] only at particular parts of the work -- B05C 1/16 covers apparatus that deliver liquids or other fluent substances to a workpiece in a localized manner, applying the material only to selected regions rather than the whole surface. Such devices are used when precise coating, cleaning, or treatment of specific parts is required, often employing nozzles, masks or controlled motion to restrict the spray to the target area.
[B05C 1/165] using a roller or other rotating member which contacts the work along a generating line -- This subclass covers apparatus that applies liquids or other fluent materials to a surface by means of a roller or other rotating element that contacts the work along a generating line, maintaining continuous contact. It falls under spraying or atomising in general and relates to devices that use a rotating member to distribute material.
[B05C 13/00] Means for manipulating or holding work, e.g. for separate articles -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Means for handling or holding work pieces, e.g. relatively small articles like cans or bottles
[B05C 13/02] for particular articles -- This subclass covers apparatus that applies sprays, atomised or other fluent materials to specific items, incorporating means for manipulating, holding or supporting the workpiece such as fixtures, jigs or holders that are tailored for particular articles during the coating or treatment process.
[B05C 13/025] relatively small cylindrical objects, e.g. cans, bottles -- The B05C 13/025 subclass covers apparatus that handles and applies relatively small cylindrical objects such as cans or bottles onto a work surface, typically as part of a spraying or atomising system. It includes means for manipulating, gripping or holding these items while the material is dispensed onto the target area.
[B05C 15/00] Enclosures for apparatus -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Cover or sheath for the apparatus  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Spray boots -> B05B16/00
[B05C 17/00] Hand tools or apparatus using hand held tools, for applying liquids or other fluent materials to, for spreading applied liquids or other fluent materials on, or for partially removing applied liquids or other fluent materials from, surfaces -- **Definition:** This place covers:  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Brushes -> A46B
[B05C 17/001] incorporating means for heating or cooling the liquid or other fluent material  B05C17/00523  takes precedence -- Hand-held tools or devices for applying, spreading or partially removing liquids or other fluent materials that integrate a heating or cooling element to adjust the temperature of the material during use, such as paint-spray guns, rollers or applicators with built-in temperature control, falling under subclass B05C 17/001 and taking precedence over B05C 17/00523.
[B05C 17/002] with feed system for supplying material from an external source; Supply controls therefor  B05C17/02  takes precedence -- The B05C 17/002 subclass covers hand-held or manually operated devices for applying liquids or other fluent substances to surfaces that incorporate a feed system drawing material from an external source, together with controls for regulating that supply. These tools are designed for spreading, coating, or partially removing applied fluids and include mechanisms such as pumps, valves or adjustable flow devices.
[B05C 17/003] with means for filling or refilling the hand tool container -- B05C 17/003 pertains to handheld devices for applying or spreading liquids, pastes or other fluent substances on surfaces that include a mechanism for filling or refilling the tool's container, such as pumps or reservoirs integrated into the hand-held unit.
[B05C 17/005] for discharging material through an outlet orifice by pressure -- **Definition:** This place covers:
[B05C 17/00503] Details of the outlet element  B05C17/00523  takes precedence; nozzles for deformable tubular containers  B65D35/38 -- This classification code, B05C 17/00503, pertains to the details of outlet elements in hand tools or apparatus used for applying liquids or other fluent materials to surfaces. It specifically addresses components like nozzles but notes that a more specific code, B05C17/00523, takes precedence for certain detailed aspects and deformable tubular containers are classified under B65D35/38.
[B05C 17/00506] Means for connecting the outlet element to, or for disconnecting it from, the hand tool or its container -- This CPC subclass covers devices that join or detach the nozzle or outlet part of a hand-held spray or atomising tool to the tool itself or to its liquid container. It includes couplings, quick-release fittings, clamps and similar mechanisms designed to secure or release the dispensing element during operation or maintenance.
[B05C 17/00509] of the bayonet type -- The B05C 17/00509 subclass concerns hand-held apparatus for applying or spreading liquids that feature a bayonet-type coupling, allowing quick attachment and removal of the spraying or dispensing head. This design is used in tools where rapid interchange of tips or nozzles is needed during surface coating or cleaning operations.
[B05C 17/00513] of the thread type -- The B05C 17/00513 subclass covers hand-operated tools that apply liquids or other fluent substances using a thread-type mechanism, such as a screw-driven nozzle or dispensing tip, to spread or partially remove the material on a surface. It falls under apparatus for applying fluent materials, specifically within spraying or atomising operations.
[B05C 17/00516] Shape or geometry of the outlet orifice or the outlet element -- The CPC subclass B05C 17/00516 covers features of the outlet orifice or outlet element that determine its shape or geometry in hand-operated devices for applying liquids or other fluent materials. It falls under the broader groups for spraying or atomising in general and apparatus for applying fluent materials to surfaces, specifying how the orifice design influences flow and distribution.
[B05C 17/0052] Accessories therefor -- The code B05C 17/0052 covers accessories for hand-operated devices that apply liquids or other fluent substances to surfaces, such as nozzles, hoses, spreader attachments, or cleaning elements. These components assist in directing, controlling, spreading, or partially removing the applied material during spraying or atomising operations.
[B05C 17/00523] provided with means to heat the material -- This subclass covers hand-held devices for spreading or removing liquids or other fluent substances that incorporate a heating element to warm the material during application.
[B05C 17/00526] the material being supplied to the apparatus in a solid state, e.g. rod, and melted before application  B05C17/0054  takes precedence -- This classification entry pertains to hand tools or apparatus that apply liquids or other fluent materials to surfaces, specifically those where the material is initially supplied in a solid state, such as rods, and must be melted prior to application. The entry clarifies that another code, B05C17/0054, takes precedence when applicable.
[B05C 17/0053] the driving means for the material being manual, mechanical or electrical -- B05C 17/0053 covers hand-held tools or apparatus for applying liquids or other fluent materials to surfaces, focusing on the driving means that move the material, whether they are manual, mechanical or electrical. It sits under performing operations, spraying or atomising in general, and apparatus for applying fluent materials to surfaces.
[B05C 17/00533] comprising a piston -- The B05C 17/00533 subclass covers hand-held tools that employ a piston mechanism to apply, spread, or partially remove liquids or other fluent substances on a surface. These devices are used for spraying, atomising, or otherwise delivering material in a controlled manner and fall under the broader category of apparatus for applying fluent materials.
[B05C 17/00536] the driving means comprising one or more rollers -- This subclass covers hand-held devices for applying, spreading or partially removing liquids or other fluent materials where the drive mechanism is provided by one or more rollers that move the application element across the surface.
[B05C 17/0054] the driving means for the material being pneumatic or hydraulic -- B05C 17/0054 covers hand-operated devices that apply liquids or other fluent substances to surfaces, where the material is propelled by pneumatic or hydraulic driving mechanisms. It includes tools such as sprayers or spreaders that use compressed air or fluid pressure to move the product rather than mechanical force.
[B05C 17/00543] comprising a piston -- B05C 17/00543 designates hand-held tools that apply liquids or other fluent substances to a surface by means of a piston that forces the material out, such as piston-driven sprayers or spreaders. It belongs to the broader group of apparatus for applying fluent materials in the spraying or atomising class.
[B05C 17/00546] Details of the heating means -- B05C 17/00546 covers the specific features of the heating elements incorporated in hand-held devices that spray, atomise or otherwise apply liquids or other fluent materials to surfaces. It addresses how the heater is integrated, powered, thermostated or otherwise configured to assist material flow or curing during application.
[B05C 17/0055] Burners, e.g. for catalytic combustion specially adapted for hand tools or apparatus using hand tools -- B05C 17/0055 classifies hand-held burners, especially catalytic combustion types, that are incorporated into tools for applying or spreading liquids, paints or other fluent substances. The entry covers portable devices designed to generate heat or flame as part of spray-or-atomising equipment used in surface treatment operations.
[B05C 17/00553] with means allowing the stock of material to consist of at least two different components -- This subclass covers hand-operated devices used to apply liquids or other fluent substances to a surface that incorporate a feed system allowing the material stock to be composed of at least two distinct components, such as two-part adhesives or mixed paints, enabling on-site combination during application.
[B05C 17/00556] with means for adjusting the proportions of the components -- The subclass B05C 17/00556 covers hand-held tools or devices for applying liquids or other fluent materials to surfaces that incorporate means for adjusting the proportion of the components being applied, enabling the user to vary the mixture ratio during spraying, spreading or partial removal.
[B05C 17/00559] the different components being stored in coaxial chambers -- B05C 17/00559 covers hand-held spray or atomising tools in which the separate liquids, powders or other fluent materials are kept in coaxial chambers, allowing the components to be stored side-by-side and delivered together through a single nozzle for application to a surface.
[B05C 17/00563] the chambers being at least initially placed in series -- This subgroup covers hand-held spraying or atomising devices in which two or more chambers are arranged sequentially, so the material passes through one chamber before entering the next. The series arrangement is used to pre-treat, mix, or condition a fluid before it is applied to a surface. It applies to tools that spread or partially remove liquids or other fluent materials.
[B05C 17/00566] with a dynamic mixer in the nozzle -- A hand-held spraying device that includes a dynamic mixing element inside the nozzle, blending two or more fluids just before they are ejected. The integrated mixer ensures continuous, homogeneous mixing while the operator applies the combined liquid to a surface, improving coverage and performance for paints, adhesives or similar materials.
[B05C 17/00569] with a pump in the hand tool -- This subclass covers hand-held devices that incorporate a pump mechanism to apply or spread liquids, aerosols or other fluent substances onto surfaces, such as sprayers, brushes or rollers with integrated pumps. It falls under apparatus for applying fluent materials, specifically hand tools with a pump.
[B05C 17/00573] the reservoir or container being pneumatically or hydraulically pressurized  B05C17/0054 ,  B05C17/015  take precedence -- This subclass covers hand-held devices for applying liquids or other fluent materials where the material is stored in a reservoir that is pressurised pneumatically or hydraulically to force the fluid onto the target surface. It builds on the broader categories of spraying or atomising equipment and takes precedence over the related groups B05C17/0054 and B05C17/015.
[B05C 17/00576] characterised by the construction of a piston as pressure exerting means, or of the co-operating container -- The entry concerns hand-held tools for spreading, smoothing or partially removing liquids or other fluent materials on surfaces that generate pressure by means of a piston or a cooperating container, rather than by conventional pumps. These devices fall within the larger group of spraying or atomising equipment.
[B05C 17/00579] comprising means for allowing entrapped air to escape to the atmosphere -- A hand-held apparatus for applying liquids or other fluent materials that incorporates a vent or passage allowing trapped air to be released to the atmosphere, preventing pressure buildup and ensuring continuous, uniform application of the material to a surface.
[B05C 17/00583] the container for the material to be dispensed being deformable -- B05C 17/00583 covers hand-held tools for applying liquids in which the reservoir itself is flexible or collapsible, allowing the container to deform as the material is discharged. This feature is typical of spray or atomising devices where the deformable container assists controlled delivery of the fluid onto a surface.
[B05C 17/00586] Means, generally located near the nozzle, for piercing or perforating the front part of a cartridge -- This subclass relates to hand-held applicators that include a piercing or perforating element positioned close to the nozzle to breach the front of a cartridge and release its fluent contents. The feature is defined for devices used in spraying or atomising operations.
[B05C 17/00589] comprising a guiding rotating element, e.g. a wheel -- A hand-held tool for applying, spreading or partially removing liquids or other fluent materials on a surface that incorporates a guiding rotating element, such as a wheel, to direct and control the material flow.
[B05C 17/00593] Hand tools of the syringe type -- The code B05C 17/00593 designates hand-held syringe-type tools that force liquids or other fluent materials through a nozzle for application, spreading, or partial removal on a surface. Such devices use a plunger mechanism to deliver precise amounts of fluid in spraying, coating or cleaning operations.
[B05C 17/00596] The liquid or other fluent material being supplied from a rigid removable cartridge having no active dispensing means, i.e. the cartridge requiring cooperation with means of the handtool to expel the material -- A hand-held tool that applies liquids or other fluent substances using a rigid, removable cartridge that contains no built-in pump or valve, so the tool itself must provide the pressure or mechanical action to expel the material onto a surface. This arrangement is typical for simple spray or spreading devices where the cartridge is a passive container.
[B05C 17/01] with mechanically or electrically actuated piston or the like -- B05C 17/01 covers hand-held devices for applying liquids, paints or other fluent substances that incorporate a mechanically or electrically driven piston or similar mechanism to dispense the material onto a surface.
[B05C 17/0103] with electrically actuated piston or the like -- B05C 17/0103 covers hand-held or hand-operated devices for applying liquids, powders or other fluent substances to surfaces that incorporate an electrically powered piston or similar actuator to drive the material onto the workpiece. The classification includes sprayers, brushes or rollers that use the piston mechanism to control flow and pressure during application.
[B05C 17/0106] with means for longitudinally cutting container walls during relative displacement of the piston and its cooperating container -- A hand-held dispensing tool that applies liquids or other fluent materials to a surface while it includes a mechanism that longitudinally cuts the wall of its container as the piston moves relative to the container, enabling controlled release of the product during operation.
[B05C 17/0109] the piston being pulled during application -- B05C 17/0109 pertains to hand tools or apparatus where a piston is pulled during the application of liquids or other fluent materials to surfaces, facilitating the spreading or partial removal of these materials. This method ensures controlled and precise application in various spraying or atomising tasks.
[B05C 17/0113] the piston rod being flexible or articulated  B05C17/0109  takes precedence -- B05C 17/0113 covers hand-held tools or apparatus for applying liquids or other fluent materials to surfaces in which the piston rod is flexible or articulated, allowing the tip to follow irregular contours during spraying or spreading. It is a narrower subclass of B05C 17/0109, which broadly covers hand tools for applying or removing fluent materials from surfaces.
[B05C 17/0116] characterised by the piston driving means  B05C17/0103 ,  B05C17/0109 ,  B05C17/0113 ,  B05C17/015  take precedence -- B05C 17/0116 covers hand-held tools for applying liquids or other fluent materials that are characterized by a piston-driving mechanism, with the specific piston configurations defined in the preceding groups B05C 17/0103, B05C 17/0109, B05C 17/0113 and B05C 17/015 taking precedence. Such devices use the piston to pressurize and deliver the material onto a surface, typically in spraying or atomising operations.
[B05C 17/012] Stepwise advancing mechanism, e.g. pawl and ratchets -- This subclass covers hand-held devices that apply liquids or other fluids to surfaces and incorporate a stepwise advancing mechanism such as a pawl and ratchet to control the motion of the applicator. The mechanism moves in discrete increments, allowing precise dosing or patterned application.
[B05C 17/0123] Lever actuated -- B05C 17/0123 includes hand-held tools that employ a lever mechanism to apply, spread or partially remove liquids or other fluent materials on surfaces. It is a subclass of spraying or atomising apparatus intended for manual operation.
[B05C 17/0126] comprising an element, e.g. an arc compensating element, articulated at one end on the lever and at the other end on the piston rod driving means, e.g. a pawl -- The subclass defines hand-held devices for applying or spreading liquids on surfaces that incorporate a lever-mounted component, such as an arc-compensating element, linked at its opposite end to a piston-rod drive mechanism, for example a pawl.
[B05C 17/013] comprising a freely rotating element, e.g. a roller, between the lever and a piston rod driving means, e.g. a pawl -- The CPC subclass B05C 17/013 covers hand-held tools for applying liquids or other fluent materials that include a freely rotating element, such as a roller, placed between the lever and the piston-rod driving means, for example a pawl, to transmit motion smoothly while spreading the material.
[B05C 17/0133] Nut and bolt advancing mechanism, e.g. threaded piston rods -- B05C 17/0133 covers hand-operated devices that use a nut-and-bolt or threaded piston rod to advance a spray or atomising element, allowing the user to control the delivery of liquids or other fluent materials onto a surface. The mechanism translates rotational motion into linear movement of the applicator tip.
[B05C 17/0136] comprising an energy storing element, e.g. a spring, for exerting, e.g. when released, pressure on the material  B05C17/015  takes precedence -- This subclass covers hand-held devices that apply liquids or other fluids to surfaces and incorporate an energy-storing element such as a spring to generate pressure on the material being applied. The mechanism releases the stored energy to press the fluid onto the target surface. The finer subclass B05C 17/015 has priority over this entry.
[B05C 17/014] comprising means for preventing oozing  B05C17/0146  takes precedence -- This subclass covers hand-held devices that spread or apply liquids or other fluids to surfaces and include features that stop the material from leaking or oozing after application. It falls under spraying or atomising, apparatus for applying fluent materials.
[B05C 17/0143] comprising means for cutting or cleaning the cartridge nozzle tip -- This subclass covers hand-operated tools or devices that apply liquids or other fluent materials to surfaces and include features for cutting or cleaning the tip of a cartridge nozzle, enabling precise or renewed spray application. It falls under spraying or atomising in general, specifically apparatus for applying fluent materials with a hand-held tool.
[B05C 17/0146] comprising a valve in the proximity of the nozzle that is not actuated by pressure, e.g. manually actuated -- This subclass covers hand-held tools or devices for applying liquids or other fluent materials to surfaces that include a valve positioned close to the nozzle and operated manually rather than by fluid pressure. The valve allows the user to start or stop flow directly at the tip, enabling precise control during spreading, coating or partial removal of the material.
[B05C 17/015] with pneumatically actuated piston or the like -- This subclass pertains to hand-held devices that apply liquids or other fluent materials by means of a piston driven with pneumatic pressure, such as air-powered spray guns or paint-applicator tools. It covers tools where a compressed-gas-actuated piston ejects or spreads the fluid onto a surface, falling under the broader category of apparatus for applying fluent materials.
[B05C 17/02] Rollers -- This subclass covers hand-operated rollers that apply, spread or partially remove liquids or other fluent materials on surfaces, forming part of apparatus for spraying or atomising in general and used in painting, coating or cleaning operations.
[B05C 17/0202] Hand tools comprising a coating endless belt -- Hand tools that incorporate a continuous coating belt for applying, spreading or partially removing liquids, paints or other fluent materials on surfaces. The endless belt moves the material along the tool, enabling uniform coverage during manual operation.
[B05C 17/0205] characterised by the handle, e.g. handle shape or material  B05C17/0217 ,  B05C17/022  take precedence -- B05C 17/0205 covers hand-held tools or apparatus used to apply, spread or partially remove liquids or other fluent materials on surfaces, where the invention is distinguished primarily by the handle's shape, material or construction. More detailed subclasses such as B05C 17/0217 and B05C 17/022 address specific handle features and therefore take precedence over this broader entry.
[B05C 17/0207] characterised by the cover, e.g. cover material or structure, special surface for producing patterns  B05C17/0212 ,  B05C17/0352  take precedence -- This CPC subgroup covers hand-held devices for applying liquids or other fluent materials to surfaces that are distinguished by a cover, such as a specific cover material or structure, or by a specially patterned surface that creates designs during application. The classification applies when the cover or patterned surface is the defining feature, and more specific subgroups B05C17/0212 and B05C17/0352 have priority.
[B05C 17/021] comprising means to avoid excessive crushing of the roller cover, e.g. a spacer -- This subclass concerns hand-operated tools that apply liquids with a roller cover and incorporate a mechanism, such as a spacer, to prevent the roller from being crushed excessively during use.
[B05C 17/0212] the coating surface of the roller being a body of revolution generated by a generatrix that is not a straight line parallel to the roller axis, e.g. for allowing coating of non planar surfaces  B05C17/0227 ,  B05C17/0352  take precedence -- B05C 17/0212 covers hand-held devices for applying liquids or other fluent materials in which the roller's coating surface is a body of revolution formed by a generatrix that is not a straight line parallel to the roller axis, enabling the tool to coat non-planar surfaces. The classification is subordinated to the broader categories of spraying or atomising and apparatus for applying fluent materials, with related subclasses B05C 17/0227 and B05C 17/0352 taking precedence.
[B05C 17/0215] the coating roller itself, i.e. not only its cover being deformable, e.g. to allow proper coating of non-plane surfaces  B05C17/0227  takes precedence -- The entry B05C 17/0215 covers coating rollers whose entire body, not only a deformable cover, can flex to follow irregular or non-planar surfaces during liquid application. It falls under hand-operated apparatus for applying or spreading fluids, enabling uniform coating on complex shapes.
[B05C 17/0217] comprising a frame supporting the coating roller at both ends or being intented to be hold at both ends by the user  B05C17/0222 ,  B05C17/0227 ,  B05C17/0352 ,  B05C17/0357  take precedence -- B05C 17/0217 covers hand-held tools or apparatus for applying, spreading or partially removing liquids or other fluent materials, featuring a frame that supports a coating roller at both ends or is designed to be held by the user at both ends. The definition supersedes narrower sub-entries such as B05C17/0222, B05C17/0227, B05C17/0352 and B05C17/0357.
[B05C 17/022] comprising means for angularly adjusting or allowing angular movement of the roller relative to its handle  B05C17/0227  takes precedence -- B05C 17/022 classifies hand-held tools for applying or spreading liquids or other fluent substances that incorporate a roller whose orientation can be angularly adjusted or allowed to move relative to the handle, enabling the user to vary the spray or coating angle. Subclass B05C 17/0227 provides a more specific definition and therefore takes precedence.
[B05C 17/0222] comprising protecting shields, drip pans, spatter guards or the like  B05C17/03  take precedence -- B05C 17/0222 covers hand-held devices for applying or spreading liquids or other fluent materials that incorporate protective elements such as shields, drip pans or spatter guards to contain overspray or runoff. These devices are used for applying fluids to surfaces and may include features that partially remove material.
[B05C 17/0225] comprising a lateral plate, edge guard or shield -- This subclass covers hand-held devices for applying, spreading or partially removing liquids or other fluent materials that incorporate a lateral plate, edge guard or shield to protect the operator or workpiece and to control the material flow. It is indexed as B05C 17/0225 in the Cooperative Patent Classification.
[B05C 17/0227] comprising several coating rollers  B05C17/0352  takes precedence -- A hand-held apparatus that uses several coating rollers to apply, spread or partially remove liquids or other fluent materials from a surface, falling under the broader category of spraying or atomising in general. This device is classified in B05C 17/0227, with the more specific subclass B05C 17/0352 taking precedence.
[B05C 17/023] all of them having parallel axises -- Category B05C 17/023 covers hand-held devices that apply liquids or other fluent materials to a surface using multiple nozzles or streams whose axes remain parallel to each other. The design is intended for spreading or partially removing the material, and the parallel-axis arrangement distinguishes it from intersecting-axis systems.
[B05C 17/0232] all of them having the same axis -- Hand-held tools or devices that apply, spread or partially remove liquids or other fluent materials from surfaces, where all moving parts share a common axis of rotation, such as rollers, brushes or similar applicators designed to operate about a single axial line.
[B05C 17/0235] Rollers comprising an additional non-rotating applicator or comprising a coating roller replaceable by a non-rotating applicator -- This subclass deals with hand-held rollers used to apply liquids or other fluent substances, where the roller includes an extra non-rotating applicator or can be fitted with a replaceable non-rotating element. It pertains to devices that combine rotating and stationary coating functions for uniform surface treatment.
[B05C 17/0237] comprising means for preventing the rotation of the roller  B05C17/024  takes precedence -- B05C 17/0237 defines hand-held tools for applying liquids or other fluent substances that incorporate means for preventing the roller from rotating, providing controlled and uniform deposition. The anti-rotation mechanism distinguishes this subclass within the broader group of spraying and atomising apparatus.
[B05C 17/024] the coating roller being motor driven -- This subclass covers handheld devices that use a motor-driven roller to apply or spread liquids, paints, inks or other fluent materials onto surfaces. The apparatus integrates an electric or pneumatic motor to rotate the roller, providing continuous coating without manual rolling.
[B05C 17/0242] comprising rotating guiding elements -- This subclass covers hand-held devices for spreading or partially removing liquids that incorporate rotating guiding elements to direct the fluent material onto the target surface, improving coverage and control. The rotating parts may be wheels, rollers or similar components that guide the flow as the tool is moved.
[B05C 17/0245] Accessories -- The B05C 17/0245 subclass groups accessories for hand-held devices that apply liquids or other fluent materials to surfaces, including supplementary parts such as nozzles, couplings, spreaders, holders, or cleaning elements that aid in dispensing, spreading or partially removing the material. These components are designed to cooperate with the primary applying tool and are not integral to the tool's basic structure.
[B05C 17/0247] Rollers for coating under water -- Rollers for coating under water are hand-operated tools designed to apply liquid or other fluent coating materials to surfaces while the workpiece is submerged, ensuring an even film in wet conditions. They belong to the subclass of apparatus for applying fluent materials to surfaces within the broader spraying and atomising group.
[B05C 17/025] with self-contained reservoir -- The class B05C 17/025 in the Cooperative Patent Classification covers hand-held tools that apply liquids or other fluent materials to surfaces and include a self-contained reservoir that stores the material within the device itself. Such apparatuses are used for spreading, coating, or partially removing liquids during manual operation.
[B05C 17/03] with feed system for supplying material from an external source -- B05C 17/03 relates to hand-held devices for applying liquids or other fluent substances to a surface that incorporate a feed system drawing the material from an external source. The classification covers tools that spread, spray or partially remove the fluid while being operated manually.
[B05C 17/0308] the liquid being supplied to the inside of the coating roller -- This sub-class covers devices in which the coating liquid is fed into the interior of a roller, usually through a channel or tubing, so the roller becomes saturated from inside rather than from its outer surface. It applies to hand-operated tools that spread or apply liquids to surfaces.
[B05C 17/0316] with pressurised or compressible container -- Hand-held tools that apply liquids or other fluent materials to surfaces using a pressurised or compressible container, such as spray bottles, paint rollers with built-in pressure, or similar applicators.
[B05C 17/0325] attached to the hand tool, e.g. into the handle -- B05C 17/0325 covers attachments that are built into or fixed to the handle of a hand-operated tool used for applying, spreading or partly removing liquids or other fluent substances on surfaces, such as sprayers, rollers or brushes that form part of the tool's grip.
[B05C 17/0333] with pump -- A hand-operated device that applies liquids or other fluent materials to a surface using a pump, typically for spreading, coating or partially removing the material. It falls under spraying or atomising apparatus that dispense fluid from a hand-held tool.
[B05C 17/0341] attached to the hand tool -- The subclass B05C 17/0341 refers to spraying or atomising devices that are mounted on a hand-held tool, either permanently or releasably, for applying, spreading or partially removing liquids or other fluent materials on a surface. It includes any brackets, handles, or fittings that integrate the spray head directly with the hand tool, enabling the user to operate the apparatus manually.
[B05C 17/035] direct to the outer surface of the roller -- The CPC subclass B05C 17/035 covers hand-held devices that use a roller to apply liquids or other fluent materials directly onto the outer surface of the roller, which then contacts the workpiece. It pertains to tools for spreading, coating or partially removing such materials from surfaces by means of a roller that contacts the material on its outer face.
[B05C 17/0352] indirectly, i.e. by using transfer means, e.g. pads, brushes, rollers or bands -- B05C 17/0352 concerns devices that apply liquids or other fluent substances to a surface indirectly, using a transfer element such as a pad, brush, roller or band rather than direct discharge. It falls under hand-operated tools for spreading, covering or partially removing applied materials.
[B05C 17/0355] using transfer rollers -- B05C 17/0355 covers hand-held tools or apparatus that employ transfer rollers to move liquids or other fluent substances onto, spread over, or partially remove them from a surface. The rollers pick up the material from a reservoir or prior coat and transfer it in a controlled manner, providing a simple means of applying or distributing fluids without spraying.
[B05C 17/0357] directly from a container attached to the hand tool, e.g. by gravity or dipping -- B05C 17/0357 pertains to hand tools for applying liquids or other fluent materials directly from a container attached to the tool, utilizing methods such as gravity or dipping.
[B05C 17/04] Stencil rollers -- Stencil rollers are hand-held tools that carry a patterned screen or stencil to deposit a controlled amount of liquid, paste or other fluent material onto a surface, creating repeatable designs or markings. They belong to the class of apparatus for applying fluent materials and are used for spreading, patterning or partially removing applied substances in tasks such as printing, decoration or marking.
[B05C 17/06] Stencils -- B05C 17/06 denotes hand-held tools or devices that employ stencils to apply, spread or partially remove liquids or other fluent materials onto surfaces, typically in spraying or atomising operations. The classification covers stencil-based apparatus used for controlled deposition of paints, inks or coatings in manual applications.
[B05C 17/08] Stencil holders -- Stencil holders are hand-operated devices that secure a stencil in position on a work surface while liquids, powders or other fluent materials are applied, removed or spread. They ensure precise pattern transfer during spraying, brushing or wiping operations, and are typically designed for easy attachment to tools or frames.
[B05C 17/10] Hand tools for removing partially or for spreading or redistributing applied liquids or other fluent materials, e.g. colour touchers -- B05C 17/10 covers hand-held tools used to spread, redistribute or partially remove liquids or other fluent substances that have already been applied to a surface, such as colour-touching implements. It falls under the CPC hierarchy for apparatus applying fluent materials, specifically spraying or atomising in general.
[B05C 17/12] Other hand tools for producing patterns -- B05C 17/12 pertains to hand-held tools that create decorative or functional patterns on surfaces by applying, spreading or partially removing liquids or other fluent materials. It falls under apparatus for applying fluent materials, specifically those used for spraying or atomising in general, and includes any other manual devices not covered elsewhere in the hand-tool sub-section.
[B05C 19/00] Apparatus specially adapted for applying particulate materials to surfaces -- **Definition:** This place covers:
[B05C 19/001] Flocking -- B05C 19/001 concerns apparatus specially adapted to apply particulate material, particularly short fibres, onto a surface to create a flocked layer. The devices normally use a spray or jet system to distribute the particles onto an adhesive-coated substrate, yielding a uniform, tufted coating used in textiles, carpets and decorative finishes.
[B05C 19/002] Electrostatic flocking  see  for electrostatic projection or pulverisation  B05B -- B05C 19/002 pertains to apparatus specially designed for electrostatic flocking, where charged particulate fibers are projected onto a surface to form a uniform coating. The devices employ electrostatic forces to direct the particles and fall within the broader class of spraying or atomising in general.
[B05C 19/004] from an outlet device in contact, or almost in contact with the work -- B05C 19/004 covers devices that deliver particulate material from an outlet that touches or lies very close to the workpiece, such as spray or powder-coating guns whose nozzle is in direct or near-contact with the surface being treated. The classification pertains to apparatus specially adapted for applying solid or powdered substances to surfaces by means of a contact or almost-contact delivery point.
[B05C 19/005] Apparatus specially designed for applying particulate materials to the inside of hollow work -- A B05C 19/005 device is an apparatus built to introduce particulate or granular material into the interior of a hollow workpiece, such as pipes, tubes or cavities. It typically uses a spraying or atomising mechanism to deliver the particles uniformly along the inner surface for coating, cleaning or treatment purposes.
[B05C 19/007] for applying the particulate materials to the inside of hollow tubes -- B05C 19/007 covers devices designed to introduce particulate substances into the interior of hollow tubes, such as pipes or conduits, for coating, cleaning or treatment. It falls under the broader class of spraying or atomising equipment, specifically apparatus for applying fluent materials to surfaces, and focuses on methods that handle powders, granules or other solid particles inside tubular structures.
[B05C 19/008] Accessories or implements for use in connection with applying particulate materials to surfaces; not provided elsewhere in  B05C19/00 -- The B05C 19/008 subclass includes accessories or implements used with equipment for applying particulate materials to surfaces that are not covered elsewhere in B05C 19/00, such as feeder assemblies, agitators, spreaders, or support structures that aid the spraying or atomising operation.
[B05C 19/02] using fluidised-bed techniques -- B05C 19/02 relates to devices that employ fluidised-bed techniques to apply particulate matter onto surfaces. The classification covers apparatus specially designed to create a fluidised layer of solids and direct the resulting spray or jet onto a target surface for coating, cleaning, or treatment purposes.
[B05C 19/025] Combined with electrostatic means -- The subclass B05C 19/025 defines apparatus for applying particulate materials to surfaces that incorporates electrostatic means to charge the particles or the target, enhancing deposition, adhesion or uniformity. It is a specific type of spraying or atomising equipment where electrostatic charging is built into the application device.
[B05C 19/04] the particulate material being projected, poured or allowed to flow onto the surface of the work -- B05C 19/04 covers devices in which the particulate material itself is the means of application, being projected, poured or otherwise allowed to flow onto the work surface. It belongs to the spray-or-atomising group, specifically to apparatus for applying fluent materials, and focuses on the manner in which solid particles are delivered to the target.
[B05C 19/06] Storage, supply or control of the application of particulate material -- This subclass covers components that store, feed or regulate the delivery of solid particles in devices that spray or atomise them onto surfaces. It includes hoppers, feeders, metering valves and control systems that ensure a consistent particulate flow during application.
[B05C 21/00] Accessories or implements for use in connection with applying liquids or other fluent materials to surfaces, not provided for in groups -- **Definition:** This place covers:
[B05C 21/005] Masking devices  stencils  B05C17/06 ; masking devices for which the means for applying liquids or other fluent material is spraying or is not important  B05B12/20 -- The B05C 21/005 subclass includes accessories or implements used with spray or atomising equipment for applying liquids or other fluent materials to surfaces that are not classified elsewhere, such as masking devices and stencils. These items aid the application process but are not the primary spraying apparatus themselves.
[B05C 3/00] Apparatus in which the work is brought into contact with a bulk quantity of liquid or other fluent material -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Apparatus in which the work is brought into contact with a greater quantity of fluent material by being immersed therein, e.g. dip coating. The work or coating fluid can be moved, e.g. transported or vibrated or agitated, to intensify the contact with the fluid.
[B05C 3/005] incorporating means for heating or cooling the liquid or other fluent material  B05C11/1042  takes precedence -- The B05C 3/005 subclass covers apparatus for applying fluent materials to surfaces in which the workpiece contacts a bulk quantity of liquid or other fluid and the device incorporates heating or cooling means for that fluid. This sub-category falls under spraying or atomising in general and is overridden by the more specific B05C11/1042 entry.
[B05C 3/02] the work being immersed in the liquid or other fluent material -- B05C 3/02 refers to apparatus in which the workpiece is brought into direct contact with a bulk liquid or other fluent material, such as baths, tanks or vats used for cleaning, coating, or treating surfaces. The design immerses the item in the fluid rather than applying the material by spray or atomisation.
[B05C 3/04] with special provision for agitating the work or the liquid or other fluent material -- B05C 3/04 covers apparatus that brings a workpiece into contact with a bulk fluid while providing mechanisms to agitate either the workpiece, the fluid, or both. Such agitation may be achieved by stirring, rotating, vibrating or recirculating the material to improve mixing, coating uniformity or heat transfer during spraying, dipping or similar surface-treatment operations.
[B05C 3/05] by applying vibrations thereto -- B05C 3/05 covers apparatus that brings a workpiece into contact with a bulk liquid or other fluent material and uses vibratory motion to apply the material to the surface. The vibrations may be generated by mechanical, acoustic or ultrasonic means to improve coating uniformity or penetration.
[B05C 3/08] the work and the liquid or other fluent material being agitated together in a container, e.g. tumbled -- B05C 3/08 covers devices where the workpiece and a bulk liquid or other fluent material are agitated together inside a container, for example by tumbling, to apply, treat or clean the surface. This subclass falls under performing operations, spraying or atomising in general, and apparatus for applying fluent materials to surfaces.
[B05C 3/09] for treating separate articles -- B05C 3/09 covers apparatus that treats individual, separate articles by bringing them into contact with a bulk supply of liquid or other fluent material, typically through spraying, atomising or similar applying methods. The devices are used for processes such as cleaning, coating, rinsing or other surface treatments of distinct items.
[B05C 3/10] the articles being moved through the liquid or other fluent material -- B05C 3/10 relates to apparatus in which the work piece is conveyed through a bulk liquid or other fluent material for processing, such as washing, coating, or treatment. The classification covers systems where the items move within the fluid rather than the fluid being sprayed onto stationary objects.
[B05C 3/109] Passing liquids or other fluent materials into or through chambers containing stationary articles -- B05C 3/109 covers machines that move liquids or other fluids through a chamber that holds fixed workpieces, allowing the material to contact the objects for coating, cleaning or treatment. The chamber contains stationary articles while the fluid passes over or through them.
[B05C 3/12] for treating work of indefinite length -- B05C 3/12 covers apparatus that treats work of indefinite length, such as continuous webs or sheets, by bringing the material into contact with a bulk liquid or other fluent substance for coating, cleaning, or similar surface operations. Typical implementations include rollers, dip-tanks, spray devices or other mechanisms that handle a continuously moving substrate without fixed dimensions.
[B05C 3/125] the work being a web, band, strip or the like -- B05C 3/125 refers to equipment that applies a liquid or other fluent material to a moving web, band, strip or similar continuous workpiece. The apparatus brings the work into contact with a bulk quantity of the material, typically as part of a spraying or atomising process for coating, cleaning or treating such elongated items.
[B05C 3/132] supported on conveying means -- The classification B05C 3/132 covers apparatus for applying a fluent material to a surface where the workpiece is brought into contact with a bulk quantity of liquid or other fluent medium while being supported on a conveying system, such as a conveyor belt or chain, that transports the work through the treatment zone.
[B05C 3/15] not supported on conveying means -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Apparatus in which the work is endless but not supported on conveying means to being brought into contact with the fluent material
[B05C 3/152] the work passing in zig-zag fashion over rollers -- B05C 3/152 covers apparatus in which the workpiece follows a zig-zag path over a series of rollers, allowing repeated contact with a bulk fluent material for coating, cleaning or treating the surface. The configuration promotes uniform application while the material is supplied continuously.
[B05C 3/172] in endless form -- Subclass B05C 3/172 covers apparatus that continuously conveys the workpiece in an endless loop-such as rollers, belts, or chain conveyors-while it remains in contact with a bulk liquid or other fluent material, enabling uninterrupted application of the fluid to the surface.
[B05C 3/18] only one side of the work coming into contact with the liquid or other fluent material -- This subclass covers devices that apply a liquid or other fluent material to a workpiece so that only one side of the work contacts the fluid, typically using a bulk supply of the material and a moving or stationary support for the work.
[B05C 3/20] for applying liquid or other fluent material only at particular parts of the work -- B05C 3/20 refers to apparatus that apply a liquid or other fluent material to only selected areas of the workpiece rather than the entire surface. The devices use localized delivery means such as targeted nozzles, pens or spot-spray mechanisms to confine the fluid to particular parts.
[B05C 5/00] Apparatus in which liquid or other fluent material is projected, poured or allowed to flow on to the surface of the work -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Apparatus in which the liquid or fluid is projected, poured or allowed to flow on the surface of the work, e.g. by using coating heads or nozzles.
[B05C 5/001] incorporating means for heating or cooling the liquid or other fluent material  B05C11/1042  takes precedence -- B05C 5/001 covers devices that apply liquids or other fluent substances to a work surface and include integrated means for heating or cooling the material during application. It falls under the broader class for spraying or atomising apparatus, and is overridden by the more specific B05C11/1042.
[B05C 5/002] the work consisting of separate articles  B05C5/0208  takes precedence -- B05C 5/002 pertains to apparatus for applying liquid or other fluent materials onto surfaces, specifically where the work consists of separate articles.
