This classification is part of a broader hierarchy dealing with spraying and atomising in general, under the Cooperative Patent Classification system used by the EPO and USPTO.
[B05C 5/004] the work consisting of separate rectangular flat articles, e.g. flat sheets -- This subgroup covers apparatus that applies a fluent material to a work consisting of separate rectangular flat articles such as flat sheets. It falls within spraying or atomising operations where the target is composed of individual flat pieces rather than a continuous surface.
[B05C 5/005] Curtain coaters  B05C5/008  takes precedence -- B05C 5/005 in the Cooperative Patent Classification covers curtain coaters, a type of apparatus used for applying liquid or other fluent materials to surfaces by allowing them to flow in a continuous sheet or curtain onto the work surface. This entry is part of a broader category dealing with spraying and atomizing equipment.
[B05C 5/007] Slide-hopper coaters, i.e. apparatus in which the liquid or other fluent material flows freely on an inclined surface before contacting the work -- B05C5/007 covers slide-hopper coaters, machines where a liquid or fluent material slides down an inclined surface (the hopper) and then is applied to a workpiece. The material flows freely before contacting the surface, enabling uniform coating in processes such as painting or printing.
[B05C 5/008] Slide-hopper curtain coaters -- Slide-hopper curtain coaters feed liquid or other fluent material from a movable hopper onto a continuously formed curtain that falls onto the workpiece, providing a uniform coating as the material is projected, poured or allowed to flow over the surface. The device is used for applying coatings in processes such as painting, printing or surface treatment.
[B05C 5/02] from an outlet device in contact or almost in contact, with the work -- B05C 5/02 pertains to apparatus that delivers liquid or other fluent material onto a workpiece from an outlet device that is in direct or near-direct contact with the surface, such as sprayers, pourers or applicators that project or flow the material onto the material being treated.
[B05C 5/0204] for applying liquid or other fluent material to the edges of essentially flat articles -- A device that directs liquid or other fluent material onto the edges of generally flat items, such as sheets, plates or panels, using spraying, pouring, or flow methods. It falls under the broader class of apparatus for applying fluent materials to surfaces.
[B05C 5/0208] for applying liquid or other fluent material to separate articles  B05C5/0204  takes precedence -- B05C 5/0208 covers apparatus that projects, pours or lets liquid or other fluent material flow onto the surfaces of separate articles, extending the general spraying and atomising group to devices designed for applying such substances to distinct items rather than a continuous workpiece.
[B05C 5/0212] only at particular parts of the articles  at particular part of web, band, strip, belt or ribbon  B05C5/025 -- B05C 5/0212 pertains to apparatus for applying liquid or other fluent materials specifically to certain parts of articles, particularly targeting distinct sections of webs, bands, strips, belts, or ribbons. This classification is part of the broader category dealing with spraying and atomizing techniques in general.
[B05C 5/0216] by relative movement of article and outlet according to a predetermined path -- A device that applies liquid or other fluent material to a surface by moving the workpiece or the discharge outlet along a predefined path, allowing the material to be projected, poured or allowed to flow onto the work. The relative motion is predetermined to ensure uniform application.
[B05C 5/022] the outlet being fixed during operation -- B05C 5/022 relates to spray or atomising apparatus in which the outlet or nozzle is held fixed while the machine is running, so the discharge point does not move relative to the workpiece during the application of liquid or other fluent material.
[B05C 5/0225] characterised by flow controlling means, e.g. valves, located proximate the outlet  B05C5/0258 ,  B05C5/0275  take precedence; supply valves upstream the coating head  B05C11/1036 -- Devices for applying liquids or other fluent substances to a work surface that incorporate flow-controlling elements, such as valves, positioned close to the discharge opening. The nearby valves regulate the quantity and timing of material leaving the apparatus, distinguishing this group from upstream-supply valve designs.
[B05C 5/0229] the valve being a gate valve or a sliding valve -- Not complete, see B05C5/0225
[B05C 5/0233] rotating valve, e.g. rotating perforated cylinder -- Not complete, see B05C5/0225
[B05C 5/0237] Fluid actuated valves -- Not complete, see B05C5/0225
[B05C 5/0241] for applying liquid or other fluent material to elongated work, e.g. wires, cables, tubes -- This CPC subclass B05C 5/0241 covers devices that deliver liquid or other fluent substances onto elongated workpieces such as wires, cables or tubes, typically by spraying, pouring or flow. It falls under performing operations, spraying or atomising in general, apparatus for applying fluent materials to surfaces.
[B05C 5/0245] for applying liquid or other fluent material to a moving work of indefinite length, e.g. to a moving web  B05C5/0241 ,  B05C5/0254 ,  B05C5/027 ,  B05C5/0283  take precedence -- B05C 5/0245 covers devices that apply liquid or other fluent substances to a continuously moving work of indefinite length, such as a web or belt, during processing. The apparatus projects, pours or lets the material flow onto the surface while the work advances, enabling uniform coating or treatment.
[B05C 5/025] only at particular part of the work  at particular part of separate articles  B05C5/0212 -- This subclass covers apparatus that direct a liquid or other fluid onto a defined portion of a workpiece or onto designated areas of separate articles, rather than applying it uniformly over the entire surface. It refines the broader category of devices that project or flow fluent materials onto surfaces.
[B05C 5/0254] Coating heads with slot-shaped outlet  B05C5/0283  takes precedence -- B05C 5/0254 covers apparatus for applying liquids or other fluent substances where the material is discharged through a slot-shaped outlet, forming a flat, elongated jet that coats a work surface. The design directs the flow to produce a uniform film, typically used in spray or atomising operations for coating processes. It is subordinate to the general category for apparatus applying fluent materials to surfaces.
[B05C 5/0258] flow controlled, e.g. by a valve  B05C5/0262 ,  B05C5/0266  take precedence -- B05C 5/0258 concerns apparatus that projects, pours or lets a liquid or other fluent material flow onto a work surface with the flow being regulated, typically by a valve, within the general class of spraying or atomising devices. More specific subclasses such as B05C5/0262 and B05C5/0266 provide finer distinctions and take precedence over this entry.
[B05C 5/0262] adjustable in width, i.e. having lips movable relative to each other in order to modify the slot width, e.g. to close it -- This CPC subclass B05C 5/0262 covers apparatus for applying fluent materials in which the slot width is adjustable by movable lips that can be shifted relative to each other to narrow or close the opening. It belongs to the hierarchy of performing operations, spraying or atomising in general, and apparatus for applying fluent materials to surfaces.
[B05C 5/0266] adjustable in length, e.g. for coating webs of different width -- B05C 5/0266 is a CPC subgroup for apparatus that applies liquid or other fluent substances to surfaces and includes a means to adjust the length of the spray or flow zone, allowing the device to coat webs of varying widths. It belongs to the spraying or atomising family of operations.
[B05C 5/027] Coating heads with several outlets, e.g. aligned transversally to the moving direction of a web to be coated  B05C5/0254  takes precedence -- B05C 5/027 classifies coating heads that have several outlets, typically arranged transversely across the travel direction of a moving web, to apply liquid or other fluent material onto the surface. These heads project, pour or let the material flow onto the workpiece in a continuous coating process.
[B05C 5/0275] flow controlled, e.g. by a valve -- This subclass covers devices that apply a liquid or other fluid to a work surface where the discharge is regulated, typically by a valve or similar flow-controlling element. The control mechanism adjusts the amount of material projected, poured or allowed to run onto the surface, improving precision in spraying or coating operations.
[B05C 5/0279] independently, e.g. individually, flow controlled -- B05C 5/0279 covers apparatus that provides separate, independent control of the flow of liquid or other fluent material for each delivery point, allowing each stream to be started, stopped or varied individually, typically through dedicated valves, pumps or actuators. This enables precise, localized application of the material on a work surface.
[B05C 5/0283] Flat jet coaters, i.e. apparatus in which the liquid or other fluent material is projected from the outlet as a cohesive flat jet in direction of the work -- Flat jet coaters are machines that apply liquid or other fluent substances to a surface by emitting the material as a cohesive flat jet directed at the workpiece. The jet stream spreads uniformly across the surface, allowing continuous coating without a spray atomization. This type of apparatus falls under CPC subclass B05C 5/0283, covering equipment that projects fluid in a flat sheet form.
[B05C 5/0287] with no relative movement between the outlet and a moving work during the application -- B05C 5/0287 pertains to apparatus where liquid or other fluent materials are applied to surfaces without any relative movement between the outlet and the moving work during the application process. This classification is specific to equipment designed for static spraying or pouring onto moving objects, ensuring consistent material distribution.
[B05C 5/0291] the material being discharged on the work through discrete orifices as discrete droplets, beads or strips that coalesce on the work or are spread on the work so as to form a continuous coating -- B05C 5/0291 covers devices that deliver liquid or other fluent material onto a workpiece through separate openings, forming individual droplets, beads or strips that merge or spread to create a continuous coating. The classification is part of the spraying or atomising group within performing operations.
[B05C 5/0295] Floating coating heads or nozzles -- Floating coating heads or nozzles are devices that are supported so they can float or move relative to the workpiece, allowing a uniform layer of liquid or other fluent material to be applied as the head or nozzle follows the surface contours during coating operations.
[B05C 7/00] Apparatus specially designed for applying liquid or other fluent material to the inside of hollow work -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Devices for applying fluent material to the inside of hollow work, e.g. a tube, a pipe or reservoir.  The work piece could be coated by projecting the fluid material into the hollow part. A different method for partly closed work pieces is to fill it with fluid material and then empty it.
[B05C 7/005] by devices in contact with moving work -- This subclass covers machines that apply liquids or other fluent substances to the interior of hollow workpieces while the work is moving, using devices that physically contact the surface, such as nozzles, brushes or rollers that deliver the material during motion.
[B05C 7/02] the liquid or other fluent material being projected -- This subclass covers the nature of the liquid or other fluent medium that is projected by spraying or atomising devices designed to apply material inside hollow workpieces. It includes specifications of the fluid's physical state, composition, viscosity, temperature, or additives that influence the projection. The focus is on the properties of the projected material rather than the apparatus itself.
[B05C 7/04] the liquid or other fluent material flowing or being moved through the work -- B05C 7/04 covers devices that apply a liquid or other fluent material into the interior of a hollow workpiece, focusing on the way the material flows through or is moved within the work. The classification addresses apparatuses designed to direct and control that internal delivery during spraying or atomising operations.
[B05C 7/06] by devices moving in contact with the work -- This subclass covers apparatus for applying liquids or other fluent substances to the interior of hollow objects where the delivery device itself moves while remaining in contact with the workpiece, such as rollers, brushes or moving heads that travel along the inner surface.
[B05C 7/08] for applying liquids or other fluent materials to the inside of tubes -- B05C 7/08 covers devices specifically designed to introduce liquids or other fluent substances into the interior of hollow workpieces such as tubes, pipes or ducts. These apparatuses may use spraying, injection or other delivery methods to ensure uniform coating, cleaning or filling of the internal surfaces.
[B05C 9/00] Apparatus or plant for applying liquid or other fluent material to surfaces by means not covered by any preceding group, or in which the means of applying the liquid or other fluent material is not important -- **Definition:** This place covers:
[B05C 9/02] for applying liquid or other fluent material to surfaces by single means not covered by groups -- B05C 9/02 covers apparatus or plant that applies liquids or other fluent materials to surfaces by a single means that is not specified in any earlier subgroup, where the particular application mechanism is not significant. It falls under the broader categories of performing operations, spraying or atomising in general, and apparatus for applying fluent materials to surfaces. This subclass captures miscellaneous single-means devices not otherwise classified.
[B05C 9/022] to obtain ornamental coatings -- B05C 9/022 covers apparatus or plant that applies liquid or other fluent material to a surface to create ornamental coatings, where the particular means of application are not specified in any other subclass or are considered unimportant. It falls under the broader categories of performing operations, spraying or atomising in general, and apparatus for applying fluent materials to surfaces.
[B05C 9/025] using silk screens or stencils -- The B05C 9/025 subclass covers apparatus that apply liquid or other fluent material to a surface by means of a silk screen or stencil, a method commonly used in screen-printing and similar coating processes. It includes devices where the screen or stencil forms the primary means of pattern transfer, regardless of other features of the equipment.
[B05C 9/027] using applicator shoes -- B05C 9/027 covers apparatus that applies liquid or other fluent materials to surfaces by means of footwear or shoe-like applicators. The classification includes systems where the shoe itself serves as the delivering element, often for evenly spreading paints, coatings, or sealants during walking or standing. It falls under the broader group for spraying or atomising in general, specifically apparatus for applying fluent materials to surfaces.
[B05C 9/04] for applying liquid or other fluent material to opposite sides of the work -- This subclass covers devices that apply a liquid or other fluent material simultaneously to the opposite sides of a workpiece, such as dual-face spray heads, rotating drums with opposite nozzles, or similar arrangements where the means of delivery is not otherwise specified.
[B05C 9/045] in which the opposite sides of the work are the internal and external surfaces of hollow articles -- This subclass covers devices for applying liquids or other fluent substances to both the inner and outer surfaces of hollow workpieces such as pipes, bottles or tubes, where the application method is not the defining feature. It includes apparatus that can treat the interior and exterior simultaneously or sequentially without being limited to any specific spraying or atomising technique.
[B05C 9/06] for applying two different liquids or other fluent materials, or the same liquid or other fluent material twice, to the same side of the work -- B05C 9/06 covers devices that apply two distinct fluids, or repeat the application of the same fluid, to the same face of a workpiece in a single operation. The classification applies to apparatus where the method of delivery is not otherwise specified and the focus is on delivering multiple fluids to one side.
[B05C 9/08] for applying liquid or other fluent material and performing an auxiliary operation -- B05C 9/08 covers devices that apply a liquid or other fluent material to a surface while simultaneously carrying out an auxiliary function such as heating, drying, curing, mixing or measuring. The classification is used for spray or atomising equipment where the method of application is not specifically defined and the additional operation is integral to the apparatus.
[B05C 9/10] the auxiliary operation being performed before the application -- B05C 9/10 covers sprayers in which an auxiliary operation is carried out before the material is applied to the target surface, such as pre-heating, filtration, mixing or pressurising, when the specific means of applying the fluid are not otherwise defined. This subclass is part of the hierarchy for apparatus or plants that apply liquid or other fluent substances to surfaces by means not covered in earlier groups.
[B05C 9/12] the auxiliary operation being performed after the application -- B05C 9/12 relates to equipment for a post-application auxiliary operation performed after a liquid or other fluent material has been applied to a surface. It includes devices that carry out drying, curing, heating, cooling, solidifying or other treatments that follow the primary coating step, where the applying means are not further specified.
[B05C 9/14] the auxiliary operation involving heating -- The B05C 9/14 subclass covers apparatus for applying liquids or fluent materials where an auxiliary heating operation is employed. It includes devices that warm the material, the spray plume, or the target surface to improve flow, atomisation, drying or coating quality, without being the main spraying mechanism.
[B05D] PROCESSES FOR APPLYING FLUENT MATERIALS TO SURFACES, IN GENERAL -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Processes for coating substrates in general only. Coating processes for specific application should be classified in the application field first, they should only be classified in B05D if they have a more general interest.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Coating on foodstuffs -> A23P20/17 , A23P20/15 , A23P20/18 - Coating on glass -> C03C - Coating on ceramic -> C04B41/00 - Coating on textiles -> D06N - Coating on paper -> D21H - Coating on semi-conductors -> H10  **Glossary:** - Coating: The applied material. A coating may be a solidified layer originally applied as a liquid (e.g. dried paint) or a layer of material which, once applied, remains in a liquid or semi-liquid state (e.g. lubricant). - Flocking: The deposition of fibre particles ('flock') upon a surface where the particles land non-parallel to the surface. The process may be facilitated by the application of electrostatic charge to the flock to cause its attraction to an adhesive-coated surface ('electrostatic flocking'). This results in the fibres standing perpendicular to the surface. - Fluidised-bed technique: A technique used to cause dry particulate material to behave like a fluid. This is commonly achieved by the introduction of a pressurised fluid into the material and promotes a high degree of contact between the fluid and material. - Liquid or fluent: designates materials which can flow, e.g. liquids, including solutions, dispersions and suspensions, as well as semi-liquids, pastes, melts and particulate materials. - Particulate materials: Solid materials in the form of very small pieces, e.g. powders, granules, short fibres or chips. - Langmuir-Blodgett films (LB): Contains one or more monolayers of an organic material, deposited from the surface of a liquid onto a solid by immersing (emersing) the solid substrate into (or from) the liquid, the layer being compressed before deposition - SAM: Self assembled monolayers that are formed spontaneously by adsorption of amphifunctional molecules at solid-liquid and solid-gas interfaces, forming organised molecular assemblies
[B05D 1/00] Processes for applying liquids or other fluent materials -- B05D 1/00 relates to general processes for applying liquids, gases or other fluent materials to a surface, such as spraying, atomising or spreading, regardless of the specific equipment or material used. The subclass is used for patents that disclose methods of delivering a fluent medium onto a substrate in a broad, technology-neutral manner.
[B05D 1/002] the substrate being rotated -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Processes where the substrate is rotated during coating or during drying/curing of the coating.
[B05D 1/005] Spin coating -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Spin coating on wafers -> H10P14/6342
[B05D 1/007] using an electrostatic field  B05D1/02  -  B05D1/16  take precedence -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Electrostatic spraying -> B05D1/04 - B05D1/06 - Electrodeposition -> C25D
[B05D 1/02] performed by spraying -- B05D 1/02 covers processes for applying liquids or other fluent substances to a surface by means of spraying. The entry includes the generation of a spray, its transport and deposition on the target material.
[B05D 1/025] using gas close to its critical state -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Spraying with supercritical solvents or solvent close to their supercritical state.
[B05D 1/04] involving the use of an electrostatic field -- This subclass covers methods that apply liquids or other fluent materials to a surface by generating an electrostatic field that charges the droplets, causing them to be attracted to and coat the target. It includes electrostatic spray painting, coating and similar processes that use the field to improve deposition efficiency.
[B05D 1/045] on non-conductive substrates -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Electrostatic spraying of substrates which are normally non-conductive e.g. wood, plastic, whether they got a treatment for enhancing their conductivity or not. The treatment for enhancing the substrate conductivity per se is classified in B05D3/005 . The type of substrate can be classified with additional information symbols.
[B05D 1/06] Applying particulate materials -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Processes for applying fluent materials by spraying only particulate materials, e.g. powder involving the use of an electrostatic field
[B05D 1/08] Flame spraying -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Spraying with flames or plasma of polymers only.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Flame/plasma spraying of inorganic compounds. -> C23C4/00
[B05D 1/10] Applying particulate materials -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Flame/plasma spraying of polymeric powders.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Flame/plasma spraying of inorganic powders. -> C23C4/00
[B05D 1/12] Applying particulate materials -- B05D 1/12 covers processes that deposit solid particles onto a surface, typically by spraying, atomising, or other methods that disperse a particulate stream. It includes techniques for applying powders, granules or other dry solids to substrates in fields such as coating, printing, or material treatment.
[B05D 1/14] Flocking -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Flocking on textiles -> D06Q1/14
[B05D 1/16] Flocking otherwise than by spraying -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Flocking on textiles -> D06Q1/14
[B05D 1/18] performed by dipping -- B05D 1/18 covers processes for applying liquids or other fluent materials to surfaces by immersion, i.e., dipping a workpiece into a bath of the material. It is a subclass of spraying or atomising in general, situated within the broader group of performing operations.
[B05D 1/185] applying monomolecular layers  B05D1/204  takes precedence -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Process for making self assembled monolayers (SAM).
[B05D 1/20] substances to be applied floating on a fluid -- The subclass concerns methods in which the material to be deposited is held up on the surface of a liquid before being applied to a target, such as a film of oil, surfactant layer or other fluid-borne coating. It includes techniques where the substance floats on a carrier fluid and is then sprayed, atomised or otherwise transferred onto the workpiece.
[B05D 1/202] Langmuir Blodgett films (LB films) -- The entry covers the formation and deposition of Langmuir-Blodgett films, which are monolayer or multilayer coatings made by transferring a film from a liquid surface onto a solid substrate in a controlled way. It includes techniques such as dipping or lifting the substrate through a Langmuir trough to create ordered thin films.
[B05D 1/204] LB techniques -- This subclass covers methods that use liquid-based techniques to deposit or spread fluids onto a surface, such as liquid-film or liquid-bridge approaches, within the broader context of spraying and atomising processes. It is indexed as B05D 1/204 in the Cooperative Patent Classification, which groups processes for applying liquids or other fluent materials.
[B05D 1/206] LB troughs -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Langmuir-Blodgett troughs that are laboratory apparatus used to compress monolayers of molecules on the surface of the subphase before deposition. It can be used to deposit single or multiple monolayers on a solid substrate.
[B05D 1/208] After-treatment of monomolecular films -- B05D 1/208 covers post-application steps applied to monomolecular films after they have been deposited on a surface. It includes drying, curing, polymerisation, cross-linking, or other chemical or physical modifications intended to stabilise or alter the film's properties.
[B05D 1/22] using fluidised-bed technique -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Coating of powders -> B01J2/00
[B05D 1/24] Applying particulate materials -- It covers methods for applying solid particles onto surfaces, such as powders, granules or other particulate matter, using spraying, dusting or similar techniques, typically for coating, forming or treating a substrate.
[B05D 1/26] performed by applying the liquid or other fluent material from an outlet device in contact with, or almost in contact with, the surface -- This subclass describes methods of applying a liquid or other fluent material to a surface by using an outlet device that is in contact with, or almost in contact with, the workpiece. The approach is employed in spraying or atomising operations where near-contact delivery enhances deposition accuracy.
[B05D 1/265] Extrusion coatings -- B05D 1/265 covers extrusion-coating, where a molten polymer or other fluent material is extruded onto a moving substrate and simultaneously solidified to form a continuous coated layer, used for producing films, sheets or laminates with controlled thickness and surface properties.
[B05D 1/28] performed by transfer from the surfaces of elements carrying the liquid or other fluent material, e.g. brushes, pads, rollers -- This subclass concerns applying liquids or other fluent substances by transferring them from the surfaces of carrier elements that hold the material, such as brushes, pads or rollers, onto a target surface. It falls under processes for applying fluent materials in general.
[B05D 1/283] Transferring monomolecular layers or solutions of molecules adapted for forming monomolecular layers from carrying elements -- The code B05D 1/283 covers techniques for moving monomolecular layers or solutions containing molecules that will form such layers from a carrier onto a target surface. It includes methods such as dip-coating, Langmuir-Blodgett transfer and other processes that deposit the layer onto a substrate.
[B05D 1/286] using a temporary backing to which the coating has been applied -- B05D 1/286 covers methods of applying a liquid or other fluent coating to a temporary backing that holds the material until it can be transferred to the final substrate. The technique is used when direct application to the target surface is impractical, allowing the coating to be formed, cured or handled on the backing before placement.
[B05D 1/30] performed by gravity only, i.e. flow coating -- The subclass B05D 1/30 includes processes for coating a surface by letting a liquid or other fluent material flow over it solely under the action of gravity, without using sprays, pressures, or mechanical assistance. It covers simple flow-coating techniques where the material spreads and adheres to the target surface as it runs downhill or across due to its own weight.
[B05D 1/305] Curtain coating -- Curtain coating is a method in which a continuous sheet or "curtain" of liquid or other fluent material is formed and directed onto a moving substrate to create a uniform coating layer. The technique is used for applying thin, consistent films of liquids, paints, adhesives or polymers in industrial processes. It falls under the spraying and atomising group for applying fluent materials.
[B05D 1/32] using means for protecting parts of a surface not to be coated, e.g. using stencils, resists -- B05D 1/32 covers methods for applying liquids or other fluent materials where selected areas of a surface are deliberately shielded from the coating. The protection may be achieved with stencils, masks or resist layers that prevent deposition on those regions, enabling patterned or selective coating.
[B05D 1/322] Removable films used as masks -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Films used as masks when they are obtained by a coating process.
[B05D 1/325] Masking layer made of peelable film -- B05D 1/325 covers mask layers made of peelable film that are applied to a surface before a coating or spraying operation and later removed to reveal the protected area. It is used in processes for applying liquids or other fluent materials where selective protection of parts of the substrate is required, such as in spray painting or printing.
[B05D 1/327] Masking layer made of washable film -- B05D 1/327 covers masking layers formed from a washable film that are applied to a surface before spraying or other fluent-material processes, protecting selected areas during coating. After the application, the film can be removed or washed away, providing a temporary, non-permanent mask.
[B05D 1/34] Applying different liquids or other fluent materials simultaneously -- This CPC subgroup covers methods that apply two or more liquids or other fluent substances at the same time to a surface, such as co-spraying, dual-jet coating, or simultaneous deposition of different fluids. It also includes apparatus designed to deliver multiple streams concurrently.
[B05D 1/36] Successively applying liquids or other fluent materials, e.g. without intermediate treatment -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Coating made of two layers wherein an important interaction exists between the layers ,e.g. one layer can only harden when in contact with the other layer or with a specific component of the other layer.
[B05D 1/38] with intermediate treatment -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Coating made of two layers wherein an important interaction exists between the layers ,e.g. one layer can only harden when in contact with the other layer or with a specific component of the other layer.  Intermediate treatment corresponds to any treatment done after the deposition of the first layer and before the deposition of the next layer, e.g. drying
[B05D 1/40] Distributing applied liquids or other fluent materials by members moving relatively to surface -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The redistribution of the coating after it is applied (e.g. flattening).
[B05D 1/42] by non-rotary members -- This subclass covers spraying or atomising processes in which the material is expelled by members that move linearly or reciprocally instead of rotating, such as pistons, plungers or oscillating nozzles. It includes methods that use non-rotary actuators to apply liquids or other fluent substances onto a surface.
[B05D 1/60] Deposition of organic layers from vapour phase  vapour phase deposition in general  C23C14/00 ,  C23C16/00 -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - PVD/CVD of inorganic layers. -> C23C14/00 - C23C16/56
[B05D 1/62] Plasma-deposition of organic layers  plasma deposition in general  C23C14/00 ,  C23C16/00 -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - PVD/CVD of inorganic layers. -> C23C14/00 - C23C16/00
[B05D 2201/00] Polymeric substrate or laminate -- B05D 2201/00 in the Cooperative Patent Classification refers to processes for applying fluent materials to polymeric substrates or laminates, detailing specific methods and techniques used in coating these types of surfaces.
[B05D 2201/02] Polymeric substrate -- The CPC subclass B05D 2201/02 covers polymeric substrates, meaning any plastic or resin-based material used as a base for further processing. It falls under spraying or atomising operations, specifically the processes for applying fluent materials to surfaces, and is intended for applications such as coatings, inks, or adhesives placed on polymer films, sheets or laminates.
[B05D 2201/04] Laminate -- B05D 2201/04 in the Cooperative Patent Classification refers to processes for applying fluent materials to surfaces where the substrate is a laminate. This classification is specific to techniques involving polymeric substrates or laminates, detailing methods of coating or treating these composite materials.
[B05D 2201/06] Laminate of which the last layer is not a polymer -- B05D 2201/06 pertains to laminates whose last layer is not a polymer, covering structures where a polymeric substrate or laminate is coated with a non-polymeric top layer, typically in spraying or atomising processes for applying fluent materials to surfaces.
[B05D 2202/00] Substrate -- The B05D 2202/00 subclass covers the substrate that receives a coating, film or particles during spraying or atomising operations. It includes the material or surface onto which a fluent medium is applied, as well as any preparation or treatment of that surface that affects the application process.
[B05D 2202/10] based on Fe -- B05D 2202/10 in the Cooperative Patent Classification refers to processes for applying fluent materials to surfaces where the substrate is based on iron. This classification is used by the European Patent Office and the United States Patent and Trademark Office to categorize inventions involving coating or spraying techniques specifically targeting iron-based substrates.
[B05D 2202/15] Stainless steel -- B05D 2202/15 in the Cooperative Patent Classification refers to processes for applying fluent materials to stainless steel surfaces. This classification is part of a broader category that deals with spraying or atomising techniques and their application to various substrates.
[B05D 2202/20] based on light metals -- This CPC subgroup addresses processes for applying fluent materials to surfaces when the substrate is composed of light-metal. It falls under the broader categories of performing operations, spraying or atomising, and general methods for applying fluent materials.
[B05D 2202/25] based on Al -- B05D 2202/25 in the Cooperative Patent Classification refers to processes for applying fluent materials to surfaces where the substrate is based on aluminum. This classification is used by the European Patent Office and the United States Patent and Trademark Office to categorize inventions involving coating or spraying techniques specifically targeting aluminum substrates.
[B05D 2202/30] based on refractory metals (Ti, V, Cr, Zr, Nb, Mo, Hf, Ta, W) -- B05D 2202/30 covers spraying or atomising processes in which the coating material consists of refractory metals such as titanium, vanadium, chromium, zirconium, niobium, molybdenum, hafnium, tantalum or tungsten. It pertains to applying these high-melting-point powders or wires onto a substrate by thermal, plasma or other energetic jets.
[B05D 2202/35] based on Ti -- B05D 2202/35 in the Cooperative Patent Classification refers to processes for applying fluent materials to surfaces where the substrate is based on titanium. This classification is used by the European Patent Office and the United States Patent and Trademark Office to categorize inventions involving coating or spraying techniques specifically targeting titanium substrates.
[B05D 2202/40] based on other transition elements -- This subgroup concerns spraying or atomising operations in which the coating material contains transition elements other than the most commonly used ones. It covers processes that employ compounds or alloys of less typical transition metals as the source of the fluent material applied to a substrate. The classification is used for patents describing such alternative transition-element-based spray technologies.
[B05D 2202/45] based on Cu -- The CPC subclass B05D 2202/45 defines spraying or atomising processes in which a copper-based material is applied to a substrate. It covers any method that uses a fluent copper-containing medium to coat or treat surfaces, such as metallic, alloy or oxide layers, by spraying, atomising or similar techniques.
[B05D 2203/00] Other substrates -- B05D 2203/00 classifies processes for applying fluent substances-such as liquids, powders or gases-to substrates that are not otherwise specified in the CPC, covering a wide range of unconventional or miscellaneous surface types. The entry includes methods for treating irregular, composite, or specialty materials where standard spraying or atomising techniques are adapted to accommodate the particular characteristics of these "other" substrates.
[B05D 2203/20] Wood or similar material -- Processes for spraying or atomising a fluent material onto wood or comparable substrates, covering methods of applying coatings, paints, adhesives, or other particulate or liquid layers to such materials. This subclass addresses techniques specifically aimed at treating wood, timber, or similar organic surfaces with sprayed or atomised substances.
[B05D 2203/22] Paper or cardboard -- This subclass covers spraying or atomising processes used to apply fluent substances-such as liquids, pastes, powders or foams-specifically onto paper or cardboard surfaces. It falls under the broader categories of performing operations, spraying or atomising in general, and processes for applying fluent materials to surfaces. The entry is used to classify inventions that target paper or cardboard as the substrate for such deposition techniques.
[B05D 2203/24] Leather -- B05D 2203/24 covers spraying or atomising processes used to apply fluent materials such as paints, dyes, lubricants or protective coatings to leather substrates, encompassing methods for evenly coating, treating or finishing leather surfaces. This subclass falls under the broader category of processes for applying fluent materials to various substrates.
[B05D 2203/30] Other inorganic substrates, e.g. ceramics, silicon -- B05D 2203/30 pertains to spraying or atomising processes that apply fluid materials to inorganic substrates other than metals, such as ceramics, glass or silicon, within the broader class of surface-coating operations.
[B05D 2203/35] Glass -- The CPC subclass B05D 2203/35 pertains to spraying or atomising processes in which the material is applied to glass surfaces. It includes methods for coating, painting, protecting or otherwise treating glass by using fluent substances such as liquids, pastes or powders. This class falls under the broader categories of performing operations, spraying or atomising in general, and processes for applying fluent materials to other substrates.
[B05D 2210/00] Applying material to more than three types of substrate materials -- The B05D 2210/00 class covers processes in which a fluent material is deposited onto more than three different kinds of substrates in a single operation. It relates to spraying or atomising techniques that apply the material to varied surfaces such as metals, plastics, fabrics, or glass.
[B05D 2252/00] Sheets -- B05D 2252/00 covers processes that apply fluent materials to sheet-like substrates, including coating, spraying, or otherwise treating flat or flexible sheets during manufacturing or finishing operations. It falls under the performing operations hierarchy for spraying or atomising in general.
[B05D 2252/02] of indefinite length -- B05D2252/02 relates to processes that apply fluent substances to surfaces using spraying or atomising techniques when the material is presented as continuous sheets of indefinite length, such as coating or treating long webs.
[B05D 2252/04] of definite length in a continuous process -- The subclass B05D 2252/04 covers continuous processes that form sheets of a predetermined length by spraying or atomising a fluent material onto a moving surface. It pertains to operations where the sheet's dimensions are fixed in advance, enabling controlled production of continuous, length-specific coatings or films.
[B05D 2252/10] Applying the material on both sides -- This subcategory covers processes where a fluent material such as a spray or coating is applied simultaneously to both sides of a sheet or similar substrate. It belongs to the broader group of processes for applying fluent materials to surfaces, specifically under spraying or atomising in general.
[B05D 2254/00] Shape of substrate -- The subclass B05D 2254/00 concerns spraying or atomising processes that are adapted to the shape of the workpiece being coated. It covers methods, equipment adjustments, or control strategies used when the substrate is flat, curved, irregular or otherwise shaped, influencing how the fluent material is applied.
[B05D 2254/02] Applying the material on the exterior of the tube -- B05D 2254/02 covers methods for coating the outer surface of a tube by spraying, atomising or otherwise applying a fluent material. It falls under processes for applying fluent materials to surfaces, specifically when the substrate is shaped as a tube.
[B05D 2254/04] Applying the material on the interior of the tube -- This subclass covers methods for applying a fluent material onto the inner surface of a tube, typically using spraying or atomising techniques, to modify or form the tube's interior shape. It falls under the broader category of processes for applying fluent materials to surfaces.
[B05D 2254/06] Applying the material on the interior and exterior of the tube -- This subclass covers processes that apply a fluid, powder or other fluent material to both the interior and exterior surfaces of a tube, typically by spraying, coating or similar deposition techniques, and is used for treating or functionalising tubular substrates.
[B05D 2256/00] Wires or fibres -- This subclass covers processes that use wires or fibres to create a spray or mist of a fluent material for application to a surface, such as coating, cooling or cleaning. It includes techniques where stretched wires, filament bundles or fibre mats break up a liquid stream, producing fine droplets or a dispersed spray.
[B05D 2258/00] Small objects (e.g. screws) -- B05D 2258/00 relates to methods of spraying or atomising fluids onto small objects such as screws - bolts or fasteners. The subclass covers any process that deposits a coating, adhesive or cleaning agent on such compact items, typically as part of surface treatment or manufacturing operations.
[B05D 2258/02] The objects being coated one after the other -- The subclass B05D 2258/02 covers processes in which a series of small items such as screws, fasteners or similar components are coated consecutively by a spray or atomising technique, allowing each object to receive a uniform layer before the next one is treated. It relates to the sequential application of fluent materials to individual objects within the broader field of spraying operations.
[B05D 2259/00] Applying the material to the internal surface of hollow articles other than tubes -- The subclass B05D 2259/00 covers processes that deposit a fluent material onto the interior walls of hollow objects that are not tubes, such as containers, cans or bottles. It includes spraying, atomising or otherwise applying coatings, inks, adhesives or other substances to the inside surfaces of these hollow articles.
[B05D 2301/00] Inorganic additives or organic salts thereof -- B05D 2301/00 covers inorganic additives or their organic salts that are employed in processes for spraying or atomising fluent materials onto surfaces. It is placed under the hierarchy of performing operations, spraying or atomising in general, and processes for applying fluent materials to surfaces. The entry is used by the EPO and USPTO to classify inventions that incorporate such additives in coating or surface-treatment applications.
[B05D 2301/10] Phosphates, phosphoric acid or organic salts thereof -- The B05D 2301/10 class covers the use of phosphates, phosphoric acid and their organic salts as inorganic additives in spraying or atomising processes that apply fluent materials to surfaces. It includes formulations where these compounds act as corrosion inhibitors, flame retardants or stabilisers in coatings, paints or other sprayed layers.
[B05D 2301/20] Chromates, chromic acid or organic salts thereof -- B05D 2301/20 covers the use of chromates, chromic acid and their organic salts as additives in spraying or atomising operations for applying fluent materials to surfaces. These compounds are employed to modify corrosion resistance, coloration or curing behavior of coatings applied by spray processes.
[B05D 2301/30] Acids -- This subgroup concerns the use of acids, including their inorganic additives or organic salts, in spray or atomising processes for applying fluent materials to surfaces. It covers methods where acids are mixed with the sprayed medium to modify surface properties, aid cleaning, etching, or improve deposition during the application.
[B05D 2301/50] Bases -- The B05D 2301/50 subclass covers the use of bases, including inorganic additives or organic salts, in processes that spray or atomise fluent materials onto surfaces. It falls under performing operations, specifically spraying or atomising in general, and relates to the broader category of processes for applying fluent materials to surfaces. This classification is used in the Cooperative Patent Classification system shared by the EPO and USPTO.
[B05D 2320/00] Additives other than fillers present in the coating material or in the coating bath -- B05D 2320/00 relates to the inclusion of additives that are not fillers in the coating composition or in the coating bath during spraying or atomising operations. These additives are intended to modify properties such as flow, adhesion, drying behaviour, corrosion protection or other functional characteristics of the applied coating.
[B05D 2320/10] Detergents -- The B05D 2320/10 subclass covers the inclusion of detergents as additives-excluding filler types-in coating materials or coating baths used in spraying or atomising processes for applying fluent substances to surfaces. It relates to the formulation of cleaning agents that improve wetting, spreading, or removal of contaminants during coating operations.
[B05D 2350/00] Pretreatment of the substrate -- The CPC subclass B05D 2350/00 covers methods for preparing a surface before the application of a fluent material, such as cleaning, roughening, coating, or chemically conditioning the substrate to improve adhesion or performance of the subsequently sprayed or atomised product.
[B05D 2350/10] Phosphatation -- B05D 2350/10 Phosphatation covers pretreatment of substrates by applying a phosphate coating through spraying or atomising a fluent material onto the surface. The process creates a thin inorganic layer that improves adhesion, corrosion resistance, and readies the surface for further treatments such as painting or plating. It belongs to the broader class of processes for applying fluent materials to surfaces.
[B05D 2350/20] Chromatation -- Chromatation is a pretreatment method that applies a thin layer of chromate-containing fluid onto a substrate by spraying or atomising the material, creating a protective or decorative coating that improves corrosion resistance and surface adhesion. It falls under processes for applying fluent materials to surfaces, specifically within the broader class of performing operations and spraying in general.
[B05D 2350/30] Change of the surface -- B05D 2350/30 refers to processes that modify a substrate's surface before or during the application of a fluent material by spraying or atomising. It covers pretreatment steps such as cleaning, roughening, coating or chemically altering the surface to improve adhesion or performance.
[B05D 2350/33] Roughening -- B05D 2350/33 covers pretreatment operations that roughen a substrate surface before further processing, typically by mechanical or abrasive action such as sand-blasting, shot peening or spraying abrasive particles, to increase adhesion of subsequently applied fluent materials.
[B05D 2350/35] by chemical means -- B05D 2350/35 covers processes that pretreat a substrate before coating by applying chemicals, typically using spraying or atomising techniques to deposit a fluent material that modifies the surface chemistry. The method is used to improve adhesion, corrosion resistance or other surface properties prior to the main coating step.
[B05D 2350/38] by mechanical means -- The subclass B05D 2350/38 refers to pretreatment of a substrate carried out by mechanical means, such as grinding, polishing, brushing, abrasive blasting or other physical actions that modify the surface before applying a fluent material. It encompasses processes that rely solely on mechanical energy rather than chemical or thermal treatments to clean, roughen or otherwise prepare the surface for subsequent coating or spraying.
[B05D 2350/40] by adding a porous layer -- The subclass covers pretreatment methods in which a porous coating is applied to a substrate as a preparatory step, typically using spraying or atomising techniques. It includes processes that form a porous layer on a surface before subsequent treatment, coating, or bonding operations.
[B05D 2350/50] Smoothing -- Smoothing covers pretreatment steps that level or even out a substrate surface before applying fluent materials such as sprays or atomised coatings. The operation may use mechanical, chemical or thermal means to reduce surface irregularities, improving adhesion and uniformity of subsequent coatings.
[B05D 2350/60] Adding a layer before coating -- The code covers methods in which a preparatory layer is deposited on a substrate before the main coating is applied, such as primers, adhesive or barrier layers applied by spraying, atomising or other fluid-based techniques. This pretreatment improves adhesion, corrosion resistance or functional performance of the subsequent coating.
[B05D 2350/63] ceramic layer -- The code B05D 2350/63 covers processes that apply a ceramic layer to a substrate as a pretreatment step when spraying or atomising fluent materials. It addresses methods for forming a ceramic coating on surfaces before subsequent coating or finishing operations.
[B05D 2350/65] metal layer -- B05D 2350/65 relates to pretreating a substrate by depositing a metal layer onto its surface using spraying or atomising methods. The entry covers processes that apply a fluent metal material as a coating prior to subsequent finishing operations.
[B05D 2400/00] Pretreatment of the substrates -- B05D 2400/00 covers processes that prepare a substrate before a coating or other fluent material is applied, such as cleaning, etching, or surface activation using sprays, atomisers, or other fluid delivery methods. The pretreatment aims to improve adhesion, corrosion resistance, or other performance characteristics of the final layer.
[B05D 2401/00] Form of the coating product, e.g. solution, water dispersion, powders or the like -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The type of coating or carrier , e.g. powder coating, organic solvent solution, etc. when they are specified and are of interest in the invention.
[B05D 2401/10] Organic solvent -- B05D 2401/10 designates spraying or atomising processes in which the coating material is delivered as an organic-solvent based solution or dispersion. It covers methods of applying fluent coatings - solutions, water-dispersions, powders etc. - where the carrier is an organic solvent, fitting within the hierarchy of performing operations, spraying in general, and form of the coating product.
[B05D 2401/20] Aqueous dispersion or solution -- The CPC subclass B05D 2401/20 covers coating formulations that are provided as aqueous dispersions or solutions. It applies to processes for applying fluent materials to surfaces, such as spraying or atomising, where the coating product is water-based rather than a powder or other medium. This subclass falls under the hierarchy of performing operations, spraying or atomising in general, and processes for applying fluent materials to surfaces.
[B05D 2401/21] Mixture of organic solvent and water -- The CPC subclass B05D 2401/21 covers mixtures of organic solvent and water used as a coating medium, such as solutions, dispersions or powders, in processes that spray or atomise fluent materials onto surfaces. It falls under the hierarchy of performing operations, spraying or atomising in general, and processes for applying fluent materials to surfaces.
[B05D 2401/30] the coating being applied in other forms than involving eliminable solvent, diluent or dispersant -- B05D 2401/30 covers methods for applying coatings that are supplied in forms without a removable solvent, diluent or dispersant, such as powders, melts or solid particles, where the material is transferred to the substrate without a subsequent evaporation step.
[B05D 2401/31] applied as mixtures of monomers and polymers -- This subclass covers spraying or atomising operations in which the coating material is delivered as a mixture of monomers and polymers, allowing in-situ polymerisation to form a film on the target surface. It falls under performing operations, spraying or atomising in general, and processes for applying fluent materials to surfaces.
[B05D 2401/32] applied as powders -- This sub-class covers processes for applying coating materials in powder form to a substrate by spraying or atomising, including the handling of powders, water dispersions or dry particles as the coating product.
[B05D 2401/33] applied as vapours polymerising -- B05D 2401/33 deals with processes where a coating material is applied in vapour form and then polymerises on the target surface. It is classified under performing operations, specifically spraying or atomising and general methods for applying fluent materials to surfaces.
[B05D 2401/40] where the carrier is not clearly specified -- It covers coating applications where the medium carrying the active material is not clearly specified, such as solutions, water dispersions, powders or similar forms, within spray or atomising processes for applying fluent materials to surfaces. The subclass falls under the hierarchy of performing operations, spraying or atomising in general, and processes for applying fluent materials to surfaces.
[B05D 2401/50] where organic solvent or water can be used as alternative -- The subclass B05D 2401/50 covers methods of applying coatings by spraying or atomising in which the carrier liquid may be either an organic solvent or water, allowing either to be used interchangeably. It relates to processes where the coating material can be formulated as a solution, dispersion, powder or similar form and the choice of liquid medium is not fixed.
[B05D 2401/60] non aqueous inorganic solvent -- This CPC subclass covers processes for applying fluent materials where the coating is delivered in a non-aqueous inorganic solvent, such as solutions, dispersions or powders rather than water-based media. It falls under spraying or atomising operations for coating surfaces.
[B05D 2401/90] at least one component of the composition being in supercritical state or close to supercritical state -- The code refers to spraying or atomising processes in which at least one ingredient of the coating composition is in a supercritical or near-supercritical fluid state, enabling deposition of solutions, dispersions, powders or similar materials onto a surface. It is a sub-category of coating operations within the broader performing-operations hierarchy.
[B05D 2420/00] Indexing scheme corresponding to the position of each layer within a multilayer coating relative to the substrate -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Symbols for indicating in a multilayer coating, the position of the layer concerned by particular information.
[B05D 2420/01] first layer from the substrate side -- This CPC subclass B05D 2420/01 falls under performing operations - spraying or atomising in general - processes for applying fluent materials to surfaces, in general, and defines the indexing scheme for multilayer coatings, indicating the layer that is closest to the substrate.
[B05D 2420/02] second layer from the substrate side -- B05D 2420/02 designates the second coating layer measured from the substrate side in a multilayer film, providing a way to index the position of each layer when fluent materials are sprayed or atomised onto a surface. It is used in patents describing layered coatings where the order of layers affects performance.
[B05D 2420/03] third layer from the substrate side -- B05D 2420/03 designates the third coating layer counted from the substrate side in a multilayer film, specifying its position within the stack for processes that apply fluent materials to surfaces. This indexing scheme helps differentiate each layer's role in the overall coating structure.
[B05D 2420/04] fourth layer from the substrate side -- B05D 2420/04 designates the fourth layer in a multilayer coating counted from the substrate side, used for indexing the position of individual layers in processes that spray or atomise fluent materials onto surfaces.
[B05D 2420/05] fifth layer from the substrate side -- B05D 2420/05 designates the fifth layer counted from the substrate side in a multilayer coating, providing an indexing scheme that specifies the position of each layer relative to the underlying substrate. This code is used to classify processes that apply fluent materials in spray or atomising operations where the sequence of layers is relevant.
[B05D 2425/00] Indexing scheme corresponding to the position of each layer within a multilayer coating relative to the surface -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Symbols for indicating in a multilayer coating, the position of the layer concerned by certain information.
[B05D 2425/01] top layer/ last layer, i.e. first layer from the top surface -- The subclass B05D 2425/01 refers to the outermost or top layer of a multilayer coating, i.e., the first layer that becomes exposed at the surface after application. It is used to index spraying or atomising processes that apply the final, surface-facing fluent material.
[B05D 2425/02] second layer from the top surface -- The code B05D 2425/02 designates the position of the second layer counted from the top surface in a multilayer coating. It is used to index that specific layer when describing processes for applying fluent materials to surfaces.
[B05D 2425/03] third layer from the top surface -- The code designates the third layer counted from the outermost surface of a multilayer coating, specifying its position for processes that apply fluent materials by spraying or atomising. It is a sub-point of the CPC group for general spraying operations and surface-application methods.
[B05D 2425/04] fourth layer from the top surface -- B05D 2425/04 identifies the fourth layer counted from the top surface in a multilayer coating, as indexed in processes that apply fluent materials by spraying or atomising. It falls under Performing Operations → Spraying or Atomising in General → Processes for Applying Fluent Materials to Surfaces, in which the layer's relative position within the stack is the key classification feature.
[B05D 2425/05] fifth layer from the top surface -- The CPC code B05D 2425/05 falls under spraying or atomising processes for applying fluent materials to surfaces and defines an indexing scheme for multilayer coatings, indicating the position of the layer that is the fifth counting down from the top surface.
[B05D 2430/00] Component used as a filler in the composition -- B05D 2430/00 covers components added to a spray or atomising composition solely as fillers, such as inert particles or powders that modify viscosity, bulk or other physical properties without providing functional activity. These filler materials are used to adjust the handling or performance of the fluent material applied to a surface. The entry falls under CPC section B05D for processes of applying fluent materials by spraying or atomising.
[B05D 2451/00] Type of carrier, type of coating (Multilayers) -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The type of coating in the meaning of B05D2401/00 symbols for multilayers.
[B05D 2490/00] Intermixed layers -- This subgroup covers processes in which two or more different fluent materials are deposited together in a single spraying or atomising operation, producing intermixed or co-deposited layers on a substrate. It includes techniques such as co-spraying, simultaneous dispensing of separate streams, or blending of sprays to form composite or graded coatings in one step.
[B05D 2490/50] compositions varying with a gradient perpendicular to the surface -- This subclass covers methods or products where the material applied to a surface is formulated so that its composition changes continuously or stepwise in the direction normal to the surface, creating a gradient layer. It includes coatings, films, or sprayed layers where properties such as chemical composition, particle size, or additive concentration vary through the thickness to achieve functional gradients.
[B05D 2490/60] compositions varying with a gradient parallel to the surface -- B05D 2490/60 relates to coating or spraying processes that produce layers whose composition changes gradually along the surface, creating a parallel gradient of constituents. It covers formulations and methods for applying such graded mixtures to achieve functional or aesthetic property variations across a coated area.
[B05D 2500/00] Indexation scheme for the composition of layers -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Type of polymer coating. The subgroups in the B05D2500/00 range concerns a broad information on the type of coating as far as this is a process feature, The coating composition should be classified in C09D .  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Coating compositions. -> C09D
[B05D 2501/00] Varnish or unspecified clear coat -- This CPC subclass covers processes and equipment for applying a varnish or an unspecified clear coat to a surface by spraying or atomising the fluid. It includes methods that deliver a protective or decorative clear layer without detailing the coating composition, and is situated under the broader categories of performing operations and spraying in general.
[B05D 2501/10] Wax -- B05D 2501/10 covers methods of applying wax, typically as a varnish or clear coat, to a surface by spraying or atomising the fluent material. It falls under performing operations, specifically spraying or atomising in general, and the broader class of processes for applying fluent materials to surfaces. This subclass addresses the techniques and apparatus used to achieve uniform wax coatings.
[B05D 2502/00] Type of polymer or polymer coating -- B05D 2502/00 pertains to processes for applying fluent materials where the specific type of polymer or polymer coating is the defining factor. It falls under Performing operations, Spraying or atomising in general, and Processes for applying fluent materials to surfaces in general, covering the choice of polymer material in spray or atomisation coating operations.
[B05D 2502/005] modified -- The subclass B05D 2502/005 concerns spraying or atomising processes where the polymer or polymer coating applied to a surface is modified, for example by chemical alteration, blending with other substances or the addition of functional additives before or during application.
[B05D 2503/00] Polyurethanes -- The B05D 2503/00 subgroup concerns processes that spray or atomise polyurethane substances onto surfaces. It includes methods for preparing, dispensing and applying liquid, foam or resin-based polyurethane mixtures as coatings, sealants or adhesives, covering both the equipment and operational steps needed to achieve uniform coverage. This falls within the broader category of applying fluent materials by spraying or atomising.
[B05D 2504/00] Epoxy polymers -- The CPC subclass B05D 2504/00 covers the use of epoxy polymers in processes that spray or atomise fluent materials onto surfaces. It relates to performing operations - spraying or atomising epoxy-based coatings, adhesives or binders applied as a spray, often for protective or decorative purposes. This subclass falls under the broader categories of performing operations, spraying or atomising in general, and processes for applying fluent materials to surfaces.
[B05D 2505/00] Polyamides -- The CPC subclass B05D 2505/00 covers spraying or atomising operations that use polyamides as the fluent material. It includes processes in which polyamide powders, melts or solutions are projected onto a substrate to form coatings, films or surface treatments. This subclass falls under the broader categories of performing operations, spraying or atomising in general, and processes for applying fluent materials to surfaces.
[B05D 2505/50] Polyimides -- The entry B05D 2505/50 in the Cooperative Patent Classification (CPC) by EPO/USPTO pertains to processes for applying polyimides, a type of high-performance polymer, to surfaces. This classification is part of a broader hierarchy that includes spraying or atomising and the application of fluent materials to surfaces.
[B05D 2506/00] Halogenated polymers -- B05D 2506/00 in the Cooperative Patent Classification system refers to processes for applying halogenated polymers to surfaces. This entry is part of a broader category that deals with spraying or atomising techniques and the application of fluent materials onto various substrates.
[B05D 2506/10] Fluorinated polymers -- The subclass B05D 2506/10 relates to the use of fluorinated polymers as the fluent material in spraying or atomising processes for coating surfaces. It includes methods and equipment for preparing, delivering and applying these halogenated polymer dispersions or solutions to achieve a fluoropolymer film on a substrate.
[B05D 2506/15] Polytetrafluoroethylene [PTFE] -- The code B05D 2506/15 designates the use of polytetrafluoroethylene (PTFE) as a fluent, halogenated polymer in processes that spray or atomise materials onto surfaces. It covers methods for applying PTFE coatings or films by spraying, atomising or similar techniques.
[B05D 2506/20] Chlorinated polymers -- The B05D 2506/20 subclass concerns the spraying or atomising of chlorinated polymers as a fluent material onto a substrate. It includes methods for applying such halogenated polymer powders or melts to surfaces for coating, film formation or surface treatment.
[B05D 2506/25] PVC -- The entry B05D 2506/25 covers the use of poly(vinyl chloride), a halogenated polymer, in spraying or atomising processes that apply fluent materials to surfaces. It falls under the hierarchy of performing operations, specifically spraying or atomising in general, and processes for applying fluent materials to surfaces.
[B05D 2507/00] Polyolefins -- The CPC subclass B05D 2507/00 covers the use of polyolefin materials in spraying or atomising processes that apply fluent substances to surfaces, such as coating, sealing or laminating operations where polyolefin powders, melts or dispersions are projected onto a substrate. It falls under performing operations, spraying or atomising in general, and processes for applying fluent materials.
[B05D 2507/005] modified -- B05D 2507/005 in the Cooperative Patent Classification refers to processes for applying fluent materials to surfaces where the material is a modified polyolefin. This classification is specific to alterations or enhancements made to polyolefins before application, such as through chemical modification or blending with other polymers.
[B05D 2507/01] Polyethylene -- B05D 2507/01 relates to the use of polyethylene as a fluent coating material applied by spraying, atomising or similar depositing operations onto a substrate. The subclass covers processes that prepare, melt, and atomise polyethylene for forming protective, functional or decorative films on surfaces, including methods of controlling spray parameters and post-application treatments.
[B05D 2507/015] modified -- This subclass covers processes in which polyolefin materials that have been chemically or physically modified are applied to a substrate by spraying or atomising. It includes preparation of modified polyolefin dispersions, melt or solution spraying, and related surface-coating operations.
[B05D 2507/02] Polypropylene -- B05D 2507/02 covers the spraying or atomising of polypropylene, a polyolefin, onto surfaces as part of processes for applying fluent materials. It falls under the hierarchy of performing operations, specifically spraying or atomising in general, and pertains to methods of coating or treating substrates with molten or softened polypropylene.
[B05D 2507/025] modified -- B05D 2507/025 in the Cooperative Patent Classification refers to processes for applying fluent materials to surfaces where the polyolefin is chemically or physically modified, enhancing its properties for specific applications. This classification is part of a broader category dealing with spraying and atomising techniques.
[B05D 2508/00] Polyesters -- B05D 2508/00 in the Cooperative Patent Classification system refers to processes for applying polyesters as fluent materials to surfaces. This classification is specific to the use of polyester materials within the broader context of spraying or atomising techniques.
[B05D 2518/00] Other type of polymers -- B05D 2518/00 covers spray or atomising processes in which polymers other than the commonly defined thermoplastic or thermosetting types are used as the fluent material applied to a surface. It falls under the broader categories of performing operations, spraying or atomising in general, and processes for applying fluent materials to surfaces. This subclass addresses special polymer formulations employed in coating, sealing, or protective applications.
[B05D 2518/10] Silicon-containing polymers -- B05D 2518/10 covers silicon-containing polymers used in spraying or atomising operations to apply fluent materials to surfaces. These polymers serve as binders, film formers or additives in coating, printing and other surface-treatment processes, and belong to the CPC hierarchy of performing operations - spraying or atomising in general - processes for applying fluent materials to surfaces.
[B05D 2518/12] Ceramic precursors (polysiloxanes, polysilazanes) -- Ceramic precursors such as polysiloxanes and polysilazanes used in spray or atomising processes for applying fluent materials to surfaces, wherein the precursors are employed to form ceramic or glass-like coatings after deposition and subsequent conversion. This falls under the CPC subclass B05D 2518/12.
[B05D 2520/00] Water-based dispersions -- B05D 2520/00 covers the use of water-based dispersions in processes that spray or atomise fluent materials onto surfaces. It pertains to formulations in which the active component is suspended in an aqueous medium, enabling uniform coating or treatment during spraying operations. This subclass falls under spraying or atomising in general and the broader category of processes for applying fluent materials.
[B05D 2520/05] Latex -- B05D 2520/05 covers the use of water-based dispersions, commonly called latex, in processes that spray or atomise fluent materials onto surfaces. It relates to applying these latex formulations as a coating or film-forming agent in general spraying operations. This subclass falls under performing operations → spraying or atomising in general → processes for applying fluent materials to surfaces, in general.
[B05D 2520/10] PVC [Plastisol] -- The subclass B05D 2520/10 covers the spraying or atomising of water-based PVC plastisol dispersions onto a surface, where the PVC particles are suspended in a liquid carrier and form a solid polymer film after drying or curing. It is used for coating, sealing or decorating substrates by applying the fluent material in a fine spray.
[B05D 2530/00] Rubber or the like -- B05D 2530/00 addresses processes that apply rubber or similar elastomeric materials to a surface by spraying, atomising or other fluent-material techniques. It falls under the broader categories of performing operations, spraying or atomising in general, and processes for applying fluent materials to surfaces. The entry covers methods for coating, sealing or treating substrates with rubber-based compounds.
[B05D 2601/00] Inorganic fillers -- B05D 2601/00 relates to the use of inorganic filler particles in processes for applying fluent materials by spraying or atomising, covering the addition of powders, granules or mineral powders to paints, coatings, adhesives or similar substances to modify properties such as texture, strength or resistance.
[B05D 2601/02] used for pigmentation effect, e.g. metallic effect -- B05D 2601/02 covers processes that apply inorganic filler particles to a surface by spraying or atomising to achieve a decorative pigmentation, such as a metallic effect.
[B05D 2601/04] Mica -- B05D 2601/04 covers the use of mica as an inorganic filler in processes that spray or atomise fluids onto surfaces, a subcategory of general operations for applying fluent materials. It pertains to methods where mica particles are dispersed in a medium and deposited by spraying, atomising, or similar techniques.
[B05D 2601/06] Coated Mica -- A coated mica is an inorganic filler whose surface has been treated by spraying or atomising a fluent coating material to modify its properties, such as adhesion, conductivity or visual effect, before being incorporated into paints, inks or other surface-applied formulations. This subclass covers processes for applying such coated particles to substrates and the resulting coated mica products.
[B05D 2601/08] Aluminium flakes or platelets -- The CPC subclass B05D 2601/08 relates to the use of aluminium flakes or platelets as inorganic fillers in spraying or atomising processes that apply fluent materials to surfaces. It is positioned under performing operations, then spraying or atomising in general, and finally processes for applying fluent materials to surfaces.
[B05D 2601/10] Other metals -- This subclass covers processes that apply metal powders other than iron, steel, aluminium, copper or their alloys as inorganic fillers to a surface by spraying, atomising or similar techniques, resulting in a coating or layer of the metal on the substrate.
[B05D 2601/20] used for non-pigmentation effect -- This subgroup B05D 2601/20 concerns the use of inorganic filler particles in spraying or atomising operations where the aim is not pigmentation but to impart other functional effects such as texture, durability or chemical resistance to a surface. It falls under the hierarchy of performing operations, spraying or atomising in general, and processes for applying fluent materials to surfaces, in general.
[B05D 2601/22] Silica -- B05D 2601/22 covers spraying or atomising processes in which silica, an inorganic filler, is the fluent material applied to a surface. It lies within the hierarchy of performing operations, spraying or atomising in general, and processes for applying fluent materials to surfaces.
[B05D 2601/24] Titanium dioxide, e.g. rutile -- B05D 2601/24 covers the use of titanium dioxide, such as rutile, as an inorganic filler in processes that spray or atomise fluid materials onto surfaces. The entry relates to applying TiO₂ particles as a coating or functional layer during general spraying operations.
[B05D 2601/26] Abrasives -- The CPC subclass B05D 2601/26 covers processes that apply inorganic abrasive filler particles to a surface by spraying, atomising or other fluid-based means. It includes methods such as abrasive blasting or spray-coating where the abrasive material is delivered in a fluent stream and deposited on the workpiece to modify its texture or shape. This falls under performing operations, spraying or atomising, and processes for applying fluent materials to surfaces.
[B05D 2601/28] Metals -- B05D 2601/28 pertains to the application of metals as inorganic fillers in processes for spraying or atomising fluent materials onto surfaces. This classification is part of a detailed system used by the European Patent Office and United States Patent and Trademark Office to categorize patent documents.
[B05D 2602/00] Organic fillers -- B05D 2602/00 relates to the use of organic filler materials-such as polymers, waxes, resins or other carbon-based substances-added to liquids, pastes or powders during spraying or atomising processes to modify the properties of the deposited layer, for example improving adhesion, bulk, texture or protective performance of coatings applied to surfaces.
[B05D 2701/00] Coatings being able to withstand changes in the shape of the substrate or to withstand welding -- B05D 2701/00 classifies spraying or atomising techniques and coating formulations that retain their integrity when the underlying substrate is deformed, stretched or welded, such as flexible paints, weld-compatible finishes or elastomeric layers. It covers the composition of the coating, the method of application, and any post-application treatment that enables the film to accommodate shape changes without cracking or delaminating.
[B05D 2701/10] withstanding draw and redraw process, punching -- B05D2701/10 covers coating processes that keep the coating intact while the substrate is deformed, such as during draw-and-redraw forming or punching, allowing the coating to survive shape changes or welding.
[B05D 2701/20] withstanding rolling -- B05D 2701/20 covers processes for applying fluent coating materials that remain intact when the coated substrate is subjected to rolling deformation. It includes methods and apparatus that ensure the coating can flex, stretch or resist cracking during the rolling of metal sheets or similar products.
