[B06B 2201/74] Underwater -- B06B 2201/74 covers methods and devices that generate or transmit infrasonic, sonic or ultrasonic vibrations for mechanical work performed underwater. It is applied to uses such as underwater cleaning, cutting, inspection or acoustic manipulation where the vibration source operates beneath a fluid medium.
[B06B 2201/75] Repelling animals, insects, humans -- B06B 2201/75 covers apparatus and methods that use mechanical vibrations in the infrasonic, sonic, or ultrasonic range to deter or repel animals, insects, or humans. Such systems employ vibration generators to create a repelling effect as a means of mechanical work.
[B06B 2201/76] Medical, dental -- B06B 2201/76 classifies medical and dental devices that use infrasonic, sonic or ultrasonic vibrations to perform mechanical work, such as ultrasound diagnostic or therapeutic equipment and dental scalers that generate or transmit sound waves for treatment, cleaning or imaging.
[B06B 2201/77] Atomizers -- B06B 2201/77 covers atomizers that employ mechanical vibrations of infrasonic, sonic or ultrasonic frequency to break a liquid into fine droplets for general mechanical work such as spraying, humidifying or fuel injection. These devices generate or transmit vibrating energy to the fluid, creating a mist without relying on pressure-driven nozzles.
[B06B 3/00] Methods or apparatus specially adapted for transmitting mechanical vibrations of infrasonic, sonic, or ultrasonic frequency -- B06B 3/00 covers methods and devices specially designed to generate or convey mechanical vibrations in the infrasonic, sonic or ultrasonic range for the purpose of performing mechanical work. It includes transducers, sonicators, vibratory tools and related apparatus that transform electrical or other energy into controlled vibration for tasks such as cleaning, material processing, testing or medical therapy.
[B06B 3/02] involving a change of amplitude -- B06B 3/02 relates to methods or apparatus that generate or transmit mechanical vibrations while varying the vibration amplitude. The amplitude modulation is used to control the amount of mechanical work delivered in infrasonic, sonic or ultrasonic systems.
[B06B 3/04] involving focusing or reflecting -- Methods or devices that generate infrasonic, sonic or ultrasonic vibrations and shape the wave energy by focusing or reflecting it to enhance transmission, concentration or directionality for mechanical work. This includes lenses, mirrors, reflectors or other wave-guiding elements incorporated into vibratory equipment.
[B07] SEPARATING SOLIDS FROM SOLIDS -- B07 covers processes and devices that separate one solid material from another solid material, such as sorting, sifting, screening, magnetic or density-based separation, and related mechanical or physical techniques. It includes apparatuses and methods for removing contaminants, reclaiming waste, or classifying granular or particulate solids.
[B07B] SEPARATING SOLIDS FROM SOLIDS BY SIEVING, SCREENING, SIFTING OR BY USING GAS CURRENTS -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Apparatus or methods for the separation of solids from solids that include:
[B07B 1/00] Sieving, screening, sifting, or sorting solid materials using networks, gratings, grids, or the like -- B07B 1/00 covers processes that separate solid particles by passing them through or over structures such as meshes, screens, gratings or grids to achieve sieving, screening, sifting or sorting. It includes both manual and mechanized methods that rely on physical openings or patterned networks to classify materials by size or shape.

The focus is on how these elements are arranged to optimise classification, transport and discharge of the material within the gas-stream apparatus.

[B07B 1/145] the material to be screened moving along the axis of the parallel elements -- The code B07B 1/145 covers sieving or screening processes in which the material to be separated travels parallel to the axis of the screen's parallel elements, such as rows of wires, gratings or grids, rather than across them. This arrangement is typical of linear or conveyor-type screens that move the feed along the length of the screen.

[B07B 1/18] Drum screens -- B07B 1/18 covers drum screens, which are cylindrical rotating devices that separate solid particles by passing material over a continuous or segmented screen surface, using sieving or screening action. The screens may be perforated, meshed or slotted and are used in mineral processing, recycling and waste treatment to classify material by size.

[B07B 1/288] Tumbling screens -- B07B 1/288 covers tumbling screens, devices that use a rotating or oscillating motion to feed solid material onto a screen or mesh for size-based separation. The tumbling action agitates the material, enhancing screening efficiency and reducing clogging, and is commonly employed in bulk handling of granular or particulate products.

[B07B 13/00] Other separating, e.g. grading, resulting automatically from the construction of the apparatus used and properties of the material concerned -- B07B 13/00 covers apparatus that separates solid particles from one another by mechanisms such as grading, where the separation occurs automatically because of the device's design and the material's own characteristics, rather than by active sieving, screening, or gas streams. It includes systems that rely on size-dependent flow paths, angles of repose, or other inherent physical properties to achieve the division of solids.

[B07B 2201/00] Details applicable to machines for screening using sieves or gratings -- B07B 2201/00 relates to the specific design features of machines that separate solid particles by passing them over or through sieves or gratings, including aspects such as sieve framing, drive arrangements, material feeding, and discharge mechanisms.
[B07B 2201/02] Fastening means for fastening screens to their frames which do not stretch or sag the screening surfaces -- The CPC subclass B07B 2201/02 covers devices that secure screens to their frames in screening or sieving machines while preventing any stretching or sagging of the screening surface. It relates to fastening means designed to keep the mesh taut and stable during operation.
[B07B 2201/04] Multiple deck screening devices comprising one or more superimposed screens -- A multiple-deck screening device uses two or more superimposed screens arranged in series so that material passes through successive openings, achieving separation on several size levels in a single machine. The construction may include fixed or interchangeable screens and is employed for bulk solids such as grain, mineral or recycled material.
[B07B 2220/00] Type of materials being separated -- B07B 2220/00 designates the category that specifies the nature of the solid materials being separated when sieving, screening, sifting or using gas streams. It covers separations of powders, granules, pellets, aggregates and similar bulk solids. The subclass is used to further differentiate processes based on the material type.
[B07B 2220/02] Plastics -- This subclass covers the separation of plastics-either alone or together with other solid materials-by means of sieving, screening, sifting or the use of gas currents, including processes for classifying, cleaning or sizing plastic particles.
[B07B 2220/04] Batteries -- B07B 2220/04 covers methods and apparatus for separating the solid components of batteries-such as electrode plates, active material particles, or casing fragments-by sieving, screening, sifting, or using gas currents. It is applied to processes that isolate or recycle battery parts through mechanical or pneumatic solid-solid separation.
[B07B 2230/00] Specific aspects relating to the whole -- This subclass groups patent disclosures that address overall features of sieving or screening equipment or processes, such as the complete apparatus, its configuration, or the entire operation of separating solids by mechanical screening or gas streams, rather than individual components. It is used for inventions concerning the whole system rather than specific parts.
[B07B 2230/01] Wet separation -- B07B 2230/01 refers to wet separation methods used to divide one solid material from another by employing a liquid medium. The process typically involves sieving, screening, or sifting the solids while the material is immersed or suspended in a fluid, which may be a water-based solution or other liquids, to improve efficiency and reduce dust. This technique is common in mineral processing, food production and chemical manufacturing.
[B07B 2230/04] The screen or the screened materials being subjected to ultrasonic vibration -- This subclass covers sieving operations in which the screen or the material on the screen is subjected to ultrasonic vibration, using high-frequency sound waves to improve particle separation and prevent blinding. Such ultrasonic assistance can increase efficiency and accuracy of solid-solid separation.
